PT P
PN CN113716934-A
TI Preparing flexible aerogel composite insulating material, comprises mixing silicon source, solvent and hydrolysis catalyst, hydrolyzing, adding softener and gel catalyst, soaking aged composite material into modified liquid, and drying.
AU LUO S
   SONG D
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO-MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 2021E8533L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing flexible aerogel composite insulating material, comprises (i) mixing silicon source, solvent and hydrolysis catalyst according to the volume ratio of 1:1-9:0.001-0.005, stirring, and hydrolyzing to obtain silicon dioxide sol, (ii) adding softener and gel catalyst, mixing uniformly, spraying into substrate, and filling the substrate with mixed solution, (iii) conveying the composite material to the temperature of 35-45 for 10-30 minutes, melting gel and substrate into a whole, fixing and molding, (iv) soaking formed composite material in the solvent, (v) soaking aged composite material in modified liquid, and (vi) drying the supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing flexible aerogel composite insulating material.
   ADVANTAGE - The insulating material has excellent heat preservation performance, noise reduction performance, sunshade performance, fireproof performance, convenient construction, effectively shortens the gel process time, reduces the reaction temperature, simplifies the process flow, enables the continuous production, and has low production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicon source comprises methyl orthosilicate, ethyl orthosilicate, and/or polypolysiloxane. The solvent comprises methanol, propanol, ethanol, and/or acetone. The hydrolysis catalyst comprises pure water, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid, and/or oxalic acid. The softener is vinyl trimethoxy silane, methyl trimethoxy silane, vinyl triethoxy silane, and/or methyl triethoxy silane. The gel catalyst is ammonia water, ammonium fluoride, ammonium carbonate, ethylenediamine, ammonium bicarbonate, and/or sodium hydroxide. The substrate is carbon fiber, polyester fiber, superfine fiber, and/or aramid fiber. The surface modifying agent comprises hexamethyl disiloxane, hexamethyl disiloxane, and/or trimethyl chlorosilane.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

The coating layer is coated on the face of the anti-corsion layer. The anti-carbonation layer is obtained by fluorination treatment on the substrate of the substrate.

TI Radio frequency carbon dioxide laser total reflection mirror adjusting method, involves providing adjusting shaft with adjusting hole, where hole is inserted with jackscrew, and top thread prevents adjusting shaft from rotating.
AU ZHANG J
AE BEIJING KINGLY PHOTOELECTRON CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021D59617
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an adjusting of total reflection mirror of radio frequency CO2 laser, comprising a total reflection mirror and a lens mounting seat, the total reflection mirror is located in the lens mounting seat, and the lens mounting seat is fixedly connected on the adjusting shaft; the adjusting shaft is penetrated with a middle shaft; the surface of the lens mounting seat and the adjusting shaft form a circular surface; and the circular surface is coaxially set with the middle shaft; the adjusting shaft can rotate around the middle shaft; the adjusting shaft is provided with an adjusting hole; and the adjusting hole is inserted with a jackscrew; and when the jackscrew is inserted into the adjusting hole, the jackscrew can prevent the adjusting shaft from rotating. In the implementation process of the utility model, it is simple and convenient and it is more practical to adjust the total reflection mirror.

PN CN113586277-A
TI On-ship host power limiting system has shaft power monitoring system used for collecting a data, host control system for judging whether value of main machine shaft power meets preset condition, and control instruction used for limiting improvement of power of host machine shaft.
AU WU S
   DING J
AE SHANGHAI WAIGAOQIAO SHIPBUILDING CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 2021C8295W
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a shaft power monitoring system used for collecting a data for calculating a main machine shaft power, converting into the main machine shaft power and transmitting the main machine shaft power to a host control system. The host control system is used for judging whether value of the main machine shaft power meets a preset condition, if so, the host control system sends a control instruction. The control instruction is used for limiting an improvement of the power of the host machine shaft.
   USE - On-ship host power limiting system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system limits the host SMCR after leaving factory, so as to optimize the oil consumption of the main engine on the ship, effectively reduce EEDI index, reduce the discharge of carbon dioxide, and reduce the effect of air pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method for limiting power of host on ship.

PN CN113509932-A
TI Semiconductor photocatalyst comprises S compound (zinc indium sulfide) as carrier, the surface of which is loaded with copper ion as the active centre.
AU DONG F
   CHEN S
   SHENG J
   XIAO L
   SUN Y
AE UNIV CHINA ELECTRONIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UEST-C)
GA 2021C5933V
AB    NOVELTY - Semiconductor photocatalyst comprises S compound as carrier, the surface of which is loaded with copper ion as the active centre.
   USE - Used as semiconductor photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The Cu-doped ZnIn2S4 semiconductor visible photocatalyst reduces carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide performance is not doped with ZnIn2S4 photocatalyst 13.7 times, and the method has mild condition, simple operation, good for mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing photocatalyst comprising (1) mixing the Zn-containing precursor and the In-containing precursor, then adding water to prepare precursor solution, stirring uniformly to obtain mixed solution a, (2) adding copper compound into the mixed solution a prepared in step (1), and uniformly stirring to obtain the mixed solution b, (3) adding surfactant into the mixed solution b prepared in step (2), and stirring uniformly to obtain mixed solution c, (4) adding S containing precursor into the mixed solution c prepared in step (3), after uniformly stirring, carrying out hydrothermal reaction to obtain solid mixture, centrifuging and washing the solid mixture and drying to obtain the Cu-doped ZnIn2S4 microsphere solid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The S compound is zinc indium sulfide (ZnIn2S4). The catalyst is composed of a micro-sphere structure of zinc ion in zinc indium sulfide replaced by copper ions. The mass fraction of copper ion in the catalyst is 0.1-1 wt.%. The zinc containing precursor is zinc sulfate heptahydrate, the In-containing precursor is indium nitrate trihydrate and the Cu compound is copper nitrate. The surfactant is hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide; the S-containing precursor is thioacetamide. The mass ratio of the copper ion in the Cu compound to the mass of the generated ZnIn2S4 is (0.1-1%): 1.Preferred Method: The reaction temperature of the hydrothermal reaction in step (4) is 160degrees Celsius and the reaction time is 16 hours. The mass ratio of the Cu ion in the Cu compound to the mass of the generated ZnIn2S4 is 0.7%:1.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.)

AU BRIGLIA A
   GRANADOS L
   LEAUTE M
   MACHON D D B D
   MOREL T
   RENOU E
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2021B3598R
AB    NOVELTY - The gas treatment installation comprises a PSA unit for gas separation, which has an adsorber (2a,2b,2c), and a gas compression unit (3) located downstream of the PSA unit. A gas delivery pipe (4) is provided, which fluidly connects the PSA unit to the gas compression unit. A gasometer device (5) is fluidly connected to the gas delivery pipe. The gasometer device is connected as a bypass (8) to the gas delivery pipe.

   ZHANG X
   SU Y

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of bone head base biological carbon comprises cleaning animal bones with deionized water and drying them in oven at 60 degrees C overnight; crushing dried solid to particle size of 1-3 cm; putting solid powder and 0.1 mol/l calcium nitrate solution in hydrothermal reaction kettle; sealing reaction kettle and placing in oven to heat for hydrothermal pre-carbonization; cooling kettle at room temperature; filtering solids; washing and drying; putting solid in tube furnace and pyrolyzing at high temperature in nitrogen atmosphere to obtain solid product; and crushing solid product to 100-150 meshes to obtain bone-based biochar.

Preferred Method: The high-temperature pyrolysis at 700-900 degrees C, preferably 800-900 degrees C for 3-5 hours, preferably 5 hours.

PN CN113072511-A; CN113072511-B
TI Preparing fluorine-containing triazine-based            organic porous material by adding fluorine-containing            aromatic compound and 2,4,6-trichloro-1,3,5-triazine to            reaction vessel, adding catalyst anhydrous aluminum            chloride, condensing and refluxing, washing, filtering            with solvent, heating and drying.
AU DONG B
   WANG R
   XI S
   WANG D
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 202185111P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing fluorine-containing                triazine-based organic porous material involves (a)                adding the fluorine-containing aromatic compound                and 2,4,6-trichloro-1,3,5-triazine to the reaction                vessel containing the reaction organic solvent, and                adding the catalyst anhydrous aluminum chloride,                condensing and refluxing at 60-80 degrees C for                16-24 hours to obtain the crude product, (b)                washing and filtering the crude product with a                solvent to obtain a pure product, and (c) heating                the pure product at 100-120 degrees C under vacuum                conditions and drying for 10-24 hours to obtain a                fluorine-containing triazine-based organic porous                material.
   USE - Method used for preparing fluorine-containing                triazine-based organic porous material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in operation, has low                reaction temperature, and avoids the occurrence of                high-temperature carbonization and defluorination,                and greatly improves the adsorption performance of                carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (a), the molar                  ratio of the fluorine-containing aromatic                  compound to 2,4,6-trichloro-1,3,5-triazine is                  (1.36-1.66):1, preferably 1.5:1. The molar ratio                  of the catalyst to 2,4,6-trichloro-1,3,5-triazine                  is (3-8):1, preferably 3.6:1. In step (a), the                  fluorine-containing aromatic compound is                  1,4-difluorobenzene, 1,2,4,5-tetrafluorobenzene,                  and/or 4H,4H-octafluorobiphenyl. In step (a),                  when the fluorine-containing aromatic compound is                  1,4-difluorobenzene, the reaction organic solvent                  is chloroform, dichloromethane, and/or                  1,2-dichloroethane, when the fluorine-containing                  aromatic compound is 1,2,4,5-tetrafluorobenzene                  or 4H,4H-octafluorobiphenyl, the reaction organic                  solvent is 1,2-dichloroethane. In step (a), the                  concentration of the                  2,4,6-trichloro-1,3,5-triazine in the reaction                  organic solvent is 0.05-0.2 mol/l. In step (b),                  the crude product is washed 3-4 times 1M                  hydrochloric acid solution, acetone,                  tetrahydrofuran, methanol, and                  N,N-dimethylformamide successively, and filtering                  to obtain a pure product.

AB    NOVELTY - Operation of blast furnace involves blowing                high concentration hydrogen-containing-gas                containing 80 mol% or more hydrogen gas, at                blowing-in temperature at more than normal                temperature to 300 degrees C or less, at blowing                amount of hydrogen gas of 200-500 Nm3/ton, or at                blowing-in temperature at 300-600 degrees C or                less, and at blowing amount of hydrogen gas of 145                Nm3/ton or more, or blowing-in temperature at more                than normal temperature to 600-900 degrees C, and                at blowing amount of hydrogen gas of 125 Nm3/ton or                more, or blowing-in temperature at more than 1200                degrees C, and at blowing amount of hydrogen gas of                100 Nm3/ton or more, from tuyere.

TI Low-density aerogel/melamine foam composite material used for rocket fairing, comprises silica aerogel and melamine foam, prepared by taking water glass, sodium citrate, anhydrous ethanol and deionized water, and adding phosphoric acid.
AU LI Y
   FAN X
AE FULAIFU SHANGHAI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (FULA-Non-standard)
GA 202164997N
AB    NOVELTY - A low-density aerogel/melamine foam composite material comprises 95-98 wt.% silica aerogel and 2-5 wt.% melamine foam; prepared by taking water glass, sodium citrate, anhydrous ethanol and deionized water, adding phosphoric acid dropwise, stirring, hydrolyzing for 4-6 hours to obtain silica sol system, pouring into mold with melamine foam, immersing melamine foam, adding 0.6-0.9 mol/L ammonia and propylene glycol, standing and aging at 40-55 degrees C for 24-36 hours to obtain silica gel-foam composite foam, using n-hexane for solvent replacement, and performing supercritical drying in carbon dioxide environment.
   USE - The low-density aerogel/melamine foam composite material used for rocket fairing (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material improves the mechanical stability of the aerogel, solves the problem that the aerogel has brittleness, non-toughness, and is easy to break when pressing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the rocket fairing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of water glass, sodium citrate, absolute ethanol, deionized water and phosphoric acid is 1:(0.4-0.9):(10-20):(8-12):(0.008-0.012). The molar ratio of ammonia water to water glass is (0.00002-0.004):1, and the molar ratio of propylene glycol to water glass is (0.4-0.8):1. Preferred Method: The number of solvent replacement is 3-6 times, and the time for each replacement is 12-24 hours.

TI Improving organic acid synthesis ability of            filamentous fungi recombinant bacteria comprises e.g.            providing gene engineering in filamentous fungi into            organic acid synthesis positive regulatory gene and            comparing recombinant strain.
AU TIAN C
   LI J
   CHEN B
   ZHAO Z
   GU S
   LIU Q
   SUN T
   SUN W
AE TIANJIN INST IND BIOTECHNOLOGY (TIIB-C)
GA 2021620128
AB    NOVELTY - Improving organic acid synthesis ability of                filamentous fungi recombinant bacteria comprises                providing gene engineering in the filamentous fungi                into organic acid synthesis positive regulatory                gene, or/and the lower adjusting acid synthesis                negative control gene, comparing the recombinant                strain with the starting strain, improving the                production capacity of binary organic acid, where                the organic acid comprises malic acid, succinic                acid, fumaric acid, oxaloacetic acid, preferably                malic acid and/or succinic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for improving organic                acid synthesis ability of filamentous fungi                recombinant bacteria.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can utilizes monosaccharide,                polysaccharide, glycan or mixed sugar, and fixing                carbon dioxide (CO2) to synthesize organic                acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1)                recombinant bacteria; and (2) producing organic                acids using the recombinant, comprising fermenting                recombinant bacteria to produce organic acids with                monosaccharides, glycans and/or plant biomass as                substrates, preferably, the recombinant bacteria is                Myceliophthora thermophila, and more preferably                Myceliophthora thermophila and Myceliophthora                heterostriata. The organic acid is malic acid                and/or succinic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The exogenous organic                  acid is introduced to synthesize the positive                  regulatory gene and the expression of the                  negative regulatory gene is downregulated. The                  filamentous fungal cell is Neurospora,                  Aspergillus, Trichoderma, Penicillium,                  Myceliophthora, Sporotrichum, Fusarium, Rhizopus,                  Mucor and Paecilomyces, more preferably, the                  Myceliophthora is Myceliophthora thermophila, and                  Myceliophthora heterothallica. The organic acid                  production capacity of the recombinant strain is                  increased or increased by at least 10% compared                  with the original strain, preferably, 10-50%,                  more preferably, 50-500%. The organic acid                  synthesis positive regulatory gene is                  5-phosphoribokinase, 1,5-bisphosphoribose                  carboxylation/oxygenase, and/or sugar                  transporter. The down-regulated organic acid                  synthesis negative regulatory gene is lactate                  dehydrogenase, pyruvate decarboxylase, and/or                  pyruvate carboxykinase. The amino acid sequence                  of the 5-phosphoribokinase is 402 amino acids                  (SEQ ID NO: 3), the amino acid sequence of the                  1,5-bisphosphoribose carboxylation/oxygenase is                  466 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 1), and the amino                  acid sequence of the sugar transporter is 574                  amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 56). The amino acid                  sequence of lactate dehydrogenase is 319 amino                  acids (SEQ ID NO: 15), the amino acid sequence of                  pyruvate decarboxylase is 574 amino acids (SEQ ID                  NO: 13), and the amino acid sequence of pyruvate                  carboxykinase is 591 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 17)                  as given in the specification. The monosaccharide                  is glucose, xylose, and/or arabinose. The glycan,                  coated crystalline cellulose, and/or                  hemicellulose. The plant biomass is crop straw,                  forestry waste, energy plant or part or all of                  the decomposition product, where the crop straw                  comprises corn straw, wheat straw, rice straw,                  sorghum straw, soybean straw, cotton straw,                  bagasse, and/or corn core. The forest waste                  comprises branches and leaves and sawdust. The                  energy plant comprises sweet sorghum,                  switchgrass, grass, and/or reed. Preferred                  Method: The method of introducing the                  positive-regulating gene for organic acid                  synthesis is realized by introducing an                  expression vector containing the                  positive-regulating gene, the down-regulation of                  organic acid synthesis negative regulatory genes                  is achieved by gene knockout or gene editing or                  inhibitors, and the inhibitors are antibody,                  inhibitory mRNA, antisense RNA, microRNA, miRNA,                  siRNA, shRNA or activity Inhibitor, preferably,                  down-regulating the expression level of                  negatively regulated genes refers to the                  realization of genome editing methods based on                  CRISPR/Cas9.

PN CN112717882-A
TI Preparing peony shell-based activated carbon for            volatile organic compounds adsorption by mechanically            crushing dried peony shell, carbonizing, adding organic            acid potassium salt, performing microwave activation,            filtering and drying.
AU LI T
   REN B
   SHA J
   LI Y
   JIANG G
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202150545Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing peony shell-based                activated carbon for volatile organic compounds                (VOCs) adsorption involves (i) mechanically                crushing dried peony shell into particles with a                particle size of 0.075-0.180 mm, and carbonizing at                400-800 degrees C for 40-150 minutes to obtain                peony shell charcoal, (ii) mixing the cooled peony                shell charcoal with organic acid potassium salt and                placing into a microwave activation device, and                performing microwave activation under the carbon                dioxide atmosphere and (iii) cooling, using lye for                alkaline washing, neutralizing, filtering and                drying to obtain peony shell-based activated                carbon, where the organic acid potassium salt                comprises potassium citrate, potassium malate,                potassium maleate or potassium succinate.
   USE - Method for preparing peony shell-based                activated carbon for volatile organic compounds                (VOCs) adsorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts abundant sources of raw                materials, realizes the resource utilization of                agricultural waste, achieves the purpose of using                waste to control pollution and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbonization                  process is carried out at 600 degrees C for 90                  minutes, the microwave power is 600 W, the                  activation time is 30 minutes, carbon dioxide                  flow rate is 100 ml/minute, the pH value of peony                  shell-based activated carbon is 6.5, after                  cleaning, the drying temperature is 373.15 K, and                  the drying time is 3 hours. Preferred                  Composition: The mass ratio of organic acid                  potassium salt to peony shell base is 1:(1-3):1.                  The particle size of mechanically pulverized                  peony shells is 0.124 mm. The mass ratio of                  organic acid potassium salt to peony shell-based                  charcoal is 1.5:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid                  potassium salt comprises potassium maleate.

PN CN112680275-A; CN112680275-B
TI Processing supercritical extraction of Ganoderma lucidum spore oil with high extraction rate comprises e.g. taking fresh Ganoderma lucidum spore powder for processing freeze-thawing circulation, steam blasting, and vacuumizing extracting.
AU LI G
   WANG J
   WANG L
   LI S
AE NANCHANG ZHIHE BIOLOGICAL ENG CO LTD (NANC-Non-standard)
GA 202149248T
AB    NOVELTY - Processing supercritical extraction of Ganoderma lucidum spore oil with high extraction rate comprises (i) taking fresh G. lucidum spore powder for processing freeze-thawing circulation, (ii) steam blasting the frozen and thawed G. lucidum spore powder, firstly passing ethanol steam to an explosion container under 0.8-1.2 MPa, and passing in water steam to the explosion container under 1.5-1.8 MPa for 50-60 seconds to obtain G. lucidum spore slurry, (iii) vacuumizing extracting and dehumidifying the obtained G. lucidum spore slurry and granulating, (iv) subjecting the G. lucidum spore powder particles to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and (v) separating the extract by using three-stage vacuum separating and collecting the G. lucidum spore oil in a separating tank I and a separating tank II.
   USE - The method is useful for processing supercritical extraction of G. lucidum spore oil with high extraction rate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high extraction rate of G. lucidum spore oil and high content of total triterpenes of active ingredients.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the freeze-thawing circulation is carried out by freezing the spore powder at -15 degrees C to -18 degrees C for 12-16 hours and thawing at 20-25oC for 6-8 hours, where the number of circulation is 5-10 times. In the step (v), the separating in the separating vessel I is carried out at 40-45 degrees C under 12-14 MPa. The separating in the separating vessel II is carried out at 35-45 degrees C under 8-9 MPa. In the step (iii), the vacuumizing extracting and dehumidifying is carried out in a low-temperature vacuum drying device at 40-50 degrees C. The moisture content of the blasted G. lucidum spore material after dehumidifying is 20-30%. The granulating further comprises includes mixing the blasted G. lucidum spores obtained by dehumidifying with low-substituted hydroxypropyl cellulose with a mass of 5-10%.The number of meshes in the granulating is 40-50 meshes. In the step (iv), the specific conditions of supercritical extraction comprises extracting is carried out at 40-50 degrees C under 20-30 MPa for 30-60 minutes, where the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-50 l/hour. Preferred Components: The ethanol steam comprises saturated steam of absolute ethyl alcohol.

PN IN201921038111-A
TI Improved method for synthesizing ionic liquid functionalized polysulfone membrane and incorporating graphene oxide.
AU NEMADE P R
   DHOPTE A M
   DHOPTE K B
   ZAMBARE R S
AE NEMADE P R (NEMA-Individual)
GA 202135364P
AB    NOVELTY - Improved method for synthesizing ionic liquid functionalized polysulfone membrane and incorporating graphene oxide.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing ionic liquid functionalized polysulfone membrane and incorporating graphene oxide, where the functionalized polysulfone membrane is used for gas separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides ionic liquid which will not leach out from the membrane surface hence the membrane is stable for long time; provides dispersed graphene oxide that improves gas separation flux and increased carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas selectivity of membrane; is for preparing casting solution in which methanol solvent is used instead of dimethylformamide, N-methylpyrrolidone and dimethylsulfoxide because of which process become greener; provides ionic liquid functionalized polysulfone membrane that can be more efficient comparing to other gas separation methods because of low energy and separation cost; utilizes the polysulfone that displays excellent chemical, thermal and mechanical stability; utilizes the polysulfone that is glassy polymer and possesses good resistance towards plasticization, hence it is a right candidate for polymer matrix for gas separation; and provides ionic liquid functionalized membrane that shows long term stability for gas separations at high pressure.
TF EXAMPLE - 5 g Polysulfone was dissolved in 50 ml dichloromethane followed by addition of 0.5 g zinc chloride and 8.5 ml chloromethylmethyl ether under refluxing conditions. The obtained polymer was precipitated by addition of methanol. It was washed and subsequently vacuum dried at 50 degrees C. 1 g Chloromethylated polysulfone was dissolved in 5 g 1-methylimadazole by stirring for 24 hours at room temperature. The temperature was then raised to 80 degrees C for 30 minutes for the completion of reaction gave imidazolium functionalized polysulfone, which was recovered by precipitation from the addition of acetone. Imidazolium functionalized polysulfone was vacuum dried at 50 degrees C for 12 hours. Graphene oxide was dispersed in 4 g methanol under sonication to which imidazolium functionalized polysulfone was added, and the solution was stirred until it becomes homogeneous. The solution was cast on a PVC sheet using a doctor blade to give a film with a dry thickness of 30-40 mu m. The cast film was dried at room temperature for 24 hours to obtain imidazolium functionalized polysulfone-graphene oxide membrane.

PN KR2021032229-A
TI Recovering lithium salt, comprises e.g. mixing lithium-containing metal salt aqueous solution with organic solvent extracting agent to transfer lithium into organic solvent extract agent and recovering solvent extractant of organic phase.
AU LEE J
   CHOI Y
   KYU K
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 202129874P
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering lithium salt, comprises (a) mixing lithium-containing metal salt aqueous solution with organic solvent extracting agent to transfer lithium contained in lithium-containing metal salt aqueous solution into organic solvent extracting agent; (b) recovering solvent extractant of organic phase by separating layer of aqueous metal salt solution and solvent extractant of organic phase to settle mixture; (c) mixing distilled water with separated and recovered organic solvent extractant and bubbling with carbon dioxide to generate carbonate ions and hydrogen ions in distilled water, and ion-exchanging lithium contained in organic solvent extractant with hydrogen contained in distilled water; (d) allowing mixture to stand to separate layers of distilled water and organic solvent extracting agent to recover distilled water; and (e) generating lithium carbonate by reacting lithium ions and carbonate ions contained in distilled water.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering lithium salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents lowering of the process stability and does not need additional purification processes to carry out the process efficiently.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent extractant of the organic phase contains hydrogen ion. The solvent extractant of the organic phase includes vinyl phosphonic acid, acrylic acid, dialkyl phosphonic acid, di-2-ethylhexylphosphoric acid and 2-ethylhexylhydrogen 2-ethylhexylphosphoric acid or a polymer. The solvent extracting agent has a viscosity of 70-100 Pa.s. Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises adding 20-400 mol.% solvent extractant of the organic phase based on 100 mol.% lithium contained in the lithium-containing metal salt aqueous solution, in which solvent extractant of the organic phase is used by mixing with a viscosity modifier. The method further comprises concentrating the distilled water by vacuum heating, and filtering.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Methanol cracking hydrogen production method by pouring methanol into heat exchanger, preheating, vaporizing, heating in superheater, passing through reactor filled with catalyst, and catalytic cracking to obtain high-temperature mixed gas.
AU LI H
   ZHANG H
   KANGJIN T
   LIU D
   WANG S
AE GUANGDONG METHANOL HYDROGEN NEW ENERGY (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202109269T
AB    NOVELTY - A methanol cracking hydrogen production method comprises pouring methanol into heat exchanger, preheating, vaporizing methanol to obtain vaporized methanol, heating in superheater to increase the temperature of vaporized methanol to temperature required for cracking reaction, and passing vaporized methanol through reactor filled with catalyst to carry out catalytic cracking to obtain high-temperature mixed gas containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide; and passing mixed gas through heat exchanger so that the heat of high-temperature mixed gas is transferred to methanol passing through the heat exchanger, preheating methanol while reducing temperature of mixed gas, cooling, passing first cooled mixed gas through cooler while passing through water vapor, condensing vaporized methanol to purify hydrogen, cooling, passing second cooled mixed gas and condensed liquid through gas-liquid separator, separating condensed liquid, discharging, and drying to obtain dried mixed gas.
   USE - The method is for methanol cracking hydrogen production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low energy consumption, easy maintenance and low cost.

   DEIJACY F C F (DEIJ-Individual)
GA 2020C72985
AB    NOVELTY - Pharmaceutical compositions comprises synthetic surfactants as an active ingredient.
   USE - Pharmaceutical compositions used for coronaviruses and other viruses, eliminating viruses in the human respiratory system, as well as treating complications resulting from infections caused by viruses, treating infections caused by viruses in the upper and lower airways, at any stage of the disease, allowing the treatment of other diseases, for example, bacterial pneumonias that would renew if the virus is active in the lungs (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The pharmaceutical compositions has the function of destroying coronaviruses and other viruses, promoting the immediate reversal of alveolar collapse, keeping alveoli open, functional, promoting a rapid and intense increase in blood oxygenation, reducing gas rates carbon dioxide and immediately help the infected, reducing the spread of viruses, decreases the viral airway population, decreasing lung compliance, increasing alveolar recruitment, resulting in greater functional residual capacity (CRF), has a chemically stable molecules, always maintain the surfactant function inside the alveolus, do not decompose when plasma proteins invade the alveolar space, in the event of acute lung injury caused by viruses or other agents.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The pharmaceutical compositions is being produced from surfactants that have in the lipophilic parts of the molecule, a caprylic or capric or lauric or myristic or myristoleic or palmitoleic or stearic or oleic or linoleic or linoleic or linolenic or beechenic or behenic chain or lignoceric or its ether or ethoxylated counterparts such as caprylil ether or ethoxylated caprylic alcohol or capryl ether or capricethoxy alcohol or lauryl ether or ethoxylated lauric alcohol or miristil ether or ethoxylated miristoleyl ether or ethoxylated or palmityl ether ethanol or palmityl ether or ethanol or palmityl ether or ethanol alcohol Page 3 of 4 ethoxylated palmitoleic or stearyl ether or ethoxylated stearic alcohol or oleyl ether or ethoxylated oleic alcohol or ethoxylated linolenic alcohol or linolenyl ether or ethoxylated or arachidyl alcohol or arachidyl ether or ethoxylated alcohol or behenyl ether or ethoxylated behenic alcohol or lignocerilether or ethoxylated lignoceric alcohol. The pharmaceutical compositions are produced from surfactants having the lipophilic end and the other lipophobic or hydrophilic end includes a hydroxyl or amine or amide or carbonyl or acid groups carboxylic or anhydrides or oxides of ethyl or polyethylene glycol or amino acid or ascorbic acid or amino alcohol or glucose or sucrose or sorbitan or glycerol or ethoxy alcohol or phosphates or sodium sulfate or ammonium sulfate or organic sodium or potassium salts or amines or theirs substituted molecules or organic salts or inorganic salts. Preferred Condition: The pharmaceutical compositions having a direct action on Coronaviruses or any other viral genus that has the outer wrap / envelope constituted by phospholipid (fat), destroying them in the airways by physical contact. The pharmaceutical compositions forms of use is ingestion, inhalation, aspiration, aerosol, nebulization or spray application. The pharmaceutical compositions having an amphiphilic character, since it has a hydrophilic group (affinity for water) at one end and a lipophilic group at the other end of the molecule (affinity for water). The synthetic surfactants are of mineral or vegetable origin. The surfactant with the viruses, forming micelles, the center of which is the virus itself, which is dissolved by the surfactant through the fat layer. The synthetic surfactants, preferably of short, linear and saturated fatty chains, in their formulations, as they present greater mobility in the formation of the micelle, greater number of demolete in the same space and better interaction with the fatty layer of the virus. The surfactants has a strongly ionic hydrophilic end, to better interact with water in the formation of ions, accentuating their polarity.
   ACTIVITY - Respiratory-Gen; Virucide. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

   WANG X
AE PINGXIANG HUAXING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (PING-Non-standard)
   PINGXIANG HUAXING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (PING-Non-standard)
   PINGXIANG HUAXING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (PING-Non-standard)
   PINGXIANG HUAXING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (PING-Non-standard)
GA 2020B5466V
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises anode plate, ion exchange                membrane and cathode plate; the anode plate and the                anion exchange membrane constitute an anode                chamber; the cathode plate and the cation exchange                membrane constitute a cathode chamber; the cation                exchange membrane and the anion exchange membrane                form an intermediate chamber.
   USE - The device is useful for producing sulfuric                acid by desulfurization product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: can simultaneously captures carbon                dioxide in the flue gas during the comprehensive                utilization of sodium bisulfate solution; is                environmentally-friendly; will promotes the                upgrading of flue gas treatment process and                relieves the pressure of desulfurization gypsum                treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                method for producing sulfuric acid by desulfurized                product comprising using the device mentioned                above, selectively introducing the                sulfur-containing anions in the intermediate                chamber into the anode chamber on the basis of                electric field drive and ion-exchange membrane ion                selective passage, to generate sulfuric acid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, selectively                  passing the cations in the intermediate chamber                  into the cathode chamber to generate alkaline                  substances; using the alkaline substances to                  capture acidic gas. In the method, passing the                  HSO4- into the anode chamber to generate sulfuric                  acid, concentrating under the electric field                  drive and the action of the ion exchange                  membrane, and passing Na+ into the cathode                  chamber to generate sodium hydroxide; introducing                  flue gas containing carbon dioxide to be                  processed from the cathode chamber and absorbing                  it and capturing by sodium hydroxide. In the                  method, the electric field drive is DC constant                  current voltage applied between the anode and the                  cathode, and the current density is 30-1000                  mA/m2; the anode material is plate-shaped                  ruthenium/iridium coated electrode, and the                  cathode is a graphite plate-shaped electrode.                  Preferred Components: The desulfurization product                  liquid in the intermediate chamber is a solution                  containing sulfate and/or sulfite ions, and the                  desulfurization product liquid is sodium                  bisulfate, sodium sulfate, sodium sulfite, and/or                  sodium bisulfite. The sulfuric acid resistance                  concentration of anion exchange membrane is not                  greater than 40wt%, anion selectivity not greater                  than 98%; cation exchange membrane cation                  selectivity is not greater than 90%; the initial                  solution of the anode chamber is 1-5 wt.% dilute                  sulfuric acid, the cathode chamber is sodium                  hydroxide solution with initial concentration of                  0.05-0.5 mol/l. The carbon dioxide-containing gas                  is flue gas after dust removal, desulfurization                  and denitration, and the carbon dioxide content                  is 5-40%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The anode chamber, the                  cathode chamber and the intermediate chamber                  include at least one liquid circulation tube; the                  anode chamber and/or the cathode chamber further                  include at least one exhaust port; the                  intermediate chamber is used to store                  desulfurization product. The anode chamber,                  cathode chamber and intermediate chamber includes                  at least two liquid circulation tubes. The at                  least one liquid circulation tube is provided on                  the cathode plate and/or anode plate. The cathode                  chamber is provided with a flue gas inlet, and                  the flue gas inlet is provided with an aeration                  device. The aeration device is an aeration stone                  and/or an aeration screen plate. The device                  further comprises DC power supply, a                  desulfurization product liquid circulation tank,                  a cathode chamber liquid circulation tank, a                  circulation pump I and a circulation pump II; the                  DC power supply is electrically connected to the                  pole plate of the device; one end of the                  circulation pump I is connected with the liquid                  circulation pipe of the intermediate chamber, and                  the other end is connected with the liquid                  circulation tank of the desulfurization product,                  the liquid circulation tank of the                  desulfurization product is simultaneously                  connected with the liquid circulation pipe of the                  intermediate chamber; one end of the circulation                  pump II is connected with the liquid circulation                  pipe of the cathode chamber, the other end is                  connected with the cathode chamber liquid                  circulation tank, and the cathode chamber liquid                  circulation tank is also connected with the                  cathode chamber liquid circulation pipe.

PN CN111909094-A
TI New multi-active center ion liquid useful for catalytically synthesizing cyclic carbonate.
AU ZHANG J
   REN T
   WANG L
   WANG T
AE UNIV HENAN (UYEH-C)
GA 2020B38062
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-active center ion liquid i.e. 3-(carboxylatomethyl)-1-((3-(carboxylatomethyl)-3H-pyrrol-1-ium-1-yl)methyl)-3H-pyrrol-1-ium bis((bis(dimethylamino)methylidene)azanium) dibromide (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for catalytically synthesizing cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problem of low catalyst performance, harsh reaction conditions, and the use of organic solvents or promoters in the existing methods for synthesizing cyclic carbonates using carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds; and has the highest yield of 98.5%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Multi-active center ion liquid i.e. 3-(carboxylatomethyl)-1-((3-(carboxylatomethyl)-3H-pyrrol-1-ium-1-yl)methyl)-3H-pyrrol-1-ium bis((bis(dimethylamino)methylidene)azanium) dibromide of formula (I) is new.
   n = 1, 2 or 3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) catalytically synthesizing cyclic carbonate using (I), comprising adding (I) and epoxy compound into the reactor at molar ratio of 2-35:100, and carrying out cycloaddition reaction at the reaction temperature of 30-80 degrees C under reaction pressure of 0.1-0.5 MPa for 1-10 hours to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) reacting imidazole and ethyl bromide to obtain ester imidazole, where the ethyl bromide is ehtyl bromoacetate, bromoacetate compounds (BrCH2CH2COOEt or BrCH2CH2CH2COOEt), (ii) reacting the ester imidazole with dibromomethane at 90-100 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours, finishing the reaction to obtain solid diester imidazole dibromide salt, then hydrolyzing by hydrobromic acid, and removing water to obtain biscarboxyimidazole dibromide, and (iii) stirring biscarboxyimidazole dibromide and 1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine at room temperature for 6-18 hours in the presence of a solvent, reacting, rotary steaming, washing and drying to obtain final product. Preferred Method: The temperature of the cycloaddition reaction is 30-60 degrees C. Preferred Components: The molar ratio between ester imidazole and dibromomethane is 1-1.5:0.5; the molar ratio between biscarboxyimidazole dibromide and 1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine is 1:2-2.5; and the solvent is methanol or ethanol. The epoxy compound is 2-(chloromethyl)oxirane, oxiran-2-ylmethanol, 2-phenyloxirane, 2-(allyloxymethyl)oxirane, 2-(phenoxymethyl)oxirane or 7-oxabicyclo(4.1.0)heptanes. The molar ratio between (I) and epoxy compound is 1-2:20.

TI Concentrated Haematococcus pluvialis oil used in food supplements comprises mixture of fractions of astaxanthin esters and free astaxanthin obtained from astaxanthin oil extracted from Haematococcus pluvialis.
AU CASADO BANARES V
AE NEOALGAE MICRO SEAWEEDS PROD SLNE (NEOA-Non-standard)
GA 2020B07815
AB    NOVELTY - Concentrated Haematococcus pluvialis oil comprises a mixture of fractions of astaxanthin esters and free astaxanthin obtained from astaxanthin oil extracted from Haematococcus pluvialis where the concentration of astaxanthin in the mixture of fractions is between 20-40% (p/pT).
   USE - Concentrated Haematococcus pluvialis oil used in food supplements (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The oil concentrate is obtained without using toxic organic solvents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for obtaining an astaxanthin concentrate by supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) chromatography which involves (a) injecting a Haematococcus pluvialis oil with a cosolvent onto a silica column; (b) isolating the astaxanthin and free astaxanthin ester fractions using supercritical CO2 in a column of normal phase as stationary phase and CO2 and cosolvent as mobile phase, at 37-60 degrees C and at a pressure of 90-350 bar; and (c) collecting the different fractions of astaxanthin esters to obtain an astaxanthin concentrate with a concentration of astaxanthin with respect to the oil of step (a) in a ratio (p/pT) of 3-5 times higher.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The astaxanthin concentration is 35-40% (p/pT). The cosolvent is selected from ethanol and/or hexane. The cosolvent is a hexane-ethanol mixture in a molar ratio of 15:1-5:1. The starting Haematococcus pluvialis oil has an astaxanthin concentration of 5-10% (p/pT) and the astaxanthin concentration of the product of the step (c) is 20-40% (p/pT). Preferred Parameters: Step (b) is carried out in for 5-60 minutes.

PN US2020323974-A1; US11376315-B2
TI Producing a solution containing high molecular            weight isolated Streptococcus pneumoniae serotype 19A            capsular polysaccharides, involves preparing            fermentation Streptococcus culture bacterial cells            producing capsular polysaccharides.
AU CRINEAN J H
AE WYETH LLC (AMHP-C)
GA 2020A03766
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a solution containing high molecular                weight isolated Streptococcus pneumoniae serotype                19A capsular polysaccharides, in which                polysaccharides comprises phosphodiester linkages                between repeat units, and the method involves                preparing a fermentation culture of Streptococcus                pneumoniae bacterial cells that produce capsular                polysaccharides comprising a phosphodiester linkage                between repeat units; supplying carbon dioxide                (CO2) to the fermentation culture by adding 5-5 mM                sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3) and subsequently adding                0.1 to 2.0 mM sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) and                fermenting the CO2-supplied fermentation culture at                a pH of 6.0 to 7.0; lysing the bacterial cells in                the resultant fermentation culture and isolating                Streptococcus pneumoniae serotype 19A capsular                polysaccharides from the resultant lysed cell                fermentation culture.
   USE - Method for producing a solution containing                high molecular weight isolated Streptococcus                pneumoniae serotype 19A capsular                polysaccharides.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The molecular weight of each of the isolated                Streptococcus pneumoniae serotype 19A capsular                polysaccharides is 480 kDa, and where a solution                containing high molecular weight isolated                Streptococcus pneumoniae serotype 19A capsular                polysaccharides comprising phosphodiester linkages                between repeat units is produced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The step of supplying                  CO2 to the fermentation culture comprises adding                  bicarbonate ion (HCO3-) to the fermentation                  culture. The step of adding HCO3 to the                  fermentation culture also involves adding NaHCO3.                  The pH of the fermentation culture is between 6.0                  and 6.6. The step of supplying CO2 to the                  fermentation culture also involves adding 5-50 mM                  NaHCO3 and a second subsequent addition of                  0.1-2.0 M Na2CO3. The step of lysing the                  Streptococcus pneumoniae in the fermentation                  culture comprises adding sodium deoxycholate to                  the resultant fermentation culture, and                  overlaying comprises about 5-10 vol.% CO2.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN RU2732921-C1
TI METHOD OF PRODUCING LIQUEUR WINE OF SHERRY TYPE.
AU GAPONOVA T V
   KALMYKOVA N N
   KALMYKOVA E N
   CHEKMAREVA M G
AE ROST FEDERAL AGRARIAN RES CENT (ROAG-Non-standard)
GA 2020A05343
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to winemaking industry. Method of producing liqueur wine of Sherry type includes harvesting of grapes, crushing, combing, sulphitation, pulping, selection of wort and low pressure fractions, wort fermentation on pure culture of yeast, higher alcoholic content, maintenance and separation of deposit, provision of pouring resistance. Wine materials are prepared from grape varieties with sugar content of not less than 180 g/dm3 using carbon dioxide maceration of whole bunches for 78 days at temperature of +20C, wherein binder upper layer sulphitation is carried out in amount of 100 mg/kg, and increase of alcohol-value of wine materials is carried out by one-time introduction of concentrated grape juice into fermenting wort in 48 hours from the beginning of fermentation at temperature 2025C from the calculation of alcohol concentration increase up to 16.0 vol.% until complete fermentation of sugars, followed by one-time stirring, without removing from yeast residue, for 2030 minutes, and maintained on yeast sediment for 912 months, followed by filtration and dispensing.
   USE - Wine industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention allows to increase wort output, as well as chemical and organoleptic characteristics of wine.
1 cl, 1 tbl, 5 ex

PN US2020283561-A1; WO2020185443-A1; US11053340-B2; CN113490701-A; EP3935096-A1; JP2022523800-W
TI Isocyanate-reactive composition used for producing rigid polyurethane-polyisocyanurate foam comprises polyol blend comprising polyether polyol and polyester polyol, blowing agent composition, and tert.-amine catalyst.
AU PARKS B W
AE COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
GA 202087944P
AB    NOVELTY - An isocyanate-reactive composition comprises (a) polyol blend comprising (1) a saccharide-initiated polyether polyol having hydroxyl (OH) number of 200-600 mg KOH/g and functionality of 4-6, (2) aromatic polyester polyol having an OH number of 150-410 mg KOH/g and functionality of 1.5-3, and (3) alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol having an OH number of greater than or equal to 500 mg KOH/g and a functionality of 2.5-4; (b) blowing agent composition comprising (1) hydrochlorofluoroolefin and (2) carbon dioxide generating chemical blowing agent; and (c) tert.-amine catalyst.
   USE - An isocyanate-reactive composition used for producing rigid polyurethane-polyisocyanurate foam (claimed). Can be used for panel insulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The isocyanate-reactive composition can exhibit a long shelf life, is shelf-stable, and can produce a foam with good physical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for producing a rigid polyurethane-polyisocyanurate foam which involves mixing diisocyanate and/or polyisocyanate with isocyanate-reactive composition at an isocyanate index of 90-150 to form a reaction mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The saccharide-initiated polyether polyol is present in an amount of 10-45 wt.% based on total weight of polyol blend. The aromatic polyester polyol is present in an amount of 50-85 wt.% based on the total weight of the polyol blend. The aromatic polyester polyol and saccharide-initiated polyether polyol are present in polyol blend in a weight ratio of 2:1-8:1. The alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol is present in an amount of 5-40 wt.% based on the total weight of the polyol blend. The alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol is a propoxylation reaction product of monoethanolamine. The aromatic polyester polyol and alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol are present in the polyol blend in a weight ratio of 2:1-8:1. The saccharide-initiated polyether polyol and alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol are present in polyol blend in a weight ratio of 0.5:1-4:1. The hydrochlorofluoroolefin comprises 1-chloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene. The hydrochlorofluoroolefin is present in an amount of 10-30 wt.% based on the total weight of the isocyanate-reactive composition. The isocyanate-reactive composition is substantially free of chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs), hydrochlorofluorocarbons (HCFCs), hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) and/or hydrocarbons. The hydrochlorofluoroolefin and carbon dioxide generating chemical blowing agent are present inblowing agent composition at a weight ratio of 4:1-10:1. The tert.-amine catalyst comprises a morpholine and/or an imidazole. The morpholine and/or imidazole is present in an amount of less than or equal to 3 wt.% based on the total weight of the isocyanate-reactive composition. The isocyanate-reactive composition is substantially free of organometallic catalysts. The isocyanate-reactive composition further comprising a trimerization catalyst comprising a quat.-ammonium salt. Preferred Parameters: The polyol blend has a weighted average functionality of 2.5-3 and a weighted average hydroxyl number of 300-400 mg KOH/g. Preferred Process: The reaction mixture is poured into a cavity of a mold of a desired portion, where the cavity is lined with a facer.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2149113-B1
TI Partially biodegradable resin composition comprises resin mixture having biodegradable resin and bio-polyethylene resin, compatibilizer (e.g. epoxidized soybean oil), stabilizer, antioxidant and slip agent (e.g. epoxidized linseed oil).
AU GIL H S
   JUNG W H
AE ECOMASS CO LTD (ECOM-Non-standard)
   GIL H S (GILH-Individual)
GA 202088468U
AB    NOVELTY - Partially biodegradable resin composition comprises a resin mixture including a biodegradable resin and a bio-polyethylene resin, a compatibilizer, a stabilizer, an antioxidant and a slip agent. The compatibilizer is epoxidized soybean oil and organic peroxide. The stabilizer is an acrylic copolymer powder. The slip agent is epoxidized linseed oil. The antioxidant is primary and secondary antioxidant. The soybean oil epoxy increases miscibility through a partial crosslinking reaction between different resins, and the primary antioxidant is phenolic or aromatic antioxidant, preferably amine-based antioxidant. The secondary antioxidant is a phosphorus-based or sulfur-based antioxidant.
   USE - Used as partially biodegradable resin composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: solves the problem of non-degradability of existing biomass by manufacturing a biomass-based partially biodegradable resin; can give properties similar or equivalent to the physical properties of the biomass-based resin; is a substitute for biomass polyethylene products in China; is environmentally friendly; and reduces the amount of carbon dioxide generation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 0.5-4 wt.% epoxidized soybean oil as a compatibilizer, 0.01-0.3 wt.% di-(2-t-butylperoxyisopropyl)benzene as an organic peroxide, 0.5-4 wt.% acrylic copolymer powder as a stabilizer, 0.1-0.5 wt.% epoxidized linseed oil as a slip agent, 0.05-0.3 wt.% first antioxidant and 0.05-0.3 wt.% second antioxidant. The composition comprises a mixture of 20-40 wt.% bio low density polyethylene and 40-60 wt.% bio linear low density polyethylene in the bio polyethylene. Preferred Components: The resin mixture contains 10-50 wt.% polycaprolactone as a biodegradable resin and 50-90 wt.% bio-high density polyethylene as a bio-polyethylene resin. The biodegradable resin is at least one of polycaprolactone, polybutylene adipate terephthalate, polylactic acid, recycled polylactic acid, and polyhydroxyalkanoate. The compatibilizer is at least one of epoxidized soybean oil, epoxidized linseed oil, and epoxidized cottonseed oil. The organic peroxide includes benzoyl peroxide, dibenzoyl peroxide, 2,5-dimethyl-2,5-di(t-butylperoxy)-hexane and di-(2-t-butylperoxy) isopropyl)benzene. The slip agent comprises at least one of oleamide and steramide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)

PN CN111592519-A; CN111592519-B
TI New fluorine-containing carboxylic acid compounds which are 2-fluoroacrylic acid compound or hexyl 2,2-difluoro-3-phenylbut-3-enoate compound, prepared by e.g. reacting fluorine-containing olefin compound and carbon dioxide.
AU YU D
   LI J
   YAN S
   WU D
   YE J
   GONG L
   ZENG X
   RAN C
   GUI Y
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202085770L
AB    NOVELTY - Fluorine-containing carboxylic acid compounds (A) which are 2-fluoroacrylic acid compound (I) or hexyl 2,2-difluoro-3-phenylbut-3-enoate compound (II) are new.
   USE - Used as fluorine-containing carboxylic acid compounds which are 2-fluoroacrylic acid compound or hexyl 2,2-difluoro-3-phenylbut-3-enoate compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes easily available raw materials; has mild reaction conditions; and avoids use of toxic substances and noble metals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fluorine-containing carboxylic acid compounds (A) which are 2-fluoroacrylic acid compound of formula (I) or hexyl 2,2-difluoro-3-phenylbut-3-enoate compound of formula (II) are new.
   R1 = 6-20C aryl, 6-20C heteroaryl, ester, alkenyl or its derivatives;
   R2 = H, 1-20C straight-chain or branched alkyl, cycloalkyl, 6-20C aryl or its derivatives; and
   R3 = H, halo, methoxy, cyano, 1-20C straight-chain or branched alkyl, cycloalkyl, carbonyl, ester, 6-20C aryl, 6-20C heteroaryl or derivatives.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (A).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (A) comprises reacting fluorine-containing olefin compound and carbon dioxide in presence of copper catalyst, ligand, alkali and boron reagent in solvent at 50-100 degrees C for 2-28 hours to obtain (A). Preferred Method: The method comprises reacting difluoroolefin compound of formula (III) and carbon dioxide in presence of copper catalyst, ligand, alkali and boron reagent in solvent at 50-100 degrees C for 2-28 hours and acid hydrolyzing to obtain (I), where the molar ratio between (III) and copper catalyst is 40:1-5:1, preferably 20:1, the molar ratio between (III) and ligand is 40:1-5:1, preferably 20:1, the molar ratio between (III) and alkali is 1:1-1:6, preferably 1:3.5, and the molar ratio between (III) and boron reagent is 1:1-1:5, preferably 1:1.5. The method comprises reacting alpha -trifluoromethylstyrene compound of formula (IV) with carbon dioxide in presence of copper catalyst, ligand, alkali and boron reagent in solvent at 50-100 degrees C for 2-28 hours, and reacting with 1-iodohexane to obtain (II), where the molar ratio between (IV) and copper catalyst is 40:1-5:1, preferably 10:1, the molar ratio between (IV) and ligand is 40:1-5:1, preferably 10:1, the molar ratio between (IV) and alkali is 1:1-1:6, preferably 1:3, and the molar ratio between (IV) and boron reagent is 1:1-1:5, preferably 1:1.5. Preferred Components: The copper catalyst is cuprous 2-thiophenecarboxylate, cuprous acetate, cuprous chloride, cuprous bromide or cuprous iodide. The ligand is 4,5-bis(diphenylphosphino)-9,9-dimethylxanthene, bis(2-diphenylphosphophenyl) ether, 4,6-bis(diphenylphosphino)phenazine, 1,2-ethylene bis(diphenylphosphine) or 1,4-bis(diphenylphosphino)butane. The alkali is potassium fluoride, cesium fluoride, potassium methoxide, potassium acetate, lithium tert-butoxide or potassium tert-butoxide. The boron reagent is bis(pinacolato)diboron, bis(neopentyl glycolato)diboron, bis(catecholato)diboron or bis(2,4-dimethylpentane-2,4-glycolato)diboron. The solvent is o-xylene, N,N-dimethylformamide or N-methylpyrrolidone. The copper catalyst used in preparation of (II) is cuprous 2-thiophenecarboxylate, copper acetylacetonate, cuprous acetate, copper acetate, cuprous chloride, copper fluoride, copper bromide or copper chloride, the alkali is potassium fluoride, cesium carbonate, potassium methoxide, sodium methoxide, lithium tert-butoxide or potassium tert-butoxide, and the solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide or N-methylpyrrolidone.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 49 Compounds (I) are specifically claimed e.g. (Z)-3-biphenyl-4-yl-2-fluoro-acrylic acid (Ia), methyl (Z)-3-biphenyl-4-yl-2-fluoro-acrylate, (Z)-2-fluoro-3-phenyl-acrylic acid, (Z)-2-fluoro-3-p-tolyl-acrylic acid and (Z)-2-fluoro-3-(4-isopropyl-phenyl)-acrylic acid.

PT P
PN CN111499351-A; CN111499351-B
TI Preparing porous purification stone by cleaning            remaining sludge of waste selective catalytic reduction            catalyst, comprises e.g. pretreating surplus sludge,            uniformly mixing sludge powder, pore former, titanium            dioxide and water, and drying.
AU SUN L
   REN Y
   FENG Y
   ZHANG X
   ZHANG B
   LUO C
   DENG L
AE LONGKING KEJIE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (LONG-Non-standard)
GA 202077225A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous purification stone by                cleaning remaining sludge of waste selective                catalytic reduction (SCR) catalyst, comprises (i)                pretreating the surplus sludge produced by cleaning                the spent SCR catalyst to obtain sludge powder,                (ii) uniformly mixing the sludge powder, pore                former, titanium dioxide, binder and water, and                drying, (iii) pressing the product into a wet body                of purified stone with a mold, and drying, (iv)                heating the dried body, and (v) cooling to room                temperature, immersing in a nutrient solution, and                drying to constant weight.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing porous                purification stone by cleaning remaining sludge of                waste SCR catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The stone: has high stability, light corrosion                resistance and non-toxicity; is environmentally                friendly; degrades the organic pollutants in the                fish tank water; and simultaneously provides the                carbon dioxide for the growth of green plants. The                method: comprehensively utilizes; realizes resource                recycling; saves natural resources; and is                environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  pretreatment method for cleaning the surplus                  sludge produced by the spent SCR catalyst,                  comprises (ia) drying the surplus sludge produced                  by cleaning the spent SCR catalyst, (ib)                  performing pyrolysis of the dried sludge, (ic)                  immersing the pyrolysis product in lye, (id)                  heating the resulting product, and (ie) washing                  the product with acid solution, then washing with                  water to neutrality, drying to constant weight,                  and grinding into sludge powder. In the step                  (ia), the drying is carried out at 80-120 degrees                  C for 12-72 hours. In the step (ib), the                  pyrolysis is carried out under the protection of                  nitrogen at 650-750 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours,                  and the heating rate is 10-20 degrees C/minute.                  In the step (ic), the lye is a saturated                  potassium hydroxide solution, and the immersion                  time is 12-48 hours. In the step (id), the heat                  treatment is carried out at 700-900 degrees C for                  12-48 hours. In the step (ie), the acid solution                  is 10 wt.% dilute hydrochloric acid, the washing                  water is deionized water and the water                  temperature is 60-70 degrees C, and powder is                  ground into powder with a fineness of 80-160                  mesh. In the step (ii), the mixing specifically                  comprises mixing the binder and water uniformly                  to obtain mixture I, mixing the sludge powder,                  pore former, and titanium dioxide to obtain                  mixture II, and then adding the mixture II to the                  mixture I and uniformly mixing, and the drying                  comprises drying at room temperature for 12-48                  hours. In the step (iii), the pressing is carried                  out at 8-10 MPa for 10-30 minutes, and drying at                  60-80 degrees C for 24-48 hours, where the water                  content is not greater than 3 wt.%. In the step                  (iv), the heat treatment is carried out at                  800-1000 degrees C for 2-4 hours, where the                  heating rate is 5-10 degrees C/minute. In the                  step (v), the nutrient solution is 0.5-0.8 wt.%                  urea phosphate solution, and immersion time is                  6-18 hours. Preferred Components: In the step                  (ii), the binder is silica solution, the mass                  ratio of sludge powder, titanium dioxide, binder                  and water is 100:30-40:7.5-8:20-25, the pore                  forming agent is 2,4,6-trinitrotoluene,                  polystyrene (PS), polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) and/or                  polyethylene glycol (PEG), and the mass ratio of                  sludge powder and pore former is 100:20-90.

PN CN111484579-A; CN111484579-B
TI New high-temperature soluble bridge plug comprising copolymer of butadiene and maleic anhydride is used in the development of unconventional gas reservoirs such as external shale gas.
AU YANG X
   SHEN Z
   WANG C
   LI P
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
   UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 202075964B
AB    NOVELTY - High-temperature soluble bridge plug comprising copolymer of butadiene and maleic anhydride (I) is new.
   USE - High-temperature soluble bridge plug used in the development of unconventional gas reservoirs such as external shale gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-temperature soluble bridge plug makes complete use of the copolymer synthesized by maleic anhydride and butadiene, which has the characteristics of high temperature resistance, pressure resistance, water solubility, and is finally degraded into water and carbon dioxide that is a biodegradable and eco-friendly material, and reduces the process of reverse discharge. The liquid in the well causes the high-temperature soluble bridge plug to dissolve by itself, that results in eliminating the need for wellbore drilling and grinding operations and reverse drainage treatment, and allowing complete borehole production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-temperature soluble bridge plug comprising copolymer of butadiene and maleic anhydride of formula (I) is new.
   n=1000-10000.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing high-temperature soluble bridge plug comprising copolymer of butadiene and maleic anhydride of formula (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Disclosed): Preparing high-temperature soluble bridge plug copolymer of butadiene and maleic anhydride of formula (I) involves taking butadiene and maleic anhydride, adding antigelling agent, initiator, coupling agent and solvent, heating in a constant temperature oil bath under the protection of nitrogen, and completely refluxing reaction at 140-180 degrees C. Centrifugal washing of the solid-liquid mixture is performed. the obtained product is vacuum dried to obtain the light yellow solid. The obtained crude product is thermoformed into a high-temperature soluble bridge plug with a mold. Preferred Conditions: The material of the high-temperature soluble bridge plug is a high-temperature soluble material. The high temperature is 80-180 degrees C. The mass of the maleic anhydride is 2-3 times that of butadiene. Preferred Compositions: The initiator is azobutyronitrile, and the added amount is 2-4% of the total monomer. The coupling agent is gamma -methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane, and the added amount is 1-1.5% of the total monomer. The antigelling agent is acetanilide, and the added amount is 4-5% of the total monomer. Preferred Component: The solvent is dimethylformamide.

General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

TI Preparing compound fertilizer and soda ash from dust ash involves mixing the collected salt-containing dust ash and water in production of the steel plant and mixing and stirring to obtain first filtrate and the iron-containing slag.

   GUO L
   YONG H
AE CISDI THERMAL & ENVIRONMENTAL ENG CO LTD (CMEG-C)

GA 202053672D

The pH value of the second filtrate is adjusted with ammonia, passed in carbon dioxide and filter third filtrate and sodium bicarbonate. The third filtrate is evaporated to obtain compound fertilizer containing potassium chloride and ammonium chloride. The sodium bicarbonate is crushed and calcined to obtain soda ash.
   USE - Method for preparing compound fertilizer and soda ash from dust ash.

PN CN111235415-A
TI Preparing magnesium-aluminum-titanium-vanadium alloy comprises e.g. melting magnesium ingot with pre-prepared aluminum alloy refining agent in first induction furnace, adding pre-prepared aluminum-titanium-vanadium alloy ingot, and casting.
AU YU Y
   LI S
   CHEN X
   ZHOU Z
AE SHENZHEN XINXING LIGHT ALLOY MATERIAL CO (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020518515
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnesium-aluminum-titanium-vanadium alloy, comprises (i) adding magnesium ingot into a first induction furnace, (ii) adding pre-prepared aluminum alloy refining agent into the first induction furnace, raising the temperature to 900-1000 degrees C, and melting the magnesium ingot, (iii) adding pre-prepared aluminum-titanium-vanadium alloy ingot into the first induction furnace, and reacting at 900-1000 degrees C for 30 minutes to obtain magnesium-aluminum-titanium-vanadium alloy liquid, and (iv) casting the magnesium-aluminum-titanium-vanadium alloy liquid into magnesium-aluminum-titanium-vanadium alloy ingot or rolling, where the alloy is composed of 70-80 wt.% magnesium, 15-25 wt.% aluminum, 0.5-3 wt.% titanium and 0.3-1 wt.% vanadium.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing magnesium-aluminum-titanium-vanadium alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids chemical reaction of magnesium with oxygen and carbon dioxide, or spontaneous combustion; does not use closed container; is easy to carry out; and has improved production efficiency and quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) further comprises adding 7-15 wt.% aluminum ingot, 2.5-15 wt.% potassium fluorotitanate, 6-20 wt.% aluminum vanadium alloy into a second induction furnace, and preparing aluminum-titanium-vanadium alloy ingot before adding magnesium ingot into the first induction furnace. The aluminum alloy refining agent is potassium magnesium chloride (K2MgCl4). The step (i) further comprises adding aluminum into third induction furnace, heating and melting aluminum to obtain aluminum melt, adding the raw material for preparing potassium magnesium chloride into the third induction furnace at a preset ratio, and stirring to obtain aluminum alloy refining agent before adding magnesium ingots in the first induction furnace. The frequency of the third induction furnace is 50-150 Hz. The frequency of the first induction furnace is 100-200 Hz. Preferred Components: The raw materials for preparing potassium magnesium chloride include potassium chloride and magnesium chloride, and the molar ratio of potassium chloride to magnesium chloride is 2:1.

PN CN111196966-A
TI Brewing steamy semi-sweet rice wine comprises e.g. taking out half-dried rice wine, sweet rice wine, isomaltose oligosaccharides, chitooligosaccharides, tea saponin, highly active dry wine and water, sterilizing and cooling.
AU XIE G
   MAO Q
   LU Y
   LIU C
   LI G
   JIN J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG SHUREN COLLEGE (UNSR-C)
GA 202049952X
AB    NOVELTY - Brewing steamy semi-sweet rice wine comprises (i) taking out half-dried rice wine, sweet rice wine, isomaltose oligosaccharides, chitooligosaccharides, tea saponin, highly active dry wine and water, (ii) packing sugar, chitooligosaccharides, tea saponin, highly active dry wine and water into pressure-resistant bottles, (iii) placing the above materials into the bottle and cap to seal, fermenting at 18-20 degrees c for 3-5 days, (iv) taking one bottle of the sample, and stopping the fermentation when the pressure in the bottle is 0.15-0.18 Mpa with a carbon dioxide pressure meter, (v) sterilizing the product at 80-90 degrees C in a water bath or spray sterilization device for 10-20 minutes, the temperature of the liquor in the bottle reaches above 85 degrees C, and (vi) cooling the product.
   USE - The method is useful for brewing steamy semi-sweet rice wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The wine increases mouthfeel, refreshing taste, coordination of the wine body and persistence of the foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the weight ratio of highly active dry wine mother and water is: 39.5-40.5 wt.% aged half-dry yellow wine, 8.5-9.5 wt.% sweet yellow wine, 2-2.5 wt.% oligomeric maltose, 0.5-1 wt.% chitooligosaccharide, 0.5-1 wt.% tea saponin, 0.003-0.004 wt.% high active dry wine mother and 40-45 wt.% water. In the step (ii), the pressure resistance of the bottle is greater than or equal to 1.8 MPa, and the bottle is capped and sealed. In the step (iv), the pressure in the bottle in the fermentation process does not exceed 0.2 Mpa. In the step (v), takes a slow temperature increase in a water bath or spray sterilization device. In the step (vi), requires slow cooling with warm water below the sterilization temperature.

PN CN111186277-A; CN111186277-B
TI Car interior air quality monitoring method, involves operating switch between inner circulation and outer circulation such that air quality in car is greater than air quality outside car to avoid glass fogging.
AU YANG Z
   WANG X
   PENG X
   WANG C
AE SAIC VOLKSWAGEN AUTOMOTIVE CO LTD (SAMO-C)
GA 202046579J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining air quality index of a vehicle. Temperature and humidity parameters are obtained in the vehicle. Dew point is calculated based on the temperature and humidity parameters difference. A damper is adjusted according to the air quality index, where outside air quality index and the dew point difference are calculated. A switch is operated between inner circulation and outer circulation such that the air quality in the car is greater than air quality outside the car to avoid glass fogging.
   USE - Car interior air quality monitoring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables timely adjusting internal and external circulation according to the air quality, ensuring the air quality in the car and reducing risk of excessive carbon dioxide concentration or glass fogging.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a vehicle interior air quality monitoring device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a car interior air quality monitoring method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
