AB    NOVELTY - A thio group-blocked mercaptosilane coupling                agent (I) is new.
   USE - New thio group-blocked mercaptosilane coupling                agent is used for compounding or vulcanizing rubber                for producing vulcanized rubber (all claimed) for                tire.
   ADVANTAGE - The thio group-blocked mercaptosilane coupling                agent is capable of improving interaction between                rubber and filler, reducing hysteresis loss,                reducing Payne effect of the rubber compound, and                improving dispersion of white carbon black, thus                reducing fuel consumption and carbon dioxide                emission when used in tire, and enhancing tire wear                resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A thio group-blocked mercaptosilane coupling                agent of formula (I) is new.
   R =1-18C linear alkyl, aryl or                cycloalkyl;
   R =absent or S;
   R =S or O;
   R =1-18C linear alkyl, aryl, cycloalkyl,                alkenyl, or alkynyl;and
   at least one of R -R =hydrolyzable Cl, Br,                alkoxy or ester, and remaining are H, linear alkyl,                aryl or cycloalkyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   preparation of compound (I); and
   use of compound (I) for rubber compounding or                vulcanizing to improve interaction between rubber                and filler, and reduce hysteresis loss.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Compound (I) is                  produced by reacting a thiocarboxylate or                  thiocarbonate compound with a sulfonyl chloride                  compound in a molar ratio of 1:1-1.2, preferably                  obtained by dissolving a thiocarboxylate or                  thiocarbonate compound in an organic solvent                  under an inert gas atmosphere to prepare 0.5-0.8                  mol/L reaction solution, dripping sulfonyl                  chloride compound to the reaction solution,                  reacting at room temperature with a magnetron                  speed of 500-800 rpm for 6-12 hours, removing the                  solvent, and performing column chromatography                  separation using 400 mesh silica gel powder with                  petroleum ether and ethyl acetate in a ratio of                  200:1-10:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: Rubber compounding or                  vulcanization is performed by applying compound                  (I) to a raw rubber, isoprene rubber or                  styrene-butadiene rubber, and mixing and                  vulcanizing to obtain a vulcanized rubber.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 7 Compounds (I) are specifically claimed,                  e.g.                  1-{[3-(triethoxysilyl)propyl]disulfanyl}ethan-1-one                  of formula (IA),                  phenyl({[3-(triethoxysilyl)propyl]disulfanyl})methanone,                  8,8-diethoxy-1-phenyl-9-oxa-1,3,4-trithia-8-silaundecane-2-thione,                  triethoxy({3-[(phenylmethanethioyl)disulfanyl]propyl})silane,                  and                  triethoxy[3-(propanethioyldisulfanyl)propyl]silane.

PN CN110982648-A
TI Production of Artemisia beer useful for resisting oxidation comprises e.g. crushing malt, stirring to soak crushed malt to precipitate maltose to obtain wort, finishing saccharifying, filtering hot wort, boiling filtered wort, and decocting.
AU ZHANG H
   LIU X
   LIU S
   CHEN B
   FAN X
   LU X
   HAN J
AE UNIV CHENGDU NORMAL (UCDN-C)
GA 202031482G
AB    NOVELTY - Production of Artemisia beer comprises (i) crushing malt, where the malt flakes are crushed and the malt bran is not crushed, (ii) stirring to soak the crushed malt at 65-68 degrees C to precipitate maltose to obtain wort, (iii) finishing saccharifying, and filtering the hot wort, (iv) boiling the filtered wort, decocting, and adding hops during decocting, (v) rapidly cooling the boiled wort to 20-25 degrees C, and adding Artemisia leaf juice extract, (vi) uniformly adding expansion cultured beer yeast to the wort mixed with Artemisia leaf juice, sterilizing, sealing in container, and culturing in incubator, carrying out fermentation at 25-27 degrees C for 3-3.5 hours until producing carbon dioxide to obtain Artemisia beer sample, and (vii) sterilizing Artemisia beer sample, and canning to obtain Artemisia beer product.
   USE - The beer is useful for resisting oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - The beer is clear and transparent, has soft body, no odor, good taste, pure color, and unique flavor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (iv) and (v), the ratio of filtered wort and Artemisia leaf juice is 10:9; the adding amount of hops in 100 ml filtered wort is 0.05-0.09 g; the adding amount of Artemisia leaf juice is 90 ml. In the step (vi), the beer yeast is US-05 American beer yeast, and the adding amount is 0.05-0.011 g. Preferred Process: The preparation of Artemisia leaf juice extract includes (1) providing dry wormwood leaf without decay and mildew, and crushing to obtain Artemisia leaf powder, (2) mixing Artemisia leaf powder and water, and performing ultrasonic extraction at 70 degrees C to obtain Artemisia leaf juice primary product, where the ratio of Artemisia leaf powder and water is 5:100 g/ml, and (3) filtering Artemisia leaf juice primary product to obtain clear Artemisia leaf juice extract.

The adhesive resin layer contains a low-density polyethylene. The barrier layer and the sealant layer adjoin through a direct or anchor-coat layer. The sealant layer comprises single-side outermost layer of the laminate and containing a linear low-density polyethylene and a low density polyethylene. The low-density polyethylene contains a plant-derived low-density polyethylene. The mass ratio of the low-density polyethylene to the linear low-density polyethylene is 3/7-7/3.

The adhesive resin layer and the barrier layer are adjacent to each other. The substrate layer contains resin film comprising polyester, and recycled polyester or plant-derived polyester.

PT P
PN CN110644271-A; CN110644271-B
TI Preparing micro-nano cellulose comprises e.g.) immersing fiber raw material in suspension, dissociating, adding crystal control agent to slurry suspension, mixing uniformly, passing gas and reaching carbonization end point.
AU CHEN J
   LIU J
   SU Y
   DU Y
   ZHANG R
   XU Z
AE CHINA NAT PULP & PAPER RES INST (POYG-C)
GA 202004022T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing micro-nano cellulose comprises (i) immersing fiber raw material in suspension of calcium hydroxide, (ii) dissociating of impregnating slurry by mechanical methods, and (iii) adding crystal control agent to slurry suspension after dissociation (also before dissociation) and mixing uniformly at specific temperature, passing gas containing carbon dioxide while stirring the suspension until the pH value of suspension reaches specific value, reaching carbonization end point, to obtain calcium carbonate-coated micro-nanocellulose.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing micro-nano cellulose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has strong applicability of raw materials, mild pretreatment conditions, low requirements on equipment, cheap drugs and high utilization rate, simple process, no need to wash materials after pretreatment, and no waste liquid and washing wastewater; requires less energy for mechanical dissociation after pretreatment; is environment-friendly, economical, and suitable for industrial production. The micro-nano cellulose: has controllable calcium carbonate morphology and can adapt to application requirements in different fields.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fiber material is bleached or unbleached coniferous pulp, broadleaf pulp, cotton pulp, bamboo pulp, wheat straw pulp, waste paper pulp or wood flour, bamboo flour, plant straw, corn cob, xylose wastes, and/or lumps and powder materials, and the fiber material has particle size of 40-80 mesh. The suspension of calcium hydroxide is prepared by dispersing calcium hydroxide in water, and can also prepared by digestion reacting of calcium oxide in water. The composition of impregnating solution comprises 0.1-20 wt.% fiber material (solid content), 0.1-200 wt.% calcium hydroxide, and water (balance). The crystal control agent is sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, citric acid, glycine, amino acids, sucrose, zinc sulfate, aluminum chloride, barium chloride, magnesium chloride, sodium chloride, sodium silicate, disodium ethylenediamine tetraacetate, sodium polyvinylbenzenesulfonate, disodium hydrogen phosphate, sodium lauryl sulfate, polyvinylpyrrolidone, and/or polyacrylamide, and the amount of crystal control agent based on calcium hydroxide is 0-200 wt.%. The gas containing carbon dioxide is pure carbon dioxide gas, mixture of carbon dioxide and other gases, and/or carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas after purification treatment. The micro-nano cellulose is calcium carbonate-coated micro-nano cellulose, and the micro-nano cellulose has diameter of 10-500 nm and length of 200 nm to 100 mu m, the content of calcium carbonate in micro-nano cellulose is 0.1-200 wt.% (measured by the increase in ash content), the minimum particle size of calcium carbonate is 10 nm to 5 mu m, and the form of calcium carbonate is spindle shape, cubic shape, needle shape, flake shape, chain shape, spherical shape, rod shape, diamond shape and irregular shape, the micro- and nano-cellulose coated with different forms of calcium carbonate meet application requirements in different fields. Preferred Method: In the step (i), immersion temperature is 0-40 degrees C, and immersion time is 1 minutes to 10 hours. In the step (ii), mechanical dissociation methods specifically comprises disc grinding, fine grinding, high pressure homogenization, ball milling, microjet, ultrasonic treatment, and/or countercurrent collision in water, where the mass concentration of material processed by disc grinding and fine grinding is 0.1-20 wt.%, the gap between the grinding discs is -150 m to 0 m, and the rotation speed of the grinding disc is 1000-2000 revolutions/minute, the mass concentration of material treated by high pressure homogenization is 0.1-3 wt.%, and the processing pressure is 20-150 MPa, the mass concentration of ball-milled material is 0.1-10 wt.%, and the speed is 100-600 revolutions/minute, the mass concentration of microjet material is 0.1-5 wt.%, and the processing pressure is 50-200 MPa, the mass concentration of sonicated material is 0.1-5 wt.%, and the power is 400-1000 W, the mass concentration of material in countercurrent collision method in water is 0.05-1 wt.%, and the pressure is 50-500 MPa. In the step (iii), carbonization temperature is 0-150 degrees C, stirring speed is 10-1000 revolutions/minute, introduction rate of carbon dioxide-containing gas is 1-1000 ml/minutes, carbonization time is 1 minutes to 24 hours, and the pH value of suspension at the end of carbonization is 6.5-8.5.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)
MC A03-A05; A10-E05B; A12-W06B; A12-W06D; F05-A02A; F05-A02B

PN CN110591822-A
TI Extracting fragrant wormwood essential oil with increased active component content comprises taking dried fragrant folium artemisiae argyi leaves, crushing, extracting, adding diluent, collecting the volatile components and removing water.
AU WANG B
   ZHANG Y
   ZHAO N
   SUN X
   ZHANG L
AE QINGDAO LIHE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2019A8609S
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting fragrant wormwood essential oil with increased active component content comprises (i) taking dried fragrant folium artemisiae argyi leaves and crushing into moxa; (ii) adding moxa into a supercritical fluid extraction device, extracting using carbon dioxide as an extractant at 65-75 degrees C at pressure 15-18Mpa and separating by a two-stage separation kettle with the pressure of the first-stage separation kettle 10-12 Mpa at 45-55 degrees C and the pressure of the secondary separation kettle 5.5-7 Mpa at 35-40 degrees C to obtain crude extract; (iii) adding 1-3 times the mass of diluent into the crude extract to obtain a diluent; (iv) transferring the diluent to a reaction kettle for distillation, collecting the volatile components by condensation and removing the lower layer of water.
   USE - The meth extracting fragrant wormwood essential oil with increased active component content.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is stable and increases the content of the main active component in the essential oil of wormwood.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The diluent is a mixture of 10-40% glycerine and purified water (remaining amount). The moisture content of folium artemisiae argyi leaves is 10-15% and the crushed particle size is 5-20 meshes. Preferred Method: The distillation is carried out at 102-105 degrees C for 3-6 hours with the condenser temperature of 1-10 degrees C. The extraction is carried out at 68-75 degrees C at pressure of 17-18 Mpa.

PN CN110539442-A
TI Method for foaming thermoplastic polyurethane            particle, involves heating thermoplastic polyurethane            raw materials to molten state, extruding through screw            extruder and granulating and placing thermoplastic            polyurethane particle in autoclave.
AU XU S
   CHEN J
AE XIAMEN FENGTE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (XIAM-Non-standard)
   XU S (XUSS-Individual)
GA 2019A44033
AB    NOVELTY - A thermoplastic polyurethane particle foaming                method involves heating a thermoplastic                polyurethane raw materials to a molten state,                extruding through a screw extruder and granulating                to obtain thermoplastic polyurethane particle. The                thermoplastic polyurethane particle are placed in                an autoclave and sealed, a supercritical fluid is                passed into the autoclave to pressurize to a                saturated state, and the supercritical fluid is                quickly evacuated to swell the thermoplastic                polyurethane particles. The swollen thermoplastic                polyurethane particle is taken out from the                autoclave, and placed the foamed thermoplastic                polyurethane particle in a constant temperature                device to obtain thermoplastic polyurethane foaming                particles. The thermoplastic polyurethane foamed                particles are dried and placed in a pressure                holding container. A mixed gas of carbon dioxide                and nitrogen is introduced into the pressure                holding container and the pressure is maintained                for 20-36 hours to obtain the finished                product.
   USE - Method for foaming thermoplastic polyurethane                particle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables foaming thermoplastic                polyurethane particle with enhanced plumpness of                the product and compressure-resilience.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The supercritical                  fluid is supercritical carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  isocyanate groups to hydroxyl groups of                  0.995-0.998.

TI Electrode comprises first and second carrier particles smaller in size than first carrier membrane supporting first and second carrier particle, and complex catalyst positioned on surfaces of first and second carrier particles.
AU JA L
   KYEJEONGLL
   KIM G
   KIM D B
   JI K
AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 2019A34891
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode comprises first carrier particles, second carrier particles smaller in size than the first carrier membrane supporting the first carrier particle and the second carrier particle, and complex catalyst positioned on the surfaces of the first carrier particles and second carrier particles. The catalyst particles are of a metal component, metal oxide or metal nitride component.
   USE - Electrode.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode controls the pore size is shortened the path (pathway) of the reactants by applying the carrier and products are smoothly supplied of the reactants and the discharge of the product is made smoothly, reduce the diffusion resistance on the electrode surface and suppress the poisoning of the catalyst to improve the durability of the catalyst and the electrode, reductively decomposes carbon dioxide and further improve the production efficiency of hydrocarbons of C2 or higher, increase the surface area of the catalyst by applying a support having pores, significantly reduce the amount of the catalyst used and greatly improves the production efficiency of C2 or more hydrocarbons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The first carrier particle has a spherical shape or a hollow shape or a spherical shape with protrusions formed on a surface. The equivalent diameter of the second carrier particle is 20 to 80% of the diameter of the first carrier particle. The diameter of the second carrier particles is 28-72% of the diameter of the first carrier particles. The content of the second carrier particles is a ratio of 5-20 wt.% relative to the first carrier particles. The size of the first carrier particles is 50 nm to 50 nm. The copper has a preferred orientation of 100 at the particle surface. The content of the complex catalyst is included in an amount of 1-60 wt.% based on the total carrier. The surface of the multicatalyst particles comprises a functional group capable of inducing a carbon surface reactant. Preferred Components: The first carrier particles and second carrier particles are selected from carbon, metal or conductive oxide. The catalyst particle of the metal component is copper (Cu), and the catalyst particle of the metal oxide or metal nitride component is cuprous oxide (Cu2O). The catalyst particles of the metal oxide or metal nitride component are Cu, Tantalum (Ta), Osmium (Os), Niobium (Nb), Molybdenum (Mo), Iridium (Ir), silver (Ag), vanadium (V), Ruthenium (Ru), Manganese (Mn), Rhodium (Rh), lead (Pd), Nickel (Ni), cobalt (Co), iorn (Fe), chromium (Cr), Hafnium (Hf) oxides or nitrides comprising Titanium (Ti), Yttrium (Y), Zirconium (Zr), or Scandium (Sc). The catalyst particles of the metal component is selected from Ag, Ni, gold (Au), platinum (Pt), Ti, zinc (Zn), cadmium (Cd) or an alloy containing these metals.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The ethanol drips into the sample solution along the bottle wall.

PT P
PN CN110451701-A
TI Removing heavy metals from incineration fly ash leachate involves filtering incineration fly ash leachate, introducing simulated cement kiln tail gas, filtering, adding chelating agent, reacting and measuring concentration of heavy metals.
AU WANG L
AE SUZHOU FEIHUI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201998025U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing heavy metals from incineration fly ash leachate involves (i) filtering incineration fly ash leachate in a filtering device to obtain an incineration fly ash leachate filtering solid ash, measuring the pH value of the incineration fly ash leachate, and measuring the concentration of heavy metals, (ii) introducing simulated cement kiln tail gas to the incineration fly ash leachate obtained in step (i) for aeration, reducing the pH value of the incineration fly ash leachate to weak acid, stopping aeration, and filtering the incineration fly ash leachate in a filtering device to obtain aerated incineration fly ash leachate, and (iii) adding macromolecular chelating agent to the incineration fly ash leachate after aeration treatment, stirring and reacting for certain time and measuring the concentration of heavy metals in incineration fly ash leachate after the reaction is completed.
   USE - The method is useful for removing heavy metals from incineration fly ash leachate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reduced carbon dioxide emission rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The aeration process is carried out by blowing gas to leachate at gas flow rate of 0.1-0.3 m3/second at 25-80 degrees C for 2-4 hours. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the pH value of incinerated fly ash leachate is more than 12. In step (iii), the reaction is carried out for 30-60 minutes. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the chelating agent and incinerated fly ash leachate in step (iii) is 10-30 g/1 l.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises copper chloride, copper bromide, copper oxide and/or copper acetate. The alkaline solution is sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate or potassium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amination modifier comprises diethylenetriamine, ethylenediamine, ethanolamine and/or aniline.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The macromolecular chelating agent is prepared by taking 50-100 g divinyl benzene crosslinked sulfonated polystyrene in a reaction container, adding 1.5-3 g catalyst and 3-8 g L-proline, stirring and reacting at 40-60 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, adding 80-300 ml amination modifier, dripping 20-50 ml alkaline solution having a concentration of 0.5-1 mol/l to the reaction container, stirring at 200-400 rpm, condensing and refluxing, reacting at 60-120 degrees C for 8-24 hours, sequentially washing with water and ethanol after the reaction is completed, filtering, and drying at 100-110 degrees C.

   USE - The method is useful for making swamp buffalo rumen fluid isolates (claimed), which is used as a source of probiotics to increase the productivity of ruminants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the isolates with high nutritional value and palatability.

In the method, the microbes are carried out by inserting 0.1 ml of the incubation results from each tube in the incubation process into 9.9 ml of diluent media, then 0.1 ml is taken from the tubes and put in 9.9 ml of diluent media in the next tube. The step of isolation (purification) of bacteria colonies of bacteria growing in each tube are taken aseptively using dose and transferred to the liquid media that has been provided, where the liquid media used is the modified BRI media (BRI + fiber) of 5 ml, then the microbes are incubated in animal feed for 7 days with the characteristics of feed having a crude protein nutrient content of 15.02%, crude fiber of 18.13%, crude fat of 3.24 % and dry matter is 45.27%, ammonia is 9.46 and total VFA is 158.72, while the partial VFA consists of acetic acid (58.42 ml/mol), propionic acid (25.26 ml/mol), acid butyrate (11.46 ml/mol), isovalerate (2.26 ml/mol) and total bacteria of 12.82 ml/mol, cellulolytic bacteria of 10.06 ml/mol, proteolytic bacteria of 11.24 ml/mol, amylolytic bacteria 9.87 ml/mol and protozoa at 4.93 ml/mol.

TI Foamed alloy useful for foaming in space environment comprises magnesium, aluminum, calcium, foaming agent, and impurities.
AU HE S
   TANG G
   LV Y
   ZHAO W
   ZHANG Y
   DAI G
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201948238D
AB    NOVELTY - Foamed alloy comprises 66.4 wt.% magnesium, 33.6 wt.% aluminum, 2 wt.% calcium, 1.5 wt.% foaming agent, and impurities, where the mass fractions of magnesium and aluminum is greater than 96.
   USE - The foamed alloy is useful for foaming in space environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The foamed alloy: has low density, low melting point and high strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the foamed alloy comprising (i) smelting the aluminum ingot and magnesium ingot into crucible to molten state to obtain a magnesium-aluminum alloy melt, (ii) introducing SF6 and carbon dioxide gas-protected magnesium-aluminum alloy melt into the crucible, adjusting the temperature at 550-600 degrees C, adding calcium particles, stirring and cooling, (iii) adding blowing agent when the temperature is lower than 450 degrees C, stirred at a speed of 900-1200 rotations/minute, and cooling by water to obtain foamed perform, and (iv) cutting the prefabricated parts in space environment, under vacuum of 0.05 kPa or under normal pressure conditions, raising the temperature to 500-800 degrees C, for 7-20 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) Tthe magnesium-aluminum alloy melt, where the mass fraction of magnesium is 66.4 wt.%, and aluminum is 33.6 wt.%. In the step (ii), the volume ratio of SF6 and carbon dioxide is 1:10. The stirring time is 8-10 minutes, and cooling to 440-450 degrees C. The calcium particles account for 2 wt.% foamed alloy. In the step (iii), the stirring time is 90-150 seconds. Preferred Components: The foaming agent is magnesite or calcium carbonate. The foaming agent accounts for 1.5 wt.% foamed alloy.

   HOU C
   YAN Z
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 201942923B
AB    NOVELTY - Supported catalyst comprises lanthanum-containing inorganic oxide support and active metal component and auxiliary agent supported on the support. The active metal component is cobalt and/or nickel. The auxiliary agent is group IVB metal element, group VB metal element, group VIB metal element and group VIIB metal element oxide.
   USE - The supported catalyst is useful for the synthesis of synthesis gas from methane dry reforming (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the supported catalyst, comprising introducing active metal component and auxiliary agent onto lanthanum-containing inorganic oxide support by an impregnation method, where the active metal component is cobalt and/or nickel and the auxiliary agent is group IVB metal element, group VB metal element, group VIB metal element and oxide of group VIIB metal element; and
   (2) processing dry reforming methane to syngas, comprising utilizing methane dry reforming syngas, contacting methane and carbon dioxide with catalyst, where the catalyst is the supported catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of the active metal component is 4-20 wt.%, preferably 6-10 wt.% based on the metal element. The auxiliary agent is contained in an amount of 0.1-5 wt.%, preferably 0.5-4 wt.%. The auxiliary agent is tungsten, molybdenum, niobium, tantalum, rhenium and hydrofluoric acid, preferably tungsten molybdenum, niobium and rhenium. The lanthanum-containing inorganic oxide carrier has a lanthanum content of 0.5-5 atoms/square nanometer, preferably 1-3 atoms/square nanometer. The lanthanum-containing inorganic oxide support has a specific surface area of 80-250 m2/g and a pore volume of 0.3-0.8 ml/g. The inorganic oxide support is one-component oxide support, two-component or three-component composite oxide support, clay, and natural zeolite. The one-component oxide carrier is silicon dioxide, titanium dioxide, magnesium oxide, aluminum oxide, zirconium dioxide, thorium dioxide and beryllium oxide. The two-component or three-component composite oxide carrier is magnesium oxide-aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide-aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide-magnesium oxide, silicon dioxide-zirconium dioxide, silicon dioxide-thorium dioxide, silicon dioxide-beryllium oxide, silicon dioxide-titanium dioxide, titanium dioxide-zirconium dioxide, aluminum oxide-zirconium dioxide, silicon dioxide-aluminum oxide-thorium dioxide, silicon dioxide-aluminum oxide-magnesium oxide, silicon dioxide-aluminum oxide-zirconium dioxide and titanium dioxide-silicon dioxide-aluminum oxide. The inorganic oxide support is magnesium oxide, aluminum oxide, magnesium oxide-aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide-aluminum oxide, and silicon dioxide-aluminum oxide-magnesium oxide, preferably magnesium oxide-aluminum oxide and/or aluminum oxide. The solution further contains surfactant, preferably, the surfactant is anionic surfactant, amphoteric surfactant, and nonionic surfactant, preferably stearic acid, oleic acid, lauric acid, lecithin, dodecylaminopropionic acid, alkyl dimethyl betaine, fatty acid glyceride, polyhydric alcohol, Tween 60 (RTM: Polyoxyethylene sorbitan monostearate) and P123, preferably P123, oleic acid and Tween 60 (RTM: Polyoxyethylene sorbitan monostearate) and the molar ratio of the surfactant to the total metal atom of the active metal component and the adjuvant is 0.01-2:1, preferably 0.01-0.03:1. Preferred Method: The manner of introducing the active metal component and the auxiliary agent onto the lanthanum-containing inorganic oxide support comprises impregnating lanthanum -containing inorganic oxide support with a solution of a soluble compound containing a soluble metal component of active metal component and auxiliary agent, drying and calcining, where the soluble compound of the active metal component is nickel nitrate, cobalt nitrate, nickel acetate, cobalt acetate, nickel acetylacetonate and cobalt acetylacetonate and the soluble compound of the auxiliary agent is ammonium metatungstate, ammonium orthomolybdate ammonium oxobisoxalato-bisaquoniobate trihydrate, and ammonium perrhenate. The method for preparing the lanthanum-containing inorganic oxide carrier comprises impregnating inorganic oxide support with a solution containing a soluble compound of cerium and calcining. The contacting is carried out in a fixed bed reactor or a fluidized bed reactor, and the conditions for dry reforming of methane to syngas include: the volume ratio of methane to carbon dioxide is 0.7-1.1:1, preferably 0.8-1:1. The reaction temperature is 550-850 degrees C, preferably 610-700 degrees C. The pressure is 0-3 MPa, preferably 0-1 MPa. The total space velocity of the feed gas is 2000-140000 ml/g/h, preferably 130000-140000 ml/g/h.

TI Preparing high base value sulfurized alkyl salicylate comprises e.g. adding salicylic acid to flask, long chain alpha-olefin and polar catalyst methanesulfonic acid, reacting, and removing lower layer polar catalyst methanesulfonic acid.
AU REN X
   WAN X
   FAN J
   WANG X
AE XINXIANG RUIFENG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (XINX-Non-standard)
GA 201941104E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high base value sulfurized alkyl salicylate comprises (i) adding salicylic acid to the flask, long chain alpha -olefin and polar catalyst methanesulfonic acid, reacting, and removing the lower layer polar catalyst methanesulfonic acid by layering and centrifuging, Obtaining an alkyl salicylic acid product containing a solvent in the upper layer; and (ii) adding alkyl salicylic acid product, co-dispersant, base oil, basic metal compound, sulfur, and then adding ethylene glycol to the and carrying out sulfurization reaction, and then introducing alkali metal compound and introducing carbon dioxide to initiate the alkalization reaction, filtering the residue and distilling the filtrate under reduced pressure.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high base value sulfurized alkyl salicylate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts a continuous synthesis process; has mild reaction conditions, has a high alkali value, does not contains free phenol; and has excellent high temperature detergency, oxidation resistance and abrasion resistance; and has environmental protection and energy saving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) is specifically comprises adding salicylic acid, long chain alpha -olefin and polarity to the flask, where the catalyst is methanesulfonic acid, heating the system to 110-150 degrees C, stirring and incubating for 8-20 hours, adding alkane solvent oil, and allowing to stand the mixture for 10-48 hours, then layering, and removing the lower layer catalyst by centrifugation to obtain the upper layer containing the solvent alkyl salicylic acid products. The step (ii) is specifically comprises adding an alkyl salicylic acid product to the flask in turn, helping dispersant, base oil, alkaline metal compounds, sulfur, turning on the heating, carrying out the neutralization reaction at 130-160 degrees C for 1-4 hours, then adding ethylene glycol to the flask for sulfurization reaction, the system is incubated at 130-180 degrees C for 3-6 hours, then the basic metal compound is placed into the carbon dioxide and the alkalization reaction is started, the carbon dioxide ventilation time is incubated for 3-5 hours, and after the end of the aeration, the temperature is reduced to 100 degrees C, and the alkane solvent oil is added, the filter aid is filtered to remove the slag, and the filtered filtrate is subjected to distillation under reduced pressure to remove ethylene glycol and a solvent to obtain a high base value sulfurized alkyl salicylate product. Preferred Components: The long chain alpha -olefin is olefins having a carbon number 10-30. The addition quality of salicylic acid, the long-chain alpha -olefin, polar catalyst methanesulfonic acid, and the alkane solvent oil satisfies: the molar ratio of the salicylic acid to the long-chain alpha -olefin is 1:1-1.5:1, the polar catalyst methanesulfonic acid is used in an amount of 20-40 wt.% long-chain alpha -olefin, and alkane solvent oil is total system formed by salicylic acid, long-chain alpha -olefin, the polar catalyst methanesulfonic acid and alkane solvent oil has 30-50%. The co-dispersing agent is polyisobutylene succinimide, polyisobutylene succinic anhydride, alkylbenzene sulfonic acids. The mass ratio of alkyl salicylic acid product, the co-dispersant, base oil, the basic metal compound, sulfur, and ethylene glycol is satisfied: the added amount of alkyl salicylic acid added is 100 pts. wt., and the dispersion is assisted agent is 2-8 pts. wt., 20-40 pts. wt. base oil, 15-30 pts. wt. basic metal compound, where the added amount of the basic metal compound added during the neutralization reaction is 5-10 pts. wt., and the added amount of the basic metal compound added during the alkalization reaction is 5-25 pts. wt., 5-10 pts. wt. sulfur and 10-50 pts. wt. ethylene glycol. The alkane solvent oil is added in an amount of 50-100 pts. wt..

   ADVANTAGE - The composition is prepared by simple process, and has low production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the composition, involving (i) crushing G.lucidum through a 10-20 mesh sieve to obtain G.lucidum powder, soaking in an ethanol solution for 1-2 hours, and subjecting the soaked G.lucidum powder to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction at a extraction kettle temperature of 38-42 degrees C under a pressure of 30-35 MPa for 3-4 hours to obtain a G.lucidum extract, (ii) taking H.hepiali mycelium, adding 20-30 times water, boiling for 1-2 hours, heat-preserving, soaking for more than 12 hours, boiling and extracting for 1-2 hours, concentrating and vacuum-drying the concentrated solution to obtain a H.hepiali mycelium extract, and (iii) mixing the G.lucidum extract and the H.hepiali mycelium extract in a weight ratio of (2:1)-(1:2).

AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)

   USE - Double-site synergistic catalyst is used in direct production of lower alcohol e.g. ethanol from syngas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The double-site synergistic catalyst has excellent selectivity, activity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst, which involves mixing lanthanum ion, iron ion, rhodium ion, cobalt ion or nickel ion, citric acid and chelating agent in a molar ratio of 1:(0.9-0.1):(0.1-0.9):(0.3-3):(0.5-5), obtaining mixed solution with total ion concentration of 0.725-7.25 mol/L, adding silica or zirconium dioxide carrier, aging, drying, reacting, and reducing in reaction gas; and
   (2) production of lower alcohol, which involves adding the catalyst to a fixed bed reactor, supplying mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a molar ratio of 0.5-3:1, and reacting.

The lanthanum ion is derived from lanthanum nitrate. The iron ion is derived from ferric nitrate. The rhodium ion is derived from rhodium nitrate. The cobalt ion is derived from cobalt nitrate. The nickel ion is derived from nickel nitrate. The reaction gas includes hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane and/or protective gas e.g. nitrogen, argon or helium gas. The usage amount of reaction gas is 1-99 vol.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The chelating agent is ethylene glycol or ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid.

The glycerine aqueous solution is injected into fixed bed reactor to produce high-purity hydrogen by absorption enhanced reforming reaction and carbon dioxide is absorbed by nickel-calcium-aluminum catalyst. The concentration of glycerine aqueous solution is 0.222-0.951 gram/milliliter. The weight hour space velocity is 0.266-1.141 h-1. The reaction temperature is 400-700 degrees C and after adsorption-enhanced reforming reaction is completed. The methane reforming reaction is carried out at space velocity of 600-12000 h-1 to produce synthesis gas at reaction temperature of 500-900 degrees C.
   USE - Method for co-producing hydrogen and synthesis gas from glycerine and methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to co-produce hydrogen and synthesis gas from glycerine and methane that has high activity and high stability in process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compostions: The mass content of nickel-calcium-aluminum catalyst is 5-20 wt.%. The concentration of methane gas is 5-100 vol.% Preferred Conditions: The ratio of calcium and aluminum in nickel-calcium-aluminum catalyst is 2.3-3.3. The concentration of glycerine aqueous solution is 0.222-0.383 gram/milliliter. The weight hourly space velocity of glycerine aqueous solution is 0.266-0.394 gram/milliliter. The reaction temperature is 500-600 degrees C.

PN CN109021779-A
TI Preparing heat-resistant ceramic coating comprises e.g. mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, red mud, additive and modifier, subjecting obtained precipitate by solid-liquid separation to flotation, calcining and grinding the obtained alumina.
AU CHEN X
   CHEN Q
   CHEN C
   ZHAO P
   WANG Q
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018A5161K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing heat-resistant ceramic coating comprises (i) mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, red mud, additive and modifier, and feeding into kiln for roasting to obtain clinker, (ii) subjecting obtained clinker to water milling, dissolving in water, and subjected to solid-liquid separation, (iii) subjecting obtained precipitate to flotation, and separating iron sulfide, (iv) roasting separated iron sulfide to obtain iron oxide, (v) adding carbon dioxide into obtained liquid and solid-liquid separating until white precipitate is no longer to produced, filtering white precipitate, washing, drying, calcining to obtain alumina, grinding alumina and calcining to obtain alumina powder, and (vi) adding epoxy resin, silicon dioxide, iron oxide into obtained alumina powder, then adding titanium dioxide and polyurethane dispersant and water, and mixing to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing heat-resistant ceramic coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramic coating: has simple iron raising process, low production cost and excellent water resistance; and utilizes easily available raw materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: In step (i), red mud is produced by Bayer process for producing alumina; additive is sodium carbonate or caustic soda; and modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. In step (i), mass ratio between mixture of phosphogypsum and Bayer red mud is 1:0.6-1.2; addition ratio of additive based on molecular ratio of sum of sodium oxide, aluminum oxide and iron(III) oxide is 1:1, and mixing ratio of modifier is 9-18% the total amount of raw materials. In step (i), kiln is industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln. In step (ii), liquid to solid ratio at time of dissolution is 5-8:1. In step (vi), heat resistant ceramic coating comprises 25-35 pts. wt. alumina powder, 20-30 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 8-15 pts. wt. silica, 5-10 pts. wt. iron oxide, 3-7 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 0.6-1.5 pts. wt. polyurethane dispersant and 10-25 pts. wt. water. Preferred Method: In step (i), calcining at 1100-1340 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours. In step (iv), placing iron sulfide in oxygen-rich environment of 25-45% and calcining at 870-1080 degrees C for 1-5 hours. In step (v), calcining white precipitate at 820-1120 degrees C for 2-4 hours, grinding alumina, calcining at 750-880 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours to obtain alumina powder. In step (vi), deriving iron oxide from iron oxide in step (iv).

TI Producing high oil-uptake ball shaped silica used in cosmetics comprises e.g. preparing reaction solution containing sodium chloride and sodium silicate, and mixed solution containing surfactant and oil, mixing and adding gelling agent.
AU TAE R S
   MOON K Y
AE HUMANCHEM INC (HUMA-Non-standard)
GA 201876779R
AB    NOVELTY - Producing high oil-uptake ball shaped silica comprises (a) preparing reaction solution by mixing sodium chloride solution with sodium silicate solution, (b) mixing surfactant with oil to prepare mixed solution, (c) mixing the prepared reaction solution and the mixed solution to emulsify the mixture, (d) transferring the emulsion to a high-pressure reactor, adding gelling agent, preferably solid carbon dioxide (dry ice), maintaining reaction mixture for 20-40 minutes, heating at 70-90 degrees C for 1 hour and 30 minutes to 2 hours and 30 minutes to complete the reaction, (e) separating the completed reactant (reaction solution) to obtain silica cake, and (f) drying the silica cake to obtain spherical silica.

AU GE R G
AE GE R G (GERG-Individual)
GA 201970475J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves controlling phytoplankton and bacterial populations which are divided into three phases with a predetermined period. The first phase of the period from the first day to the 20th day, the second phase of the period that starts from the 21st day to the 79th day, and the third phase is the 80th day period until the 100th day or when the shrimp is harvested. The control of the type and amount of phytoplankton is adjusted by the ratio between nitrogen concentration and phosphor concentration in water, by regulating the availability of carbon dioxide (CO2) in water, and by controlling the heterotrophy bacteria population by adjusting the comparison between the organic carbon and nitrogen concentration in water.
   USE - Intensive shrimp farming method.
   ADVANTAGE - The fishponds are clean of waste, ecosystems in the pond is encouraged for shrimp growth, the water becomes healthy since the pathogens are controlled, and phytoplankton and bacteria are ensured to be nutrient-rich natural food.

AU PAN Z
   YUAN H

   CAI H
AE INST CHEM MATERIAL CHINA ACAD ENG PHYSIC (CAEP-C)
GA 201869142X
AB    NOVELTY - A crystal comprises ytterbium-doped strontium aluminate lanthanum compound (I), where the doping concentration of the trivalent ytterbium ion is 5-30 at.%.
   USE - The crystal is used for generating an ultrashort pulse laser in a solid mode-locked laser (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A crystal comprises ytterbium-doped strontium aluminate lanthanum compound of formula: SrLa1-xYbxAlO4 (I), where the doping concentration of the trivalent ytterbium ion is 5-30 at.%.
   x = 0.05-0.3.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the crystal, which involves carrying out solid phase reaction on strontium carbonate with a purity of 99.99%, aluminum oxide with a purity of 99.99%, lanthanum oxide with a purity of 99.99% and ytterbium(III) oxide with a purity of 99.99% to obtain a polycrystalline material, and then carrying out pulling method, where the solid phase reaction involves reacting two moles of strontium carbonate, (1-x) moles of lanthanum carbonate, aluminum oxide and x moles of ytterbium(III) oxide to obtain compound (I) with by-product of two moles of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystal belongs to a potassium tetrafluoride nickelate(II) structure. Preferred Method: The solid-phase reaction involves uniformly mixing the above-mentioned raw materials, carrying out solid-phase reaction at 1150-1250 degrees C, preferably 1200 degrees C for 15-20 hours, preferably 15 hours, taking out and grinding into powder, mixing and tableting, and again carrying out solid-phase reaction at 1250-1350 degrees C, preferably 1300 degrees C for 20-30 hours, preferably 20 hours to obtain the polycrystalline material. The pulling method involves placing the polycrystalline material subjected to solid phase reaction in a single crystal furnace, where the pulling speed is 1-2 mm/hour during the growth process, and the rotation speed of the seed crystal rod is 8-20 rpm.

The medicine cylinder shell penetrates through weak points on the liquid module by pressing the liquid module.

The liquid module further comprises a sealed membrane, a liquid module housing, a shear pin and a limiting ring. The sealed membrane is fixed in the liquid module housing. The shear pin is located on the liquid module housing at a joint of the liquid module and the solid module. TI New genetically engineered strain of Synechocystis PCC6803, useful for producing alkaline pectin lyase.
AU DAI Y
   MENG Q
   HE A
   ZHAO X

   CHEN G
   JIA S
   ZHANG Y
   LI H
   LV H
   ZHONG C
   LIU H
   LI T
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
GA 2020D0019L
AB    NOVELTY - A genetically engineered strain of Synechocystis PCC6803 is new. The construction method of genetically engineered strain of Synechocystis PCC6803, is also claimed.
   USE - The genetically engineered strain of Synechocystis PCC6803 is useful for producing alkaline pectin lyase.
   ADVANTAGE - The Synechocystis PCC6803 engineered algae strain producing alkaline pectin lyase has higher enzyme activity than the wild Synechocystis strain PSTY1. The method obtains a new way to produce pectinase using inorganic salts and carbon dioxide and also reduces the consumption of energy (mainly carbon source) in biomass conversion process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves (a) using upstream and downstream primers comprising SEQ ID NO: 1 and SEQ ID NO: 2, using wild Synechocystis PCC6803 genome as template, obtaining light-sensitive promoter and upstream arm photosystem II protein D1 2 precursor (psbA2)-up gene by PCR amplification for fragment, introducing Hpa1 restriction site at 3'end through a primer to obtain a 501 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 3) fully defined in the specification, using upstream and downstream primers comprising SEQ ID NO: 4 and SEQ ID NO: 5 respectively, using Escherichia coli with GenBank U02439.1 as template, obtaining E.coli terminator T1/T2 gene fragment by PCR amplification technology to obtain a 540 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 6) fully defined in the specification, using a 34 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 7) fully defined in the specification and SEQ ID NO: 8 as the upstream and downstream primers, using the wild Synechocystis PCC6803 genome as template, obtaining downstream gene fragments of downstream arm by PCR amplification technology to obtain a 1292 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 9) fully defined in the specification, (b) subjecting the E.coli terminator T1T2 gene fragment obtained in step (a) to double digestion with PStI and BamHI, ligating with the pBluescript SK plasmid after the same double digestion with T4 ligase to obtain plasmid pBluescript SK T1T2, separately digesting the pBluescript SK T1T2-downstream and pUC4K plasmids with the restriction enzyme BamHI, after electrophoresis, separately recovering the pBluescript SK T1T2-downstream vector fragment and the kanamycin resistance gene fragment, phosphorylating and ligating the vector fragment with the kanamycin resistance gene fragment to obtain the plasmid pBluescript SK T1T2-npt-downstream, treating plasmid pBluescript SK T1T2-npt-downstream with restriction enzyme ApaI to obtain pBluescript SKT1T2-npt-downstream vector fragment, after phosphorylation, subjecting the vector fragment to homologous recombination with the psbA2-up gene fragment to obtain plasmid p5st1t2npt, (c) using a 40 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 10) fully defined in the specification and a 49 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 11) fully defined in the specification as upstream and downstream primers, using corresponding gene sequence of Klebsiella sp. Y1 in the GenBank database as a template, and obtaining the alkaline pectin lyase gene fragment pety1 gene fragments by PCR amplification technology to obtain a 1638 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 12) fully defined in the specification, (d) cutting the plasmid p5st1t2npt with restriction endonuclease HpaI to obtain p5st1t2npt vector fragment, after phosphorylation, subjecting the vector fragment to homologous recombination with pety1 gene fragment to obtain recombinant expression plasmid PSTY1 and (e) naturally transforming recombinant expression vector plasmid PSTY1 into Synechocystis PCC6803, and after resistance screening, obtaining transgenic Synechocystis strain i.e. PSTY1 engineered algae strain. In the step (a), the primers for PCR amplification of the light-sensitive promoter and upstream arm psbA2-up gene fragment are: psbA2-up-F: 5'-gatgtcgacgctttagcgttccagtg-3', psbA2-up-R: 5'-catttggttataattccttatgtat-3', where performing the amplification program at 94 degrees C for 3 minutes, at 98 degrees C for 10 seconds, at 55 degrees C for 15 seconds, at 72 degrees C for 30 seconds for 30 cycles, at 72 degrees C for 5 minutes and 4 degrees C for 10 minutes. In the step (a), the primers for PCR amplification of the E.coli terminator T1T2 gene fragment are: T1T2-F: 5'-atactgcagccaagcttggctgttttggc-3' and T1T2-R: 5'-ttaggatcccccattattgaagcatttat-3', where performing the amplification program at 94 degrees C for 3 minutes, at 98 degrees C for 10 seconds, at 55 degrees C for 15 seconds, at 72 degrees C for 40 seconds for 30 cycles, at 72 degrees C for 5 minutes and 4 degrees C for 10 minutes. In the step (a), the primers for PCR amplification of downstream gene fragments in the downstream arm are: downstream-F: 5'-cttcatatgccgcggatgacaacgactctccac-3' and downstream-R: 5'-agtgagctcttaaccgttgacagcagg-3', where performing the amplification program at 94 degrees C for 3 minutes, at 98 degrees C for 10 seconds, at 55 degrees C for 15 seconds, at 72 degrees C for 1.5 minutes for 30 cycles, at 72 degrees C for 5 minutes and 4 degrees C for 10 minutes. In the step (c), the primers for PCR amplification of the pety1 gene fragment of alkaline pectin lyase are: pety1-F: 5'-aggaattataaccaagttaacatgcaggagaacgagagcc-3' and pety1-R: 5'-ctcgagggggggcccgttaatgatgatgatgatgatggccgttc-3', where performing the amplification program is performed at 94 degrees C for 3 minutes, at 98 degrees C for 10 seconds, at 55 degrees C for 15 seconds, at 72 degrees C for 2 minutes for 30 cycles, 72 degrees C for 5 minutes and at 4 degrees C for 10 minutes. gatgtcgacgctttagcgttccagtg (SEQ ID NO: 1), catttggttataattccttatgtat (SEQ ID NO: 2), atactgcagccaagcttggctgttttggc (SEQ ID NO: 4), ttaggatcccccattattgaagcatttat (SEQ ID NO: 5), and agtgagctcttaaccgttgacagcagg (SEQ ID NO: 8).

TI Device for calculating carbon emission treatment cost, has cost estimator that estimates cost of treating carbon emissions, output unit that outputs calculated carbon emission treatment cost, and input unit that receives company name.

AE UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
GA 202062389J
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has an input unit (110) that receives a company name and a model name of construction equipment used at a construction site from a user. A carbon dioxide emission prediction unit (130) predicts carbon dioxide emission of construction equipment corresponding to the entered company name and model name using a database (120) in which carbon dioxide emission data for each model name of construction equipment is previously stored. The carbon emission transaction price information corresponding to the inputted information is reflected in the predicted carbon dioxide emission by accessing the website provided by the Korea Exchange server (200) and collecting real-time carbon emission transaction price information through web crawling. A cost estimator (140) estimates the cost of treating carbon emissions. An output unit (150) outputs the calculated carbon emission treatment cost.
   USE - Device for calculating carbon emission treatment cost according to amount of carbon dioxide emissions of construction equipment used in construction sites.
   ADVANTAGE - The cost of carbon emission treatment of construction equipment is calculated by reflecting the cost of real-time carbon credits in the amount of carbon dioxide emitted from construction equipment at the construction site, so that the cost of purchasing carbon credits is quickly calculated. The total carbon emission treatment cost is quickly and accurately calculated for construction equipment according to information input through an application. The carbon emission transaction price provided by the Korea exchange is crawled in real time to reflect the real-time changing carbon emission ticket purchase cost, so that the carbon emission processing cost is accurately calculated. The type, number, and combination of construction equipment is efficiently managed by comparing the calculated carbon emission treatment cost with the budget or construction period secured at the construction site.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for calculating a carbon emission treatment cost using an apparatus for calculating carbon emission treatment costs according to carbon dioxide emission of construction equipment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram showing an apparatus for calculating a carbon emission treatment cost according to carbon dioxide emission of construction equipment. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN113974048-A
TI Processing supercritical wall-breaking of cereals comprises controlling moisture content of the cereals to be processed, placing into supercritical carbon dioxide high pressure container, controlling carbon dioxide temperature, collecting the five cereals and vacuum bagging after breaking.
AU LIU C
AE LIU C (LIUC-Individual)
GA 202224273F
AB    NOVELTY - Processing supercritical wall-breaking of cereals comprises controlling moisture content of the cereals to be processed at 4-20%, placing into a supercritical carbon dioxide high pressure container, controlling the carbon dioxide temperature at 32-45degrees Celsius under 8-55 MPa, acting for 2-8 hours, opening the pressure release valve in a short time emptying the carbon dioxide to the storage tank (can be used repeatedly), collecting the five cereals and vacuum bagging after breaking. The cereals are brown rice, corn, wheat, millet, black rice, coix seed rice, red bean, mung bean, soybean, black bean, pea, black sesame, buckwheat, oat and all the edible and shell protection of all crops of a type, namely red jujube.
   USE - The method is useful for processing supercritical wall-breaking of cereals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can ensure that the existing internal and external pressure difference causes carbon dioxide to have a great impact on the cell wall in an instant, cells of the whole grain seeds swelled violently and ruptured, collect the broken grains for vacuum bagging, then according to the principle of complementarity of many types of whole grain protein to make a reasonable combination, thus increase its nutritional value, and improve the utilization rate of dietary fiber.

PN MY184730-A
TI New            1-benzoyl-3-(4-(4-cyano-phenylethynyl)-phenyl)-thiourea            derivatives useful in sensor for detecting carbon            dioxide in a sample, in which the sample is gas.
AU ZIN W M K W M
   WAHID K A A
   DAUD A I
AE UNIV MALAYSIA TERENGGANU (UYMA-Non-standard)
GA 2022A40096
AB    NOVELTY -                 1-Benzoyl-3-[4-(4-cyano-phenylethynyl)-phenyl]-thiourea                derivatives (I) are new.
   USE - (I) are useful in sensor for detection of                carbon dioxide in a sample, where the sample is a                gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): allow quick detection and measurement of                carbon dioxide; and provides the sensor that gives                highly reproducible measurements when being exposed                to the same sample or environment for multiple                times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION -                 1-Benzoyl-3-[4-(4-cyano-phenylethynyl)-phenyl]-thiourea                derivatives of formula (I) and their salts are                new.
   R=1-7C alkyl, 1-7C alkoxy, 1-7C phenyl,                phenol, 1-7C amide or 1-7C ester.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   a sensor for detecting carbon dioxide in a                sample comprising an electrode deposited with any                one or any combination of (I); and
   detecting carbon dioxide in a sample                comprising (i) depositing (I) on an electrode, (ii)                applying a constant voltage across the electrode                and measuring a first electrical resistance in the                electrode, (iii) putting the electrode in contact                with the sample, (iv) applying a constant voltage                across the electrode and measuring a second                electrical resistance in the electrode and (v)                calculating difference of the first and second                electrical resistance, where a difference in                electrical resistance in the electrode indicates                presence of carbon dioxide in the sample.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrode is an                  interdigitated electrode. The sensor has                  sensitivity towards carbon dioxide of 10-1000                  ppm. Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises correlating difference in electrical                  resistance to concentration of carbon                  dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No preparation method is                  given.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 3 Compounds (I) are disclosed i.e.                  4-ethylbenzoyl-3-(4-ethynylbenzonitrile-phenyl)-thiourea                  of formula (Ia), 4-methylbenzoyl-3-                  (4-ethynylbenzonitrile- phenyl)-thiourea and                  4-methoxybenzoyl-3-(4-ethynylbenzonitrile-phenyl)-thiourea.
                                                                                                                                 
   EXAMPLE - Solution of acetone and                4-(4-amino-phenylethynyl)-benzonitrile was added                dropwise into a solution containing acetone and                equimolar amount of 4-ethyl-benzoyl isothiocyanate.                The mixture was refluxed for 6 hours. Impurities in                the reaction mixture was filtered and                4-ethylbenzoyl-3-(4-ethynylbenzonitrile-phenyl)-thiourea                in the filtrate was recrystallized.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves rotating a sampler with end facing up, end facing down. An inside of the sampler is not dissolved in the oil sample. A free Carbon dioxide gas in a water sample is discharged. A valve is opened between the sampler and a separator to discharge a certain volume of oil sample. Volume of crude oil released in the separator and volume in the gas meter are recorded to calculate gas-oil ratio. The sampler is rotated again with the end facing down, the end facing up and let stand to an oil moisture layer. The valve is opened between the sampler and the separator to record the formation water discharged from the separator. Displacement of a displacement pump is increased to obtain volume of dissolved Carbon dioxide in the crude oil and formation water samples. A water phase is calculated.
   USE - Method for testing competition dissolving of Carbon dioxide in an oil-water mixed system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing simple and practical process for accurately evaluating the dissolving condition comparing real reservoir condition formation water and contact with the crude oil with wide market application prospect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical illustration view of a competition dissolving of Carbon dioxide. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN WO2018183244-A1; AU2018246120-A1; CA3058159-A1; EP3600590-A1; EP3600590-A4; US2021101091-A1; MX2019011640-A1; US11097205-B2; AU2018246120-B2; CA3058159-C; MX388110-B
TI Apparatus for removing hydrophobic particles using carbon dioxide has mixing compartment to facilitate contact between collection surfaces and releasing agent to separate mineral particles from collection surfaces of liquid media.
AU RYAN M S
AE CIDRA CORP SERVICES LLC (CIDR-C)
   CIDRA CORP SERVICES LLC (CIDR-C)
   CIDRA CORP SERIVICES LLC (CIDR-C)
   CIDRA CORP SERVICES LLC (CIDR-C)
   CIDRA CORP SERVICES LLC (CIDR-C)
GA 2018776805
AB    NOVELTY - An apparatus for removing hydrophobic particles using carbon dioxide comprises loaded media comprising greater than or equal to 1 collection surfaces on which mineral particles are attached; releasing agent; and mixing compartment configured to facilitate contacting between greater than or equal to 1 collection surfaces and releasing agent to separate mineral particles from greater than or equal to 1 collection surfaces, where releasing agent is selected from liquid carbon dioxide, supercritical fluid carbon dioxide, hydrofluorocarbon (HFC), alcohols, and liquid alkanes.
   USE - An apparatus for removing hydrophobic particles using carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of liquid carbon dioxide essentially prevents occurrence of release agent carry-over, protecting the overall system from release agent contamination and providing near 100% recirculation of release agent, and as a result, saving cost and providing environmental benefits. The release agents include materials that are effective at particle removal due to low surface tension while also having the ability to be evaporated from the substrate, re-condensed, and then re-used for subsequent release processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for collection of mineral particles in a slurry which involves receiving loaded media and causing the loaded media to interact with releasing agent to separate the mineral particles from collection surfaces.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Collection surfaces comprise solid-phase body having a surface functionalized to be hydrophobic. Solid-phase body is made of a synthetic material. Solid-phase body comprises a three-dimensional open cellular structure. Solid-phase body comprises open-cell foam coated with a compliant, tacky polymer of low surface energy. Solid-phase body comprises open-cell foam made from a material selected from reticulated polyurethane, silicone, polychloroprene, polyisocyanurate, polystyrene, polyolefin, polyvinylchloride, epoxy, latex, fluoropolymer, phenolic, ethylene propylene diene monomer (EPDM) rubber, and nitrile. The surface comprises a coating made of polysiloxane derivative. The surface is modified with tackifiers, plasticizers, crosslinking agents, chain transfer agents, chain extenders, adhesion promoters, aryl or alky copolymers, fluorinated copolymers, hydrophobizing agents, such as hexamethyldisilazane, and/or inorganic particles, such as silica or hydrophobic silica. The surface comprises a coating made of a material selected from pressure sensitive adhesives, e.g. acrylics, butyl rubber, ethylene vinyl acetate, natural rubber, nitriles; styrene block copolymers with ethylene, propylene, and isoprene; polyurethanes, and polyvinyl ethers. Solid-phase body comprises an open-cell structure made of a material selected from hard plastics, ceramics, carbon fiber, and metals. Solid-phase body may take the form of a sheet, a sphere, a cube, a block, a filter, or a conveyor belt. Preferred Properties: Three-dimensional open cellular structure has a cellular density of 10-200 pores per inch.

The ratio of hydrogen:carbon dioxide provided at the syngas stage inlet is 1-9, preferably 2.5-4.5. The syngas stream has a hydrogen/carbon monoxide ratio of 1-4, preferably 1.5-2.1.

PN CN113634251-A
TI Nano gold catalyst supported on praseodymium-based carrier used for carbon monoxide conversion catalysis in low-temperature water-gas shift reaction or carbon monoxide oxidation catalysis in high-concentration carbon dioxide.
AU SUN X
   SU H
   ZHANG Q
   SUN L
   SHI J
   ZHAO W
   QI C
AE UNIV YANTAI (UNYT-C)
GA 2021D3712C
AB    NOVELTY - Nano gold catalyst supported on praseodymium-based carrier comprises Au/PraTibOx catalyst, a:b=1:4 or 1:2 or 2:1, Au/Pr1Ti4Ox-Air catalyst, Au/Pr1Ti2Ox-Air catalyst, Au/Pr2Ti1Ox-Air catalyst, and Au/Pr6O11-Air catalyst, Au/Pr-TiO2 catalyst, Au/Pr-TiO2 catalyst, Au/PraZrbOx-Air catalyst, a:b=0.001:1 or 0.005:1 or 0.01:1 or 0.05:1 or 0.125:1 or .025:1 or 1:1 or 4:1, Au/PraCebOx-Air catalyst, a:b=0.001:1 or 0.005:1 or 0.01:1 or 0.125:1, Au/PraCeb(OH)x catalyst, a:b=0.25:1 or 0.5:1 or 4:1, and Au/PraLabOx-Air catalyst, a:b=0.001:1 or 0.005:1 or 0.01:1 or 0.25:1.
   USE - Nano gold catalyst supported on praseodymium-based carrier used for carbon monoxide conversion catalysis in low-temperature water-gas shift reaction or carbon monoxide oxidation catalysis in high-concentration carbon dioxide oxygen-poor atmosphere (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The praseodymium-based composite oxide supported nano-gold catalyst has various synthesis methods, uniform catalyst size, and good dispersion effect, and it has excellent catalytic activity and high stability in both the low-temperature water-gas shift reaction and the carbon monoxide oxidation reaction in a simulated carbon dioxide laser containing a high concentration of carbon dioxide in an oxygen-poor atmosphere, and it has a good application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing nano-gold catalyst supported by praseodymium-based carrier, which involves: (a) using theoretical loading of gold is 0.5-1 wt.%;(b)impregnating the praseodymium-based composite oxide support or the praseodymium-based composite hydroxide support with a chloroauric acid solution with a pH of 9-10;(c)soaking by aqueous ammonia, filtering by suction, washing and drying to obtain catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium salt is selected from tetraethyl titanate, tetrabutyl titanate and isopropyl titanate. The praseodymium nitrate is praseodymium nitrate hexahydrate. The praseodymium-based composite oxide is selected from praseodymium-zirconium composite oxide, praseodymium-cerium composite oxide, or praseodymium-lanthanum composite oxide. The zirconium salt is selected form zirconium nitrate, zirconyl nitrate and zirconium chloride. The cerium salt includes cerium nitrate or cerium chloride. The lanthanum salt includes lanthanum chloride or lanthanum nitrate. Preferred Process: The preparation of praseodymium-titanium composite oxide involves dissolving praseodymium nitrate and titanium salt in ethanol, adding concentrated ammonia, stirring at 30-75degrees Celsius to obtain uniform praseodymium titanium sol, obtaining a gel after 20-30 hours reaction, and curing, drying, calcining the obtained gel to obtain the praseodymium-titanium composite oxide, adding the praseodymium nitrate solution to the nano-sized titanium dioxide, drying, and calcining the reaction product to obtain the praseodymium-titanium composite oxide. The preparation of praseodymium-zirconium composite oxide involves weighing a certain mass of praseodymium nitrate hexahydrate and zirconium salt with molar ratio of 0.0005-4, dissolving in a container, adding to the alkaline solution under stirring conditions of 30-75degrees Celsius, stirring, dispersing, and performing hydrothermal synthesis method to obtain the praseodymium-zirconium composite oxide. The preparation of praseodymium-cerium composite oxide involves weighing a certain mass of praseodymium nitrate hexahydrate and cerium salt with molar ratio of 0.0005-4, dissolving in a container, adding to the alkaline solution under stirring at 30-75degrees Celsius, stirring, dispersing, and performing hydrothermal synthesis to obtain praseodymium-cerium composite oxide. The preparation of praseodymium-lanthanum composite oxide involves weighing a certain mass of praseodymium nitrate hexahydrate and lanthanum salt at mass ratio of 0.0005-4, dissolving in a container, adding to the alkaline solution under stirring at 30-75degrees Celsius, stirring, dispersing, and performing hydrothermal synthesis method to obtain praseodymium-lanthanum composite oxide. Preferred Conditions: The obtained catalyst is calcining in a nitrogen, hydrogen, argon, air or oxygen atmosphere at 180-350degrees Celsius for 2-4 hours. The reaction product is calcining at temperature of 300-450degrees Celsius. The molar ratio of the praseodymium nitrate to the ester reagent of titanium is 0.25-4. The volume ratio of titanium salt to ethanol is 100-250. The volume ratio of concentrated aqueous ammonia to ethanol is 0.001-0.005. The mass ratio of praseodymium nitrate hexahydrate to nano-sized titanium dioxide is 0.01-0.2. The reaction temperature of the hydrothermal synthesis method is 100-250degrees Celsius, the reaction time is 18-24 hours. The praseodymium-based composite oxide is obtained after washing, drying and calcination, and the calcination temperature is 300-500degrees Celsius.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN111689494-A
TI Preparing activated carbon by using catalytic activation of non-corrosive catalyst, comprises e.g. crushing activated carbon precursor, mixing grinding, drying and washing using deionized water.
AU ZHANG S
   LI Q
   FAN Z
   WEI T
   ZHU J
   ZHAO J
AE UNIV XINJIANG (UXIJ-C)
GA 202095376S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing activated carbon by using catalytic activation of non-corrosive catalyst, comprises e.g. (i) crushing activated carbon precursor composed of coal, charcoal, petroleum coke or biomass through 200 mesh screen to obtain carbon precursor powder, (ii) taking carbon precursor powder and non-corrosive catalyst in mortar, mixing the carbon precursor powder with distilled water and ethanol dispersion medium, grinding, drying, (iii) placing dried sample in tube furnace, increasing the temperature to activation temperature under the protection of inert gas, entering carbon dioxide activated gas for catalytic activation treatment, and (iv) washing the activated product using dilute hydrochloric acid to remove catalyst, then washing using deionized water to neutral and drying to obtain activated carbon with large specific surface area; and the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 1-10 ml/minute.
   USE - The activated carbon by using catalytic activation of non-corrosive catalyst is useful in chemical synthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing activated carbon by using catalytic activation of non-corrosive catalyst, comprises (i) crushing activated carbon precursor composed of coal, charcoal, petroleum coke or biomass through 200 mesh screen to obtain carbon precursor powder, (ii) taking carbon precursor powder and non-corrosive catalyst in mortar, mixing the carbon precursor powder with distilled water and ethanol dispersion medium in a ratio of 10:1-1:1 g/ml, grinding, drying, (iii) placing dried sample in tube furnace, increasing the temperature to activation temperature under the protection of inert gas, entering carbon dioxide activated gas for catalytic activation treatment, and (iv) washing the activated product using 0.1 mol/l dilute hydrochloric acid in a ratio of 1:2-1:10 g/ml to remove catalyst, then washing using deionized water to neutral and drying to obtain activated carbon with large specific surface area; the inert gas is nitrogen or argon, the flow rate of nitrogen or argon is 5-25 ml/minute, the heating rate is 5-20 degrees C/minute and the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 1-10 ml/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii), the mass ratio betweem carbon precursor powder and catalyst is 1:0.001 to 1:0.5; the activation temperature in step (iii) is 700-1000 degrees C and the activation time is 0.1-2 hours. Preferred Method: The catalyst is prepared by dissolving potassium salt in distilled water in a ratio of 1:20 g/ml, adding surfactant in a ratio of potassium salt and surfactant is 1:0.5 to 1:3, stirring thoroughly to dissolve, then adding transition metal salt with catalytic activity in a ratio of potassium salt and transition metal salt 1:0.1-1:5 to obtain mixed solution; heating the resulting mixed solution to reflux at 100 degrees C for 3-4 hours; then evaporating the mixed solution, placing the obtained powder in oven and heating at 150 degrees C for 2 hours and grinding to obtain non-corrosive catalyst. The potassium salt used to prepare the catalyst is potassium chloride, potassium carbonate and/or potassium bicarbonate; surfactant is surfactant which forms strong coordination effect with potassium ions and metal ions, composed of anionic surfactant sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, sodium dodecyl sulfate, non-ionic surfactants polyethylene glycol, polyvinyl alcohol, polyoxyethylene; the transition metal salt is iron(III) chloride, cobalt(II) chloride, nickel(II) chloride, iron(III) sulfate, cobalt(II) sulfate and/or nickel(II) sulfate.

GA 202090746G
AB    NOVELTY - The box has a solenoid valve provided in a transfer pipe (400) to block transfer of carbon dioxide. The solenoid valve is interlocked with a fire sensor (10). An injection pipe is installed horizontally on an inner upper part of an opening (500) in a form crossing an interior of an enclosure (100). An end of the injection pipe is connected to a through hole formed in the enclosure to receive carbon dioxide transferred to the enclosure. A spray nozzle is mounted along an outer diameter surface of a spray pipe to spray carbon dioxide supplied to the spray pipe into an interior receiving space of the enclosure. A communication module wirelessly connects the fire sensor and a solenoid valve to wirelessly transmit a fire signal detected through the fire sensor to the solenoid valve. A hollow air pack is filled with a powder fire extinguishing agent in the receiving space, where a flame retardant film wound around the injection pipe is unwound from the injection pipe to shield the opening.
   USE - Internet-of-things based communication terminal box for a building.
   ADVANTAGE - The box ensures that carbon dioxide is sprayed into the interior of the enclosure installed in the interior wall of the building when the fire sensor detects a fire in the building, thus preventing inflow of external flames into the interior of the enclosure and the flame retardant film is unwound from the injection pipe installed in the opening of the enclosure to shield the opening when the fire occurs in the building, thus preventing inflow of flames into the interior of the enclosure to protect the communication equipment contained inside the enclosure from fire.
