TI Polyvinyl chloride supercritical foam composite material involves polyvinly chloride, maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene, maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene modifier, carboxylated double-walled carbon nanotubes, graphene oxide.
AU DING Y
   YANG W
   DING X
   LIN Q
   LV F
AE FUJIAN ANDAFU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202058075T
AB    NOVELTY - Polyvinyl chloride supercritical foam composite material involves 80-88 pts. wt. polyvinly chloride, 7-10 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene, 16-19 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene modifier, 3-4 pts. wt. carboxylated double-walled carbon nanotubes, 2.5-3 pts. wt. graphene oxide, 5-8 pts. wt. nano silica, 2-3 pts. wt. dispersant, 1.5-2 pts. wt. crosslinking agent, 2-2.5 pts. wt. light stabilizer, and 3.5-4.5 pts. wt. antioxidants.
   USE - Polyvinyl chloride supercritical foam composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyvinyl chloride supercritical foam composite material has good mechanical property, low compression permanent deformation rate, excellent resilience, high elasticity, high cell density, small cell size (diameter), uniform foam, and good foaming effect, and improves the tensile strength of the polyvinylchloride supercritical foaming composite material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a polyvinyl chloride supercritical foam composite material, which involves:
   (A) weighing each raw material separately;
   (B) mixing polyvinyl chloride, maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene, maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene modifier, carboxylated double-walled carbon nanotubes, graphene oxide, nano-silica, dispersing agent and resistance, oxygen agent, transferring to the internal mixer, and the internal temperature is 115-125 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, adding the crosslinking agent and light stabilizer at the temperature of 122-126 degrees C, mixing and blending components to obtain a blending blend;
   (C) using the tableting machine to compress the dense blend into a plate and cutting;
   (D) placing the board into an oven, preheat to 125-135 degrees C; and
   (E) placing the preheated foamed mother board into a high-temperature mold to pass carbon dioxide gas when the carbon dioxide gas reaches saturation in the sheet, releasing carbon dioxide gas in the mold is quickly to rapidly foam the sheet to obtain a polyvinyl chloride supercritical foam composite material, and the temperature of the high-temperature mold is 132-138 degrees C, and the pressure of the carbon dioxide gas in the mold is 14-16 megaPascal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polyvinyl chloride supercritical foam composite material involves 84 pts. wt. polyvinly chloride, 8.5 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene, 17.5 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene modifier, 3.5 pts. wt. carboxylated double-walled carbon nanotubes, 2.8 pts. wt. graphene oxide, 6.5 pts. wt. nano silica, 2.5 pts. wt. dispersant, 1.8 pts. wt. crosslinking agent, 2.3 pts. wt. light stabilizer, and 4 pts. wt. antioxidants. The maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene modifier is a ring-containing compound with oxypolysiloxane and maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene in a mass ratio of 1:(2.2-2.8), and prepared by grafting polyethylene-containing silicon group copolymer. Preferred Conditions: The diameter of the carboxylated double-walled carbon nanotube is 2-4 nanometer, and the length is 25-45 micrometer, and the carboxylated double carboxyl group content of the wall carbon nanotubes is 2.55-2.65 wt.%. Preferred Components: The dispersant is a dispersant HT-5020. The crosslinking agent is an organic peroxide crosslinking agent and/or an inorganic peroxide crosslinking agent. The organic peroxide crosslinking agent is t-butyl hydroperoxide, dicumyl peroxide and benzene peroxide, formyl, and the inorganic peroxide crosslinking agent is zinc peroxide and tin peroxide. The light stabilizer is benzotriazole and/or benzophenone. The antioxidant is a mixture of antioxidant 1171 and antioxidant 168 where the mass ratio of antioxidant 1171 and antioxidant in the mixture of the agent 168 is 1:(0.35-0.45).

TI Processing alumina ceramic useful for processing carbon-dioxide laser guided fiber, comprises e.g. pre-cutting on the alumina ceramic with carbon-dioxide laser, pre-cutting, and post-cutting by fiber laser.
AU SUN Q
   HE X
   WANG B
   DAI H
AE FERROTEC CN CORP (FRRF-C)
   JIANGSU FULEDE SEMICONDUCTOR TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201923133U
AB    NOVELTY - Processing alumina ceramic comprises (i) pre-cutting on the alumina ceramic with carbon-dioxide laser to form pre-cut seam on the alumina ceramic, and (ii) pre-cutting, after the holes and black marks on the surface of the alumina ceramic, post-cutting by fiber laser, when post-cutting, the fiber laser is repeatedly cut along the pre-cut seam.
   USE - The alumina ceramic is useful for processing carbon-dioxide laser guided fiber (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The alumina ceramic: reduces thermal stress and jumper defect of material during cutting, reduces the crack of the ceramic plate, poor fracture and damage at the time of cutting, and improves the product yield.

The fiber laser has depth of 30-60% the thickness of the alumina ceramic. The ratio between laser output power and total power of the carbon-dioxide laser is 7-12%. The ratio between laser output power and total power of the fiber laser is 7-12%. The alumina ceramic is ceramic plate in direct bond copper-clad ceramic substrate.

AU HSU Y
AE UNIV KUN SHAN (UNKS-C)
GA 201911328T
AB    NOVELTY - A member quantity control system and member quantity control method are used for dynamically adjusting the member number of indoor space limits and show the number. This is to avoid the concentration of carbon dioxide exceeding the safety threshold. The member quantity control system comprises a processor, a flow calculation module, and a carbon dioxide concentration detector. The flow calculation module is used to calculate the member number of indoor space and displayed. When the processor determines that the concentration of carbon dioxide detected by the carbon dioxide concentration detector exceeds the safety threshold, the processor will reduce the member quantity. Wherein, the member number of new restrictions should be lower than the member number in the indoor space.

   CHENG H
   GAO X
   CHU C
   DAI Q
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 202191472W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing long-acting low-toxic anthelmintic                ointment comprises (i) adding anthelmintic medicine                and solid medical Vaseline (RTM: Petroleum jelly)                into reaction kettle, (ii) pumping carbon dioxide                into the reaction kettle by high pressure plunger                pump, controlling pressure at 15-20 MPa, (iii)                controlling the temperature at 35-40 degrees C                using metal bath around the reaction kettle, and                (iv) magnetically stirring for 1-2 hours,                depressurize through the back pressure valve, and                collecting the prepared insect-expelling                medicine-Vaseline (RTM: Petroleum jelly)                ointment.
   USE - The method is useful for long-acting low-toxic                anthelmintic ointment used for skin.
   ADVANTAGE - The ointment: can be stored for long time;                ensures medicine will not be aggregated and                separated out; forms layer of shielding environment                on the skin surface so as to reduce the insect                repellent medicine permeable skin absorption; and                reduces toxic risk of long-term exposure in                vivo.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1)                long-acting low-toxic anthelmintic ointment,                comprising 10-20 wt.% anthelmintic drug stably                dispersed in medical Vaseline (RTM: Petroleum                jelly) in the form of microparticles, the medical                Vaseline (RTM: Petroleum jelly) is sealed and                wrapped on the outer periphery of the anthelmintic                drug particles; (2) use of supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid in preparation of long-acting and                low-toxic anthelmintic ointment, comprising                pressurizing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid to                realize the dispersion of the anthelmintic drug                into primary particles, and to achieve the                dispersion of the primary particles of the                anthelmintic drug into the medical Vaseline (RTM:                Petroleum jelly) during the depressurization                process, the medical Vaseline (RTM: Petroleum                jelly) is sealed and wrapped around the outer                periphery of the anthelmintic drug particles; and                (3) use of long-acting low-toxic anthelmintic                ointment for skin, comprising applying ointment                directly to the skin surface, when the content of                mosquito repellent is 10 wt.%, the average                effective repelling time is 9.125 hours.

   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has highly stable; and catalyzes the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce ethanol with the selectivity of greater than 90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) carrying out the reduction pretreatment of the cerium dioxide carrier at 250-700 degrees C under pure hydrogen or mixed gas containing hydrogen for 0.5-10 hours, (ii) dispersing the pretreated cerium dioxide carrier obtained in the step (i) in a solvent, ultrasonically processing for 1-10 hours, adding the precursor aqueous solution of the active component under the action of ultrasound, further stirring the suspension liquid for 5-20 hours, separating solid-liquid to obtain solids, washing with water and ethanol, drying, and roasting at 200-300 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours, and (iii) dispersing the solid powder obtained in the step (ii) in a solvent, ultrasonically processing for 0.5-5 hours, adding the precursor of the structure auxiliary agent under the action of ultrasound, stirring for 0.5-5 hours, rotary evaporating the solid-liquid mixture, drying, and finally roasting at 200-600 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours, where the when the content of the structural auxiliary agent is 0, the step (iii) is omitting; and
   (2) producing ethanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprising using the catalyst as a hydrogenation catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises 0.01-2% active component, 0-10% structural auxiliary agent, and 88-99.99% carrier. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mixed gas comprises hydrogen/carbon monoxide, hydrogen/carbon dioxide, hydrogen/nitrogen, hydrogen/argon, and/or hydrogen/helium. In the step (ii) or step (iii), the solvent comprises water, methanol, ethanol, and/or glycol. In the step (ii) the precursor of the active component comprises acetylacetonate, acetate, citrate, nitrate, carbonate, chloride, chlorate, and/or sodium salt containing the active component. The precursor of the structural auxiliary agent comprises an organic salt or/and an inorganic salt containing the structural auxiliary agent. Preferred Method: The reaction conditions for the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce ethanol comprises carbon dioxide/hydrogen=1:1-8, space velocity is 1-10 l/gcat/hour, reaction temperature is 200-320 degrees C and reaction pressure is 0.5-10 MPa. The method comprises pre-treating the catalyst before use at 200-600 degrees C under 0.1-2 MPa in the atmosphere of hydrogen, hydrogen/carbon monoxide, hydrogen/nitrogen, hydrogen/helium, or hydrogen/argon with the space velocity of 2-10 l/gcat/hour. A reactor for producing ethanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide comprises fixed bed, a slurry bed or a kettle-type reactor.

PN CN113002454-A; CN113002454-B
TI Method for alarming on-board diagnostics (OBD)            fault, involves calculating pollutant emission value of            each window, and selectively controlling vehicle to            carry out OBD fault alarm according to pollutant            emission value of each window.
AU QIAN K
   CHEN C
   DAI G
AE ZHEJIANG GEELY HOLDING GROUP CO LTD (GEEL-C)
   ZHEJIANG GEELY NEW ENERGY COMML VEHICLE (GEEL-C)
   GEELY SICHUAN COMML VEHICLE CO LTD (GEEL-C)
   JIANGXI GEELY NEW ENERGY COMML VEHICLE (GEEL-C)
   ZHEJIANG YUANCHENG COMML VEHICLE RES (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202175861H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves continuously obtaining                (S100) a carbon dioxide content and a pollutant                content emitted by a vehicle when the vehicle meets                preset emission monitoring conditions. The carbon                dioxide content is accumulatively calculated (S200)                in multiple adjacent time periods, an accumulative                calculation is ended until an accumulative value                obtained by the accumulative calculation reaches a                preset accumulative value, an accumulative time                period is recorded as a window, and a starting time                period of next window is set as second time period                of previous window. A pollutant emission value of                each window is calculated (S300) according to the                accumulative value of the pollutant content in each                window and a running parameter information of the                vehicle. The vehicle is selectively controlled                (S400) to carry out an OBD fault alarm according to                the pollutant emission value of each window.
   USE - Method for alarming on-board diagnostics (OBD)                fault of vehicle (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system for alarming the OBD fault adopts                the method of moving the window to calculate the                emission value of the vehicle, accurately judging                whether to carry out OBD fault alarm or not in the                actual running process of the vehicle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for alarming on-board diagnostics fault of                vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flowchart illustrating                a method for alarming the OBD fault of the vehicle.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for continuously obtaining the carbon                dioxide content and the pollutant content emitted                by the vehicle when the vehicle meets preset                emission monitoring conditions (S100)
   Step for accumulatively calculating the                carbon dioxide content in multiple adjacent time                periods (S200)
   Step for calculating the pollutant emission                value of each window according to the accumulative                value of the pollutant content in each window and                the running parameter information of the vehicle                (S300)
   Step for selectively controlling the vehicle                to carry out the OBD fault alarm according to the                pollutant emission value of each window                (S400)

TI Combined cooling, heating and power system based            on supercritical carbon dioxide cycle and trough solar            absorption refrigeration, has generator that returns to            solar heat collector heat exchanger, and heat user that            form small loop.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a supercritical carbon dioxide                Brayton cycle system comprises a tubular combustion                furnace (7), a high pressure turbine (6), a                re-heater (4), a flue gas regenerator (14), a                low-pressure turbine (16), a second generator (15),                a third generator (21), a steam regenerator (17), a                pre-cooler (18) and a compressor (20). The groove                type solar heat collecting system comprises a                groove type solar heat collector (1), a heat                storage tank (2), a heat exchanger (3) and a heat                user (5). The pipeline from the groove type solar                heat collector is divided into branches that are                connected in parallel. The first branch passes                through heat storage tank and returns to groove                type solar heat collector. The second branch                returns to solar heat collector through the heat                exchanger. The third branch passes through the                exhaust steam re-heater and tubular combustion                furnace. A first generator (9) returns to solar                heat collector. The heat exchanger and the heat                user form a small loop independently.

   ADVANTAGE - The system controls on-off of a pipeline valve                and flow size of gas fuel so as to realize perfect                coupling of three subsystems, thus, ensuring stable                operation of the system and improving energy                utilization rate.

PN CN110898849-A
TI Catalytically converting carbon dioxide into            polyhydric alcohol and polycarbon involves hydrolyzing            alkyl group formed on surface to produce multi-carbon            alcohol under action of catalyst.
AU WANG Y
   YU Y
AE UNIV PEKING (UYPK-C)
GA 202024245E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for catalytically converting carbon                dioxide into polyhydric alcohol and polycarbon                involves hydrolyzing the alkyl group formed on the                surface to produce multi-carbon alcohol under the                action of the catalyst.
   USE - Method for catalytically converting carbon                dioxide into polyhydric alcohol and polycarbon, for                preparing catalyst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables the catalytic conversion of                carbon dioxide to form multi-carbon alcohol at very                low temperature, and surface of the catalytic                system to have sufficient active sites to activate                hydrogen even at low temperatures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the catalyst                involving (i) dissolving the soluble salt or acid                of the transition metal in the alcohol or                alcohol-water mixture, preparing a solution with a                metal concentration of 0.1-50 g/l, and mixing the                resulting solution with an alcohol solution or                aqueous alcohol solution of an alkali metal                hydroxide, heating the resulting mixed liquid at                343-533K to prepare a metal nano-cluster colloid                solution, (ii) adding hydrochloric acid to the                metal nano-cluster colloid solution obtained in                step (i) to precipitate the metal nano-cluster,                separating and dispersing the precipitate in                acetone to prepare a metal nano-cluster acetone                colloid solution, dispersing the metal oxide in                organic solvent to obtain a mixture (A), mixing the                above colloidal solution with mixture (A), stirring                the mixture, separating the solids in the mixture,                washing, and drying to obtain metal                nanocluster/metal oxide composite, and (iii)                dispersing the transition metal nanoclusters/metal                oxide composite obtained in step (ii) in water,                reacting the basic compound with a transition metal                compound to generate transition metal compound                nanoparticles to be deposited on the transition                metal nanocluster/metal oxide composite, treating                the mixture with hydrogen at 80-400 degrees C,                washing and drying the obtained solid particles to                obtain the product; and
   (2) a catalyst is prepared using the                above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The conversion is                  carried out at 25-130 degrees C, preferably 40-60                  degrees C. The pressure of hydrogen in the                  hydrogen treatment is 0.1-10 MPa. Preferred                  Method: The method involves carrying out                  conversion in water or water-containing systems.                  The general formula of the multi-carbon alcohol                  is CnH2n+1OH, where n is an integer of greater                  than or equal to 3. The general formula of                  multi-carbon hydrocarbon is CnH2n+2, where n is                  an integer of greater than or equal to -2.                  Preferred Composition: The size of the metal                  nanoclusters is 0.5-5 nm. Preferred Components:                  The catalyst comprises metal oxide-supported                  transition metal nanoclusters, bimetallic                  nanoclusters or multimetal nanoclusters. The                  metal nanoclusters comprise platinum, ruthenium ,                  rhodium, iridium, palladium, gold, and/or alloy                  nanoclusters, preferably platinum, ruthenium and                  alloy nanoclusters. The basic compound comprises                  alkali metal carbonate, bicarbonate, sodium                  carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide,                  potassium hydroxide, potassium bicarbonate and/or                  potassium carbonate. In step (iii), the                  transition metal compound comprises ruthenium                  compound, and the generated transition metal                  compound nanoparticles are ruthenium compound                  nanoparticles. The metal oxide comprises iron                  oxide or composite formed with other metal                  oxides. The iron oxide comprises iron-oxygen of                  formula: FexOy.x = less than or equal to 1;andy = less than or equal to 1.5.

AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of cancer, reducing inflammation,                hyperproliferative disorder, suppressing expression                of Nuclear factor-kappaB and cyclooxygenase,                modifying epidermal growth factor receptor                signaling activity, or inducing apoptosis of cell                comprises identifying a subject in need of                treatment; and administering a composition                containing supercritical carbon dioxide neem                extract. The supercritical carbon dioxide neem                extract comprises nimbolide, nimbin and                salinin.
   USE - The method is used for treating cancer,                reducing inflammation, hyperproliferative disorder,                suppressing expression of Nuclear factor-kappaB and                cyclooxygenase, modifying epidermal growth factor                receptor signaling activity, or inducing apoptosis                of cell (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (A) a                method for preparing standardized carbon dioxide                (CO2) extract of Azadirachta indica leaves                comprising powder the clean and matured dried                Azadirachta indica leaves having moisture content                less than 12% to obtain powder with fine particles                having size below 0.42 mm; subjecting the powder to                supercritical CO2 extraction at 80-350 Bar, 31-45                degrees C and 10-40 kg CO2 per kg raw material;                separating the CO2 extractives at 40-65 Bar at a                temperature lower than the extraction temperature                to obtain Extract A; subjecting the remaining                residual powder after separating Extract A to                further extraction using mixture of CO2 and ethanol                at 80-350 Bar and 31-45 degrees C; collecting the                ethanol laced with extract from separator by                reducing the solvent pressure at 40-65 Bar at a                temperature lower than the extraction temperature,                followed by vacuum distillation of ethanol to                obtain Extract B; and combining Extract A and                Extract B to obtain standardized CO2 extract of                Azadirachta indica leaves; (B) a standardized CO2                extract of Azadirachta indica comprises 3 mg/g                nimbolide, 130 mu g/g nimbin, and 200 mu g/g                salinin; and (C) a therapeutic herbal composition                comprising 50-400 mg standardized CO2 extract of                Azadirachta indica along with                carrier(s)/excipient(s). The composition can be                formulated into oral solid or liquid dosage                forms.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The supercritical                  carbon dioxide neem extract (SCNE) comprises                  liminoids. The composition further comprises                  tocopherols and/or sesame oil. The composition                  further comprises tocopherols, sesame oil, and/or                  aerated or fumed silica. The                  ethanol is used at 3-10% of the CO2. The extract                  further contains active phytoconstituents such as                  desacetylnimbin, azadiradione, azadirone,                  nimbolin or nimbinene in minor amounts.

   ADMINISTRATION - Dosage is 50-1000 mg/day, and administered                orally (all claimed).

PT P
PN CN110694631-A
TI Catalyst useful in directed synthesis of methanol by carbon dioxide hydrogenation, comprises copper, zinc, zirconium, aluminum, cerium and lanthanum.
AU TAN Y
   CHEN S
   XIE H
   ZHANG J
   PAN J
   SONG F
   WANG X
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202008501E
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises 0.3-0.6 atomic percentage copper, 0.3-0.6 atomic percentage zinc, 0.01-0.1 atomic percentage zirconium, 0.01-0.1 atomic percentage aluminum, 0.01-0.1 atomic percentage cerium, and 0.01-0.1 atomic percentage lanthanum.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in directed synthesis of methanol by carbon dioxide hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to operate and adopts mild reaction conditions. The catalyst has special active site, when it is used to produce methanol from hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, inhibits formation of carbon monoxide and improves selectivity of methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the catalyst, involving (a) mixing the nitrate of copper, zinc, zirconium, aluminum, cerium and lanthanum to obtain 0.1-1 M solution as mixed solution (A), mixing sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate and/or potassium bicarbonate to obtain 0.1-1 M solution as mixed solution (B), titrating mixed solution (A) and mixed solution (B) at 60-80 degrees C and stirring at a speed of 300-500 rpm/minute, precipitating and maintaining the pH of the precipitation liquid to 6-10, aging at the same precipitation temperature and stirring for 1-3 hours, carrying out suction filtration, washing the obtained filter cake with distilled water for 4-10 times, drying in an oven at a temperature of 90-130 degrees C for 10-20 hours, roasting at 300-450 degrees C for 6-10 hours and grinding to obtain an initial catalyst, and (b) using ethanol as a solvent, completely dissolving 0.5-1 g/ml impregnation reagent, adding 0.5-1.5 g/ml initial catalyst into the impregnation reagent, performing ultrasonic treatment in an ultrasonic cleaner for 2-12 hours, drying in an oven and crushing to obtain the catalyst with the particle size of 20-40 mesh; and
   (2) an application of the catalyst in directed synthesis of methanol by carbon dioxide hydrogenation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The impregnation reagent comprises sodium bromide, potassium bromide, tetrapropylammonium bromide and/or tetraethylammonium bromide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The drying in step (b) is carried out in the oven at a temperature of 90-130 degrees C for 10-20 hours. The roasting in step (b) is carried out at a temperature of 400-550 degrees C for 6-10 hours. The application of the catalyst is carried out by (i) using hydrogen with a volume content of 3-10% and argon mixed gas as reducing gas and reducing the catalyst at a gas space velocity of 50-800 hour-1, a reducing pressure of 0.1-0.8 MPa and a reducing temperature of 260-380 degrees C for 10-20 hours, and (ii) passing the reduced catalyst through a feed gas with a hydrogen/carbon dioxide mol ratio of 0.8-3 for reaction, and synthesizing methanol at a pressure of 3-8 MPa, at a temperature of 190-300 degrees C for 1000-10000 hour-1.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN110594111-A; CN210919356-U
TI Coupling system for solar transcritical carbon            dioxide Rankine cycle and compressed air energy            storage, has heater, condenser, liquid storage tank,            and working medium pump which are connected to form            circuit.
AU WEI M
   MENG J
   SONG P
AE BEIJING INST TECHNOLOGY (BEIT-C)
GA 2019A7966B
AB    NOVELTY - The coupling system has an oil pump (1), a                heat collector (2), an evaporator (3), a fuel tank                (4), a regenerator (6), a condenser (7), a liquid                storage tank (8), a working medium pump (9), an                expander (5), a compressor (10) and a gas storage                chamber (11). The heat collector is connected to                the oil inlet of the evaporator through a pipeline,                and the oil outlet of the evaporator is connected                to the oil tank. The heat exchanger, evaporator and                fuel tank are connected by pipelines in order to                form a loop. The outlet of the working fluid of the                evaporator is connected to the inlet of the                expander through the pipeline. The working fluid                inlet of the evaporator is connected to the                regenerator through the pipeline. The heater,                condenser, liquid storage tank, and working medium                pump are connected to form a circuit. The expander                and the compressor are connected coaxially, and the                compressed air is stored in the air storage                chamber.
   USE - Coupling system for solar transcritical carbon                dioxide Rankine cycle and compressed air energy                storage.
   ADVANTAGE - The coupling system is environmentally                friendly, stable, cheap, and allows a low critical                temperature carbon dioxide as the working medium of                the Rankine cycle system. The carbon dioxide                working medium and the heat source are matched                well, and there is no isothermal evaporation, thus                improving the thermal efficiency of the                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for stabilizing solar photovoltaic thermal power                conversion by the coupling system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural                diagram of a coupling system. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Oil pump (1)
   Heat collector (2)
   Evaporator (3)
   Fuel tank (4)
   Expander (5)
   Regenerator (6)
   Condenser (7)
   Liquid storage tank (8)
   Working medium pump (9)
   Compressor (10)
   Gas storage chamber (11)
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P)); Q54 (Non-positive displacement fluid engines (i.e. driven by fluid); Miscellaneous motors and machines for producing mechanical power/thrust (F03B,D,G,H)); Q55 (Positive displacement fluid machines/pumps/compressors (i.e. for driving fluid) (F04B,C)); X15 (Non-Fossil Fuel Power Generating Systems)
MC Q51-C; Q54-H; Q55-A; X15-A
IP F01B-023/08; F01K-025/10; F03G-006/06; F04B-041/02

PT P
PN CN107988304-A
TI Producing astaxanthin using red yeast by grinding and crushing shrimp waste, enzymolyzing using complex protease and papain, culturing yeast in medium, performing supercritical extraction and purifying crude astaxanthin extract.
AU LUO S
AE LUO S (LUOS-Individual)
GA 201836624R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing astaxanthin using red yeast, involves (a) grinding and crushing shrimp waste, boiling and cooling crushed product, adding protease and papain for enzymolysis, adding straw cellulose dry matter, ammonium sulfate and molasses to the enzymolyzed solution, mixing uniformly and performing high-pressure steam sterilization, and (b) inoculating the red yeast into an activation medium and culturing the red yeast in a shaker, (c) inoculating the activated red yeast into an expansion medium and culturing under stirring conditions, mixing with the fermented substrate and introducing sterile oxygen, introducing mixture of sterile carbon dioxide, oxygen and ethylene, ventilating and culturing the culture and (d) adding the red yeast with higher astaxanthin content from step (c) into an extraction kettle and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction using methanol as and purifying crude astaxanthin extract.
   USE - The method is useful for producing astaxanthin using red yeast.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing astaxanthin using red yeast, involves (a) grinding and crushing shrimp waste, boiling and cooling, adding protease and papain for enzymolysis, adding straw cellulose dry matter, ammonium sulfate and molasses to the enzymolyzed solution, mixing uniformly and performing high-pressure steam sterilization and (b) inoculating the red yeast into an activation medium and culturing the red yeast in a shaker, (c) inoculating the activated red yeast into an expansion medium and cultivating the mixture under stirring conditions for 45-50 hours, mixing with the fermented substrate and introducing sterile oxygen continuously for 45-50 hours, introducing mixture of sterile carbon dioxide, oxygen and ethylene in a volume ratio of 1-2:(0.5-1):(0.01-0.03), ventilating at 0.2-0.5 volume/volume/minute and culturing the culture for 45-50 hours and (d) placing the red yeast with higher astaxanthin content from step (c) in an extraction kettle and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction using methanol and purifying the crude astaxanthin extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the particle size of crushed product is greater than or equal to 30 meshes. The mass ratio of protease and papain is (3-4):1. The enzymolysis is performed at 45-50 degrees C and pH of 607 for 60-80 minutes. The mass ratio of enzymolyzed solution, straw cellulose dry matter, ammonium sulfate and molasses is (1-2):(1-2):(0.2-0.4):(1-2). The length of straw cellulose dry matter length is 1-20 mm. In step (b), the activation medium comprises 8-10 g/l glucose, 2-3 g/l yeast extract, 4-5 g/l peptone, 2-3 g/l wort and the pH is 5.5-6.5. In step (c), the expansion medium is prepared by dissolving 0.01-0.05 g cinnamaldehyde, 3-4 g molasses, 4-5 g glucose, 2-3 g yeast extract, 4-5 g peptone and 2-3g wort in 1 l water and adjusting the pH 4.5-5.5. In step (d), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction temperature is 41-43 degrees C, pressure is 38-42 MPa and the extraction time is 30-50 min.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E15 (Alicyclics.)
MC D05-A02C; D05-C18; E10-E04C2; E11-M

PT P
PN WO2018046269-A1; CA3035803-A1; AU2017324138-A1; BR112019004490-A2; CN109922841-A; IN201917011152-A; EP3509657-A1; US2019216980-A1; JP2019528870-W; RU2019109723-A; RU2753283-C2; AU2017324138-B2; US11318229-B2; CN109922841-B; JP7133545-B2; BR112019004490-B1
TI Manufacture of implant used in medical            applications, involves using microstructured            particle-containing composite powder obtained by            connecting polymer-based large particles with spherical            precipitated calcium carbonate small particles.
AU REINAUER F
   LUGER S
   VUCAK M
AE LEIBINGER MEDIZINTECHNIK KARL (KRLL-C)
   LEIBINGER MEDIZINTECHNIK KARL (KRLL-C)
GA 201821649C
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of implant involves using a                microstructured particle-containing composite                powder obtained by connecting large particles with                small particles. The large particles contain a                polymer(s) and have average particle diameter of                0.1 mu m to 10 mm. The small particles comprise                spherical precipitated calcium carbonate particles                having mean diameter of 0.05-50 mu m, and obtained                by supplying carbon dioxide or a carbon                dioxide-containing gas mixture into a calcium                hydroxide suspension, adding 0.3-0.7 wt.%                aminotris(alkylenephosphonic acid), and separating                calcium carbonate particles.
   USE - Manufacture of implant used in neurosurgery,                oral surgery, jaw surgery, maxillofacial surgery,                neck surgery, ear, nose and throat surgery, and                hand, foot, thorax, rib and shoulder surgery                applications (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient manufacture of                implant with excellent surface finish                characteristics, density of 95% or more                (preferably, 97% or more), contraction                characteristics, dimensional stability, pH                stability, biocompatibility, osteoconductivity,                resorbable property, biodegradability, flexural                strength of more than 60 MPa (preferably, more than                70 MPa), and elastic modulus of more than 3120                N/mm2 (preferably, more than 4500 N/mm2), low                porosity, and suppressed generation of                defects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of implant involves using a                microstructured particle-containing composite                powder obtained by connecting large particles with                small particles. The large particles contain a                polymer(s) and have average particle diameter of                0.1 mu m to 10 mm. The small particles are arranged                on the surface of the large particles and/or                distributed homogeneously within the large                particles. The small particles comprise spherical                precipitated calcium carbonate particles having                mean diameter of 0.05-50 mu m. The spherical                calcium carbonate particles are obtained by                supplying carbon dioxide or a carbon                dioxide-containing gas mixture into a calcium                hydroxide suspension, adding 0.3-0.7 wt.%                aminotris(alkylenephosphonic acid), and separating                calcium carbonate particles. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is included for usage of the spherical calcium                carbonate particles as an additive in resorbable                polymer for producing implant in medical                applications.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The introduction of the                  carbon dioxide or the carbon dioxide-containing                  gas mixture is performed until the reaction                  mixture has a pH of less than 9 at less than 25                  degrees C. The spherical calcium carbonate                  particles contain particles having a mean                  diameter of more than 3.0 mu m and/or particles                  having a mean diameter of less than 30.0 mu m                  (preferably, 20 mu m). Preferred Property: The                  ratio of the minimum particle diameter and the                  maximum particle diameter of the spherical                  calcium carbonate particles is more than                  0.90.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The                  aminotris(alkylenephosphonic acid) is chosen from                  aminotris(methylenephosphonic acid),                  aminotris(ethylenephosphonic acid),                  aminotris(propylenephosphonic acid), and                  aminotris(butylenephosphonic acid).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The large particles                  contain a thermoplastic polymer or a resorbable                  polymer having inherent viscosity of 0.3-8 dl/g                  measured using a solution containing chloroform                  and 0.1% polymer at 25 degrees C, preferably                  poly-D-lactic acid and/or poly-L-lactic acid. The                  composite powder contains 40-80 wt.%                  poly-L-lactic acid and 20-60 wt.% precipitated                  calcium carbonate particles.

PT P
PN CN114686909-A
TI Promoting microbial electrocatalysis of carbon            dioxide to synthesize acetic acid, by assembling            three-chamber reactor, separating cathode and            extraction compartments, adding pure carbon dioxide,            and monitoring reaction.
AU ZHANG R
   LIU G
   LIU S
   LUO H
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 202290297A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for promoting microbial                electrocatalysis of carbon dioxide to synthesize                acetic acid by membrane extraction involves                assembling a three-chamber reactor, separating the                cathode compartment and the extraction compartment                by an ion exchange membrane, separating the                extraction compartment and the anode compartment by                a cation exchange membrane, adding cultured                bacterial liquid into a 50 ml centrifuge tube for                centrifugation, adding the supernatant, measuring                catholyte and mixing with the precipitate in the                centrifuge tube, adding into the cathode chamber to                complete the cathode chamber, injecting the                prepared solution matrix into the extraction                chamber and the anode chamber, adding pure carbon                dioxide gas into the cathode chamber, measuring the                pH of the cathode compartment, flowing                nitrogen/carbon dioxide gas for 5 minutes, applying                a voltage, placing in a biochemical incubator, and                monitoring the reaction in real time power                generation of the device.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting microbial                electrocatalysis of carbon dioxide to synthesize                acetic acid, and extracting and recovering acetic                acid from catholyte.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for promoting microbial                electrocatalysis of carbon dioxide to synthesize                acetic acid by membrane extraction involves                assembling a three-chamber reactor, separating the                cathode compartment and the extraction compartment                by an ion exchange membrane, where an anion                exchange membrane is used in the experimental                group, and a cation exchange membrane is used in                the control group, separating the extraction                compartment and the anode compartment by a cation                exchange membrane, adding cultured bacterial liquid                into a 50 ml centrifuge tube for centrifugation at                6000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5 minutes,                adding the supernatant, measuring 40 ml catholyte                and mixing with the precipitate in the centrifuge                tube, adding into the cathode chamber to complete                the cathode chamber, injecting the prepared                solution matrix into the extraction chamber and the                anode chamber correspondingly, where the volume of                the injected solution is 15 ml to complete the                construction of the three-chamber reactor, adding                pure carbon dioxide gas into the cathode chamber                for 5 minutes to provide sufficient carbon source                for acetogenic bacteria, measuring the pH of the                cathode compartment, flowing nitrogen/carbon                dioxide gas for 5 minutes to remove dissolved                oxygen to prevent reactor from producing 2 ml                acetylene is introduced into the air bag and                communicated with the cathode chamber, applying a                voltage of 2.4 V to the reactor, placing in a                biochemical incubator at 32&#176;C for                cultivation, and monitoring the reaction in real                time power generation of the device using                multi-channel voltage data collector.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device                for promoting microbial electrocatalysis of carbon                dioxide to synthesize acetic acid by membrane                extraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  adopting cathode with carbon felt electrode                  (diameter&#215;thickness=3 cm&#215;3 mm),                  and adopting anode with customized                  iridium-tantalum-coated titanium plate                  (length&#215;width&#215;thickness=6                  cm&#215;6 cm&#215;0.5 cm), where the                  distance between the two electrodes is 5.5 cm,                  the cylindrical glass tube of the cathode chamber                  is connected to a 5 ml air bag, the applied                  voltage is 2.4 V, the cathode is connected with                  external resistance of 10 &#937;, and                  collecting the voltage generated by the reactor                  in real time using multi-channel voltage data                  collector. The concrete flow process of cathode                  material carbon felt pretreatment method involves                  soaking material in 1 mol/l sodium hydroxide                  solution, placing on an electric heater to heat                  and boil for 30 minutes, after soaking with                  deionized water, soaking in 1 mol/l hydrochloric                  acid solution, placing in an electric heater,                  heating on the heater for 30 minutes, after                  soaking with deionized water, soaking in                  deionized water, placing on an electric heater to                  heat for 30 minutes, and wrapping with titanium                  wire. The inorganic salt composition of                  extraction chamber solution is identical with                  cathode chamber (without trace element solution                  and vitamin solution), the salt concentration is                  four times that of the catholyte. In addition,                  carbamazepine and ciprofloxacin (both 40 mg/l)                  are added to inhibit the growth of heterotrophic                  microorganisms. Finally, phosphoric acid is added                  dropwise to adjust the pH to 2. The substrate is                  a 7.2 g/l sodium sulfate solution, and the pH is                  adjusted to 2 by dropwise addition of sulfuric                  acid to ensure sufficient initial proton to                  transfer across the cation exchange membrane. The                  microorganism inoculated in the cathode chamber                  is acetogenic mixed bacteria. The initial control                  conditions are 25-35&#176;C and pH 7.Preferred Component: The device comprises                  external power source, cathode carbon felt,                  cathode chamber, extraction chamber, anode                  chamber, iridium tantalum coated titanium plate                  anode, cation exchange membrane, anion exchange                  membrane and air bag.

AU MELNICHUK L J
   KELLY K S
AE MELNICHUK L J (MELN-Individual)
   KELLY K S (KELL-Individual)
GA 202096183N
AB    NOVELTY - Optimizing the carbon yield in the production of synthetic chemicals or fuels from organic feedstock involves processing the organic feedstock in a method to generate synthesis gas stream A comprises carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, removing substantially all unwanted solids and gasses from synthesis gas stream A such that consists of only carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons to form synthesis gas stream B, removing a measured amount of carbon dioxide from synthesis gas stream B based on data from a gas analyzer to form synthesis gas stream C comprised substantially of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, hydrocarbons and a reduced amount of carbon dioxide, sending synthesis gas stream C to a reformer which applies heat, pressure and a catalyst such that the hydrocarbons are reformed by a reaction with the carbon dioxide in the synthesis gas into carbon monoxide.
   USE - Method for optimizing the carbon yield in the production of synthetic chemicals or fuels from organic feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to optimize the carbon yield in the production of synthetic chemicals or fuels from organic feedstock increases product yield and reduces total carbon emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Optimizing the carbon yield in the production of synthetic chemicals or fuels from organic feedstock involves processing the organic feedstock in a method to generate synthesis gas stream A comprises carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, removing substantially all unwanted solids and gasses from synthesis gas stream A such that consists of only carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons to form synthesis gas stream B, removing a measured amount of carbon dioxide from synthesis gas stream B based on data from a gas analyzer to form synthesis gas stream C comprised substantially of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, hydrocarbons and a reduced amount of carbon dioxide, sending synthesis gas stream C to a reformer which applies heat, pressure and a catalyst such that the hydrocarbons are reformed by a reaction with the carbon dioxide in the synthesis gas into carbon monoxide and hydrogen thus forming synthesis gas stream consisting substantially of carbon monoxide and hydrogen, compressing synthesis gas stream F, reacting synthesis gas stream F in a reactor with a catalyst, heat and pressure to form a product, selectively obtaining the product by separating it from unreacted gasses and formed hydrocarbon gasses unrelated to the product, combining the unreacted gasses and formed hydrocarbon gasses to form a mixture, removing a sample from the mixture and combining with the sample from synthesis gas stream B and sending to the gas analyzer to determine the composition of the combined stream, sending the mixture to the reformer in which applies heat, pressure and a catalyst such that the mixture is reformed into substantially carbon monoxide and hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic feedstock is selected from plant material, a mixture of plastics and organic waste, natural gas, landfill gas, or gas from anaerobic digesters. The processing method or methods for the organic feedstock is gasification, pyrolysis or hydro- treating. Preferred Conditions: The process to generate synthesis gas from the feedstock takes place at a temperature range of 200-1200 degrees C. The process to generate synthesis gas from the feedstock takes place at a pressure range of 0.1-10.7 megaPascal. The carbon dioxide removal system is used to regulated by process controls. The method involves to operates at a temperature range of 800-1000 degrees C at a pressure of 0.1-5 megaPascal, the multiple reactors and the first reactor produces dimethyl ether and the second reactor produces gasoline range hydrocarbons.

GA 202254168H

   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based degradable shockproof bubble film prepared by using inexpensive raw materials, is 100% degradable, hence environmentally safe. The preparation of the bubble film involves one-step molding, plasticizing, tape casting and bubble absorption processes. The formed bubble film has excellent performance.

   1) mixing the raw materials of PPC and PBAT in a mixer, plasticizing, air-cooling and granulating to form a degradable modified mixture; 2) adding the degradable modified mixture into a bubble film machine, plasticizing, extruding and casting to form a film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The PPC used in the preparation of carbon dioxide-based degradable shockproof bubble film, is a polymer prepared by taking carbon dioxide and propylene oxide as raw materials, the molecular weight of the polymer is 200,000 to 300,000 or the PBAT is a copolymer prepared by taking butanediol adipate and butanediol terephthalate as raw materials, the molecular weight of PBAT is 10-200,000.Preferred Process: In step 1 of the preparation method, high temperature mixing and low temperature mixing are carried out in sequence The high temperature mixing is carried out at 70-80degrees Celsius for 15-25 minutes. The low-temperature mixing is carried out at 40-50degrees Celsius for 5-15 minutes.During plasticizing in step 1, the temperature of the extruder used is such that the heating is divided into four areas, the temperature is maintained at 145-155degrees Celsius and the die zone temperature is 155degrees Celsius. The air cooling performed in step 1, involves reducing the temperature of the plasticized strand below 40degrees Celsius and pelletizing.In step 2, when the bubble film is plasticized, extruded and cast into a film, the temperature of extruder is such that the heating is divided into four areas, the temperature is 140-150degrees Celsius. The die temperature is such that heating is divided into four zones, the temperature is 150degrees Celsius. The temperature of the foam forming roller is such that the bubbles are absorbed by adhering the front layer to the roller surface at 70degrees Celsius, followed by adhering the rear layer to wrap gas to form a bubble film.Preferred Property: In step 2 of the process, When the bubble film is plasticized, extruded and cast to form a film, the length-diameter ratio of a screw rod of the extruder is 30-35: 1.

   LIU L
AE HEBEI MINGRUN COMPOSITE MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202260311H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polypropylene resin comprises (i) grafting polypropylene by fluorinated carbon chain alcohol and maleic anhydride to obtain fluorinated polypropylene, and (ii) mixing the fluorinated polypropylene and polypropylene and foaming by supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain a polypropylene resin.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polypropylene resin, which is useful for supercritical carbon dioxide foaming in the field of materials (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple. The polypropylene resin: is suitable for transportation, and daily restaurant; has high foaming ratio, low cell density, uniform cell size, and improved compatibility and solubility of carbon dioxide in the fluorinated polyethylene glycol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a polypropylene resin, which has the density of below 0.06 g/cm3, preferably 0.04 g/cm3, the foaming ratio of above 15, preferably above 25, the cell size of below 70 microns, preferably 55-65 microns, and the cell density of above 5x107/cm3, preferably above 6x107/cm3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fluorinated carbon chain alcohol comprises 3,3,3-trifluoropropan-1-ol, 4,4,4-trifluorobutanol, hexafluorobutanol, and/or heptfluorohexane-1-ol. The catalyst comprises p-toluenesulfonic acid and/or toluenesulfonic acid. The solvent comprises toluene, xylene, and/or chlorobenzene. The content of the catalyst is 0.3-1 wt.% compared to the amount of the fluorinated carbon chain alcohol. The dehydrating agent comprises cyclohexane. In the step (ii), the gas injection volume of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 2-4 ml/minute, and the gas injection pressure is 15-25 MPa. The nucleating agent comprises calcium carbonate and/or talc. The antioxidant comprises 2,4-di-tert-butyl-6-(5-chloro-2H-benzotriazol-2-yl)phenol, pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate), and/or tris-(2,4-di-tert-butyl phenyl)phosphite. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the fluorinated polypropylene is prepared by mixing 2-5 moles of fluorinated carbon chain alcohol and 1 mole of maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene, and carrying out the reaction at 120-150degreesC under the action of solvent, dehydrating agent and catalyst for 5-8 hours. In the step (ii), the method further comprises mixing the dried polypropylene with polypropylene, nucleating agent and anti-aging agent, and adding it to a foaming extruder, injecting the supercritical carbon dioxide into the foaming extruder, extruding and foaming to obtain the polypropylene resin, where The extrusion temperature is 165-190degrees Celsius, the screw speed is 80-150 revolutions/minute (rpm), the feeding speed is 8-15 rpm, and the cutting speed is 250-300 rpm, the gas injection pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 15-25 MPa, and the gas injection volume is 2-4 ml/minute. The bead product is obtained by water-cooling the polypropylene resin, shearing and drying. Preferred Composition: The polypropylene resin is a bead product.

TI Method for moving of water transport facility has first pipe segment, in which the excess pressure and the flow.
AU BELETSKII I A
AE BELETSKII I A (BELE-Individual)
GA 202216143E
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to shipbuilding, namely to water-jet propellers of ships. To move the water vehicle, containers are used to store compressed carbon dioxide and, via a high-pressure pump, carbon dioxide is supplied to the nozzles of the first pipe segment, in which the excess pressure and the flow of the gas-water mixture are accelerated along the pipe and acts on the turbines, thereby rotating them. Then, at the outlet of the first pipe segment, the gas-water stream is divided into a two-water stream that extends in the direction of the nozzle and extends beyond the turbine engine, creating a reactive force, and a carbon dioxide stream that rises up along the inclined surface of the second pipe segment into the gas intake and then enters the vessel and then through the compressor enters the compressed carbon dioxide storage tank, thereby forming a closed cycle of carbon dioxide movement.
   USE - Shipbuilding.
   ADVANTAGE - Increase in the energy efficiency of the water jet turbine engine is achieved.

TI Software-based verification method for thermodynamic analysis, involves comparing first result with second result to verify accuracy of thermodynamic analysis of Engineering Equation Solver two-stage compression carbon dioxide transcritical refrigeration system.
AU WANG J
   GAO J
   XIE J
AE UNIV SHANGHAI OCEAN (USHO-C)
GA 2022028255
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a program algorithm design for thermodynamic analysis of two-stage compression carbon dioxide transcritical refrigeration system in Engineering Equation Solver software. The calculate button of the Engineering Equation Solver software is clicked. The thermodynamic analysis and calculation results of the two-stage compression carbon dioxide transcritical refrigeration system are obtained. The working substance properties of each state point of the two-stage compressed carbon dioxide transcritical refrigeration system are obtained by querying the REFPROP software, and calculated through the program algorithm to obtain second calculation result. The first result is compared with the second result to verify the accuracy of the thermodynamic analysis of the Engineering Equation Solver two-stage compression carbon dioxide transcritical refrigeration system.
   USE - Software-based verification method for thermodynamic analysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The verification method avoids the complicated experimental design in the previous verification, under the condition that the assumption conditions are satisfied, which improves the work efficiency of researchers.

PT P
PN CN113509903-A; CN113509903-B
TI Light stimulus responsive material used for promoting photosynthesis of plant in carbon dioxide adsorbent, has silica nanosphere as carrier, where carrier is doped with silver particle, and surface of silver-doped carrier.
AU LI C
   LI R
   WANG Y
   SUN M
   ZHU Y
   YAO Y
   LI Q
   ZHANG Y
   JIA X
AE UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
   UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
GA 2021C2513K
AB    NOVELTY - Light stimulus responsive material has silica nanosphere as carrier. The carrier is doped with silver particle, and surface of silver-doped carrier is modified by amine compound.
   USE - Light stimulus responsive material used for promoting photosynthesis of plant in carbon dioxide adsorbent (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The light stimulus responsive material has adsorbs carbon dioxide in the dark and releasing carbon dioxide under light stimulation under low concentration, normal temperature and normal pressure, when sprayed on plant leaf, it can establish a carbon dioxide microenvironment around the plant stomata and realize the regulation of plant photosynthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing light stimulus responsive material, which involves: (a)mixing;(b)reacting silica nanosphere doped with morphological template, silane coupling agent, silver nitrate and reducing agent;(c)removing the morphological template to obtain silver-doped silica nanosphere;(d)mixing;(e)reacting the silver-doped silica nanosphere, an impregnant, and an amine compound to obtain the light stimulus-responsive material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicon-containing precursor is selected from orthosilicate, silicate and silane. The silane coupling agent is selected from 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES), vinyltrimethoxysilane and vinyltriethoxysilane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The morphology template is selected from cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB), polyethylene oxide-polypropylene oxide-polyethylene oxide triblock copolymer and polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether block copolymer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine compound is selected from polyethyleneimine (PEI), tetraethylenepentamine, 3-(trimethoxysilyl)propylamine, N-(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)ethylenediamine and N-(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)diethylenetriamine. The reducing agent includes formaldehyde and polyvinylpyrrolidone. The impregnant is alcohol solvent. Preferred Process: The preparation method of silica nanosphere doped with the shape template agent involves dissolving the shape template agent in water, and adding alkali to obtain mixed solution, heating up, adding silicon-containing precursor or its solution to the system, and reacting to obtain the silica nanosphere doped with a morphology template. The preparation method of the light stimulus-responsive material involves dissolving the morphological template in water, adding alkali to obtain a mixed solution, adding tetraethyl orthosilicate solution to the system after the temperature is raised, reacting to obtain silica nanosphere doped with the morphological template, mixing, reacting the silica nanosphere doped with the topography template, silane coupling agent, silver nitrate and reducing agent, removing the topography template to obtain silver-doped silica nanospheres ball, mixing and reacting the silver-doped silica nanosphere, the impregnant, and polyethyleneimine to obtain the light stimulus-responsive material. Preferred Conditions: The particle size of the light stimulus responsive material is 100-900 nanometer. The particle size of the silver particle is 1-20 nanometer. The doping amount of the silver particle is 0.5-13%. The particle size of the silica nanosphere is 100-800 nanometer. The particle size of the silica nanosphere doped with morphology template is 100-800 nanometer. The molar ratio of the silica nanosphere doped with the morphology template agent, the silane coupling agent, the silver nitrate and the reducing agent is 100:(30-50):(1-10):(120-1400). The weight ratio of the amine compound to the silver-doped silica nanosphere is 1:(1-3).

ADR4468, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. lubricants and 3-5 pts. wt. fillers.

   ADVANTAGE - The injection special material: has excellent heat resistance, excellent mechanical properties, high stiffness and excellent degradability; completely degrades to produce carbon dioxide and water, while using carbon dioxide-based PPC raw materials under compostable conditions; consumes carbon dioxide; responds to the national carbon neutral initiative; and solves the global white pollution problem.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the injection special material, comprising uniformly mixing the raw materials to obtain mixture material, adding the mixture material to the twin-screw base material machine to melt and blend, extruding, and cooling and granulating.

Preferred Method: The barrel temperature of the twin-screw extruder is 160-180 degrees C, and the die temperature is 170 degrees C. The screw speed of the twin-screw extruder is 400-600 revolution per minute, and the screw length-to-diameter ratio L/D is 52:1.

PT P
PN CN112851999-A
TI Preparing high-expansion ratio polymer-based foam beads by secondary foaming comprises e.g. placing polymer-based beads in high pressure kettle, and introducing carbon dioxide gas, reaching carbon dioxide gas to supercritical state.
AU LIU P
   XU D
   LI L
   WANG Q
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202161952R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-expansion ratio polymer-based foam beads by secondary foaming comprises (i) placing the polymer-based beads in a high pressure kettle, introducing carbon dioxide gas, reaching the carbon dioxide gas to the supercritical state under the primary foaming condition, performing primary foaming of polymer-based beads in the supercritical carbon dioxide gas environment, and releasing the pressure is relieved to obtain the primary foamed beads, and (ii) introducing the primary foamed beads in the high pressure kettle, introducing physical foaming agent, performing secondary foaming under secondary foaming conditions, and releasing pressure.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-expansion ratio polymer-based foam beads by secondary foaming.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has application prospects in important fields of high-tech industry; overcomes the problem that conventional foaming methods can only prepare foamed beads with a lower expansion ratio; broadens the application range of bead foaming in polymer materials; and prepares large and high expansion rate polymer foam products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the primary foaming is carried out at 20-50 degrees C lower than the melting point of the polymer under 10-15 Mpa, and the immersing for 10-60 minutes. In the step (ii), the secondary foaming is carried out at 40-100 degrees C under 6-8 MPa, and an immersing for 30-120 minutes. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the expansion ratio of the primary foamed beads is 1-4 times, and the pore size is 0-50 m. The polymer-based beads comprises a single polymer system, a polymer/inorganic filler complex system, or a polymer blend system. The single polymer system comprises polyvinyl alcohol, poly-adipic acid/butylene terephthalate, polylactic acid, polycarbonate, polysulfone, polyetherimide, or polyimide. In the polymer/inorganic filler complex system the polymer comprises polyvinyl alcohol, poly-adipic acid/butylene terephthalate, polylactic acid, polycarbonate, polysulfone, polyetherimide, and/or polyimide, and the inorganic filler comprising graphite, carbon fiber, carbon nanotube, graphene, barium titanate, calcium carbonate, ferroferric oxide, hydroxyapatite, and/or sodium silicate. The polymer blend system comprises polyvinyl alcohol, poly-adipic acid/butylene terephthalate, polylactic acid, polycarbonate, polysulfone, polyetherimide, and/or polyimide. In the step (ii), the physical foaming agent comprises carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide/nitrogen, carbon dioxide/ethanol, carbon dioxide/water, carbon dioxide/acetone, and/or carbon dioxide/tetrahydrofuran.

PN CN112457912-A
TI Method for enhanced supercritical carbon dioxide            extraction of hazelnut oil, involves introducing            solution after instantaneous high-pressure treatment            into extraction kettle for supercritical carbon dioxide            extraction and collecting extract.
AU WANG J
   LIU J
   RUAN B
   GAO Y
AE XIANYANG VOCATIONAL & TECH COLLEGE (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202125202F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for enhanced supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction of hazelnut oil involves (S1)                dehulling hazelnut kernels, placing at -18 degrees                C, freezing and embrittling for 1-3 hours,                conducting ultrasonic breaking treatment at 30-40                degrees C to obtain hazelnut kernel powder, drying                and ready for use, (S2) subjecting to instantaneous                high-pressure treatment by an ultra-high pressure                homogenizer, after the dried hazelnut kernel powder                is mixed with the entrainer, adjusting the pressure                to 100-200 MPa, the cycle treatment is 2-3 times,                the processing flow rate is 5-8 L/hour and the                temperature is 20-30 degrees C and (S3) introducing                the solution after the instantaneous high-pressure                treatment into the extraction kettle for                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and                collecting the extract.
   USE - Method for enhanced supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction of hazelnut oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method significantly improves the                extraction rate of hazelnut oil by ultrasonic wall                breaking treatment and instantaneous high pressure                treatment, which can reach about 93.7%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: In step (S1), the                  particle size of the hazelnut kernel powder is                  40-60 mesh. Preferred Conditions: In step (S1),                  the conditions for ultrasonic wall breaking are                  the ultrasonic frequency is 20-25 KHZ, the time                  is 10-15 minutes and the rotation speed is 20-50                  rpm. In step (S3), the temperature of                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 35-40                  degrees C, and the pressure is 20-25 MPa. In step                  (S3), the carbon dioxide flow rate is 18-22                  L/hour. Preferred Composition: In step (S2), the                  entrainer contains 15 wt.% ethyl acetate. The                  weight ratio of the entrainer with respect to                  hazelnut kernel powder is 3:100.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC D10-A01; E10-G02H2E; E11-Q01C; E31-N05C
IP C11B-001/04; C11B-001/10

PN CN112338201-A
TI Synchronous heat treatment in three-dimensional printing involves using carbon dioxide laser as heat source of synchronous heat treatment for printing additive manufacture of components, and measuring temperature of printing process.
AU ZHAO Y
   TANG C
   HU Z
AE SUZHOU ZHUYOU 3-DIMENSION TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021181124
AB    NOVELTY - Synchronous heat treatment in three-dimensional printing involves: (S1) using carbon dioxide laser as a heat source of synchronous heat treatment for three-dimensional printing additive manufacture of components; (S2) performing intelligent temperature measurement of printing process by infrared imaging thermometer; (S3) printing and manufacturing metal components using fiber laser three-dimensional printing and simultaneous carbon dioxide laser heat treatment method; (S4) determining the laser power of the carbon dioxide laser, and using the fiber laser printing method to synchronize the heat treatment of the carbon dioxide laser for printing and manufacturing of metal components; (S5) carrying out printing additive manufacture; and (S6) calculating the initial cooling rate.
   USE - Synchronous heat treatment in three-dimensional printing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: monitors the temperature change of the printing path in real time through an infrared imaging thermometer; intelligently controls the laser output power to reduce internal stress of the printing component; and avoids crack deformation of the printing component caused by thermal stress to improve the production efficiency.

PN US2020368937-A1; WO2020236207-A1; CN113840704-A; US11254028-B2; EP3972793-A1; JP2022533741-W
TI System used for accelerated carbonation curing of            pre-cast cementitious structure, comprises ejector            operable to combine lesser-pressure carbon dioxide with            greater-pressure steam, and curing chamber downstream            of ejector.
AU JAMAL A
   FADHEL B A
   AMR I T
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2020B6458L
AB    NOVELTY - System (100) comprises (i) an ejector (110)                operable to combine a lesser-pressure carbon                dioxide containing stream from a carbon dioxide                source with a greater-pressure steam to produce a                mixed stream comprising at least steam and carbon                dioxide and having a pressure greater than a                pressure of the lesser-pressure carbon dioxide                containing stream, and (ii) a curing chamber (120)                downstream of the ejector, where the curing chamber                is operable to receive the mixed stream from the                ejector and contact the mixed stream with the                pre-cast cementitious structure during curing of                the pre-cast cementitious structure.
   USE - The system is used for accelerated carbonation                curing of a pre-cast cementitious structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The system enhances accelerated carbonation                curing for pre-cast cementitious structures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process                for accelerated carbonation curing of a pre-cast                cementitious structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system.
   System (100)
   Ejector (110)
   Curing chamber (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The ejector comprises an                  inlet in fluid communication with a steam source,                  a nozzle, a vacuum chamber in fluid communication                  with the carbon dioxide source, and a diffuser.                  Preferred Components: The mixed stream comprises                  1-50 vol % carbon dioxide based on the total                  volume flow rate of the mixed stream. The carbon                  dioxide source comprises a hydrocarbon combustion                  process and the lesser-pressure carbon dioxide                  containing stream comprises at least a portion of                  combustion gases from the hydrocarbon combustion                  process. The greater-pressure steam is produced                  by a steam source, and the steam source comprises                  a steam system operable to produce the                  greater-pressure steam through combustion of a                  hydrocarbon fuel. The carbon dioxide source                  comprises at least a portion of combustion gases                  from combustion of the hydrocarbon fuel in the                  steam system. Preferred Properties: The                  lesser-pressure carbon dioxide containing stream                  has a pressure of 90-110 kPa. The                  greater-pressure steam has a pressure of 150-2000                  kPa. The mixed stream has a pressure of 150-1500                  kPa.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); P64 (Working cement, clay, stone (B28).)
