PT P
PN CN111740174-A; CN111740174-B
TI Application method of carbon material in lead/carbon battery, involves adding carbon material prepared by electrolytically reducing carbon dioxide in molten alkali metal carbonate, to cathode of lead-acid battery to form electrode.
AU WANG D
   ZHAO X
   DENG Y
   JIANG R
   DOU Y
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 202099279M
AB    NOVELTY - Application method of high-hydrogen evolution over-potential carbon material involves adding carbon material to a cathode of a lead-acid battery, and forming a lead/carbon electrode used in electrochemical energy storage device manufacturing field. The carbon material is prepared by electrolytically reducing carbon dioxide in molten alkali metal carbonate. During the electrolysis process, carbon dioxide is captured by alkali metal oxide produced by the decomposition of carbonate, and reduced on the cathode, to generate a solid carbon dioxide-derived carbon material.
   USE - Application method of high-hydrogen evolution over-potential carbon material in lead/carbon battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-derived carbon material has large specific surface area, high specific capacitance, conductivity, and hydrogen evolution over-potential in high-concentration sulfuric acid solution, and inhibits occurrence of hydrogen evolution reaction in the battery charging process, prevents battery failure and effectively improves cycle life of the storage battery, when used as an additive for the cathode of the lead-acid battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkali metal carbonate is lithium carbonate, lithium carbonate-potassium carbonate or lithium carbonate-sodium carbonate-potassium carbonate. The content of oxygen atoms on the surface of the carbon material is 10-22 mol%. The amount of carbon material in cathode lead paste of the lead-acid battery containing carbon material, is 2-5 %mass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The anode used in electrolysis is nickel-iron-copper alloy anode, graphite anode or titanium dioxide anode. The cathode is made of copper, titanium, stainless steel and/or nickel-chromium alloy.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC L03-A02B; L03-E01B3; L03-E01B5; L03-E08B; X16-B01B; X16-E01C1

PN CN111514877-A; CN111514877-B
TI Preparing methane and carbon dioxide reforming            catalyst using hazardous waste resource comprises            mixing crushed and dehydrated e.g. petrochemical sludge            and binder, kneading, granulating, carbonizing and            activating.
AU LIU J
   ZHANG Q
   ZHANG G
   LIU X
   WANG Y
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202078760H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methane and carbon dioxide reforming                catalyst using hazardous waste resource comprises                (i) weighing petrochemical sludge, volatile organic                compounds (VOCs) waste carbon and coking sludge and                using a squeeze dehydrating machine to dehydrate                the petrochemical sludge, VOCs waste carbon and                coking sludge, (ii) weighing binder and wet                crushing the petrochemical sludge, VOCs waste                carbon, coking sludge and binder after dehydration                and oil recovery and (iii) mixing the crushed                petrochemical sludge, VOCs waste carbon, coking                sludge and binder, kneading and granulating by                adding conditioning agent aqueous solution,                carbonizing and activating to obtain the                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methane and                carbon dioxide reforming catalyst using hazardous                waste resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the harmless treatment of                hazardous waste; can apply hazardous waste raw                materials for the conversion of methane and carbon                dioxide; utilizes widely available and economical                raw material. The catalyst: has high catalytic                activity; and ensures high conversion rate of                methane and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The conditioning agent                  aqueous solution is sodium carboxymethyl                  cellulose aqueous solution. The ratio of quality                  of conditioning agent aqueous solution to the                  total mass of petrochemical sludge, VOCs waste                  carbon and coking sludge is 1-10:10. The mass                  ratio of petrochemical sludge is 25-35%, VOCs                  waste carbon is 20-40%, coking sludge is 25-35%                  and binder is 5-10%. The water content of the                  dewatered petrochemical sludge, VOCs waste carbon                  and coking sludge is less than 10%. The binder is                  at least one of epoxy resin, sodium humate,                  starch and modified asphalt. The particle size of                  petrochemical sludge, VOCs waste carbon, coking                  sludge and binder after dehydrating and oil                  recovery is 20-250 mesh after wet crushing.                  Preferred Method: The kneading process is carried                  out at 70-90?Oc for 30-60 minutes. The process                  comprises heating granulated particles at a rate                  of 5-10 degrees C/minutes, carrying out                  carbonization process at 550-750 degrees C for                  30-60 minutes, heating carbonized particles at a                  rate of 10-20 degrees C/minute and activating at                  850-950 degrees C for 50-90 minutes.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E31-A01; J04-E04; J04-E11; A05-A01E; A10-E21A; A11-C03A; A12-W11F; A12-W11K; N05-E03A; N07-J

PN CN210912824-U
TI Life jacket, has first air bag and second air bag that are formed as strip-shaped structure, where first air bag and second air bag are provided with exhaust valve and carbon dioxide gas jar is connected with first air chamber through air pipe.
AU LIU M
AE LIU M (LIUM-Individual)
GA 202064604E
AB    NOVELTY - A life jacket, two shoulder of life jacket are respectively provided with a first group of carbon dioxide tank and second group carbon dioxide tank, lower part of life jacket arranged in parallel two groups of airbags, which are respectively the first airbag and the second airbag, first groups of carbon dioxide tank connected with the first ballonet through air pipe; the second group of carbon dioxide tank, connected with the second air chamber, the first air bag and the second air bag is strip-shaped and surrounding set around the jacket, a first air bag and a second air bag is provided with an exhaust valve. The utility model is portable, close-fitting, simple and fast trigger function, after wearing the daily activities without any obstruct, will not increase extra physical burden and psychological burden of daily life, avoid the danger of drowning.
DC Q24 (Ships; waterborne vessels; related equipment (B63))

PT P
PN US2019040427-A1
TI Bacterial cell of genus Cupriavidus, Xanthobacter, Hydrogenobacter, or Hydrogenovibrio comprises first exogenous nucleic acid sequence, where cell converts gaseous carbon dioxide and/or gaseous hydrogen and/or syngas into one or more lipids.
AU KUREK I
   REED J S
   DYSON L
   FYRST H
   JANSSON C
   GALGOCZY D
AE KIVERDI INC (KIVE-Non-standard)
GA 201913813S
AB    NOVELTY - Bacterial cell of the genus Cupriavidus, Xanthobacter, Hydrogenobacter, or Hydrogenovibrio comprises a first exogenous nucleic acid sequence, where the cell converts gaseous carbon dioxide and/or gaseous hydrogen and/or syngas into one or more lipids or hydrocarbons.
   USE - Bacterial cell of the genus Cupriavidus, Xanthobacter, Hydrogenobacter, or Hydrogenovibrio.
   ADVANTAGE - The bacterial cell of the genus Cupriavidus, Xanthobacter, Hydrogenobacter, or Hydrogenovibrio enhances production of carbon-based products to an organism such that it converts carbon dioxide, or other inorganic carbon sources, and inorganic energy, including chemical energy from an inorganic chemical or directly from an electrical source, into carbon-based products of commercial value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing lipids or hydrocarbons, which involves culturing a bacterial cell of the genus Cupriavidus, Xanthobacter, Hydrogenobacter, or Hydrogenovibrio in a bioreactor or solution with a feedstock comprising syngas and/or gaseous carbon dioxide and/or a mixture comprising gaseous carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The first exogenous nucleic acid sequence encodes a fatty acyl-CoA binding protein. The bacterial cell further comprises a second exogenous nucleic acid sequence encoding a thioesterase. The bacterial cell is a knallgas microorganism, chemoautrophic microorganism, or hydrogen-oxidizing chemoautotroph. The bacterial cell is capable of growing on syngas as the sole energy and carbon source. The bacterial cell produces and/or secretes lipids in a quantity that is 10% of the dry cell mass. The 50% of one or more lipids or hydrocarbons comprise 6-30 carbon atoms. The bacterial cell is a Cupriavidus necator or Cupriavidus metallidurans cell. The feedstock comprises syngas as the sole energy and carbon source. The one or more lipids or hydrocarbons are separated from the bioreactor or solution. Preferred Process: The method further involves up-regulating an endogenous or exogenous thioesterase gene of the bacterial cell, down-regulating an endogenous or exogenous thioesterase gene of the bacterial cell, and down-regulating an endogenous or exogenous acyl carrier protein gene of the bacterial cell.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-C; D05-H04

PT P
PN CN109270584-A; CN109270584-B
TI Method for detecting non-contact vital signs of human body in closed cabin, involves recognizing human body accidentally, and closed cabin enters emergency mode when use time of closed compartment reaches use time.
AU HONG L
   DENG Q
AE HANGZHOU XINGKETING NETWORK TECHNOLOGY (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2019123530
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves arranging an infrared detector, a carbon dioxide sensor, a hall sensor, an inner circulation air passage and a remote server in the closed compartment. The human body is entered the closed compartment to limit the use time. The closed chamber is worked normally when the carbon dioxide sensor monitors that the carbon dioxide concentration is raised within time. The human body or the closed compartment is abnormal, and the data is reported to the remote server. The human body is accidentally recognized, and the closed cabin enters the emergency mode when the use time of the closed compartment reaches the limited use time.
   USE - Method for detecting non-contact vital signs of human body in closed cabin.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide sensor specifically monitors the carbon dioxide in the closed compartment in real time to control the closed compartment. The process has the characteristics of non-contact type, fast reaction time, low cost and high accuracy.

PT P
PN WO2018195045-A1; WO2018195045-A8
TI Composition used as e.g. catalyst for reducing            carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide, hydrocarbon, and            alcohol, comprises electrically conductive support            material, and transition metal atom or cluster            incorporated within support material.
AU WANG H
   JIANG K
AE HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
GA 201883982G
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises an electrically                conductive support material, and a transition metal                atom or cluster incorporated within the support                material.
   USE - Composition used as catalyst for reducing                carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide, hydrocarbon,                and alcohol (all claimed). Carbon monoxide is used                as a feedstock for carbon-based fuel. Can be used                as material in electrode such as membrane electrode                assembly in electrocatalytic cell and                electrochemical cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has high Faradaic efficiency,                higher current-per-mass catalyst value and fast                turnover frequencies.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) fabrication of catalyst (c1), which                involves mixing together a transition metal salt,                carbon source, and optionally a dopant source,                heating the mixture to form the electrically                conductive support material having transition metal                atom or cluster; and
   (2) method of reducing carbon dioxide in                liquid medium, which involves providing the                composition, contacting gaseous carbon dioxide with                the composition in the liquid medium, and applying                a voltage potential to the composition to reduce                carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The fabrication method                  further involves forming the mixture into a                  morphology chosen from fibers, nanosheets,                  particles, and powders by electrospinning. The                  carbon dioxide reduction method further involves                  oxidizing water into oxygen, in which the water                  is in contact with a catalyst (c2) which is                  separated from the catalyst (c1) by an ion                  permeable membrane and the catalysts are                  electrically connected and oxidizing formic acid,                  methanol, ethanol, or sulfur trioxide, in contact                  with an electrode separated from the catalyst                  (c1), which are electrically connected. The                  voltage potential is supplied by a photovoltaic                  cell. Preferred Composition: The support material                  is chosen from carbon particles, carbon powder,                  graphene, reduced graphene oxide, graphene oxide,                  and carbon nanotubes, preferably graphene or                  graphene oxide. The transition metal is chosen                  from nickel, cobalt, iron, manganese, rhodium,                  platinum, copper, molybdenum, and tungsten,                  preferably nickel. The composition optionally                  comprises a core of the transition metal                  surrounded by a shell of transition metal atoms                  or clusters in the support material, and further                  comprises a dopant atom incorporated in the                  support material. The dopant atom is chosen from                  elements from group 13, group 15, and group 16 of                  the periodic table, preferably nitrogen. The                  liquid medium is aqueous or non-aqueous. The                  catalyst (c2) comprises an oxygen evolution                  reaction catalyst chosen from cobalt(II,III)                  oxide-doped with lithium ions, tungsten dioxide,                  a transition metal oxide, a transition metal                  sulfide, a transition metal phosphide, iridium,                  or ruthenium, preferably cobalt(II,III)                  oxide-doped with lithium ions and tungsten                  oxide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN108435124-A
TI Preparing high-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent involves crushing coal series kaolin, sieving, putting into muffle furnace, raising temperature to obtain meta-kaolin, mixing meta-kaolin in hydrochloric acid solution, and stirring.
AU DING T
   LIU Y
   XIE H
   LI X
   TIAN Y
   CHEN W
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201868320U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent involves crushing coal series kaolin, sieving, putting into muffle furnace, raising temperature to 650-850 degrees C, keeping constant temperature for 1-4 hours to obtain meta-kaolin, mixing meta-kaolin in 1-4 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution at 60-95 degrees C, stirring for removing aluminum to obtain silicon dioxide. The silicon dioxide is washed by water after acid treatment until no chlorine ion is detected, and then dried 110-120 degrees C to obtain silicon dioxide. The silicon dioxide is mixed with lithium carbonate in a stoichiometric ratio in muffle furnace. The temperature is raised to 650-950 degrees C for 4-8 hours, and furnace is cooled to room temperature to obtain final product.
   USE - Method for preparing high-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent that has low adsorbent preparation cost and good carbon dioxide absorption capacity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The heating speed is 20-30 degrees C/minute. The ratio of meta-kaoline and hydrochloric acid is 1:(10-30) g/mL. The drying temperature is 60-95 degrees C. The stirring time is 2-6 hours. The stoichiometric ratio of 1:2.

PN WO2018002771-A1
TI Producing olefins by oxidative dehydrogenation involves introducing hydrocarbon feed stream and carbon dioxide to chromium oxide catalyst modified with potassium and manganese and generating product stream comprising olefins and syngas.
AU MAMEDOV A
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)
GA 201801203B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing olefins by an oxidative dehydrogenation reaction, comprises introducing a feed stream comprising 2C and 3C hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide to a chromium oxide catalyst modified with potassium and manganese; and generating a product stream comprising olefins and syngas, where the syngas has a molar ratio of carbon monoxide to hydrogen of 1.4-1.8.
   USE - The method is useful for producing olefins by oxidative dehydrogenation reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: facilitates conversion of at least 75 mol.% of ethane, at least 80 mol.% of propane and at least 50 mol.% of carbon dioxide, and thus provides olefins with an yield of greater than 55% and simultaneously generates valuable syngas, while resulting in less coke formation and without generating deep oxidation products such as carbon dioxide, hence efficient with increased conversion of light hydrocarbons and olefins yield; allows for full oxidation to carbon dioxide and thus results in partial loss of the feed stock; and is less prone to cause heat runaway from oxidation by molecular oxygen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chromium oxide catalyst: is supported on either alumina or silica; comprises 2 wt.% potassium, 8 wt.% chromium, and 15 wt.% manganese supported on silica.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises reacting the feed stream in the presence of the chromium oxide catalyst at 600-900 degrees C, preferably 800-830 degrees C and a space velocity of 2800/hour. Preferred Components: The feed stream comprises 30-60 (preferably 35) mol.% 2C and 3C hydrocarbons and 40-90 (preferably 65) mol.% carbon dioxide. The C2 hydrocarbons comprise ethane and the C3 hydrocarbons comprise propane.
   EXAMPLE - Feed stream comprised of 35 mol.% of 2-3C fraction (containing 50 mol.% ethane and 50 mol.% propane) and 65 mol.% carbon dioxide was dehydrogenated in the presence of a modified chromium catalyst comprised of 2 wt.% potassium, 8 wt.% chromium and 15 wt.% manganese/silica in a fixed bed reactor at a flow rate or 373 cc/minute. The catalyst loading was 8 ml and the space velocity of the reaction was 2800/hour. The reaction was carried out at 800 degrees C. The product composition was analyzed with a gas chromatography, and the conversion of ethane was 70.5 mol.%, the conversion of propane was 74.6 mol.% and carbon dioxide conversion was 44.2 mol.%. The product stream contained ethylene, propylene, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and methane, and the carbon monoxide to hydrogen ratio was 1.5.

PT P
PN WO2020130954-A1
TI New metal-organic framework comprising copper-bromide-methanol containing compound useful for removing mercury vapor, hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide gas and/or carbon monoxide from petroleum fluid with high performance.
AU PIROMCHART T
   CHAINOK K
AE PTT EXPLORATION & PRODN PUBLIC CO LTD (PTTE-Non-standard)
   KIT CHEM THAILAND CO LTD (KITC-Non-standard)
GA 202057562R
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-organic framework comprising copper-bromide-methanol containing compound (I) is new.
   USE - The metal-organic framework (I) is useful for removing mercury vapor, hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide gas and/or carbon monoxide from petroleum fluid by an adsorption between the petroleum fluid and the metal-organic framework (claimed) with a high performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal-organic framework comprising copper-bromide-methanol containing compound of formula (-(Cum(Br4BDC)n(MeOH)x)-) (I) is new.
   m, n, x = 1-5.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing metal-organic framework (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): Preparation of metal-organic framework (I) comprises mixing (i) copper (II) salt (Cu2+) and 1,2,4,5-tetrabromobenzenedicarboxylic acid (Br4BDC), methanol and water together, and (ii) heating the mixture from step (i) and collecting product, where the mixture from step (ii) is heated at 90-130 degrees C for 1-3 days. Preferred Method: The salt of copper comprises copper (II) nitrate (preferred), copper (II) chloride, copper (II) sulfate and/or copper (II) acetate. Mole ratio of the salt of copper and 1,2,4,5-tetrabromobenzenedicarboxylic acid is 4:1-6:1. A mole ratio of methanol and water is 4:1-6:1. In step (a) 0.05 to 0.15 mole of benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid is further added into the mixture. The mixture of step (ii) is heated at 100-125 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 days. Preferred Components: (I) is preferably copper-(1,2,4,5-tetrabromobenzenedicarboxylic acid)(methanol).
   Preferred Definitions:
   m, n = 1-2; and
   x = 1-4.
   EXAMPLE - Copper(II) nitrate trihydrate, tetrabromoterephthalic acid and trimethylamine were mixed in the reactor containing a solvent mixture, and stirred at room temperature for 10 minutes. Then the reaction mixture was heated to the temperature 120 degrees C under autogenous pressure and maintained the temperature for 48 hours. The heated reaction mixture was allowed to cool to room temperature. After filtration, the obtained product was washed with methanol and dried in air at room temperature.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

   USE - The magnesium-based composite hydroxide is useful for desulfurization flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite hydroxide has high specific surface area and high desulfurization efficiency, partially replaces expensive sodium hydroxide, reduces usage of chemicals, and is economical.

AU LIU Y
   DENG L
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201917865X
AB    NOVELTY - A cadmium sulfide/metal organic framework                composite photocatalytic material comprises metal                organic framework modified on the surface of                cadmium sulfide nanoparticles.
   USE - Cadmium sulfide/metal organic framework                composite photocatalytic material for                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cadmium sulfide/metal organic framework                composite photocatalytic material has photoelectric                stability, conversion efficiency, cycle stability                and catalytic selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                Preparation of cadmium sulfide/metal organic                framework composite photocatalytic material, which                involves using thiourea and cadmium salts as raw                materials, synthesizing cadmium sulfide                nanoparticles by solvothermal method, modifying                metal organic framework on the surface of cadmium                sulfide nanoparticles by heterogeneous                deposition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal organic                  framework is ZIF-8. Preferred Process: The method                  involves sequentially adding metal salt solution                  and organic ligand solution, stirring and                  centrifuging. After adding the metal salt                  solution, the reaction time is stirred for 0.5-3                  hours. The reaction time after stirring the                  organic ligand solution is 0.5-6 hours. Preferred                  Composition: The volume ratio of cadmium sulfide                  nanoparticle dispersion to metal salt solution                  and organic ligand solution is                  5-100:20-500:20-500. The concentration of the                  cadmium sulfide nanoparticle dispersion is 1-20                  mg/mL. The concentration of the metal salt                  solution is 5-100 mM. The metal salt solution is                  zinc salt-methanol solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The organic solution                  containing thiourea, cadmium salt and dispersant                  are subjected to solvothermal reaction to obtain                  cadmium sulfide nanoparticles. Preferred                  Composition: The molar ratio of thiourea to                  cadmium salt is 0.2:1-5:1. The molar ratio of                  thiourea to dispersant is 0.5:1-3:1. The                  dispersant is polyvinylpyrrolidone. The solvent                  in organic solution is ethylene glycol. The                  concentration of the organic ligand solution is                  5-400 mM. The cadmium sulfide nanoparticle                  dispersion uses methanol as a solvent. The                  organic ligand solution is 2-methylimidazole                  methanol solution. Preferred Conditions: The                  solvothermal reaction is carried out at a                  temperature of 100-180 degrees C for 4-24                  hours.

   SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)

   USE - Method for preparing catalyst for preparing ethylene by oxidizing ethane with carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, low cost, it has higher ethane conversion and ethylene selectivity, and it reduces energy consumptions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicon dioxide microsphere carrier is made of water, ammonia water, ethanol, ethyl orthosilicate under the alkaline condition to prepare the powder after dry.Preferred Composition: The volume of the silicon dioxide micro- sphere is 0.52-0.52 0.74mL/g. The baking formed small ball to obtain the catalyst. The roasting temperature is 600-700 degreesC. The method further involves activating the catalyst for 1-3 hours, in the carbon dioxide atmosphere at 600-700 degreesC, the reaction pressure is 0.1-0 4MPa, the mol ratio of carbon dioxide to ethane in the reaction is 2.1-4.1.

PN CN111826986-A
TI Separating components of wood fiber comprises pre-treating wood fiber raw material by using carbon dioxide auxiliary hydrothermal method to achieve degradation of hemicellulose, using eutectic solvent treatment, and realizing separation.
AU WANG R
   JIANG J
   XU J
   WANG K
   ZHOU M
   LI J
   LIU P
   YE J
   XIA H
   ZHAO J
AE INST CHEM IND FOREST PROD CHINESE ACAD (CLYK-C)
GA 2020A7388Y
AB    NOVELTY - Separating components of wood fiber comprises pre-treating the wood fiber raw material by using carbon dioxide auxiliary hydrothermal method to achieve the degradation of hemicellulose, using eutectic solvent treatment, and realizing the separation of cellulose and lignin to obtain high-purity cellulose and lignin.
   USE - The method is useful for separating components of wood fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the separation of the lignocellulosic components by using different eutectic solvent systems and adjusting the reaction temperature; is green and environmentally friendly, and realizes high-efficiency separation of components of wood fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The eutectic solvent is choline chloride/lactic acid, betaine/ lactic acid. Preferred Method: The separation of components of wood fiber specifically comprises (1) adding the wood fiber raw material and deionized water into the reaction kettle, introducing high purity carbon dioxide to react, after finishing reaction, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing the solid residue with de-ionized water, and drying, (2) adding the solid residue and the eutectic solvent into the reaction container to react, after finishing the reaction, centrifuging the reactant to obtain supernatant and solid residue, adding de-ionized water into the supernatant, precipitating the lignin, centrifuging and washing with ethanol aqueous solution, obtaining the lignin component by freezing and drying method, washing the solid residue with ethanol aqueous solution, drying to obtain the cellulose component, and separating the cellulose and lignin. The preparation of the eutectic solvent comprises mixing choline chloride and betaine with lactic acid separately, heating in a water bath at 60 degrees C until the solids in the reaction system dissolve and a uniform and transparent solution to prepare eutectic solvents choline chloride /lactic acid and betaine/ lactic acid, the molar ratio of choline chloride to lactic acid is 1:10, and the molar ratio of betaine to lactic acid is 1:2. The wood fiber raw material is moso bamboo, which is pulverized to a particle size of 40-60 mesh, and drying at 105 degrees C for 12 hours. The carbon dioxide assists the hydrothermal process with a solid-liquid ratio of 1:8-1:12, the reaction temperature is 180-200 degrees C, the reaction time is 10-30 minutes, and the pressure of carbon dioxide is 20-40 bar. In the process of separating lignin with eutectic solvent, the reaction temperature is 100-140 degrees C, and the reaction time is 4-8 hours. In the process of separating lignin with eutectic solvent, the reaction temperature is 140 degrees C, and the reaction time is 6 hours. In the process of separating lignin with eutectic solvent, the mass ratio of solid residue to eutectic solvent is 1:15-1:25, preferably 1:20.

PT P
PN CN111035622-A; CN111035622-B
TI Preparing chalcone microcapsules of Angelica keiskei involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of Angelica keiskei to obtain Angelica keiskei extract, adding to macroporous resin and performing dynamic adsorption.
AU LIU N
   LI J
   MA Y
   HUANG H
AE DONGGUAN BODUN SPICE CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 202034877Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chalcone microcapsules of Angelica keiskei involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of Angelica keiskei to obtain Angelica keiskei extract. The Angelica keiskei extract is added to the macroporous resin, then performing dynamic adsorption, eluting and concentrating to obtain chalcone concentrated solution. The chalcone concentrated solution is mixed with Maillard reactant solution and emulsifier to obtain a pre-emulsion, and homogenizing the pre-emulsion and spray drying to obtain microcapsules of Angelica keiskei chalcone.
   USE - Method for preparing chalcone microcapsules of Angelica keiskei.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare chalcone microcapsules of Angelica keiskei in a simple manner and microcapsules has good stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The process of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of Angelica keiskei leaves includes by static extraction for 20-50 minutes, and then dynamic extraction 45-70 minutes. The process of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of Angelica keiskei, the entrainer used is an ethanol solution, and the concentration of ethanol in the entraining agent is 60-85 vol.%, and the amount of the entraining agent is 0.1 to 0.5 times the mass of Angelica keiskei. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of Angelica keiskei is carried out under pressure of 20-35 MPa and extraction temperature is 40-60 degrees C. The extract is added to a macroporous resin, followed by dynamic adsorption, followed by steps of elution and concentration including adding the extract to the macroporous resin, first performing dynamic adsorption under the condition of an adsorption flow rate of 1-5 mL/min, and then rinsing with water until colorless, with a concentration of 30-70 vol.% ethanol solution, which is an analytical agent, and the elution is carried out under the conditions of an analytical flow rate of 0.5-3.0 mL/minutes, and then concentrated to make the mass concentration of solids in the chalcone concentrated solution be 40-90 g/L. The chalcone concentrate, Maillard reactant solution and emulsifier are mixed with whey protein, konjac gum, alpha -galactooligosaccharide and water to obtain a mixed solution, where the mass ratio of whey protein, konjac gum and alpha -galactooligosaccharide is (0.5-2): (1-3): (0.5-1), the mass concentration of the total solids in the mixed solution is 50g / L-80g/L. The pH value of the mixed solution is adjusted to 7-10, and the Maillard reaction is performed under the conditions of a vacuum of 100 mbar to 1000 mbar and a reaction temperature of 50-90 degrees C for 2-6 hours to obtain the Maillard reaction material solution. The chalcone concentrate, Maillard reactant solution and emulsifier are mixed to obtain a pre-emulsion by mixing 1-30 pts. wt. chalcone concentrate, 1-100 pts. wt. Maillard reactant solution, and 1-20 pts. wt. emulsifier and then subjecting to ultrasonic dispersion to obtain the pre-emulsion. The step of homogenizing the pre-emulsion, the homogenizing pressure is 10,000 psi to 20,000 psi, and the number of cycle homogenization is 1 to 3 times, and during spray drying, inlet air temperature is 170-180 degrees C and the outlet air temperature is 80-90 degrees C. The step of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of Angelica keiskei leaves, the method further involves crushing the Angelica keiskei leaves to 10-50 mesh. Preferred Components: The emulsifier is selected from soap bark extract, soluble soybean polysaccharide, casein, or soybean lecithin species.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Composition useful in medicine for prophylaxing and treating respiratory diseases in mammal, comprises carbon dioxide absorbent or adsorbent, and polymeric coating chosen from silicone rubber from liquid silicone rubber and cellulose.
AU JOCKENHOEVEL S
   CORNELISSEN C
   SPILLNER J
   KOK R J
   JAN KOK R
   CORNELISSEN C G
   JOCKENHOVEL S
AE RHEINISCH WESTFAELISCHE TECH HOCHSCHULE (RWTH-C)
   RHEINISCH WESTFAELISCHE TECH HOCHSCHULE (RWTH-C)
GA 2020144693
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises 10-99 wt.% carbon dioxide absorbent or adsorbent, and 1-90 wt.% polymeric coating chosen from silicone rubber obtained from liquid silicone rubber and cellulose.
   USE - The composition is useful in medicine for prophylaxing and treating respiratory diseases in a mammal chosen from ungulates including horses and camels, carnivores including cats, dogs and humans, preferably humans (all claimed). The respiratory diseases are chosen from acute and chronic airway diseases such as adult respiratory distress syndrome, asthma and mucoviscidosis, including chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), preferably COPD.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 10-99 wt.%, preferably 50-99 wt.% carbon dioxide absorbent or adsorbent, and 1-90 wt.%, preferably 1-50 wt.% polymeric coating chosen from silicon rubber obtained from liquid silicone rubber and cellulose. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide absorbent or adsorbent is chosen from inorganic hydroxides of alkali hydroxides and/or alkaline earth metal hydroxides, molecular sieves, zeolites, active carbon, zinc (ii) ions, deep eutectic solvents and/or silica. The carbon dioxide absorbent or adsorbent is either in granular form having a diameter of 0.1-10 mm, preferably 2-4 mm of United States Pharmacopeia 26 or being pulverized in a per is useful for known manner or the carbon dioxide absorbent or adsorbent is in a liquid form. The silicone rubber is obtained from a liquid silicone rubber, where the liquid rubber is vulcanized, preferably either as a two-part, platinum-catalyzed liquid silicone rubber or as one-part liquid silicone rubber with peroxide initiator(s). The cellulose is either pure cellulose or its derivative comprising cellulose ester, regenerated cellulose and/or a water insoluble cellulose ether. The absorbance of carbon dioxide from the gastrointestinal tract of a mammal, under conditions where an excess of carbon dioxide is present in the gastrointestinal tract of a mammal, preferably for the prophylaxis and treatment of respiratory diseases. The carbon dioxide absorbent or adsorbent is embedded in silicone rubber or with a capsule, where the carbon dioxide absorbent or adsorbent is contained in a capsule made of vulcanized silicone rubber being preferably sealed with a silicone rubber coating or of a water insoluble cellulose. Preferred Method: The administration is performed with a tablet having a weight of 0.01 mg-0.6 g, preferably 0.01-0.1 g.
   ACTIVITY - Respiratory-Gen.; Antiasthmatic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The composition in the form of tablet, granule, or minitablet is administered by oral route at least one, preferably at least 3 times per day (all claimed). No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2020013411-A; KR2136928-B1
TI Manufacture of adsorbent for adsorbing radionuclide containing cesium, involves preparing waste concrete, producing waste cement paste comprising silicates, and providing carbon dioxide to waste cement paste to produce carbonated paste.
AU JANG J
   PARK S
AE UNIV INCHEON IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIH-C)
GA 202014563M
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of radionuclide adsorbent involves preparing waste concrete, producing waste cement paste comprising silicates from the waste concrete, and providing carbon dioxide to waste cement paste to produce carbonated waste cement paste.
   USE - Manufacture of adsorbent for adsorbing radionuclide containing cesium (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent capable of adsorbing cesium easily can be produced safely. The radioactive material can be stored safely without leakage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) adsorption of radionuclides, which involves providing the radionuclide adsorbent to a radioactive material comprising cesium, and adsorbing cesium to the radionuclide adsorbent; and
   (2) radioactive waste disposal device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The waste cement paste is charged into a chamber, and carbon dioxide gas is provided. The waste cement paste is ground. The carbonated waste cement paste is washed with an organic solvent and then dried. The waste cement paste contains cement paste (a) obtained from the surface portion of the waste concrete, and cement paste (b) obtained from the interior of the waste concrete. The waste cement paste (a) contains calcium carbonate in amount more than that of waste cement paste (b). The waste cement paste contains inorganic mineral components ((CaO)3(SiO2), (CaO)2(SiO2), (CaO)3(Al2O3), and (CaO)4(Al2O3)(Fe2O3)).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The organic solvent is acetone and/or isopropyl alcohol.

TI Preparing nickel-nitrogen co-doped carbon-based            electro-catalyst useful for carbon dioxide reduction            reaction, comprises dispersing nickel salt,            dicyandiamide and ammonium chloride in water, drying            and calcining.
AU WU C
   LI X
   XIE Y
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 201921819H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-nitrogen co-doped                carbon-based electro-catalyst comprises (i)                dispersing nickel salt, dicyandiamide and ammonium                chloride in water, drying and calcining; (ii)                hydrothermally reacting the calcined product with                glucose in water; and (iii) calcining the                hydrothermally reacted product.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide                reduction reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and low cost, and                ensures large yield and suitable for industrial                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                nickel-nitrogen co-doped carbon-based                electro-catalyst is prepared by above mentioned                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel salt is a                  divalent soluble nickel salt. The mass ratio of                  the nickel salt to dicyandiamide is 0.2-5:100.                  The mass ratio of the nickel salt to ammonium                  chloride is 0.2-5:500. In the step (i), the mass                  ratio of the product after calcination to glucose                  is 0.7-1.5:5. The nickel-nitrogen co-doped                  carbon-based electro-catalyst has mass fraction                  of nitrogen of 3-6%. The nickel-nitrogen co-doped                  carbon-based electro-catalyst has mass fraction                  of nickel of 1-3%. Preferred Method: In the step                  (i), the temperature of calcination is 500-650                  degrees C and the calcination time is 1-4 hours.                  The temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is                  160-200 degrees C and the hydrothermal reaction                  time is 10-20 hours. In the step (iii), the                  temperature of calcination is 800-1100 degrees C                  and the calcination time is 0.5-4 hours.

PN CN107827858-A
TI Preparation of cyclic carbonate using aluminum-based catalyst involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in presence of conjugated aluminum porphyrin bipyridyl/conjugated aluminum porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material.
AU WANG N
   ZHANG X
   CHEN L
   CAO Y
   WANG Y
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 201824341N
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of cyclic carbonate using aluminum-based catalyst involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in the presence of conjugated aluminum porphyrin bipyridyl/conjugated aluminum porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material as heterogeneous catalyst and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as co-catalyst at normal temperature and normal pressure for 12-48 hours without solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical, rapid and safe preparation of cyclic carbonate with excellent catalytic activity, efficiency and stability under mild reaction condition, and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The conjugated aluminum porphyrin bipyridyl microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of aluminum tetraphenylporphyrin of formula (I) and 2,2-bipyridyl as monomer in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. The conjugated aluminum porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of aluminum tetraphenylporphyrin of formula (II) and pyrrole as monomer in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. Preferred Components: The epoxide is propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide capturing system comprises                a rotary kiln, which is provided with a kiln head,                a kiln tail and a roller, where the kiln head is                provided with a feeding bin. The feeding bin is                filled with a cement raw material, where the kiln                tail is provided with a discharging port and an air                outlet. The roller is rotatably connected with the                kiln head and the kiln tail, where a burner and a                blower are provided. The burner is sleeved on the                roller, where a combustion chamber is formed with                the roller. An end of a flue gas pipe is connected                with an inlet, where the inlet of a gas storage                tank is connected with an outlet.

The carbon                capturing device is provided with a heat exchange                wall for transferring the heat between the                reactors, so as to prevent the heat from                transferring to the reactor. The system prevents                the discharging of the material from the discharge                port.

The injection well distance is determined as a technical limit well distance until the injection is tangent.
   USE - Method for determining ultra-low permeability reservoir carbon dioxide drive technology limit well distance.

PN CN113234886-A; CN113234886-B
TI Controlled realization of ultra-low iron oxide in            converter end slag, involves adding bottom-blowing            spray guns on original bottom-blowing venting bricks,            using nitrogen, argon, and carbon-dioxide as carriers,            dividing blowing process, and blowing portion of            mixture of carbon-dioxide and argon.
AU WANG D
   ZHANG Y
   RU Z
   HAN Z
   WANG C
   ZHAO Z
AE GANSU JIU STEEL GROUP HONGXING IRON & (JGHX-C)
GA 202194779L
AB    NOVELTY - Controlled realization of ultra-low iron oxide                in converter end slag involves (a) adding 2 or more                bottom-blowing spray guns on basis of the original                bottom-blowing venting bricks at the bottom of the                converter, using nitrogen, argon, and                carbon-dioxide as carriers to spray lime powder                from the spray guns into the converter furnace, (b)                dividing the blowing process into three stages of                early stage-middle stage-later stage in which the                gas supply in the early stage is 50-60 wt.% oxygen                and 30-40 wt.% nitrogen, the gas supply in the                middle stage is 30-40 wt.% oxygen and 50-60 wt.%                nitrogen/carbon-dioxide, and the later gas supply                is 60-70 wt.% carbon-dioxide and 20-30 wt.% oxygen,                (c) pulling carbon to tapping and the converter is                in a vertical waiting process, and blowing a                portion of mixture of carbon-dioxide and argon into                furnace through bottom blowing spray gun where the                flow of carbon-dioxide injection is 700-900                Nm3/hours, and flow of argon is 1000-1300                Nm3/hours.
   USE - Controlled realization of ultra-low iron oxide                in converter end slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The slag dephosphorizing ability caused by                returning slag in the blowing and refining medium                is reduced to effectively overcome. The iron oxide                content in the slag of the tapping process is                continuously reduced. The iron oxide in the final                slag is reduced in the bottom powder spraying                process. The loss of the metal iron is reduced, and                the iron yield is improved. The steel and iron                material consumption of the converter steel-making                is reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The 400-500 kg lime                  powder is sprayed in the early stage of the                  blowing, and the 300-400kg lime powder is sprayed                  in the later stage. The total amount of lime                  powder injection is controlled at 800-1000                  kg/furnace. The rate of bottom blowing air supply                  reaches 0.21-0.25 Nm3/t/minute.

PT P
PN CN112725823-A; CN112725823-B
TI Method for coupling coal oxidation and carbon            dioxide reduction by efficient use of electric energy            combines the reduction of carbon dioxide at cathode and            oxidation of coal-water slurry at the anode comprises            e.g. crushing and centrifuging.
AU ZHANG Q
   FAN Y
   HE D
   GUAN J
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021474800
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (1) crushing coal sample                to 150 meshes, adding potassium hydroxide solution,                ultrasonically processing to obtain anode water                coal slurry with uniformly dispersed coal                particles, and potassium bicarbonate cathode                electrolyte, (2) adding cathode electrolyte and                anode water coal slurry into cathode and anode                electrolytic cell, and introducing quantitative                pure carbon dioxide into gas buffer tank through                gas inlet, (3) opening the water bath to set the                reaction temperature of cathode and anode, and                dissolving carbon dioxide in the catholyte, (4)                starting control current using two electrode system                to electrolyze in the reaction, and collecting the                anode gas by the gas collecting bag, and (5)                centrifuging and separating the anode water coal                slurry to obtain solid residual coal and filtrate,                and measuring the content of humic acid in residual                coal and filter liquid, and discharging the gas in                the gas buffer tank through gas outlet for                detection and separation.
   USE - Method for coupling coal oxidation and carbon                dioxide reduction by efficient use of electric                energy combines the reduction of carbon dioxide at                cathode and oxidation of coal-water slurry at the                anode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the cell voltage of the                electrolytic cell; improves the energy utilization                rate; increases the final concentration of gaseous                products; reduces the difficulty of separation;                greatly improves the stability of the silver                electrode for the cathode; cooperates with the                anode for long-term electrolysis; alleviates the                deposition of coal particles on the surface of the                anode; reduces the excessive oxidation of organic                matter in the coal to produce ineffective carbon                dioxide; and increases the production of humic                acid.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PT P
PN CN112126046-A
TI Carbon dioxide-based copolyester used for forming film and bag, is formed by connecting segments.
AU ZHANG Y
   XU Q
   JIN J
   JIANG Q
   ZHU Y
   CUI Y
   TANG J
AE SHANGHAI HUAFON NEW MATERIALS RES & DEV (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021016232
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide-based copolyester is formed by connecting a segment (A), a segment (B), and a segment (C).
   USE - Carbon dioxide-based copolyester used for forming film and bag (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based copolyester has low water vapor transmission rate and excellent water vapor barrier, melt strength during the polymerization process, viscosity, heat resistance, and mechanical properties and solves the problem of insufficient toughness of polycarbonate and significant reduction in molecular weight caused by trace hydroxyl impurities in the raw material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide-based copolyester is formed by connecting a segment of formula (A), a segment of formula (B), and a segment of formula (C).
   p = 2-12;
   q = 2-12;
   a = 5-8;
   t = 2-12;
   b = 2-6;
   n+m = 2-30;
   Ar' = 6-30C aryl or heteroaryl (both optionally substituted by halo, hydroxy, carboxy, nitro, 1-10C alkyl or 6-30C aryl);and
   R = 1-10C alkyl or 6-30C aryl (both optionally substituted by halo, hydroxy, carboxy, nitro, 1-10C alkyl or 6-30C aryl).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of carbon dioxide-based copolyester, which involves (i) subjecting aliphatic dibasic acid and aliphatic diol (D1) to esterification reaction (R1) to obtain the linear aliphatic polyester prepolymer (a), in the presence of the esterification catalyst (C1), (ii) subjecting the aromatic dibasic acid or its esterified product and aliphatic diol (D2) to esterification reaction (R2) to obtain the aromatic polyester prepolymer (b), in the presence of the esterification catalyst (C2), and (iii) mixing the linear aliphatic polyester prepolymer (a), the aromatic polyester prepolymer (b) and carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate polyol (c), in the presence of polycondensation catalyst, and performing the transesterification reaction to obtain the prepolymer, and subjecting the prepolymer to prepolymerization reaction, a polycondensation reaction and viscosity increasing reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the aliphatic dibasic acid to the aliphatic diol (D1) is 1:(1.02-2.2). The aliphatic dibasic acid includes oxalic acid, malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid or azelaic acid. The aliphatic diol (D1) includes 2-8C alkyl diols, preferably ethylene glycol, 1,2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, neopentyl glycol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,7-heptanediol, or 1,8-octanediol. The molar ratio of the aromatic dibasic acid or its esterified product to the aliphatic diol (D2) is 1:(1.05-4.5). The aromatic dibasic acid or its esters include terephthalic acid, dimethyl terephthalate, isophthalic acid, phthalic acid, furandicarboxylic acid, 1,4-naphthalenedicarboxylic acid or 2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylic acid. The aliphatic diol (D2) includes 2-8C alkyl diols, preferably ethylene glycol, 1,2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, neopentyl glycol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,7-heptanediol or 1,8-octanediol. The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate polyol (c) is formed by polymerizing carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds. The epoxy compound includes ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1-butylene oxide, 2,3-butylene oxide, 2-methyl-1,2-butylene oxide, 1-epoxypentane, 2,3-epoxypentane, 2-methyl-1,2-epoxybutane, 3-methyl-1,2-epoxybutane, 1-epoxyhexane, 2,3-epoxyhexane, 3,4-epoxyhexane, 2-methyl-1,2-epoxy pentane, epoxycyclopentane, epoxycyclohexane, epoxycycloheptane, epoxycyclooctane, monoglycerides, diglycerides, triglycerides, epoxidized fatty acids, 1-24C esters of epoxidized fatty acids, or epoxy silanes. The epoxy silane includes 3-glycidyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane, 3-glycidyloxypropyltriethoxysilane or 3-glycidyloxypropyltrimethylpropoxysilane. Each esterification catalyst (C1) and (C2) include p-toluenesulfonic acid, m-toluenesulfonic acid, benzenesulfonic acid, tetraethoxytitanium, tetrapropoxytitanium, tetrabutyl titanate, tetraisopropyl titanate, titanium tetrahexoxide, titanium tetra(2-ethylhexyloxy), titanium tetraoctoxide, or titanium glycolate. The polycondensation catalyst in the step (iii) includes titanium dioxide, silica/titanium dioxide composite, titanium ethylene glycol, tetrabutyl titanate, tetraisopropyl titanate, lithium titanyl oxalate, antimony trioxide, antimony ethylene glycol, aluminum oxide, stannous chloride, stannous octoate, or zinc acetate. The stabilizer includes phosphoric acid, phosphorous acid, hypophosphorus acid, pyrophosphoric acid, trimethyl phosphate, triethyl phosphate, triphenyl phosphite, triphenyl phosphate, ammonium phosphite or ammonium dihydrogen phosphate. The content of phosphorus in the stabilizer is 10-100 ppm. The antioxidant comprises antioxidant 1010 (pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3',5'-di-tert-butyl-4'-hydroxyphenyl)propionate)), antioxidant 1076 (octadecyl 3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate), antioxidant B215 (3-(4-bromo-2-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)-2-methylquinazolin-4(3H)-one) or antioxidant B245 (bis (oxyethylene) bis-(3-(5-tert-butyl-4-hydroxy-m-tolyl)-propionate)). The added amount of the antioxidant is 0.1-1.5 %mass based on the copolyester.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The step (iii) further involves adding stabilizers and/or antioxidants. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-based copolyester is connected in the manner of -(A)-(C)-(B)-(C)- by segment (A), segment (B) and segment (C). The molar ratio of segments (A), (B) and (C) are 30-65%, 28-55% and 1-20%, respectively. The carbon dioxide-based copolyester is prepared by using raw materials comprising 30-65 mol% linear aliphatic polyester prepolymer (a), 28-55 mol% aromatic polyester prepolymer (b) and 1-20 mol% carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate polyol (c). The linear aliphatic polyester prepolymer (a) is a segment (A) terminated by a reactive group. The aromatic polyester prepolymer (b) is a reactive group-terminated segment (B). The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate polyol (c) is a segment (C) terminated by a reactive group. The reactive group includes a hydroxyl group, a carboxyl group or an amino group. Preferred Properties: The copolyester has weight average molecular weight of 50000-150000 g/mol, and molecular weight distribution of 1.5-5.8 and crystallization temperature of 5-120 degrees C. The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate polyol (c) has weight average molecular weight of 500-6000 g/mol. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the esterification reaction (R1) and esterification reaction (R2) are 150-260 degrees C; The time of the esterification reaction (R1) and the esterification reaction (R2) are 2-7 hours. In step (iii), the carbon dioxide-based copolyester is in a molten state. In the pre-shrinking reaction in the step (iii), the temperature is 200-240 degrees C, the pressure is 500-12000 Pa, the time is 0.5-5 hours, and the viscosity of the prepolymer obtained after the reaction is 0.15-0.6 dl/g. In the polycondensation reaction, the temperature is 220-280 degrees C, the pressure is 10-8000 Pa, the time is 0.5-8h, and the viscosity of the prepolymer obtained by the reaction is 1.0-1.6 dl/g. In the thickening reaction, the temperature is 220-280 degrees C, the pressure is 10-2000 Pa, and the time is 0.5-20 hours and viscosity of the prepolymer obtained by the reaction is 1.5-1.8 dl/g.
   Preferred Definitions:
   Ar' = phenylenyl, naphthylenyl, anthrylenyl or furanyl.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A02-A00A; A05-E02A; A05-E06B; A05-H01B; A10-D03; A10-D05; A12-P02; A12-S06

PN JP2020184972-A
TI Culture medium for culturing a cell, tissue, organ, regenerated tissue or regenerated organ under high concentration of carbon dioxide used for manufacturing e.g., protein comprises downstream product of carbamoyl phosphate synthetase II.
AU MATSUYAMA A
   OKURA K
   OKURA Y
AE REGENE PHARM KK (REGE-Non-standard)
GA 2020B4654L
AB    NOVELTY - A culture medium for culturing a cell, tissue, organ, regenerated tissue or regenerated organ under non-phsyiologically high concentration of carbon dioxide comprises downstream product of carbamoyl phosphate synthetase II.
   USE - A culture medium for culturing a cell, tissue, organ, regenerated tissue or regenerated organ under non-phsyiologically high concentration of carbon dioxide used for manufacturing protein, exosome or virus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The manufacture of substance is cost-saving and space-saving using the culture medium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) culturing a cell on conditions which are non-phsyiologically high concentration of carbon dioxide which involves cultivating cell, tissue, organ and regenerated tissue or organ;
   (2) preserving a cell, tissue, organ or regenerated organ or tissue which involves preserving cell in the culture medium;
   (3) conveying a cell, tissue, organ or regenerated organ or tissue which involves conveying cell, tissue, organ or regenerated organ or tissue in culture medium on conditions which are non-phsyiologically high concentration of carbon dioxide;
   (4) manufacturing cell, tissue, organ or regenerated organ or tissue which involves conveying cell, tissue, organ or regenerated organ or tissue which involves culturing cell, tissue, organ or regenerated organ or tissue by culture medium on conditions which are non-phsyiologically high concentration carbon dioxide;
   (5) manufacture of protein, exosome or virus which involves culturing cell, tissue, organ or regenerated tissue or organ by culture medium on conditions which are non-phsyiologically high concentration of carbon dioxide; and
   (6) composition for preparing culture medium for cultivating a cell, tissue, organ or regenerated tissue or organ comprising downstream product of carbamoyl phosphate synthetase II.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The downstream products of carbamoyl phosphate synthetase II are orotic acid, greater than or equal to 1 type pyrimidine nucleoside, and/or greater than or equal to 1 type pyrimidine base. The cell is a cell derived from mesenchymal tissue. The cell is chosen from fat-tissue origin multi-lineprogenitor cell (ADMPC), myltilineage differentiating stress enduring (MUSE) cell (SSEA-3 positive cell), cancer cell, cancer tissue, pluripotent stem cell, pluripotent stem cell derived cell, directory programming cell, lymphocyte, cutaneous squamous cell, hair root cell, vascular endothelial cell, endothelial precursor cell, intestinal tract epithelium skin (stem) cell, a vaccine production cell, a cell line, or gene transfer cell. Preferred Parameters: The culture medium does not contain bicarbonate ion. The condition is ambient condition.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The culture medium contains galactose as saccharide. Composition contains galactose as saccharide and does not contain bicarbonate.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN111365131-A; CN111365131-B
TI Combined power and cooling system driven by            exhaust heat of gas turbine, has ammonia absorption            refrigeration cycle system that recovers heat of carbon            dioxide in pipeline of recompressed supercritical            carbon dioxide power cycle system.
AU YU Z
   SU R
   FENG C
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202064041W
AB    NOVELTY - The system increases net electricity output,                which is supplemented by the cooling output, to                realize combined power and cooling, through the                coupling operation of the compression supercritical                carbon dioxide (CO2) power cycle subsystem, Karina                cycle subsystem and ammonia absorption                refrigeration cycle subsystem. The recompressed                supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle system is                configured to use the exhaust gas of a gas turbine                (GT) to drive a first turbine (Tur1) to generate                electricity. The Karina cycle subsystem is                configured to transfer the waste heat of the                exhaust gas of the gas turbine used by the                recompressed supercritical carbon dioxide power                cycle subsystem to a waste heat boiler to drive a                second turbine (Tur2) to generate electricity. The                ammonia absorption refrigeration cycle system is                configured to recover portion of the heat of carbon                dioxide in the main compression pipeline of the                recompressed supercritical carbon dioxide power                cycle system for refrigeration.
   USE - Combined power and cooling system driven by                exhaust heat of gas turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The problems of low power generation of the                combined power and cooling system and low                utilization rate of exhaust heat of the gas turbine                are solved and hence, the effects of high                generation power and high utilization rate of waste                heat of exhaust gas of the gas turbine are                achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a working                method of combined power and cooling system driven                by exhaust heat of gas turbine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                combined power and cooling system driven by exhaust                heat of gas turbine.
   Air compressor (AC)
   Combustion chamber (CC)
   Gas turbine (GT)
   Heater (H)
   Turbines (Tur1,Tur2)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P)); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M)); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

PN WO2020074784-A1; FI201805852-A; EP3864194-A1; EP3864194-A4
TI Method for recovering and using oxygen in            combustion device for generating energy, involves            forming oxygen from water, recovering oxygen and            conveying to combustion device for generating energy of            fuel by utilizing oxygen.
AU PIISPANEN A
   VAINIKKA P
AE SOLETAIR POWER OY (SOLE-Non-standard)
   SOLETAIR POWER OY (SOLE-Non-standard)
GA 2020297364
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering and using oxygen (6) in                combustion device utilizing oxygen involves forming                oxygen from water (3) recovered in connection with                the processing of recovered carbon dioxide (1), by                means of electrolysis, recovering the oxygen and                conveying to combustion device (8) utilizing oxygen                such as internal combustion engine, fuel cell or                turbine, and generating energy (11) of the fuel (9)                by utilizing oxygen.
   USE - Method for recovering and using oxygen in                combustion utilizing oxygen device such as internal                combustion engine, fuel cell or turbine for                generating energy for electricity, and recovering                carbon dioxide, processing the indoor air of a                building, producing hydrocarbons, recovering carbon                dioxide formed during combustion, property-specific                energy production system, and building complex                specific energy production system (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly and                enables energy-efficient recovering and using of                oxygen effectively reduces the emissions and                cleaner combustion achieved at the combustion stage                taking place by utilizing oxygen. The apparatus can                be implemented easily and economically, and easily                incorporated in connection with buildings and                building complexes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus, which comprises at least one carbon                dioxide processing device (2), in which recovered                carbon dioxide is processed to form a chemical                compound (4), at least one electrolyzer (8) for                forming oxygen of water by means of electrolysis in                connection with the processing of the recovered                carbon dioxide (1), and at least one combustion                device (8), to which the oxygen (6) formed in the                electrolysis is conveyed and in which the fuel (9)                is combusted in the presence of oxygen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                apparatus.
   Carbon dioxide (1)
   Processing device (2)
   Water (3)
   Hydrocarbon (4)
   Electrolyzer (5)
   Oxygen (6)
   Hydrogen (7)
   Combustion device (8)
   Fuel (9)
   Exhaust gases (10)
   Energy (11)
   Refrigerating machine (12)
   Gas (13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The oxygen is formed of                  water recovered in connection with the processing                  of carbon dioxide. The water (3) recovered at the                  carbon dioxide processing stage (2) is conveyed                  to electrolysis, in which oxygen and hydrogen (7)                  are formed of the water. The carbon dioxide is                  recovered from the air, a gas or a fluid. The                  carbon dioxide is conveyed to the carbon dioxide                  processing stage (2). The water is removed from                  the air, a gas or a fluid in connection with the                  recovery of carbon dioxide and the water is                  conveyed to electrolysis. The hydrogen is                  conveyed from the electrolysis to the carbon                  dioxide processing stage.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The exhaust gases (10)                  of internal combustion engine, fuel cell or                  turbine are conveyed to a refrigerating machine                  (12). The apparatus comprises at least one water                  supply device for supplying water from the carbon                  dioxide processing device to the electrolyzer and                  an oxygen supply device for conveying oxygen from                  the electrolyzer to the combustion device.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the carbon dioxide                  processing stage, carbon dioxide is processed                  with hydrogen (7) to form a chemical compound (4)                  which is hydrocarbon.

TI Apparatus for manufacturing lithium carbonate, has            nozzle that is provided at tip portion of lithium            hydroxide aqueous solution circulating unit and having            diameter gradually reduced from base end toward tip            portion.
AU SAKUMA Y
AE ASAKA RIKEN CO LTD (ASAK-Non-standard)
   ASAKA RIKEN CO LTD (ASAK-Non-standard)
   ASAKA RIKEN CO LTD (ASAK-Non-standard)
   ASAKA RIKEN CO LTD (ASAK-Non-standard)
   ASAKA RIKEN CO LTD (ASAK-Non-standard)
GA 2019515091
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has a sealed reaction tank                (2) that stores an aqueous solution of lithium                hydroxide. A lithium hydroxide aqueous solution                supply unit (3) supplies the lithium hydroxide                aqueous solution to the reaction vessel. A carbon                dioxide gas supply unit (4) supplies carbon dioxide                gas to the space above the liquid surface of the                aqueous lithium hydroxide solution in the reaction                vessel. The lithium hydroxide aqueous solution is                stored in the reaction tank. The solution is                dropped into the lithium hydroxide aqueous solution                from above the liquid surface of the lithium                hydroxide aqueous solution in the reaction vessel.                A lithium hydroxide aqueous solution circulating                unit (21) is reacted with the lithium hydroxide                aqueous solution to produce lithium carbonate. A                nozzle is provided at a tip portion of the lithium                hydroxide aqueous solution circulating unit and                having a diameter gradually reduced from a base end                toward the tip portion.
   USE - Apparatus for manufacturing lithium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The lithium carbonate can be manufactured                efficiently, without wasting the carbon dioxide gas                which does not react with the lithium ion by                bubbling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The reaction vessel is provided with pressure                detection unit for detecting the pressure of carbon                dioxide stored in the reaction vessel. The carbon                dioxide gas supply unit is equipped with the on-off                valve opened and closed according to the pressure                of the carbon dioxide gas detected by pressure                detection unit. The reaction tank is equipped with                the pH detection unit to detect pH of lithium                hydroxide aqueous solution stored in reaction                tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                apparatus for manufacturing lithium                carbonate.
   Apparatus for manufacturing lithium carbonate                (1)
   Reaction tank (2)
   Lithium hydroxide aqueous solution supply                unit (3)
   Carbon dioxide gas supply unit (4)
   Lithium hydroxide aqueous solution                circulating unit (21)

TI Carbon-dioxide application apparatus for supplying carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas in greenhouse has drainage channel having drain pipe whose edge portion is connected to wastewater port of liquid storage tank and valve.
AU KATO H
AE FUTABA SANGYO KK (FUTS-C)
GA 201926185T
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-dioxide application apparatus for supplying carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas in a greenhouse comprises greater than or equal to 1 liquid storage tank (3) comprised so that liquid (L) containing compound may be stored, and combustion exhaust gas may pass through the inside of liquid, supply flow path comprised so that combustion exhaust gas that passes through the inside of liquid may be supplied to downstream side of liquid storage tank, and drainage channel (17) which discharges liquid out of the system and has drain pipe (18) by which edge portion is connected to wastewater port (3A) of greater than or equal to 1 liquid storage tank and on-off valve (19) attached to drain pipe and arranged so that communication starting point (19B) connecting primary side and secondary side initially in an open operation may be located upwards rather than the uppermost point of the wastewater port.
   USE - A carbon-dioxide application apparatus for supplying carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas in a greenhouse (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The by-product generated by the reaction between the liquid and the combustion exhaust gas accumulates at the communication start point of an on-off valve. As a result, insufficiency of a closed operation of an on-off valve is suppressed. Control of the liquid level in the liquid storage tank by the drainage channel is maintained, and entry of external air into the liquid storage tank is also suppressed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the drainage channel of the liquid storage tank in the carbon-dioxide application apparatus.
   Liquid storage tank (3)
   Wastewater port (3A)
   Drainage channel (17)
   Drain pipe (18)
   On-off valve (19)
   Communication starting point (19B)
   Liquid (L)
