TI Airflow bed device for humidifying and mineralizing carbon dioxide comprises e.g. feeding unit, gas inlet unit, reaction unit and separating circulating unit, and feeding unit comprising raw material humidifying machine, and feeding machine.
AU LIU R
   WANG X
   WANG Q
   HE Z
   XIAO T
AE CHINA HUANENG GROUP CO LTD (CHHN-C)
   HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2018976165
AB    NOVELTY - Airflow bed device comprises a feeding unit, a gas inlet unit, a reaction unit and a separating circulating unit, where the feeding unit comprises a raw material humidifying machine (1), a drier (2), a first spiral feeding machine (3) and a second feeding machine (4); and the gas inlet unit comprises a gas mixer (5). The reaction unit comprises an airflow bed reactor and furnace humidifying nozzles set on multiple positions of airflow bed reactor; and the separating circulating unit comprises a bag-type dust remover and a draught fan. The specific connection relation is: the outlet of the raw material humidifying machine is connected with the inlet of the upper end of the drier; and the outlet of the lower end of the drier is connected to the inlet of the first spiral feeding machine. The inlet of first spiral feeding machine is connected with inlet of venturi pipe at the bottom of the entrained-flow bed reactor; and the non-removed carbon dioxide smoke is connected with gas mixer.
   USE - The airflow bed device is for humidifying and mineralizing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The airflow bed device and method: can effectively improve the utilization of waste and can reduce discharge of carbon dioxide of electric power plant, which is a greenhouse gas emission reducing technology, and is very suitable for nation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for performing carbon dioxide humidification mineralization by using airflow bed device of carbon dioxide humidification mineralization.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the airflow bed device for humidifying and mineralizing carbon dioxide.

   Drier (2)
   First spiral feeding machine (3)
   Second spiral feeding machine (4)
   Gas mixer (5)

PT P
PN JP2018136069-A
TI Smart conditioner mounted in high-rise building, has low temperature heat exchanger which expands and vaporizes carbon dioxide gas liquefied with phase converter to cool heat medium.
AU YAMAUCHI T
AE YAMAUCHI T (YAMA-Individual)
GA 201868490H
AB    NOVELTY - The smart conditioner has compressor (8) which compresses carbon dioxide as coolant. The secondary function of high temperature heat exchanger (4) is made to release heat energy and carries out heat transfer to heat medium. A constant-pressure stabilizer (12) releases carbon dioxide of refrigerant deprived of thermal energy in the high-temperature heat exchanger at super-high pressure state. A phase converter (23) cools and liquefies the carbon dioxide refrigerant gas. A low temperature heat exchanger (5) expands and vaporizes the carbon dioxide gas liquefied with the phase converter to cool the heat medium. The low temperature carbon dioxide refrigerant gasified and expanded by low temperature heat exchanger is fed back to the compressor via phase converter. The carbon dioxide refrigerant received by low temperature heat exchanger is provided with residual heat including latent heat of liquefaction.
   USE - Smart conditioner mounted in high-rise building.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermal energy released into the atmosphere is eliminated, so that abandoned thermal energy is efficiently utilized. The heat generation process is performed during required time, so that peak of electricity usage is eliminated. The nighttime wind force is utilized effectively to save the energy, so that smart conditioner is efficiently used for summer time and winter time. The number of thermal storage tanks is adjusted, when smart conditioner is operated through winter and summer season. The cold heat leaks in the hot weather and warm heat leaks in the cold weather, so that generation of environment problem is prevented.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the smart conditioner.
   High temperature heat exchanger (4)
   Low temperature heat exchanger (5)
   Compressor (8)
   Constant-pressure stabilizer (12)
   Phase converter (23)

TI Method for manufacturing nitrogen and oxygen, involves depressurizing pressure in nitrogen deoxygenation tower to atmospheric pressure, when switching from nitrogen collection process to oxygen collection process.
AU 
AE TAIYO NIPPON SANSO CORP (NIIO-C)
GA 2018471485
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves removing the carbon dioxide contained in the air. The nitrogen is collected from a nitrogen deoxygenation tower (9A, 9B) by occluding the oxygen contained in the air. The oxygen is collected from the nitrogen deoxygenation tower by reducing the pressure. A carbon-dioxide removal tower (4A, 4B) is filled with the adsorption agent and adsorbs and desorbs the carbon dioxide in the air at room temperature. Some nitrogen collected. When switching from the nitrogen collection process to the oxygen collection process, the pressure in the nitrogen deoxygenation tower is depressurized to atmospheric pressure. The oxygen selection type occlusion agent is provided with the metal oxide which has oxygen non-stoichiometry and perovskite structure. The zeolite or activated alumina is used as the adsorption agent.
   USE - Method for manufacturing nitrogen and oxygen.
   ADVANTAGE - The highly purified nitrogen and oxygen are collected from air efficiently and the oxygen is provided using the oxygen selection type occlusion agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a manufacturing system of nitrogen and oxygen. The system (1) has a carbon dioxide scrubber compartment (2) from which the carbon dioxide contained in the air is removed. A nitrogen deoxygenation apparatus (3) isolates and collects the removed nitrogen and oxygen which is contained in the air. A heater (12) heats the oxygen selection type occlusion agent. A pressure reduction pump (13) reduces the pressure inside the nitrogen deoxygenation tower. The oxygen from the nitrogen deoxygenation tower is collected by reducing the pressure inside of the nitrogen deoxygenation tower by the pressure reduction pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of manufacturing system of nitrogen and oxygen.
   Manufacturing system of nitrogen and oxygen (1)
   Carbon dioxide scrubber compartment (2)
   Nitrogen deoxygenation apparatus (3)
   Carbon-dioxide removal tower (4A, 4B)
   Nitrogen deoxygenation tower (9A, 9B)
   Heater (12)
   Pressure reduction pump (13)

TI Culturing system comprises bioreactoradapted to propagate microalgae in culture solution using in combination with natural and artificial light, water conditioning assembly, algae nutrient supply, 1st controller, and carbon dioxide source.
AU AYERS A D
   EDWARDS M R
   HAGUE D
   THOMPSON R
   HAGER D
AE MYLAND CO LLC (MYLA-Non-standard)
   MYLAND CO LLC (MYLA-Non-standard)
   MYLAND CO LLC (MYLA-Non-standard)
GA 202079722B
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing system (1) comprises a bioreactor (6a,6b,6c) adapted to propagate microalgae in a culture solution using in combination at least one of natural and artificial light, and at least one nutrient comprising at least a carbon source, where the microalgae are freely suspended in and form portion of the culture solution; and a water conditioning assembly; and an algae nutrient supply coupled to the bioreactor; a first controller configured for controlling fluid flow between the water conditioning assembly and the bioreactor, the water conditioning assembly coupled as an input of supply water to the bioreactor, and configured to condition the supply water to a specified purity that enables substantially unhindered growth of the microalgae in the culture solution to a specified microalgae concentration; and a carbon dioxide source (5) coupled to the bioreactor. The carbon dioxide is injected into the culture solution as the carbon source.
   USE - A culturing system.
   ADVANTAGE - By using the system, inorganic agricultural chemicals can be used more efficiently after being converted into a bioavailable form by the algae; thus, the amount of chemicals needed is reduced. The system and its method of use can reduce total energy consumption, and/or reduce ecological pollution, and/or reduce greenhouse gas emission, and/or increase bioavailability of micronutrients and macronutrients, and/or reduce the use of chemical fertilizers, and/or reduce overall crop production cost, and/or reduce tillage cost, and/or reduce the need for and use of fungicides, herbicides and/or pesticides, and/or reduce soil compaction, and/or improve soil porosity, and/or increase microbial content of soil, and/or increase the organics content of soil, and/or reduce the amount of irrigation water needed to grow a crop, and/or reduce the occurrence of over fertilization, and/or reduce run-off and soil erosion, and/or improve plant characteristics and/or improve water/moisture retention by soil, all as compared to untreated crop and croplands.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method which involves preparing one or more microbe-containing samples from at least one location of a current or planned plant growth area; preparing at least one cultured sample by culturing microbes from the sample; selecting at least one target species of microbe from the cultured sample; propagating the selected target species of microbe to increase the concentration of the target species of microbe in the cultured sample by: providing a bioreactor adapted to propagate the selected target species in a culture solution, the selected target species being freely suspended in and forming portion of the culture solution; coupling a feed source to the bioreactor and a first controller for controlling flow between a water conditioning assembly and the bioreactor, the water conditioning assembly coupled as an input of supply water to the bioreactor to condition the supply water to a specified purity that enables substantially unhindered growth of the selected target species in the culture solution to a specified concentration, and where the first controller controls supply of the feed source to the bioreactor; and providing a carbon dioxide source coupled to the bioreactor, and regulating the release of carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide source to the bioreactor, where the carbon dioxide is injected into the culture solution as a carbon source enabling propagation of the selected target species of microbe; and
   (2) a method which involves sampling algal flora from an agricultural location; selecting at least one desired algae species for propagation from the algal flora, the desired algae species being present in the algal flora of the agricultural location at an initial concentration; propagating the desired algae species in at least one bioreactor; and delivering the desired species to the agricultural location to increase the concentration of the algae species to a concentration greater than the initial concentration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microalgae algae nutrient supply comprises at least one of a fertilizer, a macro-nutrient, a micro-nutrient, and at least two different microalgae species; and the macro-nutrient is selected from phosphorus, nitrogen, carbon, silicon, calcium salt, magnesium salt, sodium salt, potassium salt, and sulfur; and the micronutrients is selected from manganese, copper, zinc, cobalt, molybdenum, vitamins and trace elements; and the micro-nutrient comprises at least one of a vitamin and a mineral added to the conditioned supply water. The algae supply comprises at least one of a 1st algae type, and a 2nd algae type. Portion of the target species of microbe being delivered comprises at least one live microbe. The live microbe is an endemic species of algae to the delivery location. The live microbe is a live species selected to restore a normal soil flora mix of a cropland. The live species of algae is selected for its specific desired properties for improving the soil in the delivery location. Preferred Method: The method further involves using a 2nd controller coupled to a probe and the bioreactor, where the second controller regulates the release of carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide source to the bioreactor. It also involves delivering portion of the target species of microbe to portion of the location. It further involves positioning a solids filter upstream from the ozone contactor.

PN KR2074613-B1
TI Circulation filtration system useful in fish farming, comprises e.g. breeding tank for storing breeding water in which organisms are bred, drum screen for removing solids primarily from breeding water, and first pump for providing power.
AU CHO H R
AE CHO H R (CHOH-Individual)
GA 2020139868
AB    NOVELTY - Circulation filtration system (10) comprises e.g. a breeding tank (100) for storing breeding water in which organisms are bred; a drum screen (110) for removing solids primarily from breeding water discharged from breeding tank, and connected to breeding tank through a drain pipe; a first pump (120) for providing power to transport primary filtered breeding water from drum screen; a bio-filtration degassing apparatus (130) including a spray nozzle installed in a ceiling for spraying breeding water transported by first pump, a filter medium for degassing carbon dioxide in water by colliding with breeding water particles injected from spray nozzle, and a gas discharge port installed in the ceiling for discharging degassed carbon dioxide gas; a second pump for providing power to transfer degassed breeding water stored in storage space in bio-filtration degassing apparatus; and an oxygen dissolving device for receiving breeding water transferred from second pump to further supply oxygen.
   USE - The circulation filtration system is useful in fish farming.
   ADVANTAGE - The circulation filtration system removes various contaminants i.e. organic matter and solid generated in aquaculture system, and carbon dioxide from water, hydrogen sulfide, and protozoa and harmful bacteria and unnecessary floating microorganisms, reuses breeding water in circulating manner, minimizes discharge of pollutants to outside environment, performs nitrification process by nitrifying bacteria using biofilm of filter medium, raises pH of breeding water, prevents acidification of breeding water without use of other separate chemicals (i.e. bicarbonate), achieves stronger sterilization effect than chlorine, improves turbidity of breeding water by oxidatively decomposing fine organic/inorganic compounds passed through filter, reduces consumption of oxygen and production of carbon dioxide by microorganisms in breeding water, facilitates gas exchange of carbon dioxide and air, and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Circulation filtration system (10) comprises a breeding tank (100) for storing breeding water in which organisms are bred; a drum screen (110) for removing solids primarily from the breeding water discharged from the breeding tank, and connected to the breeding tank through a drain pipe; a first pump (120) for providing power to transport primary filtered breeding water from the drum screen; a bio-filtration degassing apparatus (130) including a spray nozzle installed in a ceiling for spraying the breeding water transported by the first pump, a filter medium for degassing carbon dioxide in water by colliding with the breeding water particles injected from the spray nozzle, and a gas discharge port installed in the ceiling for discharging the degassed carbon dioxide gas; a second pump for providing power to transfer degassed breeding water stored in the storage space in the bio-filtration degassing apparatus; an oxygen dissolving device for receiving the breeding water transferred from the second pump to further supply oxygen; an UV sterilizer for sterilizing the breeding water which is passed through the oxygen dissolving device using ozone lamp, and which is provided back to the breeding tank; and a biofilm which is formed on a surface of the filter medium to perform nitrification on breeding water. The filter medium has a circular structure having diameter of 48-50 mm, height of 12-15 mm, and a cylindrical support having hollow shape which is disposed at center of the filter medium. The support is arranged with multiple plates having a fan blade structure at equal intervals, and each multiple plate is formed obliquely inclined to have an angle of 60 degrees with respect to ground, and the breeding water sprayed through the spray nozzle where the area which hits the surface of the multiple plates is 65%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the circulation filtration system.
   Circulation filtration system (10)
   Breeding tank (100)
   Drum screen (110)
   First pump (120)
   Bio-filtration degassing apparatus (130)

PN CN109355136-A
TI Preparing tropaeolum essential oil comprises e.g. drying fresh tropaeolum flower in oven, pulverizing, loading into extraction column, compacting and assembling, turning on carbon dioxide pressure pump, heating and taking supernatant.
AU GAO X
   LIU T
   WANG Y
AE GUANGDONG DAPENG PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201919183J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tropaeolum essential oil comprises (i) placing the fresh tropaeolum flower picked in an oven, drying at 50 degrees C for 4 hours, and pulverizing to 10-40 mesh to obtain tropaeolum powder, (ii) loading the tropaeolum powder prepared in step (i) into the extraction column, compacting and assembling, (iii) first turn on the cooling water tower and the cold machine, turning on the carbon dioxide supercritical equipment, and stepping through the valves to open the gas circulation and exhausting the air, (iv) turning on the carbon dioxide pressure pump and adjusting the frequency of the pressure pump to 18-24 hz, (v) adjusting the extraction kettle discharge valve, and (vi) closing the carbon dioxide pressure pump, collecting the tropaeolum essential oil from the secondary analytical tank product discharge valve, heating and taking the supernatant.
   USE - The tropaeolum essential oil is useful for handmade soap (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The soap has good moisture performance, improves gloss of the skin and delays senility effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing tropaeolum essential oil comprises (i) placing the fresh tropaeolum flower picked in an oven, drying at 50 degrees C for 4 hours, and pulverizing to 10-40 mesh to obtain tropaeolum powder, (ii) loading the tropaeolum powder prepared in step (i) into the extraction column, compacting and assembling, (iii) first turn on the cooling water tower and the cold machine, turning on the carbon dioxide supercritical equipment, setting the temperature of the extraction tank water tank to 50-60 degrees C, the temperature of the secondary analytical tank water tank is 60-65 degrees C, and the temperature of the three-stage analytical reactor water tank is 30-40 degrees C and stepping through the valves to open the gas circulation and exhausting the air, (iv) turning on the carbon dioxide pressure pump and adjusting the frequency of the pressure pump to 18-24 hz, (v) adjusting the extraction kettle discharge valve, maintaining the pressure in the extraction kettle at 25-30 MPa, adjusting the discharge valve of the secondary analytical reactor, maintaining the pressure in the kettle at 6-10 MPa, adjusting the discharge valve of the three-stage analytical reactor, keeping the pressure inside the kettle is 5MPa or extracting for 2.5-3 hours, and (vi) closing the carbon dioxide pressure pump, collecting the tropaeolum essential oil from the secondary analytical tank product discharge valve, heating to 48-52 degrees C, adding 3-5% boiling water, quickly stirring for 10-20 minutes, warming to 78-80 degrees C, stop stirring, keeping warm for 5-8 hours, taking the supernatant and centrifuging at high speed for 12000 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes and taking the supernatant. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) tropaeolum essential oil obtained by the method as mentioned above;
   (2) tropaeolum essential oil in handmade soap comprises 3-5 pts. wt. sodium palmitate, 5-10 pts. wt. myristyl sodium, 8-15 pts. wt. sodium carboxymethyl starch, 10-20 pts. wt. glycerol, 2-6 pts. wt. antioxidant, 0.3-1 pts. wt. essence and 45-55 pts. wt. de-ionized water; and
   (3) preparing tropaeolum essential oil handmade soap comprising (a) mixing sodium palmitate, sodium myristate, sodium carboxymethyl starch, glycerin, antioxidant, flavor and deionized water, heating in a water bath at 70-75 degrees C until completely dissolved, stirring, cooling and incubating at 50-55 degrees C to prepare a mixture A, (b) adding the tropaeolum essential oil to the mixed liquid A and stirring uniformly to obtain a mixed liquid B, (c) pouring the mixed liquid B into the mold for curing for 12-24 hours, after the soap liquid is solidified, releasing the mold, and placing in a cool, ventilated and dry place for 3-5 days and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The soap comprises 4 pts. wt. sodium palmitate, 8 pts. wt. myristyl sodium, 12 pts. wt. sodium carboxymethyl starch, 15 pts. wt. glycerol, 6 pts. wt. antioxidant, 0.7 pts. wt. essence and 50.3 pts. wt. de-ionized water Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (i) placing the fresh tropaeolum flower picked in an oven, drying at 50 degrees C for 4 hours, and pulverizing to 30 mesh to obtain tropaeolum powder, (ii) loading the tropaeolum powder prepared in step (i) into the extraction column, compacting and assembling, (iii) first turn on the cooling water tower and the cold machine, turning on the carbon dioxide supercritical equipment, setting the temperature of the extraction tank water tank to 55 degrees C, the temperature of the secondary analytical tank water tank is 62 degrees C, and the temperature of the three-stage analytical reactor water tank is 35 degrees C and stepping through the valves to open the gas circulation and exhausting the air, (iv) turning on the carbon dioxide pressure pump and adjusting the frequency of the pressure pump to 20 hz, (v) adjusting the extraction kettle discharge valve, maintaining the pressure in the extraction kettle at 28 MPa, adjusting the discharge valve of the secondary analytical reactor, maintaining the pressure in the kettle at 8 MPa, adjusting the discharge valve of the three-stage analytical reactor, keeping the pressure inside the kettle is 4 MPa or extracting for 3 hours, and (vi) closing the carbon dioxide pressure pump, collecting the tropaeolum essential oil from the secondary analytical tank product discharge valve, heating to 50 degrees C, adding 4% boiling water, quickly stirring for 15 minutes, warming to 80 degrees C, stop stirring, keeping warm for 7 hours, taking the supernatant and centrifuging at high speed for 12000 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes and taking the supernatant. Preferred Components: The antioxidant is a combination of gallic acid and ginkgo flavonoid, and the weight ratio of the gallic acid to the ginkgo flavonoid is (0.2-0.6):1. The weight ratio of the gallic acid to the ginkgo flavonoid is 0.4:1.

TI Producing dimethyl ether using synthesis reactor, comprises producing dimethyl ether via direct synthesis by mixture of catalysts in synthesis gas reactor from improved lean gas, operating the synthesis gas reactor at a pressure.
AU GMBER-SCHMIDT J
AE GS GMBER-SCHMIDT (GSGM-Non-standard)
GA 201864822F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing dimethyl ether (DME) using a synthesis reactor, comprises producing dimethyl ether via direct synthesis by a mixture of catalysts in the synthesis gas reactor from the improved lean gas, operating the synthesis gas reactor at a pressure of 30-70 bar, preferably 50 bar. The synthesis gas reactor is operated at a temperature of 200-300 degrees C, preferably 250 degrees C. The synthesis gas contains a mixture of catalysts, for the synthesis of methanol from copper oxide, zinc oxide and aluminum oxide, and for dehydration the zeolite HSMZ-5. The product gas from the synthesis gas reactor is throttled to a pressure with the throttle controller to 10-25 bar, preferably 15 bar. The product gas from the synthesis gas reactor is cooled to a temperature of 25-50 degrees C, preferably 25 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for producing dimethyl ether.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures the zero Emission property, and ensures scalability in production performance and utilization of carbon dioxide and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing dimethyl ether (DME) using a synthesis reactor for lean gas comprising compressor, the carbon dioxide separator, the preheater, the mixer, regulator, synthesis reactor for synthesis gas, the throttle controller, the heat exchanger, carbon dioxide tank, the regulator, the mixer, the heat exchanger the mixer, the mixer, the regulator, the syngas reactor, the regulator, the heat exchanger, the compressor, the reforming reactor, the throttle controller, the heat exchanger, tank, comprises producing dimethyl ether via direct synthesis by a mixture of catalysts in the synthesis gas reactor from the improved lean gas, operating the synthesis gas reactor at a pressure of 30-70 bar, preferably 50 bar. The synthesis gas reactor is operated at a temperature of 200-300 degrees C, preferably 250 degrees C. The synthesis gas contains a mixture of catalysts, for the synthesis of methanol from copper oxide, zinc oxide and aluminum oxide, and for dehydration the zeolite HSMZ-5. The product gas from the synthesis gas reactor is throttled to a pressure with the throttle controller to 10-25 bar, preferably 15 bar. The product gas from the synthesis gas reactor is cooled to a temperature of 25-50 degrees C, preferably 25 degrees C. The product gas from the synthesis gas reactor via the heat exchanger is separated into a gas phase and liquid phase. The liquid phase of the product gas from the synthesis gas reactor has a mixture of dimethyl ether, methanol and water. The liquid phase of the product gas from the synthesis gas reactor is stored in the tank. The lean gas comprises a gas mixture of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen. The lean gas has a concentration of carbon dioxide, minimum 0 vol.%, preferably 12 vol.%. The lean gas accumulates at a pressure of at least 0.01 bar, preferably 0.25 bar. The lean gas has a temperature of 10-40 degrees C, preferably 25 degrees C. The lean gas is compressed via a compressor to a pressure of at least 30-70 bar, preferably 50 bar. The compressor is designed as a hydraulic linear piston compressor. The compressed lean gas is cooled to a temperature via the heat exchanger to -10 degrees C, preferably 5 degrees C. The carbon dioxide is separated from the lean gas at a pressure of 50 bar in the liquid phase. The reduced lean gas after the heat exchanger has a carbon dioxide concentration, at least 0 vol.%, preferably 0.1 vol.%. The reduced lean gas is heated to a temperature via the preheater, of 25-150 degrees C, preferably 100 degrees C. The hydrogen is added to the reduced lean gas via the mixing chamber to achieve a molar ratio of carbon monoxide to hydrogen of 1:0.8-1:1.25, preferably 1:1. The concentration moderately improved lean gas is passed to the mixing chamber via a volume flow regulator to control the volume flow, of 1-1000 Nm3/h, preferably 600 Nm3/h.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the reforming reactor the residual gas from the synthesis reactor after the heat exchangers is reformed by steam reforming to carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The steam for the reforming reactor from the process water recovered from the separation of the liquid phase from the synthesis reactors stored in the tank is generated. The process water is brought to an evaporation pressure with the pump of 30-70 bar, in the heat exchanger, the process water is evaporated, the process steam is supplied via the volumetric flow controller to the reforming reactor. In the reforming reactor, the mixture of water vapor and gas phases from the synthesis gas reactors is converted to a mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen with nickel-based catalyst. The pump, the dehydration reactor, the throttle controller, the condense, the volumetric flow controller. The methanol, which in the separation of the liquid phase from the heat exchanger, stored in the tank, in the streams dimethyl ether, methanol and process water. The methanol is brought to a pressure with the pump of 10-70 bar, preferably 15 bar. The methanol is fed via the pressure and volume flow regulator to the dehydration reactor, with 0.1-15 Nm3/h. In the dehydration reactor (58), the methanol (45) is converted into a gas mixture of dimethyl ether (DME), water vapor (H 2 O) and methanoi (MeOH) by means of a catalyst. The dehydration reactor is operated at a temperature of at least 200 degrees C. The gas mixture is fed via the throttle controller to the heat exchanger. The gas mixture is throttled to a pressure with the throttle controller of 10 bar, preferably 15 bar. The gas mixture is cooled in the heat exchanger to a temperature of 10-50 degrees C, preferably 25 degrees C and fed as liquid phase to the tank. The concentration measurement of the substance components including carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide of the lean gas after the separation of carbon dioxide are detected based on the measurement data of the transducers the proportion of hydrogen transducer supplied in the mixer is controlled. The concentration measurement of the substance components carbon dioxide, hydrogen of the synthesis gas from the heat exchanger are detected, the concentration measurement of the substance components carbon dioxide, hydrogen of the synthesis gas from the mixer are detected based on the measurement data of the encoder, the proportion of hydrogen encoder supplied in the mixer is controlled.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The inner wall of the steel bushing is in transitional engagement with the outer wall of the light guiding rod. The other end of the joint sliding rod-M20iA is connected to one end of the light input joint.

TI Stabilized aged polyisocyanurate and/or            polyurethane useful for thermal insulation and/or in            insulation panels, comprises insulation foam made at            specified isocyanate index.
AU JONCHERAY T J
   VANDENBROECK J
   CARILLO L
   DEDECKER K
   GEUMEZ G J
   GOMESS G J
AE HUNTSMAN INT LLC (HUTM-C)
   HUNTSMAN INT LLC (HUTM-C)
   HUNTSMAN INT LLC (HUTM-C)
GA 201993792Y
AB    NOVELTY - Stabilized aged polyisocyanurate (PIR)                comprises insulation foam made at an isocyanate                index of above 180 and/or polyurethane (PUR)                comprises insulation foam made at an isocyanate                index of 123-180, where the foam comprises at least                one physical blowing agent with a lambda gas of                less than or equal to 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C, at                least one carbon dioxide scavenging compound                including sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide                and/or reaction products of the carbon dioxide                scavenging compound with carbon dioxide and a gas                diffusion tight sealing to avoid exchange of air                between the foam and the environment and which                covers at least 50% of the foam surfaces, and the                molar% carbon dioxide in the stabilized aged foam                is 0-33% calculated on the total moles of carbon                dioxide and physical blowing agents in the                stabilized aged foam and the amount of residual                carbon dioxide scavenging compound in the                stabilized aged foam is 0-10 wt.% calculated on the                total weight of the stabilized aged foam.
   USE - The polyisocyanurate and/or polyurethane are                useful for thermal insulation and/or in insulation                panels (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyisocyanurate and/or polyurethane                exhibits improved long term insulation values when                used under diffusion tight conditions so that low                thermal conductivity (lambda value) is achieved                during the average economic lifetime of the                foam.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                making the stabilized aged PIR and/or PUR,                comprising (a) combining and/or mixing at least the                following ingredients at an isocyanate index of                123-180 for PUR comprising insulation foam and at                an isocyanate index of 180 for PIR comprising                insulation foam i.e. a polyisocyanate composition                comprising at least one polyisocyanate compound, at                least one catalyst compound for making PIR and/or                PUR comprising foam, an isocyanate-reactive                composition comprising at least one isocyanate                reactive compound, at least one physical blowing                agent having a lambda gas value of less than or                equal to 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C, and at least                one carbon dioxide scavenging compound including                sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide, (b)                covering at least 50% of the foam surfaces with a                gas diffusion tight sealing to avoid exchange of                air between the foam and the environment, and then                (c) ageing the foam to achieve a stabilized aged                foam, where the foam is covered with a gas                diffusion tight sealing and the content of carbon                dioxide scavenging compound is such that the molar%                carbon dioxide in the stabilized aged foam is 0-33%                calculated on the total moles of carbon dioxide and                physical blowing agents in the stabilized aged foam                and the amount of residual scavenging compound in                the stabilized aged foam is 0-10 wt.% calculated on                the total weight of the stabilized aged foam and                where the ratio of the molar amount of scavenging                compound (X) to be added over the theoretical                calculated molar content of carbon dioxide (X1)                generated by the reaction of the molar content of                water with isocyanate being present in the                formulation used to make the foam (X)/(X1) is                higher than 3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The molar% carbon                  dioxide in the stabilized aged foam is 0-30%,                  preferably 0-27% calculated on the total moles of                  carbon dioxide and physical blowing agents in the                  stabilized aged foam. The content of residual                  scavenging compound in the stabilized aged foam                  is 0-5 wt.%, preferably 0-3 wt.% calculated on                  the total weight of the stabilized aged foam. The                  molar% carbon dioxide in the stabilized aged foam                  is 0-33%, preferably 0-27%, calculated on the                  total moles of carbon dioxide and physical                  blowing agents in the stabilized aged foam and                  the content of residual scavenging compounds in                  the stabilized aged foam is 0-5 wt.%, preferably                  0-3 wt.% calculated on the total weight of the                  stabilized aged foam. The gas diffusion tight                  sealing is gas barrier polymeric resin layers                  i.e. ethylene vinyl alcohol copolymer resin                  layers and/or multilayers comprising the resin                  layers, where at least 90%, preferably 90-100% of                  the foam surfaces are covered with the gas                  diffusion tight sealing. The gas diffusion tight                  sealing is metal foils i.e. aluminum foil or                  metal multilayers comprising aluminum foil and                  where 50-95%, preferably 50-75% of the foam                  surfaces are covered with the gas diffusion tight                  sealing. The blowing agents comprise                  Hydrofluoroolefins (HFOs) blowing agents, HCFOs                  blowing agents and/or hydrocarbon blowing agents                  i.e. cyclopentane having a lambda gas value of                  less than or equal to 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C.                  The blowing agents comprise: cis                  1,1,1,4,4,4-hexafluorobut-2-ene and/or trans                  1-chloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene; and                  chlorofluorocarbons, hydrofluorocarbons and/or                  hydrochlorofluorocarbons having a lambda gas                  value of less than or equal to 12 mW/m.K at 10                  degrees C. In the method: the molar% carbon                  dioxide in the stabilized aged foam is 0-33%,                  preferably 0-27% calculated on the total moles of                  carbon dioxide and physical blowing agents in the                  stabilized aged foam and the amount of residual                  carbon dioxide scavenging compound in the                  stabilized aged foam is 0-5 (preferably 0-3) wt.%                  calculated on the total weight of the stabilized                  aged foam; the foam is PIR comprising insulation                  foam and the method is performed at an isocyanate                  index of greater than or equal to 180, preferably                  at an isocyanate index higher than 250 and the                  catalyst compound is at least one trimerisation                  catalyst and the insulation foam is PIR                  comprising foam; the foam is PIR comprising                  insulation foam and the method is performed at an                  isocyanate index of greater than or equal to 180,                  preferably at an isocyanate index higher than                  250, where the ratio of the molar content of                  scavenging compound (X) to be added over the                  theoretical calculated molar content of carbon                  dioxide (X1) generated by the reaction of the                  molar content of water with isocyanate being                  present in the formulation used to make the foam                  (X)/(X1) is higher than 6, preferably higher than                  15. The polyisocyanate compounds are a toluene                  diisocyanate, a methylene diphenyl diisocyanate                  or a polyisocyanate composition comprising a                  methylene diphenyl diisocyanate or a mixture of                  such polyisocyanates; the isocyanate reactive                  compounds comprise polyols and polyol mixtures                  having average hydroxy numbers of 50-1000,                  preferably 150-700 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and                  hydroxy functionalities of 2-8, preferably 3-8;                  and the blowing agent is present in a content of                  1-60 pts. wt., preferably 2-45 pts. wt. per 100                  pts. wt. isocyanate reactive compounds. Preferred                  Method: The method further comprises beside the                  blowing agents having a lambda gas value of less                  than or equal to 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C and                  additional blowing agents having a lambda gas                  value of greater than 12 mW/m.K at 10 degrees C,                  where the weight ratio of blowing agents having a                  lambda gas value of less than or equal to 12                  mW/m.K at 10 degrees C to the additional blowing                  agents is 95/5 up to 5/95 calculated on the total                  weight of all blowing agents.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparation of polydimethylsiloxane/polysulfone            ultra-thin composite membrane for gas-liquid two-phase            gas separation, involves dissolving polysulfonate in            N,N-dimethyl acetamide, and placing film in phase            deionized water soaking.
AU HE G
   DU B
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202299236D
AB    NOVELTY - Polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS)/polysulfone (PSF)                ultrathin composite membrane comprises a PDMS dense                separation layer and a PSF porous supporting base                membrane, where the thickness of the PDMS dense                separation layer in the PDMS/PSF ultra-thin                composite membrane can be adjusted to 0.4-5                &#181;m according to the needs, and the overall                thickness of the polydimethylsiloxane/polysulfone                ultrathin composite membrane can be adjusted to                100-150 &#181;m.
   USE - The PDMS/PSF ultra-thin composite membrane is                used in gas-liquid phase interface as a membrane                material for gas exchange (claimed), for separating                gas from liquid. Uses include but are not limited                to collecting hydrogen in synthetic ammonia tail                gas, enriching oxygen from air, separating                hydrogen, carbon monoxide and other gas from                petroleum cracking mixed gas, carbon dioxide                capture and recycling VOCs.
   ADVANTAGE - The inventive polydimethylsiloxane/polysulfone                ultra-thin composite film has high carbon                dioxide/oxygen selectivity, stable chemical                performance, and anti-pollution performance. It can                resist high temperature and high pressure, and has                great application potential. It is safe and                non-toxic, and can be directly contacted with                several kinds of solution or mixture, and it is not                easy to decompose under normal high-temperature and                high-pressure sterilization environment. It                prevents liquid leakage and gas bolt. It has strong                mechanical support of the base film effectively                solves the problem that the PDMS is not easily to                form film, and the presence of the coating                obviously improves the anti- pollution performance                of the polysulfon film, at the same time, it                realizes the good gas selectivity.

solvent, preparing the membrane solution, where the                mass ratio of PSF, PEG and DMAc in the membrane                solution is 3:2:15, stirring and dissolving at                65-75&#8451;, using a flat scraping machine and                a scraping knife to scrape the membrane, placing                the membrane after scraping in deionized water for                at least 24 hours, and finally phase-separating and                curing to form a PSF porous supporting base                membrane, preparing PDMS/PSF ultrathin composite                membrane by wet-film dip coating method, first,                preparing the coating liquid, taking 35% PDMS                toluene solution as the solute and isooctane as the                solvent, calculating the added mass of the two                according to the required PDMS concentration in the                coating liquid, stirring until completely                dissolved, and adding the crosslinking agent and                catalyst, where the mass ratio of crosslinking                agent to PDMS is 1:10, and the mass ratio of                catalyst to PDMS is 1:100, in order to prevent                local crosslinking, the crosslinking agent and                catalyst should be slowly added dropwise, pouring                the coating liquid into the tank after standing and                defoaming, and immersing the smooth side of the PSF                porous support base film into the coating liquid,                so that the coating liquid is evenly coated on the                surface of the PSF base film, and the film for 2-3                second, drying the coated membrane for 2 hours                under 85&#8451; of temperature, and flattening                to obtain a PDMS/PSF ultrathin composite                membrane.

TI Screening probe for inducing epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT) drugs for screening EMT-related drugs by inducing epithelial tumor cells to produce EMT side effects, is modified at anti-Epcam aptamer (SYL3C) and modified at end of aptamer SYL3C with 6-carboxyl fluorescence prime.
AU FENG F
   LIU W
   ZHENG F
   HAN C
   WANG Y
AE UNIV CHINA PHARM (UYCP-C)
GA 202255992L
AB    NOVELTY - Screening probe is modified at the 5` end of the anti-Epcam aptamer (SYL3C) and modified at the 3` end of the aptamer SYL3C with 6-carboxyl fluorescence prime, the formed probe tetraphenylethene (TPE)-SYL3C-carboxyfluorescein (FAM).
   USE - Screening probe for inducing epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT) drugs for screening EMT-related drugs by inducing epithelial tumor cells to produce EMT side effects, and chemosensitizers that antagonize the EMT process (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: 1. a method for preparing a screening probe, which involves utilizing a mixed solvent of water and dimethyl sulfoxide as a reaction solvent, and reacting H2N-SYL3C-FAM and (1- (4-carboxyphenyl)-1,2,2-triphenyl)ethylene N-hydroxysuccinimide ester to obtain the probe; and
   2. a method for screening EMT-related drugs, when EMT-related drugs are drugs that induce epithelial tumor cells to produce EMT side effects, includes: inoculating passable tumor cells in a black 96-well plate, placing in a cell incubator at 37degrees Celsius and containing 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, and discarding them, removing the culture medium, adding the drug to be tested with a concentration of 5 muM, incubating at 37degrees Celsius for 24 hours, washing with Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), adding the probe solution with a concentration of 10 nM prepared by PBS buffer, and incubating, utilizing PBS buffer as blank control group, measuring fluorescence intensity I445/I524 by multi-function microplate, measuring cell viability by (3-(4,5-dimethylthiazol-2-yl)-2,5 diphenyl tetrazolium bromide) (MTT) method using fluorescence intensity of drug to be tested and blank control group to normalize by MTT normalized fluorescence intensity of fluorescence intensity/cell viability, compared with the blank control group, if the MTT normalized fluorescence intensity of the test drug decreases, it means that the test drug induces epithelial-induced cancer cells to produce EMT transformation; when EMT-related drugs are chemosensitizers that antagonize the EMT process, include inoculating passable tumor cells in a black 96-well plate, placing in a cell incubator at 37degreesC and containing 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, and discarding, removing culture medium, starved cells with fetal bovine serum medium for 48 hours, washing with PBS, and continued to treat cells with fetal bovine serum medium containing 5 ng.mL-1TGF-beta1 and 1-10 muM of the chemosensitizer to be tested for 48 hours, and culturing cells for 48 hours in a medium without fetal bovine serum containing 5 ng.mL-1TGF-beta1 as a control group, discarding medium, washing with PBS, and adding probe solution with PBS buffer at a concentration of 10 nM, incubating, measuring the fluorescence intensity I445/I524 measured with a multifunctional microplate reader, the cell viability measured by MTT method, the fluorescence intensity of the chemosensitizer to be tested and the control group normalized by MTT normalized fluorescence intensity equals to fluorescence intensity/cell viability, compared with the control group, if the MTT normalized fluorescence intensity of the chemosensitizer to be tested increases, it means that the chemosensitizer to be tested antagonizes the EMT process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The screening probe is an amino group modified at the 5` end of the aptamer SYL3C, and a 6-carboxyfluorescein is used to obtain H2N-SYL3C-FAM, and tetraphenylethylene is modified at the 5`-terminal amino group of H2N-SYL3C-FAM to obtain the probe TPE-SYL3C-FAM.

TI Device for conversion of first hydrocarbon feedstock comprising aromatic compounds has fractionation train suitable for extracting benzene-comprising cut, one toluene-comprising cut and xylenes and ethylbenzene from hydrocarbon feedstock.
AU FEUGNET F
   JOLY J
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 2022459878
AB    NOVELTY - Device has a fractionation train suitable for extracting benzene-comprising cut, one toluene-comprising cut and one cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene from the first hydrocarbon feedstock. A xylene separation unit treats cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene and producing an extract comprising para-xylene and a raffinate comprising ortho-xylene, meta-xylene and ethylbenzene. An isomerization unit treats raffinate and producing an isomerate enriched in para-xylene which is sent to the fractionation train. A pyrolysis unit treats a second hydrocarbon feedstock and produces pyrolysis effluent comprising hydrocarbon compounds having 6-10 carbon atoms feeding. The first hydrocarbon feedstock and a pyrolysis gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen. A reverse water gas shift reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction section for treats the pyrolysis gas and produces an RWGS gas enriched in carbon monoxide and in water.
   USE - Device for the conversion of a first hydrocarbon feedstock comprising aromatic compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The device increases the production of aromatics, and produces a hydrogenolysis effluent enriched in methyl-substituted aromatic compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device has a fractionation train suitable for extracting benzene-comprising cut, one toluene-comprising cut and one cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene from the first hydrocarbon feedstock (2). A xylene separation unit treats cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene and producing an extract comprising para-xylene and a raffinate comprising ortho-xylene, meta-xylene and ethylbenzene. An isomerization unit treats raffinate and producing an isomerate enriched in para-xylene which is sent to the fractionation train. A pyrolysis unit treats a second hydrocarbon feedstock and produces pyrolysis effluent comprising hydrocarbon compounds having 6-10 carbon atoms feeding. The first hydrocarbon feedstock and a pyrolysis gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen. A reverse water gas shift reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction section for treats the pyrolysis gas and produces an RWGS gas enriched in carbon monoxide and in water. A fermentation reaction section treats RWGS gas enriched in carbon monoxide and in water, produces ethanol and recycles the ethanol to the inlet of the pyrolysis unit (13).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for conversion of a first hydrocarbon feedstock comprising aromatic compounds, which involves:
   (a) fractionating the first hydrocarbon feedstock in a fractionation train in order to extract benzene-comprising cut, one toluene-comprising cut and one cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene;
   (b) separating the cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene in a xylene separation unit and producing an extract comprising para-xylene and a raffinate comprising ortho-xylene, meta-xylene and ethylbenzene;
   (c) isomerizing the raffinate in an isomerization unit (11) and producing an isomerate enriched in para-xylene;
   (d) sending the isomerate enriched in para-xylene to the fractionation train;
   (e) treating a second hydrocarbon feedstock in a pyrolysis unit in order to produce pyrolysis effluent comprising hydrocarbon compounds having 6-10 carbon atoms feeding, the first hydrocarbon feedstock, and producing a pyrolysis gas;
   (f) treating RWGS gas enriched in carbon monoxide and in water in a fermentation reaction section in order to produce ethanol; and
   (g) recycling the ethanol to the inlet of the pyrolysis unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of first hydrocarbon feedstock device.
   First hydrocarbon feedstock (2)
   Transalkylation unit (8)
   Selective hydrogenolysis unit (9)
   Isomerization unit (11)
   Pyrolysis unit (13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The conversion device further comprises a makeup line for providing a supply of hydrogen to the pyrolysis gas. The fractionation train is suitable for extracting a C9-C10 monoaromatics cut from the first hydrocarbon feedstock. The device further comprises a transalkylation unit suitable for treating the C9-C10 monoaromatics cut with the toluene-comprising cut and producing xylenes which are sent to the fractionation train and selective hydrogenolysis unit suitable for treating the C9-C10 monoaromatics cut, hydrogenolysis effluent enriched in methyl-substituted aromatic compounds which is sent to the transalkylation unit. The conversion device further comprises a disproportionation unit suitable for treating the toluene-comprising cut and a xylene-enriched cut which is recycled to the isomerization unit. The reactor is operated under operating conditions of hourly space velocity (HSV) of 0.01 and 5 h-1, and temperature of 400-650 degreesC. THE reactor is operated under operating conditions of 500-800 degreesC, and pressure of 0 0.1 and 5 MPa.

PN CN113866344-A
TI Method for determining mass of alumina in an aluminum-based water treatment agent by potentiometric titration involves adding sample solution, hydrochloric acid solution, EDTA solution and acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution, boiling, and titrating with copper sulfate standard solution.
AU LU H
   CAI Z
   LIN H
   WU Z
   LIAO S
   HU D
   LU Y
   SHEN S
   SHI R
   YE T
AE ZHEJIANG FANGYUAN TEST GROUP CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202210573N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for determining mass of alumina in an aluminum-based water treatment agent by potentiometric titration involves (1) weighing 8 g liquid sample or 2.5 g solid sample, (2) dissolving the sample with water without carbon dioxide into 250 mL volumetric flask, diluting up to the volume, shaking well to obtain test solution (A), (3) pipetting out 10 mL of test solution (A) with a pipette, placing in 150 mL beaker, adding 2 mL of hydrochloric acid solution, boiling for 1 minute, pipetting out 20.00 mL of EDTA solution, adjusting pH to 4.0 with ammonia solution, adding 10 mL of acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution, boiling for 5 minutes, cooling, transferring to potentiometric titrator, (4) equipping the potentiometric titrator with copper ion selective electrode and a silver-silver chloride reference electrode, titrating with copper sulfate standard solution, and (5) measuring the content of alumina in mass fraction.
   USE - Method for determining mass of alumina in an aluminum-based water treatment agent by potentiometric titration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the detection accuracy, at the same time, it can realize the batch sample semi-automatic detection and improve the detection efficiency. The method uses copper electrode monitoring solution in electric potential change instead of the indicator color change in the original method to realize titration point determination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for determining mass of alumina in an aluminum-based water treatment agent by potentiometric titration involves (1) weighing 8 g liquid sample or 2.5 g solid sample, (2) dissolving with water without carbon dioxide into 250 mL volumetric flask, diluting to volume, shaking well to obtain test solution (A), (3) pipetting out 10 mL of test solution (A) with a pipette, placing in 150 mL beaker, adding 2 mL of hydrochloric acid solution, boiling for 1 minute, pipetting out 20.00 mL of EDTA solution, adjusting pH to 4.0 with ammonia solution, adding 10 mL of acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution, boiling for 5 minutes, cooling, transferring to a potentiometric burette, taking 15 mL of water to clean the beaker three times, transferring to potentiometric titrator, (4) equipping the potentiometric titrator with copper ion selective electrode and a silver-silver chloride reference electrode, setting the maximum single addition volume to 60 muL and potential evaluation level to 5 mV, titrating with copper sulfate standard solution, and repeating this step with blank, and (5) measuring the content of alumina in mass fraction. The content of alumina (w) is calculated by using formula: (((V'0/1000-V'/1000)cM/2)/mx(10/250))x100, where V'0 is the value of the volume of the copper sulfate standard titration solution consumed by the blank (mL), V' is the value of the volume of the copper sulfate standard titration solution consumed by the sample (mL), c is the exact value of the concentration of the copper sulfate standard titration solution (mol/L), m is the value of the mass of the sample (g), and M is a numerical value of the molar mass of alumina (101.96 g/mol).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the hydrochloric acid solution, the volume ratio of hydrochloric acid and water is 1:1. In the ammonia solution, the volume ratio of aqueous ammonia and water is 1:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The concentration of EDTA solution is 0.05 mol/L.

AB    NOVELTY - Increasing production of ethylene by utilizing ethane product of methanol to olefin by-product comprises mixing ethane product of methanol to olefin by-product with diluent gas and oxidant, pre-heating, generating ethylene-rich oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane reaction gas, sending into the ethane catalytic oxidation dehydrogenation gas-liquid separator to obtain gas-phase product and liquid-phase product I, pre-heating oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane product gas, sending to a deoxidizing device, reacting to obtain oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane deoxidizing product gas, cooling, performing physical absorption and/or chemical reaction to obtain oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane de-carbonation product gas and liquid phase product III and sending oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane decarbonation product gas into a methanol to olefin system, mixing with methanol to olefin product gas, entering a methanol to olefin oxygenate separation device and obtaining ethylene, products.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing production of ethylene by utilizing ethane product of methanol to olefin by-product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple; has low device investment and improves the yield of ethylene products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Increasing production of ethylene by utilizing ethane product of methanol to olefin by-product comprises (a) mixing the ethane product of methanol to olefin by-product with diluent gas and oxidant, sending to a ethane catalytic oxidation dehydrogenation reactor, pre-heating, generating ethylene-rich oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane reaction gas under the action of the catalyst, (b) sending the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane reaction gas obtained in step (a) into the ethane catalytic oxidation dehydrogenation gas-liquid separator after heat recovery to obtain gas-phase product and liquid-phase product I, sending the gas phase product to the bottom of the ethane catalytic oxidation dehydrogenation product separation tower and is in countercurrent contact with the coolant I and/or the absorbent passed from the middle and upper part of the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane product separation tower, obtaining oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane product gas at the top of the tower, obtaining liquid phase product II at the bottom of the tower, (c) pre-heating the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane product gas obtained in the step (b), sending to the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane deoxidizing device, reacting with the oxygen removing gas to obtain the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane deoxidizing product gas, (d) sending the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane de-oxygenation product gas obtained in step (c) to the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane de-carbon dioxide device, cooling, performing physical absorption and/or chemical reaction with the decarburization agent to obtain the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane de-carbonation product gas and liquid phase product III and (e) sending the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane decarbonation product gas obtained in step (d) into methanol to olefin product gas compressor of a methanol to olefin system, mixing with the methanol to olefin product gas, entering a methanol to olefin oxygenate separation device, a methanol to olefin alkali elution carbon dioxide device, a methanol to olefin product gas drying device and a methanol to olefin separation device and obtaining ethylene products, propylene products, ethane products and other products, where the ethane products are returned to the inlet of the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a) the diluent comprises methanol, nitrogen, water vapor, and/or carbon dioxide. In the step (a) the oxidant comprises pure oxygen and/or air. In the step (a) the ratio of ethane product to diluent gas and oxidant is 1:0.6-4.65:0.27-0.55. In the step (a) the active component of the catalyst is a transition metal oxide. In the step (b) the coolant I is water and/or methanol-containing water. In the step (b) the absorbent is water and/or methanol-containing water. In the step (c) the oxygen-removing gas reacted with the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane product gas is derived from carbon monoxide, ethylene or ethane in the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane product gas and part is derived from an external auxiliary oxygen-removing gas. The auxiliary oxygen- removing gas comprises carbon monoxide, hydrogen, methane, ethylene and/or ethane. The decarburizing agent comprises sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, organic amine solvent, potassium carbonate aqueous solution, sulfolane and alcohol amine aqueous solution, propylene carbonate, polyethylene glycol dimethyl ether and/or methanol.Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises evenly mixing the ethane product with the diluent gas and the oxidant and preheating to 150-340degrees Celsius. In the step (a) the reaction temperature in the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane reactor is 350-450degrees Celsius and the reaction pressure is 0.20-1.00 MPaG. The step (b) comprises cooling the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane reaction gas to 40-300degrees Celsius and sending to the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane gas-liquid separator. The step (b) comprises mixing the liquid-phase product I obtained from the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane gas-liquid separator and the liquid-phase product II obtained from the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane product separation tower to obtain a mixed liquid and sending the mixed liquid to the methanol to olefin feed vaporizer of the methanol to olefin system and sending as a raw material in the methanol to olefin reactor. The step (c) comprises heating oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane product gas to 100-230degrees Celsius and sending into the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane deoxidizing device. The step (d) comprises cooling the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane deoxygenation product gas to 40-50degrees Celsius and sending to the oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane de-carbon dioxide device.

AB    NOVELTY - Ammonia alcohol co-production method for                realizing low carbon discharge by using                photo-catalysis combined coal gas comprises (1)                generating hydrogen and oxygen by photocatalysis                hydrogen production device, dividing the hydrogen                into two strands, (2) entering of the crude                synthesis gas into the purifying device, passing                the purifying device through the heat recovery                cooling, removing the acidic gas to obtain the                purified gas, mixing the purified gas with the                hydrogen of the photo-catalytic hydrogen production                device, (3) sending the methanol synthesis gas to                the methanol synthesis device to produce the                methanol product, (4) mixing the hydrogen generated                by the photo-catalysis hydrogen production device                and the nitrogen generated to obtain the ammonia                synthesis gas, (5) sending the ammonia synthesis                gas to the synthetic ammonia device to produce                liquid ammonia product and (6) conveying the liquid                ammonia product to the downstream urea device, and                producing urea.
   USE - The method is useful for ammonia alcohol                co-production for realizing low carbon discharge by                using photo-catalysis combined coal gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the green production of                hydrogen through the comprehensive utilization of                photocatalytic hydrogen production; couples with                the traditional coal chemical production process;                eliminates the conversion device; avoids a large                amount of carbon emissions caused by hydrogen                production; improves the utilization rate of carbon                elements in coal, and realizes low-carbon                production of coal chemical industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ammonia alcohol co-production method for                realizing low carbon discharge by using                photo-catalysis combined coal gas comprises (1)                generating hydrogen and oxygen by photocatalysis                hydrogen production device, dividing the hydrogen                into two strands, sending one strand to the inlet                of the ammonia synthesis device and the other                strand to the outlet of the purifying device,                mixing the oxygen and the oxygen generated by the                air separation device, sending to the coal                gasification device, where the gasification                reaction with the coal to obtain crude synthesis                gas, (2) entering of the crude synthesis gas into                the purifying device, passing the purifying device                through the heat recovery cooling, removing the                acidic gas to obtain the purified gas, sending the                separated carbon dioxide gas to the urea device for                synthesizing urea, mixing the purified gas with the                hydrogen of the photo-catalytic hydrogen production                device, obtaining the methanol synthesis gas with                the mol ratio of hydrogen to carbon of 2.05-2.15,                (3) sending the methanol synthesis gas to the                methanol synthesis device to produce the methanol                product, (4) mixing the hydrogen generated by the                photo-catalysis hydrogen production device and the                nitrogen generated by the air separation device to                obtain the ammonia synthesis gas with the                hydrogen-nitrogen mol ratio of 2.5-3.5, (5) sending                the ammonia synthesis gas to the synthetic ammonia                device to produce liquid ammonia product and (6)                conveying the liquid ammonia product to the                downstream urea device, and producing urea together                with the carbon dioxide gas generated by the                purifying device.

PN CN113845950-A
TI Device useful for producing methane by catalytic gasification of oxygen carrier coal, comprises throat structure whose lower end is connected with top of oxidation gasification section and upper end is communicated with bottom of pyrolysis methane section, and gasifier furnace separating unit.
AU LI Y
   XU J
   ZHONG S
   JIN W
AE SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202209850S
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises gasification furnace comprising oxidation gasification section, throat structure and pyrolysis methane section that are communicated in sequence. The oxidation gasification section is provided with oxidant inlet, semi-coke return inlet, oxidation carrier feeding port and slag discharging outlet. The lower end of the throat structure is connected with the top of the oxidation gasification section, and the upper end of the throat structure is communicated with bottom of pyrolysis methane section. The pyrolysis methane section is provided with gasification furnace outlet, raw material inlet and first hydrogen inlet. A gasifier furnace separating unit comprising first cyclone separator, second cyclone separator and cooling and purifying unit. The inlet of the first cyclone separator is connected with the outlet of the gasification furnace through pipeline. The first cyclone separator is connected with the hydrogen production furnace through the first conveying device.
   USE - The device is useful for producing methane by catalytic gasification of oxygen carrier coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: realizes cyclic regeneration of the oxidation carrier; balances the heat flow and the material flow; improves the gasification intensity, carbon conversion rate and methane yield; and can reach the carbon conversion rate at the outlet to 98%, and outlet methane content to 70%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing methane by catalytic gasification of oxygen carrier coal comprises adding semi-coke, oxidizing carrier and oxidant to the oxidation gasification section at the lower part of the gasifier and mixing and contacting, reacting to generate synthesis gas, reducing carrier and coarse slag, where the synthesis gas comprises carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, entering the synthesis gas and oxidizing carrier produced in the oxidation gasification section to the pyrolysis methane section in the upper part of the gasifier through the throat structure in the middle of the gasifier, pre-mixing with part of the high-purity hydrogen added to the throat structure, mixing and contacting with the coal added to the pyrolysis methane section and the rest of the high-purity hydrogen, where the reaction produces methane-enriched synthesis gas, semi-coke, and reducing carrier, the methane-enriched synthesis gas comprises tar, methane, hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, discharging the mixture of methane-rich synthesis gas, semi-coke and reducing carrier from the gasifier enters the gasifier separation unit for separation, obtaining reduction carrier, semi-coke, tar and high-purity methane, separating the reducing carrier by the separation unit of the gasifier enters the hydrogen production furnace and reacting with water vapor to generate hydrogen-rich gas and oxidizing carrier, entering the hydrogen-rich gas to the cooling and separation unit of the hydrogen production furnace to remove moisture to obtain high-purity hydrogen, entering the high-purity hydrogen to the throat structure and the pyrolysis methane section as hydrogen source, producing the oxidized carrier by the hydrogen production furnace enters the oxidation gasification section for recycling, and obtaining the semi-coke by the separation unit of the gasifier enters the oxidation gasification section for reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the device for producing methane by catalytic gasification of oxygen carrier coal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidation carrier is alkali metal, transition metal and carrier. The alkali metal comprises potassium, sodium, lithium and/or cesium. The transition metal comprises iron, cobalt, nickel, copper and/or zinc. The carrier comprises aluminium oxide, zirconium dioxide, titanium dioxide and/or silicon dioxide. The alkali metal component accounts for 0.1-30 wt.% of the oxidation support, and the transition metal component accounts for 10-50 wt.% of the oxidation support.

TI Recycling anode material of waste lithium iron phosphate battery for electric automobile, comprises shredding, crushing, separating and recycling positive and negative components of battery, adding water to recycled battery anode powder, and preparing slurry.
AU MA T
   CHEN X
   LI F
   CHAI G
   LIU A
   LIU S
   CHEN D
   YI S
AE JINCHUAN GROUP CO LTD (JCJT-C)
GA 2021E7030S
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling anode material of waste lithium iron phosphate battery involves (i) adding raw materials into a shredder for shredding, (ii) tearing the battery into a special crusher for crushing, (iii) scattering the material into the collector through the draught fan, (iv) introducing the hydrogen peroxide into the liquid surface of the bottom liquid by the pipeline, and (v) spraying and adding alkali liquid and the impurity removing solution by the spraying device, adjusting the pH value of the solution to 3.3-4.0, completely oxidizing and aging for 5-7 hours after precipitating, obtaining ferric phosphate precipitate and lithium-containing solution by solid-liquid separation, adjusting pH to 10-11.5, evaporating and concentrating to the solution in lithium concentration, and introducing carbon dioxide into the filtrate at 90-100degrees Celsius to obtain precipitated lithium.
   USE - Method for recycling anode material of waste lithium iron phosphate battery used for electric automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and efficient for recycling anode material of waste lithium iron phosphate battery, which is easy for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling anode material of waste lithium iron phosphate battery, comprises (1) shredding raw material in a shredder, crushing the battery after tearing, scattering the battery inner positive and negative plates and diaphragm paper into a collector through draught fan, and collecting dust and purifying by pulse dust remover; entering the material in air flow separating sieve through air closing device; collecting the diaphragm paper in the positive and negative plates through air flow and vibration; at the same time, collecting the dust generated by air flow sorting machine; then crushing the mixture by hammer vibration; and separating and recycling the positive and negative components of the waste lithium battery by combined technique of vibration sieving and air flow separation; (2) adding water to the recycled battery anode powder, preparing 0.1-0.3 kg/L slurry, reacting the slurry with acidic solution for 2-4 hours, and adding alkali to adjust the pH value to 1.5-2.5; (3) obtaining the content of iron and phosphorus in solution according to step (2), and adding sodium phosphate to adjust the mass ratio of the iron phosphorus is 0.97-1.02: 1; (4) providing the reaction container with double-layer stirring blade, adding pure water or iron after the solution as the bottom liquid, stirring the bottom liquid at linear speed of not less than 4 m/s, heating to 50-70degrees Celsius, adding acid to adjust the pH value to 3.3-4.0, introducing hydrogen peroxide into the liquid surface of the bottom liquid by pipeline, spraying and adding alkali liquid and the impurity removing solution obtained in step (3) by spraying device; adjusting the pH value of the solution to 3.3-4.0, where the iron in the solution is completely oxidized, and aging for 5-7 hours after precipitating, obtaining ferric phosphate precipitate and lithium-containing solution by solid-liquid separation respectively; and (5) adjusting the pH value of the lithium-containing solution to 10.0-11.5, evaporating and concentrating the solution in lithium concentration of 12-15 g/L after solid-liquid separation to obtain the filtrate; and introducing carbon dioxide into the filtrate at 90-100degrees Celsius to obtain the precipitated lithium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali liquid in steps (2) and (4) is 100-200 g/L sodium hydroxide solution.
