GA 2021A80569
AB    NOVELTY - Simultaneous extraction of                Nannochloropsisprotein and dietary fiber by                ultrasonic wave comprises removing residual fat of                Nannochloropsispowder by supercritical carbon                dioxide (CO2), extracting, separating, and                obtaining defatted Nannochloropsispowder; breaking                wall by adding the defatted Nannochloropsispowder                into water at 1:10 mass ratio, and ultrasonically                wall-breaking for 20 minutes at energy-gathering                type ultrasonic wave frequency of 20 kHz and power                of 1000 W; double-enzyme enzymolysis by heating at                50&#176; C, adding neutral protease according                to the enzyme and substrate mass ratio of 3%,                controlling the pH, adding cellulase according to                the enzyme and substrate mass ratio of 3%, and                conducting ultrasonic auxiliary enzymolysis                reaction for 15 minutes at divergent ultrasonic                frequency of 40 kHz and power of 300 W; and                ultrasonically sterilizing and inactivating                enzyme.
   USE - The method is for simultaneous extraction of                Nannochloropsis protein and dietary fiber by                ultrasonic wave.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, high protein                and dietary fiber extraction rate, and low heavy                metal content, and enables to simultaneously obtain                the three products of Nannochloropsisprotein,                Nannochloropsissoluble dietary fiber, and                Nannochloropsisinsoluble dietary fiber in one                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Simultaneous extraction of                Nannochloropsisprotein and dietary fiber by                ultrasonic wave comprises removing residual fat of                Nannochloropsispowder by supercritical carbon                dioxide (CO2), extracting at 20 MPa and                37.5&#176; C, and separating at 4 MPa and                40&#176; C, where the extracting time is 2                hours, and obtaining defatted                Nannochloropsispowder; breaking wall by adding the                defatted Nannochloropsispowder into water at 1:10                mass ratio, and ultrasonically wall-breaking for 20                minutes at energy-gathering type ultrasonic wave                frequency of 20 kHz and power of 1000 W;                double-enzyme enzymolysis by heating at                50&#176; C, adding neutral protease according                to the enzyme and substrate mass ratio of 3%,                controlling the pH, adding cellulase according to                the enzyme and substrate mass ratio of 3%, and                conducting ultrasonic auxiliary enzymolysis                reaction for 15 minutes at divergent ultrasonic                frequency of 40 kHz and power of 300 W;                ultrasonically sterilizing for 10 minutes and                inactivating enzyme at energy-gathering type                ultrasonic frequency of 20 kHz and power of 2000 W;                solid-liquid separating and centrifugally                separating at 3000 revolutions/minute (rpm);                protein extracting to obtain supernatant, adding                ammonia water to adjust the pH at 11,                ultrasonically extracting for 30 minutes at                scattered ultrasonic frequency of 20 kHz and power                of 1200 W, centrifuging for 20 minutes to obtain                supernatant, adding 1 M organic acid mixture to                adjust the pH at 4.3, centrifuging at 3000 rpm to                obtain precipitates, washing and drying to obtain                Nannochloropsisprotein; separating soluble dietary                fiber by adding one-tenth volume of 95% ethanol to                the supernatant, centrifuging to obtain                precipitates, washing and drying to obtain soluble                dietary fiber; and removing heavy metal by adding                water to disperse according to the mass ratio of                1:3, adding 1 M organic acid mixture to adjust the                pH at 6, ultrasonically processing at 100 kHz and                300 W for 10 minutes, adding 1 M desalting agent                according to the volume ratio of 10%, and                ultrasonically processing at 100 kHz and 300 W for                10 minutes, centrifuging, and obtaining                precipitates i.e. insoluble dietary fiber.

AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon-doped indium sulfide porous microsphere                material comprises porous layered microsphere                structure, and is prepared by dissolving indium                chloride tetrahydrate and L-cysteine in an                triethylenetetramine aqueous solution, stirring,                then adding the mixed solution in a reaction                kettle, heating, naturally cooling, washing the                product, and drying to obtain organic-inorganic                hybrid, i.e. indium sulfide-triethylenetetramine                (InS-TETA), dissolving organic-inorganic hybrid                (InS-TETA) in deionized water to obtain                organic-inorganic hybrid (InS-TETA) aqueous                solution, placing the above aqueous solution in a                reactor for reacting, washing the product, and                drying to obtain carbon-doped indium sulfide porous                microsphere material (C-In2S3).
   USE - The porous microsphere material is useful in                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous microsphere material: has more                active sites and stable structure; and can                accelerate the transfer and transmission of                photo-generated charge carriers using doped                carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon-doped indium sulfide porous microsphere                material (C-In2S3) comprises porous layered                microsphere structure, where the diameter of                carbon-doped indium sulfide porous microsphere                material is 1-2 microns, the thickness of indium                sulfide layer is 0.13-0.35 nm, and the carbon-doped                indium sulfide porous microsphere material is                prepared by (a) dissolving indium chloride                tetrahydrate and L-cysteine in an                triethylenetetramine aqueous solution, stirring for                20-40 minutes, then adding the mixed solution in a                reaction kettle, heating at 150-200 degrees C for                20-30 hours, naturally cooling to 20-25 degrees C,                washing the product, and drying to obtain                organic-inorganic hybrid, i.e. indium                sulfide-triethylenetetramine (InS-TETA), where the                mass ratio of indium chloride tetrahydrate and                L-cysteine is 0.1-0.5:1, and the mass percentage of                triethylenetetramine in triethylenetetramine                aqueous solution is 30-35 wt.%, and (b) dissolving                organic-inorganic hybrid (InS-TETA) in deionized                water to obtain organic-inorganic hybrid (InS-TETA)                aqueous solution, placing the above aqueous                solution in a reactor for reacting at 160-200                degrees C for 8-12 hours, washing the product, and                drying to obtain carbon-doped indium sulfide porous                microsphere material (C-In2S3), where the mass                percentage of organic-inorganic hybrid (InS-TETA)                in organic-inorganic hybrid (InS-TETA) aqueous                solution is 0.05-0.12 wt.%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                also included for preparing the carbon-doped indium                sulfide porous microsphere material comprising (a)                dissolving indium chloride tetrahydrate and                L-cysteine in an triethylenetetramine aqueous                solution, stirring for 20-40 minutes, then adding                the mixed solution in a reaction kettle, heating at                150-200 degrees C for 20-30 hours, naturally                cooling to 20-25 degrees C, washing the product,                and drying to obtain organic-inorganic hybrid, i.e.                indium sulfide-triethylenetetramine (InS-TETA),                where the mass ratio of indium chloride                tetrahydrate and L-cysteine is 0.1-0.5:1, and the                mass percentage of triethylenetetramine in                triethylenetetramine aqueous solution is 30-35                wt.%, and (b) dissolving organic-inorganic hybrid                (InS-TETA) in deionized water to obtain                organic-inorganic hybrid (InS-TETA) aqueous                solution, placing the above aqueous solution in a                reactor for reacting at 160-200 degrees C for 8-12                hours, washing the product, and drying to obtain                carbon-doped indium sulfide porous microsphere                material (C-In2S3), where the mass percentage of                organic-inorganic hybrid (InS-TETA) in                organic-inorganic hybrid (InS-TETA) aqueous                solution is 0.05-0.12 wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  indium chloride tetrahydrate and L-cysteine is                  0.2-0.3:1. The method comprises adding above aqueous                  solution to a reaction kettle and reacting at                  160-180 degrees C for 10 hours.

PT P
PN CN113016503-A
TI Cultivation for Phellinus igniarius, by excavating cylindrical planting pits, sprinkling quicklime to planting pit, treating mulberry tree segments, inoculating Phellinus igniarius strain on medium, and adding nutrients to planting pits.
AU WU G
   PENG J
   LU Q
AE UNIV GUANGXI NORMAL NATIONALITIES (UYGU-Non-standard)
GA 202176815R
AB    NOVELTY - Wild cultivation method for Phellinus igniarius, involves excavating cylindrical planting pits, where the diameter and height of the planting pits are both 30-40 cm, sterilizing the planting pit, covering the planting pit completely, sprinkling quicklime into the planting pit, completely covering with a black plastic sheet in the planting pit, filling the planting pit with carbon dioxide, completely covering the planting pit with a black plastic sheet, spraying acetic acid in the planting pit for sterilization, treating mulberry tree segments, mixing raw materials, sterilizing, inoculating P.igniarius strain on the medium, adding mulberry tree section into the planting pit, with the top of the mulberry tree section facing up, covering with a black sheet of plastic board to send hyphae, changing to a semi-transparent plastic sheet to cover the planting pit, and adding nutrients to the planting pits.
   USE - The wild method is useful for cultivation of P.igniarius (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases taste and yield of P.igniarius, provides anti-insect and anti-bacterial effects, avoids formation of bacteria, improves quality, and shortens cultivation cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Wild cultivation method for Phellinus igniarius, involves excavating cylindrical planting pits, where the diameter and height of the planting pits are both 30-40 cm, sterilizing the planting pit, covering the planting pit completely with a black plastic sheet, maintaining at room temperature for 2-4 days, sprinkling quicklime into the planting pit, completely covering with a black plastic sheet in the planting pit for 2-4 days, filling the planting pit with carbon dioxide, completely covering the planting pit with a black plastic sheet, maintaining for 2-4 days, repeating the steps, spraying acetic acid in the planting pit for sterilization, treating mulberry tree segments, selecting mulberry tree with a diameter of 10-35 cm, obtaining mulberry tree section with a length of 25-35 cm, cutting shallow groove for placing the culture medium at position 5-10 cm from top of the mulberry tree section, maintaining mulberry tree section for 28-32 seconds, where the medium comprises 18-22 pts. wt. mulberry branch powder, 18-22 pts. wt. sweet corn powder, 4-6 pts. wt. Camellia leaf powder, 4-6 pts. wt. glutinous rice flour, 13-17 pts. wt. bagasse powder, 13-17 pts. wt. cottonseed hull powder, 4-6 pts. wt. pine needle powder, 0.4-0.6 pt. wt. star anise leaf powder, 4-6 pts. wt. agar powder and 8-12 pts. wt. water, mixing raw materials, sterilizing to obtain the culture medium, attaching the medium on the top and shallow groove of the mulberry tree section, inoculating P.igniarius strain on the medium, adding mulberry tree section into the planting pit, with the top of the mulberry tree section facing up, covering with a black sheet of plastic board to send hyphae, waiting until the mycelium is full of the medium surface, changing to a semi-transparent plastic sheet to cover the planting pit, and adding nutrients to the planting pits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The sterilization of the planting pit involves completely covering the planting pit with a black plastic sheet, maintaining at room temperature for 2-4 days, sprinkling quicklime into the planting pit, where the amount of quicklime in each planting pit is 10-25 g, covering the planting pit completely with a black plastic sheet, maintaining for 2-4 days, infusing carbon dioxide into the planting pit for 1-3 minutes, completely covering the planting pit with a black plastic sheet, maintaining for 2-4 days, and spraying acetic acid in the pit for sterilization, where the amount of acetic acid in each planting pit is 7-15 ml. The method involves adding mulberry tree section into boiling snapdragon and mulberry leaves, soaking in the mixed solution for 15-20 minutes, mixing 15-20 pts. wt. mulberry leaves and 350-400 pts. wt. water, crushing mulberry leaves to obtain a mixed juice, adding the mixed juice into the water and heating the water to obtain mulberry leaf mixed liquid. The method involves adding mulberry tree section into boiling mulberry leaves, soaking in the mixed liquid for 15-20 minutes, crushing mulberry leaves to obtain a mixed juice, adding the mixed juice into the water, and heating the water to obtain mulberry leaf mixed liquid. The height of the shallow groove for placing the culture medium is 1-1.5 cm, and the depth is 1-1.5 cm. The culture medium comprises 20 pts. wt. mulberry branch powder, 20 pts. wt. sweet corn powder, 5 pts. wt. Camellia leaf powder, 5 pts. wt. glutinous rice flour, 15 pts. wt. bagasse powder, 15 pts. wt. cottonseed hull powder, 5 pts. wt. pine needle powder, 0.5 pt. wt. star anise leaf powder, 5 pts. wt. agar powder and 10 pts. wt. water. The method involves controlling temperature of the planting pit at 22-24 degrees C after the mycelium is overgrown on the culture medium surface, and relative humidity of the air at 80-90%. The method involves replacing with a semi-transparent plastic sheet to cover the planting pits at 5-7 after the hyphae are overgrown with the culture medium surface, covering the planting pit with a polyethylene film with a thickness of 0.12-0.15 mm for 10-15 days, and covering the planting pit with a polyethylene film with a thickness of 0.075-0.080 mm for 4-6 days, where thickness of polyethylene film is 0.050-0.055 mm with planting pit. The method involves obtaining semi-transparent plastic sheet to cover the planting pits after the hyphae are overgrown on the culture medium surface, controlling the relative humidity of the air in the planting pit at 85.5-86.5%, covering the planting pit with a polyethylene film with a thickness of 0.12-0.15mm for 10-15 days, controlling the relative humidity of the air in the planting pit at 87-88%, covering planting pit with a polyethylene film with a thickness of 0.075-0.080 mm for 4-6 days, controlling the relative humidity of the air in the planting pit at 88-89%, covering with a polyethylene film with a thickness of 0.050-0.055 mm, and planting pits to maturity of P.linteus, when controlling the relative humidity of the air in the planting pits to 80-90%.

TI Device useful for concentrating carbon dioxide in flue gas, comprises a base and a washing cooling tower, where the side of the washing cooling tower is fixedly connected to the end surface of the base.
AU LING J
   WU W
   ZOU L
   YANG R
AE HANGZHOU JINGWANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2021709724
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a base (1) and a washing cooling tower (2), where the side of the washing cooling tower is fixedly connected to the end surface of the base, and the base top is fixedly connected with multiple adsorption towers (14). The adsorption tower is provided with a control valve inside, and the inside of the washing cooling tower is fixedly connected with a gas-liquid separation buffer tank (3). The bottom of the gas-liquid separation buffer tank is provided with a roots blower (4), the washing cooling tower is slidably connected with a baffle, and the baffle is provided with multiple communicating holes. The baffle is located directly below the roots blower, the washing and cooling tower is rotatably connected with a rotating rod, and the rotating rod is rotatably connected to the inside of the washing and cooling tower.
   USE - The device is useful for concentrating carbon dioxide in flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device improves the flue gas dust removal effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises a base (1) and a washing cooling tower (2), where the side of the washing cooling tower is fixedly connected to the end surface of the base, and the base top is fixedly connected with multiple adsorption towers (14). The adsorption tower is provided with a control valve inside, and the inside of the washing cooling tower is fixedly connected with a gas-liquid separation buffer tank (3). The bottom of the gas-liquid separation buffer tank is provided with a roots blower (4), the washing cooling tower is slidably connected with a baffle, and the baffle is provided with multiple communicating holes. The baffle is located directly below the roots blower, the washing and cooling tower is rotatably connected with a rotating rod, and the rotating rod is rotatably connected to the inside of the washing and cooling tower. Multiple water drains are rotatably connected to the rod through a rotating shaft, and multiple contact rods are fixed and sleeved on the rotating rod, and the washing cooling tower is fixed inside with a water spray pipe. The bottom of the water spray pipe is provided with multiple spray heads, and the top of the water spray tube is provided with multiple limit blocks. Multiple inclined plates are arranged inside the washing cooling tower, where a water tank is fixedly connected to the side of the washing cooling tower, and the limit block is far away from the spray pipe, where one end is located inside the communicating hole and slidably connected to the inner side wall of the communicating hole, and the end of the limiting block close to the communicating hole is provided with a limiting groove, so the inside of the adsorption tower is provided with a filter layer. The inside of the adsorption tower is provided with an adsorption tower bed, and the adsorption tower bed is rotatably connected by a rotating block. There is an adsorption chamber, and a fan refrigeration dryer arranged on the top of the gas-liquid separation buffer tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device.
   Base (1)
   Washing cooling tower (2)
   Gas-liquid separation buffer tank (3)
   Roots blower (4)
   Multiple adsorption towers (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: One end of the water spray pipe extends to the outside of the washing cooling tower and is connected to the water tank. A water pump is installed inside the water tank, one end of the water spray pipe located inside the water tank is fixedly connected with the water pump, and the top of the gas-liquid separation buffer tank is provided with a second air duct, the second air duct penetrates the washing cooling tower and is connected to one of the adsorption towers, and the top of the adsorption tower is provided with a vacuum pump. A second connecting pipe is connected through the side of the washing cooling tower, and the other end of the second connecting pipe extends to the inside of the water tank and is fixedly connected to the water pump. The end of the water drain is provided with a critical cavity, and a ball is rotatably connected to the inside of the critical cavity, the gas-liquid separation buffer tank is provided with a stainless steel wire mesh packing inside, and the side of the gas-liquid separation buffer tank is connected with a first connecting pipe through the side surface, and the first connecting pipe is connected through the side of the gas-liquid separation buffer tank. The end of the pipe away from the gas-liquid separation buffer tank extends to the inside of the water tank. The sloping plate is located directly below the corresponding nozzle, and the sloping plate is provided inside the groove, the obliquely downward end of the inclined plate is provided with a water outlet, and the water outlet is located directly above the outer corresponding critical cavity. The two corresponding adsorption towers are connected through a connecting pipe, and the adsorption chamber is equipped with characteristic adsorbent. Multiple water discharges are evenly arranged on a rotating shaft, and multiple rotating shafts are fixedly sleeved on the rotating rod. The limiting groove has a T-shaped design, and the three ends of the limiting groove are connected to the limiting block respectively. Through arrangement, the gas-liquid separation buffer tank is provided with a water collection chamber inside, and the side of the water collection chamber is provided with an automatic drain valve. The top of the base is fixedly connected with a gas buffer tank, and the top of the adsorption tower is connected through a first air duct, where one end of the first air duct away from the adsorption tower is in a through connection with the gas buffer tank, and the side of the scrubbing cooling tower is provided with smoke inlet tube.

PT P
PN CN112655855-A; CN112655855-B
TI Laba garlic lactic acid beverage is prepared by adding green tea to laba garlic, cutting, adding sodium alginate, dripping into calcium chloride solution, calcifying, adding skimmed milk, fermenting, adding honey, and homogenizing beverage.
AU ZHANG J
   LENG C
   LI X
   CHEN L
   XIN G
   YANG Y
   HUANG R
   YANG N
   ZHANG Q
   ZHANG Y
AE SDIC ZHONGLU FRUIT JUICE CO LTD (SDIC-C)
GA 2021515821
AB    NOVELTY - Laba garlic lactic acid beverage is prepared by adding cool green tea to laba garlic with crisp green color and full garlic cloves in a high-density carbon dioxide cutting container and cutting, uniformly mixing laba garlic juice with sodium alginate solution, gradually dripping the mixed solution into calcium chloride solution, calcifying, reacting the beads with chitosan solution, washing the film with physiological saline, then liquefying with sodium citrate, washing to obtain laba garlic sustained-release microcapsules, uniformly mixing with skimmed milk and white granulated sugar, inoculating Lactobacillus bulgaricus and Streptococcus thermophilus, fermenting to obtain fermentation broth, adding honey, stevioside, lemon juice and ginger juice to fermentation broth for flavoring and homogenizing to obtain laba garlic lactic acid beverage.
   USE - As laba garlic lactic acid beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage allows consumers to enjoy the special taste of garlic-flavored beverages according to their own preferences, without worrying about the garlic flavor affecting people around them. The method adopts the high-density CO2 inert enzyme cutting technology, realizes enzyme inactivation and tissue fragmentation under low temperature environment, tissue allicin degradation and allicin production without affecting the original taste, flavor and nutritional value of laba garlic, makes the concentration of sulfur-containing substances such as allicin in the fermentation broth less than 0.01%, which will not affect the fermentation of lactic acid bacteria, and increases types of secondary metabolites of the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Laba garlic lactic acid beverage is prepared by (a) selecting laba garlic with crisp green color and full garlic cloves, adding into a high-density carbon dioxide cutting container, adding cool green tea into the container, where the weight ratio of laba garlic to tea water is 1:1 and cutting at 25-27 degrees C and 15-18 MPa for 5-7 minutes to obtain laba garlic juice, (b) uniformly mixing laba garlic juice with 2.5 vol.% sodium alginate solution in a volume ratio of 1:5, evenly stirring the mixture, gradually dripping the mixed solution into 1.5 %mass calcium chloride solution, calcifying for 4-4.5 hours while stirring at 4 degrees C to obtain calcium alginate gel beads, reacting the beads with 0.5 %mass chitosan solution having pH of 6 for 40-50 minutes to form a film, washing the film with physiological saline, then liquefying with 0.055-0.06 mol/l sodium citrate for 20-30 minutes and washing to obtain laba garlic sustained-release microcapsules, (c) uniformly mixing skimmed milk, laba garlic sustained-release microcapsules and white granulated sugar at a mass ratio of 3:1:0.1, using 5% malic acid to adjust the pH of the mixture to 6-7, inoculating domesticated and activated Lactobacillus bulgaricus and Streptococcus thermophilus in a ratio of 2:1 and fermenting for 48-54 hours at 28 degrees C to obtain fermentation broth and (d) adding 35-38 pts. wt. honey, 35-38 pts. wt. stevioside, 1-2 pts. wt. lemon juice, and 12-15 pts. wt. ginger juice to every 10 pts. wt. fermentation broth obtained in step (c) for flavoring and homogenizing for 20-25 minutes at 20-25 MPa and 40-55 degrees C to obtain laba garlic lactic acid beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The laba garlic lactic acid beverage further comprises deodorizing protein capsules. Preferred Method: In step (a), the medium and high-density carbon dioxide cutting container includes a high-density carbon dioxide sterilization and inactivating enzyme equipment body and a cutter, and the high-density carbon dioxide sterilization and inactivating enzyme equipment includes a carbon dioxide gas cylinder, cold box, high-pressure pump, processing kettle, thermometer, filter plate, electric heating water bath circulation system and exhaust port, where a processing chamber is provided in the processing vessel, the upper and lower portions of the processing chamber are provided with filter plates, the filter plate is connected to the inner wall of the processing vessel, the processing chamber between the two filter plates can contain the materials to be processed, the processing vessel can be opened and sealed after putting the sample into it, an electric heating water bath circulation system is connected to the treatment kettle, the electric heating water bath circulation system can perform a water bath circulation operation on the processing chamber, a cutting knife is connected to the lower filter plate, the cutting knife can cut the material to be processed, and the cutting knife can be connected with the motor, the thermometer is connected to the treatment kettle, the thermometer can monitor the temperature of the water bath, the first valve is set above the filter plate, which is a safety valve, the carbon dioxide gas cylinder stores carbon dioxide, the carbon dioxide gas cylinder passes through the second valve, the first pressure gauge, the cold box, the high pressure pump, the third valve, the second pressure gauge, and the lower part of the filter plate, the processing chambers are closely connected and arranged, the carbon dioxide gas cylinder is also connected to the exhaust port through the second valve, the first pressure gauge, the cold box, the high pressure pump, the third valve, the second pressure gauge, and the fourth valve in turn, the fourth valve is the exhaust port valve, when the sample is crushed with high-density carbon dioxide, the exhaust valve is opened to exhaust gas through the exhaust port and when the pressure in the processing vessel reaches a safe pressure, the processing vessel is opened for sampling. The deodorizing protein capsules are prepared by (i) washing the spinach, removing the stems, squeezing a mixture of spinach, distilled water, 0.3-0.4% ascorbic acid, and 0.4-0.5% zinc acetate, where the mass ratio of spinach to distilled water is 1:50 and filtering the spinach juice through a 100-mesh sieve, (ii) uniformly mixing almonds, peanut kernels, ginger and distilled water in a mass ratio of 1:1:0.5:30, squeezing and filtering the protein juice through a 100-mesh sieve, (c) measuring 5-6 parts spinach juice and 5-6 pts. wt. protein juice, adding 0.5-0.7 pt. wt. calcium lactate and evenly stirring the mixture to obtain the core material of deodorizing protein capsules, (iv) taking sodium alginate, xanthan gum, acacia gum, guar gum, carrageenan, and gelatin, adding water to the wall material respectively, where the concentration of each substance is 2-2.25 %mass, extruding and dispersing to obtain wall material solution and (v) adding the core material to the wall material solution, where the wall material to core material are in a mass ratio of 5:1, stirring and mixing, allowing to stand for 12-15 minutes to remove the formed capsules, washing and shaping in clean water and naturally drying the capsules. When preparing the laba garlic lactic acid beverage, aseptically filling the deodorizing protein capsules with crisp green deodorant protein capsule particles into a special food-grade rubber bottle cap, filling the laba garlic lactic acid beverage aseptically into the packing bottle, and tightly and detachably setting the food-grade rubber bottle cap on the top of the packing bottle, where the food-grade rubber bottle cap includes a rubber bottle cap, bulge, sealed rubber-type loading bin and air and pressure sensitive holes, the bulge is formed on the top of the rubber bottle cap, several pressure sensitive holes are formed at intervals on the bottom of the rubber bottle cap and the pressure sensitive holes are automatically closed when no pressure is applied, when no pressure is applied to the pressure sensitive hole, it will open automatically, a sealed rubber-type loading bin is formed inside the rubber bottle cap, the top of the sealed rubber-type loading bin is connected with the inner space of the bulge, and the bottom of the sealed rubber-type loading bin is connected with the pressure sensitive hole, rhe upper portion of the sealed rubber-type loading bin is provided with air, the sealed rubber-type loading bin under the air is provided with crisp green deodorizing protein capsule particles, when the pressure sensitive hole is automatically opened, the crisp green deodorizing protein capsule particles can be removed from The pressure sensitive hole is released, when pressure is applied to the bulge from the outside of the bottle cap, the pressure-sensitive hole opens and the deodorant protein capsule is released into the beverage, when no pressure is applied, the hole is automatically closed, the number of times and strength of the bottle cap is different, the release amount of the deodorant protein capsule is different, during the beverage drinking process, adjust the protein capsule content in accordance with taste, where the sealed rubber-type loading bin is cylindrical.

PT P
PN CN111808674-A
TI Preparation of tea oil used for cosmetic by            removing Camellia oleifera seed shell to obtain            Camellia oleifera seed, vacuum drying, crushing,            supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and            deacidification.
AU HU X
AE UNIV GANNAN MEDICAL (UGAM-C)
GA 2020A67456
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of tea oil includes pretreatment                of tea seed by removing Camellia oleifera seed                shell to obtain Camellia oleifera seed, vacuum                drying, and crushing C. oleifera seed until                granularity is 300-400 mesh; supercritical carbon                dioxide (CO2) extraction by putting the crushed C.                oleifera seed in supercritical CO2 extraction                device, extracting at extracting kettle pressure of                20-27 MPa, 40-50 degrees C and CO2 flow rate of                8-12 L/hour for 1-2 hours, where separating kettle                pressure is 5-7 MPa and separating kettle                temperature is 40-50 degrees C; and deacidification                by pouring the tea oil extracted by supercritical                CO2 into alkali refining pot, sampling and                detecting the acid value of the oil, calculating                the adding amount of sodium hydroxide solution                according to the acid value, adding sodium                hydroxide solution into the crude oil, stirring for                20-40 minutes, and after centrifugal separation,                and transferring the tea oil to decoloring                kettle.
   USE - The method is for preparation of tea oil used                for cosmetic (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The tea oil has acid value of less than or                equal to 0.1 mg potassium hydroxide (KOH)/g,                peroxide value of less than or equal to 0.3                mmoL/kg, vitamin E value of greater than or equal                to 28 (mg/100 g), and no peculiar smell, is                colorless, and meets the cosmetic use standard. The                method has high yield, low energy consumption,                small environment pollution, and short production                period, and can keep the tea oil active                component.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of tea oil comprises pretreatment                of tea seed by removing C. oleifera seed shell to                obtain Camellia oleifera seed, vacuum drying, and                crushing C. oleifera seed until granularity is                300-400 mesh; supercritical CO2 extraction by                putting the crushed C. oleifera seed in                supercritical CO2 extraction device, extracting at                extracting kettle pressure of 20-27 MPa, 40-50                degrees C and CO2 flow rate of 8-12 L/hour for 1-2                hours, where separating kettle pressure is 5-7 MPa                and separating kettle temperature is 40-50 degrees                C; deacidification by pouring the tea oil extracted                by supercritical CO2 into alkali refining pot,                sampling and detecting the acid value of the oil,                calculating the adding amount of sodium hydroxide                solution according to the acid value, adding sodium                hydroxide solution into the crude oil, stirring for                20-40 minutes, and after centrifugal separation,                transferring the tea oil to decoloring kettle;                decoloring comprising after washing the tea oil in                decoloring kettle, heating at 55-70 degrees C,                adding hydrogen peroxide solution, stirring for                60-80 minutes, after centrifugal separation,                transferring the tea oil to water washing kettle,                and water washing at pH 7; flash evaporation,                deodorization, dehydration by heating the separated                tea oil at 98-110 degrees C, putting in flash tank                connected with vacuum tank, vacuumizing until the                vacuum degree in the vacuum tank is 1-3 kPa,                opening the connecting valve, so that the flash                tank immediately reaches the vacuum pressure                condition, instantaneously boiling the tea oil,                removing peculiar smell and residual water, and                continuously vacuumizing for 20-30 minutes to                finish the deodorization and dehydration; and                winterization comprising transferring the tea oil                after deodorizing to the cooling crystallizing                tank, stirring and cooling to 1-4 degrees C, adding                crystallization promoter, keeping the temperature                for 2-4 hours, and filtering to obtain the tea                oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sodium hydroxide is                  food grade and has a concentration of 8-15 wt.%,                  preferably 10 wt.%. The hydrogen peroxide                  solution is food grade and has a concentration of                  30-35%. The adding amount of hydrogen peroxide                  solution is 10-13 wt.% of the oil weight. The                  dropping speed of the hydrogen peroxide solution                  is 0.01-0.02 kg/minute. The stirring speed in the                  sixth step is 10-15 revolutions/minute (rpm),                  preferably 12 rpm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The vacuum drying is at                  45-55 degrees C and 1-3 kPa for 2-3 hours. The                  crystallization promoter in the sixth step is                  food-grade diatomite or hydroxystearic acid. It                  is added at 0.1-0.2 wt.% of the weight of oil.                  The pressure filtering pressure in the sixth step                  is 0.12-0.2 MPa.
DC D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC D08-B; E04-A; E11-Q01C; E11-W

TI Chemical plant used for manufacturing chemical            product, contains reforming section comprising            electrically heated reforming reactor and autothermal            reforming reactor, optional post processing unit and            water separation unit.
AU MORTENSEN P M
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2020855264
AB    NOVELTY - A chemical plant (100) comprises a reforming                section (110), an optional post processing unit                (112) which is arranged to receive the combined                synthesis gas stream (30) and provide a post                processed synthesis gas stream, a water separation                unit (114) which is arranged to separate the                combined synthesis gas stream or post processed                synthesis gas stream into a water condensate (27)                and an intermediate synthesis gas (34), and a                downstream section (116). The reforming section                comprises an electrically heated reforming reactor                (108), which is arranged for receiving portion                (25a) of feed gas and generating a synthesis gas                stream (30a), and an autothermal reforming reactor                (109) which is provided in parallel with reforming                reactor, and is arranged for receiving a portion                (25b) of feed gas and outputting a synthesis gas                stream (30b). The reforming section is arranged to                output a combined synthesis gas stream comprising                at least portion of streams (30a) and/or streams                (30b).
   USE - Chemical plant used for manufacturing chemical                product (claimed) such as methanol, ammonia and                mixture of higher hydrocarbons such as ethane and                propane.
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical plant has increased overall heat                efficiency and reduces the overall emission of                carbon dioxide and other emissions detrimental to                the climate, such as nitrogen oxide and sulfur                oxide, by minimizing the amount of hydrocarbons                used for providing heat for the reforming                reactions. Since the autothermal reforming re-actor                is provided with electrically heated reforming                reactor instead of steam methane reformer or gas                heated reforming reactor, the accumulated                generation of carbon dioxide is low. Since the                electrically heated reforming reactor is very                compact reactor, it can be fitted on to the same                piece of land as the existing chemical plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A chemical plant comprises a reforming section                which is arranged to receive a feed gas comprising                hydrocarbons and provide a combined synthesis gas                stream, an optional post processing unit downstream                the reforming section, which is arranged to receive                the combined synthesis gas stream and provide a                post processed synthesis gas stream, a water                separation unit which is arranged to separate the                combined synthesis gas stream or post processed                synthesis gas stream into a water condensate and an                intermediate synthesis gas, and a downstream                section which is arranged to receive the                intermediate synthesis gas and to process the                intermediate synthesis gas to a chemical product                (40) and an off-gas (45). The reforming section                comprises an electrically heated reforming reactor                housing a catalyst (C1), which is arranged for                receiving portion (25a) of feed gas and generating                a synthesis gas stream (30a), and an autothermal                reforming reactor which is provided in parallel                with electrically heated re-forming reactor, which                housing a catalyst (C2), and is arranged for                receiving a portion (25b) of feed gas and                outputting a synthesis gas stream (30b). The                reforming section is arranged to output a combined                synthesis gas stream comprising at least portion of                streams (30a) and/or streams (30b). An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for manufacture of chemical                product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                chemical plant.
   Portions of feed gas (25a, 25b)
   Condensate (27)
   Combined synthesis gas stream (30)
   Synthesis gas stream (30a, 30b)
   Intermediate synthesis gas (34)
   Chemical product (40)
   Off-gas (45)
   Chemical plant (100)
   Electrically heated reforming reactor                (108)
   Autothermal reforming reactor (109)
   Reforming section (110)
   Post processing unit (112)
   Water separation unit (114)
   Downstream section (116)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The downstream section                  comprises gas separation unit(s) which is                  arranged to separate a stream of pure carbon                  dioxide, hydrogen, and/or carbon monoxide from                  intermediate synthesis gas, thereby providing a                  refined synthesis gas. The downstream section                  comprises an ammonia reactor to convert said                  intermediate synthesis gas or refined synthesis                  gas to ammonia, a methanol reactor to convert                  inter mediate synthesis gas or refined synthesis                  gas to methanol, or a Fischer-Tropsch reactor to                  convert said intermediate synthesis gas or                  refined synthesis gas to a mixture of higher                  hydrocarbons.

TI Cutter-shaped contact fuse protection device, has spring whose top part is fixedly connected with bottom end of telescopic rod, and contact whose right side is extended from inner wall to outer wall through left side of fusion tube.
AU LI H
AE XIAN SURONG ELECTRIC APPLIANCE CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
   XIAN SURONG ELECTRIC APPLIANCE CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202067509D
AB    (CN210984677-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical field of fuse protection, and claims a cutter-shaped contact fuse with protection device, comprising a fusion tube, two sides of the melting pipe of the right side are respectively fixedly connected with two groups of groove block, the inner side of the two groups of the groove block is connected with the left side of two groups of auxiliary inserting rod is inserted, the two groups of the auxiliary inserting rod right side respectively face fixedly connected with the middle part of the two. the cutter-shaped contact fuse with protection device, set through the setting of the lock, and the main inserting rod matched with the sliding groove, the melting tube and reach the effect of mutually fixed, is matched to the first spring through set on the sliding rod, and a telescopic rod; effect to retract the lock head in the lock groove, which solves the problem that the dismounting and mounting is not convenient for most fuse condition, through the dry powder layer without the cooperation of the oxygen layer and the carbon layer, which solves the problem that the fuse can not stop in time when fire occurs.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural view of the knife-shaped contact fuse with protective device.
   Fusion tube (1)
   Groove block (2)
   Auxiliary inserting rod (3)
   Melting cover (4)
   Main inserting rod (5)
EA    NOVELTY - The fuse has two sets of groove blocks (2) that are connected to the front and rear sides of the fusion tube (1). The inner sides of the two groups of groove blocks are inserted into the left sides of the two groups of auxiliary inserting rods (3). The right sides of the two sets of auxiliary plungers are connected to the middle of the front and rear sides of the melting cover (4). The top of the melting cover is connected to the main inserting rod (5). The inner wall of the melting tube is connected to the outer surface of the dry powder layer. The inner side of the carbon dioxide layer is connected with the outer surface of the melt. Two sets of contacts are connected to the left and right sides of the melt. The contact on the left side of the melt extends through the left inner wall of the melt tube to the outside of the melt tube. The contact located on the right side of the melt extends through the left inner wall of the melt cover to the outside of the melt cover.
   USE - Knife-shaped contact fuse with protective device.
   ADVANTAGE - The fuse is convenient to disassemble and assemble. The fuse is stopped in time when a fire occurs. The melting tube and the melting cover achieve the effect of limiting and fixing. The sliding rod achieves the effect of automatic reset through the setting of the second spring and the coordinated setting of the limiting block and the limiting groove.

PN CN110566172-A
TI Method for cracking coal seam in high-pressure thermal power plant, involves taking gas at mouth of desulfurization tower, determining corresponding hole spacing parameters, inserting extraction pipe, and adsorbing nitrogen.
AU WU Y
   JIN Z
   DENG C
   HAN Q
   WANG X
   HAO C
   CHEN X
   LI Y
   WANG Y
   GAO T
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2019A66054
AB    NOVELTY - A coal seam in a high-pressure thermal power plant cracking method involves taking gas at the mouth of the desulfurization tower of the mine. The corresponding hole spacing parameters are determined according to the actual geological conditions of the coal seam before the mining of a coal seam, group of holes are drilled in the coal seam, and drill holes from the coal seam floor to the coal seam roof followed by selecting the middle drilling hole as the fracturing hole, inserting the fracturing extraction pipe, and drilling holes on both sides as grouting holes, inserting the grouting pipe, and injecting grouting through the grouting hole, injecting high pressure flue gas into the fracturing extraction pipe, adsorbing nitrogen and carbon dioxide on the coal pores and fractures, producing methane during the fracturing process in the fracturing stage, after the fracturing is completed, the manhole will be discharged transporting along the roadway to the mine.
   USE - Method for cracking coal seam in a high-pressure thermal power plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A coal seam in a high-pressure thermal power plant cracking method involves taking gas at the mouth of the desulfurization tower of the mine, where flow rate of the flue gas transported along the pipeline by the fan is 4000 m3/h. The corresponding hole spacing parameters are determined according to the actual geological conditions of the coal seam before the mining of a coal seam, group of holes are drilled in the coal seam in advance, and drill holes from the coal seam floor to the coal seam roof to ensure the drilling and coal seam level, where inclination angle between is greater than 30 degrees , end of the borehole pass through the roof of the coal seam and enter the rock layer above 0.5m. Return pipeline is drilled on the roof of the coal seam, after drilling construction is completed, followed by selecting the middle drilling hole as the fracturing hole, inserting the fracturing extraction pipe, and drilling holes on both sides as grouting holes, inserting the grouting pipe, and injecting grouting through the grouting hole to achieve drilling and sealing of coal bodies after sealing is completed, connecting fracturing hole to the compressor and the gas transportation pipeline, and valves, flow meters and pressure sensors added to the corresponding pipeline, injecting high pressure flue gas into the fracturing extraction pipe, where the pressure value of the high pressure flue gas is 30-50 MPa, when the pressure after injection is reduced to less than 25 MPa, the gas needs to be injected for more than 60 minutes, the temperature of the flue gas injection in the flue gas injection system is 40-50 degrees C, adsorbing nitrogen and carbon dioxide on the coal pores and fractures, producing methane during the fracturing process in the fracturing stage, after the fracturing is completed, the manhole will be discharged. After the manhole is completed, returing remaining flue gas, transporting along the roadway to the mine return air lane through the dedicated pipeline.

AE UNIV FOSHAN (UYFS-C)
GA 201997434N
AB    NOVELTY - Treating rural cattle and sheep breeding wastewater comprises (i) discharging the sewage from the livestock farm into the regulating tank, adjusting the pH of the wastewater to 9-10 with alkali solution; (ii) discharging sewage into the biogas tank, inoculating the sludge, adjusting the pH, carrying out fermentation in the biogas tank after the biogas production to obtain biogas slurry and sludge are obtained; (iii) adding anaerobic vaccine, facultative bacteria and licensed massagetherapist (LMT) treatment agent into the biogas digester, adsorbing and fermenting by anaerobic bacteria and facultative bacteria; (iv) crushing straw, immersing zeolite and the straw in a liquid obtained after one fermentation treatment to carry out closed fermentation and injecting oxygen into the closed fermentation chamber and fermenting for 20-30 hours; (v) carrying out fermentation to obtain a sticky substance is obtained, crushing sticky material after the composting treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for treating rural cattle and sheep breeding wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes manure treatment in the livestock and poultry industry, accelerates the reaction rate of each reactant in the biogas tank; and is efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating rural cattle and sheep breeding wastewater comprises (i) discharging the sewage from the livestock farm into the regulating tank, adjusting the pH of the wastewater to 9-10 with alkali solution, and adjusting the volume of the organic load to 9 kg cod/(m3.d) which will adjust the solid in the tank and discharging sewage after the precipitation of the substance into the anaerobic acidification tank; (ii) discharging sewage into the biogas tank, inoculating the sludge, adjusting the pH, carrying out fermentation in the biogas tank after the biogas production to obtain biogas slurry and sludge are obtained, subjecting to sedimentation, where the tail water still contains a large amount of organic and inorganic pollutants and testing the chemical oxygen demand and technetium concentrations in the sewage are sampled and tested; (iii) adding anaerobic vaccine, facultative bacterin and licensed massagetherapist (LMT) treatment agent into the biogas digester, adsorbing and fermenting by anaerobic bacteria and facultative bacteria, and stirring by a stirring device in methane working together to break down organic matter into methane and carbon dioxide, improving the B/C value of the wastewater by anaerobic treatment, the biodegradability of the wastewater, and generating methane and carbon dioxide to collect biogas through a gas collecting device; (iv) crushing straw, immersing zeolite and the straw in a liquid obtained after one fermentation treatment to carry out closed fermentation and injecting oxygen into the closed fermentation chamber and fermenting for 20-30 hours; (v) carrying out fermentation to obtain a sticky substance is obtained, where the sticky material and the straw are layered and stacked in turn for composting, the ratio of the thickness of the stick and thickness of the straw is 5:1-5:3, and the thickness of the stick is not greater than 10 cm, crushing sticky material after the composting treatment, and then adding water for aerobic fermentation to finally obtain the watering fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The LMT treatment agent is a composite microorganism composed of different proportions of digested and sub-digested bacteria including Thiobacillus, Bacillus subtilis, Bacillus licheniformis, Bacillus polymyxa, and Pseudomonas, and the microorganisms have mutual benefit relationship and different metabolic performance characteristics under different environmental conditions. The zeolite particles have a size of 0.2-1.2 mm and a zeolite addition amount of 10-15 g/l. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the method comprises injecting the oxygen into the fermentation chamber not greater than 5 times the volume of the sewage in the fermentation chamber for not greater than 2 hours. In the step (i), placing the stirring device on the side of the biogas tank, and pressing the fixing plate with heavy objects to fix the device, then rotating the threaded tube and the threaded tube on threaded rod, where surface moves up and down, the threaded tube drives the support rod on the surface thereof to move together and driving the working plate by support rod at one end thereof to move up and down and in the step (ii), adjusting the working plate to the appropriate position, immersing the stirring rod in the biogas tank, turning on the motor and driving output shaft of the motor to rotate and the stirring rod and stirring the liquid in the biogas tank.

PN CN110423720-A
TI Differentiating functional islet beta-cells useful in medicine preparation for treating diabetes, by trypsinizing second-third generation of amnion epithelial stem cells, terminating digestion, washing, culturing and observing cell state.
AU XIN H
   LI J
   LIU Q
AE UNIV NANCHANG (NANU-C)
GA 2019955386
AB    NOVELTY - Method for differentiating functional islet beta -cells by using human amniotic epithelial stem cells, involves (a) trypsinizing second-third generation of amnion epithelial stem cells with good growth state, when the cells are observed to be a single circle under the microscope, terminating digestion in Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM) containing 10% fetal bovine serum, and washing the resulting cell pellet once with phosphate buffered saline (PBS), and (b) inoculating the cell pellet into a 10 cm culture dish for induction culture at seed density of 1x 106 cells/dish, adding 10 ml differentiation induction medium I to each dish, then culturing in an incubator at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide and 95% humidity, after 2 days, discarding the induction medium I, replacing with 10 ml induction medium II, changing the solution every 2 days for 12 days, and observing the state of the cells to obtain insulin-secreting cells having islet beta -cell function.
   USE - The method is useful for differentiating functional islet beta -cells which is used in the preparation of medicine for treating diabetes (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method differentiates functional islet beta -cells which alleviates hyperglycemia in mice when transplanted into mice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for differentiating functional islet beta -cells by using human amniotic epithelial stem cells, involves (a) trypsinizing second-third generation of amnion epithelial stem cells with good growth state, when the cells are observed to be a single circle under the microscope, terminating digestion in Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM) containing 10% fetal bovine serum, and washing the resulting cell pellet once with phosphate buffered saline (PBS), and (b) inoculating the cell pellet into a 10 cm culture dish for induction culture at seed density of 1x 106 cells/dish, adding 10 ml differentiation induction medium I to each dish, then culturing in an incubator at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide and 95% humidity, after 2 days, discarding the induction medium I, replacing with 10 ml induction medium II, changing the solution every 2 days for 12 days, and observing the state of the cells under an inverted microscope every day for 14 days to obtain insulin-secreting cells having islet beta -cell function. The induction medium I is prepared by adding 4 ml 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS), 0.4 ml 100 mM non-essential amino acids, 0.4 ml 200 mM L-glutamine, 0.04 mu l 55 mM 2-mercaptoethanol and 0.4 ml double antibody to 34.32 ml DMDM medium, where the double antibody is 10000 U/ml benzylpenicillin sodium and 10000 mu g/ml streptomycin, 400 ng human epidermal growth factor, and 0.4 ml 1 M nicotinamide. The induction medium II is prepared by adding 4 ml FBS, 0.4 ml 100 mM non-essential amino acids, 0.4 ml 200 mM L-glutamine, 0.04 mu l 55 mM 2-mercaptoethanol, 0.4 ml double antibody and 0.4 ml 1 M nicotinamide into 34.36 ml DMDM medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The human amniotic epithelial stem cells are obtained by (i) collecting amniotic membrane by placing the amniotic membrane peeled from the placenta in PBS containing antibiotics, treating at room temperature for 1-2 hours, washing the amniotic membrane for 3 times with PBS, and transferring the amniotic membrane to a beaker containing fresh PBS each time, (ii) performing primary culture by transferring the amniotic membrane to a 50 ml centrifuge tube, adding 30 ml fresh pre-warmed trypsin/EDTA, incubating in a 37 degrees C water bath, shaking once every 10 minutes, after 40 minutes, terminating the digestion by adding 2 volumes DMEM medium containing 10% FBS, centrifuging the cell suspension to remove the supernatant, adding 7-8 ml amniotic epithelial stem cell culture medium, inoculating in a 10 cm cell culture dish, placing in the incubator at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide and 95% humidity, and (iii) performing cell subculture by once the primary cultured cells are grown to 70-80% confluence, performing trypsin digestion, by when the primary cultured cells are grown to 70-80% confluence, removing medium, then washing with PBS containing no calcium and magnesium ions, adding 1-2 ml trypsin, incubating in the incubator for 2-3 minutes, inactivating trypsin by adding 2-4 ml DMEM medium containing 10% FBS, gently blowing to separate the adherent cells into a single cell state, centrifuging to remove the supernatant, adding 3 ml amniotic epithelial stem cell universal medium, passaging, adding 7-8 ml amniotic epithelial stem cell universal medium to each 1 ml cell suspension, inoculating into a 10 cm cell culture dish, and culturing in the incubator at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide and 95% humidity. The amniotic epithelial stem cell culture medium is prepared by adding 430 ml DMEM-high glucose, 5 ml sodium benzoate at a concentration of 100 mM, 50 ml FBS and 5 ml non-essential amino acids at a concentration of 100 mM, in a sterile container, adding 5 ml 200 mM L-glutamine, 500 mu l 55 mM 2-mercaptoethanol, 5 ml double antibody, 10000 U/ml benzylpenicillin, 10000 mu g/ml streptomycin and 5 mu g human epidermal growth factor, mixing and storing at 4 degrees C. Preferred Components: The solution containing antibiotics comprises 20 ml Hank's balanced salt solution system, comprising 60-100 mu g/ml vancomycin, 150-300 mu g/ml cephalexin, 50-150 mu g/ml kanamycin, 2-3 mu g/ml amphotericin B and 300-500 units heparin sodium.
   ACTIVITY - Antidiabetic.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN110182832-A
TI Producing magnesium oxide for petroleum catalyst comprises adding water to anhydrous magnesium chloride to form magnesium chloride solution, adding small amount of magnesium sulfate, and filtering to remove calcium ions.
AU LIU L
   FENG W
AE XINGTAI MESSI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (XING-Non-standard)
GA 201976045G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing magnesium oxide for petroleum catalyst comprises adding water to anhydrous magnesium chloride to form magnesium chloride solution, adding small amount of magnesium sulfate, filtering to remove calcium ions, adding specific amount of barium chloride to the above solution, removing the sulfate ion by stirring and filtering, adding hydrogen peroxide to the above filtrate, stirring until homogeneous, adding hydrochloric acid to adjust the pH, adding small amount of hydrated basic magnesium carbonate and stirring until uniform, stirring the static precipitate, filtering to remove the iron, manganese and heavy metal impurities, adding extracting agent organic amine in the magnesium chloride solution with hydrogen ion in the acidic medium, forming ammonium salt into the organic dissolved phase with the acid, passing high-purity carbon dioxide into the magnesium chloride solution to form magnesium bicarbonate in the solution, adjusting the pH of magnesium bicarbonate to 7.5.
   USE - The method is useful for producing magnesium oxide for petroleum catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The magnesium oxide has magnesium oxide content more than 98%, calcium oxide content of less than 0.5%, iron content of less than 3 ppm, manganese content of less than 3 ppm, chloride content of less than 0.01%, sulfate content of less than 0.01%, has particle size of D50 less than 3 microns, D90 less than 12 microns, double peak of particle size distribution, hexahedron shape and citric acid value of 200-250 seconds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing magnesium oxide for petroleum catalyst comprises adding water to anhydrous magnesium chloride to form magnesium chloride solution, adding small amount of magnesium sulfate, filtering to remove calcium ions, adding specific amount of barium chloride to the above solution, removing the sulfate ion by stirring and filtering, adding hydrogen peroxide to the above filtrate, stirring until homogeneous, adding hydrochloric acid to adjust the pH, adding small amount of hydrated basic magnesium carbonate and stirring until uniform, stirring the static precipitate, filtering to remove the iron, manganese and heavy metal impurities, adding extracting agent organic amine in the magnesium chloride solution with hydrogen ion in the acidic medium, forming ammonium salt into the organic dissolved phase with the acid, passing high-purity carbon dioxide into the magnesium chloride solution to form magnesium bicarbonate in the solution, adjusting the pH of magnesium bicarbonate to 7.5, adding sodium thiosulfate to reduce F2+ to F2+ ions, adding hydrogen peroxide to oxidize F2+ to F3+ ions, precipitating for two hours, filtering to remove iron and manganese metal impurities, so that iron and manganese are reduced to less than 3ppm, subjecting the magnesium carbonate solution to low-temperature pyrolysis to form magnesium carbonate-magnesium hydroxide dihydrate (8MgCO3-Mg(OH)2-2H2O), ultrasonically assisted crushing, adjusting the ultrasonic frequency, the resulting precursor is magnesium carbonate-magnesium hydroxide dihydrate (8MgCO3-Mg(OH)2-2H2O), the particle size meets the requirements, and the morphology is hexahedron, washing the magnesium carbonate filter cake using deionized water for 4 times to remove chloride ions and sulfate ions, filtering and washing the solution, calcining at high temperature in lock kiln, controlling certain heating rate, pushing out the product to the lock kiln and cooling to normal temperature, so that the produced product has a citric acid value of 200-500 seconds and the magnesium oxide has an iodine value of 6-10 mgI2/gMgO, and obtaining catalyst-specific magnesium oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of the magnesium chloride solution is 2 mol/l, and the volume of the magnesium chloride solution is 1000 ml. The mass of the barium chloride added is 20 g. The hydrogen peroxide added is 1 ml, the hydrated basic magnesium carbonate is 2 g, the stirring time is 10 minutes, hydrochloric acid is added to adjust the pH to 3, and the precipitate is statically precipitated for 12 hours. The extracting agent organic amine is R3N.R = C7H10-C10H21.. The concentration of the organic amine is 50 vol.%. Preferred Method: The extraction is ion-assisted extraction, and phosphate ester is used as diluent, and an organic amine extraction system having a concentration of 50% is used. The magnesium carbonate solution is heated by steam. The heating temperature and pressure at the time of heating the steam are 50-60 degrees C and 5.5-6 kg/cm2, respectively. The high temperature calcination temperature is 1000 degrees C.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Flame retardant carbon nanotube-montmorillonite composite aerogel material, preprared by e.g. adding sodium dodecyl sulfate, hexabromocyclododecane to water, performing uniform ultrasonic vibration and adding carbon nanotubes.
AU LI H
   FU Y
   LIU H
   WEI D
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201962041Y
AB    NOVELTY - Flame retardant carbon nanotube-montmorillonite composite aerogel material, having an average specific surface area of 600-604 m2/g, nanoscale and microscale pores exist in both carbon nanotube-montmorillonite composite aerogel materials, nanoscale pores average of 10-11 nm and micron-scale pores average of 19-21 mu m, is claimed. The preparation comprises e.g. adding 0.6-12 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS), 0.01-18 pts. wt. hexabromocyclododecane to 100 pts. wt. water, performing uniform ultrasonic vibration, adding 0.88-12 pts. wt. carbon nanotubes, performing uniform ultrasonic vibration again, maintaining vacuum while vacuuming, returning to normal pressure and repeating the vacuuming step three times to obtain wet gel of step A; drying 0.09-12 pts. wt. montmorillonite, dissolving the montmorillonite dispersion with a solvent of 0.06-24 wt.%, uniformly stirring the montmorillonite dispersion and then ultrasonically obtaining wet gel B; and stirring wet gel A and wet gel B.
   USE - Used as flame retardant carbon nanotube-montmorillonite composite aerogel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material achieves synergistic flame retardant effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Flame retardant carbon nanotube-montmorillonite composite aerogel material, having an average specific surface area of 600-604 m2/g, nanoscale and microscale pores exist in both carbon nanotube-montmorillonite composite aerogel materials, nanoscale pores average of 10-11 nm and micron-scale pores average of 19-21 mu m, is claimed. The preparation comprises (a) adding 0.6-12 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS), 0.01-18 pts. wt. hexabromocyclododecane to 100 pts. wt. water, performing uniform ultrasonic vibration, adding 0.88-12 pts. wt. carbon nanotubes, performing uniform ultrasonic vibration again, maintaining vacuum while vacuuming, returning to normal pressure and repeating the vacuuming step three times to obtain the wet gel of step A; (b) drying 0.09-12 pts. wt. montmorillonite, dissolving the montmorillonite dispersion with a solvent of 0.06-24 wt.%, uniformly stirring the montmorillonite dispersion and then ultrasonically obtaining wet gel B; and (c) uniformly stirring 0.77-32 pts. wt. wet gel A, 0.8-30 pts. wt. wet gel B, 0.3-16 pts. wt. mixture of bisphenol bis(diphenyl phosphate) having a mass fraction of 0.05-18 wt.%, subjecting to ultrasonication and centrifuging to obtain product, placing the product into a carbon-dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction, subjecting the product to supercritical drying with carbon-dioxide as medium at a temperature of 10-300 degrees C and a pressure of 1-20 MPa for at least 1 hours, dispersing hexabromocyclododecane into a hollow structure of the carbon nanotube, where hollow structure of the carbon nanotube provides a microporous structure for the material, and supporting hexabromocyclododecane in the carbon nanotubes and forming 3D network structure between the carbon nanotubes, adding montmorillonite successfully constructed as a network pore structure, using the above 3D network structure and carbon nanotubes are successfully constructed as a network pore structure to form a 3D network pore structure 3D network pore structure provides a mesoporous structure for material, simultaneously bisphenol bis(diphenyl phosphate) is supported in the mesoporous structure. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the material as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (c), the supercritical drying time is 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours.

PN CN109845572-A
TI Growing baobab trees involves choosing land with sufficient sunshine, fertile soil, thick soil layer and convenient irrigation and transportation as planting place, selecting baobab seed with full flesh, soaking seeds in sulfuric acid.
AU SHI A
AE SHI A (SHIA-Individual)
GA 2019534865
AB    NOVELTY - Growing baobab trees involves choosing the land with sufficient sunshine, fertile soil, thick soil layer and convenient irrigation and transportation as the planting place, selecting the baobab seed with full flesh, soaking the selected seeds in 70-80% sulfuric acid solution for 6-12 hours. The white layer is exposed and taken out, after the pulp on the surface of the seed is corroded. The seeds are rinsed with clean water and dried indoors for one day. The dried seeds are then sown in a seedling container, and the seeds are sown in the soil at 3-5 cm to maintain the soil temperature at 15-18 degrees C. The sprouted baobab seed is covered with a blackout plastic cloth, after the seeds are germinated, and the germinated baobab seed is shaded for 7-10 days. The transparency of the shading plastic cloth is changed, so that the sprouted baobab seeds slowly adapt to the sun.
   USE - Method for growing baobab trees.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to grow baobab trees, which has low input cost, low harm to trees in harvesting, high yield and good yield, so that the cultivation of baobab trees is not limited to the tropical provinces, and the germination rate of baobab cultivation is increased by soaking seeds and drying indoors, which shortening the planting time, where proper pruning and harvesting time makes the baobab tree body not harmed, so that the quality of the fruit is good, the cellulose content in the leaves is high, and the heat emitted by the mushroom is used to warm the greenhouse and release a large amount of carbon dioxide, and increase in the concentration of carbon dioxide in the greenhouse not only reduces the cost of insulation in the greenhouse, but also promotes the photosynthesis of the baobab trees, and also increases the planting income of the growers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The 11-12 baobab seedlings are completely exposed to the sun, after the germination, and are watered once a day in the early morning and evening. The seedlings of the baobab tree grow to 30-50cm and grow 2-3 leaves to transplant the seedlings into the planting ground, after 3-4 months. The density of planting points is 10m/asterisk10m, the size of planting holes is 56-60cm, organic fertilizer is applied as base fertilizer, and water is poured once a day in the early morning and evening. The saplings are trimmed after winter leaves to control the tree shape, enhance germination and improve the quality of the leaves. The bark of the baobab tree to be matured becomes dark gray, and the trunk turns black to pick up its leaves. The fruit can be harvested, when the bark salt water turns pink or purple and the trunk turns red. A plastic greenhouse is build on the outside of the planting area, when the temperature is lower than 10 degrees C, wrapped black plastic cloth on the main pole of the baobab tree, built a mushroom stick fixing frame around the baobab tree in the built plastic greenhouse, and put the mushroom sticks in a ratio of 10-25 per square meter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Conditions: The seedlings can carry out 5-6 times of topdressing to promote growth and development every year, and stop fertilizing in winter. The topdressing is based on organic fertilizer, with appropriate amount of compound fertilizer.

PN CN109750392-A
TI Preparing pitch-based activated carbon fiber cloth comprises e.g. using asphalt fiber cloth as raw material, washing with distilled water, immersing in acetone, degreasing and dehydration, and drying asphalt fiber cloth at room temperature.
AU GAO J
AE SHAOXING YONGZE TEXTILE CO LTD (SHAO-Non-standard)
GA 201944461T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pitch-based activated carbon fiber cloth comprises e.g. (i) using asphalt fiber cloth as raw material, washing with distilled water, immersing in acetone for 3-5 hours, degreasing and dehydration, and drying the asphalt fiber cloth at room temperature, (ii) arranging pitch fiber obtained in the step (i) in oxygen atmosphere having initial temperature of 200 degrees C, heating to 400 degrees C at a temperature increase rate of 5 degrees C/minutes for 3-5 hours, removing out natural cooling, adding cooled asphalt fiber cloth into the phosphate solution, controlling the concentration of phosphate solution at 5-10 wt., where the phosphate solution is ammonium phosphate, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate and/or diammonium hydrogen phosphate, adding asphalt fiber cloth into phosphate solution for 4-8 hours, heating the phosphate solution at a temperature of 1 degrees C/minutes, controlling phosphate solution at a temperature of 110-125 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and removing out the asphalt fiber cloth for drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pitch-based activated carbon fiber cloth.
   ADVANTAGE - The fiber cloth is mainly uses mesopores and micropores, and has large specific surface area and high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing pitch-based activated carbon fiber cloth comprises (i) using asphalt fiber cloth as raw material, washing with distilled water, immersing in acetone for 3-5 hours for degreasing and dehydration, and drying the asphalt fiber cloth at room temperature, (ii) arranging pitch fiber obtained in the step (i) in oxygen atmosphere having initial temperature of 200 degrees C, heating to 400 degrees C at a temperature increase rate of 5 degrees C/minutes for 3-5 hours, removing out natural cooling, adding cooled asphalt fiber cloth into the phosphate solution, controlling the concentration of phosphate solution at 5-10 wt., where the phosphate solution is ammonium phosphate, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate and/or diammonium hydrogen phosphate, adding asphalt fiber cloth into phosphate solution for 4-8 hours, heating the phosphate solution at a temperature of 1 degrees C/minutes, controlling phosphate solution at a temperature of 110-125 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and removing out the asphalt fiber cloth for drying, where the drying temperature is controlled at 125-140 degrees C, (iii) introducing asphalt fiber cloth obtained in the step (ii) to carbonization equipment, introducing nitrogen gas into the carbonization equipment to temperature increase rate of 5-8 degrees C/minutes to 250-300 degrees C for 15 minutes, increasing the heating rate to 500-600 degrees C for 15 minutes, introducing carbon dioxide at a temperature rising rate of 5 degrees C/minutes to 750-850 degrees C for 15-20 minutes, stopping the carbon dioxide, passing water vapor and maintaining at a temperature increase rate of 8 degrees C/minutes to 900-950 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, and (iv) stop heating, stopping inletting water vapour, continuously maintaining nitrogen gas, naturally cooling to not less than 200 degrees C, removing off, air-cooling, washing to neutral and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The asphalt fiber cloth is woven fabric composed of asphalt fiber, and the weight of the asphalt fiber cloth is 300-1000 g/m.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)
