PN CN109699687-A
TI Preparing eco-friendly pyrethrin controlled release microspheres involves mixing magnetic nanoparticles with alkaline water, stirring and cleaning with ultrasonic waves, followed by suction filtration, and adding ethanol.
AU LI Y
   DAI J
AE SHAANXI MICROBIOLOGY INST (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201942432N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing eco-friendly pyrethrin controlled release microspheres involves mixing magnetic nanoparticles with alkaline water in 1:(10-25) volume ratio, stirring and cleaning with ultrasonic waves for 10-15 minutes, followed by suction filtration, adding ethanol to prepare magnetic nanoparticle solution having 14-18% magnetic nanoparticle concentration, dividing into 2 groups of magnetic nanoparticle solution A and magnetic nanoparticle solution B, adding 45-50 vol.% pyrethrin into the magnetic nanoparticle solution A, performing ultrasonic dispersion uniformly, dissolving eco-friendly polymer in the oil phase in 1:(2-4) ratio, and ultrasonically mixing to obtain an oil phase group, dissolving the magnetic magnetic nanoparticle solution B and emulsifier in an aqueous phase in (4-6):0.01 ratio, and stirring at a high speed of 6000-8000 rotations/minute to obtain an aqueous phase group, and placing oil phase group in paramagnetic field environment for clockwise stirring for 2-4 minutes.
   USE - Method used for preparing eco-friendly pyrethrin controlled release microspheres (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method offers product having uniform particles and long duration of action, can reduce the number of application times and total amount of application, prolong the application interval, and improve the utilization rate of pesticide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing eco-friendly pyrethrin controlled release microspheres involves mixing magnetic nanoparticles with alkaline water in 1:(10-25) volume ratio, stirring and cleaning with ultrasonic waves for 10-15 minutes, followed by suction filtration, adding ethanol to prepare magnetic nanoparticle solution having 14-18% magnetic nanoparticle concentration, dividing into 2 groups of magnetic nanoparticle solution A and magnetic nanoparticle solution B, adding 45-50 vol.% pyrethrin into the magnetic nanoparticle solution A, performing ultrasonic dispersion uniformly, dissolving eco-friendly polymer in the oil phase in 1:(2-4) ratio, and ultrasonically mixing to obtain an oil phase group, dissolving the magnetic magnetic nanoparticle solution B and emulsifier in an aqueous phase in (4-6):0.01 ratio, and stirring at a high speed of 6000-8000 rotations/minute to obtain an aqueous phase group, placing the oil phase group in a paramagnetic field environment for clockwise stirring for 2-4 minutes, and placing the aqueous phase group in a reverse magnetic field environment for counter stirring for 2-4 minutes to ensure oil phase group and aqueous phase group in opposite magnetic field, adding a supercritical carbon dioxide solvent into the mixing and stirring vessel, and extruding the S4 magnetized oil phase group and the water phase group to the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent at the same rate under stirring, dropping 4-8 drops of antifoaming agent, reacting at 20-25 degrees C temperature under 16-18 Mpa pressure for 12-14 hours, heating at 40-50 degrees C temperature for 1 hour, followed by depressurization to 4-6 Mpa, drying to obtain the microspheres, adding a dispersing agent, a wetting agent, an adsorbing agent, and uniformly mixing to obtain microsphere wettable powder. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a mixing and stirring vessel, which comprises base, water bath jacket, conical container bottle, Y-shaped tube, pusher one and pusher two.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the mixing and stirring vessel.
   Base (1)
   Water bath jacket (2)
   Conical container bottle (3)
   Y-shaped tube (4)
   Pusher one (5)
   Pusher two (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: Preparing eco-friendly pyrethrin controlled release microspheres involves adding the alkaline water as sodium carbonate aqueous solution having 11-12 pH. The eco-friendly polymer is polybutylene succinate, aliphatic polycarbonate or polylactic acid. The oil phase is dichloromethane, ethyl acetate or acetone. The emulsifier is gelatin, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether or tween. The antifoaming agent is polydimethylsiloxane or lauryl phenylacetate. The dispersing agent is 1-3 wt.% weak saponin. The wetting agent is 3-5 wt.% SOPA or sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate. The adsorbent is 4-6 wt.% kaolin, activated clay or bentonite. Preferred Conditions: The magnetic field strength of paramagnetic field environment and the reverse magnetic field environment is 2000-4000 Gauss. The oil phase group and aqueous phase group are extruded into a supercritical carbon dioxide solvent at an equivalent rate of 5-10 ml/minute.

PN CN109692632-A
TI Flame retardant graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material has nano-scale pores, average micron-scale pores and average specific surface area of specified ranges, prepared by mixing halloysite nanotube into mixed solution.
AU LI H
   FU Y
   LIU H
   WEI D
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201941793J
AB    NOVELTY - A flame retardant graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material has nano-scale pores of 10-11 nm, average micron-scale pores of 20-21 mu m and average specific surface area of 601-605 m2/g. The material is prepared by mixing halloysite nanotube into deionized water and ethanol mixed solution, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding sodium styrene sulfonate, polyvinylsiloxane, initiator and hexabromocyclododecane into deionized water and ethanol mixed solution, uniformly stirring, adding into halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing, washing product, dispersing in water, water bath heating, initiating polymerization, and washing and dispersing into water; and mixing concentrated sulfuric acid and graphite powder, adding potassium permanganate, stirring and reacting, adding water, uniformly stirring, adding water and hydrogen peroxide, uniformly stirring, washing, adding water, and ultrasonically and uniformly dispersing.
   USE - Flame retardant graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines different properties of flame retardants and achieves synergistic flame retardant effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A flame retardant graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material has average specific surface area of 601-605 m2/g, nano-scale pores of 10-11 nm and average micron-scale pores of 20-21 mu m. The material is prepared by:
   (A) mixing 0.6-12 pts. wt. halloysite nanotube into mixed solution of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding 0.6-12 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.06-1.8 pts. wt. polyvinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.4 pt. wt. initiator and 0.08-9 pts. wt. hexabromocyclododecane into mixed solution of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, uniformly stirring, adding into halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing, vacuumizing, returning to normal pressure, repeatedly vacuumizing 3 times, washing product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, water bath heating to 70-80 degrees C, initiating polymerization for 50 hours, and washing and dispersing into 100 pts. wt. water to obtain dispersion;
   (B) mixing 0.8-8 pts. wt. concentrated sulfuric acid and 0.3-12 pts. wt. graphite powder, adding 0.6-12 pts. wt. potassium permanganate, putting mixture into ice water bath, stirring and reacting for 12 hours, adding 75-225 pts. wt. water, uniformly stirring, adding 360-520 pts. wt. water and 9-52 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide, uniformly stirring, washing product to neutral, adding 100 pts. wt. water, and ultrasonically and uniformly dispersing to obtain dispersion; and
   (C) mixing dispersions, adding 0.01-1.5 pts. wt. initiator, 0.03-6 pts. wt. bisphenol bis(diphenylphosphate), uniformly stirring, water bath heating to 70-80 degrees C, initiating polymerization for 60 hours, adding reaction solution with 0.7-12 pts. wt. ascorbic acid, continuously reacting for 10-40 hours, putting product in carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extracting device, taking carbon dioxide as medium, and supercritical drying at 31-56 degrees C for 1 hour at pressure of 6-12 MPa.
   The number average molecular weight of polyvinylsiloxane is 500-5000, preferably 1000-3000. The halloysite nanotube wall has positive charge on inner side and negative charge on outer side. The sodium styrene sulfonate has negative charge, and is adsorbed on inner wall of halloysite nanotube by electrostatic action. The hollow structure of halloysite nanotube provides microporous structure for material. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the flame retardant graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves:(A) mixing 1-10 pts. wt. halloysite nanotube into deionized water and ethanol mixed solution, ultrasonically dispersing for 1 hour to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding 1-10 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.1-1 pt. wt. polyvinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.1 pt. wt. initiator and 1-5 pts. wt. hexabromocyclododecane into deionized water and ethanol mixed solution, uniformly stirring for 10-60 minutes, adding into halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing for 25-35 minutes, vacuumizing, keeping vacuum for 0.5-2 hours, washing product, dispersing in water, water bath heating, initiating polymerization at 70-80 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours and 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C and 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours, and washing and dispersing into water to obtain dispersion;(B) mixing 1-5 pts. wt. concentrated sulfuric acid and 0.5-10 pts. wt. graphite powder, adding 1-10 pts. wt. potassium permanganate, putting mixture into ice water bath, stirring and reacting, adding 100-200 pts. wt. water, uniformly stirring for 1-4 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding 400-500 pts. wt. water and 10-50 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide, uniformly stirring for 1-6 hours, washing product to neutral, adding water, and ultrasonically and uniformly dispersing to obtain dispersion; and(C) mixing dispersions, adding 0.01-1 pt. wt. initiator, 0.05-5 pts. wt. bisphenol bis(diphenylphosphate), uniformly stirring for 1-10 hours, pre-polymerizing in water bath at 70-80 C for 30-60 minutes, polymerizing in constant temperature water bath at 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours and 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C and 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours, placing in hydrothermal kettle at 80-200 degrees C for 1-10 hours, adding reaction solution with 1-10 pts. wt. ascorbic acid, continuously reacting at 20-100 degrees C, putting product in carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extracting device, taking carbon dioxide as medium, and supercritical drying at 33-55 degrees C for 2-4 hours at pressure of 7-10 MPa, preferably 3 hours.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).)

PN CN109608911-A
TI Preparing fly ash-based carbonized modified composite filler comprises e.g. pre-treating fly ash, adding calcium oxide and water, reacting, filtering, aging and mixing pretreated fly ash with calcium hydroxide paste.
AU FAN H
   LIU Q
   LIU J
   XIE K
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201935802A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fly ash-based carbonized modified composite filler comprises e.g. (i) taking fly ash as raw material, then adding trapping agent to flotation concentration for 5-20 minutes and adding foaming agent, then air floating and performing the foaming, then directly concentrating the remaining tailings slurry in flotation tank, separating, placing fly ash suspension under a magnetic stirrer, then stirring, can completely remove iron-containing particles in the fly ash particles through 4 cycles to obtained pretreated fly ash, (ii) adding calcium oxide and water, then reacting, and taking out the lime milk, filtering, then aging, (iii) mixing pretreated fly ash in step (i) with calcium hydroxide paste in step (ii), adding reversed phase solvent and surfactant, stirring, and transferring t resulting mixture to treaction system, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide gas and reacting, and (iv) separating the mixture obtained in step (iii) by centrifugation, washing and drying.
   USE - The fly ash-based carbonized modified composite filler is useful for paper filling (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fly ash-based carbonized modified composite filler: enhances the whiteness and strength of the paper.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing fly ash-based carbonized modified composite filler comprises (i) taking fly ash as raw material, then adding 0.5-2.5 kg/t trapping agent to 10-30 wt.% flotation concentration for 5-20 minutes and adding 0.5-2.5 kg/t foaming agent for 5-20 minutes, then air floating at a speed of 1000-3000 revolutions/minute and the aeration amount of 0.5-0.8 m3/m2.minutes, and performing the foaming until the color of the foam layer is consistent with the color of the lower layer, then directly concentrating the remaining tailings slurry in the flotation tank, magnetically separating to remove iron and further improve the whiteness, placing fly ash suspension having a solid concentration of 5-20 wt.% under a magnetic stirrer composed of a permanent magnet assembly, then stirring for 2-10 minutes at a speed of 100-500 revolutions/minute, can completely remove iron-containing particles in the fly ash particles through 4 cycles to obtained pretreated fly ash for further use, (ii) adding calcium oxide and water at a mass ratio of 1:0.5-1:2, then reacting at 60-100 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, and taking out the lime milk, filtering impurity through 200-mesh sieve, then aging more than 24 hours, and then reconstituted as a fixed concentration of calcium hydroxide paste for retention, (iii) mixing thoroughly 2.5-10 g pretreated fly ash in the step (i) with 2.5-10 g calcium hydroxide paste in the step (ii), adding 20-80 g reversed phase solvent and 2-8 g surfactant, stirring again uniformly, and transferring the resulting mixture to the reaction system, keeping the temperature of the whole system at 10-30 degrees C, and stirring well at a speed of 300-800 revolutions/minute for 5-15 minutes under the action of a multi-tooth disc-shaped dispersing stirrer, then introducing carbon dioxide gas at a rate of 0.1-0.4 l/minute, reacting for 0.5-2 hours, and (iv) separating the mixture obtained in the step (iii) by centrifugation, washing three times with absolute ethanol and distilled water, and finally drying to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The trapping agent is kerosene or diesel. The foaming agent is octanol, eucalyptus oil or camphor oil. In the step (iii), the reverse phase solvent is liquid paraffin; the surfactant is Span 80 (RTM: Sorbitan monooleate) or Tween 80 (RTM: Polyethylene glycol sorbitan monooleate). The fly ash raw material is 500 meshes original fly ash.

PT P
PN CN109455752-A; CN109455752-B
TI Preparing calcium sulfate by desulfurization ash residue comprises e.g. testing content of e.g. calcium carbonate, mixing with water, reacting sulfuric acid, hydrolyzing sulfur dioxide gas and filtering by a plate and frame filter press.
AU TIAN H
   TIAN Z
   ZONG X
   HUANG L
   YANG S
AE UNIV HUANGGANG NORMAL (UHGN-C)
   TIAN H (TIAN-Individual)
GA 201925390Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium sulfate by desulfurization ash residue comprises e.g. testing the content of calcium carbonate, calcium sulfite hydrate, calcium hydroxide in desulfurized ash for treating, providing desulfurized ash, mixing with tap water to make a mass fraction upto 40-60% slurry in the reaction kettle, calculating and providing sulfuric acid in a required amount, completely reacting with desulfurization ash and ferrate in a required amount for complete oxidation of calcium sulfite, where the sulfuric acid is excessively 10-30%, and the ferrate is excessively 5-10%, adding metered sulfuric acid into the reaction kettle under stirring, simultaneously opening induced draft fan and spray absorption tower, reacting sulfuric acid with calcium carbonate and calcium hydroxide to obtain calcium sulfate, carbon dioxide gas and water, reacting with calcium sulfite hydrate to obtain calcium sulfate, emitting sulfur dioxide gas, hydrolyzing part of the sulfur dioxide gas to sulfuric acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium sulfate by desulfurization ash residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and quick, and is suitable for industrial applications prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing calcium sulfate by desulfurization ash comprises (i) testing the content of calcium carbonate, calcium sulfite hydrate, calcium hydroxide in desulfurized ash for treating, (ii) providing desulfurized ash, mixing with tap water to make a mass fraction upto 40-60% slurry in the reaction kettle, calculating and providing sulfuric acid in a required amount, completely reacting with desulfurization ash and ferrate in a required amount for complete oxidation of calcium sulfite, where the sulfuric acid is excessively 10-30%, and the ferrate is excessively 5-10%, (iii) adding metered sulfuric acid into the reaction kettle under stirring, simultaneously opening induced draft fan and spray absorption tower, reacting sulfuric acid with calcium carbonate and calcium hydroxide to obtain calcium sulfate, carbon dioxide gas and water, reacting with calcium sulfite hydrate to obtain calcium sulfate and emitting sulfur dioxide gas, hydrolyzing part of the sulfur dioxidegas to sulfuric acid left in the reaction vessel, escaping a part of the SO2 gas from the reaction vessel, adding ferrate solution dropwise into the reaction kettle before or after the addition of sulfuric acid solution or after the addition of the sulfuric acid solution, adding ferrate solution dropwise into the reaction vessel, and ferrate ion oxidizes sulfite ion to obtain a sulfate ion, (iv) introducing SO2 gas into the absorption tower which is an absorption liquid composed of an acid solution composed of sulfuric acid and chlorine dioxide by an induced draft fan to obtain sulfate ions and chloride ions, and (v) after completing reaction in the reaction vessel, testing pH value in the reaction kettle, neutralizing the reaction solution in the reaction vessel with ash calcium powder until neutrality of pH, filtering by a plate and frame filter press, then washing for three times with tap water, dehydrating and drying to obtain calcium sulfate product
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the preparation of calcium sulfate by desulfurized ash residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iv), recycling acid solution containing sulfate ion and the chloride ion recovered as absorption liquid or directly neutralizing with calcium ions to obtain a corresponding salt. Preferred Components: In the step (i), according to the calcium ion content measure: the amount required for the complete reaction of the amount of sulfuric acid with the calcium ion in the desulfurized ash residue is (1.1-1.2):1. In the step (iii), the ferrate solution is a ferrate aqueous solution having a mass fraction of 1-10%. The device comprises reaction vessel, acid mist absorption device and acid circulation device are arranged. The top of the reaction vessel is connected to the bottom of the acid mist absorption device through a pipe. The outlet of the acid circulation device is connected to an acid spray port located at an upper portion of acid mist absorption device. The inlet of the acid circulation device is connected to an acid recovery port at the bottom of acid mist absorption device. The acid mist absorbing device is provided with an oxidizing gas inlet, and is connected to the gas source through oxidizing gas inlet. The top of the reaction vessel is provided with a ferrate solution inlet, first escape gas outlet, slurry inlet, and a sulfuric acid solution inlet. The first escape gas outlet is connected to the bottom of the acid mist absorbing device through a pipe. The reactor is also provided with a stirring device and a thermometer and pH meter for monitoring the reaction conditions. The acid mist absorbing device comprises: first-stage absorption tower and a second-stage absorption tower connected in series. The primary absorption tower is provided with a first acid liquid recovery port, first oxidation gas inlet, first escape gas inlet, first acid liquid spray port and a second escape gas outlet in this order from bottom to top. The secondary absorption tower is provided with a second acid liquid recovery port, second oxidation gas inlet, second escape gas inlet, second acid liquid spray port and a third escape gas outlet in this order from bottom to top. The first escape gas inlet is connected to the first escape gas outlet. The second escape gas outlet is connected to the second escape gas inlet. The third escape gas outlet is connected to the atmosphere through an induced draft fan or connected to a tertiary absorption tower. The first acid liquid spray port and the second acid liquid spray port are connected to the outlet of the acid liquid circulation device through an acid-proof pump. The acid liquid circulation device comprises: a first acid liquid storage tank, second acid liquid storage tank, third acid liquid storage tank, and fourth acid liquid storage tank. The first acid liquid storage tank and the second acid liquid storage tank are connected in parallel and connected to the first acid liquid recovery port and the first acid liquid spray port. The third acid liquid storage tank and the fourth acid liquid storage tank are connected in parallel and connected to the second acid liquid recovery port and the second acid liquid spray port. The bottom of the acid circulation device is provided with a discharge port, and a discharge valve is arranged at the discharge opening.

PN CN109385054-A
TI Preparing toughened thermally conductive plastic comprises e.g. mixing copper sulfate solution and sodium tartrate solution to obtain mixed solution, placing in three-necked flask and adding sodium hydroxide solution.
AU WU Y
   ZHANG H
   WANG S
AE WU Y (WUYY-Individual)
GA 201921279U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing toughened thermally conductive plastic comprises e.g. mixing copper sulfate solution and sodium tartrate solution to obtain mixed solution, placing in three-necked flask, adding sodium hydroxide solution to heat the three-necked flask in water bath and subjecting to heat preservation reaction, continuing to keep warm, adding glucose solution, removing filtrate by filtration to obtain red particles, washing and drying to obtain nano cuprous oxide particles, mixing isophthalic acid, neopentyl glycol, trimethylolpropane, and monobutyltin oxide and adding into four-necked flask, starting stirrer, stirring, heating and adding 1,4-cyclohexanedicarboxylic acid, subjecting to acid hydrolysis reaction, cooling, doping quartz sand, continue the reaction and cooling to obtain thermoplastic polyester resin, adding sodium aluminate solution into the beaker, passing carbon dioxide gas into beaker, adding mullite powder and ammonium polyacrylate to beaker and placing beaker in water bath.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing toughened thermally conductive plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The plastic enhances the heat dissipation performance of heat conductive filler.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing toughened thermally conductive plastic comprises (i) uniformly mixing copper sulfate solution and sodium tartrate solution to obtain mixed solution, placing the mixture in three-necked flask provided with dropping funnel, adding sodium hydroxide solution into the three-necked flask using dropping funnel to heat the three-necked flask in water bath and subjecting to heat preservation reaction; (ii) continuing to keep warm for 10-15 minutes after the solution in the above three flasks appears blue, adding glucose solution into the three-necked flask until the blue color disappeared, removing the filtrate by filtration to obtain red particles, washing the red particles successively with water and absolute ethanol for 3-4 times and drying in an oven to obtain nano cuprous oxide particles; (iii) mixing 30-40 pts. wt. isophthalic acid, 20-25 pts. wt. neopentyl glycol, 5-10 pts. wt. trimethylolpropane, and 1-3 pts. wt. monobutyltin oxide and adding into a four-necked flask provided with condensing device, starting the stirrer, stirring at 400-450 rotations/minute, heating and adding 10-15 pts. wt. 1,4-cyclohexanedicarboxylic acid under the protection of nitrogen, subjecting to acid hydrolysis reaction, cooling to room temperature, doping 20-30 pts. wt. quartz sand, continue the reaction and cooling to room temperature to obtain thermoplastic polyester resin; (iv) adding 700-800 ml sodium aluminate solution into the beaker, passing carbon dioxide gas into the beaker, adding 150-180g mullite powder and 8-10g ammonium polyacrylate to the beaker after ventilation, and placing the beaker in a water bath, heating, stirring and mixing for 30-35 minutes to obtain colloidal solution; (v) pouring the colloidal solution into the crucible, and heating to obtain molten liquid, removing the perforated alumina fiber cooled to room temperature on the cotton collector using a three-roll centrifugal reeling machine; and (vi) mixing the nano cuprous oxide particles with thermoplastic polyester resin, heating to 150-170 degrees C to obtain hot melt resin, adding 40-50 pts. wt. hot melt resin, 8-10 pts. wt. boron nitrate, 10-12 pts. wt. colloidal graphite, 5-8 pts. wt. calcium stearate, adding to the first cylinder of the twin-screw extruder and adding the perforated alumina fiber into the fourth cylinder, extruding the master batch and subjecting to injection molding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (i) the mass fraction of copper sulfate solution is 25 wt.%, mass fraction of sodium tartrate solution is 30 wt.%, the volume ratio of copper sulfate solution to sodium tartrate solution is 4:1, the dropping rate of the dropping funnel is 4-5 ml/minute, mass fraction of sodium hydroxide solution is 40 wt.%, the volume of sodium hydroxide solution added is the volume of the mixed solution 1/4-1/3 and the temperature of the three-necked flask was 90-100 degrees C after heating. In step (ii) the mass fraction of glucose solution is 10 wt.% and the oven set temperature is 50-60 degrees C and the drying time is 7-8 hours. In step (iii) the temperature is 230-250 degrees C after heating, and the reaction time is 2-3 hours, the quartz sand has a particle size of 200 mesh and the reaction time is 20-30 minutes. In step (iv) the mass fraction of sodium aluminate solution is 20 wt.%, the aeration rate of the carbon dioxide gas is 20-30 ml/minutes, the aeration time is 40-45 minutes and the temperature in the water bath is heated to 70-80 degrees C. In step (v) the heating temperature of the crucible is 1600-1700 degrees C, the speed of the first-stage reeling roller is 70-80 m/second, and the speed of the secondary reeling roller is 80-100 m/second and the three-stage twisting roller speed is 150-160 m/second. In step (vi) the mass ratio of the nano cuprous oxide particles to the thermoplastic polyester resin is 4:1, the colloidal graphite particle size is 10-100 nm and the control head temperature is 200-220 degrees C.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).)

PN CN108975359-A; CN108975359-B
TI Chlorination extraction of rare earth beryllium            silicate ore molten salt comprises heating chloride to            melt, adding rare earth cerium silicate mineral powder            and petroleum coke, molten salt chlorination, adding            acid/water, and filtering.
AU LI M
   GAO K
   ZHANG D
AE INNER MOGOLIA ZHALUTEQILUAN MINE IND CO LTD (INNE-Non-standard)
   LI M (LIMM-Individual)
   GAO K (GAOK-Individual)
   ZHANG D (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2018A2980W
AB    NOVELTY - Chlorination extraction of rare earth                beryllium silicate ore molten salt comprises adding                chloride to a reactor, heating to melt, adding rare                earth cerium silicate mineral powder and petroleum                coke, simultaneously introducing chlorine gas,                performing molten salt chlorination, periodically                discharging the reactor hard boiling furnace rock,                passing chlorine gas, subjecting to molten salt                chlorination reaction, cooling, separating to                obtain a high melting point chloride molten salt                and a first gaseous chloride, two-stage cooling                separation by cooling the first gaseous chloride,                separating the liquid/solid beryllium chloride and                second gaseous chloride, cooling the second gaseous                chloride to obtain a mixture of carbon dioxide and                chlorine and liquid silicon tetrachloride,                combining the difficult-boiling furnace rock with                the molten salt, adding acid/water to the mixture,                filtering to obtain a filtrate, which is beryllium                chloride solution, drying filter residue and                returning to reactor.
   USE - The silicon tetrachloride is useful in                preparing of silica by gas phase method. The                by-product hydrochloric acid is used as an                auxiliary for the extraction and separation of rare                earths.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high extracting efficiency,                high product purity (beryllium chloride purity of                more than 86.3% and beryllium recycling rate of                more than 90.1, silicon tetrachloride purity of                more than 92.4%, silicon recovery rate of more than                90% and rare earth recycling rate of more than                95.4%); meets atom economy; ensures high added                value utilization of the associated resource                silicon; reduces the discharge amount of the three                wastes; is economical and environmentally friendly;                produces rare earth chloride, which can be directly                used as a raw material for the molten salt                chlorination reaction; and ensures chlorine                recycling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chlorination extraction of rare earth                beryllium silicate ore molten salt comprises (a)                molten salt chlorination by adding chloride to a                reactor, heating to melt, adding rare earth cerium                silicate mineral powder and petroleum coke,                simultaneously introducing chlorine gas, performing                molten salt chlorination, periodically discharging                the reactor hard boiling furnace rock, where the                weight ratio of chloride, rare earth beryllium                silicate to petroleum coke is 30-40:1:0.15-0.45,                introducing chlorine gas (2.5-4.5 times the weight                of the rare earth beryllium silicate), and                subjecting to molten salt chlorination reaction at                800-1000 degrees C, (b) cooling the product of the                molten salt chlorination reaction to 550-700                degrees C for separation to obtain a high melting                point chloride molten salt and a first gaseous                chloride, (c) two-stage cooling separation by                cooling the first gaseous chloride to 200-400                degrees C, and separating the liquid or solid                beryllium chloride and the second gaseous chloride,                (d) cooling the second gaseous chloride to -30 to                35 degrees C to obtain a mixture of carbon dioxide                and chlorine and liquid silicon tetrachloride, and                (e) combining the difficult-boiling furnace rock                obtained in the step (a) with the molten salt of                the high-melting-point chloride obtained in the                step (b) to obtain a mixture, adding acid or water                to the mixture, and filtering to obtain a filtrate,                which is beryllium chloride solution, drying the                obtained filter residue and returning to the                reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a): the                  rare earth-silicate is a concentrate of a                  silicon-geranium or a concentrate of hingganite                  ore, a concentrate of silicon-antimony ore, where                  hingganite ore has rare earth oxide content of                  more than 30% and niobium oxide content of more                  than 3%. The chloride is potassium chloride,                  sodium chloride, magnesium chloride and/or rare                  earth chloride. In the step (a): the chloride is                  rare earth chloride; and the weight ratio of the                  rare earth chloride to sodium chloride is 3:1,                  preferably the chloride is rare earth chloride,                  magnesium chloride and potassium chloride in a                  weight ratio of 1:2:1. The acid in step (e) is 6                  mol/l hydrochloric acid, where the weight ratio                  of hydrochloric acid and the mixture is 2-6:1.                  Preferred Method: The extraction process in the                  step (e) is carried out at 20-95 degrees C.

PN US2018327263-A1; WO2018208893-A1; US10399852-B2; IN201917044919-A; CN110621615-A; EP3621919-A1; EP3621919-A4; IN377084-B
TI Integrated process for treating sour synthesis gas            and recovering acidic gasses involves passing sour            synthesis gas stream to acid gas removal unit, and            passing portion of carbon dioxide rich stream to            thermal oxidizer unit.
AU DE REN J
   BEUCKELS B J T
   WHYMAN W J
   DE R J
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 201888901D
AB    NOVELTY - An integrated process for treating sour                synthesis gas and recovering greater than or equal                to 1 acidic gasses containing hydrogen sulfide                (H2S), carbonyl sulfide (COS) and carbon dioxide                (CO2) present in sour synthesis gas involves (a)                passing a sour synthesis gas stream to acid gas                removal unit (110) to provide a treated synthesis                gas stream and CO2 rich stream; (b) passing greater                than or equal to 1 portion of CO2 rich stream to                thermal oxidizer unit (130) to provide treated CO2                gas stream; (c) passing greater than or equal to 1                portion of treated synthesis gas stream to pressure                swing adsorption unit (120) to obtain purified                hydrogen stream and gas stream; and (d) passing                greater than or equal to 1 portion of gas stream to                thermal oxidizer unit.
   USE - An integrated process for treating sour                synthesis gas and recovering greater than or equal                to 1 acidic gases.
   ADVANTAGE - External source of fuel gas e.g. natural gas,                syngas, is reduced by making use of internal source                i.e. the gas stream from pressure swing adsorption                unit. By sending gas stream from the pressure swing                adsorption unit to the thermal oxidizer unit, less                external fuel gas is required and hence resulting                in fuel savings.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) integrated process for treating sour                synthesis gas and recovering greater than or equal                to 1 gasses comprising H2S, COS and CO2 present in                sour synthesis gas which involves (a) passing sour                synthesis gas stream to acid gas removal unit (110)                to provide a treated synthesis gas stream and CO2                rich stream; (b) passing greater than or equal to 1                portion of CO2 rich stream to thermal oxidizer unit                (130) to provide a treated CO2 gas stream; (c)                passing a flue gas produced in thermal oxidizer                unit to waste heat recovery unit (140); (d) passing                greater than or equal to 1 portion of steam                recovered from waste heat recovery unit to acid gas                removal unit; and (e) passing greater than or equal                to 1 portion of treated synthesis gas stream to                pressure swing adsorption unit (120) to obtain                purified hydrogen stream and gas stream; and
   (2) integrated process for treating sour                synthesis gas and recovering greater than or equal                to 1 gasses comprising H2S, COS and CO2 present in                sour synthesis gas which involves (a) passing a                sour synthesis gas stream to acid gas removal unit                to provide treated synthesis gas stream and CO2                rich stream; (b) passing greater than or equal to 1                portion of CO2 rich stream to thermal oxidizer unit                to provide a treated CO2 gas stream; (c) passing a                flue gas produced in thermal oxidizer unit to waste                heat recovery unit; (d) passing greater than or                equal to 1 portion of steam recovered from the                waste heat recovery unit to acid gas removal unit;                (e) passing greater than or equal to 1 portion of                treated synthesis gas stream to a pressure swing                adsorption unit to obtain purified hydrogen stream                and gas stream; and (f) passing greater than or                equal to 1 portion of gas stream to thermal                oxidizer unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                integrated complex for obtaining purified hydrogen                from synthesis gas.
   Apparatus (100)
   Acid gas removal unit (110)
   Pressure swing adsorption unit (120)
   Thermal oxidizer unit (130)
   Waste heat recovery unit (140)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Impurities containing                  carbon monoxide and hydrogen present in CO2 rich                  stream are removed via oxidation in the thermal                  oxidizer unit. Sour synthesis gas is obtained                  from partial oxidation of hydrocarbonaceous                  fuel.

PT P
PN CN108552577-A
TI Preparing jasmine-flavored reconstituted tobacco by subjecting unbleached hardwood pulp board to defibering and pulping, extracting tobacco stems with distilled water, hot-grinding and grinding to obtain tobacco stem pulp.
AU HAN J
   YANG R
   WANG P
   XIE J
AE MINFENG SPECIAL PAPER CO LTD (MINF-Non-standard)
GA 201878099M
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing jasmine-flavored reconstituted tobacco involves (a) subjecting unbleached hardwood pulp board to defibering and pulping until the pulping degree is 18-22 degrees SR to obtain hardwood mechanical pulp, (b) extracting the tobacco stems with distilled water and collecting extracted tobacco stems and tobacco stem leaching liquid, hot grinding extracted tobacco stems with high-concentration plate and carrying out two-stage grinding to obtain tobacco stem pulp, (c) leaching tobacco powder with distilled water and collecting tobacco powder and tobacco powder extracting solution, (d) hot-grinding hot-dipped tobacco powder with high-concentration plate and carrying out two-stage grinding to obtain tobacco powder slurry, (e) cleaning fresh jasmine flowers, vacuum drying to obtain dry petals and extracting the dry petals in carbon dioxide supercritical extraction machine to obtain jasmine essential oil, and (f) mixing hardwood pulp, tobacco stem pulp and pulp tobacco powder.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing jasmine-flavored reconstituted tobacco.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and stable preparation of jasmine-flavored reconstituted tobacco with stable product quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for preparing jasmine-flavored reconstituted tobacco involves (a) subjecting unbleached hardwood pulp board to defibering and pulping until the pulping degree is 18-22 degrees SR to obtain hardwood mechanical pulp, (b) extracting the tobacco stems with distilled water in a mass ratio of 1:4-8 at 70-80 degrees C for 80-120 minutes and collecting extracted tobacco stems and tobacco stem leaching liquid, hot grinding extracted tobacco stems with high-concentration plate according to a distance of 0.3-0.5 mm and 0.1-0.3 mm millstone and carrying out two-stage grinding to obtain tobacco stem pulp, (c) leaching tobacco powder with distilled water in a mass ratio of 1:8-12 at 50-70 degrees C for 80-120 minutes and collecting tobacco powder and tobacco powder extracting solution, (d) hot-grinding hot-dipped tobacco powder with high-concentration plate according to a distance of 0.3-0.5 mm and 0.1-0.3 mm millstone and carrying out two-stage grinding to obtain tobacco powder slurry, (e) cleaning fresh jasmine flowers, vacuum drying at 50-70 degrees C for 4-8 hours to obtain dry petals and extracting the dry petals in carbon dioxide supercritical extraction machine to obtain jasmine essential oil, (f) mixing hardwood pulp, tobacco stem pulp and pulp tobacco powder in a mass ratio of 5-6:1-5:1-3, beating the mixed pulp in a PFI mill until a beating degree is 22-28 degrees , (g) disintegrating the mixed pulp into tobacco sheets having a basis weight of 38-42 g/m2, (h) concentrating tobacco stem extract and tobacco extract in a ratio of 1:1-3 to a solid content of 36-40% and mixing to obtain a coating liquid, and (h) coating tobacco sheet at a speed of 25-35 m/minute and 45-55 degrees C to obtain a reconstituted tobacco leaf containing jasmine.
DC F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); D18 (Skins, hides, pelts, leather and chemical treatment of tobacco.); P15 (Tobacco (A24).)
MC F05-A06; D07-C; D07-D; P15-L05
IP A24B-015/24; A24B-003/14

PT P
PN CN108384745-A
TI Culturing testicular supporting cell by improved            two-step enzymatic separation, comprises e.g. cutting            testicular parenchyma of Sprague Dawley rat,            transferring into centrifuge tube, centrifuging,            culturing and separating.
AU LEE S
   ZHANG Q
   XU J
   REN M
   HU Q
   JIN E
AE UNIV ANHUI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (ANHI-C)
GA 2018642414
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing testicular supporting cell by                improved two-step enzymatic separation comprises                (i) (ia) selecting Sprague Dawley rat testicles,                and removing blood vessels and testicular capsule                in the testicular parenchyma; and (ib) cutting the                testicular parenchyma; (ii) (iia) transferring the                tissue block into centrifuge tube, and oscillating;                (iib) placing the centrifugal tube in a water bath,                adding fetal bovine serum for stopping digestion,                centrifuging, and using a pipette for transferring                the suspended cells; (iic) centrifuging, removing                the supernatant, adding type IV collagenase, and                stopping the digestion by adding dulbecco's                modified eagle's medium; and (iid) filtering the                tissue block, and centrifuging to obtain testicular                support cells; (iii) re-suspending cells in                dulbecco's modified eagle's medium, and placing in                a carbon dioxide incubator for cultivation; and                (iv) culturing, washing, separating, and performing                cell passage after the cells are grown.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing testicular                supporting cell by improved two-step enzymatic                separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high supporting cell yield                and enhances cell viability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culturing testicular supporting cell by                improved two-step enzymatic separation comprises                (i) (ia) selecting Sprague Dawley rat testicles of                18-22 days, placing in a sterile phosphate buffered                saline culture dish and pre-cooled at 4-6 degrees                C, washing mucus and blood present on the testicle                surface, and using ophthalmic scissors and                ophthalmic forceps for removing blood vessels and                testicular capsule in the testicular parenchyma;                and (ib) placing the testicular parenchyma in a                penicillin bottle and adding pre-cooled phosphate                buffered saline, cutting the testicular parenchyma                into 1-2 mm3 tissue block with ophthalmic scissors                and removing supernatant after statically placing;                (ii) (iia) transferring the tissue block into 15 ml                centrifuge tube, adding 4-5 times the tissue block                volume of 0.25% trypsin, and oscillating the fine                tubules in the tissue block for spreading apart;                (iib) placing the centrifugal tube in a water bath                for digestion until the tissue block is digested                into pieces, adding 1-2 ml fetal bovine serum for                stopping digestion, centrifuging at 1500                revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, removing the                supernatant, adding phosphate buffered saline for                suspending the cells and using a pipette for                transferring the suspended cells; (iic)                centrifuging the tube at 1000 revolutions/minute                for 5 minutes, removing the supernatant, adding 3-4                times the tissue mass of 0.1% type IV collagenase,                digesting in a water bath until the tissue pieces                become mucous, and stopping the digestion by adding                dulbecco's modified eagle's medium; and (iid)                filtering the tissue block digested in step (iic),                collecting the filtrate in centrifuge tube,                centrifuging at 800 revolutions/minute for 5                minutes, and removing the supernatant to obtain                testicular support cells; (iii) re-suspending                testicular support cells in dulbecco's modified                eagle's medium, performing cell counting using a                cell counter and adjusting the cell concentration                to (0.5-1)x 106/ml, inoculating in a 25 cm2 culture                flask, and disperse the cells evenly and placing in                a carbon dioxide incubator for cultivation; and                (iv) culturing for 24 hours, observing under                microscope, washing with phosphate buffered saline                at room temperature, changing to 37 degrees C                preheating, separating the culture for 72 hours,                changing the medium once in every 24 hours, and                performing cell passage after the cells are grown                to 85-95% in the flask.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (ib), the                  amount of pre-cooled phosphate buffered saline is                  3-4 ml, where the pre-cooling temperature is 4-6                  degrees C, statically placing for 3-5 minutes and                  removing supernatant using 1 ml pipette. In the                  step (iib), the water bath temperature is 37                  degrees C, digestion time is 12-15 minutes,                  oscillating once in every 2 minutes during                  digestion and the amount of phosphate buffered                  saline is 10 ml. In the step (iic), the water                  bath temperature is 37 degrees C, digestion time                  is 8-10 minutes, and shaking the centrifuge tube                  once in every 2 minutes during digestion. In the                  step (iid), the filtering is carried out using                  100 mesh metal mesh screen. In the step (iii),                  the dulbecco's modified eagle's medium comprises                  100 IU/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml streptomycin                  and 10% fetal bovine serum, and maintaining the                  culture environment of carbon dioxide incubator                  to 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide and                  saturated humidity.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C07 (Apparatus, formulation, general. including veterinary syringes, general formulations where the active compound is not central to the invention (e.g. wettable powders) and analysis.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-B04D4; B04-F03; B04-L05C; B11-A04A; C04-B04D4; C04-F03; C04-L05C; C11-A04A; D05-A02C; D05-H08B

PN CN107890748-A; CN107890748-B
TI Processing acid gas pre-concentration comprises e.g. taking sulfur-rich absorbent from the upstream, sending to flash tower for vacuum flashing and processing from top of flash tower to the downstream and performing gas stripping operation.
AU WANG T
   ZHOU X
   FU L
   YAN D
   CHU Y
   LIU Q
   HU Y
   CHEN G
AE SINOPEC NINGBO ENG CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NINGBO TECHNOLOGY INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC REFINING & CHEM ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 201828920E
AB    NOVELTY - Processing acid gas pre-concentration comprises e.g. taking sulfur-rich absorbent from the upstream, sending to the flash tower for vacuum flashing, processing from the top of the flash tower to the downstream, sending the sulfur rich absorbent at the bottom of the flash distillation tower to the stripping tower for gas stripping operation, introducing the stripping tower into the lower part of the nitrogen, further desorbing carbon dioxide (CO2) in the sulfur-rich absorbent and a small amount of H2S, combining gas from gas with the above flash gas and cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for processing acid gas pre-concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances to process at intermediate temperature, controls the temperature of sulfur-rich absorbent entering the flash tower, and ensures the sulfur-rich absorbent to a different degree at a suitable temperature and generates different concentrations of Claus gas to meet the requirements of the downstream sulfur recovery section.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing acid gas pre-concentration comprises taking sulfur-rich absorbent from the upstream having a temperature of -20 to -60 degrees C and a pressure of 0.6-1.5 MPaG, where the molar content of hydrogen sulfide (H2S) is 0.05-5%, exchanging heat with lean absorbent from the hot regeneration tower to 70-110 degrees C, sending to the flash tower for vacuum flashing, processing from the top of the flash tower to the downstream, sending the sulfur rich absorbent at the bottom of the flash distillation tower to the stripping tower for gas stripping operation, introducing the stripping tower into the lower part of the nitrogen temperature of 10-110 degrees C, controlling the pressure within the stripping tower to 0.15-0.45 MPaG, where in the gas stripping operation, further desorbing carbon dioxide (CO2) in the sulfur-rich absorbent and a small amount of H2S, combining gas from gas with the above flash gas, cooling and passing to the downstream process, discharging the stripped sulfur-rich absorbent from the bottom of the stripper, discharging the sulfur-rich absorbent from the bottom of the stripper to the thermal regeneration tower for thermal regeneration, where the operating temperature of the thermal regeneration tower is controlled to 80-105 degrees C, where operating pressure is 0.2-0.3 MPaG, dissolving acid gas sulfide in the rich absorbent and desorbing to form Claus gas, where the top of the heat output is from the regeneration tower and cooing to 30 degrees C, where the content of H2S in Claus gas is 40-80%, passing gas to the downstream sulfur recovery process, separating the condensate and returning to the top of the hot regeneration tower as reflux, heating lower portion of the hot regenerated lean absorbent from the regeneration column i.e. output, exchanging with the sulfur-rich absorbent, and sending to the downstream acid gas absorption process, where the aqueous absorbent solution is sent downstream from the bottom output of the thermal regeneration column.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The inlet of the flash evaporation tower is connected with a conveying first liquid inlet pipeline to be processed rich sulfur absorbent. the first liquid inlet pipeline is orderly connected in series with the first heat exchanger and the second heat exchanger. The flash evaporation tower is provided with a gas outlet. The first gas outlet is connected with the first air outlet pipe. The first outlet pipe is provided with a control valve for adjusting the flash tower is flash evaporation pressure. The liquid phase outlet of the flash evaporation tower is connected with the inlet of the stripping tower. The lower part of the stripping tower is connected with the air lift gas conveying pipeline, the upper part of the gas stripping tower is provided with a second gas phase outlet and liquid phase outlet of the stripping tower is connected with the heat regeneration tower. The thermal regenerating tower comprises upper and lower I section and II section two parts. The heat regeneration on part of I section of the tower and the liquid phase outlet of the gas stripping tower is connected through second liquid inlet pipeline. The I section of the heat regenerating tower is connected at the lower part of output lean absorbent first outlet pipeline. The II section of the heat regenerating tower is provided for providing the reboiler of the heat regeneration heat. The heat regenerating tower is connected with the second liquid outlet pipe conveying the absorbent aqueous solution. The molar flow rate of the gas stripping nitrogen with the rich sulfur into the gas stripping tower and the molar ratio of flow agent is 1:10000-450:10000. The first liquid outlet pipeline is provided with a bypass, the two end of the first bypass are respectively located upstream and downstream of the second heat exchanger. The first bypass is provided with a first valve. The sulfur-rich absorbent conveying pipeline is provided with a second bypass. The two end of the second bypass are respectively located upstream and downstream of the first heat exchanger and the second bypass is provided with a second valve. The first valve and the second valve are respectively used for controlling the lean absorbent enters into the first heat exchanger of the flow amount and the sulfur-rich absorbent to the flow of the second heat exchanger and controlling access to the flash temperature of sulfur-rich absorbent of the flash evaporation tower. The first bypass and the flow rate ratio of the first bypass path is 0-1:3 and the second bypass and the second bypass-flow ratio of the master is 0-1:3. The flash evaporation tower and the gas stripping tower share the same tower, between which the first end enclosure is separated and the flash evaporation tower is located above the gas stripping tower. The inlet of the flash evaporation tower and the liquid phase outlet of gas stripping tower are connected by an external pipeline. The liquid phase outlet of the stripping tower through the first circulating pump is connected with the inlet of the heat regenerating tower. The operation temperature of the flash evaporation tower is 90-100 degrees C, flash evaporation pressure is 0.3-0.4 MPaG and operating temperature of the gas stripping tower is 90-100 degrees C, and the operation pressure is 0.3-0.4 MPaG. The flash evaporation tower and the gas stripping tower share the same tower and two between the tray is isolated. The flash evaporation tower is located above the gas stripping tower. The tray is set with rising pipe enters the flash evaporation tower by the vapor in the gas stripping tower. The rising pipe forms the second vapor outlet of the stripping tower on the upper part; the descending pipe of the tray is further provided with in the flash column of the liquid phase into the gas stripping tower. The descending pipe is connected with the liquid phase outlet of the flash evaporation tower is connected with the inlet of the stripping tower. The gas stripping tower of the liquid phase outlet through the first circulating pump is connected with the inlet of the heat regenerating tower, where the tower in the operation temperature is 90-100 degrees C. The expansion pressure is 0.3-0.4 MPaG and the operating temperature of gas stripping tower is 90-100 degrees C, and the operation pressure is 0.3-0.4 MPaG. The flash evaporation tower, gas stripping tower and the heat regenerating tower share the same tower. The flash evaporation tower and the gas stripping tower are isolated by a first enclosure, gas stripping tower and the heat regenerating tower are isolated by a second enclosure and the flash and the gas stripping tower and the heat regenerating tower from top to bottom are arranged. The inlet of the flash evaporation tower and the liquid phase outlet of gas stripping tower are connected by an external pipeline, where the flash tower in the operation temperature is 90-100 degrees C, where flash evaporation pressure is 0.30 to 0.40MPaG. The operating temperature of gas stripping tower is 90-100 degrees C. The operation pressure is 0.3-0.4 MPaG, a heat regenerating tower in the operation temperature is 87-104 degrees C and the operation pressure is 0.22-0.28 MPaG.

PT P
PN CN107648165-A
TI Cream useful for e.g. improving immunity, contains macroelement, trace elements and vitamin mixed nano-powder, zinc oxide multi-element nano-composite antibacterial inorganic silver-based mixed nano-powder and viper snake gall nano-powder.
AU CHENG G
   CHENG J
AE NANTONG SNAKES TREATING INST (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2018115917
AB    NOVELTY - Cream comprises macroelement, trace elements and vitamin mixed nano-powder, zinc oxide multi-element nano-composite antibacterial inorganic silver-based mixed nano-powder, viper snake gall nano-powder and traditional Chinese medicine fluid extract. The traditional Chinese medicine fluid extract is prepared by extracting cortex phellodendri, radix scutellariae, radix angelicae sinensis and radix polygoni multiflori, with ethanol or using existing carbon dioxide, evaporating filtrate through a thin film to remove ethanol and concentrating to obtain traditional Chinese medicine fluid extract.
   USE - Cream is useful for promoting metabolism of human body and improving immunity.
   ADVANTAGE - The cream has balanced nutritional value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cream comprises 2-4 %mass macroelement, trace elements and vitamin mixed nano-powder, 4-8 %mass zinc oxide multi-element nano-composite antibacterial inorganic silver-based mixed nano-powder, 6-10 %mass viper snake gall nano-powder and 8-12 %mass traditional Chinese medicine fluid extract. The traditional Chinese medicine fluid extract is prepared by extracting cortex phellodendri, radix scutellariae, radix angelicae sinensis, radix polygoni multiflori, rhizoma polygonati odorati, rhizoma anemarrhenae, radix stemonae, cortex mori, herba hedyotidis diffusae, spica prunellae, radix scrophulariae, flos chrysanthemi indici, semen coicis, raw hawthorn, fructus kochiae, radix paeoniae rubra, cortex moutan, radix salviae miltiorrhizae, cacumen platycladi, herba violae, radix sophorae flavescentis, fructus cnidii, Pseudolarix amabilis, fructus mume and rhizoma smilacis glabrae with 60% ethanol or using existing carbon dioxide, filtering, evaporating the filtrate through a thin film to remove ethanol and concentrating the filtrate to obtain traditional Chinese medicine fluid extract with a relative density of 1.03-1.05. The macroelement comprises 40-120 mass% oxygen, 4-12 mass% carbon, 5-15 mass% hydrogen, 0.02-0.075 mass% nitrogen, 0.02-0.06 mass% calcium, 0.02-0.06 mass% phosphorus, 0.01-0.03 mass% sulfur, 0.05-0.15 mass% potassium, 0.07-0.21 mass% sodium, 0.08-0.24 mass% chlorine, 0.01-0.03 mass% magnesium and 0.01-0.03 %mass silicon. The total amount of macroelement is 99.95 %mass. PN CN113293330-A; CN113293330-B
TI Preparation of magnesium alloy containing micro ytterbium used for preparing alternative material of degradable medical implant by melting pure magnesium with sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide mixed gas, and adding intermediate alloy.
AU LI L
   LIU C
   ZHANG C
   ZHOU W
   BAI X
   ZHONG H
   JIANG J
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST (UYSW-C)
GA 2021A0458N
AB    NOVELTY - A magnesium alloy comprises 0.5-1.5 wt.% ytterbium, and balance magnesium. The magnesium alloy is prepared by melting pure magnesium under protection of sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide mixed gas, fully dissolving, adding magnesium-ytterbium intermediate alloy with ytterbium content of 0.5-1.5%, melting alloy at 750-770 degrees C, continuously stirring for 5-10 minutes by using spiral electromagnetic field, standing and keeping temperature for 15-20 minutes at 700-710 degrees C, discharging and casting, and naturally cooling to room temperature; keeping temperature for 24-36 hours at 400-450 degrees C, ensuring second phase to be dissolved and crystal grain has no abnormal growth, and discharging hydrocortisone to room temperature; heating blank after solution treatment to 300-350 degrees C, and hot extruding; and heating extruded blank to 400-450 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, and obtaining alloy is uniform isoxial crystal grain, average grain size is less than or equal to 15 mu m, and residual second phase area percentage is less than or equal to 1 %.
   USE - Micro-ytterbium-containing magnesium alloy is used for preparing biodegradable medical implant material.
   ADVANTAGE - The micro ytterbium-containing magnesium alloy has good strength, plasticity, corrosion resistance and cell compatibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Magnesium alloy comprises 0.5-1.5 wt.% ytterbium, and balance magnesium. The magnesium alloy is prepared by melting pure magnesium under protection of sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide mixed gas, fully dissolving, adding magnesium-ytterbium intermediate alloy with ytterbium content of 0.5-1.5%, melting alloy at 750-770 degrees C, continuously stirring for 5-10 minutes by using spiral electromagnetic field, standing and keeping temperature for 15-20 minutes at 700-710 degrees C, discharging and casting, and naturally cooling to room temperature; keeping temperature for 24-36 hours at 400-450 degrees C, ensuring second phase to be dissolved and crystal grain has no abnormal growth, and discharging hydrocortisone to room temperature; heating blank after solution treatment to 300-350 degrees C, hot extruding with extrusion ratio of 15-30:1 and extrusion speed of 0.05-0.1 mm/second, monitoring temperature in extrusion cylinder in real time, ensuring temperature difference between extrusion cylinder and extrusion blank is less than or equal to 10 degrees C, and quickly extruding to room temperature; and heating extruded blank under protection of argon gas to 400-450 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, and obtaining alloy is uniform isoxial crystal grain, average grain size is less than or equal to 15 mu m, and residual second phase area percentage is less than or equal to 1 %.

PT P
PN CN112661682-A
TI Producing DL-methionine, by hydrolyzing hydantoin solution, stripping saponification liquid to remove ammonia and carbon dioxide, acidifying, crystallizing, re-dissolving secondary methionine in saponification solution and extracting.
AU CAO H
   ZHANG C
   WU Y
   LIAN X
   LIU B
   FAN Q
   ZENG T
   LIU Y
AE NINGXIA ZIGUANG TIANHUA METHIONINE CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202150949C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing DL-methionine, involves (i) hydrolyzing hydantoin solution, (ii) stripping saponification liquid to remove ammonia and carbon dioxide, (iii) acidifying deammonia saponification liquid, cooling and crystallizing, (iv) concentrating primary mother liquor, and performing solid-liquid separation, (v) adding a solution containing sulfuric acid to filtrate, and performing cooling and crystallization, (vi) re-dissolving secondary methionine in saponification solution to continue to extract methionine product, (vii) adding sulfuric acid to mother liquor of sodium sulfate to obtain an acidified solution, (viii) using an organic solvent to perform extraction on oil phase of acidified liquid, separating organic impurity-containing solution and aqueous phase, and using aqueous phase to acidify deammonia saponification liquid, and (ix) using an alkaline solution to perform secondary extraction on oil phase in organic impurity-containing solution.
   USE - The method is used for producing DL-methionine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing DL-methionine, involves (i) hydrolyzing hydantoin solution with an alkaline solution of sodium to obtain a saponified liquid, (ii) stripping the saponification liquid to remove ammonia and carbon dioxide to obtain a stripping liquid and a de-ammonia saponification liquid, (iii) acidifying the deammonia saponification liquid, cooling and crystallizing to separate to obtain a methionine filter cake and a primary mother liquor, (iv) concentrating the primary mother liquor, adjusting the pH value of the primary mother liquor using sodium alkali solution or saponification solution during the concentration process, and performing solid-liquid separation to obtain sodium sulfate and filtrate, (v) adding a solution containing sulfuric acid to the filtrate obtained in step (iv) to adjust the pH value of the filtrate, and performing cooling and crystallization separation to obtain secondary methionine and a secondary mother liquor, (vi) re-dissolving the secondary methionine in the saponification solution of step (i) to continue to extract the methionine product, when the ratio of the impurity content in the secondary methionine to the methionine content not greater than 3%, making the secondary mother liquor as a new primary mother liquor, returning to step (iv), when the ratio of the impurity content in the secondary methionine to the methionine content exceeds 3%, the secondary mother liquor is sodium sulfate mother liquor, and performing step (vii), (vii) adding sulfuric acid to the mother liquor of sodium sulfate to obtain an acidified solution, (viii) using an organic solvent to perform one-stage extraction on the oil phase of the acidified liquid, separating the organic impurity-containing solution and the aqueous phase, and using the aqueous phase as an acidifier to acidify the deammonia saponification liquid or filtrate after high-temperature steam stripping, and (ix) using an alkaline solution to perform secondary extraction on the oil phase in the organic impurity-containing solution to obtain a clean organic solvent and an impurity-containing aqueous solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (iii) involves (iiia) heating the deammonia saponification solution at a constant temperature, maintaining the temperature of the deammination saponification solution at 60-120 degrees C, adding a solution containing sulfuric acid to acidify the deammination saponification solution, where the pH value of the acidified deammonia saponification solution is 5-7, and heat-preserving for 10-30 minutes, (iiib) decreasing the temperature of the acidified deaminated saponification liquid obtained in step (iiia) to 35-45 degrees C, heat-preserving for 20-60 minutes, and stirring the deaminated saponification liquid during the heat preservation process, and (iiic) performing solid-liquid separation of the deammoniacated saponification liquid obtained in step (iiib) after cooling to obtain a methionine filter cake and a primary mother liquor. The step (iv) involves adjusting the pH value of the first-stage mother liquor using sodium alkali solution or saponification solution, stopping the concentration when the methionine content in the first-stage mother liquor is greater than 8 wt.% and the pH value of the first-stage mother liquor after concentration is 7.5-9, performing direct solid-liquid separation of the first-level mother liquor after concentration to obtain sodium sulfate and filtrate, and using an acidic ion exchange resin to recover the distilled water generated during the concentration of the primary mother liquor. The step (v) involves (va) heating the filtrate obtained in step (iv) at 60-120 degrees C, adding a solution containing sulfuric acid to acidify the filtrate, where the pH value of the acidified filtrate is 6-8, and maintaining the temperature for 10-30 minutes, (vb) reducing the temperature of the acidified filtrate obtained in step (va) to 35-45 degrees C, heat-preserving for 20-60 minutes, and stirring the filtrate during the heat preservation process, and (vc) performing solid-liquid separation on the cooled filtrate obtained in step (vb) to obtain secondary methionine and secondary mother liquor. The step (viii) involves separating the formic acid in the aqueous phase by steam stripping, and collecting and processing the separated formic acid. The method involves after the impurity-containing aqueous solution is incinerated in step (ix), using the residue to prepare an alkali solution to continue to extract the organic impurity-containing solution. Preferred Conditions: In step (vii), sulfuric acid is added to the sodium sulfate mother liquor until the pH value of the sodium sulfate mother liquor is below 3. Preferred Composition: The concentration of sulfuric acid added in the mother liquor of sodium sulfate in step (vii) is 20-98 wt.%. Preferred Components: The organic solvent used in step (viii)is an alcohol with a carbon chain greater than 5.

AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)

AB    (CN212504738-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a gasification furnace containing carbon waste, using the gasification furnace can conveniently integrate the combustible waste gas and combustible carbon-containing waste in a gasification furnace for gasification to generate synthesis gas. the gasification furnace comprises a gasification chamber and a chilling chamber; the bottom of the gasification chamber is provided with a lower slag hole; the lower slag opening is provided with a descending pipe; the top of the gasification chamber is provided with a nozzle; the nozzle comprises a central annular channel for inputting the combustible waste gas from inside to outside; a first annular channel for inputting the oxidant; a second annular channel for inputting combustible carbon-containing waste; a third annular channel for inputting oxidant; a combustion space for reacting the combustible waste gas and the oxidant is arranged between the outlet at the bottom of the central annular channel and the outlet at the bottom of the first annular channel; a sweeping device is installed between the lower slag opening and the descending pipe; the sweeping device is provided with a sweeping port.
