PN CN111690551-A
TI Separating, purifying, culturing and identifying Brucella comprises e.g. collecting target sample, sterile collecting sample, testing sterile collected sample, adding melted glass test tube semi-solid culture medium, and slightly vibrating.
AU TIAN G
AE NAT INST COMMUNICABLE DISEASES (CCDC-C)
GA 202095355V
AB    NOVELTY - Separating, purifying, culturing and identifying the Brucella comprises e.g. (i) collecting the target sample, sterile collecting the sample to be tested, and (ii) separating, purifying, culturing and identifying comprises e.g. (iia) testing sterile collected sample, adding melted glass test tube semi-solid culture medium in 2 minutes, slightly vibrating and mixing uniformly, pouring into flat solid culture medium beside the alcohol lamp, rotating lightly for 3 times, placing for 1 hour at room temperature, and solidifying, (iib) adding the solidified sample solid culture medium, placing at 37 degrees C, culturing in the environment containing 5% carbon dioxide, and (iic) culturing for 24 hours, under the condition of sufficient light, observing the dish solid culture medium loaded with specimen, if there is colony growth, removing Brucella after 10 days, observing the colony on the basis of eliminating 24 hour observation, observing the growth condition of bacteria.
   USE - The method is useful for separating, purifying, culturing and identifying the Brucella.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has easily prepared and low cost medium, has simple and easy sample processing, simple culture conditions, few observation procedures, easily observed cultivation results, improves detection rate of Brucella, and solves difficult culturing problems of brucella, and has low isolation rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating, purifying, culturing and identifying the Brucella comprises (i) collecting the target sample, sterile collecting the sample to be tested, and (ii) Separating, purifying, culturing and identifying comprises (iia) testing sterile collected sample, adding melted glass test tube semi-solid culture medium in 2 minutes, slightly vibrating and mixing uniformly, pouring into flat solid culture medium beside the alcohol lamp, rotating lightly for 3 times, placing for 1 hour at room temperature, and solidifying, (iib) adding the solidified sample solid culture medium in step (i), placing at 37 degrees C, culturing in the environment containing 5% carbon dioxide, (iic) culturing for 24 hours, under the condition of sufficient light, observing the dish solid culture medium loaded with specimen, if there is colony growth, removing Brucella after 10 days, observing the colony on the basis of eliminating 24 hour observation, observing the growth condition of bacteria, if there is transparent colony, primarily identifying as Brucella, and recording the colony number, (iid) selecting single colony from the solid culture medium, inoculating on the solid culture medium, placing the solid culture medium at 37 degrees C, purifying and culturing in the environment containing 5% carbon dioxide, culturing for 48 hours, and (iie) selecting bacteria from purified flat solid culture medium, preparing bacteria suspension, extracting the bacterial genome DNA, detecting by polymerase chain reaction (PCR) method, PCR positive is identified as Brucella.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method, the preparation of the glass test tube semi-solid culture medium is as follows: using 10 g peptone, 5 g beef liver dipping powder, 10 g glucose, 5 g sodium chloride, 0.1 g sodium bisulfite, 1 g dextrose, 7 g agar, using distilled water for uniformly dissolving the raw material, modulating to 1000 ml, dissolving into transparent shape in water boiling, packing in the glass test tube, filling each test tube with 10 ml culture medium solution, high pressure sterilizing at 121 degrees C for 30 minutes, cooling at room temperature, preparing into glass test tube semi-solid culture medium, where is increased in different multiples. The preparation of the solid culture medium is as follows: using 10 g peptone, 5 g beef liver dipping powder, 10 g glucose, 5 g sodium chloride, 0.1 g sodium bisulfite, 1 g dextrose, 15 g agar, using distilled water for uniformly dissolving the raw material, modulating to 1000ml, high pressure sterilizing at 121 degrees C for 30 minutes, cooling to 60 degrees C, preparing into dish solid culture medium, where is increased in different multiples. In the method, placing at 37 degrees C environment and containing 5% carbon dioxide is incubator or glass cylinder that forms 5% carbon dioxide environment. In the method, the PCR method detection is PCR technique method with primers proved to be sensitive and specific to Brucella, adding genomic DNA to be detected to the PCR reaction mixture to form PCR reaction system for PCR amplification, where the PCR reaction mixture comprises PCR buffer 10XBuffer, 4 kinds of deoxynucleoside triphosphate (dNTP) mixtures, Taq enzyme, forward primer B4 and reverse primer B5, and mixture of double distilled water, (1) PCR primers comprising forward primer B4 comprises sequence represented by 5'-tggctcggttgccaatatcaa-3' (SEQ ID NO: 1), reverse primer B5 comprises sequence represented by 5'-cgcgcttgcctttcaggtctg-3' (SEQ ID NO: 2), (2) the PCR reaction system comprises 2.5 mu l PCR buffer 10XBuffer (Mg2+, 15 mmol/l), 2 mu l 4 kinds of dNTP mixture, 0.2 mu l Taq enzyme (TaKaRa Taq (RTM: Recombinant version of full-length Taq polymerase) enzyme 5 U/ mu l), 0.6 mu l forward primer B4 and reverse primer B5, 4 mu l genomic DNA, adding double distilled water into 25 mu l volume, (3) the amplification condition is as follows: pre-denaturing at 95 degrees C for 4 minutes, denaturing at 95 degrees C for 30 seconds, annealing at 55 degrees C for 30 seconds, extending at 72 degrees C for 30 seconds, 30 cycles, extending at 72 degrees C for 5 minutes, (4) subjecting the amplified products to 1% agarose gel electrophoresis, the 1% agarose gel is agarose, the mass to volume ratio of the gel is 0.01 g/ml, subjecting the PCR products to 90 V voltage, 1% agarose gel electrophoresis for 1 hour, subjecting to gel imaging analysis, as result of electrophoresis, the tested sample PCR amplified 224 bp band of interest, indicating the presence of Brucella nucleic acid DNA in the sample, i.e., the PCR test is positive, if the tested sample does not has 224 bp band, indicates that there is no Brucella nucleic acid DNA in the tested sample, or the amount of nucleic acid DNA is too small to be detected by PCR, i.e., the PCR test is negative. Preferred Components: The glass test tube has semi-solid medium, and 50 ml glass test tube is filled with 10 ml culture medium solution. The flat dish solid culture medium adopts plastic product round container having diameter of 90 mm and whole height of 20 mm, and each of the dishes is charged with 25 ml culture medium.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.)

PN CN111393630-A; CN111393630-B
TI Preparing polymer polyol involves subjecting one or more of epoxides, carbon dioxide, cyclic esters, and cyclic anhydrides under action of non-heavy metal center catalytic system to ring-opening (co)polymerization to obtain polymer polyols.
AU CAO H
   ZHOU Q
   WANG X
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202066700D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polymer polyol involves subjecting one or more of epoxides, carbon dioxide, cyclic esters, and cyclic anhydrides under the action of the non-heavy metal center catalytic system to ring-opening (co)polymerization to obtain polymer polyols. The non-heavy metal central catalytic system includes an aluminum-based catalyst and an initiator. The aluminum-based catalyst is an aluminum porphyrin oligomer catalyst and aluminum porphyrin derivative (I). The initiator is selected from one of water, small alcohol, phenol, mercaptan, carboxylic acid, hydroxy acid and hydroxyl-containing oligomer or more.
   USE - Method for preparing polymer polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare polymer polyol which realizes the efficient preparation of poly(carbonate-ether) polyols with different carbonate unit content (carbonate content 20-80%), and content of the cyclic carbonate by-product in the product is less than 1%, reduces the separation energy consumption and cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polymer polyol involves subjecting one or more of epoxides, carbon dioxide, cyclic esters, and cyclic anhydrides under the action of the non-heavy metal center catalytic system to ring-opening (co)polymerization to obtain polymer polyols. The non-heavy metal central catalytic system includes an aluminum-based catalyst and an initiator. The aluminum-based catalyst is an aluminum porphyrin oligomer catalyst and aluminum porphyrin derivative of formula (I).
   C1=main chain structure of formula (II);
   C2=bond chain group of formula (III);
   por-Al'=aluminum porphyrin complex of formula (IV);
   Ra' and Rb'=hydrogen, halogen, aliphatic group substituted by aliphatic group, substituted heteroaliphatic group, aryl group, substituted aryl group or substituted heteroaryl group;
   Z=formula a or b;
   Rc=C1-C12 alkyl groups;
   q=0 or 1;
   Rd=selected from 1-12C alkyl groups;
   R'=selected from the formula c;
   m=chain length or the degree of polymerization, with a value of 4-20;
   Nu=quaternary ammonium salt anion with ability to initiate polymerization;
   x=number of Nu, with a value of 0 to 3;
   R1, R2 and R3=functionalized substituents or quaternary ammonium salt cations of the porphyrin complex; and
   X=group consisting of halide, NO3, CH3COO, CCl3COO, CF3COO, ClO4, BF4, BPh4, CN, N3, p-toluate, p-toluenesulfonate, o-nitrophenol oxygen anion, p-nitrophenol oxygen anion, m-nitrophenol oxygen anion, 2,4-dinitro phenoxy anion, 3,5-dinitrophenol oxygen anion, 2,4,6-trinitrophenol oxygen anion, 3,5-dichlorophenol oxygen anion, 3,5-difluorophenol oxygen anion, 3, 5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenol oxygen anion or pentafluorophenol oxygen anion.
   .. The initiator is selected from one of water, small alcohol, phenol, mercaptan, carboxylic acid, hydroxy acid and hydroxyl-containing oligomer or more.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The functionalized substituent is selected from hydrogen, halogen or quaternary ammonium cation. The quaternary ammonium salt cation is selected from tetra-n-butylammonium, tetraisobutylammonium, tetra-n-hexylammonium, and tetra-n-decylammonium. The one or two of the epoxide, carbon dioxide, cyclic ester, and cyclic anhydride undergo ring-opening (co)polymerization, including ring-opening polymerization of epoxides, ring-opening polymerization of cyclic esters, and epoxy ring-opening copolymerization of compound and cyclic anhydride and ring-opening copolymerization of epoxide and carbon dioxide. The polymer polyol is selected from polyether polyols, polyester polyols, polyester-ether polyols, poly(carbonate-ether) polyols or their multi-copolymer polyols. The epoxide comprises ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1-butylene oxide, 2-butylene oxide, cyclohexane oxide, epoxycyclopentane, epichlorohydrin glycidyl methacrylate, methyl glycidyl ether, phenyl glycidyl ether, styrene alkylene oxide, 4-vinyl-1,2-epoxycyclohexane, and one or more of vinyl propylene oxide. The cyclic ester is selected from one or more of L-lactide, DL-lactide, beta-butyrolactone, delta-valerolactone, and epsilon -caprolactone. The cyclic anhydride is selected from maleic anhydride, phthalic anhydride, cyclobutane-1,2-dicarboxylic anhydride, methyl nadic anhydride, norbornenic anhydride, cyclohexanedicarboxylic anhydride, succinic anhydride, hexahydrophthalic anhydride, itaconic anhydride, cyclopentane-1,2-formic anhydride, dodecenyl succinic anhydride, allyl butyric anhydride, methyltetrahydrophthalic anhydride, glutaric anhydride, trimethylglutaric anhydride, 3-oxabicyclo(3.1.0)hexane-2,4-dion, beta-(4-chlorophenyl)glutaric anhydride, and 3,3-dimethylglutaric anhydride. The initiator is further selected from one or more of water, small molecular alcohol, phenol, mercaptan, carboxylic acid, hydroxy acid and hydroxyl-containing oligomer. The small molecular alcohol is ethylene glycol, 1,2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,2-butanediol, 1,3-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, 1,5-hexanediol, 1,6-hexanediol, octanediol, hexadiol, dipropylene glycol, 1,3-cyclopentanediol, 1,2- Cyclohexanediol, 1,3-cyclohexanediol, 1,4-cyclohexanediol, 1,2-cyclohexanedimethanol, diethylene glycol, triethylene glycol, tetraethylene glycol, polyethylene glycol, dipropylene glycol, tripropylene glycol, polypropylene glycol, trimethylolethane, trimethylolpropane, glycerin, 1,2,4-butanetriol, polyestertriol, pentaerythritol, xylitol, sorbitol, tripentanol and polyglycidol oligomers. The phenol is catechol, resorcinol, hydroquinone, pyrogallol, phloroglucinol, 4,4'-ethylene bisphenol, 4,4'-(2-methyl Propylene) bisphenol, 4,4-(2-ethylhexylene)bisphenol, 2,2'-methylenebisphenol or 2,2'-(1,2-cyclohexadiyl-bisnitrosoethylene)bis phenol. The carboxylic acid is preferably malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid, terephthalic acid, phthalic acid, isophthalic acid, maleic acid, homo trimellitic acid, pyromellitic acid or oleic acid. The hydroxy acid is lactic acid, hydroxybutyric acid, hydroxyvaleric acid, hydroxysuccinic acid, tartaric acid, citric acid or salicylic acid. Preferred Compositions: The molar ratio of the metal center and reactive monomer is 1:(2000-200000). The molar ratio of the monomer and the initiator is 100:(1-12).
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 6 compounds of aluminum porphyrin oligomer derivative based catalyst are specifically claimed such as group of formulae (V)-(VII)..

PN CN111362330-A; CN111362330-B
TI Preparing hydrogen sulfide by stepwise reduction of sulfur dioxide, comprises using high-concentration sulfur dioxide gas, mixing with reducing agent, controlling temperature of mixed gas and entering sulfur dioxide pre-reductor.
AU HUANG W
   YAN N
   QU Z
   XU H
   SUN X
   FENG S
   FENG J
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
   NANTONG SUNSHINE GRAPHITE EQUIP TECHNOLO (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 202063656Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen sulfide by stepwise reduction of sulfur dioxide, comprises using high-concentration sulfur dioxide gas as a raw material, mixing it with a reducing agent, and controlling the temperature of the mixed gas at 200-500 degrees C range, and then entering the sulfur dioxide pre-reductor. The sulfur dioxide is reduced to active elemental sulfur through the action of a catalyst. The hot gas stream from the pre-reductor is mixed with natural gas, and after temperature adjustment, it enters the secondary reducer, and forms a mixture of carbon disulfide, hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide through the reaction of active elemental sulfur and methane. The mixed gas from the secondary reducer is mixed with water vapor and then enters the primary hydrolysis reactor, where most of the carbon disulfide in the gas stream is hydrolyzed into hydrogen sulfide gas under the action of the catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing hydrogen sulfide by cascade reduction of sulfur dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare hydrogen sulfide by cascade reduction of sulfur dioxide, which has low sulfur dioxide reduction temperature, high hydrogen sulfide conversion rate, and good controllability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hydrogen sulfide by stepwise reduction of sulfur dioxide, comprises using high-concentration sulfur dioxide gas as a raw material, mixing it with a reducing agent, and controlling the temperature of the mixed gas at 200-500 degrees C range, and then entering the sulfur dioxide pre-reductor. The sulfur dioxide is reduced to active elemental sulfur through the action of a catalyst. The hot gas stream from the pre-reductor is mixed with natural gas, and after temperature adjustment, it enters the secondary reducer, and forms a mixture of carbon disulfide, hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide through the reaction of active elemental sulfur and methane. The mixed gas from the secondary reducer is mixed with water vapor and then enters the primary hydrolysis reactor, where most of the carbon disulfide in the gas stream is hydrolyzed into hydrogen sulfide gas under the action of the catalyst. The gas stream flowing out of the primary hydrolysis reactor is divided into two streams, of which the first stream is returned to the sulfur dioxide pre-reductor to be used as a reducing agent. The second stream of remaining gas stream is supplemented with steam, and then enters the deep hydrolysis reactor. The function is to fully hydrolyze the residual carbon disulfide, carbonyl sulfide and organic sulfur into hydrogen sulfide, and then after cooling down, the residual moisture is condensed to form a mixture of high concentration hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of carbon disulfide in the mixed gas from the secondary reducer to the added water vapor is 1:1.5-2.0. The first share accounts for 50-70 wt.% (Vol/Vol), which is returned to the pre-reductor as a reducing agent for sulfur dioxide. The second residual gas stream is mixed with the supplementary steam and then enters the deep hydrolysis reactor. The ratio of the supplementary steam amount to the residual carbon disulfide in the second residual gas stream is 4 to 5:1.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Facility-based and efficient reuse system useful            for agricultural waste, comprises a fermentation tank,            where the top of the fermentation tank is provided with            an inlet for adding agricultural waste.
AU ZHONG F
   SHANG C
   WANG S
   XU R
   XIE H
   MA X
   LIN Y
   WANG J
   HE X
   YANG D
   DU Z
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 2020636474
AB    NOVELTY - Facility-based and efficient reuse system,                comprises a fermentation tank, where the top of the                fermentation tank is provided with an inlet for                adding agricultural waste, and the left and right                sides are respectively provided with an air inlet                and an air outlet. The bottom is provided with a                discharge port, the air inlet is connected to the                gas generation box, the gas generation box is                provided with a blower, the air outlet is connected                to the greenhouse through an outdoor vent pipe, and                an indoor vent pipe is provided in the greenhouse.                The indoor vent pipe is connected with the outdoor                vent pipe, the indoor vent pipe is provided with                multiple vent holes, and the discharge port is                connected with the fermentation residue                solid-liquid separation device.
   USE - The facility-based and efficient reuse system                is useful for agricultural waste (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device promotes growth and development of                plant facilities, and also can make full use of                agricultural waste, protects environment and                resource recycling effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                facility-based and efficient reuse method for                agricultural waste using the system, comprising (i)                mixing agricultural waste, fermentation inoculants,                amino acid additives, and water, and adding into                the fermentation tank from the feed port for                fermentation, during the fermentation process, heat                and carbon dioxide gas will be continuously                produced, using the blower in the gas generating                box for drying the air, passing into the fermenter                through the air inlet, and passing the heat and                carbon dioxide gas generated in the fermenter                through the air outlet under the action of the air                pressure, and then passing the outdoor air duct                into the indoor air duct in the greenhouse, and                then slowly passing through the air outlet, letting                the stomata spread to the whole greenhouse, and                increasing the temperature and carbon dioxide                concentration of the greenhouse; (ii) passing the                fermented solid residue and fermentation liquid                from fermentor into the granular resin permeable                ceramic in the fermentation residue solid-liquid                separation device through the discharge port at the                bottom of the fermentor to separate the solid                residue from the fermentation liquid, and storing                the separated solid residue in the granular resin                permeable ceramics, storing the fermentation broth                between the granular resin permeable ceramics and                the fermentation residue collection tank; and (iii)                mixing the solid residue obtained in step (ii) with                coconut bran, vermiculite, perlite, and plant ash                in a ratio of 1:3:1:1:2 to make organic soil, which                is used as a greenhouse cultivation substrate;                diluting the fermentation liquid 50 times, and                directly using for spray irrigation of cultivated                plants in the greenhouse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The agricultural waste                  includes straw and mushroom residue. The mass                  ratio of the agricultural waste, the fermentation                  agent, the amino acid-containing additive and                  water is 1000:2:3:100. The amino acid-containing                  additive includes 20% seaweed polysaccharide, 30%                  chitosan, 17% tryptophan, 15% lysine A mixed                  additive of acid, 13% threonine, 10% glycine and                  5% arginine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The outer layer of the                  fermentation tank is provided with a                  rubber-plastic insulation layer, and the                  thickness of the insulation layer is 2 cm. The                  outdoor vent pipe and the indoor vent pipe                  material are both PCV materials, and the pipe                  diameter is 20 cm, and the vent on the indoor                  vent pipe stomatal spacing is 10 cm and the                  diameter is 5 cm. The fermentation residue                  solid-liquid separation device includes a                  particulate resin permeable ceramic and a                  fermentation residue collection tank, the                  particulate resin permeable ceramic is located in                  the fermentation residue collection tank                  internal. The air volume of the blower is 120                  m3/minute, the power of the fan is 22 KW, and the                  fan blade speed of the fan is 2950 revolutions                  per minute.

PN CN110972808-A; CN110972808-B
TI Cultivating industrialized bottled spores of            Hymenopellis raphanipes comprises e.g. filling hole            with solid culture medium, sealing bottle mouth,            sterilizing, cooling, inoculating, dark culturing            primordium, and harvesting in time.
AU MO M
   YUAN Z
   SU C
   YANG Y
   LU Y
   XUE L
   CHEN D
   JIANG Y
   LI W
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA AGRIC (USCG-C)
GA 202030546W
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating industrialized bottled spores of                Hymenopellis raphanipes comprises e.g. (i) filling                culture medium with 80-85% solid culture medium,                filling the bottle with hole in the middle to the                bottom of the bottle, sealing the bottle mouth,                sterilizing, cooling, inoculating each bottle with                solid medium volume of 4/100-7/100 of the second                species of Hymenopellis raphanipes or 3-5 liquids                of the first species of Hymenopellis raphanipes,                placing the cultivation bottle horizontally,                maintaining in the dark at 26-30 degrees C,                periodically turning the bottle over for 4-6 days,                when the mycelium ratio is 90-95% of the total                volume of the solid medium, slowly cooling to 18-22                degrees C, continuously culturing, when the                mycelium is eaten through the fungus, adjusting the                temperature to 24-26 degrees C to carry out the                post-ripening cultivation, adjusting temperature to                28-32 degrees C, and continuously cultivating to                obtain cultivar.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating                industrialized bottled spores of Hymenopellis                raphanipes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short production cycle. The                Hymenopellis raphanipes has good quality, stable                quality and has consistent growth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating industrialized bottled spores of                Hymenopellis raphanipes further comprises e.g. (ii)                scratching the cultivars obtained in step (i),                where the depth of scratching bacteria is 2-2.5 cm                from the bottom of the bottle to the material                surface, above the shoulder of the bottle to                material surface is 1 cm, opening the bottle mouth,                placing upside down on sterilized wet cloth, where                the temperature is 24-26 degrees C, the humidity is                85-98%, and the carbon dioxide concentration is                400-800ppm, controlling the temperature at 26-30                degrees C, the humidity at 85-95%, restoring the                mycelium and preventing the contamination of                miscellaneous bacteria, completing restoring,                adjusting the temperature to 30-32 degrees C, the                humidity is 92-98%, continuously culturing in the                dark until the mycelium of Hymenopellis raphanipes                is kinked to form primordial, (iii) the inducement                temperature is 26-30 degrees C, the humidity is                90-98%, dark culturing primordium obtained in the                step (ii) under the condition of carbon dioxide                concentration of 800-2000 ppm, when the mushroom                buds grow to 0.3-0.7 cm, removing the wet cloth,                placing the cultivation bottle upright, and (iv)                the growth temperature is 26-32 degrees C, the                humidity is 85-98%, the concentration of carbon                dioxide is 300-1500 ppm and the illumination                intensity is 120-600 lx under the condition of                culturing buds obtained in step (iii), when the                mushroom grows to 12-23 cm, completing growing, and                harvesting in time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises in the step (i), the condition of                  turning over the bottle and adjusting the                  temperature to the ratio of the mycelium is as                  follows: (ia) cultivating for 1 day, controlling                  the temperature at 26-28 degrees C, (ib)                  cultivating for 5-7 days, controlling the                  temperature at 28-30 degrees C, and the bottle is                  turned horizontally by 180 degrees C on the 5th                  day, (ic) cultivating for 5-9 days, controlling                  the temperature at 28-30 degrees C, and the                  bottle is turned horizontally by 180 degrees C on                  the 5th day, (id) cultivating 4-6 days, when the                  mycelium feed is 90-95% of the total volume of                  the solid medium, controlling the temperature at                  18-22 degrees C, (ie) cultivating for 1 day, when                  the mycelium eats the fungus, turning the bottle                  body horizontally by 180 degrees , controlling                  the temperature at 24-26 degrees C, (if)                  cultivating for 10-15 days, controlling the                  temperature at 28-32 degrees C, and producing                  yellow water on the surface of the mycelium of                  Hymenopellis raphanipes to obtain cultivated                  cultivar, and (ig) cultivating for 1 day,                  controlling the temperature at 28-32 degrees C,                  and completing the cultivation, In the step (ii),                  the control of temperature and humidity is as                  follows: (iia) cultivating for 1 day, controlling                  the temperature at 24-26 degrees C, humidity at                  85-98%, and the carbon dioxide concentration at                  400-800 ppm, (iib) cultivating for 6-9 days,                  controlling the temperature at 24-26 degrees C,                  humidity at 85-98%, and carbon dioxide                  concentration control at 400-800 ppm, (iic)                  cultivating for 1 day, controlling the                  temperature at 26-30 degrees C, humidity at                  85-95%, and the concentration of carbon dioxide                  at 400-800 ppm to restore the mycelium and                  preventing the contamination of miscellaneous                  bacteria, and (iid) cultivating for 7-10 days,                  completely recovering mycelium, controlling the                  temperature at 30-32 degrees C, humidity at                  92-98%, the carbon dioxide concentration at                  400-800 ppm, and the surface of the mycelium                  formed by the mycelia produces milky yellow                  bright spots when the primordium is formed, and                  completing cultivation, in the step (iii), the                  control of temperature, humidity and carbon                  dioxide concentration is as follows: (iiia)                  cultivating for 1 day, controlling the                  temperature at 26-30 degrees C, humidity at                  90-98%, and the carbon dioxide concentration at                  800-2000 ppm, and (iiib) cultivating for 3-4                  days, controlling the temperature at 26-30                  degrees C, humidity at 90-98%, carbon dioxide                  concentration at 800-2000 ppm, growing mushroom                  bud to 0.3-0.7 cm, fetching wet cloth, and                  vertically placing the culture bottle, and in the                  step (iv), controlling the temperature, humidity,                  carbon dioxide concentration and light is as                  follows: (iva) cultivating for 3 days,                  controlling the temperature at 26-30 degrees C,                  humidity at 90-98%, carbon dioxide concentration                  at 300-700 ppm, light intensity at 120-600 lx,                  and the light time is 8 hours, (ivb) cultivating                  for 1 day, performing bud thinning, controlling                  the temperature at 30-32 degrees C, humidity at                  85-90%, carbon dioxide concentration at 700-1500                  ppm, light intensity at 120-600 lx, and the light                  time is 8 hours, and (ivc) cultivating for 5-6                  days, controlling the temperature at 28-32                  degrees C, humidity at 90-98%, carbon dioxide                  concentration at 700-1000 ppm, light intensity at                  120-600 lx, the light time is 8 hours, growing                  mushroom to 15-23 cm, and harvesting. In the step                  (i), preparing solid medium is as follows: adding                  85.2 wt.% fermented cottonseed husk, 10.2 wt.%                  wheat bran, 2.6 wt.% glucose, 0.3 wt.% magnesium                  sulfate, 1.2 wt.% monopotassium phosphate, and                  0.5% calcium carbonate into water, stirring to                  mix, adjusting the water content of the solid                  medium to 58-68%, and pH to 7-9, obtaining                  fermented cottonseed husk by adding water and                  stirring, adding lime water to adjust the pH to                  8-9, piling up and fermenting for 7-10 days. In                  the step (i), the condition of turning over the                  bottle and adjusting the temperature to the ratio                  of the mycelium is specifically as follows: (iA)                  cultivating for 1 day, controlling the                  temperature at 26 degrees C, (iB) cultivating for                  5-7 days, controlling the temperature at 30                  degrees C, and the bottle is turned horizontally                  by 180 degrees C on the 5th day, (iC) cultivating                  for 5-9 days, controlling the temperature at 30                  degrees C, and the bottle is turned horizontally                  by 180 degrees C on the 5th day, (iD) cultivating                  4-6 days, when the mycelium feed is 92% of the                  total volume of the solid medium, controlling the                  temperature at 22 degrees C, (iE) cultivating for                  1 day, when the mycelium eats the fungus, turning                  the bottle body horizontally by 180 degrees ,                  controlling the temperature at 26 degrees C, (iF)                  cultivating for 10-15 days, controlling the                  temperature at 32 degrees C, and producing yellow                  water on the surface of the mycelium of                  Hymenopellis raphanipes to obtain cultivated                  cultivar, and (iG) cultivating for 1 day,                  controlling the temperature at 32 degrees C, and                  completing the cultivation. In the step (ii), the                  control of temperature and humidity is                  specifically as follows: (iiA) cultivating for 1                  day, controlling the temperature at 24 degrees C,                  humidity at 90%, and the carbon dioxide                  concentration at 800 ppm, (iiB) cultivating for                  6-9 days, controlling the temperature at 24                  degrees C, humidity at 90%, and carbon dioxide                  concentration control at 800 ppm, (iiC)                  cultivating for 1 day, controlling the                  temperature at 30 degrees C, humidity at 90%, and                  the concentration of carbon dioxide at 800 ppm to                  restore the mycelium and preventing the                  contamination of miscellaneous bacteria, and                  (iiD) cultivating for 7-10 days, completely                  recovering mycelium, controlling the temperature                  at 32 degrees C, humidity at 92%, the carbon                  dioxide concentration at 800 ppm, and the surface                  of the mycelium formed by the mycelia produces                  milky yellow bright spots when the primordium is                  formed, and completing cultivation. In the step                  (iii), the control of temperature, humidity and                  carbon dioxide concentration is specifically as                  follows: (iiiA) cultivating for 1 day,                  controlling the temperature at 30 degrees C,                  humidity at 95%, and the carbon dioxide                  concentration at 1500 ppm, and (iiiB) cultivating                  for 3-4 days, controlling the temperature at 30                  degrees C, humidity at 95%, carbon dioxide                  concentration at 1500 ppm, growing mushroom bud                  to 0.3-0.7 cm, fetching wet cloth, and vertically                  placing the culture bottle. In the step (iv),                  controlling the temperature, humidity, carbon                  dioxide concentration and light is as follows:                  (ivA) cultivating for 3 days, controlling the                  temperature at 30 degrees C, humidity at 95%,                  carbon dioxide concentration at 700 ppm, light                  intensity at 600 lx, and the light time is 8                  hours, (ivB) cultivating for 1 day, performing                  bud thinning, controlling the temperature at 32                  degrees C, humidity at 86%, carbon dioxide                  concentration at 1200 ppm, light intensity at 600                  lx, and the light time is 8 hours, and (ivC)                  cultivating for 5-6 days, controlling the                  temperature at 32 degrees C, humidity at 95%,                  carbon dioxide concentration at 800 ppm, light                  intensity at 600 lx, the light time is 8 hours,                  growing mushroom to 15-23 cm, and harvesting. In                  the step (i), the sterilization is high-pressure                  steam sterilization at temperature of 121 degrees                  C, and the pressure is 1.034 MPa for 2.5                  hours.

PT P
PN CN110331400-A; CN110331400-B
TI Preparing aluminum/copper/chromium/nickel/manganese/titanium (AlxCoCrNiMnTi) high entropy alloy coating using axial flow laser comprises e. g. pretreating substrate to obtain pre-formed cladding composed of aluminum powder.
AU QIU X
AE SICHUAN COLLEGE ARCHITECTURAL TECHNOLOGY (SIHC-C)
GA 201986810Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aluminum/copper/chromium/nickel/manganese/titanium (AlxCoCrNiMnTi) high entropy alloy coating using axial flow laser comprises e. g. (i) pretreating the substrate to obtain pre-formed cladding composed of aluminum powder, copper powder, chromium powder, nickel powder, manganese powder and titanium powder in a molar ratio of 1.5:1:1:1:1:1; (ii) wetting and stirring the pre-cladding metal powder uniformly by using a dispersing agent, and forming a cladding pre-layer on the surface of the substrate; (iii) using an axial flow carbon dioxide laser and taking four process parameters of the powder thickness d, the laser power P, the scanning speed V and the spot diameter D to perform orthogonal laser cladding treatment under the protective gas to obtain multiple sample samples; and (iv) taking a sample with a good adhesion between the coating and the substrate to calculate the depth of heat affected at the center.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aluminum/copper/chromium/nickel/manganese/titanium (AlxCoCrNiMnTi) high entropy alloy coating using axial flow laser.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing aluminum/copper/chromium/nickel/manganese/titanium (AlxCoCrNiMnTi) high entropy alloy coating using axial flow laser comprises (i) pretreating the substrate to obtain pre-formed cladding composed of aluminum powder, copper powder, chromium powder, nickel powder, manganese powder and titanium powder in a molar ratio of 1.5:1:1:1:1:1; (ii) wetting and stirring the pre-cladding metal powder uniformly by using a dispersing agent, and forming a cladding pre-layer on the surface of the substrate; (iii) using an axial flow carbon dioxide laser and taking four process parameters of the powder thickness d, the laser power P, the scanning speed V and the spot diameter D to perform orthogonal laser cladding treatment under the protective gas to obtain multiple sample samples; (iv) taking a sample with a good adhesion between the coating and the substrate to calculate the depth of heat affected at the center, and taking the process parameters corresponding to the sample with the lowest thermal influence depth at the center as the optimum process parameters; and (iv) repeating steps (i) and (ii) and using axial flow carbon dioxide laser and the optimal process parameters for laser cladding under protective gas to obtain aluminum/copper/chromium/nickel/manganese/titanium (AlxCoCrNiMnTi) high-entropy alloy coating, where the preset molten metal powder is replaced with different molten metal powder prepared by adjusting the molar ratio of aluminum powder, copper powder, chromium powder, nickel powder, manganese powder and titanium powder. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for aluminum/copper/chromium/nickel/manganese/titanium (AlxCoCrNiMnTi) high entropy alloy coating is prepared by using axial flow laser
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The substrate is a steel-based matrix material, and the pretreatment includes surface grinding treatment, cutting treatment, surface cleaning treatment, and drying treatment which are sequentially performed. The cladding metal powder is composed of aluminum powder, copper powder, chromium powder, nickel powder, manganese powder and titanium powder in a molar ratio of 1-1.8:1-1.3:1-1.3:1-1.3:1-1.3:1-1.3. The particle size of each element powder is 200-300 mesh, and the purity of each element powder is greater than or equal to 099.9 wt/%. The dispersing agent is ethanol having purity of 90-95% by weight. The thickness d of the cladding powder is 1.4-1.5 mm, the spot diameter D is 3-4 mm, the power P is 3,000-3,500 watts, the scanning speed V is 10-20 mm/second, and the protective gas is argon gas. The orthogonal laser cladding process selects 3-5 horizontal value design orthogonal tests for each process parameter. The depth of heat affected at the center is measured by scanning electron microscope scale, and the depth from the coating-substrate bonding zone to the junction between the heat-affected zone and the substrate is measured at a magnification of 200 times. The molar fraction of each element in the AlxCoCrNiMnTi high-entropy alloy coating layer does not exceed 35%, where x is 1-1.8. Preferred Method: The optimal process parameters are the thickness d of the cladding powder is 1.4 mm, the laser power P is 3500 W, the scanning speed V is 5 mm/second, and the spot diameter D is 4 mm.
DC M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).); M26 (Non-ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).)
MC M13-H01; M26-B; M26-B00A; M26-B00C; M26-B00M; M26-B00N; M26-B00T

PT P
PN CN110127805-A; CN110127805-B
TI Processing sewage e.g. adding amount of sewage treatment material to the sewage for processing, preparing hazelnut shell-based activated carbon, crushing and performing supercritical carbon dioxide treatment and activation treatment.
AU INVENTOR U
AE SHANDONG HULI MACHINERY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU FOREVERWAY ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOG (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201972759N
AB    NOVELTY - Processing sewage comprises e.g. adding amount of sewage treatment material to the sewage for processing, where the sewage treatment material is spherical and is made of a three-layer structure, which is core layer, intermediate layer and outer layer respectively, preparing core layer by mixing activated sludge and hazelnut-shell activated carbon in the ratio of 10-15:1 by mass, preparing hazelnut shell-based activated carbon, crushing the hazelnut shell through a 120 mesh sieve, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide treatment at a the treatment pressure of 6MPa for 20-25 minutes, where the carbonization temperature is 380 degrees C, and the carbonization time is 2 hours, then performing activation treatment at a activation temperature of 620 degrees C for 90 minutes, mixing intermediate layer is composed of algae oligosaccharides, cyclodextrin and gelatin in the mass ratio of 3-5:6-8:4-6, and adding ultrafine carbon powder, sintering agent and binder.
   USE - The method is useful for processing sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: remove chemical oxygen demand (COD) and suspended solids (SS) in the sewage, has good removal rate, thorough removal effect and high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing sewage comprises adding amount of sewage treatment material to the sewage for treating, where the sewage treatment material is spherical and is made of a three-layer structure, which is core layer, intermediate layer and outer layer respectively, preparing core layer by mixing activated sludge and hazelnut-shell activated carbon in the ratio of 10-15:1 by mass, preparing hazelnut shell-based activated carbon, crushing the hazelnut shell through a 120 mesh sieve, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide treatment at a the treatment pressure of 6MPa for 20-25 minutes, where the carbonization temperature is 380 degrees C, and the carbonization time is 2 hours, then performing activation treatment at a activation temperature of 620 degrees C for 90 minutes, mixing intermediate layer is composed of algae oligosaccharides, cyclodextrin and gelatin in the mass ratio of 3-5:6-8:4-6, where the outer layer is mainly composed of a mixture of modified attapulgite and montmorillonite, adding ultrafine carbon powder, sintering agent and binder, sequentially mixing in the mass ratio of 18:2:6:0.8, preparing outer layer by uniformly mixing a mixture of the modified attapulgite and montmorillonite with a sintering agent, passing through a 250 meshes sieve, performing calcination at 500-540 degrees C for 2 hours, naturally cooling to 450 degrees C, adding ultra-fine carbon powder, mixing uniformly, maintaining for 30 minutes, then naturally cooling to room temperature, mixing with binder to prepare outer layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sintering agent is bentonite. The binder is a shellac resin. Preferred Method: The mixture of the modified attapulgite and montmorillonite is prepared by: mixing with attapulgite and montmorillonite at the mass ratio of 1:1, passing through a 250 meshes sieve to obtain a mixture of attapulgite and montmorillonite, simultaneously adding bis-( gamma -(triethoxysilyl)propyl)tetrasulfide and sodium dioctyl sulfosuccinate to the mixture, thoroughly mixing, placing in a pulverizer for high-speed reaction to obtain mixture of modified attapulgite and montmorillonite, where the amount of the bis-( gamma -(triethoxysilyl)propyl)tetrasulfide and sodium dioctyl sulfosuccinate sulfonate is 5 wt.%, respectively based on the weight of the mixture of attapulgite and montmorillonite.

PN CN208341960-U; CN110394529-A
TI Round welding machine, has gun handle supporting rod that is arranged in U-shaped groove of U-shaped supporting plate.
AU ZHANG Q
   CHEN C
   ZHENG X
AE HENAN MOXI MACHINERY MFG CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201906658D
AB    (CN208341960-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a circular welding machine, comprising a chassis, the inside of the bottom frame is fixed with a drive motor, the upper end of the driving motor is connected with a rotating shaft, upper end of the rotating shaft passes through the upper surface of the bottom frame and connected with a rotary platform; the upper surface of the rotary platform is fixed with a round welding work piece, one side of the bottom frame is provided with a carbon dioxide protection welding machine, lower end of the carbon dioxide protection welding machine connected with the bottom frame through the ground wire, upper end of the carbon dioxide protection welding machine through the welding handle wire connected with a welding gun. the front side of the bottom frame upper end fixedly provided with a swing power machine, the swing power machine is movably connected with a first connecting ball hinge, the first connecting ball hinge is fixedly connected with a gun handle supporting rod; the round welding machine, it is very convenient to use, and it can effectively improve the welding stability, fast welding speed, welding efficiency can be improved, and it has good popularization effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural diagram of the circular welding machine.
   Chassis (1)
   Welding gun (9)
   Oscillating power machine (10)
   First connecting ball joint (11)
   Handlebar support rod (12)
   Second connecting ball joint (13)
   Limiting rod (14)
EA    NOVELTY - The machine has an oscillating power machine (10) that is fixedly mounted on an upper end of a front side of a chassis (1). A first connecting ball joint (11) is movably connected to the oscillating power machine. A handlebar support rod (12) is fixedly coupled to the first connecting ball joint. A second connecting ball joint (13) is connected to one end of the handlebar support rod away from the first connecting ball joint. The second connecting ball joint is movably connected to the lower end of a welding gun (9). The upper end of the chassis is connected to a limiting rod (14) on the side of the handlebar support rod. The upper end of the limiting rod is provided with a U-shaped plate. The handlebar support rod is erected in the U-shaped groove of the U-shaped plate.
   USE - Circular welding machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The circular welding machine is very convenient to use, can effectively improve the stability of welding, has fast welding speed and improved welding efficiency, and has a good promotion effect.

PT P
PN CN108609582-A
TI Producing hydrogen from raw coke oven gas by            compressing raw coke oven gas, then washing and            desalting, carrying out pretreatment, followed by            sulfur-resistant wide-temperature conversion process,            concentrating and purifying hydrogen.
AU CHEN L
   ZHU D
   YU L
   ZHENG Y
   DAI X
   WANG L
   CHEN C
AE SICHUAN DAKETE CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201880432E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen from raw coke oven gas                involves compressing and pressurizing the raw                material waste gas to 0.5-2.5 MPa, introducing a                compressor outlet gas to the water washing and                removing ammonium salt in the waste gas,                introducing a pretreated process unit to remove                tar, naphthalene, heavy hydrocarbons, ammonia,                hydrogen cyanide and other impurities in the raw                gas to obtain pretreated purified gas, then                introducing the purified gas into a two-stage inlet                compression of the compressor, using a                sulfur-resistant wide-temperature conversion                process unit to prepare a maximum amount of                hydrogen through conversion reaction of carbon                monoxide and water vapor, and introducing the                obtained variable air exchange into a                vacuum/pressure swing adsorption-I (VPSA-I)                concentrated hydrogen unit to remove hydrocarbons,                water, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, portion of                nitrogen and a small amount of carbon monoxide in                the variable air exchange.
   USE - The method is useful for producing hydrogen                from raw coke oven gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical production of                hydrogen from the raw coke oven gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing hydrogen from raw coke oven gas                involves compressing and pressurizing the raw                material waste gas to 0.5-2.5 MPa, introducing a                compressor outlet gas to the water washing and                removing ammonium salt in the waste gas,                introducing a pretreated process unit to remove                tar, naphthalene, heavy hydrocarbons, ammonia,                hydrogen cyanide and other impurities in the raw                gas to obtain pretreated purified gas, then                introducing the purified gas into a two-stage inlet                compression of the compressor, using a                sulfur-resistant wide-temperature conversion                process unit to prepare a maximum amount of                hydrogen through conversion reaction of carbon                monoxide and water vapor, introducing the obtained                variable air exchange into a vacuum/pressure swing                adsorption-I (VPSA-I) concentrated hydrogen unit to                remove hydrocarbons, water, carbon dioxide,                hydrogen sulfide, portion of nitrogen and a small                amount of carbon monoxide in the variable air                exchange remaining after conversion to obtain                VPSA-I hydrogen-rich gas and VPSA-I desorbed gas,                directly introducing the VPSA-I hydrogen-rich gas                into VPSA-II purification hydrogen unit to remove a                large amount of nitrogen and trace other impurity                components in the hydrogen-rich gas to obtain                VPSA-II desorbed gas having a purity of greater                than 99.9 vol.%, a carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide content of less than 20 ppm and nitrogen                content of greater than 97 vol.%, introducing the                VPSA-I desorbed gas into a stripping acid gas sent                from the coal tar hydrogenation unit and then                introducing the mixture into a wet desulfurization                unit to remove hydrogen sulfide to obtain purified                fuel gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The VPSA-I concentrated                  hydrogen unit adopts a special pressure swing                  adsorption adsorbent resistant to hydrogen                  sulfide, and adopts the raw material gas with                  hydrogen sulfide content of 5 g/Nm3.

PN CN107926196-A
TI Storage method for increasing germination rate of rice seeds by layering rice seeds on floor, spraying abscisic acid, mixing seeds, Chrysanthemum powder and lime powder, packing, exposing under sunlight and spraying osmotic regulator.
AU HU B
AE BENGBU NONGXING MODERN AGRIC SPECIALIZED (BENG-Non-standard)
GA 201832134T
AB    NOVELTY - Storage method for increasing the germination rate of rice seeds involves layering mature rice seeds on the concrete floor, drying, spraying naphthaleneacetic acid and abscisic acid, mixing the seeds, Chrysanthemum powder and lime powder, adding the mixed seeds into permeable linen bag, filling the bag with a pack of plant aromatherapy bag, sealing, exposing the bags under sunlight, spraying osmotic regulator, drying, heating, increasing the relative humidity in the storage room and adjusting the carbon dioxide and oxygen concentration, temperature and relative humidity.
   USE - The storage method for increasing the germination rate of rice seeds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls of the respiratory metabolism of seeds at the lowest level of life activities, reduces the consumption of stored substances within seeds to a minimum, prevent the adverse effects of the environment and mold on the seeds, hence prevents loss of activity, rapidly awakens dormant seeds and promotes seed germination, maintains vigor of the seeds, delays decay and reduce the speed of aging in seeds, eliminates the impact of biological factors on the seeds, protects the structure and function of the nutrient components of the seed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Storage method for increasing the germination rate of rice seeds involves (a) layering mature rice seeds on the concrete floor into layer having thickness 0.6-0.8 cm, drying for 3-4 days in a dry place while turning once in every 2-3 hours, spraying 80-100 ppm naphthaleneacetic acid (NAA) and 60-80 ppm abscisic acid to wet the seed surface, drying and mixing the seeds with 0.2-2.5% Chrysanthemum powder and 0.5-0.8% lime powder, (b) adding the mixed seeds obtained in step (a) into permeable linen bag at the bottom such that each bag weighs 50-60 kg, filling each bag with a pack of plant aromatherapy bag in the middle, sealing, where each pack weighs 0.3-0.5 kg, selecting indoor with ventilation as storage areas, where the indoor carbon dioxide concentration is 6-8% and the indoor oxygen concentration is 3.5-3.8%, temperature is 10-15 degrees C and relative humidity is 60-70% and (c) exposing the bags under sunlight for 5-7 hours once every 20-25 days during storage, spraying osmotic regulator using a sprayer, drying the seeds, turning during the drying process, uniformly heating 3-4 days before sowing, gradually increasing the relative humidity in the storage room and adjusting the carbon dioxide concentration to 5.5-6.5%, oxygen concentration to 4.5-5.5%, temperature to 20-25 degrees C and relative humidity to 90-95%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In step (a), the water content of the Chrysanthemum powder is 1-1.5%. In step (b), the plant aromatherapy pack is prepared by (i) weighing 12-14 pts. wt. folium artemisiae argyi, 10-12 pts. wt. lavender, 9.5-10 pts. wt. Geranium, 9-9.5 pts. wt. orange peel, 8.5-9 pts. wt. reed leaves, 8-8.5 pts. wt. cocklebur, 7.8-8 pts. wt. Pinellia tuber, 5.5-6.5 pts. wt. rice bran, 4.5-5.5 pts. wt. lotus leaves, 3.5-4 pts. wt. mint and 3-3.5 pts. wt. rosemary and (ii) drying the above-mentioned raw materials until the moisture content is 10-15%, steaming the dried material at 110-120 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, cooling to room temperature, packing in paper bags and continuing drying until the moisture content is 3-5%. In step (c), the osmotic regulator comprises 150-180 ppm sodium bicarbonate and 200-220 ppm potassium chloride.
DC C05 (Biological control - excluding veterinary medicine, but including use of microorganisms, predators and natural products.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); P11 (Soil working, planting (A01B, C).)
MC C04-A08; C04-A09; C04-A10; C04-A98; C05-A01; C05-C04; C05-C07; C10-C04A; C10-C04C; C14-S18; C14-U01C; P11-C02; P11-C03A; P11-C99; P11-E01; P11-E03
IP A01C-001/00; A01N-065/44

PN CA3147046-A1; US2022249977-A1
TI Extracting biomass, by cooling, and extracting            biomass with liquid carbon dioxide mono-solvent,            extracting biomass with liquid carbon dioxide and            co-solvent, and evaporating mono-, co-solvent fractions            to remove carbon dioxide.
AU SEABROOK J A
   DAYARAM K
   MARTIN S H
   BROWN R L
   DEMERS S C
AE VITALIS EXTRACTION TECHNOLOGY INC (VITA-Non-standard)
   VITALIS EXTRACTION TECHNOLOGY INC (VITA-Non-standard)
GA 202295758G
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting (M1) biomass, comprises: cooling                the biomass with liquid carbon dioxide to                &#8804; 0&#176; C at a pressure of less                than 500psi; extracting the biomass with liquid                carbon dioxide mono-solvent while maintaining the                temperature of the biomass at &#8804;                0&#176; C to obtain at least one mono-solvent                fraction; extracting the biomass with liquid carbon                dioxide and a co-solvent to obtain at least one                co-solvent fraction; and evaporating the                mono-solvent fractions and co-solvent fractions to                remove the carbon dioxide and obtain an extractant                oil.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting                biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes solvent which has low                toxicity, non-flammability, low cost, and low                environmental impact compared to other                solvents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   extracting (M2) molecules from biomass,                comprising extracting cryogenically frozen biomass                at &#8804; 0&#176; C with liquid carbon                dioxide mono-solvent while maintaining the                temperature of the biomass at &#8804;                0&#176; C to obtain at least one mono-solvent                fraction, extracting the biomass with liquid carbon                dioxide and a co-solvent to obtain at least one                cosolvent fraction, and evaporating the                mono-solvent fractions and cosolvent fractions to                remove the carbon dioxide and obtain an extractant                oil;
   uperfluid cosolvent system (10) (S1)                comprising a solvent pump for receiving and                pressurizing solvent, high pressure fluid lines                connecting the solvent pump to a superfluid                extraction system, a collection vessel (16)                comprising a collection vessel intake for receiving                high pressure supercritical fluid and extractant                frorn the superfluid extraction system, a                collection vessel outlet for transferring                extractant out of the collection vessel, a                depressurizing valve, and an exhaust vent line for                directing gas and vapor away from the collection                vessel;
   extracting (M3) oil from a biomass with a                superfluid, comprising putting the biomass into an                extraction column, providing a mixture of                superfluid and co-solvent into the extraction                column to extract extractant oils from the biomass,                directing superfluid, co-solvent, and extractant                from the extraction column to superfluid separator                to remove the superfluid, directing extractant and                co-solvent from the separator to a collection                vessel under high pressure, depressurizing the                collection vessel, and collecting the extractant                and co-solvent;
   extracting (M4) cannabinoids from a                cannabinoid-containing biomass, comprising exposing                the biomass to mono-solvent superfluid carbon                dioxide and capturing a first extract fraction, and                exposing the biomass with a mixture of superfluid                carbon dioxide and a cosolvent and capturing a                second fraction comprising high concentration of                cannabinoids; and
   uperfluid extraction system (S2) comprising                an extraction column for receiving biomass, a                separator fluidly connected to the extraction                column, a superfluid management system comprising a                superfluid pump for pressurizing superfluid, a                superfluid reservoir for storing and supplying                superfluid, a supercritical fluid condenser, and a                heat exchanger, a co-solvent pump for receiving and                pressurizing co-solvent, a collection vessel                comprising a collection vessel intake for receiving                high pressure supercritical fluid and extractant                from the superfluid extraction system, an outlet                for transferring extractant out of the collection                vessel, a depressurizing valve, and an exhaust vent                line for directing gas and vapor away from the                collection vessel, and high pressure superfluid                supply lines fluidly connecting the superfluid                management system to the extraction column,                separator, cosolvent pump, and collection                vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a front isometric view of                the superfluid cosolvent system.
   10Superfluid cosolvent system
   12Support base
   16Collection vessel
   24Extract transfer tank
   26Display screen
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system (S1)                  further comprises: a control system for                  controlling depressurization of the                  depressurizing valve; a pump enclosure for the                  solvent pump; a solvent supply tank fluidly                  connected to the solvent pump; an integrated                  spill and vapor management vented containment                  device; many exhaust vent lines and a liquid trap                  connected to the plurality of exhaust vent lines                  to capture vaporized solvent; and a transfer tank                  (24) for receiving extractant from the collection                  vessel. The control system further controls                  solvent transfer from the solvent pump to the                  superfluid extraction system. The system (S2)                  further comprises: a control system for                  controlling superfluid pressure in the superfluid                  extraction system, where the separator is a                  cyclone separator; a chemical sensor for                  detecting chemical species downstream the                  extraction column, where the extraction column is                  chromatography column: a cosolvent supply tank;                  at least one temperature sensor, pressure gauge,                  pressure release valve, and flow sensor; more                  than one extraction column; more than one                  separator; and an integrated spill and vapor                  management vented containment device.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In method (M1): the liquid                  carbon dioxide in the monosolvent extraction step                  and the co-solvent extraction step is in a                  subcritical state or supercritical state; the                  biomass is cooled at a pressure of &#8804;                  400 psi; the cooling step is performed under                  isobaric conditions; the co-solvent extraction                  step is carried out at a pressure of 1500-2200                  psi; the co-solvent in the co-solvent extraction                  step is 0.1-10 vol.% in sub-cooled liquid carbon                  dioxide; and the co-solvent extraction step                  co-solvent is added in an isocratic, step, or                  linear regime to the liquid carbon dioxide. The                  method (M1) further comprises: dewaxing the                  extractant oil by at least one of filtration,                  dewaxing, or nanofiltration; desolvation of the                  co-solvent fractions; reclaiming the co-solvent;                  and after the co-solvent extraction step,                  remediating the biomass by treatment with carbon                  dioxide mono-solvent to remove residual                  co-solvent. The method (M2) further comprises, a                  cryogenic cooling step comprising cooling the                  biomass with liquid carbon dioxide to &#8804;                  0&#176; C at a pressure of less than 500 psi.                  In methods (M1), and (M2): before the                  mono-solvent extraction step the biomass is dry,                  fresh, or fresh frozen; and the mono-solvent                  extraction step and the co-solvent extraction                  step are carried out at &#8804; 0&#176;                  C. In method (M2), the mono-solvent extraction                  step is carried out at a pressure of 400-800 psi                  and the co-solvent extraction step is carried out                  at a pressure of 1500-2200 psi. The method (M3)                  further comprises: reclaiming the co-solvent;                  recycling the superfluid removed from the                  separator; secondary processing of the extractant                  and co-solvent to remove the co-solvent;                  chromatographic preanalysis of the cannabis                  biomass to determine the total available oil for                  extraction; exposing the biomass to a                  mono-solvent superfluid carbon dioxide; and                  directing the extractant and co-solvent to a                  secondary processing unit. In method (M3), the                  chromatographic pre-analysis is liquid                  chromatography, gas chromatography mass                  spectrometry, thin layer chromatography, or high                  performance liquid chromatography and gas                  chromatography mass spectrometry; and the                  mono-solvent superfluid carbon dioxide                  substantially remediates the biomass from                  residual co-solvent. In method (M4): the second                  extract fraction contains at least 50% of                  available cannabinoids from the biomass; the                  first extract fraction comprises a high                  concentration of terpenes; each of the first                  fraction and second fraction are collected for                  bulk secondary processing; the remediated biomass                  has a co-solvent residual of less than 5000ppm                  when removed from the extraction column; and the                  superfluid pressure and temperature, cosolvent                  injection rate, and column linear velocity over                  the duration of the extraction process reduce                  co-extraction of undesirables. The method (M4)                  further comprises: secondary processing of the                  second fraction to remove the co-solvent, where                  secondary processing of the second fraction to                  remove the superfluid carbon dioxide and                  co-solvent results in an extract comprising more                  than 70% cannabinoids; chromatographic                  preanalysis of the biomass to determine the total                  available cannabinoids and total available                  terpenoids, where the chromatographic                  pre-analysis is at least one of high performance                  liquid chromatography, gas chromatography mass                  spectrometry, and thin layer chromatography; and                  after collecting the second fraction, exposing                  the biomass with a mono-solvent superfluid carbon                  dioxide and capturing a third fraction. The                  method (M4) is adjusted based on total available                  terpenes and cannabinoids. Preferred Components:                  The remediated biomass has a co-solvent residual                  of less than 5000 ppm when removed from the                  extraction column. The co-solvent is ethanol,                  ethyl acetate, isopropyl alcohol, and/or acetone.                  The co-solvent comprises a class 3 solvent. The                  co-solvent is 0.110 vol.% superfluid carbon                  dioxide. The superfluid carbon dioxide is                  subcritical carbon dioxide or supercritical                  carbon dioxide. The mono-solvent superfluid                  carbon dioxide substantially remediates the                  biomass from residual co-solvent.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of recovery of                cannabinoids, monosolvent compared to cosolvent                mixture, recovery of available cannabinoids from                cannabis biomass, and cryogenic pre-treatment of                cannabis and oil extraction that do not support the                claims.

TI Thermal conversion of carboxylic acid of            cannabinoid used as therapeutics to treat pain, opioid            addiction, multiple sclerosis, Parkinson's disease,            nausea and emesis, involves heating such carboxylic            acid of cannabinoid in pressurized chamber in inert            atmosphere.
AU CASTOR T P
AE CASTOR T P (CAST-Individual)
GA 2022D3514W
AB    NOVELTY - Thermal conversion of a carboxylic acid of a                cannabinoid involves heating such carboxylic acid                of a cannabinoid in a pressurized chamber in an                inert atmosphere, where the carboxylic acid of the                cannabinoid is dissolved in solvent such as carbon                dioxide (CO2), alcohol or a mixture of CO2and an                alcohol, and the pressure in the reactor vessel is                greater than the vapor pressure of the solvent at                thermal conversion temperature of the carboxylic                acid for a specific period of time.
   USE - Method for thermal conversion of a carboxylic                acid of a cannabinoid used as therapeutics to treat                pain, opioid addiction, multiple sclerosis,                Parkinson's disease, and nausea and emesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the manufacturing                pharmaceutical grade cannabidiol (CBD), and other                cannabinoids following current good manufacturing                practices.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) an apparatus for thermal conversion of a                carboxylic acid of a cannabinoid by heating such                carboxylic acid of a cannabinoid in a pressurized                chamber in an inert atmosphere; and
   (2) a cannabinoid product which is formed by                the high-pressure thermal conversion of a                carboxylic acid of a cannabinoid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Process: The reaction vessel is                  heated over a temperature range of                  60-150&#8451;. The reactor vessel is heated                  for a period for 30 minutes-6 hours. The                  carboxylic compound is dissolved in                  supercritical, near-critical, critical or                  subcritical carbon dioxide with or without a                  cosolvent at a temperature in the range of 25-                  60&#8451; and a pressure in the range of                  1,000-5,000 psig. The apparatus operates in a                  batch mode, or continuous flow mode.Preferred Components: The carboxylic acid of                  the cannabinoid is cannabidiolic acid (CBDA). The                  carboxylic acid of the cannabinoid is                  &#916;9-tetrahydrocannabinoic acid                  (&#916;9-THCA). The alcohol is methanol or                  ethanol, preferred alcohol is methanol. The inert                  atmosphere is CO2, nitrogen or argon, preferred                  inert atmosphere is argon. The cannabinoid is a                  single molecular entity.
   ACTIVITY - Analgesic; Antiaddictive; Neuroprotective;                Antiparkinsonian; Antiemetic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

TI High permeability membrane useful for selectively            separating oxygen from air, comprises porous electrode            active layer formed on surfaces of composite layer            containing electron-conducting material and            ion-conducting material.
AU JONG-HOON J
   DEOG N K
   SONG S J
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
   UNIV CHONNAM NAT IND FOUND (UCHO-C)
   UNIV CHUNGBUK NAT IND ACADEMIC COOP FOU (UYCK-C)
GA 2022D2080R
AB    NOVELTY - High permeability membrane (30) comprises a                composite layer (10) composed of composite in which                electron-conducting material and ion-conducting                material are mixed; and a porous electrode active                layer (20) formed on one or both surfaces of                composite layer.
   USE - The high permeability membrane is useful for                selectively separating oxygen from air.
   ADVANTAGE - The high permeability membrane has improved                mechanical and thermal stability while having high                oxygen permeability; excellent effect of chemical                stability even in atmosphere; can be produced in                simple and easy manner; can be easily apply to                4-terminal reactor facility type industrial company                utilizing exhaust gas i.e. carbon dioxide; and                enable various combined power generation and                combustion technologies according to the                environment of the pure oxygen generator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                manufacturing oxygen permeable membrane comprising                (a) preparing composite layer comprising                electron-conducting phase material and                ion-conducting phase material, (b) sintering                composite layer and (c) forming porous electrode                active layer on one or both surfaces of composite                layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic cross-sectional                view illustrating high permeability                membrane.
   10Composite layer
   20Porous electrode active layer
   30High permeability membrane
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite layer is                  composed of composite in which                  electron-conducting phase material and                  ion-conducting phase material are mixed in volume                  ratio of 8:2-2:8. The electron-conducting phase                  material includes lanthanum strontium ferrite,                  lanthanum strontium manganite, lanthanum                  strontium cobaltite, lanthanum strontium                  chromite, lanthanum strontium cobalt ferrite,                  manganese ferrite and/or nickel ferrite. The ion                  conductive material includes gadolinia                  doped-ceria, samarium doped-ceria and/or                  yttria-stabilized ceria. The porous electrode                  active layer contains strontium-based compound of                  formula (SrCo0.1Fe0.8Nb0.1O3-d) (I). The                  composite layer has thickness of 20-300                  &#956; m.d=0-1.Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises                  forming composite in which the                  electron-conducting phase material and                  ion-conducting phase material are mixed in volume                  ratio of 8:2-2:8 and preparing composite layer                  using tape casting process. In the step (b),                  sintering the composite layer at                  1200-1400&#176; C. The step (c) is to form                  porous electrode active layer by coating material                  containing strontium-based compound (I) on one or                  both surfaces of the composite layer.
