PN WO2022173810-A1
TI New engineered Cupriavidus necator bacterium            comprising exogenous copy of functional acyltransferase            gene encoding acyltransferase enzyme and/or exogenous            copy of functional phosphatidic acid phosphatase gene            used to form milk fats.
AU NANGLE S N
   ZIESACK M
AE HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
GA 2022A6607G
AB    NOVELTY - Engineered Cupriavidus necatorbacterium                comprising (a) at least one exogenous copy of at                least one functional acyltransferase gene encoding                an acyltransferase enzyme that catalyzes                transesterification of the sn3 hydroxy group of a                diacylglycerol with a fatty acid and/or (b) at                least one exogenous copy of at least one functional                phosphatidic acid (PA) phosphatase gene is                new.
   USE - The bacteria are useful in systems or                bioreactors for producing triacylglycerides,                preferably animal triacylglycerides i.e. milk fats                (all claimed). Test details are described but no                results given.
   ADVANTAGE - The bacteria: permit the production of                tailored polymers; and reduce greenhouse gas                emissions, e.g. when industrially scaled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   producing triacylglycerides (TAGs), comprising                (a) culturing the engineered bacterium in a culture                medium comprising carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen or                fructose and hydrogen or glycerol and/or hydrogen,                and (b) isolating, collecting, or concentrating                TAGs from the engineered bacterium or from the                culture medium of the engineered bacterium;                and
   a system comprising (a) a reactor chamber with                a solution contained in it, where the solution                comprises hydrogen and a carbon source and (b) the                engineered bacterium in the solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preparation: No preparation method is given.                  Preferred Components: The acyltransferase gene is                  a functional diglyceride acyltransferase (DGAT)                  gene, a functional wax synthase (WS) gene, or its                  hybrid, where the functional DGAT gene is                  heterologous. The functional heterologous DGAT                  gene comprises a Acinetobacter baylyiDGAT gene,                  Thermomonospora curvataDGAT gene, a Theobroma                  cacaoDGAT gene, or a Rhodococcus opacusDGAT gene.                  The acyltransferase gene is a functional                  lysophosphatidic acid acyltransferase (LPAT)                  gene. The functional LPAT gene is heterologous,                  where the functional heterologous LPAT gene                  comprises a Theobroma cacaoLPAT gene. The                  acyltransferase gene is a functional                  glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase (GPAT) gene,                  where the functional GPAT gene is heterologous.                  The functional heterologous GPAT gene comprises a                  Durio zibethinusGPAT gene, Gossypium arboreumGPAT                  gene, Hibiscus syriacusGPAT gene, or a Theobroma                  cacaoGPAT gene. The PA phosphatase gene is a                  functional phosphatidate phosphatase (PAP) gene,                  where the functional PAP gene is heterologous.                  The functional heterologous PAP gene comprises a                  Rhodococcus opacusPAP gene, or a Rhodococcus                  jostiiPAP gene. The bacterium further comprises                  at least one exogenous copy of at least one                  functional thioesterase (TE) gene, where the                  functional TE gene is heterologous. The                  functional heterologous TE gene comprises                  Marvinbryantia formatexigensTE gene, Cuphea                  palustrisFatB1 gene, Cuphea palustrisFatB2 gene,                  Cuphea palustrisFatB2-FatB1 hybrid gene, Arachis                  hypogaeaFatB2-1 gene, a Mangifera indicaFatA                  gene, a Morella rubraFatA gene, a Pistacia                  veraFatA gene, a Theobroma cacaoFatA gene, a                  Theobroma cacaoFatB gene (e.g. FatB1, FatB2,                  FatB3, BatB4, FatB5, or FatB6), or a                  Limosilactobacillus reuteriTE gene. The bacterium                  further comprises: (i) at least one endogenous                  polyhydroxyalkanoate (PHA) synthase gene                  comprising at least one engineered inactivating                  modification or (ii) at least one exogenous                  inhibitor of an endogenous PHA synthase gene or                  gene product. The engineered inactivating                  modification of the endogenous PHA synthase                  comprises at least one of (i) deletion of the                  entire coding sequence, (ii) deletion of the                  promoter of the gene, (iii) a frameshift                  mutation, (iv) a nonsense mutation (i.e. a                  premature termination codon), (v) a point                  mutation, (vi) a deletion or (vii) an insertion.                  The endogenous PHA synthase comprises phaC. The                  bacterium further comprises (i) at least one                  endogenous diacylglycerol kinase gene comprising                  at least one engineered inactivating modification                  or (ii) at least one exogenous inhibitor of an                  endogenous diacylglycerol kinase gene or gene                  product. The engineered inactivating modification                  of the endogenous diacylglycerol kinase comprises                  at least one of (i) deletion of the entire coding                  sequence, (ii) deletion of the promoter of the                  gene, (iii) a frameshift mutation, (iv) a                  nonsense mutation (i.e. a premature termination                  codon), (v) a point mutation, (vi) a deletion or                  (vii) an insertion. The endogenous diacylglycerol                  kinase comprises diacylglycerol kinase A (dgkA).                  The bacterium further comprises (i) at least one                  endogenous &#946; -oxidation gene comprising                  at least one engineered inactivating modification                  or (ii) at least one exogenous inhibitor of an                  endogenous &#946; -oxidation gene or gene                  product. The engineered inactivating modification                  of the endogenous &#946; -oxidation gene                  comprises at least one of (i) deletion of the                  entire coding sequence, (ii) deletion of the                  promoter of the gene, (iii) a frameshift                  mutation, (iv) a nonsense mutation (i.e. a                  premature termination codon), (v) a point                  mutation, (vi) a deletion or (vii) an insertion.                  The endogenous &#946; -oxidation gene                  comprises FadE or FadB. The engineered bacteria                  are chemoautotroph. The engineered bacteria use                  carbon dioxide, hydrogen, fructose and glycerol                  as its sole carbon source. The culture medium                  comprises carbon dioxide as the sole carbon                  source, and/or the culture medium comprises                  hydrogen as the sole energy source. The total TAG                  isolated comprises at least 50% TAGs comprising                  4-18C R-group fatty acids. The total TAG isolated                  comprises at least 50% TAGs comprising 4-8C                  R-group fatty acids. The total TAG isolated                  comprises at least 50% TAGs comprising 16C                  R-group fatty acids. The culture medium comprises                  fructose as the sole carbon source, and/or the                  culture medium comprises hydrogen as the sole                  energy source. The culture medium comprises                  glycerol as the sole carbon source, and/or the                  culture medium comprises hydrogen as the sole                  energy source. The system further comprises: a                  pair of electrodes in contact with the solution                  that split water to form the hydrogen, where the                  carbon source is carbon dioxide, fructose, and/or                  glycerol; and isolated gas volume above a surface                  of the solution within a head space of a reactor                  chamber, where the isolated gas volume comprises                  primarily carbon dioxide; and a power source                  comprising a renewable source of energy, where                  the renewable source of energy comprises a solar                  cell, wind turbine, generator, battery, or grid                  power. Preferred Method: The acyltransferase gene                  encodes for an acyltransferase enzyme that                  catalyzes transesterification of the sn3 hydroxy                  group, the sn2 hydroxy group, or the sn1 hydroxy                  group of a triacylglycerol (TAG) precursor with a                  fatty acid. The acyltransferase gene encodes an                  acyltransferase enzyme that catalyzes                  transesterification of the sn3 hydroxy group of a                  diacylglycerol with a fatty acid. The                  acyltransferase gene encodes an acyltransferase                  enzyme that catalyzes transesterification of the                  sn2 hydroxy group of a lysophosphatidic acid with                  a fatty acid. The acyltransferase gene encodes an                  acyltransferase enzyme that catalyzes                  transesterification of the sn1 hydroxy group of a                  glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate with a fatty acid. The                  fatty acid is esterified with acyl carrier                  protein (ACP) or with acetyl coenzyme A. The                  functional PA phosphatase gene encodes PA                  phosphatase enzyme that catalyzes                  dephosphorylation at the sn3 position of                  PA.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of biological                entities that do not support the claims.

PN US2022257942-A1
TI Method for generating phrenic nerve pacing            signals, involves providing second output signal to            second electrode, thus causing second portion of            diaphragm muscles to contract, while providing first            output signal.
AU STEGEMANN B
   BHATTACHARYA A
AE COVIDIEN LP (COVI-C)
GA 2022A6529E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining two different                output signals of two electrodes 152,156 in                proximity to two phrenic nerves 128,132 of a                patient 104. The output signals are provided to the                electrodes to cause two portions of diaphragm 136                muscles of the patient to stiffen and contract,                respectively. A contraction threshold is identified                at which the portions of the muscles contract to                draw a desired tidal volume into lungs of the                patients. An end-tidal carbon dioxide measurement                is performed, and the signals are modified based on                the measurement.
   USE - Method for generating pacing signals for                stimulating phrenic nerves to provide smooth                breathing patterns. Used in treatment of a patient                suffering from acute respiratory distress syndrome                (ARDS), ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP),                diaphragm dystrophy, and delay of ventilator                weaning.
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanical interaction of the right and                left sides of the diaphragm can be utilized to                achieve smooth breathing. The phrenic nerve                stimulation can provide a smooth breathing pattern                with a desired tidal volume to a patient in the                absence of any other breathing support.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a block diagram of a phrenic                nerve stimulation system.
   Phrenic nerve stimulation system (100)
   Patient (104)
   Phrenic nerves (128,132)
   Diaphragm (136)
   Electrodes (152,156)

An                electromagnetic device converts rotational motion                from the track into electricity. The magnets are                equally spaced around the circumference of the                circular tracks.
   USE - Electromagnetic circular track assembly e.g.                rotor and stator, for converting rotational motion                from a circular track into clean and renewable                electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The assembly generates clean energy with                little to no resource consumption to meet global                energy demands, and reduces pollution and carbon                dioxide emissions. TI Separating and detecting pyrethroid enantiomers,            comprises detecting solution to be analyzed by            supercritical fluid chromatography-tandem mass            spectrometry, calculating enantiomer content of            pyrethroids in sample by using matrix matching standard            curve method.
AU TANG G
   BIAN Z
   FAN Z
   WANG Y
   LIU S
   YANG F
   SUN Y
   DENG H
AE NAT TOBACCO QUALITY SUPERVISION & INSPEC (CNTA-C)

The dried wood powder and zinc stearate were mixed to coat the surface of the wood powder, 5 pts. wt. zinc stearate was used based on 100 pts. wt. dried wood powder. 30 wt.% coated wood flour, 40 wt.% polylactic acid, 18 wt.% calcium carbonate, 3 wt.% sweet potato starch, 2 wt.% paraffin wax, 3 wt.% oleic acid, 3 wt.% citric acid and 1 wt.% benzoyl peroxide are mixed. Composition was prepared by hot air drying at 500 rotations/minute at 80 degrees C for 50 minutes. The composition was extruded by an extruder to prepare a biodegradable polymer composite (pellet).

PN CN114195171-A
TI Preparing acicular off molecular sieve involves            adding aluminum source and boron source to alkali            source solution, rotating and dissolving, cooling,            adding template agent tetraethyl ammonium hydroxide and            silicon source and growing at high-temperature dynamic            crystallization.
AU WU J
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 202245633T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing acicular off molecular sieve,                involves (a) adding aluminum source and boron                source to an alkali source solution, rotating and                dissolving in a homogeneous reactor, cooling to                room temperature to obtain a uniform transparent                mixed solution, (b) adding template agent                tetraethyl ammonium hydroxide and silicon source to                the solution, stirring at a speed of 600-800                revolutions/minute, continuously stirring for 2-4                hours, placing into a reaction kettle, and                transferring to the reaction kettle to grow at                high-temperature dynamic crystallization to obtain                an acicular OFF molecular sieved raw powder, and                (c) washing the raw powder of the acicular                ON/O/Molecular sieve and drying and roasting to                obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of                needle-shaped OFF molecular sieve used in                carbon-chemical field, e.g. methanol preparation                hydrocarbon, synthesis gas into olefin, metanol and                dimethyl ether carbonylation reaction, and carbon                dioxide hydrogenation conversion field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that the special shape is                good for improving the catalytic selectivity, and                prolonging the catalyst service life, expanding the                application range. The method is simple,                cost-effective, and has industrial application                prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  molar ratio of raw materials in the solution is                  alumina:boric acid:potassium                  hydroxide:water=1:(1.15-1.6):(24-27):(800-950).                  In the step (2), the gel ratio of the raw                  materials is alumina: boric acid: potassium                  hydroxide: silica: Tetraethylammonium hydroxide:                  water = 1: (1.15-1.6): (24-27): (35-40): (4-6):                  (800-950). The aluminium source is aluminium                  powder, aluminium hydroxide and/or aluminium                  isopropoxide. The alkali source is potassium                  hydroxide. The silicon source is silica sol,                  and/or white carbon black. The boron source is                  boric acid. The template agent is tetraethyl                  ammonium hydroxide.

   USE - Denitration ceramic fiber filter tube.
   ADVANTAGE - The denitration ceramic fiber filter tube                maintains the basic properties such as high                activity of the original denitration catalyst                active components, uniform particle size and                particle size distribution, large specific surface                area, etc., and at the same time, the coating load                is high and uniform, and the bonding firmness is                high. , and the polymer copolymer layer on the                surface of titanium dioxide becomes carbon dioxide,                water, etc. to volatilize, which has no effect on                the porosity, pressure drop, strength, etc. of the                filter tube, effectively improving the shedding,                failure, and deactivation of the existing catalyst                active components, there are adverse factors such                as performance impact, does not contain alcohol                solvents, is easy to control in production, and has                high safety. The denitration catalyst-coated                ceramic fiber filter tube for denitration meets                more stringent environmental protection denitration                index requirements and has broad market                prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The loading amount of                  the active nano titanium dioxide particles on the                  ceramic fiber filter tube is 5-15%. The active                  nano titanium dioxide particles are uniformly                  dispersed in the outer surface, inner surface and                  tube wall of the ceramic fiber filter tube. The                  fiber length of the ceramic fiber filter tube is                  2-3 micrometer. The length of the ceramic fiber                  filter tube is 2.5-3.5 m. The diameter of the                  ceramic fiber filter tube is 10-25 cm. The                  porosity of the ceramic fiber filter tube is                  65-85%. The pressure drop of the ceramic fiber                  filter tube is 650-1100 pascal. The C-ring                  compressive strength of the ceramic fiber filter                  tube is greater than or equal to 0.35 megapascal.                  Preferred Components: The oxide denitration                  catalyst includes vanadium oxide, tungsten oxide,                  molybdenum oxide or rare earth element oxide. The                  active nano-titanium dioxide particles are                  obtained by alkaline hydrolysis of active                  nano-titanium dioxide microcapsules and then                  sintering. The active nano-titanium dioxide                  microcapsules include nano-titanium dioxide                  microcapsules and denitration catalyst active                  components compounded on the surface of the                  nano-titanium dioxide microcapsules. The                  nano-titanium dioxide microcapsules include                  nano-titanium dioxide particles and a                  poly(acrylonitrile-acrylic acid) copolymer layer                  coated on the surface of the nano-titanium                  dioxide particles. The active components of the                  denitration catalyst include vanadium ions,                  tungsten ions, molybdenum ions and/or rare earth                  element ions. The nano-titania microcapsules are                  prepared by an emulsion method. The nano-titanium                  dioxide microcapsules include nano-titanium                  dioxide microcapsules microemulsion. The                  poly(acrylonitrile-acrylic acid) copolymer                  comprises a modified poly(acrylonitrile-acrylic                  acid) copolymer. The modified functional monomers                  include 2-hydroxyethyl methacrylate, glycidyl                  methacrylate and/or isobornyl methacrylate. The                  surface of the nano-titania microcapsules                  contains functional polar groups. The functional                  polar group includes hydroxyl, amine and/or                  carboxyl. The functional monomers include alkyl                  methacrylate and/or alkyl acrylate monomers. The                  emulsifier includes NP-10 (RTM: Nonylphenol                  Ethoxylate), Tween-80 (RTM: Non-ionic surfactant)                  and/or oxycodone (OP-10). The wetting agent                  includes sodium diisobutylnaphthalene sulfonate,                  sodium dialkyl succinate sulfonate,                  2-ethylhexanol polyoxyethylene ether phosphate,                  sodium methylene dinaphthalene sulfonate and/or                  dodecyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether. The                  emulsifying and dispersing agent includes one or                  more of polyvinyl alcohol, sodium pyrophosphate,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, trisodium phosphate, sodium                  dihydrogen phosphate and/or sodium                  tripolyphosphate. The pH adjuster includes one or                  more of hydrochloric acid, succinic acid, adipic                  acid, citric acid, salicylic acid and/or tartaric                  acid. The redox initiator includes an oxidizing                  agent and/or a reducing agent. The crosslinking                  agent includes dipentaerythritol hexaacrylate,                  hexanediol diacrylate, and dimethylaminoethyl                  acrylate. The antioxidant comprises                  beta-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)                  propionic acid n-octadecyl ester), tris                  (2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl) phosphite and/or                  tetrakis                  (beta-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)                  propionic acid) pentaerythritol ester. The silane                  coupling agent includes vinyltriethoxysilane,                  vinyltrimethoxysilane,                  vinyltris(beta-methoxyethoxy)silane and                  gamma-methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane. The                  oxidizing agent includes persulfate, peroxysalt                  and/or alkali metal peroxide. The reducing agent                  includes one or more of sulfurous acid, sulfite,                  bisulfite, thiosulfate, formaldehyde sulfoxylate,                  alkali metal metabisulfite, and/or metabisulfite. The compounding method includes                  coating, spraying, brushing and/or dipping.

PN CN113926439-A
TI Preparing phosphorus removal adsorbent based on sycamore flocculent involves removing particle on sycamore flocs, placing sycamore flocs in ultrasonic cleaning machine, drying and dehydrating after ultrasonic treatment.
AU CAO J
   LUO J
   FANG F
   XUE C
   WANG S
   GE R
   FENG Q
AE UNIV HOHAI (UYHO-C)
GA 2022174748
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing phosphorus removal adsorbent based on sycamore flocculent involves removing particle on the sycamore flocs, placing the sycamore flocs in an ultrasonic cleaning machine, drying, dehydrating after ultrasonic treatment, placing the dried flocculent material in a tube furnace, calcining under carbon dioxide atmosphere, grinding the calcined product through a screen to obtain carbonized sycamore flocculation powder, mixing the obtained sycamore flocculation powder with a dilute nitric acid solution, reacting under stirring, filtering to remove supernatant after reaction, washing an initial product with water to remove residual dilute nitric acid, drying after cleaning, mixing the sycamore flocculation powder with the polyhydroxyalkanoate, mixing uniformly, preparing into spherical particles through a granulator, and drying to obtain the phosphorus removal adsorbent.
   USE - Method for preparing phosphorus removal adsorbent based on sycamore flocculent.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared adsorbent has large specific surface area, good adsorption effect and eco-friendly and pollution-free, and the adsorption capacity for phosphate can reach 39.08 mg/g, which has high application value and realizes the transformation of sycamore flocs from waste into treasure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a prepared adsorption of phosphate over time by the sycamore flocculent phosphorus removal adsorbent (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation phosphorus removal adsorbent based on sycamore flocculent specifically involves removing particle on the sycamore flocs, placing the sycamore flocs in an ultrasonic cleaning machine, drying at temperature of 105-110degrees Celsius, dehydrating after ultrasonic treatment, where the ultrasonic frequency is 40-80 kilohertz, the ultrasonic time is 1-2 hours, placing the dried flocculent material in a tube furnace, calcining under carbon dioxide atmosphere at 450-650degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours, and the heating rate is 2degrees Celsius/minutes, grinding the calcined product through a screen to obtain carbonized sycamore flocculation powder, where the particle size of the sycamore flocculation powder is 50 microm, adding 1 g the obtained carbonized sycamore powder to every 20 mL the dilute nitric acid solution, reacting under stirring in a magnetic stirrer for 12-14 hours, the rotational speed of the magnetic stirrer is 400-450 revolutions per minute, where the concentration of the dilute nitric acid is 2-4 mol/L, filtering to remove supernatant after reaction, washing an initial product with water to remove residual dilute nitric acid, drying after cleaning, mixing the sycamore flocculation powder with the polyhydroxyalkanoate with mass ratio of 10:1, mixing uniformly, preparing into spherical particle through a granulator, and drying to obtain the phosphorus removal adsorbent.

The compensation setting and the dispensing setting of the dispensing unit are designed to be controllable through a common control element. AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst with at least one metallic surface state is made by identifying all topological insulators in the Inorganic Crystal Structure Database (ICSD); calculating real space invariants of valence bands for all topologically insulators to identify Wyckoff Positions, where irreducible Wannier charge centers (WCCs) are localized; selecting as potentially catalytic active compound a topological insulator; and synthesizing or cutting a crystal of the selected potentially catalytically active compound either so that it is grown in a predefined crystallographic direction.

PN CN113789165-A
TI Coating anti-settling fluorescent powder by mixing nano-silicon dioxide aqueous solution and ethyl orthosilicate, adding nano-silicon dioxide, adding silicone oil into deionized water and adding deionized water into fluorescent powder.
AU LIU H
   YAN J
   ZHU H
   DOU F
   LIU T
AE YANTAI SHIELD MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YANT-Non-standard)
GA 202200140S
AB    NOVELTY - Coating method of anti-settling fluorescent powder involves mixing nano-silicon dioxide aqueous solution, alcohol and ethyl orthosilicate, adding nano-silicon dioxide, dispersing to obtain coating liquid A, adding silicone oil into deionized water, dispersing and adding nano-silicon dioxide, dispersing to obtain coating liquid B, adding deionized water into fluorescent powder, adding coating liquid A, controlling stirring time, adding coating liquid B, fully stirring and settling, taking out coated fluorescent powder, drying, cooling and sieving.
   USE - The method is used for coating anti-settling fluorescent powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method forms emulsion with strong adsorption capacity under the action of alcohol, firmly adsorbs added nano-silicon dioxide on surface of powder, adsorbs layer of silicone oil that is invisible to naked eye on surface of powder particles coated with nano-silicon dioxide, protects and enhances adsorption of nano-silicon dioxide on surface of powder by silicone oil layer, and blocks corrosion of silicon dioxide gel on silicon dioxide. The product is heat-resistant, non-toxic and pollution-free, has stable physical and chemical performance, does not react with water, oxygen and carbon dioxide in high temperature and high humidity environment, and improves anti-settling effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of nano-silicon dioxide aqueous solution to ethyl orthosilicate is 1:1:1. The adding amount of nano-silicon dioxide in coating liquid A is 1-1.09 of weight of fluorescent powder. The mass ratio of silicone oil to deionized water is 1:4-1:5. The temperature of deionized water is 75-80degrees Celsius. The weight of nano-silicon dioxide in coating liquid B is 0.1-0.7% of weight of fluorescent powder. The mass ratio of fluorescent powder to deionized water is 1:3.5-1:3.8.Preferred Method: Stirring is done for 2-5 minutes. Fully stirring is done at 75-80degrees Celsius for 1.5-2 hours at stirring paddle speed of 80-90 revolutions/minute. Drying is done at 300degrees Celsius for 24 hours.

An environment conditioner controls conditions of the environment, and a sensor senses the conditions. A controller receives measurements of the conditions from the sensor (126), and produces an output based on a comparison between the measurements and corresponding values of a desired growing environment. The controller controls the conditioner based on the output, so that the conditions are maintained within corresponding values, where each condition is one of light, temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide level, pH, nutrient water and/or spray frequency.
   USE - System for cultivation of plants in indoor, vertical farming, plant enclosure that mimics optimal environments for plants to grow and thrive. Can also be used in urban agriculture.
   ADVANTAGE - The enclosure allows environmental conditions to be efficiently adjusted to meet plant specific conditions for optimal growth. The enclosure is stackable atop like a like enclosure, or across multiple enclosures for a tessellate design.

PN CN113314349-A; CN113314349-B
TI Polyacrylonitrile/wood-based carbon derived porous            material used in preparing supercapacitor electrode            material, comprises carbonized wood chips and            carbonized polyacrylonitrile filled in pores of            carbonized wood chips in porous sheet structure.
AU ZHOU X
   WANG Q
   SHENG L
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV BEIHUA (UYBH-C)
GA 2021A2562B
AB    NOVELTY - A polyacrylonitrile/wood-based carbon derived                porous material comprises carbonized wood chips and                carbonized polyacrylonitrile filled in pores of the                carbonized wood chips in porous sheet                structure.
   USE - Polyacrylonitrile/wood-based carbon derived                porous material used in preparing supercapacitor                electrode material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyacrylonitrile/wood-based carbon porous                material has high specific surface area and                exhibits excellent supercapacitor                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the polyacrylonitrile/wood-based                carbon derived porous material, which involves (1)                washing wood chips with deionized water and                absolute ethanol in order, drying and pretreating                the wood chips, (2) performing low-temperature                pre-oxidation and high-temperature carbonization on                pretreated wood chips to obtain carbonized wood                chips, (3) dissolving polyacrylonitrile powder and                polyvinylpyrrolidone powder in                N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring to obtain mixed                solution (A), (4) immersing carbonized wood chips                in mixed solution (A), vacuum filling                polyacrylonitrile into pores of carbonized wood                chips, soaking in deionized water, washing with                deionized water, drying to obtain                polyacrylonitrile-filled carbonized wood chips, and                (5) subjecting polyacrylonitrile-filled carbonized                wood chips to pre-oxidation, and one-step carbon                dioxide carbonization and activation process in                order.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The                  polyacrylonitrile/wood-based carbon derived                  porous material has specific surface area of up                  to 668.9 m2/g, area specific capacity of 4612.2                  mF/cm2 at current density of 2 mA/cm2 and area                  specific capacity of up to 3431.5 mF/cm2 at                  current density of 100 mA/cm2. Preferred                  Component: The wood chips are poplar wood chips                  with moisture content of less than 30%. Preferred                  Process: The step (4) is carried out by immersing                  carbonized wood chips in mixed solution (A),                  placing them in closed container, pumping                  polyacrylonitrile mixed solution into pores of                  carbonized wood chips by repeatedly vacuuming and                  nitrogen filling, and soaking in environment                  filled with nitrogen for 0.5-24 hours, soaking in                  deionized water, washing with deionized water and                  freeze-drying. Preferred Condition: The drying                  method in step (1) is freeze-drying process                  carried out at vacuum degree of 10-30 Pa and                  condensation temperature of less than -50 degrees                  C for 12-48 hours. The low-temperature                  pre-oxidation is carried out at 200-300 degrees C                  for 1-6 hours. The high-temperature carbonization                  in step (2) is carried out at 800-1200 degrees C                  in inert atmosphere for 6-10 hours. The                  pre-oxidation process in step (5) is carried out                  at 200-300 degrees C for 1-6 hours. The                  carbonization and activation process in step (5)                  is carried out at 800-1000 degrees C under gas                  flow rate of 80-100 cm3/minute for 6-10                  hours
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The polyacrylonitrile                  powder has molecular weight of 30000-500000.                  Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  polyacrylonitrile powder and polyvinylpyrrolidone                  powder is 1:0.5-2.

PN CN113290982-A; CN113290982-B
TI Refractory silica gel material comprises flame            retardant silica gel material, glass fiber material,            silica gel, sodium bicarbonate, aluminum sulfate,            calcium hydroxide, fiber, agar, gelatin and deionized            water.
AU LI D
   HU W
AE LI D (LIDD-Individual)
   FUJIAN NAXIN SILICON IND TECHNOLOGY CO (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2021A0530W
AB    NOVELTY - Refractory silica gel material comprises                surface layer, functional layer and base layer. The                surface layer is made of flame retardant silica gel                material. The base layer is made of glass fiber                material. The functional layer comprises 32-35 pts.                wt. silica gel, 30-40 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate,                5-6 pts. wt. aluminum sulfate, 12-14 pts. wt.                calcium hydroxide, 16-18 pts. wt. fiber, 12-24 pts.                wt. agar, 12-14 pts. wt. gelatin and 20-22 pts. wt.                deionized water. The preparation of functional                layer comprises mixing gelatin and deionized water,                adding sodium bicarbonate powder to gelatin                solution, placing fiber in cold storage, spraying                calcium hydroxide powder, cooling to obtain                adhesive layer, mixing agar and deionized water,                adding aluminum sulfate powder to gelatin solution,                spraying agar solution on gelatin layer, forming                agar layer, spraying silica gel solution on agar                layer and preparing silica gel coated functional                fiber and functional layer.
   USE - Refractory silica gel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide condenses internal calcium                hydroxide into calcium carbonate, adsorbs on                surface of fiber to enhance hardness of fiber,                plays role of skeleton support and hinders                combustion of flame. The material has better flame                retardant and fire resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The preparation of                  refractory silica gel material comprises mixing                  silica gel and flame retardant at ratio of                  98:2,sending to spinning machine for spinning to                  obtain flame retardant silica gel yarn,                  controlling diameter of flame retardant silica                  gel yarn to 0.2-0.3 mm, cross weaving flame                  retardant silica gel yarn to obtain flame                  retardant braid, placing flame retardant braid in                  hot press, heating at 220-260 degrees C for 1-2                  seconds, rapid pressing, spraying mixed powder of                  anhydrous copper sulfate and calcium hydroxide                  particles on rough surface, cooling, spinning                  glass fiber to obtain base layer, coating binder                  on surface of functional layer and pressing with                  surface layer and base layer. Preferred                  Components: The surface layer is flame retardant                  silicone layer woven after spinning of flame                  retardant silica gel doped with flame retardant.                  The surface of rough surface layer is fixedly                  connected with anhydrous copper sulfate powder                  and calcium hydroxide particles. The calcium                  hydroxide particles are particles prepared by                  mixing calcium hydroxide, agarose and foaming                  agent at ratio of 13:25:1. The fiber is made of                  nano zirconia powder, silica gel and sintering                  additive at ratio of 6.5:3:0.5.

PN US2021242448-A1
TI Manufacturing a battery negative electrode material involves mixing an organic material composition and coated silicon material with silicon oxide and obtaining composite substance, and removing silicon oxide from a composite substance.
AU MIYAMAE R
   KATOU D
   HOTEHAMA K
   KOBAYASHI S
   SAKAKIBARA Y
   IMAJI M
AE PANASONIC CORP (MATU-C)
   KUREHA CORP (KURE-C)
GA 2021910513
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing a battery negative electrode material involves mixing an organic material composition and a coated silicon material that is a silicon precursor coated with silicon oxide and obtaining a composite substance of the organic material composition and the coated silicon material. The silicon oxide is removed from a composite substance. An organic material constituting the organic material composition is carbonized by subjecting a composite substance to a main firing at a temperature of 1,000-1,500degrees Celsius in a non-oxidizing gas atmosphere. An (002) average interlayer spacing d002 of the carbon material area determined by powder X-ray diffraction using copper kappa alpha (CuKalpha) rays is from 0.365-0.390 nanometer, and where a maximum particle size of the coated silicon material is 1,000 nanometer or less.
   USE - Method for manufacturing a battery negative electrode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses a non-graphitizable carbon material as the material configuring the area surrounding the spaces around silicon (Si) while maintaining those spaces is obtained, and through this, the matter of carbon atoms configuring the organic material being removed as carbon dioxide or the like beyond the necessary level can be prevented, and resulting in an excellent yield of residual carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic material composition comprises at least one selected from the group consisting of petroleum-based pitch, petroleum-based tar, coal-based pitch, and coal-based tar. Preferred Conditions: The composite substance is a composite substance in which the coated silicon material is dispersed in the organic material composition. The content amount of the silicon material is 5 mass.% or greater and 25 mass.% or less relative to 100 mass.% of the negative electrode material.

TI Preparing durable stainless steel pot comprises e.g. smelting carbon, manganese, silicon, bismuth, nickel, lanthanum, tungsten and iron, annealing, punch-forming, soaking into sodium dihydrogen phosphate and sodium silicate, and washing.
AU GE Y
AE GE Y (GEYY-Individual)
GA 202190127X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing durable stainless steel pot comprises: (i) smelting 0.08-0.12 wt.% carbon, 1-1.5 wt.% manganese, 0.1-0.3 wt.% silicon, 0.3-0.6 wt.% bismuth, 2-4 wt.% nickel, 0.01-0.03 wt.% lanthanum, 0.1-0.3 wt.% tungsten and iron (remaining amount) to obtain steel plate; (ii) annealing steel plate, heating in smelting furnace, and punch-forming to obtain stainless steel pot primary product; (iii) polishing surface of primary product, heating, cooling, placing into diluted acid solution, microwave-processing, washing with water, vacuum-drying, placing into vacuum chamber, and depositing target material; (iv) soaking pot into sodium dihydrogen phosphate solution, placing into reactor, atomizing calcium chloride solution by ultrasonic atomizer, injecting into reactor, spraying on surface of pot, injecting nitrogen, and washing with water; (v) soaking pot into sodium silicate solution, placing into reactor, and filling carbon dioxide; and (vi) placing into heater, cooling, washing with water.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing durable stainless steel pot.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare durable stainless steel pot, which has good adaptability, excellent wear resistance, firmness and durability, and outstanding economic value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The target material is composed of 1-3 pts. wt. zirconium oxide, 4-6 pts. wt. boron nitride, 1-3 pts. wt. silicon carbide, 5-6 pts. wt. boron carbide and 0.5-1 pts. wt. titanium nitride. TI Economic environment and social benefit based waste product re-manufacturing decision realizing method, involves performing entropy weight process based on economic, environment and social benefit data of remanufacturing strategies.
AU ZHANG X
   HE Q
AE UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 202165515M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing economic benefit analysis to different remanufacturing strategies of a recycled product under re-manufacturing technical conditions. Failure detection and simulation repair is performed to the recovered product. Resource loss product is converted into carbon dioxide emissions after standard carbon combustion to evaluate environmental benefits. Social cognition and performance reliability of the re-manufactured product are analyzed by questionnaire survey to evaluate social benefits. Entropy weight process is performed according to economic, environment and social benefit data of different remanufacturing strategies to analyze the obtained weight coefficient and solve benefit of different remanufacturing strategies.
   USE - Economic environment and social benefit based waste product re-manufacturing decision realizing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing interference of human factors and remanufacturing process dependence on historical remanufacturing data, and improving precision and generalization capability of the decision model. The method enables solving the decision of the waste product remanufacturing strategy of high actability. The method enables avoiding the risks for remanufacturing waste products with required remanufacturing ability.

PT P
PN CN112876878-A
TI Preparing ultra-high whiteness pigment-grade core-shell structured nano calcium carbonate by adding ammonium chloride and ammonium bromide, reacting, filtering, adding crystal nucleating agent, carbonizing, adding suet and dispersing.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG Z
   LI J
   WU C
   LEI T
   HU J
   LI Y
   HAN Y
AE JIANGXI SHITONG CALCIUM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021641620
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing ultra-high whiteness pigment-grade core-shell structured nano calcium carbonate involves (i) adding ammonium chloride, ammonium bromide and/or ammonium iodide to the calcium hydroxide slurry to react, and filtering the reacted mixture to obtain fine filtrate containing calcium ions, (ii) adding crystal nucleating agent and crystal form control agent to the fine filtrate, and passing pure carbon dioxide gas for carbonization reaction to obtain nano calcium carbonate slurry, (iii) heating the water, adding surfactant to the water, and continuously adding suet to form a uniform emulsion, adding titanium dioxide to the emulsion and continuously stirring to form a surface coating agent, and (iv) adding surface coating agent obtained in the step (iii) to the nano-calcium carbonate slurry in the step (ii), and dispersing to prepare ultra-high whiteness pigment-grade core-shell structured nano-calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing ultra-high whiteness pigment-grade core-shell structured nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the dispersibility of nano calcium carbonate, enables nano calcium carbonate to have the advantages of high refractive index and high whiteness value of titanium dioxide and replaces the application of titanium dioxide in pigments.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves adding ammonium bromide to the calcium hydroxide slurry for reaction, where the mass ratio of calcium hydroxide slurry to ammonium bromide is 70-78:192-200. The step (i) involves adding ammonium iodide to the calcium hydroxide slurry for reaction, where the mass ratio of the calcium hydroxide slurry to the ammonium iodide is (70-78):(280-300). The step (i) involves filtering the reaction mixture, taking the filtrate, filtering the filtrate through sand filtration and activating carbon to obtain a fine filtrate containing calcium ions. Preferred Composition: In step (ii), the crystal nucleating agent comprises ammonium carbonate, and the addition amount of the crystal nucleating agent is 0.1-0.5 %mass of the dry-based nano-calcium carbonate produced. In step (ii), the crystal form control agent comprises honey, and the addition amount of the crystal form control agent is 0.1-0.5 %mass of the dry-based nano-calcium carbonate produced. In step (iii), the mass ratio of water to surfactant, lanolin, and titanium dioxide is (9-11):1:1:1. In step (iv), the addition amount of the surface coating agent is 1-5 %mass in the dry basis nano-calcium carbonate. Preferred Conditions: The step (iii) involves heating the water at 75-85 degrees C. Preferred Components: In step (iii), the surfactant comprises sodium lauryl sulfate, and the titanium dioxide comprises rutile titanium dioxide.

PN CN112851159-A; CN112851159-B
TI Preparing calcium sulfoaluminate modified silicate            cement comprises e.g. mixing limestone, sandstone,            calcium carbide slag, and shale, adding to raw material            to grind, adding to rotary kiln for drying, sintering,            cooling, and rolling.
AU LI H
   ZHANG S
   ZHANG X
   LIU X
   ZHANG J
AE ZHENGZHOU JIANWEN SPECIAL MATERIAL TECHN (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202162072B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium sulfoaluminate modified                silicate cement comprises (i) mixing 40-80 pts. wt.                limestone, 5-15 pts. wt. sandstone, 4-10 pts. wt.                calcium carbide slag, and 1-5 pts. wt. shale,                adding to raw material to grind, adding to rotary                kiln for drying, sintering at 1350-1420 degrees C                for 20-40 minutes, cooling at 100 degrees C,                rolling, dispersing and treating silicate cement                milling, and (ii) 4-10 pts. wt. calcium                sulfoaluminate, adding 20-40 pts. wt. absolute                ethanol to high-speed stirrer, stirring at                10000-20000 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes to                obtain pretreated calcium sulfoaluminate, adding                1-3 pts. wt. nano wollastonite and 0.1-1 pts. wt.                titanate coupling agent to 2-6 pts. wt. absolute                ethanol, stirring at 200-400 rpm for 20-40 minutes,                adding to high pressure kettle, injecting carbon                dioxide under 25-30 MPa, and heating system at                60-80 degrees C under stirring, injecting                pre-treated calcium sulfoaluminate with preset                pressure of 19-24 MPa, and completely mixing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium                sulfoaluminate modified silicate cement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises continuously stirring for 10-30                  minutes, depressurizing, washing with absolute                  ethanol, and drying to obtain coated calcium                  sulfoaluminate, (iii) mixing 100 pts. wt.                  silicate cement clinker, 5-15 pts. wt. coated                  calcium sulfoaluminate, 1-5 pts. wt. coal gangue,                  and 1-5 pts. wt. desulfurized gypsum, and adding                  to a grinder to grind to obtain a mixture with a                  particle size of 100-300 mu m, and (iv) adding                  the mixture to the rotary kiln, calcining at                  1300-1400 degrees C for 40-60 minutes, naturally                  cooling at room temperature, adding to the                  grinder to grind to obtain calcium sulfoaluminate                  modified silicate cement with a particle size of                  100-200 mu m. Preferred Component: In the step                  (ii), the titanate coupling agent comprises                  mono-alkoxy pyrophosphate ester, preferably                  isopropyl tris (dioctyl pyrophosphoryl) titanate.                  The mass ratio of calcium oxide to silicon                  dioxide in nano wollastonite is 1-1.2:1. In the                  step (iii), the content of alumina in coal gangue                  is 5-15 wt.%.

   USE - The deep desulfurization and decarburization solvent is useful in synthetic purification device of a petrochemical enterprise.
   ADVANTAGE - The deep desulfurization and decarburization solvent: ensures purification of low-sulfur and high-carbon natural gas; and has higher acid gas load of the solution, low corrosiveness, and excellent desulfurization and decarburization performance, which meets the requirements including hydrogen sulfide content of less than 4 parts/million, carbon dioxide content of less than 0.1% and carbonyl sulfide content of less than 13 parts/million in the purified gas. The purification device: has stable operation and excellent solution stability; has easy control of regeneration solution level, which reduces the operating difficulty of the system; ensures easy regeneration of solution having excellent anti-foaming performance, which meets the needs of device process; and is energy-saving, and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the deep desulfurization and decarburization solvent, comprising mixing a part of N-methyldiethanolamine, alcohol compound, defoamer and stabilizer for 20-30 minutes, then adding the remaining N-methyldiethanolamine and recirculating with a circulating pump for 50-80 minutes to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol compound is diethanolamine. The defoamer is an organosilicon defoaming agent. The stabilizer is rare earth oxide. The first addition amount of the N-methyldiethanolamine is 5-10% of its total mass. Preferred Method: N-methyldiethanolamine is prepared by mixing ethylene oxide and monomethylamine in a weight ratio of 2.2-2.8:1 and reacting at 130-150 degrees C under 60-80 atmospheres pressure, and then vacuum distilling.

Preferred Method: In the step (a), the method further comprises adding curing accelerator and antifoaming agent into the component-A. The raw materials are added into a dispersing container, dispersing for 15-25 minutes at the rotating speed of 500-1000 revolutions/minute, and filtering using 100-200 meshes filter screen to obtain the A component. In the step (b), the method comprises adding the raw materials into the dispersing container, dispersing for 30-30 minutes at the rotating speed of 200-500 revolutions/minute, filtering using a 100-150 meshes filter screen to obtain the component B.

PT P
PN CN112625312-A
TI Producing degradable sealant comprises plasticizing modified rubber and chlorinated polyethylene, mixing e.g. plasticized material, and calcium stearate, cooling, cutting into granules, and dispersing uniformly e.g. in butyl acetate.
AU LI X
   MA D
   WANG Y
AE LI X (LIXX-Individual)
GA 2021401140
AB    NOVELTY - Producing degradable sealant comprises (a) weighing 40-60 pts. wt. modified rubber, 4-8 pts. wt. chlorinated polyethylene, 0.2-0.4 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 0.02-0.04 pts. wt. antioxidants, 2-5 pts. wt. plasticizers, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. photocatalyst, 3-5 pts. wt. degradable material, 15-25 pts. wt. butyl acetate and 12-20 pts. wt. methyl cyclohexane, (b) plasticizing the modified rubber and chlorinated polyethylene to obtain plasticized material, mixing plasticized material, calcium stearate, antioxidant, plasticizer, photocatalyst, and degradable material uniformly, cooling and cutting into granules to obtain mixed material, and (c) adding the mixed material to the mixed liquid of butyl acetate and methyl cyclohexane, and dispersing uniformly at a speed of 500-800 revolutions/minute.
   USE - The method is useful for producing degradable sealant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can improve the photocatalytic effect of the photocatalyst by adding urea; and uses corn fiber as main raw material to prepare degradable materials which can be decomposed into water, carbon dioxide and small inorganic molecules by microorganisms in nature. The sealant: has fast biodegradation efficiency, and has excellent mechanical properties; and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The photocatalyst is modified titanium dioxide and is prepared by (i) adding 15 ml butyl titanate to 20 ml absolute ethanol, stirring and dispersing to form transparent butyl titanate-ethanol solution, (ii) adding urea to 30 ml nitric acid solution (1 mol/l), stirring and dispersing to form urea-nitric acid solution, (iii) adding butyl titanate-ethanol solution in a drop wise manner to urea-nitric acid solution, stirring uniformly, adjusting the pH to 7 by using sodium hydroxide solution with a substance concentration of 1 mol/l, heating in an oil bath, stirring the reaction at 70-90 degrees C at a speed of 100-200 revolutions/minute for 2-3 hours, washing the reaction solution with deionized water, and centrifuging for collecting the precipitate, and (iv) drying the precipitate, heating at 400 degrees C at a heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute, and sintering at a constant temperature for 4 hours to obtain modified titanium dioxide. The degradable material is prepared by (a) weighing 90-110 pts. wt. corn fiber and 40-60 pts. wt. polypropylene, and (b) heating and melting polypropylene, adding corn fiber to molten polypropylene, mixing uniformly, adding polypropylene and corn fiber mixture to twin-screw granulator for granulating, dehydrating the prepared particles, spin-drying, air-cooling, and drying at 110-140 degrees C for 1-2 hours. The modified rubber is prepared by (i) weighing 60-80 pts. wt. natural rubber, 20-40 pts. wt. styrene-butadiene rubber, 1-3 pts. wt. sulfur, 0.5-0.9 pts. wt. accelerator, 1-5 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 1-3 pts. wt. stearic acid, 1-2 pts. wt. anti-aging agent CPPD, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. paraffin wax, 40-60 pts. wt. super abrasion furnace black, 7-13 pts. wt. xylene, 4-6 pts. wt. modified nylon staple fiber, and 20-40 pts. wt. modified montmorillonite, (ii) firstly plasticizing natural rubber for 5-10 minutes, then adding styrene-butadiene rubber, mixing for 10-20 minutes, then adding accelerator CZ, zinc oxide, stearic acid, and antioxidant CPPD, mixing for 30-40 minutes, and finally adding paraffin wax, super abrasion-resistant furnace black, and xylene for mixing uniformly to obtain mixed rubber compound, and (iii) adding modified nylon short fiber, and modified montmorillonite to the mixed rubber material, mixing for 1-2 hours, adding sulfur, and mixing uniformly to obtain modified rubber. The modified nylon staple fiber is prepared by (i) dissolving coupling agent in absolute ethanol, preparing 2% coupling agent-ethanol solution, spraying coupling agent-ethanol solution uniformly on the nylon staple fiber, then placing in an oven at 80-90 degrees C for removing absolute ethanol, heating at 130 degrees C and reacting for 30 minutes to obtain surface-treated nylon staple fibers, (ii) adding benzoyl peroxide to toluene to prepare 1% benzoyl peroxide-toluene solution, then adding carboxy styrene butadiene latex to benzoyl peroxide-toluene solution and mixing uniformly to obtain 5% carboxy styrene butadiene latex solution, (iii) adding the surface-treated nylon short fiber into carboxy styrene butadiene latex solution, soaking for 10-20 minutes, then placing in an drying oven for drying at 110 degrees C for 90 minutes to obtain modified nylon staple fibers. The modified montmorillonite is prepared by (i) mixing sodium-based montmorillonite and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide in a weight ratio of 10:1, adding mixture to distilled water, stirring and dispersing uniformly at 80-90 degrees C to obtain 5% mixed solution, and (ii) subjecting the mixed solution to ultrasonic treatment for 40-60 minutes, stirring for 1-2 hours to obtain floccules, filtering and washing the floccules with distilled water until no precipitation is detected with 0.1 mol/l silver nitrate solution, drying the floccules, and crushing. Preferred Components: The antioxidant is at least one of antioxidant 168, antioxidant 1010, and antioxidant 1076. The plasticizer is epoxy soybean oil. The weight ratio of butyl titanate to urea is 1:1-3.
DC A81 (Adhesives and binders - including chipboard.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)

AU ZHAN R

   GUO Y
   NI W
AE UNIV ARMY MEDICAL FIRST AFFILIATED HOSPI (UAMT-C)
GA 2021402267
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cell membrane comprises (i) coating silica gel solution on the medical dressing, allowing to stand, solidifying to obtain the silica gel film, then performing hydrophilic modification treatment, (ii) taking postoperative skin, digesting, separating, removing dermis tissue to retain epidermal tissue, crushing the epidermal tissue, digesting, filtering, centrifuging, and collecting the cell, adjusting cell concentration by K-SFM or Epilife culture solution as (1-2)x 106 cells/25 cm2, inoculating to the culture bottle with IV type collagen packet, conventional culturing to obtain epidermal stem cells, (iii) digesting and collecting the epidermal stem cells cultured in the logarithmic phase of step (ii), adjusting the concentration to 0.2-0.5x 106 cells/ml, adding to the medical dressing with silica gel film, culturing under the condition of 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide to obtain the cell membrane.
   USE - The cell membrane is useful for burn wound dressing (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes uniform growth of cells on the silica gel membrane. The cell membrane sheet has the functions of quickly promoting wound healing and improving the quality of wound healing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silica gel solution is a mixed solution of A-type silica gel and B-type silica gel, and the weight ratio of A-type silica gel and B-type silica gel in the mixed solution is 1:1. In the step (i), plasma is used to perform hydrophilic modification treatment on the silica gel film on the surface of the medical dressing. Preferred Method: The pulverization, digestion, filtration and centrifugation of step (ii) specifically comprises cutting the epidermal tissue into small pieces and digesting with a trypsin solution with a concentration of 0.25-0.5% (wt./vol.) for 10-15 minutes at a temperature of 37 degrees C, using serum-containing 1640 medium to stop the digestion, and then filtering with a 200 mesh screen, then centrifuging at 800-1000 revolutions/minute for 4-8 minutes. In the step (ii), adjusting the cell concentration to (1-2)x 106 cells/25 cm2 with K-SFM or Epilife medium, and inoculate it in a culture flask coated with type IV collagen, incubating for 10-15 minutes, aspirate and discard non-adherent cells, then use PBS solution to wash twice, replace with new K SFM or Epilife culture medium for routine culture, change the medium the next day, and then change the medium every three days.
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary.

The mesenchymal stem cell source comprises umbilical cord of human or animal, cord blood, bone marrow, tooth or adipose tissue.

PN FR3100888-A1; EP3795973-A1; FR3100888-B1
TI Method for detecting sealing defect of packaging produced in production line for packaging products, such as food products, involves circulating package to be tested on a conveyor of product packaging production line.
AU IBARRA D
   AVILES C
   CARRE M
   LESORT A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202126908S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves circulating a package (1) to be tested on a conveyor (2) of the product packaging production line. The packaging is provided with a protective atmosphere that contains a tracer gas such as Carbon dioxide (CO2). A system (4) is used for detecting the presence of packaging to be tested opposite the cover (3). The cover is lowered onto the packaging when a package to be tested moving on the conveyor is detected. A force is exerted on the packaging so as to create pressure on the packaging. An analysis is carried out of the atmosphere present in the cover following the application of the force. The cover is raised after the analysis, letting the tested packaging continue its way on the production line.
   USE - Method for detecting a sealing defect of a packaging produced in a production line for packaging products, such as food products.
   ADVANTAGE - By rising, the underside of the cover is rinsed by the ambient air, minimizing the risk of tracer gas which would generate false positives and provides a reduced cost and low energy consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the production line.
   Package (1)
   Conveyor (2)
   Cover (3)
   System (4)
   Miniature gas analyzers (5)

PN CN112520765-A; CN112520765-B
TI Method for recycling calcium leaching waste water by light burning powder useful in field of magnesium oxide preparation, comprises performing solid-liquid separation of decalcified slurry, concentrating decalcified liquid, and recycling.
AU RUAN S
   DONG B
   WANG Z
   JIANG P
   DONG X
   ZHANG Y
   JIE X
   WANG D
   YIN F
   GAO W
   CUI C
AE BGRIMM TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (BEJM-C)
   SUBEI HIGH-TECH MAGNESIA CO LTD (SUBE-Non-standard)
GA 202134881M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recycling calcium leaching waste water by light burning powder, comprises (i) providing calcium-containing light-burned powder with lime-leaching wastewater formed by leaching calcium-leaching powder with leaching agent; (ii) mixing the mixed liquid formed by leaching calcium waste water of light burned powder and light burning powder containing calcium with carbon dioxide containing waste gas in an ejector to decalcify to obtain a decalcified slurry, and performing solid-liquid separation of the decalcified slurry to obtain a decalcified liquid; and (iii) concentrating the decalcified liquid, and using the leaching agent of calcium-containing light-burning powder and/or the washing water of solid-liquid separation after the calcium-containing light-burning powder is soaked in calcium, and recycling.
   USE - The method for recycling light burned powdered calcium leaching wastewater is useful in the field of magnesium oxide preparation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the efficiency of decalcification, realizes the recycling of light burned powdered calcium leaching wastewater through the treatment of the decalcified liquid, and discharges zero wastewater, so that carbon emissions and the cost of decalcification are effectively reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the calcium-containing light burned powder is obtained by calcining magnesium ore, where the magnesium ore comprises magnesite and/or dolomite mine. In the step (ii), the decalcification is carried out at 20-50 degrees C for 2-15 hours, and/or the decalcification pH is 7.0-8.5. In the step (iii), concentrating the decalcified liquid when the chlorine content in the decalcified liquid is less than 20 g/l to obtain salty concentrated water and salty fresh water, reusing the salt-containing concentrated water as the leaching agent of the calcium-containing light-burning powder, and the salt-containing fresh water as the washing water after the solid-liquid separation after the calcium-containing light-burning powder is soaked in the calcium; or reusing the decalcified liquid as leaching agent of calcium-containing calcined powder when the chlorine content in the decalcified liquid is 20-45 g/l. A reverse osmosis membrane is used for concentration treatment. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the leaching agent comprises magnesium chloride leaching agent and/or brine leaching agent. The calcium leaching wastewater of light burned powder has calcium content of 2-16 g/l, magnesium content of 0.3-5.0 g/l, and the chlorine content of 5-45 g/l. In the step (ii), the mass of calcium in the calcium-containing light burnt powder is 0.5-3 times the mass of calcium in the calcium leaching wastewater of the light burnt powder. The carbon dioxide-containing waste gas comprises waste gas generated during the calcination process of magnesium ore and/or waste gas generated during the calcination process of magnesium hydroxide formed by leaching calcium-containing light calcined powder through the leaching agent. The volume fraction of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide-containing waste gas is 1-30%. The flow rate of exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide is calculated as given in the specification.

PN CN112500562-A; CN112500562-B
TI New triptycene amino derivative-based three-dimensional covalent organic framework material, obtained by Schiff base condensation of 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(4-formylphenyl)triptycene and 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(2',6'-diisopropyl-4'-amino)triptycene.
AU FANG Q
   LI H
   CHEN F
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 202128550W
AB    NOVELTY - Triptycene amino derivative-based three-dimensional covalent organic framework material (I) is new. (I) is obtained by subjecting 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(4-formylphenyl)triptycene and 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(2',6'-diisopropyl-4'-amino)triptycene to Schiff base condensation reaction.
   USE - Used as triptycene amino derivative-based three-dimensional covalent organic framework material.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has high crystallinity, permanent porosity and excellent thermal stability, and has excellent hydrogen adsorption capacity and excellent carbon dioxide and methane adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Triptycene amino derivative-based three-dimensional covalent organic framework material (I) is new. (I) is obtained by subjecting 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(4-formylphenyl)triptycene and 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(2',6'-diisopropyl-4'-amino)triptycene to Schiff base condensation reaction.
   A = 4,5,11,12,17,18-hexakis(4-methyl-3,5-bis(propan-2-yl)phenyl)pentacyclo(6.6.6.0(2,7).0(9,14).0(15,20))icosa-2(7),3,5,9(14),10,12,15(20),16,18-nonaene.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) uniformly grinding two stereo-three-dimensional six-node building unit 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(4-formylphenyl)triptycene and 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(2',6'-diisopropyl-4'-amino)triptycene in a mortar, adding into a glass tube, adding organic solvent, then adding catalyst i.e. aqueous acetic acid solution, placing the glass tube in liquid nitrogen for freezing, vacuumizing and flame sealing the tube, and placing in a oven for heating to obtain final product. Preferred Components: The molar ratio between 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(4-formylphenyl)triptycene and 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(2',6'-diisopropyl-4'-amino)triptycene is 1:0.8-1. The organic solvent is mixed solvent of 1-butanol and o-dichlorobenzene, and the mixing volume ratio is 1:0.2-0.25. The concentration of aqueous acetic acid solution is 6-9 M. The molar ratio between 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexabromotriptycene, (N-(diphenylmethylene)-2,6-diisopropyl-4-(4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-1,3,2-dioxaborolan-2-yl)aniline), tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0) and cesium carbonate is 1:10-12:0.1-0.3:12-14, the volume ratio between tetrahydrofuran and water is 2.5:1, stirring is carried out at 80 degrees C for 72 hours, the addition amount of hydrochloric acid solution is 4 mol/l, and stirring is carried out at 70 degrees C for 24 hours. Preferred Method: In the preparation of (I), the heating temperature is 120-140 degrees C, and the reaction time is 3-7 days. The 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexa(2',6'-diisopropyl-4'-amino)triptycene is prepared by reacting 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexabromotriptycene, (N-(diphenylmethylene)-2,6-diisopropyl-4-(4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-1,3,2-dioxaborolan-2-yl)aniline), tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0) and cesium carbonate at 75-80 degrees C in anhydrous tetrahydrofuran and water, separating liquid to remove aqueous phase, adding ethyl acetate to promote the product to separate out, suction filtering, washing with ethyl acetate and drying to obtain first step product, dissolving the first step product in tetrahydrofuran solution, adding hydrochloric acid and hydrolyzing at 70-75 degrees C to obtain final product. The 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexabromotriptycene is prepared from triptycene through bromination, coupling with Suzuki and hydrolysis reaction includes dissolving triptycene and iron powder in dichloroethane, and slowly adding liquid bromine and refluxing at 80 degrees C, cooling to room temperature, removing the residual liquid bromine, spin-drying to remove the solvent, washing and drying to obtain 2,3,6,7,14,15-hexabromotriptycene, purifying after extracting, washing and drying in the reaction and separating and purifying the mixture by using dichloromethane as developing agent column and refluxing at 85 degrees C for 6 hours, where the molar ratio between triptycene, iron powder and liquid bromine is 1:0.35-0.4:6-7, preferably 1:0.37:6.5, and the volume of dichloroethane is 60 ml.

PN CN212716840-U
TI Urea mixer for use in engine of automobile, has inner layer pipe connected with rotational flow pipe, where inner surface of rotational flow pipe and/or inner layer pipe is provided with catalyst layer for promoting urea hydrolysis.
AU LV Z
   CHU Z
   MA W
   JIA D
AE WEICHAI POWER CO LTD (WEIC-C)
GA 202129019L
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a urea mixer, comprising a rotational flow pipe and an inner layer pipe connected with the rotational flow pipe, wherein it comprises: the inner surface of the rotational flow pipe and/or the inner layer pipe is provided with a catalyst layer for promoting the urea hydrolysis. the urea aqueous solution evaporates to generate solid urea and water vapour; the solid urea is pyrolyzed to generate isocyanic acid and ammonia; the isocyanic acid hydrolysis generates ammonia gas and carbon dioxide. Urea molecules in the urea solution can form intermolecular hydrogen bonds with water molecules or urea molecules. The catalyst layer is set on the surface of the rotational flow pipe and/or the inner layer pipe, which can help reduce the formation of the hydrogen bond between the molecules in the urea solution, so as to accelerate the evaporation process of the aqueous solution, so as to reduce the risk of crystallization, reduce the discharge, and improve the reliability of the product. In addition, the utility model further claims an engine and automobile with the urea mixer.
