AB    NOVELTY - Separating adult yak rumen epithelial cells                comprises (i) collecting adult yak rumen epithelial                tissue, adding into beaker to clean by 70% of                ethanol, removing all epithelial tissue edge,                adding rest tissue in centrifugal tube, cutting                into paste, (ii) digesting crushed epithelial                tissue with EDTA mixed solution of I-type                collagenase and trypsin, digesting for four times                with 0.5% of trypsin, collecting cell suspension,                (iii) centrifuging, firstly using fetal calf serum                MEM culture liquid re-suspending cell, centrifuging                and collecting the cell precipitation and using                phosphate buffered saline buffer solution of                antibiotics, using nylon filter screen to filter                cell suspension, dispersing cell, centrifuging,                (iv) cleaning cell precipitation with low-sugar                DMEM complete culture medium re-suspending cell, 5%                carbon dioxide culture box to culture, changing                culture medium after culturing for 2 days, growing                cell adherent wall, (v) adding low sugar DMEM                complete culture medium.
   USE - The method is useful for separating adult yak                rumen epithelial cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: fast adherent growth and high                vigor and stable passage can be obtained. The test                cell model for the physiological regulation and                nutrient absorption mechanism of yak rumen                epithelium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i)                  collecting adult yak rumen epithelial tissue,                  adding into a beaker to clean by 70% of ethanol,                  removing all the epithelial tissue edge, adding                  the rest tissue in the centrifugal tube, cutting                  into paste, (ii) digesting the crushed epithelial                  tissue with EDTA mixed solution of I-type                  collagenase and trypsin, then digesting for four                  times with 0.5% of trypsin, collecting cell                  suspension, (iii) centrifuging the collected cell                  suspension centrifugal collecting cell                  precipitate, firstly using 10 % fetal calf serum                  MEM culture liquid re-suspending cell,                  centrifuging and collecting the cell                  precipitation and then using phosphate buffered                  saline (PBS) buffer solution of antibiotics to                  re-suspend cell precipitation, using 45 microns                  nylon filter screen to filter cell suspension,                  dispersing cell, centrifuging the collected                  single cell suspension to clean the cell, where                  the PBS buffer solution of the antibiotic                  contains 200U/ml penicillin, 0.2mg/ml                  streptomycin, 100 g/ml gentamicin and 0.5 mu g/ml                  amphotericin B, (iv) cleaning the cell                  precipitation with low-sugar DMEM complete                  culture medium re-suspending cell after                  inoculating in the culture bottle culture, the                  culture is at 37 degrees C, 5 % of carbon dioxide                  culture box to culture, changing new culture                  bottle every 40min to remove the fibroblasts,                  repeating for four times, changing the culture                  medium after culturing for 2 days, growing the                  cell adherent wall, where the culture medium                  contains 15% fetal calf serum,15 ng/mL epidermal                  growth factor, 0.5 % insulin-transferrin-selenium                  additive, 110 mg/l sodiumpyruvate, 4 mM                  L-glutamine, 200 U/ml penicillin,0.2mg/ml                  streptomycin, 100 mu g/ml gentamicin and 0.5 mu                  g/ml of amphotericin B low sugar DMEM complete                  culture medium, (v) when the cell adherent stable                  growth 50 %, using 0.25 % trypsin -0.02 EDTA                  digestion for 3 minutes to remove the fibroblast,                  adding low sugar DMEM complete culture medium to                  continuously culture, and when the cell grows to                  culture bottle. In the step (ii), the mass                  percentage contents of type I collagenase,                  trypsin and EDTA in the mixed solution are                  respectively 0.1%, 0.25% and 0.02%. In the step                  (ii), the EDTA mixture digestion of type I                  collagenase and trypsin is processed in a 37                  degrees C water bath shaker for 30 minutes, and                  the volume ratio of the cut epithelial tissue to                  the mixture is 1:2. After digestion, the mixture                  was removed by centrifugation and then washed by                  centrifugation with 4 degrees C pre-cooled PBS                  buffer containing antibiotics. The PBS buffer                  containing antibiotics contains 200 U/ml                  penicillin, 0.2 mg/ml streptomycin, 100 mu g/ml                  gentamicin and 0.5 mu g/ml amphotericin B. In the                  step (ii), the specific operation of the 0.5%                  trypsin digestion is transferring the epithelial                  tissue digested with the mixed solution to 2                  times the volume of 0.5% trypsin and adding it in                  a 37 degrees C water bath shaker for 30 minutes,                  passing the trypsinized liquid through a 300                  meshes nylon filter to filter the digestion                  solution to collect the cell suspension, the cell                  suspension was digested with MEM medium                  containing 10% fetal bovine serum. Use D-Hanks                  solution to wash the tissues and the remaining                  cells on the filter and collect them, adding new                  0.5% trypsin and circulate three times in this                  way, and the digestion times for the last three                  times are 20 minutes, 20 minutes and 15 minutes                  in sequence. In the step (iii), the                  centrifugation method is 4 degrees C, the                  centrifuge speed is 3000 rpm, and the                  centrifugation time is 8 minutes. In the step                  (iii), the dispersed cells use a 45 mu m cell                  sieve to disperse the cells, collect the cell                  suspension and disperse the cells twice. In the                  step (v), the passage is when the cells grow to                  80% of the culture flask, trypsin digestion with                  0.25% trypsin-0.02% EDTA for 5-10 minutes to                  collect the cells for passage. In the step (v),                  the cryopreserved liquid is DMEM culture medium,                  and the fetal bovine serum and DMSO are                  configured in a volume ratio of 6:3:1.

PN CN112374464-A
TI Preparation of hypochlorous acid involves preparing hypochlorous acid mixed solution, subjecting to ion exchange through ion exchanger to remove heavy metal ions, electrolyzing, and reducing content of chloride ions.
AU DONGFANG X
AE ZHONGSHAN YUANYU ENTERPRISE INVESTMENT (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2021202980
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hypochlorous acid involves (1) using pre-filter to treat tap water, obtaining purified water, (2) adding hypochlorite solution and the purified water into hypochlorite solution diluter, obtaining diluted hypochlorite solution, (3) adding the hypochlorite solution and acid solution to mixer for mixing reaction, obtaining hypochlorous acid mixed solution, or adding the hypochlorite solution to the mixer, passing carbon dioxide gas to the mixer through pipeline for mixing reaction, obtaining hypochlorous acid mixed solution with pH of 4.0-8.0, (4) subjecting the hypochlorous acid mixed solution to ion exchange through ion exchanger to remove heavy metal ions, obtaining heavy metal ion hypochlorous acid, (5) feeding the hypochlorous acid mixed solution into electrolysis device for electrolysis at current of 0.05-1.0 A to convert chloride ions in solution into hypochlorous acid molecules, reducing content of chloride ions in solution at the same time remove heavy metal ions.
   USE - Preparation of hypochlorous acid by ion exchange method combined with electrolysis method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and can remove heavy metal ions in hypochlorous acid, reduce the content of chloride ions in the solution, and increase the concentration of hypochlorous acid, thereby improving the concentration and stability of hypochlorous acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The concentration of the carbon dioxide gas in step (3) is 1-99 vol.%. The concentration of the hypochlorite solution in step (2) is 4-15%. The concentration of the diluted hypochlorite solution is 10-5000 ppm. The concentration of the acidic solution in step (3) is 1-37%. Preferred Condition: The flow rate of water in step (1) is adjusted to 5-100 L/minutes. The water pressure in step (1) is controlled at 0.01-0.5 MPa. Preferred Components: The hypochlorite solution is chosen from one or more of sodium hypochlorite, calcium hypochlorite, and potassium hypochlorite. The acidic solution is chosen from one of hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid, phosphoric acid, chloric acid, disodium hydrogen phosphate buffer and sodium dihydrogen phosphate buffer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The ion exchanger contains acidic cation exchange resin. The acidic cation exchange resin is a fatty sulfonic acid-based resin or/and a carboxylic acid-based resin.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)
MC A12-M; E31-C; J03-B
IP C01B-011/04; C25B-001/26; C25B-015/08

PN KR2021017064-A
TI Recovering lithium component in basic waste liquid generated during manufacturing positive electrode active material by converting lithium hydroxide in waste liquid into lithium carbonate, removing sulfate and concentrating waste liquid.
AU CHOI Y
   LEE J
   KYU K
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 202117382X
AB    NOVELTY - A lithium component in basic waste liquid containing lithium, metal containing nickel, and sulfate generated during a manufacturing process of positive electrode active material is recovered by (1) filtering basic waste liquid to filter the solid metal contained in the basic waste liquid; (2) converting lithium hydroxide in the waste liquid into lithium carbonate by introducing carbon dioxide into waste liquid obtained by filtering solid metals; (3) converting sulfate contained in the basic waste liquid into barium sulfate by adding barium carbonate to waste liquid in which lithium carbonate is generated; (4) filtering the barium sulfate to remove sulfate from the waste liquid; and (5) recovering lithium carbonate by concentrating the waste liquid.
   USE - Recovery of lithium component in basic waste liquid generated during a manufacturing process of positive electrode active material.
   ADVANTAGE - Lithium ions and sulfates contained in the waste liquid generated during the production of positive electrode active material can be easily removed, and since no additional purification process is required, the process can be efficiently carried out, and the metal components in the waste liquid are removed in advance. Waste treatment costs can also be reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The addition of aqueous barium carbonate solution to waste solution comprises adding 0.02-0.13 pt. wt. aqueous barium carbonate solution based on the total weight of the waste solution. Nickel in the waste liquid is converted to nickel carbonate by adding barium carbonate to the waste liquid. Cobalt in the waste liquid is converted into cobalt carbonate by adding barium carbonate to waste liquid. Addition of barium carbonate to the waste liquid comprises adding carbon dioxide to a slurry obtained by mixing distilled water and barium carbonate, and adding in the form of a barium bicarbonate solution. The distilled water and barium carbonate are mixed in a weight ratio of 3:1-7:1. Preferred Components: The metal in the waste solution contains greater than or equal to 1 of nickel, cobalt, and manganese. Preferred Parameters: The waste solution is a basic solution having a pH of 7-14.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PN CN112285039-A
TI Determining phosphate in fluorosilicic acid for quality control of fluosilicic acid production, involves performing standard curve production, and weighing a sample of fluorosilicic acid into a platinum dish, then adding perchloric acid.
AU REN Z
   LIU Z
   YANG Z
   AN D
   MA F
   LIU J
AE QINGHAI XIKUANGTONGXIN CHEM CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202113548L
AB    NOVELTY - Determining phosphate in fluorosilicic acid involves performing standard curve production, and weighing a sample of fluorosilicic acid into a platinum dish, then adding perchloric acid, and heat and evaporating until the white smoke is exhausted. The platinum dish is rinsed with carbon dioxide-free water, continue to heated the water bath to steam to a small volume, repeated this operation 3 times to completely volatilize all the volatile acids, and finally transferred the resulting solution into a volumetric flask for constant volume. The solution is dispensed to a volumetric flask, and then added p-nitrophenol indicator, and adjusted the acidity with ammonia and hydrochloric acid, and added hydrochloric acid to maintain a certain amount of acidity. The potassium antimony tartrate, ascorbic acid and ammonium molybdate are added in sequence to make the volume constant, and waited for the color to develop. The phosphate content is measured on a spectrophotometer.
   USE - Method for determining phosphate in fluorosilicic acid for quality control of fluosilicic acid production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, high test result accuracy and precision, and can satisfy the measurement of phosphate content in fluosilicic acid, and for quality control of fluosilicic acid production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The volumetric flask is made of polyethylene.

TI Fabricating carbon foam used in silicon-carbon anode, involves pulverizing feedstock into powder and filling containment device with powder, placing containment device in reactor coupled to backpressure valve and pressurizing the reactor.
AU BELMONT E
   STAHLFELD K
AE UNIV WYOMING (UYWO-C)
GA 2021115316
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pulverizing feedstock into a powder and filling a containment device with the powder, placing the containment device in a reactor coupled to a backpressure valve, pressurizing the reactor housing the containment device to subcritical or supercritical conditions for an environment of carbon dioxide (CO2) or nitrogen (N2), loading the reactor in a furnace, where the backpressure valve is coupled to a collection container, heating the furnace at a constant heat rate until the reactor reaches a maximum temperature, pyrolyzing the feedstock powder to form carbon foam in the reactor, and collecting a bio-oil co-product in the collection container.
   USE - Method for fabricating carbon foam used in silicon-carbon anode (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to fabricate carbon foam having favorable mechanical strength and electrical conductivity properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a silicon-carbon anode, which comprises a carbon foam comprising a matrix having multiple pores, silicon particles encapsulated within the matrix and/or the pores, where silicon content of the carbon foam is between 1-85 wt.%, and a current collector, where the carbon foam comprising silicon is coupled to the current collector.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pulverized feedstock is one or more of corn stover, pine, Miscanthus giganteus, hemicellulose, cellulose, lignin, bituminous coal, or combinations or mixtures. The powder has a particle size less than about 0.40 mm. The range of a ratio of a volume of the powder loaded into the containment device to a volume of the reactor is between about 1:10 to about 8.5:10. The powder is compressed into pellets prior to filling the containment device. The collection container coupled to the backpressure valve is maintained at a temperature of between about - 65 degrees C to about - 85 degrees C. The reactor is heated in the furnace at a rate of at least 10 degrees C/minute. The CO2 or N2 environment further comprises H2O. The ratio of carbon to silicon of the carbon foam is between about 2:1 and about 1:2. The pores have a diameter between 1 micrometer and about 500 micrometers.

AU LIU T
   ZHAO X
   ZHAO D
   JIANG J
   SHEN Q
AE HEZE RUIPU PEONY IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (HEZE-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2966L
AB    NOVELTY - Functional beverage comprises moutan peony, Chinese peony, rhizoma polygonati, Cistanche, fructus momordicae and Stevia rebaudiana.
   USE - The functional beverage is useful for improving and enhancing physical fitness, immunity, and vitality.
   ADVANTAGE - The functional beverage has rich nutrition, neutralized bitter taste of peony, and different flavor such as chocolate flavor, coffee flavor, Coca-Cola flavor, ice tea and herbal tea flavor. The method ensures that the nutrients are not damaged, not denatured, pollution-free, and residue-free, so that nutrients are more conducive to comprehensive absorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the functional beverage, which involves (a) preparing materials by weighing the above-mentioned raw material, and removing dust and impurity, (b) pre-processing the raw material by adding the raw materials into an ultrasonic cleaning machine for cleaning, drying all the cleaned raw materials, placing into a microwave dryer for drying, sterilizing, and using a crusher to crush the raw materials, (c) extracting the crushed raw materials by supercritical carbon dioxide, (d) drying by performing vacuum drying of extract to obtain extract powder, (e) adding the extract powder to auxiliary materials to make a functional beverage, and (f) performing inspection and packing of the functional beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The functional beverage comprises 80-140 parts mass, preferably 110 parts mass moutan peony, 60-120 parts mass, preferably 90 parts mass Chinese peony, 40-80 parts mass, preferably 60 parts mass rhizoma polygonati, 45-75 parts mass, preferably 60 parts mass Cistanche, 50-70 parts mass, preferably 60 parts mass fructus momordicae and 60-80 parts mass, preferably 70 parts mass S.rebaudiana. The finished peony functional drink is a liquid beverage or a solid beverage. Preferred Components: The moutan peony is one or more of peony petal, peony stamen, peony pistil, peony pollen, peony leaf, cortex moutan, peony seed, peony seed shell and peony fruit shell. The Chinese peony is one or more of peony petal, peony pistil, peony stamen, peony pollen, peony leaf, radix paeoniae rubra, peony seed, peony seed shell, and peony shell. Preferred Method: The step (b) involves performing ultrasonic cleaning under a frequency of 15-25 KHz for 35-40 minutes, where the crushed particle size is 0.3-0.5 mm. In step (c), the extraction pressure is 33-50 MPa, the temperature is 25-45 degrees C, and the extraction time is 1-4 hours. The separator has a separation pressure of 13-15 kPa and temperature of 50-60 degrees C. The drying temperature is 45-60 degrees C, and the vacuum degree is 0.02-0.06 MPa. In step (e), purified water and food additives are added to the extract powder to prepare a liquid functional beverage or a solid functional beverage with a diameter of 0.5-1.5 mm through a granulator.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN111826496-A; CN111826496-B
TI Electric furnace smelting period shortening method involves utilizing last furnace smelted steel, adding lime into furnace, adding scrap steel materials and alloy materials, melting scrap material, and adding carbon powder, ferrosilicon.
AU CHEN L
   WANG J
AE PANGANG GROUP RES INST CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2020A7380A
AB    NOVELTY - An electric furnace smelting period shortening method comprises utilizing last furnace smelted 10 ton steel, adding 18-25 kg/ton steel lime into the furnace, total of 40-50 ton of scrap steel materials and alloy materials, melting scrap material, adding 40-60 kg/ton steel carbon powder, 1.2-2.5 kg/ton steel ferrosilicon and 2-5 kg/ton steel slagging agent, blowing oxygen at 10-30 m3/ton steel under 0.8-1.2 MPa, feeding nitrogen gas at flow rate of 0.06-0.25 Nm3/ton.minute, utilizing carbon dioxide during oxidation period at flow rate of 0.10-0.35 Nm3/ton.minute, feeding natural gas during reduction period at flow rate of 0.2-0.45 Nm3/ton.minute, adding 1-2 kg/ton steel carbon powder into the ladle during reduction period; adding 1.5-2.2 kg/ton steel ferrosilicon, controlling steel slag basicity during slagging process at 2.8-3.2, and controlling smelting end temperature at 1650-1680 degrees C.
   USE - Electric furnace smelting period shortening method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has shortened electric furnace smelting cycle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lime is active lime containing 85-90 wt.% calcium oxide. The carbon powder is carbon powder with carbon content of greater than or equal to 95 wt.%. The ferrosilicon contains 75 wt.% silicon, 22 wt.% iron and balance unavoidable impurities. The slagging agent comprises 70-75 wt.% calcium oxide, 8-14 wt.% calcium fluoride, 4-5 wt.% alumina, 3-5 wt.% silica and balance unavoidable impurities; and has particle size of less than or equal to 15 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The alloy material is nickel, chromium, silicon, manganese, molybdenum, vanadium, titanium, niobium, boron and/or lead. The scrap steel material is scrap steel with iron content of not greater than 80%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The methane content in the natural gas is greater than or equal to 95 wt.%.

PN CN111792658-A; CN212560006-U; CN111792658-B
TI Producing high-purity magnesia comprises e.g.            crushing and grinding the large crystal auxiliary            materials, sintering magnesium carbonate crystals and            then crushing, calcining in a shaft kiln and            cooling.
AU CAI X
   ZHANG L
   YU Q
   LIU W
   ZHANG Y
AE HAICHENG MAGNESITE GROUP CO LTD (HAIC-Non-standard)
GA 2020A64550
AB    NOVELTY - Producing high-purity magnesia comprises (i)                crushing and grinding the large crystal auxiliary                materials, and grinding them with a ball mill to a                particle size of 200 mesh, where high-purity                magnesia is processed from magnesite flotation                powder and large crystalline by-products, (ii)                mixing magnesite flotation powder, large                crystalline auxiliary material powder and water in                a mass ratio of 1:6-8, and passing in carbon                dioxide and stirring for more than 30 minutes,                (iii) separating solid and liquid, and heating                separated aqueous solution and distilling to obtain                magnesium carbonate crystals, (iv) sintering                magnesium carbonate crystals and then crushing to                obtain light magnesium oxide powder, pressing the                light magnesium oxide powder into a ball using ball                press, drying, and then placing dried magnesium                ball in kiln, calcining in a shaft kiln, and                finally cooling to 150-200 degrees C through a                cooling zone to obtain high-purity magnesia using                the furnace.
   USE - The method is useful for producing high-purity                magnesia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves mineral resources, increases                output of high-purity magnesia, and solves the                problem that large crystalline product by-materials                are difficult to handle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The large crystalline                  secondary material is product secondary material                  for producing large crystalline fused magnesium,                  having particle size of 10-200 mm. The physical                  and chemical indexes of the large crystalline                  by-materials are ignition loss is less than or                  equal to 0.15 wt.%, and magnesium oxide is                  greater than or equal to 92 wt.%. The mass ratio                  of magnesite flotation powder and large                  crystalline auxiliary powder is 0-100% magnesite                  flotation powder and 0-100% large crystalline                  auxiliary powder. Preferred Method: The heating                  distillation in the above step (iii) is 95-110                  degrees C. The sintering temperature of magnesium                  carbonate crystals in the above step (iv) is                  750-800 degrees C, and the calcination                  temperature in the shaft kiln is 1950-2050                  degrees C.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                MOF-based composition, which comprises a                metal-organic framework comprising a central metal                and a metal cluster containing a hydroxyl group                bonded to the central metal, a metal-organic                complex in which dissimilar metal ions are                substituted in place of a proton in the hydroxyl                group, in which one to three types of gas                adsorption different metal ions selected from the                group consisting of volatile organic compounds                (VOC), carbon dioxide, and water vapor are                substituted.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal cations are                  selected from Ni+, Li+, Na+, Li+, Cs+, K+, Ca2+,                  Sr2+, Ba2+, Ru2+, Ru3+, Ru4+, Pt+, Pt3+, Fe2+,                  Fe3+, Co2+, Co3+, Ni2+, Cu+, Cu2+, Zn2+, Ti4+,                  Zr4+, Nb5+, Pd2+, Pd4+, Ag+, Ag2+, Au+, Au3+,                  Ir3+, and Ir4+. The MOF is selected from UiO-66,                  UiO-67, NU-1000, MOF-808, MOF-801, PCN-777,                  DUT-67, MIL-100 (X1) (X1 is iron, chromium or                  aluminum), MIL- 101 (X2) (X2 is iron or                  chromiumr), MIL-53 (X3) (X3 is aluminum Al,                  gallium Ga, or indium In) or MOF A520. Preferred                  Conditions: The metal-organic complex substituted                  with the cationic organic compound and the                  metal-organic skeleton cation-exchanged maintain                  the crystal structure of the metal-organic                  skeleton in which heterometallic ion exchange is                  performed. The metal-organic framework having a                  metal cluster contains central metal and a                  hydroxyl group bonded to the central metal. The                  method for producing a hetero-metal ion-exchanged                  metal-organic framework involves contacting a                  metal-organic framework having a metal cluster                  containing a central metal and a hydroxyl group                  bonded to the central metal with a gaseous                  organic compound. The metal-organic skeleton is                  pretreated and activated under vacuum reduced                  conditions of 1x 10-1 to 1x 10-5 Torr at 100-200                  degrees C. The organic compound is pre-treated in                  a vacuum pressure condition of 15-30 degrees C                  for removing moisture. The cationic organic                  compound of the metal-organic complex is                  exchangeable for dissimilar metal ions at the                  substituted site.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cationic organic                  compound is selected from the group consisting of                  protonated triethylenediamine (TEDA),                  ethylenediamine, hexamethylenetetramine, and                  triazaphosphaadamantane. The MOF-based                  composition comprises MOF substituted with the                  different metal ions as an active                  ingredient.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111362251-A; CN111362251-B
TI High phosphorus-nitrogen co-doped three-dimensional porous carbon block is used as battery electrode material comprises carbon block having three-dimensional interconnected macroporous open-pore structure with mesopores and micropores.
AU ZHAO G
   GONG J
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202063658K
AB    NOVELTY - A high- phosphorus-nitrogen co-doped three-dimensional porous carbon block comprises carbon block having three-dimensional interconnected macroporous open-pore structure with small amount of mesopores and micropores distributed in the structure. The carbon block has Brunauer, Emmett and Teller specific surface area of 5-30 m2/g and contains 50-85 at.% carbon element, 2-20 at.% phosphorus, 2-20 at.% nitrogen, and 5-40 at.% oxygen.
   USE - The high- phosphorus-nitrogen co-doped three-dimensional porous carbon block is used as battery electrode material, particularly potassium ion battery self-supporting negative electrode material (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous carbon block has excellent reversible specific capacity, first coulombic efficiency, rate performance, and cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the porous carbon block comprising dissolving polyacrylonitrile powder and pore-forming agent in dimethyl sulfoxide, evaporating portion of the solvent, cutting, hot pressing into block, punching, and supercritical carbon dioxide foaming to obtain polyacrylonitrile foam; preparing hydroxylamine hydrochloride aqueous solution to adjust pH value of the solution to neutral, immersing polyacrylonitrile-based foam, stirring, washing, and drying; immersing in phosphorus source solution, stirring, washing, and drying; and stabilizing and carbonizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of phosphorus source solution is 20-85 wt.%, where phosphorus source is phosphoric acid, polyphosphoric acid, or phytic acid, preferably phosphoric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of polyacrylonitrile powder and pore-forming agent is 5:1-3:1. The mass ratio of dimethyl sulfoxide to total of polyacrylonitrile powder and pore forming agent is 1:12-1:6. Preferred Method: The first step is done by hot-pressing at 140-160 degrees C and 10-20 MPa for 10-20 minutes and supercritical carbon dioxide foaming at 100-150 degrees C and 10-50 MPa for 1-5 hours. The reaction condition in second step is done at 80-100 degrees C for 1-5 hours with 10-40 g/L hydroxylamine hydrochloride aqueous solution. The fourth step is done by heating to 230-280 degrees C for 2-8 hours and carbonizing at 500-1000 degrees C for 1-5 hours.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing zinc oxide doped mixed crystal                titanium dioxide nano-photocatalytic composite                material comprises taking titanium mesh as anode                and platinum sheet as the cathode, placing anode                and cathode in the electrolyte, carrying out                anodizing reaction to obtain titanium dioxide                nano-network array based on the titanium substrate,                immersing titanium dioxide nano-network array on                the titanium substrate in ethanol solution in which                zinc acetate is dissolved, taking out and drying                and heat-treating.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolyte is                  glycol aqueous solution containing ammonium                  fluoride, where the amount of ammonium fluoride                  in the electrolyte is 0.5-0.6 wt.% and water is                  5-5.5 vol.%. In the zinc acetate ethanol                  solution, the concentration of zinc acetate is                  0.25 mol/l. Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises pretreating the titanium mesh by:                  ultrasonically treating and drying the titanium                  substrate, carrying out mixed acid treatment                  using hydrofluoric acid and glacial acetic acid                  in volume ratio of 1:8, washing with water to                  obtain the pretreated titanium mesh, preferably,                  placing the titanium mesh sequentially in                  propanol, methanol, and isopropanol for                  ultrasonic treatment, washing with water and                  drying. In the method, the anodizing adopts                  constant voltage direct current power supply with                  constant temperature stirring, where the                  anodizing voltage is 60 V, the reaction                  temperature is 25 degrees C, the electrode                  spacing is 7 cm and the reaction time is 120                  minutes. The dipping treatment time is 5-10                  minutes, preferably 8 minutes. The method of heat                  treatment comprises: carrying out heat treatment                  in air atmosphere at 450-650 degrees C,                  preferably 500 degrees C at rate of 5-10 degrees                  C/minute, preferably 10 degrees C/minute for 2                  hours using muffle furnace and heat preservation                  treatment, cooling to room temperature with the                  furnace.

AB    NOVELTY - Performing mammalian cell suspension culture                resuscitation involves removing frozen cells from                liquid nitrogen, putting in a 37 degrees C constant                temperature water bath and incubating for 150-190                seconds. The                cells treated in the first step are added to the                culture flask containing the cell recovery solution                in the second step, and the cells are mixed and                placed in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide (CO2)                constant temperature and humidity incubator for                static culture recovery. The cell culture flask is                removed after 2 days of resuscitation, transferred                all the cell suspension in the flask to a shaker                flask, and placed at 37 degrees C, 5% CO2, 100rpm                constant temperature and humidity shaker for 2                days.

PN CN110684722-A
TI Preparing mesenchymal stem cells derived from            placental chorionic plate tissue comprises e.g.            providing placenta tissue sample, washing with tissue            cleaning solution separating the chorionic tissue,            soaking in alcohol and culturing.
AU LI J
   ZHAO L
   LIU X
   JIANG J
AE GUANGDONG VITALIFE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202008659T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mesenchymal stem cells derived from                placental chorionic plate tissue comprises (i)                providing placenta tissue sample, washing with                tissue cleaning solution, separating the chorionic                tissue, and then washing with tissue cleaning                solution until the chorionic tissue is translucent,                (ii) placing the chorionic tissues in a                cryopreservation solution, cooling and                cryopreservating according to predetermined                procedure, (iii) removing the frozen chorion                tissue, soaking in alcohol solution, washing with                tissue cleaning solution, washing away residual                alcohol and blood, removing placental leaflet                tissue, villus stems, blood vessels and blood clots                in blood vessels, removing tissues and impurities                and cutting the tissue into pieces and (iv) placing                the tissue block in a culture flask, adding                mesenchymal stem cell selection medium, culturing                in a carbon dioxide incubator, passing the cells                when the cell fusion reaches 80 plus minus 10%,                continue to passage cells, expanding and/or                cryopreservating.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mesenchymal                stem cells derived from placental chorionic plate                tissue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enables both placental chorion                plate tissue and mesenchymal stem cells prepared by                separation, can store for a longer time in a liquid                nitrogen environment, and maintains the cell                viability after resuscitation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The mesenchymal stem                  cell selection medium includes: mesenchymal stem                  cell serum-free medium, 5 plus minus 0.5% human                  blood albumin, 15 plus minus 5 ng/ml basic                  fibroblast growth factor (bFGF), and 10 plus                  minus 1% transforming growth factor. Preferred                  Components: The tissue cleaning solution                  includes: saline, 25 plus minus 5 mu g/ml                  gentamicin, and 5 plus minus 1 mu g/ml                  amphotericin B. In the                  cryopreservation solution, the concentration of                  dextran-based sucrose is 5 plus minus 0.5%, the                  concentration of non-essential amino acid is 2                  plus minus 0.2%, the concentration of trehalose                  concentration is 0.2 plus minus 0.02 mol/l, the                  concentration of dimethyl sulfoxide is 10 plus                  minus 1%, and the concentration of human serum                  albumin is 5 plus minus 0.5%. Preferred Method:                  In the tissue freezing step, reducing temperature                  according to the following procedure: maintaining                  at 4 degrees C for 40 minutes, and at -20 degrees                  C for 50 minutes, maintaining at -40 degrees C                  for 30 minutes and -80 degrees C for 12 hours,                  then placing the chorionic tissue in a liquid                  nitrogen tank and storing at low temperature. In                  the tissue processing step, soaking in 75%                  alcohol for 1 plus minus 0.5 minutes. In the                  tissue processing step, cutting the tissue into                  tissue pieces having a maximum area of 1-2 mm2.                  In the method, before the tissue separation step,                  further comprises a tissue collection step                  including collecting the placenta tissue,                  detecting microorganisms and viruses, storing the                  qualified placenta and/or transporting at 0-4                  degrees C in the presence of tissue protection                  solution. In the cell culture step, detecting the                  placental chorionic mesenchymal stem cell                  phenotype obtained from subculture, where the                  positive rates of cluster of differentiation 73                  (CD73), cluster of differentiation 90 (CD90), and                  cluster of differentiation 105 (CD105) are all                  greater than or equal to 95%, the positive rates                  of cluster of differentiation 34 (CD34) and                  cluster of differentiation 45 (CD45) are less                  than or equal to 5% and stop passaging and                  harvesting the cells.

   QIN Y

GA 2019A3088A
AB    NOVELTY - Method of preserving fresh and wet noodle by using fence technology involves (a) kneading dough, adding flour into a container, preparing salt and water to a salt aqueous solution, adding compound preservative into the saline solution, uniformly mixing, slowly pouring the mixture into the flour, and stirring, uniformly mixing and stirring into a snowflake state for 8 minutes, (b) fermenting the dough, wrapping the dough tightly with preservative film, leaving still for 25 minutes at 25 degrees C and (c) forming and preserving the dough, pressing the dough through a dough pressing machine for 6-8 times, pressing the formed dough sheet into noodles, carrying out convection heat treatment on the cut noodles at 95 degrees C for 25 minutes, cooling, loading into a packaging bag, filling with carbon dioxide, and sealing tightly.
   USE - The method is used for preserving fresh and wet noodle by using fence technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method preserves the fresh and wet noodles for more than 17 days at normal temperature, delays spoilage and deterioration of fresh wet noodles to great extent, improves quality of fresh and wet noodles, and effectively extends the preservation of fresh and wet noodles period.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The composite preservative comprises 0.3 pt. wt. sorbitol, 0.3 pt. wt. sodium carbonate, 0.15 pt. wt. propylene glycol, 0.1 pt. wt. glyceryl monocaprylate, and 2.25 pts. wt. alcohol. In the step (a) adding 1.5-2.5 wt.%, preferably 2 wt.% common salt, 30-35 wt.%, preferably 32 wt.% water, and 2.5-3.5 wt.%, preferably 3.1 wt.% composite preservative based on the flour.

PT P
PN CN110479307-A
TI Preparing high efficiency durable formaldehyde-based wood composite material, comprises e.g. immersing wood in mixed solution of nickel salt and ferrous salt, adding urea, sealing in reaction vessel, and performing hydrothermal reaction.
AU SUN Q
   CAI T
   HUANG Q
   WANG Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG A & F (UZAF-C)
   YIHUA LIFE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YIHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019A0502X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high efficiency durable formaldehyde-based wood composite material, comprises (i) immersing wood in a mixed solution of nickel salt and ferrous salt, and adding urea, (ii) adjusting the pH of the solution concentration to alkaline, sealing in a reaction vessel, performing hydrothermal reaction, and cooling to room temperature, (iii) adding a chloric noble metal acid solution to the solution, adding a reducing agent, and performing a reduction reaction at normal temperature, and (iv) taking out the wood, and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high efficiency durable formaldehyde-based wood composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes a low-temperature hydrothermal reaction to construct a catalyst active layer on the surface of wood; enhances the catalytic activity by decorating precious metal nanoparticles; maintains the original physical and chemical properties of the wood; utilizes natural hierarchical structure with rich pores to adsorb formaldehyde gas; captures formaldehyde molecules by using the hydroxy groups on the wood surface; improves the catalytic activity of the catalyst; collects formaldehyde gas into the channel and oxidized by the catalyst supported on the wood; and decomposes into carbon dioxide and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the nickel salt is nickel sulfate, nickel chloride or nickel nitrate, the ferrous salt is ferrous sulfate, ferrous chloride or ferrous nitrate, and the mixed solution is a mixture of a nickel salt solution and a ferrous salt solution having a volume ratio of 2:1 and a concentration of 0.01-5 mol/l. In the step (i), the concentration of urea in the solution is 0.15-0.20 mol/l. In the step (iii), the chloro noble metal acid is chloroplatinic acid, chloroauric acid or chloropalladium acid, the concentration of the chloro noble metal acid solution is 0.01-0.1 mol/l, and the volume ratio of the chlorine noble metal acid solution and the nickel-iron mixed solution is 1:99. In the step (iii), the reducing agent is a sodium borohydride solution having a concentration of 0.1 mol/l, and the volume ratio of the sodium borohydride solution and the chloro noble metal acid solution is 20:1. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the pH value is 7-9, and the hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 60-200 degrees C for 8-16 hours. In the step (iii), the reduction reaction is carried out for 1-3 hours. In the step (iv), the drying is carried out at 40-60 degrees C for 10-15 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the wood is cotton wood, birch wood, fir wood or light wood, and the surface of the wood needs to-be cleaned or polished before use.

PT P
PN CN110438489-A
TI Preparing continuous casting mold copper surface coating by cleaning mold copper material, mixing and stirring nickel powder and asphalt, coating, drying, performing laser cladding, preheating, carrying out annealing treatment and cooling.
AU GUO X
AE GUO X (GUOX-Individual)
GA 2019968198
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing continuous casting mold copper surface coating, involves (i) cleaning mold copper material to obtain substrate, mixing and stirring nickel powder and asphalt to obtain pre-coating material and coating on the substrate, and drying to obtain pretreated substrate, (ii) preheating pretreated substrate, placing in carbon dioxide laser, and performing laser cladding to obtain semi-finished product, (iii) placing semi-finished product in muffle furnace and preheating to obtain preheated semi-finished product, mixing cobalt-based powder and zirconium-based powder, placing in planetary ball mill, ball-milling to obtain mixed powder, drying mixed powder in oven at 60-80 degrees C to constant weight, and cooling to room temperature to obtain dry powder, and (iv) preheating the semi-finished product, synchronously feeding dry powder to obtain precursor, placing precursor in muffle furnace, carrying out stress-relief annealing treatment, and cooling to room temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing continuous casting mold copper surface coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The surface coating has low thermal conductivity, excellent chemical stability, wear resistance and toughening effect, high corrosion resistance and high hardness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves polishing the surface of the mold copper material with sandpaper, cleaning with anhydrous ethanol and acetone 3-5 times, naturally drying, mixing nickel powder and asphalt at a mass ratio of 1:2, stirring at 400-500 rpm for 10-15 minutes to obtain pre-coating material, uniformly coating the pre-coating material on the surface of the substrate, and drying in an oven at 60-80 degrees C to constant weight, and cooling to room temperature. The step (ii) involves preheating the pretreated substrate at 180-200 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, and performing laser cladding treatment under argon protection at a laser power of 800-1150 W, scanning speed of 1-2 m/minute and spot size of 5 mmx 5 mm. The step (iii) involves placing the semi-finished product in muffle furnace, preheating at 400-500 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, mixing cobalt-based powder and zirconium-based powder in planetary ball mill at a mass ratio of 1:1, and ball-milling at 200-300 rpm for 1-2 hours. The step (iv) involves synchronous feeding dry powder on the preheated semi-finished product at a laser power of 330-350 W, pulse frequency of 60-65 Hz, scanning speed of 5-7 mm/second, pulse width of 1-1.2 m.second, spot size of 1-1.5 mm, and the overlap rate of 40-50% to obtain the precursor, performing stress-relief annealing in the muffle furnace at 250-350 degrees C for 5-7 hours, and cooling to room temperature.

PN CN110387348-A
TI Isolating and culturing human skin keratinocytes, by cutting fresh skin tissue, cleaning, digesting, centrifuging, detecting cell activity, re-suspending cells in complete culture medium, culturing, removing fibroblast and identifying cells.
AU INVENTOR U
AE JIANGSU CHI SCI CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019924702
AB    NOVELTY - Method for isolating and culturing human skin keratinocytes, involves (i) cutting fresh skin tissues under aseptic condition, and sealing and transporting the fresh skin tissues back to a laboratory at 4 degrees C, (ii) cleaning skin tissues 3 times in a sterile operation table using pre-cooled phosphate buffered saline to remove surface blood vessels, fat and connective tissues, (iii) cutting the tissue and digesting with neutral protease at 4 degrees C, (iv) separating the dermis and the epidermis, (v) continuously digesting the epidermal tissue with preheated trypsin, (vi) stop digesting, centrifuging, and discarding the supernatant, (vii) taking cell filtrate, and detecting cell activity by using tryphan blue staining method, (viii) counting the cells, re-suspending the cells in a complete culture medium of a culture bottle, and culturing in a 5% carbon dioxide environment at 37 degrees C, (ix) removing fibroblasts by adopting differential adherence method, and (x) carrying out cell identification.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and culturing human skin keratinocytes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables the isolation and culturing of human skin keratinocytes with large number of cells and high survival rate, provides reliable cellular resources for the experiment, and is simple to operate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the neutral protease is 0.1-0.5%. The concentration of trypsin is 0.05-0.2%. The trypsin comprises 0.01% EDTA to absorb calcium ions and magnesium ions in the tissue and promote cell separation. The complete culture medium comprises 10% fetal bovine serum, 100 U/ml penicillin and 100 U/ml streptomycin, and Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium/Ham's nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12) (1:1) medium of 5 mu g/ml insulin. Preferred Method: The culture bottle in step (viii) is coated with 3-4 mu g/cm2 polylysine. Preferred Conditions: In step (iii), the digestion is carried out for 15-20 hours. In step (v), the digestion is carried out for 10-20 minutes. In step (vi), the centrifuging is carried out at 300-400 g for 5-8 minutes. The differential adherence method used in step (ix) has a differential time of 15-30 minutes.

TI METHOD OF PRODUCING AIR REGENERATION PRODUCT.
AU DOROKHOV R V
   FERAPONTOV YU A
   PLOTNIKOV M YU
   TAROVA A A
AE ROSKHIMZASHCHITA CORP STOCK CO (ROSK-Non-standard)
GA 201999210H
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to methods of producing air regeneration products for human respiratory protection systems against chemical and biological agents. Method of producing a product for air regeneration involves reacting a hydrogen peroxide solution stabilized with magnesium sulphate in molar ratio hydrogen peroxide / magnesium sulphate (H2O2/MgSO4) = 450700 and sodium and potassium hydroxides obtained by applying any known method (impregnation, aerosol sputtering, etc.) of the obtained alkaline solution of hydrogen peroxide to an indifferent porous matrix of required geometrical dimensions and subsequent dehydration of the system. Dehydration of the indifferent porous matrix impregnated with the initial alkaline hydrogen peroxide solution is carried out either by drying in a vacuum at temperature of 30150C, or in a stream of dried decarbonized gas at atmospheric pressure and temperature of 60220C. In alkaline solution of hydrogen peroxide after addition of magnesium sulphate after 13 minutes orthoboric acid is introduced in molar ratio hydrogen peroxide / orthoboric acid (H2O2/H3BO3) = 285850, wherein sodium and potassium hydroxides are introduced into the liquid phase simultaneously in two portions with interval of 610 minutes such that the temperature in the synthesis zone does not exceed 60C.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Product for air regeneration obtained according to the invention has a higher degree of carbon dioxide treatment and provides a longer time of protective action during its operation in human life support systems.

Preferred Components: The epoxy                  group-containing compound comprises ethylene                  oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide,                  epichlorohydrin, styrene oxide, epoxycyclohexane                  and/or oxidized cyclopentane. Preferred Method: The polymer is                  prepared by subjecting methacrylate-containing                  compound of formula (II) and halogen-containing                  compound of formula (III) to quaternization                  reaction. The silane coupling agent is                  vinyltrimethoxysilane, vinyltriethoxysilane                  and/or methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane.X=iodine atom, chlorine atom or bromine                  atom, preferably a bromine atom;R4=hydroxyl group;R1=H or 1-3C alkyl;R2, R3=1-5C alkyl; andm=1-4.

PN IN201731039417-A
TI Beneficiation of bauxite ore to obtain upgraded alumina, by calcining bauxite to eliminate moisture and volatile materials, reducing in hydrogen plasma smelting reactor, grinding, mixing with water, and treating in wet magnetic separator.
AU PARHI B R
   SAHOO S K
   MISHRA S C
   MOHANTY U K
   BHOI B
   KAR S
   BANDOPADHYAY P
AE NAT ALUMINIUM CO LTD (NAAL-Non-standard)
GA 201943388F
AB    NOVELTY - Process for beneficiation of bauxite ore to obtain an upgraded alumina, involves calcining the bauxite to eliminate moisture and volatile materials, introducing the calcined bauxite into a hydrogen plasma smelting reactor for reduction, grinding the reduced bauxite to a powder having particle size of 50-100 mu m, mixing the powder with water to form a slurry, treating the slurry in a SALA wet magnetic separator for separation of magnetic constituents at high magnetic intensities, and characterizing the constituents by X-ray diffraction techniques.
   USE - The process is useful for beneficiation of bauxite ore to obtain upgraded alumina, which is further treated for direct extraction of aluminum with high yield.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enhances purity of alumina and decreases gangue content, reduces handling cost and manpower cost, reduces greenhouse problems, ensures safety and reliability, reduces generation of alkaline toxic solid waste, preferably red mud, utilizes hydrogen as reducing agent and generates water, which is an eco-friendly side product in place of carbon dioxide, generates iron bearing tailings to facilitate extraction of iron metal, and adopts hydrogen plasma smelting route, which saves manpower cost and minimizes global pollution related issues.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Properties: In the smelting reactor, the hydrogen flow rate is 8-12 l/minute, preferably 10 l/minute, the hydrogen flow time is 20-40 minutes, preferably 30 minutes, and temperature during hydrogen plasma reduction is 1400-1600 degrees C, preferably 1500 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The bauxite is calcined at 400-800 degrees C for 1-5 hours. The ratio of powder to water is 1:20-1:5 mass/volume. The magnetic separation is carried out at high magnetic field intensities of 4900-15400 Gauss.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN109705172-A; CN109705172-B
TI New diglycolamine-bridged diaryloxy rare earth            polyphenoxy magnesium heterocyclic metal complex used            as catalyst in preparation of cyclic carbonate.
AU YAO Y
   HUA L
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 2019423021
AB    NOVELTY - A diglycolamine-bridged diaryloxy rare earth                polyphenoxy magnesium heterocyclic metal complex                (I) is new.
   USE - New diglycolamine-bridged diaryloxy rare earth                polyphenoxy magnesium heterocyclic metal complex is                used as catalyst in preparation of cyclic carbonate                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The diglycolamine-bridged diaryloxy rare earth                polyphenoxy magnesium heterocyclic metal complex is                capable of economically and easily producing cyclic                carbonate with high yield and high-selectivity                conversion under ambient temperature and normal                pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A diglycolamine-bridged diaryloxy rare earth                polyphenoxy magnesium heterocyclic metal complex of                formula (I) is new.RE = rare-earth metal ion chosen                from ions of yttrium, ytterbium, neodymium and                lanthanum.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of complex (I); and
   (2) preparation of cyclic carbonate of formula                (A), which involves carrying out ring-opening                addition reaction of an alkylene oxide compound of                formula (B) with carbon dioxide at 20-100 degrees C                and a pressure of 0.05-1.5 MPa under the action of                a catalyst including a main catalyst which is                complex (I), and a cocatalyst which is a quaternary                ammonium salt.
   R1,R2 = H, alkyl, alkoxy, unsaturated                hydrocarbyl, aryl or ester, or linear or branched                1-18C alkyl or linear or branched 1-18C alkoxy                (both optionally substituted);and
   R1+R2 = 3-18C carbocyclyl or 2-17C                oxygen-containing heterocyclyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The complex (I) is                  produced by refluxing 2-(2-aminoethoxy)ethanol of                  formula (II), formaldehyde and                  2,4-di-tert-butylphenol of formula (III), in a                  molar ratio of 1:2:2 in an organic solvent at                  90-110 degrees C for 8-24 hours to obtain a                  diethylene glycol amine-bridged bisaryloxy ligand                  precursor of formula (IV), reacting compound (IV)                  with a rare earth tricyclopentadienyl in                  tetrahydrofuran, in a molar ratio of 1:1, under                  anhydrous and anaerobic conditions at 20-40                  degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain a                  diethylene-glycol-amine-bridged bisaryloxy rare                  earth metal compound of formula (V), reacting                  tetrabutyl magnesium and benzyl alcohol in a                  molar ratio of 1:2, in an organic solvent at                  20-40 degrees C for 4-24 hours under anhydrous                  and anaerobic conditions to obtain                  benzyloxymagnesium, and reacting                  benzyloxymagnesium with compound (V) in a molar                  ratio of 1:2 at 45-60 degrees C for 72 hours or                  more. Preferred Composition: The quaternary                  ammonium salt is tetrabutyl ammonium chloride,                  tetrabutyl ammonium iodide, tetrabutyl ammonium                  bromide, tetraoctylammonium bromide,                  tetramethylammonium bromide,                  benzyltrioctylammonium bromide and/or                  bis(triphenylphosphine) ammonium chloride.                  Compound (B) is chosen from propylene oxide,                  epichlorohydrin, 1,2-butylene oxide,                  1,2-epoxyhexane, 1,2-epoxyheptane,                  1,2-epoxyoctane, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl                  ether, (4-fluorophenoxy)methyloxirane,                  2-(4-methylphenoxy)methyloxirane, 4-chlorophenyl                  glycidyl ether, 2-chlorophenyl glycidyl ether,                  3-chlorophenyl glycidyl ether,                  1,2-epoxy-5-hexene, methyl glycidyl ether, allyl                  glycidyl ether, butyl glycidyl ether, glycidyl                  4-tert-butylbenzoate, glycidol,                  4-(2,3-epoxypropyl)morpholine, 1,2,7,8-diketene,                  1,2-dimethyloxirane, epoxy cyclohexane,                  epoxycyclopentane, and 3,4-epoxytetrahydrofuran.                  The molar ratio of complex (I) to epoxy compound                  is 1:50-500. The molar ratio of quaternary                  ammonium salt to complex (I) is 0.5-6:1.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a substrate (100). An interlayered insulating layer (300) is formed on the substrate. PN CN109401311-A
TI Preparation of corrosion-resistant plastic packaging material, involves blow-molding pellets prepared using e.g. diatomaceous earth, montmorillonite, silica, tert-butyl hydroquinone, polyimide, polystyrene, polylactic acid, and melamine.
AU NI D
AE WUJIANG YINGLIDA PLASTIC PACKING CO LTD (WUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201922662B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of corrosion-resistant plastic packaging material, involves high-speed ball-milling 2-5 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth, 1-9 pts. wt. montmorillonite, 2-5 pts. wt. silica, and 3-7 pts. wt. tert-butyl hydroquinone for 40-60 minutes, subjecting the product to distillation washing, heating to 80-85 degrees C, refluxing for 30 minutes, reducing temperature to 40-45 degrees C, filtering to remove impurities, washing the solid thrice with 1.5% sodium citrate solution, drying at 65 degrees C, mixing with 20-29 pts. wt. polyimide, 14-18 pts. wt. polystyrene, 5-8 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 1-3 pts. wt. dimethyl sulfoxide, 3-7 pts. wt. melamine, 3-9 pts. wt. diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate, and 1-4 pts. wt. stabilizer, autoclaving at 140 degrees C for 40-60 minutes, charging carbon dioxide at 0.5 cm3/minute, maintaining the pressure at 5 MPa for 60 minutes, returning pressure to normal, melting, extruding and granulating using a parallel twin-screw extruder, blow-molding into a film, heat sealing, and reel molding.
   USE - Preparation of corrosion-resistant plastic packaging material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides corrosion-resistant plastic packaging materials having stable performance characteristics, acid resistance, and alkali resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The distillation medium is absolute ethanol. The stabilizer is ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine, m-xylene and/or diaminodiphenylsulfone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The ball/material ratio is 1:99. The ball mill rotating speed is 2000 rpm. The impurities are removed using filter with an aperture of 10 mu m. The screw temperature is 220-260 degrees C and screw rotation speed is 500 rpm. The temperature of charging barrel used in blow molding is 150-155 degrees C and the blow ratio is 2.5-4. The heat-sealing temperature is 180-185 degrees C.

GA 201922665F
AB    NOVELTY - Black high flame retardant polypropylene expanded bead comprises 100 phr polypropylene resin, 0.5-3 phr carbon black, 5-15 phr borax, 5-8 phr antimony trioxide, 0.3-2 phr red phosphorus, and 0.1-5 phr other foaming aid.
   USE - Black high flame retardant polypropylene expanded bead.
   ADVANTAGE - The micropores on the surface of carbon black of 25 mm particle size can improve the compatibility with the matrix and flame retardant, make the internal molecular chain of system more closely connected, and improve the regularity of molecular chain in the system. The flame retardant effect is improved. The comprehensive mechanical properties are improved and the polypropylene expanded beads provide excellent strengthening and toughening effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of black high flame retardant polypropylene expanded bead, which involves kneading, extruding, drawing of mixture by an extruder after mixing the components at high speed, and granulating using a pelletizer to form modified polypropylene particles, placing (2) polypropylene microparticles into the reaction vessel, and introducing carbon dioxide, generating (3) internal pressure which expands outward under high temperature and high pressure, and instantaneously releasing to atmospheric pressure to obtain a foamed polypropylene bead of a certain magnification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred properties: The carbon black powder has a particle size of 10-25 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The foaming aid includes nucleating aid, stabilizer, antioxidant, lubricant, and/or antistatic agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polypropylene resin is one or more of propylene-ethylene block or random copolymer and a homopolypropylene. Preferred Composition: The propylene content is greater than or equal to 80 mol%. Preferred Process: The preparation of black high flame retardant polypropylene expanded bead involves maintaining melt temperature of the modified polypropylene microparticles in the extrusion blending process within 180 degrees C, using high-torque twin-screw extrusion granulation to ensure uniform mixing and torque greater than 95 N.m.

PN CN108889283-A
TI Aerogel type calcium magnesium ion adsorbing material preparation involves mixing tetraethyl orthosilicate, silica sol, sodium hydroxide, polyacrylamide, isopropanol and deionized water with stirring at high speed.
AU ZHANG Y
   LIU W
AE YANG C (YANG-Individual)
GA 201897645G
AB    NOVELTY - Aerogel type calcium magnesium ion adsorbing material preparation involves mixing 60 ml tetraethyl orthosilicate, 20 ml silica sol, 1.5 g sodium hydroxide, 3 g polyacrylamide, 10 ml isopropanol and 15 ml deionized water with stirring at high speed at 50 degrees C under ultrasonic dispersion to obtain transparent microemulsion. 2 g tetrabutylammonium fluoride, 30 g n-hexane and 20 g phenyltriethoxysilane are added to the obtained transparent microemulsion with stirring at a high speed to obtain semi-gel solution. 50 g porous silicone organic framework material is added into the prepared mold with stirring uniformly to obtain inorganic additive-fiber composite gel material. The obtained gel material is aged in ethanol at 60 degrees C for 5 hours and then placed in a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction autoclave at 50 degrees C for 6 hours to obtain reinforced fiber composite porous aerogel ion adsorbent.
   USE - Method for preparing aerogel type calcium magnesium ion adsorbing material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the aerogel type calcium magnesium ion adsorbing material in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with improved stability and significantly improved softening effect on hard water quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The porous silicone organic framework material is prepared by adding 9.6 mmol 1,5-cyclooctadiene to 100 ml flask of 9.6 mmol bis,5-cyclooctadiene nickel and 4.8 mmol 2,2'-bipyridyl in anhydrous toluene at 80 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain a purple toluene solution. The obtained purple toluene solution is mixed with p-octaiodophenylsilsesquioxane with stirring at 80 degrees C overnight to obtain dark blue suspension. The obtained dark blue suspension is cooled at room temperature, and then mixed with 50 ml acetic acid with stirring for 12 hours. The obtained product is filtered, washed with water, ethanol and chloroform, and vacuum dried at 100 degrees C to obtain the porous silicone organic framework material.

A high carbon and high pollution traditional internal combustion engine is converted into rich carbon hydrogen internal combustion engine. Emulsifying diesel fuel is injected into hydrocarbon. The combustion chamber is provided with first and second catalyst coating groups. The first catalyst coating group is uniformly mixed with iron, titanium transition metal valence oxide and tungsten atom and metal oxide composite rare earth superfine powder of a composite metal coating layer. The second catalyst coating group is mixed with tungsten trioxide and aluminum oxide, mixing nano titanium dioxide earth powder composite coating lanthanide rare earth element and transition metal composite oxide.
   USE - Flameless combustion hydrogen internal combustion engine for a car, a ship, a farm machinery and generator set (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The engine converts chemical energy into mechanical work energy so as to reduce discharge pollution of the engine, improve energy utilization efficiency and avoid environment pollution in an effective manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a flameless combustion hydrogen internal combustion engine.

PN CN108875823-A; CN108875823-B
TI Method for combining evidence based on new evidence metrics, involves adjusting basic probability assignment vector according to determined weight coefficient, and combining DS evidence theory to get more reasonable conclusion.
AU YANG X
   LIAN W
   LI M
   LI J
   CHEN X
   LI L
AE LI L (LILL-Individual)
GA 201895863W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves deploying a sensor having sensor group with of various types of sensors is installed in a corner of the room, and various types of sensors include temperature sensors, humidity sensors, Smoke concentration sensor, carbon dioxide concentration sensor, and infrared sensor. A new evidence conflict metric Pcor is established. The evidence is determined based on the data collected by the sensor, and the conflict between the evidences is analyzed based on the evidence. A support matrix is constructed according to the evidence conflict standard Pcor value, and trust is established according to the support matrix. The weight coefficient of evidence is finally established using the degree of trust. The basic probability assignment vector is adjusted according to the determined weight coefficient, and finally the DS evidence theory is combined to get a more reasonable conclusion.
   USE - Method for combining evidence based on new evidence metrics.
   ADVANTAGE - The defects brought by the high conflict evidence to the DS evidence theory can be effectively solved. The evidence is reasonably distributed, such that the fusion result is more accurate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for combining evidence based on new evidence metrics. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

TI Mouse oocytes in-vitro maturation culture solution comprises follicle stimulating hormone, human chorionic gonadotropin, E2, sodium pyruvate, and FMS M199 medium.
AU ZHANG J
   JING N
   GUO C
   ZHANG Z
   ZHU K
   XU Q
   TANG G
AE UNIV HENAN ANIMAL HUSBANDRY & ECONOMY (UAHE-C)
GA 201891886X
AB    NOVELTY - A mouse oocytes in-vitro maturation culture solution comprises 0.5-2 IU/mL follicle stimulating hormone (FSH), 0.5-2 IU/mL human chorionic gonadotropin (HCG), 0.5-2 mu g/mL E2, 0.02-0.08 mg/mL sodium pyruvate, and 10 vol.% FMS M199 medium.
   USE - Mouse oocytes in-vitro maturation culture solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The solution explores the effects of sodium pyruvate and three hormones comprising FSH, HCG and E2 in in-vitro maturation of mouse oocytes, and has average first polar body 1 rate of 75.8% number of mortality rate of 11.37%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for in-vitro maturation of mouse oocytes comprises selecting healthy non-pregnant female mouse with body weight of 20-25 g, subcutaneously injecting 10 IU pregnant mare serum gonadotropin into abdominal cavity, dislocating neck after 45-48 hours, collecting ovaries, placing on Petri dish containing in-vitro working solution, puncturing follicles on surface of ovary to release oocytes, picking out germinal vesicle stage oocytes with regular shape, uniform cytoplasm and normal color, washing with in-vitro maturation culture solution for 2-3 times, adding 2 times its droplets of in-vitro mature culture solution, and incubating to 37 degrees C for 14 hours through 5% carbon dioxide incubator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The collecting of ovaries is done by soaking dead mouse in 0.5 wt.% alcohol for 0.5-1 minute, gently scraping fur of mouse using tweezers, putting mouse into tray, tilting upwards, opening belly, gently clamping fallopian tube with tweezers, separating ovary using scissors, cutting fallopian tube at the front end, placing to phosphate buffered saline to wash away blood and lipid droplets, placing to 60 mm culture dish at 37 degrees C. The in-vitro maturation culture solution is added with glycerol, and filtered through 0.22 mm disposable needle filter. The carbon dioxide incubator's distilled water is changed once a week.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The mouse oocytes in-vitro maturation culture solution comprises 1 IU/mL FSH, 1 IU/mL HCG, 1 mu g/mL E2, 0.04 mg/mL sodium pyruvate, and 10 vol.% FMS M199 medium. The in-vitro working solution is Gibco M199 medium number 12340-030.

TI Processing refractory biodegradable organic waste            water, comprises e.g. injecting organic wastewater to            be processed into reactor filled with catalyst            particles and separating catalyst particles in reactor            effluent by membrane separation.
AU LI H
   JING Q
AE UNIV TSINGHUA SHENZHEN GRADUATE SCHOOL (THIG-C)
GA 2018885834
AB    NOVELTY - Processing refractory biodegradable organic                waste water, comprises injecting organic wastewater                to be processed into a reactor filled with catalyst                particles, so that the organic matter in the                organic waste water under the action of the                catalyst particles are oxidized by oxygen to form                inorganic substance, passing the effluent from the                reactor into the separator, separating the catalyst                particles in the reactor effluent by membrane                separation or magnetic field separation and                returned to the reactor, where the effluent of the                separator is the final effluent.
   USE - The method is useful for processing refractory                biodegradable organic waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require the use of a                precious metal catalyst; utilizes low-cost                transition metals as catalyst materials; reduces                catalyst cost and promotes strong activity and                stability of the catalyst which is not easy to be                poisoned; requires only low temperature and normal                pressure reaction conditions which eliminates the                need for a pressure vessel; has easy process; is                economical; and can degrade the biodegradable                organic matter into inorganic substances e.g.                carbon-dioxide, nitrogen and water under low                temperature and normal pressure conditions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the device utilized in the above mentioned                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active component                  of the catalyst particles is metal spinel of                  nickel, cobalt, manganese or iron. The catalyst                  particles are nickel cobaltite spinel particles.                  Preferred Method: The reaction temperature is                  60-90 degrees C, pressure is normal pressure, pH                  is neutral or alkaline and aerated oxygen flow                  rate is 0.02-0.25 l/minute when the organic                  wastewater is treated in the reactor.

GA 201879582J
AB    NOVELTY - The method for calcining alumina clinker using industrial waste, involves preparing raw materials by mixing 5-25 wt.% aluminum ash, 5-25 wt.% coal gangue or fly ash, 45-65 wt.% carbide slag or limestone and 0-5 wt.% iron raw material, placing prepared raw materials in dry decomposition kiln, and calcining at a certain calcination temperature to form qualified alumina clinker. The saturation coefficient of limestone is 0.8 and 1.2.
   USE - Method for calcining alumina clinker using industrial waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables calcination of alumina clinker at wide calcination temperature by environmentally-friendly process, achieves large material, realizes waste utilization rate, and effectively removes impurities in extracted alumina.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for extracting alumina by using industrial waste, which involves grinding alumina clinker by wet grinding, performing alkali process to obtain solution containing sodium aluminate and sodium metaaluminate, passing solution through carbon dioxide gas, performing carbonation decomposition, filtering, removing impurities to obtain aluminum hydroxide precipitate, taking out aluminum hydroxide precipitate and subjecting to pyrolysis to obtain finished alumina product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron raw material is iron oxide and ferric oxide. Aluminum in aluminum clinker is composed of mineral composition of calcium aluminate, calcium laurate heptahydrate and tetra-calcium aluminoferrite. The alkaline solution is treated with sodium carbonate solution. Preferred Conditions: The calcining temperature is 1200-1400 degrees C. The calcination time is 20-40 minutes. The pyrolysis temperature is 1000-1500 degrees C.

AU PANG Y
   ZHENG W
