   WU M
   QIN K
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 2018712943
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid based copolyester foaming material comprises (i) placing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based copolyester plastic material in mold, pressing it into sheet-shaped preform, and quenching sheet-shaped preform, (ii) placing pre-formed product after quenching treatment and first blowing agent in closed container, where first blowing agent is not in contact with preform, and first blowing agent is chloroform, acetone, diethyl ether, pentane and/or hexafluoroisopropanol, (iii) further introducing second blowing agent into closed container, and maintaining temperature in closed container at 40-65 degrees C, causing the first blowing agent to change from liquid state to gaseous state, maintaining pressure in closed container to 2-5 MPa, and pressure holding time of 5-10 hours, where second blowing agent is carbon dioxide and nitrogen, and (iv) discharging gas in closed container, taking out preform and placing it in foaming medium for foaming, and then cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid based copolyester foaming material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid based copolyester foaming material obtained by above method, where 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based copolyester foaming material has cell size of 5-50 mu m and swelling ratio of 2-15 times.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid based copolyester plastic material is polyethylene terephthalate-2,5-furandicarboxylate, poly(terephthalic acid-2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-1,3-propanediol ester, polybutylene terephthalate-2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-1,4-butanediol ester, poly(terephthalic acid-2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-1,6-hexanediol ester and/or poly(terephthalic acid-2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-1,8-octanediol ester. In step (iv), foaming medium is dimethyl silicone oil, glycerol and/or motor oil. In step (ii), ratio between preform and first blowing agent is 50:1-5 g/ml. In step (i), water content of 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based copolyester plastic is less than or equal to 0.05 wt.%. In step (i), preform has thickness of less than or equal to 2 mm. In step (i), thin plate-shaped preform is in molten state, and quenching treatment time is 5-10 minutes. Preferred Method: In step (iv), gas in the closed container is removed within 30 seconds. In step (iv), foaming temperature is 100-140 degrees C, and foaming time is 10-20 seconds.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the granule comprising (i) cleaning the herba blumeae ripariae, airing, crushing into crude powder, then introducing air at density of 1.29 kg/m3, pressurizing to 5-10 MP under the condition of normal temperature, swelling for 4-6 minutes, instantaneously releasing pressure, replacing the gas with the density of 1.98 kg/m3 carbon dioxide, pressurizing to 6-12 MP under the condition of normal temperature, swelling for 4-6 minutes, and instantaneously releasing pressure to obtain material A, (ii) mixing the material A and mixed solvent, adding specific amount of zinc sulfate, exposing to high pressure pulse electromagnetic field treatment for 6-12 minutes under normal temperature conditions to obtain herba blumeae ripariae extract, controlling pulse electromagnetic field intensity at 15-20 kv/cm, and the frequency is 10-20 Hz, and (iii) filtering the herba blumeae ripariae extract, combining the filtrate, concentrating the filtrate to 60 degrees C to thick paste with the relative density of 1.36-1.38, adding filling agent and specific amount of flavoring, uniformly mixing, granulating, drying and sieving to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The granule comprises 1000 pts. wt. herba blumeae ripariae, 150 pts. wt. filling agent, and flavoring agent. The filling agent is mixture of dextrin, soluble starch, microcrystalline cellulose, pre-gelatinized starch, alpha -lactose and mannitol. The filling agent is alpha -lactose and microcrystalline cellulose in weight ratio of 1:3.5-4.5, preferably 1:4.25. The flavoring agent is sucrose, xylitol, sorbitol, maltitol or mannitol. The mixed solvent is composed of purified water and acetic acid in volume ratio of 1:0.08-0.1. The material-liquid ratio of the material A and the mixed solvent is 1:20-35 g/ml. The zinc sulfate is added in an amount of 0.1-0.4% wt./wt. based on the weight of the material A.
   ACTIVITY - Antianemic.

PN CN108212204-A; CN108212204-B
TI Catalyst for directly preparing high carbon olefins by microwave-assisted methane, comprises a nickel-molybdenum composite catalyst supported by zeolite Socony Mobil-5 molecular sieve, and is eco-friendly and cost-effective.
AU LU Y
   LI K
   YANG T
   CHEN Y
   FENG W
   LI H
AE UNIV ANHUI SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY (UYLG-C)
   UNIV ANHUI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYLG-C)
GA 201853514W
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises a nickel-molybdenum composite catalyst supported by zeolite Socony Mobil-5molecular sieve, abbreviated as NiOx-MoOy/ZSM-5 composite catalyst, where x=0-1, y is 0-3, in the NiOx-MoOy/ZSM-5 composite catalyst. The mass content of Ni metal atom is 0-15%, and the mass content of molybdenum metal atom is 0-15%.
   USE - Catalyst for directly preparing high carbon olefins by microwave-assisted methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is eco-friendly and cost-effective, and has high selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing catalyst, which involves:
   (A) dissolving hexahydrate nickel nitrate, ammonium heptamolybdate tetrahydrate and pore-forming agent in 5-10 times de-ionized water;
   (B) stirring and dispersing it uniformly;
   (C) obtaining the emitting hole of the metal salt aqueous solution;
   (D) putting zeolite Socony Mobil-5molecular sieve in metal salt aqueous solution at 80-90 degrees C for 16-24 hours;
   (E) dehydrating for 8-10h at 90 degrees C;
   (F) drying at 500-550 degrees C for 4-8 hours;
   (G) to remove water, oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen dioxide; and
   (H) carrying out hydrogenation reduction at 600-700 degrees C for 1-2 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The ZSM-5 molecular sieve has a silica-alumina ratio of 20 to 100, a specific surface area of 340 m2/g, a relative crystallinity of 95%, and a sodium oxide content of less than 0.1%. The hydrogen flow rate is 6-120 ml/min.The mass ratio of hexahydrate nickel nitrate, ammonium heptamolybdate tetrahydrate and pore with nickel, molybdenum , pore-forming agent is 1:0.1-1.0:1.0 -2.0. The microwave assistant heating and induction is carried out in a microwave reactor, wavelength is 0.1-100 cm, frequency is 300 ghz-300 mhz, power is 300-3000 W.The gas total pressure of hydrogen and methane is 0.1 to 1.0mpa.The reaction temperature is 80-250 degrees C. The volume ratio of hydrogen and methane is 3-5:1. Preferred Process: The methane is first subjected to catalytic dehydrogenation coupling of nickel powder and then catalyzed by nickel-molybdenum under conditions of microwave-assisted heating and induction, and excess hydrogen. The oligomerized product is purified by rectification and subjected to a polycondensation reaction under the catalysis of nickel and molybdenum to further synthesize a high carbon olefin liquid fuel, which is a dimer C8 and a trimer C12 liquid fuel.

TI Nano-nickel catalyst useful for hydrogenation method of carbon oxides and producing methane, ethane or propane comprises metallic nickel body and microstructures connected to the metallic nickel body on surface of metallic nickel body.
AU LAN P
   HUANG C
   HOU Y
   WANG C
   CHEN C
   LA B
   WANG Z
   CHEN Z
   LAN P W
   HUANG C W
   HOU Y W
   WANG C C
   CHEN C Y
   LAN B
   HUANG S
AE UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG (UNCK-C)
   UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG (UNCK-C)
   UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG (UNCK-C)
   UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG (UNCK-C)
   UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG (UNCK-C)
   UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG (UNCK-C)
GA 201849873J
AB    NOVELTY - Nano-nickel catalyst comprises: metallic nickel body; and many microstructures connected to the metallic nickel body on at least one surface of the metallic nickel body where the microstructures are sharp, and have a length-diameter ratio of 2-5.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for hydrogenation method of carbon oxides (claimed) and converting carbon dioxide-containing gas, such as exhaust gas or flue gas discharged from a factory, into an economically valuable substance and producing carbon monoxide, methane, ethane or propane.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst utilizes the microstructures that increase specific surface area of the catalyst, and maintain a specific distance between each other. The catalyst achieves fairly good methane conversion (hydrogenation of carbon oxides) at atmospheric pressure, and avoids the need to react at a relative high temperature thus save energy, and reduces the requirement of the reaction conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for hydrogenation method of carbon oxides, comprising performing a first hydrogenation reaction on carbon oxides and hydrogen under a first catalyst bed to form a first gas mixture, where the first catalyst bed comprises the nano-nickel catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The microstructures contain metallic nickel. The microstructures are made of metallic nickel. The metallic nickel body is spherical. The metallic nickel body is solid. The metallic nickel body is hollow. The metallic nickel body is porous, and has an adsorption pore volume of 0.0024-0.0062 cm3/g. The nano-nickel catalyst has a specific surface area of 1.5-2 m2/g. Preferred Method: The first hydrogenation reaction is carried out at a temperature T1 of 180-250 degrees C, and a flow rate of the carbon oxides is 2-7.2 ml/min, flow rate of the hydrogen is 30-33 ml/min. The hydrogenation method further comprises performing a second hydrogenation reaction on the first gas mixture and hydrogen under second catalyst bed to form a low-carbon hydrocarbons mixture stream, where the second catalyst bed comprises the nano-nickel catalyst according. The second hydrogenation reaction is carried out at 600-800 degrees C. The method further comprises heating the first catalyst bed and the second catalyst bed in hydrogen to 250 degrees C to perform a reduction reaction on the first catalyst bed and the second catalyst bed before the step hydrogenation. The first hydrogenation reaction step comprises mixing an inert gas, the carbon oxides, and the hydrogen to form a reaction stream, and the first gas mixture comprises methane and carbon monoxide, and the low-carbon hydrocarbons mixture stream is methane, ethane and/or propane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN MX2016016082-A1
TI Air distribution system for microclimatization of greenhouses, comprises a cooling-air heating machine that is provided inside a duct, and fan that is provided with a micro tunnel, which is divided inside by a transparent plastic film.
AU CHACON R B
   CAMARA ARANDA V L
AE CENT INVESTIGACION EN MATERIALES AVANZAD (INVE-Non-standard)
GA 201883430B
AB    NOVELTY - The air distribution system comprises a cooling-air heating machine that is provided inside a duct. A fan is provided with a micro tunnel, which is horizontally divided inside by a transparent plastic film that provides a supply conduit on the upper unit. A return duct is provided on the lower unit of the micro tunnel. The gases, such as oxygen and carbon dioxide are added to the air mixture through an injection system, which is provided with multiple translucent plastic sheet carriers that are fed by a liquefied gas tank. The gasses and compounds that are added to the air mixture, are separated. The cooling and heating apparatus provides air at a certain temperature. The fan is located at an end of the distribution duct. A discharge hatch is provided with a feed gate. The longitudinal section of the micro tunnels allows to observe two sections, which are separated horizontally by a translucent plastic wall.
   USE - Air distribution system for microclimatization of greenhouses.
   ADVANTAGE - The consumption of energy is reduced by reducing the space to the exterior of the air distribution system.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves alternately operating an activated carbon scrubber apparatus in an adsorption configuration and a regeneration configuration over a set of cycles (708), where each cycle comprises a single period in the adsorption configuration and a single period in the regeneration configuration and the scrubber apparatus is operated at a cycle frequency of between 4 and 30 cycles per hour.    USE - Method for operating an activated carbon scrubber apparatus of a controlled atmosphere system i.e. transport refrigeration system (all claimed), in a shipping container, a refrigerated lorry and a sea-going container, for removing carbon dioxide from a controlled environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables operating the scrubber apparatus in the adsorption configuration and the regeneration configuration during operation in the carbon dioxide reduction mode in an effective manner. The method allows a controller to control air movers, so that a volumetric flow rate of gas through the sorbent bed is higher during operation in regeneration configuration and during operation of the apparatus in an efficient manner, thus improving efficiency of carbon dioxide removal from the controlled environment in a cost-effective manner, and hence reducing a level of the activated carbon in the controlled atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (a) Scrubber apparatus for removing carbon dioxide from a controlled environment;
   (b) Controlled atmosphere system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for operating an activated carbon scrubber apparatus of a controlled atmosphere system.
   Operating controller of scrubber apparatus in standby mode (702)
   Determining requirement that carbon dioxide level in controlled environment is to be reduced (704)
   Instructing controller of scrubber apparatus to operate in carbon dioxide reduction mode until carbon dioxide level is reduced to target (706)
   A alternately operating scrubber apparatus in adsorption configuration (708)
   Controlling heater to heat sorbent bed in regeneration configuration (710)

TI Method for removing carbon dioxide removal, and optionally sulphide from produced gas in recovery processes using produced water, involves separating cooled mixture into carbonated water stream and hydrocarbon streams.
AU HARANDI M N
   YAZDI A Z

AE EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
   IMPERIAL OIL RESOURCES LTD (IMOI-C)
GA 2020434357
AB    NOVELTY - The method (100) involves performing a heavy oil in situ (HOIS) recovery process on a heavy oil to yield water, heavy oil, fines, and produced gas (102). The produced gas is provided with light hydrocarbons, and carbon dioxide. The produced gas is separated from the water, the heavy oil, and the fines. The produced gas is compressed (104) to yield a compressed produced gas (106). The compressed produced gas is mixed (110) with an absorption water (108) to yield a mixture (112). The mixture is cooled (114) to yield a cooled mixture (116). The cooled mixture is separated (118) into a carbonated water stream (120) having a portion of the carbon dioxide dissolved in the absorption water at a higher concentration than in the absorption water and the hydrocarbon streams (122).
   USE - Method for removing carbon dioxide removal, and optionally sulphide from produced gas in HOIS recovery processes using produced water.
   ADVANTAGE - The HOIS recovery processes contains separation systems for separating water from the fluids produced from the HOIS recovery process which is utilized as the water effluent stream in the processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for removing carbon dioxide removal, and optionally sulphide from produced gas in HOIS recovery processes using produced water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a method for removing carbon dioxide removal, and optionally sulphide from produced gas in HOIS recovery processes using produced water.
   Method for removing carbon dioxide removal, and optionally sulphide from produced gas in HOIS recovery processes using produced water (100)
   Produced gas (102)
   Step for compressing the produced gas to yield a compressed produced gas (104)
   Compressed produced gas (106)
   Absorption water (108)
   Step for mixing compressed produced gas with an absorption water to yield a mixture (110)
   Mixture (112)
   Step for cooling the mixture to yield a cooled mixture (114)
   Cooled mixture (116)
   Step for separating the cooled mixture into a carbonated water stream having a portion of the carbon dioxide dissolved in the absorption water at a higher concentration than in the absorption water and the hydrocarbon streams (118)
   Carbonated water stream (120)
   Hydrocarbon stream (122)

PN KR2071415-B1; WO2020111678-A1; US2021283307-A1; CN113365672-A
TI Method for extraction of extracellular matrix that is utilized as biomaterial for tissue regeneration, involves washing adipose tissue, maintaining tissue followed by removing and degreasing cells in tissue and recovering substrate.
AU SHIN Y W
   KIM H S
   KIM K
   NO S
   SEOL Y
   SHIN J H
   SHIN Y
   SHIN J
   KIM H
   XUE Y
AE DOF INC (DOFD-Non-standard)
   DOF INC (DOFD-Non-standard)
GA 2020102173
AB    NOVELTY - An extracellular matrix extraction method involves washing the adipose tissue obtained during the liposuction procedure with deionized water, allowing to stand for 15-20 minutes to remove blood from the adipose tissue, washing, treating the tissue by grinding for 6-12 minutes with an ultrasonic wave of 400-500 W, and preparing and maintaining the adipose tissue at 2-5 degrees C using a cooler during the ultrasonic treatment. The prepared adipose tissue is centrifuged to remove water and lipids followed by arranging a carbon dioxide cylinder, arranging a co-solvent chamber on one side of the carbon dioxide cylinder, and pressurizing carbon dioxide discharged from the carbon dioxide cylinder to produce supercritical fluid, where pretreated adipose tissue is disposed while the solvent is preheated through a preheater, removing and degreasing cells in the adipose tissue by adding to reactor, discharging the solvent to one side of the reactor, and recovering substrate remaining in the reactor.
   USE - Method for extraction of extracellular matrix that is utilized as a biomaterial for tissue regeneration.
   ADVANTAGE - The matrix has increased biocompatibility by performing decellularization and degreasing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An extracellular matrix extraction method involves washing the adipose tissue obtained during the liposuction procedure with deionized water, allowing to stand for 15-20 minutes to remove blood from the adipose tissue, washing, treating the tissue by grinding for 6-12 minutes with an ultrasonic wave of 400-500 W, and preparing and maintaining the adipose tissue at 2-5 degrees C using a cooler during the ultrasonic treatment. The prepared adipose tissue is centrifuged to remove water and lipids followed by arranging a carbon dioxide cylinder, arranging a co-solvent chamber on one side of the carbon dioxide cylinder, and pressurizing carbon dioxide discharged from the carbon dioxide cylinder to produce a supercritical fluid, where pretreated adipose tissue is disposed while the solvent is preheated through a preheater, removing and degreasing cells in the adipose tissue by adding to the reactor, discharging the solvent to one side of the reactor, and recovering the extracellular substrate remaining in the reactor, preventing recovered extracellular substrate from contamination and maintaining protein content.

GA 2018579796
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a bed of particles located in a first chamber. Multiple conduits positioned inside the first chamber are provided. A process stream is passed (602) includes carbon dioxide and carrier gas via the bed of particles located in the first chamber. A portion of the solid particles of bed is fluidized. The carrier gas is cooled (604). The portion of solid particles is sent to the heat exchanger located outside of the first chamber. The portion of the solid particles are sub-cooled (610) in the heat exchanger to a temperature that lowers solid carbon dioxide to a lower temperature. The portion of the solid particles are re-injected (612) in the heat exchanger into the bed of particles of the first chamber so as to de-sublimate a second portion of carbon dioxide located in the first chamber. The gaseous carbon dioxide and carrier gas selected from the group consisting of nitrogen (N2), methane (CH4), syngas, hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), argon and oxygen.
   USE - Method for separating condensable vapors from gases to form solid.
   ADVANTAGE - The additional cooling of process stream at some point in system is provided to achieve the desired low temperature for condensing the vapors. The designs can be used to facilitate installation, cleaning and repair. By using alternating compressors and heat exchangers in series can improve the efficiency of reducing the temperature and increasing the pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for separating condensable vapors from gases to form a solid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for separating condensable vapors.
   Step for passing a process stream that includes carbon dioxide and carrier gas through the bed of particles located in the first chamber (602)
   Step for cooling the carrier gas (604)
   Step for sorting the gas and carbon dioxide (608)
   Step for sub-cooling the portion of the solid particles in the heat exchanger to a temperature that lowers solid carbon dioxide to a lower temperature (610)
   Step for re-injecting substantially all of the portion of the solid particles in the heat exchanger into the bed of particles of the first chamber (612)

TI Carbon-fixing slag foam concrete wall material            comprises slag powder, fly ash, ultrafine powder,            bulking agent, fiber, humectant, stimulator, water            reducing agent, polymer emulsion, water repellent,            carbon dioxide foaming stabilizer and water.
AU WANG L
   SHANG H
   ZHANG Y
   JIN C
AE YITIANLAN SHANDONG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (YITI-Non-standard)
GA 2022A8101F
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon-fixing slag foam concrete wall material                comprises 39.5-53.4 wt.% slag powder, 2.6-3.6 wt.%                fly ash, 1.8-2.4 wt.% ultrafine powder, 0.4-0.59                wt.% bulking agent, 0.12-0.47 wt.% fiber,                0.007-0.009 wt.% humectant, 20.4-28.3 wt.%                stimulator, 0.22-0.3 wt.% water reducing agent,                0.44-0.47 wt.% polymer emulsion, 0.4-0.59 wt.%                water repellent, 3.7-17.4 wt.% carbon dioxide                foaming stabilizer and 10.4-14.2 wt.% water. The                carbon dioxide foaming stabilizer and carbon                dioxide gas are used in a foam foaming machine to                obtain carbon dioxide foam by a compressed gas                method.
   USE - Used as carbon-fixing slag foam concrete wall                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid carbon slag foam concrete wall                material has high strength, good size stability,                low water absorption, durable, reduces the heat                conducting coefficient, and can be shoulder organic                heat preservation material, at the same time,                finding a path for the captured carbon dioxide. It                completely replaces the use of cement gel material                so as to reduce the carbon emission, to realize the                national double-carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                preparation method of the solid carbon slag foam                concrete wall material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  foaming and stabilizing agent is a solution                  composed of surfactant, foam stabilizing agent,                  thickening film-forming agent and water. The                  surfactant is sodium lauryl sulfate, sodium                  lauryl alcohol ether sulfate and fluorocarbon                  surfactant, foam stabilizer is modified silicone                  resin polyether emulsion, the thickening                  film-forming agent is a mixture of xanthan gum,                  konjac micropowder, hypromellose and benzyl                  alcohol. The expansion agent is calcium                  sulfoaluminate-calcium oxide expansion agent and                  the fiber is polypropylene fiber or basalt fiber.                  The stimulator is an alkaline solution prepared                  from sodium sodium silicate and sodium hydroxide,                  the water reducing agent is a polycarboxylate                  water reducing agent, the polymer emulsion is                  silicone-acrylic emulsion, pure acrylic emulsion                  or styrene-acrylic emulsion and the waterproofing                  agent is potassium methylsiliconate, sodium                  methylsiliconate or ammonium stearate                  emulsion.

AU KIM T
AE NONGUP HOLYGROUND CO LTD (NONG-Non-standard)
GA 202283867N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a boiler for receiving plant fuel. The boiler discharges the smoke containing carbon dioxide during combustion. A blowing fan forcibly distributes and supplies the smoke delivered from the boiler to multiple supply pipes. An underground supply pipe supplies the carbon dioxide by a timer. A burner ignites and burns the vegetable fuel supplied only for a time determined by the timer. The blowing fan operates the smoke carbon dioxide. A plant is grown in a plant cultivation container which is installed in a greenhouse.
   USE - Plant root smoke supply system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system generates the smoke generated when burning vegetable fuel in the boiler transferred to the underground supply pipe embedded in the soil of the greenhouse through the supply pipe, so that the plant is discharged through multiple outlet holes by supplying carbon dioxide by smoke to the roots, thus renewing power to grow crops. The system directly supplies nutrients to the root to strengthen the roots and sterilize sterilization effect that kills bacteria in soil, thus eliminating pests e.g. anthrax and helping growth of stem and root plants e.g. ginseng, potato, sweet potato and taro.

   ZHAO Z
AE GANSU JIU STEEL GROUP HONGXING IRON & (JGHX-C)
GA 2021939973
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing free oxygen content of converter                final point molten steel involves on the basis of                bottom blowing air brick on the bottom of the                converter, adding 2 or more bottom blowing spray                gun, using nitrogen, argon gas, carbon dioxide as                carrier, and spraying lime powder into the                converter furnace from the spray gun. The blowing                process is divided into three stages of pre-period,                middle-stage and later stage, where the early gas                supply is 60% oxygen, 40% nitrogen, the medium-term                gas supply is 40% oxygen and 60% carbon dioxide,                the gas supply in the later period is 70% carbon                dioxide and 30% oxygen, of which carbon dioxide gas                is blown in the middle and late stages.
   USE - Method for reducing free oxygen content of                molten steel in final point of converter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving the air supply                strength of the bottom blowing powder, effectively                improving the decarburization speed by the strong                bottom blowing system, reducing the oxygen                consumption, and the end point excess oxygen                content, increasing the stirring intensity of the                molten pool due to the use of carbon dioxide,                reducing the free oxygen content of the final point                molten steel, reducing the end point free oxygen                and the total amount of impurities in the molten                steel from the source, and improving the quality                and reducing the alloy cost.

AB    NOVELTY - Recycling alkali by waste alkali liquor                comprises adding aluminum hydroxide seed crystals                into the waste alkali liquor, decomposing the                sodium tetrahydroxy sodium aluminate dissolved in                the waste alkali liquid into sodium hydroxide and                aluminum hydroxide under the action of the added                aluminum hydroxide seed crystal, and separating out                aluminum hydroxide; introducing the waste gas                containing carbon dioxide, and converting aluminate                into aluminum hydroxide, where the reactions                involved includes when carbon dioxide is excessive,                reacting aluminum oxide, carbon dioxide and water                to form a solution of aluminum hydroxide and                bicarbonate ion; when carbon dioxide is less,                reacting aluminum oxide, carbon dioxide and water                to form a solution of aluminum hydroxide and                carbonate ion; reacting aluminum hydroxide and                sodium aluminum hydroxide to produce aluminum                hydroxide and sodium hydroxide; and reacting carbon                dioxide, sodium carbonate and water to produce                sodium bicarbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling alkali by                waste alkali liquor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation; is easy to                control; prepares clear alkali liquor and aluminum                hydroxide with high purity and recovery rate, has                low processing cost, and avoids the waste liquid                pollution, promotes the precipitation reaction                balance forward movement, accelerates the process                of the precipitation reaction, promote the                efficiency of the treatment; is energy saving,                utilizes less energy consumption, reduces the loss                of the container device, and prolongs the service                life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, adding 1-20                  g aluminum hydroxide seed crystals per liter of                  waste alkali liquor. The method for preparing                  alkali liquor includes (1) adding aluminum                  hydroxide to the waste alkali liquid until the                  slurry is pasty; (2) introducing waste gas into                  the pasty slurry; (3) allowing to stand; (4)                  adding water to the pasty slurry; (5) stirring;                  (6) allowing to stand; (7) separating the mixed                  liquid obtained in the step (6) to obtain clear                  alkali liquor and aluminum hydroxide. The waste                  alkali liquor is any one of the simmering liquid                  of the aluminum profile extrusion die, the                  diluent of the simmering liquid, the aluminum ash                  liquid dissolved with sodium hydroxide, or the                  alkaline etching liquid pre-oxidation treatment.                  In the step (3), the time for allowing to stand                  is not less than 1 day. In the step (5), the                  stirring time is not less than 30 minutes. The                  temperature of the paste slurry when the exhaust                  gas is passed through is 20-100 degrees C.                  Preferred Components: In the step (4), the volume                  ratio of the added volume of water to the waste                  alkali liquor is 1:1-2:1. In the step (7), the                  separation method is one of centrifuge                  centrifugal separation or filter press slag                  separation. The exhaust gas is tail gas produced                  by the combustion of natural gas.

PT P
PN WO2018219441-A1; TW201903241-A; CN110662863-A; EP3631072-A1; EP3631072-B1
TI Device for removing liquid carbon dioxide from            fabric in pressurized treatment chamber, has thermal            removal unit that is configured to heat fabric and make            residual portion of carbon dioxide still trapped in            fibers evaporate.
AU SCORTEGAGNA B
AE LAFER SPA (LAFE-Non-standard)
   LAFER SPA (LAFE-Non-standard)
GA 2018968753
AB    NOVELTY - The removal device (29) has a mechanical                removal unit (27) that is provided to exert a                mechanical action on a fabric (12) in order to                remove a portion of a carbon dioxide trapped in the                fibers. A thermal removal unit (28) is configured                to heat the fabric and make the residual portion of                the carbon dioxide still trapped in the fibers                evaporate. The thermal removal unit is arranged                directly downstream of mechanical removal                unit.
   USE - Device for fabric washing apparatus (claimed)                for removing liquid carbon dioxide from fabric,                used in pressurized treatment chamber, in                industrial field of manufacture of textile                products, and in textile industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanical removal action and the thermal                removal action are performed in temporal sequence                one immediately after the other, so as to optimize                the overall efficiency of the removal and to obtain                a dry fabric before winding it onto the                winding/unwinding roller at exit. The roll of                fabric exiting from the treatment chamber is used                directly, or subjected to further work processes,                without needing to provide intermediate treatment                steps to remove the residual carbon dioxide, with a                consequent reduction in overall production times                and costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for washing fabrics.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                washing apparatus.
   Washing apparatus (10)
   Fabric (12)
   Mechanical removal unit (27)
   Thermal removal unit (28)
   Removal device (29)
DC F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).); F07 (Other textile applications - include mechanical treatment of fabrics (D06C, F, G, H, J, L, M).)

AU ZHANG R
   YU Y
   ZHENG J
AE TIANJIN YONGLI EDIBLE ADDITIVES CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201856941R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a high-purity liquid carbon dioxide purifying device, comprising a feeding tank, feed tank is provided with a guide plate, the guide plate is provided with multiple parallel guide groove, the guide groove runs through the feed tank of the bottom part and the top part, the bottom of the feed tank is provided with an air inlet, the top part of the feed tank is provided with a cleaning liquid supply pipe, supply tank is provided with a cleaning liquid supply tube is connected with the liquid tray, a hollow liquid supply plate, a liquid supply plate is connected with multiple spray head, the end part of the spray towards the right above of the guide plate. spray the liquid sprayed out with the end of the guide groove of the carbon dioxide gas is mixed in the sulfur dioxide absorbing and dissolving carbon dioxide gas, entering into the purifying tank further from the top of the supply tank by carbon dioxide of preliminary cleaning, the purifying device is mainly convenient for cleaning liquid and carbon dioxide gas through the structure design of the guide groove and the head fully contact, improving the cleaning effect, guarantees the efficiency of purification.

PT P
PN EP4050126-A1
TI Method for electrolytic processing of gaseous raw            feed stream containing carbon dioxide, involves            subjecting raw feed stream with first water vapor            content to humidification to obtain feed stream, and            subjecting feed stream to electrolysis to obtain crude            product stream containing carbon monoxide.
AU SCHOEDEL N
   PESCHEL A
   HENTSCHEL B
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 2022B0046D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves subjecting the raw feed                stream (1) with first water vapor content to                humidification (B) to obtain a feed stream (3). The                feed stream is subjected to electrolysis to obtain                a crude product stream (4) containing carbon                monoxide. The first water vapor content during                humidification is raised to second water vapor                content in a controlled manner in order to obtain                the feed stream with a water vapor saturation of                more than 80%, 90%, 95% or 99%. The electrolysis is                carried out using gas diffusion electrode and/or a                membrane electrode assembly. The humidification is                carried out using gas-water contact vessel. The                humidification is carried out partially using                membrane humidifier. The raw product stream and/or                an aqueous catholyte and/or anolyte is depleted in                water (9) in the membrane humidifier. The                vaporization enthalpy is applied by heat                integration, by removing heat downstream of the                electrolysis.
   USE - Method for electrolytic processing of gaseous                raw feed stream containing carbon dioxide in                chemical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane or gas diffusion electrode used                in the electrolysis is prevented from drying out                more effectively in an economical manner. The                method for electrolytic processing of gaseous raw                feed stream is particularly easy to implement and                is associated with relatively low investment and                operating costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The moistening is carried out partially by                admixing superheated steam. The feed stream                upstream of the electrolysis is cooled to the                electrolysis temperature level, with condensing                water being separated off, when the feed stream is                humidified at temperature level which is above an                electrolysis temperature level. The raw product                stream is partially subjected to a separation to                obtain a product stream (6) containing carbon                monoxide and a recyclate stream (7) containing                carbon dioxide, and wherein the recyclate stream is                partially is used to form the raw feed stream                and/or the feed stream. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a plant for electrolytic processing of                raw feed stream, which includes the humidifier                which is set up to control a first water vapor                content in the raw feed stream to increase the                second water vapor content in order to obtain the                feed stream which is more than 80%, 90%, 95% or 99%                saturated with water vapor. An electrolyser (E) is                set up to convert the feed stream to electrolyze                partially to obtain a crude product stream                containing carbon monoxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system for electrolytic processing of gaseous raw                feed stream.
   1Raw feed stream
   3Feed stream
   4Crude product stream
   6Product stream
   7Recyclate stream
   9Water
   BHumidification
   EElectrolyser
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25)); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC E11-N; E31-N05B1; J03-B01; J03-B02; J03-B03; J03-B05; Q75-F; X25-R01A; X25-R01D

PT P
PN CN114573859-A
TI Preparing thermoplastic expanded microsphere            useful in e.g. sound absorption, heat insulation by            mixing e.g. styrene monomer, oil-based initiator and            oil-soluble reagent, mixing e.g. polymerization            inhibitor, antistatic agent, mixing oil phase and            aqueous phase, homogenizing and reacting emulsion.
AU WU H
   GUO X
   DONG X
   LIU H
   BAI J
   XUE C
   MA Y
   LI Z
   ZHOU K
   LI Y
   WANG W
AE SHANXI JUYI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 202278276D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing thermoplastic expanded microsphere                comprises (S1) mixing the styrene monomer,                oil-based initiator, foaming agent, cross-linking                agent and oil-soluble reagent uniformly to obtain                an oil phase, where the oil-soluble reagent is an                oil-soluble oxidant or the oil-soluble reducing                agent, the foaming agent is a mixture of petroleum                ether and carbon dioxide with a mass ratio of                3.5-4.5:5.5-6.5, the cross-linking agent is                diethylene glycol divinyl ether, triethylene glycol                divinyl ether, triacyl formal, triallyl                isocyanurate and/or divinyl ether, (S2) mixing                deionized water, stabilizer, co-stabilizer,                polymerization inhibitor, antistatic agent and an                aqueous-soluble reagent uniformly to obtain an                aqueous- phase, (S3) mixing the oil phase and the                aqueous phase, homogenizing at high speed to obtain                an emulsion, and (S4) stirring and reacting the                emulsion at 80-100&#8451; for 150-450 minutes                to obtain the thermoplastic expanded                microsphere.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                thermoplastic expanded microsphere, which is useful                in sound absorption, heat insulation, heat                preservation and light weight and                biomedicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses styrene as plastic monomer,                capable of inhibiting foaming agent diffusion after                being heated, ensuring the microsphere is not                shrunk and not deformed and uses the presence of                petroleum ether to reduce the leakage of carbon                dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing thermoplastic expanded microsphere                comprises (S1) mixing the styrene monomer,                oil-based initiator, foaming agent, cross-linking                agent and oil-soluble reagent uniformly to obtain                an oil phase, where the oil-soluble reagent is an                oil-soluble oxidant or the oil-soluble reducing                agent, the foaming agent is a mixture of petroleum                ether and carbon dioxide with a mass ratio of                3.5-4.5:5.5-6.5, the cross-linking agent is                diethylene glycol divinyl ether, triethylene glycol                divinyl ether, triacyl formal, triallyl                isocyanurate and/or divinyl ether, (S2) mixing                deionized water, stabilizer, co-stabilizer,                polymerization inhibitor, antistatic agent and an                aqueous-soluble reagent uniformly to obtain an                aqueous-phase, (S3) mixing the oil phase and the                aqueous phase, homogenizing at high speed to obtain                an emulsion, where the aqueous-soluble reagent is a                aqueous-soluble reducing agent, when the                oil-soluble reagent is an oil-soluble oxidant and                the water-soluble reagent is a water-soluble                oxidant, when the oil-soluble reagent is an                oil-soluble reducing agent and (S4) stirring and                reacting the emulsion at 80-100&#8451; for                150-450 minutes to obtain the thermoplastic                expanded microsphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The boiling range of the                  petroleum ether is 90-120&#8451;. In the step                  (4): using gradient heating method, reacting at                  80-90&#8451; for 75-225 minutes and heating                  at 85-100&#8451; at a speed of                  2.5-3.5&#8451;/minutes and reacting for                  75-225 minutes and the stirring speed is                  3000-5000 revolutions/minute. In the step (3),                  the high speed homogenizing condition is: the                  stirring speed is 4000-5000 revolutions/minute,                  the time is 5-10 minutes and the temperature is                  50-60&#8451;.Preferred Components: The foaming agent is a                  mixture of petroleum ether and carbon dioxide                  with a mass ratio of 4:6. The oil initiator is                  methanol solution of azodiisobutyronitrile with                  concentration of 4-6 wt.%. The oil-soluble                  oxidant is diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate,                  peroxydicarbonate, di-sec-butyl                  peroxydicarbonate, dicarbonate peroxide and/or                  di-sec-butyl peroxydicarbonate. The oil-soluble                  reducing agent is mercaptopropionic acid,                  triethylamine, N,N-dimethyl-p-toluidine and/or                  tris(dimethylaminomethyl)phenol (DMP 30). The                  aqueous-soluble oxidant is nitrate, peroxyacid                  salt and/or alkali metal peroxide. The aqueous                  -soluble reducing agent is citric acid, sodium                  citrate and/or tea polyphenol. The stabilizer is                  calcium hydroxide, magnesium nitrate, calcium                  chloride, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide                  and/or magnesium sulfate. The auxiliary                  stabilizer is sorbitan fatty acid ester, gelatin,                  polyvinyl alcohol acetic acid, lauramide, stearic                  acid amide, lauryl trimethyl ammonium chloride,                  sodium oleate, ricinoleic acid potassium, lauryl                  dimethyl amine oxide and/or glycerol fatty acid                  ester. The polymerization inhibitor is citric                  acid and/or sodium sulfide; the antistatic agent                  is methacrylic acid. The mass of the styrene                  monomer is 100%, the adding amount of the oil                  phase initiator is 0.6-0.8%. The adding amount of                  the foaming agent is 70-75%. The adding amount of                  the cross-linking agent is 10-14%. The adding                  amount of the oil-soluble oxidant is 3-5%. The                  adding amount of the oil-soluble reducing agent                  is 3-5%. The adding amount of the stabilizer is                  18-22%. The adding amount of the auxiliary                  stabilizer is 7-9%. The adding amount of the                  polymerization inhibitor is 13-17%. The adding                  amount of the antistatic agent is 4-6%. The                  adding amount of the deionized water is 400-500%.                  The molar ratio of the aqueous-soluble reducing                  agent to the oil-soluble oxidant is 0.6:0.9-1.1.                  The molar ratio of the aqueous-soluble oxidant to                  the oil-soluble reducing agent is 0.75:0.9-1.1.                  The droplet diameter of the emulsion is 1-110                  &#956;m.
DC A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); ; E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)

GA 202231684C
AB    NOVELTY - Fermentation system comprises dark-light combined hydrogen production device (1), hydrogen quality detection system (2), hydrogen purification device (3), gas collecting tank (4), ultra-micro bubble nano-device (5), methane production device (6) and methane purification device (7). The dark-light combined hydrogen production device is an integrated reactor, which is provided with a dark fermentation hydrogen production unit and a photosynthetic fermentation hydrogen production unit. The dark-light combined hydrogen production device is connected with the hydrogen quality detection system through a metal hose. The hydrogen quality detection system is connected to the hydrogen purification device through a metal hose. The hydrogen quality detection system and the hydrogen purification device are connected by metal hoses.
   USE - The fermentation system is useful for co-production of biohydrogen alkane.
   ADVANTAGE - The fermentation system has negative carbon emission, reduces cost of hydrogen purification, increases amount of methane produced and recycles carbon dioxide captured in purification process of high-quality hydrogen and methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for fermentation method for co-production of biohydrogen alkane, involves (a) taking mixed bacteria comprising dark fermentation hydrogen-producing bacteria and photosynthetic hydrogen-producing bacteria, and fermentation substrate i.e. enzymatic hydrolyzate of crop straw, (b) allowing the hydrogen quality detection system to send feedback information to the control system, where the control system turns on the gas flow pump in the direction of the hydrogen purification device, passes the gas into the hydrogen purification device through a metal hose for purification, detection and shunting, the separated pure hydrogen flows to the gas collecting tank through the metal hose under the action of the gas flow pump, and passing the separated carbon dioxide into the methane production device through the metal hose under the action of the gas pump and (c) setting the fermentation temperature of the methane production device at 35degrees Celsius, inoculating 20 volume/volume% methanogenic bacteria, allowing the gas generated from the methane production device to flow into the methane purification device through the metal hose pipeline under the action of the gas flow pump, where the purified methane enters the gas tank through the metal hose under the action of the gas flow pump, and returning the separated carbon dioxide to the methane production device through the metal hose under the action of the gas flow pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a fermentation system.
   Dark-light combined hydrogen production device (1)
   Hydrogen quality detection system (2)
   Hydrogen purification device (3)
   Gas collecting tank (4)
   Methane production device (6)
   Methane purification device (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred System: A 0.2 mum cellulose acetate membrane is arranged between the dark fermentation hydrogen production unit and the photosynthetic fermentation hydrogen production unit. The hydrogen quality detection system optionally includes an on-line gas analyzer. The hydrogen quality detection system is connected to the hydrogen purification device through a gas flow pump. The hydrogen purification device includes a solid/liquid impurity separator and a membrane separator. The gas separation membrane in the membrane separator of the hydrogen purification device is a graphene-like carbon nitrogen separation membrane of 0.51 nm. The separation membrane in the membrane separator of the methane purification device adopts a polyimide hollow fiber complex membrane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves setting the dark fermentation unit temperature at 35degrees Celsius and pH 5.5-6.5, where the inoculum size is 20 volume/volume% and setting the photosynthetic fermentation unit temperature at 35degrees Celsius, illumination at 3500 lx and pH at 6.5-7, where the inoculum size is 20 volume/volume%. In step (b), when the hydrogen quality detection system detects that the hydrogen concentration in the mixed gas is more than 50%, the hydrogen quality detection system feeds back the information to the control system, then the control system turns on the gas flow pump that flows to the hydrogen purification device, passes into the hydrogen purification device to separate pure hydrogen that comes out flows to the gas collecting tank through the metal hose under the action of the gas flow pump, and the separated carbon dioxide is passed into the methane production device through the metal hose under the action of the air pump and when the hydrogen quality detection system detects low-quality hydrogen, the hydrogen quality detection system feeds back the information to the control system, and the control system turns on the gas flow pump in the direction of the gas flow to the methane-producing device, and transports the gas to the ultra-fine bubble nano-device inside the methane-producing device.

PN CN112980445-A; CN112980445-B
TI Extracting phenolic antioxidant from Chinese prickly ash oil processing by-product by obtaining degreased Chinese prickly ash oil meal, supercritical carbon dioxide extraction using defatted Chinese prickly ash oil meal and freeze drying.
AU ZHAO Z
   ZHOU M
   LIU F
   WAN J
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2021718913
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting phenolic antioxidant from Chinese prickly ash oil processing by-product involves (1) performing material preparation by crushing Chinese prickly ash oil meal, sieving using a 40-mesh sieve, putting into a filter, adding an isohexane solution, degreasing for 8-12 hours in a fume hood at 60 degrees C, repeating to obtain degreased Chinese prickly ash oil meal and placing in a dark place, (2) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and (3) performing sample collection by centrifuging the mixed solution, recovering the upper oil phase, diluting the crude product with a 60% ethanol solution, passing through a 0.45 mu m organic filter membrane, repeating for 3 times, placing the obtained filtrate in a vacuum concentration at 40 degrees C to 10 ml and performing freeze drying to obtain a phenolic antioxidant and performing vacuum drying preservation at -20 degrees C, where the phenolic antioxidant has a phenolic content of 7.855-7.926 mg/g.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting phenolic antioxidant from Chinese prickly ash oil processing by-product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes Chinese prickly ash oil processing by-product fully, reduces the environmental pollution problem caused by the oil meal, energy consumption, cost, and pollution and obtains phenolic antioxidants with high purity and high activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting phenolic antioxidant from Chinese prickly ash oil processing by-product involves (1) performing material preparation by crushing Chinese prickly ash oil meal, sieving using a 40-mesh sieve, putting into a filter, adding an isohexane solution, degreasing for 8-12 hours in a fume hood at 60 degrees C, repeating for 3 times to obtain degreased Chinese prickly ash oil meal and placing in a dark place, (2) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of a phenolic antioxidant by loading defatted Chinese prickly ash oil meal into a 1 l extraction tank, pumping 300-500 ml entrainer ethanol for supercritical extraction and collecting a crude product from a separation kettle and (3) performing sample collection by centrifuging the mixed solution, recovering the upper oil phase, diluting the crude product with a 60% ethanol solution, passing through a 0.45 mu m organic filter membrane, repeating for 3 times, placing the obtained filtrate in a vacuum concentration at 40 degrees C to 10 ml and performing freeze drying to obtain a phenolic antioxidant and performing vacuum drying preservation at -20 degrees C, where the phenolic antioxidant has a phenolic content of 7.855-7.926 mg/g. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the phenolic antioxidant prepared by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (1) involves adopting the volume of the filter of 0.2 m3, where the Chinese prickly ash oil meal and the isohexane solution are mixed according to a material-to-liquid ratio of 1:4 (m/v). The step (2) involves adding 100-400 g defatted Chinese prickly ash oil meal. The step (2) involves adopting ethanol in a mass fraction of 60-80%, where the temperature of the separation kettle is 35-45 degrees C and the pressure of the separation kettle is 5-15 Mpa. The step (2) involves adopting the supercritical extraction temperature of 40-60 degrees C, where the extraction pressure is 20-40 Mpa and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 30-50 L/hour and the extraction time is 2-3 hours. The step (3) involves adopting the centrifugal speed of 8000 rpm, where the centrifugal time is 15 minutes. Preferred Components: The Chinese prickly ash oil meal is any one of red Chinese prickly ash oil meal, green Chinese prickly ash oil meal and rattan Chinese prickly ash oil meal. Preferred Composition: The crude product and 60% ethanol in step (3) are in a volume ratio of 1:(10-25).

PN US2021045808-A1; WO2021034645-A1; US10945790-B2; CA3150854-A1; WO2021034645-A8; EP4013330-A1; KR2022128981-A; JP2022546246-W
TI System for contracting area of soft tissue, has            controller that is fixedly adapted to control carbon            dioxide laser source and beam guidance system to            fixedly achieve therapeutically effective contraction            of soft tissue area at specific rate.
AU KERBAGE C
   BADREDDINE A
   COUITT S
   MILLER S
   JELISAVCIC N
   STEPHEN Q
   SABINENAD J
AE CONVERGENT DENTAL INC (CONV-Non-standard)
   CONVERGENT DENTAL INC (CONV-Non-standard)
   CONVERGENT DENTAL INC (CONV-Non-standard)
GA 202122053M
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide laser source                that is used for generating multiple laser pulses                of a laser beam having a wavelength in a range from                9 mum to 11 mum. A beam guidance system is used for                directing the laser pulses to respective tissue                locations within the soft tissue area. A controller                is adapted to control the carbon dioxide laser                source and the beam guidance system to achieve a                therapeutically effective contraction of the soft                tissue area at a rate of 1 cm2 in no more than 25                seconds. The therapeutically effective contraction                has 10 percent of a full contraction of the soft                tissue area. The beam guidance system prevents                damage to the soft tissue. The damage to the soft                tissue is selected from a group consisting of                ablation, burning, and charring.
   USE - System for contracting area of soft tissue for                treating snoring.
   ADVANTAGE - The laser radiation provides enough energy to                increase the biomechanical stiffness of the soft                tissue such that it is fully contracted without                further damage or charring of the tissue. The                optical cartridge enables the use of high power                lasers in a non-ablative procedures by modifying                the beam size using optical lenses mounted in the                optical cartridge hence optimizing the fluence                and/or energy density and reducing the time of                procedure. The laser therapy include increased                collagen contraction and regeneration that leads to                increased tissue stiffness with minimal pain or                sensation. The handpiece accomplishes treatment                efficiently and with a fast treatment time and                without need for anesthesia. The laser is pulsed                and scanned in a certain pattern to allow optimal                collagen contraction of the oral mucosa and other                soft tissue and minimal heat accumulation. The                laser treatment device tolerates a longer working                range to enable an operator to move his/her hand,                while still treating the treatment area. The                measured increase in stiffness over the 2 week to 6                week range confirms that the soft tissue                biomechanical changes persist into the remodeling                phase, which is based on the duration of the                fibrosis or otherwise altered collagen structure                according to the wound healing process. The                increased stiffness is maintained for significantly                longer periods without significant deterioration.                The laser parameters is designed to have an optimal                outcome of efficiency to remove diseased tissue or                bone without damaging the material itself.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for contracting an area of soft tissue.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side cross-sectional                schematic view of a straight handpiece including a                tongue depressor.
   Handpiece (1)
   Integrated tongue depressor (10)

PN CN112028760-A
TI Combined preparation of cyclobutylformaldehyde for            chemical raw material production by putting            cyclobutane-1,1-dicarboxylic acid into distillation            device, heating, decarboxylating and discharging carbon            dioxide, heating and distilling.
AU HE X
AE SHANGHAI SCOCHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C6170X
AB    NOVELTY - Combined preparation of cyclobutylformaldehyde                includes preparation of cyclobutylformic acid by                putting cyclobutane-1,1-dicarboxylic acid into a                distillation device for heating, decarboxylating                and discharging carbon dioxide, heating and                distilling, collecting 180-200 degrees C fraction                to obtain crude cyclobutylformic acid, and                re-distilling to obtain finished product of                cyclobutylformic acid; and preparation of                cyclobutylmethanol by adding anhydrous                tetrahydrofuran (THF) and sodium borohydride                (NaBH4) into a reaction bottle installed with a                stirrer and thermometer, stirring and adding                iodine, dissolving in THF, cooling, slowly dripping                cyclobutylformic acid in THF, stirring and                reacting, refluxing and reacting, evaporating part                of THF, cooling in ice water bath, dripping                hydrochloric acid, controlling the internal                temperature at 1-5 degrees C, extracting by                methylene dichloride, combining the organic layers,                water washing, and drying by anhydrous magnesium                sulfate.
   USE - The method is for combined preparation of                cyclobutylformaldehyde for chemical raw material                production (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need to supply purchased                cyclobutylformic acid and cyclobutylmethanol,                reduces the cost of obtaining cyclobutylformic acid                and cyclobutylmethanol, so as to reduce the                preparation cost of cyclobutylformaldehyde, and                enables to continuously supply cyclobutylformic                acid and cyclobutylmethanol so as to increase the                working efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Combined preparation of cyclobutylformaldehyde                comprises preparation of cyclobutylformic acid by                putting cyclobutane-1,1-dicarboxylic acid into a                distillation device for heating, decarboxylating at                150-180 degrees C and discharging carbon dioxide,                heating and distilling, collecting 180-200 degrees                C fraction to obtain crude cyclobutylformic acid,                and re-distilling to obtain finished product of                cyclobutylformic acid; preparation of                cyclobutylmethanol by adding 200-300 ml anhydrous                THF and 7.6-50 g NaBH4 into a reaction bottle                installed with a stirrer and thermometer, stirring                and adding 22.8-25 g iodine, dissolving in 200-300                ml THF, cooling to 0-6 degrees C, slowly dripping                10-20 g cyclobutylformic acid in 30-40 ml THF,                stirring and reacting for 10-12 hours at 20-25                degrees C, refluxing and reacting for 4-5 hours,                evaporating part of THF, cooling in ice water bath,                dripping 60-80 ml of 3-5 mol/L hydrochloric acid,                controlling the internal temperature at 1-5 degrees                C, extracting by methylene dichloride, combining                the organic layers, water washing, drying by                anhydrous magnesium sulfate, distilling, collecting                the fraction at 140-142 degrees C, and obtaining                7-8 g colorless liquid cyclobutylmethanol; and                preparing cyclobutylformaldehyde by adding                colorless liquid cyclobutylmethanol into the                reaction kettle, using inert gas as carrier, under                the action of the catalyst, introducing oxygen into                the reaction kettle, heating, carrying out                oxidation and deoxidation reaction of                cyclobutylmethanol at 600-700 degrees C, and                generating cyclobutylformaldehyde.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The inert gas is nitrogen,                  argon gas or helium gas. The catalyst is platinum                  tin (PtSn)/silicon dioxide (SiO2) catalyst, which                  is prepared by co-precipitation method or dipping                  method, where the content of platinum in                  PtSn/SiO2 catalyst is 0.5-5 wt.%, and the content                  of tin is 0.5-5 wt.%.

PN CN111388389-A
TI Preparing Dendrobium officinale concentrate            comprises e.g. carrying out fumigation and            sterilization of high-quality Dendrobium officinale,            adding cellulase and pectinase, heating, concentrating,            adding activated carbon and evaporating.
AU YU Q
   QIN L
   XU D
   ZHAO Y
   ZHANG W
   LIANG Z
AE ANHUI MULONGSHAN DENDROBIUM OFFICINALE (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020668274
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Dendrobium officinale concentrate                comprises e.g. washing high-quality Dendrobium                officinale, placing in high-temperature steam                boiler, carrying out fumigation and sterilization,                and cutting, adding deionized water, placing in a                grinder to prepare slurry, adding cellulase and                pectinase into the above-mentioned slurry, and                placing in a water bath for 15-20 hours for                enzymatic hydrolysis, adding into ultrasonic                homogenizer and performing ultrasonic                homogenization treatment, adding into a                high-pressure reaction kettle, passing in                supercritical carbon dioxide, heating, filtering,                adding into deionized water, heating, filtering,                repeating the above operation for 2-3 times, and                combining the filtrate, concentrating the                above-mentioned filtrate to 1/3 of its original                volume in a vacuum environment, adding activated                carbon and stirring, then placing in a high-speed                centrifuge for centrifugation, and collecting the                supernatant, and evaporating.
   USE - The Dendrobium officinale concentrate is                useful for facial masks (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: overcomes the shortcomings of the                prior art; adopts enzymatic hydrolysis,                homogenization, supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction, filtration and centrifugation steps;                increases the extraction rate of Dendrobium                officinale; increases the content of active                ingredients in the extract; and reduces the loss of                active ingredients at the same time; and improves                economic benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Dendrobium officinale concentrate                comprises (i) taking high-quality Dendrobium                officinale, washing, placing Dendrobium officinale                in a high-temperature steam boiler and carrying out                fumigation and sterilization using high-temperature                hot steam, and then cutting into sections, (ii)                adding deionized water into the above-mentioned                Dendrobium officinale, and placing in a grinder to                prepare slurry, (iii) adding cellulase and                pectinase into the above-mentioned slurry, and                placing in a water bath for 15-20 hours for                enzymatic hydrolysis to obtain enzymatic hydrolyzed                slurry, (iv) adding the above-mentioned enzymatic                hydrolysis slurry into ultrasonic homogenizer and                performing ultrasonic homogenization treatment to                obtain homogeneous slurry, (v) adding the                above-mentioned homogeneous slurry into a                high-pressure reaction kettle, passing in                supercritical carbon dioxide, and then taking out                after keeping under heat preservation and                pressuring for 2-3 hours to obtain treated slurry,                (vi) heating the above-mentioned treated slurry to                60-70 degrees C, reacting for period of time after                keeping warm, filtering while hot, adding the                filter residue into deionized water and heating at                60-70 degrees C, filtering, repeating the above                operation for 2-3 times, and combining the                filtrate, (vii) concentrating the above-mentioned                filtrate to 1/3 of its original volume in a vacuum                environment, adding activated carbon and stirring,                then placing in a high-speed centrifuge for                centrifugation, and collecting the supernatant, and                (viii) evaporating the above clear liquid at low                temperature to 1/5-1/6 of the original volume to                obtain concentrated solution of Dendrobium                officinale.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  fumigation time of high temperature hot steam is                  10-15 minutes. In the step (ii), the liquid to                  material ratio between Dendrobium officinale with                  deionized water is 2:1. In the step (iii),                  utilizes 5 wt.% cellulase and 2 wt.% and                  pectinase of the original pulp mass, and the                  enzymolysis temperature is 30-35 degrees C. In                  the step (iv), the power of ultrasonic                  homogenization is 250-300 W, the frequency is 30                  KHz, the homogenization time is 5 minutes, and                  the homogenization temperature is 50-55 degrees                  C. In the step (v), the pressure of the                  supercritical carbon dioxide is 6-8 MPa, the                  temperature is 50-55 degrees C, and the volume of                  the supercritical carbon dioxide is twice the                  volume of the homogeneous slurry. In the step                  (viii), the low-temperature evaporation                  temperature is 40-45 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: In the step (vii), the addition of                  activated carbon is 2-4 wt.% of the volume of the                  concentrated filtrate, the centrifugal rotation                  speed is 8000 revolutions per minute, and the                  centrifugation time is 10 minutes.

PN CN109982521-A; CN109982521-B
TI Method for preparing 16-layer arbitrary            interconnected circuit board, involves browning upper            copper foil layer and lower copper foil layer of            printed circuit board, followed by chemical            micro-etching and improving plating uniformity.
AU LI H
   ZHANG Z
AE GUL JIANGSU ELECTRONICS TECHNOLOGY CO (GULJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019606998
AB    NOVELTY - A 16-layer arbitrary interconnected circuit                board preparing method involves browning an upper                copper foil layer and the lower copper foil layer                of the printed circuit board to form a browning                layer, placing the processed printed circuit board                on a carbon dioxide laser processing table,                removing the browning layer of the upper copper                foil layer and the lower copper foil layer,                utilizing an insoluble anode vertical continuous                plating equipment, utilizing a copper oxide powder                to provide copper ions, improving plating                uniformity and plating flatness, filling the laser                holes with copper, forming a line pattern on the                upper copper foil layer and the lower copper foil                layer, laminating a layer of prepreg and pressing,                and heating the prepreg, forming a 16-layer                arbitrary interconnected circuit board in order to                avoid the excessive curing of the previously                pressed material, deteriorating the reliability of                the material, and increasing the pressing pressure                with the number of presses.
   USE - Method for preparing 16-layer arbitrary                interconnected circuit board.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing 16-layer                arbitrary interconnected circuit board in simple                and easy manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A 16-layer arbitrary interconnected circuit                board preparing method involves browning an upper                copper foil layer and the lower copper foil layer                of the printed circuit board to form a browning                layer under the browning layer has a micro-etching                amount of 2.5-3.5 mu m, placing the processed                printed circuit board on a carbon dioxide laser                processing table for carbon dioxide laser drilling,                removing the browning layer of the upper copper                foil layer and the lower copper foil layer by                chemical micro-etching and de-gelatinized copper                under the chemical micro-etching micro-etching                amount of 0.8 mu m, utilizing an insoluble anode                vertical continuous plating equipment, utilizing a                copper oxide powder to provide copper ions,                improving plating uniformity and plating flatness,                filling the laser holes with copper, forming a line                pattern on the upper copper foil layer and the                lower copper foil layer by a laser direct imaging                process, laminating a layer of prepreg under the                upper copper foil layer and below the lower copper                foil layer and pressing with a hydraulic device,                and heating the prepreg during the pressing process                under the heating temperature up to 205 degrees C                and the heating rate of 3 degrees C/minute, forming                a 16-layer arbitrary interconnected circuit board                in order to avoid the excessive curing of the                previously pressed material, deteriorating the                reliability of the material, and increasing the                pressing pressure with the number of presses under                the pressure ranges from 320-380 psi.
DC V04 (Printed Circuits and Connectors)
