PN CN109485900-A
TI Preparing antibacterial deodorizing polyurethane elastomer for production of shoe midsole, involves mixing polyurethane, antibacterial agent, deodorant and additive, plasticizing, granulating, introducing carbon dioxide, cooling, molding.
AU ZHENG S
AE FUJIAN ANZHI EDUCATION SERVICES CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201927154T
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing antibacterial deodorizing polyurethane elastomer, involves pulverizing Chinese medicinal material, mixing obtained medicinal material powder and ethanol-water mixture, ultrasonically extracting, filtering, extracting, concentrating the filtrate, adding salt solution, ultrasonically mixing, leaving still, evaporating upper phase, pulverizing to obtain antibacterial agent, crushing the bamboo chips, mixing obtained bamboo powder and phosphoric acid solution, filtering, washing solid portion, drying, subjecting phosphoric acid-treated bamboo powder to low-temperature carbonization treatment to obtain deodorant, mixing thermoplastic polyurethane, antibacterial agent, deodorant and additive, plasticizing, cooling and granulating, placing obtained antibacterial deodorizing polyurethane elastomer material in high-pressure reactor, introducing carbon dioxide into reactor, leaving still, heating, foaming, cooling and molding.
   USE - Method for preparing antibacterial deodorizing polyurethane elastomer for production of shoe midsole.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of antibacterial deodorizing polyurethane elastomer with excellent mechanical properties, and antibacterial and deodorizing effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing antibacterial deodorizing polyurethane elastomer, involves (1) pulverizing Chinese medicinal material through a 60 mesh sieve to obtain a medicinal material powder, mixing medicinal material powder and ethanol-water mixture in a mass-to-volume ratio of 1:20-35, and ultrasonically extracting at power of 400-600 W and temperature of 35-60 degrees C for 25-35 minutes, filtering, extracting the obtained residue by ultrasonic extraction-filtration 2-3 times, combining the filtrates, concentrating the filtrate to 0.1-0.25 times the original volume, adding equal volume of salt solution, ultrasonically mixing, leaving still, separating upper phase by a separating funnel, evaporating upper phase to dryness under reduced pressure, pulverizing through a 60 mesh sieve to obtain antibacterial agent, (2) crushing the bamboo chips, passing through a 60 mesh sieve to obtain bamboo powder, mixing bamboo powder and 50 wt.% phosphoric acid solution in a mass-to-volume ratio of 1:4-8 at 45-60 degrees C, immersing for 1 hour, filtering the mixture, washing solid portion 2-3 times with water, drying, placing phosphoric acid-treated bamboo powder in a box-type electric resistance furnace, and subjecting to low-temperature carbonization treatment at 250-400 degrees C for 1-2 hours, crushing the bamboo and passing through a 60 mesh sieve to obtain deodorant, (3) mixing thermoplastic polyurethane, antibacterial agent, deodorant and additive, transferring the mixture to a twin-screw extruder, plasticizing, cooling and granulating after extrusion to obtain antibacterial deodorizing polyurethane elastomer material, (4) placing antibacterial deodorizing polyurethane elastomer material in a high-pressure reactor, introducing carbon dioxide into reactor by a booster pump, controlling the pressure at 0.3-0.6 MPa and temperature at 115-125 degrees C, leaving still for 3-5 hours, quickly relieving pressure, reducing the pressure to normal pressure, transferring the raw materials to the foaming equipment, heating, foaming, cooling and molding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The traditional Chinese medicine is selected from coptidis rhizoma, Houttuynia cordata and/or honeysuckle.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salt solution is selected from sodium chloride, potassium chloride, dipotassium phosphate, disodium phosphate or sodium sulfate. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the salt solution is 0.2-0.35 g/mL.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ethanol-water mixture has ethanol volume amount of 35-50 vol.%. Preferred Components: The additive is selected from glycerin or ethylene glycol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The step (3) comprises reacting 90-97 parts mass thermoplastic polyurethane, 2-3 parts mass antibacterial agent, 2-3 parts mass deodorant and 5-10 parts mass additive. Preferred Conditions: The twin-screw extruder has screw temperature of 220-245 degrees C, head temperature of 220-230 degrees C and host speed of 200-250 rpm. The pressure relief speed during the rapid pressure relief is 1-3 MPa/minute.

TI Device for co-production of industrial grade, food grade and high purity liquid carbon dioxide by multi-tower rectification, comprises raw material gas compressor, pre-processing section, cold energy recycling and cooling section.
AU HE D
AE HE D (HEDD-Individual)
GA 2018A5215H
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises raw material gas compressor (1), pre-processing section (2), cold energy recycling and cooling section (3), T1 rectifying tower reboiler (4), T1 rectifying tower (5), T1 rectification tower tower top condenser, T1 rectifying tower reflux pump, T2 rectifying tower re-boiler, T2 rectifying tower, T2 rectification tower top condenser, T2 rectifying tower reflux pump, T3 rectifying tower re-boiler, T3 rectifying tower, T3 rectification tower top condenser, where pre-processing section of raw material gas in carbon dioxide high boiling point impurity removing to T1 rectifying tower, bottom product impurity control index, generally comprises desulfurizing, dehydrating, removing water and heavy hydrocarbon, single orcombined set of alcoholic aldehyde ester and so on,connected with the cold energy recycling andcooling section outlet of feeding port of T1 rectifying tower. PN CN108633644-A
TI Method for planting strawberry, involves selecting plot, followed by removing garden weeds, plowing, turning, spraying phoxim lime liquid, applying fertilizer of farmyard manure, selected strong seedling without disease and planting.
AU ZHANG W
AE DAOZHEN AUTONOMOUS COUNTY JIAHUI MOUNTAIN ECOLOGICAL CHARACT (DAOZ-Non-standard)
GA 201882939T
AB    NOVELTY - A strawberry planting method involves selecting plot as industrial pollution-free, sunshine, flat ground, convenient irrigation and drainage, soil fertile and loose, removing the garden weeds, plowing, turning the soil, spraying 50% phoxim lime liquid, re-plowing the garden, making soil furrow and applying fertilizer of farmyard manure as base fertilizer, urea, cake fertilized, compound fertilizer, calcium superphosphate and potassium chloride to each acre. The greenhouse is built, taken canopy rice, provided the rice with a temperature and humidity monitoring device, selected strong seedling without disease for planting, planted, kept the seedling furrow surface exposed root embedded loose earth layer, watered the soil root wet, buried the exposed root in the soil layer, planted the seedling, covered mulching film greenhouse, utilized carbon dioxide, kept the temperature at night of 12-15 degrees C, and followed by performing the steps given in the procedure.
   USE - Method for planting strawberry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables planting the strawberry with effectively ensuring better growth of strawberry and high yield, reducing the strawberry planting cost and increasing the strawberry planting benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A strawberry planting method involves selecting plot as industrial pollution-free, sunshine, flat ground, convenient irrigation and drainage, soil fertile and loose, removing the garden weeds, plowing depth of 8-10 cm, turning the soil, spraying 50% phoxim lime liquid for 1000 times for killing underground insects, re-plowing the garden after 8-10 days to incomplete depth of 20-30 cm, making soil furrow with depth of 25-30 cm and width of 55-60 cm and 30-35 cm and applying 4200-4600 kg fertilizer of farmyard manure as base fertilizer, 200-240 kg urea, 10-12 kg cake fertilized, 20-30 kg compound fertilizer, 8-10 kg calcium superphosphate and 15-20 kg potassium chloride to each acre. The greenhouse is built as 14-18 long and the height of 1.8-2 m, taken canopy rice with width of 3-3.5 mm, provided the rice with a temperature and humidity monitoring device, ventilation device and dehumidifying device, automatic spraying device, a carbon dioxide generator, selected strong seedling without disease for planting, planted with row spacing of 28-32 cm and planting distance of 15-20 cm, kept the seedling furrow surface exposed root embedded loose earth layer, watered the soil root wet, buried the exposed root in the soil layer, planted the seedling for 3-5 days, covered mulching film greenhouse in daytime temperature of 23-25 degrees C and the humidity of 62-68%, utilized carbon dioxide with concentration of 300-350 mg/liter, kept the temperature at night of 12-15 degrees C, and followed by performing the steps given in the procedure.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); C01 (Organophosphorus; organometallic - i.e. compounds containing other than H, C, N, O, S and halogen.); P11 (Soil working, planting (A01B, C).); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC C04-A08; C04-A09; C04-B04B; C05-A01; C05-B01N; C05-B02A4; C05-C07; C10-A13C; C14-B04B; C14-S18; C14-T; P11-A02; P11-B01; P11-E01; P11-E03; P13-A01C; P13-A01E; P13-A08; P13-B01; P13-E01; P13-E03

PT P
PN CN108374024-A; CN108374024-B
TI Co-producing ethanol, fructose and multiple by-products with sweet Sorghum stalks and corn comprises preprocessing sweet Sorghum, preprocessing corn, preparing fruit syrup and fructose, preparing fuel ethanol, and preparing silage.
AU LIN S
   DENG C
   JIANG R
   ZU X
AE BEIHAI MINGSHI TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (BEIH-Non-standard)
   BEIHAI MINGSHI TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (BEIH-Non-standard)
GA 201866108R
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing ethanol, fructose and multiple by-products with sweet Sorghum stalks and corn comprises e.g. (1) crushing the fresh sweet Sorghum stalk and juicing, obtaining Sorghum juice and Sorghum residue; (2) crushing dry corn grain to obtain corn powder; (3) using the sweet Sorghum juice for preparing fructose and fructose, the fructose comprises crystalline fructose and medicinal fructose, the byproduct fructose syrup can be directly used as product, (4) mixing corn powder and Sorghum juice, glucose syrup according to material liquid ratio of 1: 1.2-2.2, after pasting, liquefying, saccharifying and fermenting and dehydrating technique, preparing into fuel ethanol, glucose syrup generated by fructose production line without returning to isomerization step, but as fuel ethanol produced substrate to improve liquor component, and (5) when preparing fuel ethanol, generated wine lees after quality inspection.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing ethanol, fructose and multiple by-products with sweet Sorghum stalks and corn.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can utilizes sweet Sorghum stalk and corn as raw materials, can ensure a more stable source of raw materials for each product, can realize co-production of various products including ethanol, fructose, liquefied natural gas, and feed, etc., reduce energy consumption of the final product, and low comprehensive cost of raw materials, has high utilization rate, energy saving and environmental protection are of great significance for making full use of and renovating the saline-alkali wasteland in the Northeast.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-producing ethanol, fructose and multiple by-products with sweet Sorghum stalks and corn comprises (1) crushing the fresh sweet Sorghum stalk and juicing, obtaining Sorghum juice and Sorghum residue; (2) crushing dry corn grain to obtain corn powder; (3) using the sweet Sorghum juice for preparing fructose and fructose, the fructose comprises crystalline fructose and medicinal fructose, the byproduct fructose syrup can be directly used as product, (4) mixing corn powder and Sorghum juice, glucose syrup according to material liquid ratio of 1:1.2-2.2 to obtain the mixed material, after pasting, liquefying, saccharifying and fermenting and dehydrating technique, preparing into fuel ethanol, glucose syrup generated by fructose production line without returning to isomerization step, but as fuel ethanol produced substrate to improve the liquor component, (5) when preparing fuel ethanol, generated wine lees after quality inspection, mixing qualified wine lees with corn residue for preparing distillers dried grains with solubles (DDGS) feed, and the wine lees of mycotoxin exceeding standard is used for preparing liquefied natural gas (LNG); (6) preparing silage, Sorghum residue by silage fermenting to prepare into silage; and (7) recycling carbon dioxide: discharged carbon dioxide gas when preparing fuel ethanol, processing and recycling into food-grade carbon dioxide product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method, pretreatment of sweet Sorghum stalk, and the preparation process comprises: crushing fresh and sweet Sorghum stalks with a sweet Sorghum juice extracting device, respectively obtaining Sorghum juice containing 18-25% sugar content and containing Sorghum slag with a water content of 50-65% and a sugar content of 4.5-6.5%. The preparation of fructose syrup comprises (a) first-grade decolorization and impurity removal: removing Sorghum juice by filtration system to remove 50-60% of water, after hydrolysis and neutralization, then entering to the ceramic membrane filtration system for decolorization and impurity removal; (b) secondary decolorization and impurity removal: entering the secondary activated carbon for decolorization and impurity removal, and then passing through the fine filter or sterilization filter; (c) sugar liquid refining: enter the anion and cation exchange system for adsorption refining, remove impurities in the sugar liquid; (d) syrup refining: entering the adsorption decolorization, deodorization system, deodorization and removing fine impurities; (e) adsorption separation: placing into the simulated moving bed chromatography separation system for adsorption separation to obtain a high-purity fructose solution containing not less than 96% fructose and a dry concentration of 22-32%,(f) vacuum concentration: entering the vacuum concentration system to obtain a high-purity fructose syrup with a dry basis concentration of 76-80%; and (g) blending: according to the market demand, the fructose syrup containing 78-95% of fructose is prepared to obtain the finished product of fructose syrup. The preparation of fructose comprises preparing crystalline fructose and medicinal fructose, and preparation process comprises (i) high-purity fructose syrup obtained from a fructose production line, metered into a vacuum concentration scouring system, and the sugar liquid is concentrated concentration to the dry basis is 87-89%; (ii) adding to the seed crystal, after smelting the solid crystal, enter the crystal assisting system to raise crystal, self-control cooling to help crystallize to obtain crystalline fructose cream; (iii) entering the centrifuge for high-speed solid honey screening to obtain the wet crystal of fructose with a water content of 0.8-1.5%; and (iv) purifying the crystal mother liquor obtained by centrifugation, refining and concentrating to obtain a finished fructose syrup; entering the fructose wet crystal to a vacuum drying system to remove excess water and is sent to the packaging workshop to complete, in the refining process to obtain the crystalline fructose product having a fructose purity of not less than 99.9%; and the medicinal fructose finished product is prepared in the GMP workshop by the above method. The process for preparing fuel ethanol comprises (1) gelatinization: mixing the corn flour with Sorghum juice and glucose syrup according to the ratio of material to liquid 1:1.2-2.2, and the corn starch is gelatinizing and changing at 60-72 degrees C to form a paste; (2) liquefaction: adding liquefaction enzyme to hydrolyze gelatinized starch into dextrin and oligosaccharide; (3) saccharification: adding acid to adjust pH to 4-4.5, adding saccharifying enzyme, and incubating at 55-60 degrees C for 25-60 minutes, get mashing; (4) fermentation: reducing the obtained mash to normal temperature and adjust the optimum pH of 3.5-4, adding yeast, uniformly mixing and carry out continuous concentrated fermentation; adding about 80-120 ml of yeast culture solution/kg mash, adjusting the temperature of the fermenter to 30-35 degrees C, controlling the number of yeast is 2.2-320 million/ml, fermentation time is 24-30 hours; fermented mature alcohol is not less than 15%; and (5) distillation dehydration: using differential pressure distillation to obtain a volume concentration of not less than 95.5% of half of the alcohol semi-finished product, and then dehydrated by molecular sieve to obtain fuel ethanol with a volume concentration of not less than 99.5% to obtain the distiller's grains. The preparation of DDGS and liquefied natural gas comprises using the distiller's grains with good quality inspection are made into DDGS feed through the processes of separation, drying, evaporation and packaging, and the distiller's waste liquid at the bottom of the turret is passed through the pressurized pump to enter the distiller's grains. the steamer, flashing to remove part of the moisture and heat, entering to the temporary storage tank, and then pumps it to the separation section, separated After the distiller's grains are, the filter residue and the clear liquid are obtained. The clear liquid is pumped to the evaporation system, and the filter residue is sent to the drying system for treatment, after the material is mixed, the package becomes DDGS feed; the quality test is unqualified distiller's grains with the mycotoxin exceeding the standard, which is processed into biogas, which contains 50-70% of methane, and then is liquefied through purification, dehydration and compression processed natural gas (LNG). The preparation of silage comprises using the by-product Sorghum slag obtained by the above-mentioned juice extraction process as a raw material, adding or not adding silage, compacting and sealing, and cultivating lactic acid bacteria in an anaerobic environment, starch and soluble sugar in the feed become lactic acid, which is silage. The process of carbon dioxide recovery comprises preparing carbon dioxide gas discharged from fuel ethanol, and performing foaming, and the carbon dioxide scrubber, recovering the entrained ethanol and entering the buffer tank, the carbon dioxide gas coming out of the buffer tank is compressed by the compressor, adsorbing and purifying in the adsorption tower, and the carbon dioxide gas after the adsorption and purification is liquefied into the plate liquefier, directly entering the distillation column, the light components are excluded from the tower, and the food grade carbon dioxide product is obtained from the reboiler, the liquid product is taken out at the bottom of the reboiler and directly sent to the finished tank for storage, bottling or loading.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)
MC D03-G04; D03-H01A; D05-A02; D05-A04; D05-B03; D05-C08; E07-A02H; E10-E04E2; E11-Q01C; E11-W; H06-B08; H06-P
IP A23K-030/18; C10L-003/08; C12F-003/02; C12P-019/12; C12P-007/10

PN CN108048269-A
TI Terminalia beer comprises malt, rice, residual sugar, hops, Terminalia extract, water, where beer has antibacterial, anti-viral, anti-oxidation, cardiac, detoxification, anti-tumor, antidiarrheal, antispasmodic and anti-AIDS effect.
AU ZHONG X
   CHEN Z
   SU S
   HUANG G
   FENG J
   GUO Y
AE UNIV FOSHAN (UYFS-C)
GA 2018409391
AB    NOVELTY - Terminalia beer comprises 20-45 pts. wt. malt, 10-20 pts. wt. rice, 2.5-3 pts. wt. residual sugar, 4-8 pts. wt. hops, 0.01- 0.05 Terminalia extract, 50-80 pts. wt. water.
   USE - Terminalia beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The Terminalia beer has antibacterial, anti-viral, anti-oxidation, cardiac, detoxification, anti-tumor, contraction of tracheal smooth muscle, antidiarrheal, antispasmodic and anti-AIDS, and has outstanding efficacy, great development prospects and markets.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing Terminalia beer, which involves:
   (A) crushing rice and putting into gelatinized water, adding high-temperature alpha -amylased, liquifying starch after temperature is raised for the first time when stirring;
   (B) raising temperature after the second heating to make the starch fully;
   (C) putting crushed malt into the saccharification water for heat preservation, left protein, and incorporating gelatinized mash into the saccharification pot until the saccharification is completed to obtain the mash;
   (D) using hot water is used top of the wort drain valve, so that the water is flooded through the filter trough sieve, pumping mash into the filter trough, filtering and returning to the wort for clearness and formal filtration;
   (E) keeping small amount of steam to keep warm;
   (F) increasing amount of steam when wort is completely filtered, and gradually warming up, adding hops three times, adding little hops during the initial boiling to control first wheat juice concentration, washing tank, controlling content of residual sugar, filtering, controlling concentration of the mixed wort;
   (G) adding second hop after the second boiling, adding third hop 10-20 minutes before the end of the third boiling, boiling the wort;
   (H) boiling and pumping wort into swirling sedimentation tank for standing;
   (I) cooling wort under oxygen and sterile air conditions;
   (J) adding yeast full cans, placing sediments after a period of time, cooling down into the temperature storage stage, performing double-acetyl reduction, first boost, pressure reduction and heat preservation after sealing, when diacetyl is reduced to 0.02-0.07mg/L, discharging yeast and cooling for the second time to obtain the fermentation broth;
   (K) dissolving Terminalia extract in edible alcohol, adding to the obtained broth, thoroughly stirring to dissolve;
   (L) pouring considered fermentation broth into a carbon dioxide reserve yeast addition tank, opening lid of the addition tank, pouring Terminalia extract, filling with carbon dioxide, adding tank with the filter and connecting filter channel, filtering, pressing carbon dioxide into the pipe, filtering together with the fermentation broth and entered into a sake tank filled with edible alcohol; and
   (M) sterilizing for microorganisms present in the liquor are sterilized to obtain Terminalia beer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The Terminalia extract is prepared bt extracting ripe fruit of Terminalia fruit by acetone, extracting by heating under reflux, discarding residue, obtaining filtrate, and collecting combined filtrate, adding ether to filtrate to remove impurities, discarding ether layer, collecting aqueous layer, extracting with ethyl acetate, collecting ethyl acetate layer, recovering ethyl acetate, and evaporating resulting powder to obtain Terminalia extract. Preferred Condition: The number of extractions is 1-5, the extracted temperature is 45-65 degrees C, and the collection is combined. The number of times is 2-5 times. The volume ratio of the filtrate and the ether is (1-3):(1-3), and the volume ratio of the ethyl acetate and the water layer is (1-3): (1-3), the number of times of the extraction is 4-6 times, and the evaporating temperature is 40-60 degrees C. The sieve has a pore size of 30-50 mesh, the first temperature increase rate is 0.5-2 degrees C/minutes, and the first temperature increase temperature is 80-95 degrees C. The second warming temperature is 90-110 degrees C and the second incubation time is 20-40 minutes. The mass ratio of malt and saccharification water is 1:(2-3), and the temperature of the heat preservation is 40-60 degrees C. The saccharification temperature is 65-80 degrees C, the incubation time is 50-70 minutes, and the temperature increase temperature is 70-80 degrees C. The temperature of the hot water is 70-90 degrees C, the thickness of the sieve tray is 5-20cm, and the rest time is 20-40 minutes. The temperature of the holding is 80-100 degrees C. The temperature of the rising temperature is 90-120 degrees C. The concentration of the first wort is 10-20 wt.%, the washing of grooves is performed for 1-5 times, and the residual sugar concentration is 0.5-3 wt.%. The filtration time is 2-5 hours, and the mixed wort concentration is 8.3-9.0 wt.%. The second hop quality is 40-60 wt.% of the first hop content. The third hop is added in an amount of 30-60 wt.% of the hop content for the first time. The boiling time is 80-100 minutes, and the total boiling time is 60-80 hours. The wort is cooled for 20-40 minutes, temperature for cooling is 6-8 degrees C, oxygen is supplied, and dissolved oxygen in sterile air is 7-11mg/L. The standing time in for fermentation is 1-3 hours, temperature of the first cooling is 5 1010 degrees C, the pressure of the first step-up is 0.5-1.5 mega Pascal, and the first cooling is performed at the rate of 0.2-0.5 degrees C/hours, the first incubation time is 18-24 hours, the second cooling rate is 0.1-0.4 degrees C/hour, and the second cooling temperature is 0.5-2 degrees C, the second incubation period is 5-12 days. The volume ratio of the Terminalia extract to the fermentation broth is 10-30 mg:1L. The volume ratio of the edible alcohol and the fermentation broth is 130-160 ml:1L, and the concentration of the edible alcohol is 80-95 wt.%. The volume of the fermentation broth is 1/4-1/2 of the yeast addition tank volume, and the time for charging CO2 is 15-30 minutes. The sterilization temperature is 55-65 degrees C, and the sterilization strength is 20-30 per unit.
   ACTIVITY - Antidiarrheic; Cytostatic; Anti-HIV.

PN CN109335665-A
TI Construction handling device for carrying a tubular article comprises chassis outer edge of chassis provided with hinge seat, guide column arranged vertically in middle of chassis, top end of guide strut provided with limit block.
AU SUN C
AE SUN C (SUNC-Individual)
GA 201918522L
AB    NOVELTY - Construction handling device for carrying a tubular article comprises a chassis. The outer edge of the chassis is provided with a hinge seat. A guide column is arranged vertically in the middle of the chassis. The top end of the guide strut is provided with a limit block, a sliding block is mounted on the sliding column, a cylindrical cavity in the vertical direction is arranged in the vertical direction of the slider. The side wall of the cylindrical cavity is provided with an upper guiding groove and a lower guiding groove. The upper guiding groove and the lower guiding groove have the same wave shape. The upper guiding groove and the lower guiding groove are spaced apart from each other and are arranged in a wrong position forming a wavy guiding channel. The lower guiding groove has four identical wave tips and four identical wave bottoms. The wave tip portion of the lower guide groove is not on the same vertical plane as the wave bottom portion of the upper guide groove.
   USE - Used as construction handling device for carrying a tubular article.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Construction handling device for carrying a tubular article comprises a chassis. The outer edge of the chassis is provided with a hinge seat. A guide column is arranged vertically in the middle of the chassis. The top end of the guide strut is provided with a limit block, a sliding block is mounted on the sliding column, a cylindrical cavity in the vertical direction is arranged in the vertical direction of the slider. The side wall of the cylindrical cavity is provided with an upper guiding groove and a lower guiding groove. The upper guiding groove and the lower guiding groove have the same wave shape. The upper guiding groove and the lower guiding groove are spaced apart from each other and are arranged in a wrong position forming a wavy guiding channel. The lower guiding groove has four identical wave tips and four identical wave bottoms. The wave tip portion of the lower guide groove is not on the same vertical plane as the wave bottom portion of the upper guide groove. A cylinder is slidably disposed in the cylindrical cavity of the slider. A guide column is symmetrically arranged on both sides of the cylindrical body. The guide post is received on the guiding channel. A first link is disposed below the cylinder. A traction block is disposed at a lower end portion of the first link. A traction plate is also fixedly disposed above the chassis. The middle portion of the traction plate is provided with a through groove. The first link passes through the through slot. The traction block is disposed below the through slot. The upward movement of the traction block can pass through the through slot or drive the traction plate to move up synchronously. A second link is hinged to a side of the slider. The outer end of the second link is hinged with a clamping rod. The clamping rod is L-shaped. The clamping rod includes a first strut and a second strut. The first strut is hinged to the second link. The second strut is hinged to the hinge seat of the chassis, The outer end of the second pole is provided with a clamping jaw. A lifting support is arranged above the slider. The outer side wall of the slider is coated with an anti-rust coating. The anti-rust coating comprises 10 pts. wt. chlorosulfonated polyethylene resin, 20 pts. wt. water-soluble epoxy resin, 20 pts. wt. styrene-acrylic emulsion, and 20 pts. wt. reinforcement fluororesin hydrophobic material, 3 parts curing agent, 10 parts filler, and 2 parts leveling agent. The filler is obtained by mixing aluminum tripolyphosphate, zinc phosphate and barium sulfate in a weight ratio of 5:5:5. The fluororesin-reinforced hydrophobic material is prepared by (i) mixing 2 pts. wt. n-butyl titanate, 10 pts. wt. absolute ethanol, 1 pts. wt. a molar solution of 10mol/l of nitric acid mixed to obtain solution A, (ii) mixing uniformly 5 pts. wt. montmorillonite, 10 pts. wt. anhydrous ethanol and placing into 30 pts. wt. solution A, stirring, allowing to stand, drying, calcining at 500 degrees C for 4 hours, (iii) uniformly mixing 10 pts. wt. hexafluorobutyl methacrylate, 10 pts. wt. butyl acrylate, 10 pts. wt. methyl methacrylate, 2 pts. wt. modified montmorillonite, 2 pts. wt. silane coupling agent KH792, 2 pts. wt. potassium persulfate, placing into the first processing equipment, and the first processing equipment is filled with carbon dioxide for gas replacement, and placing first processing equipment in a water bath, and continuously charging the carbon dioxide into the first processing equipment to the first internal pressure of the processing equipment is 10 MPa, and maintaining for 10 hours, the carbon dioxide is discharged to obtain the material B, and (iv) placing the material B into the second processing equipment containing xylene, raising the temperature of the second processing equipment to 60 degrees C for 5 minutes, cooling, and taking out the material C, and washing with anhydrous ethanol and deionized water successively and drying.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the construction handling device for carrying a tubular article.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The chassis is further provided with a guide column.

PN CN113000070-A
TI Fluorine-containing superhydrophobic modified material for catalyzing cycloaddition of carbon dioxide and epoxide, is obtained using metal organic framework with grafted active site and performing Mannich reaction with 4-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde.
AU HAN Z
   LIU S
   GAO M
   LIU L
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 202175852F
AB    NOVELTY - A fluorine-containing superhydrophobic modified metal organic framework (MOF) materials, is obtained by using metal organic framework with grafted active sites as a substrate, performing Mannich reaction with 4-trifluoromethylbenzaldehyde for post-synthetic modification (PSM) functionalization to obtain a series of fluorine-containing superhydrophobic MOF composite materials.
   USE - Fluorine-containing superhydrophobic modified MOFs material used as a catalyst in catalysis of cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fluorine-containing superhydrophobic modified MOFs material has excellent hydrophobic properties, and efficiently catalyzes the cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of fluorine-containing superhydrophobic modified MOFs material as a catalyst in catalysis of cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is MIL-101(chromium)-amide, MIL-101(aluminum)-amide, UiO-66-(zirconium)-amide, UiO-66-(hafnium)-amide. The superhydrophobic modified MOFs material comprises MIL-101(chlorine)-3fluoride, MIL-101(aluminum)-3fluoride, UiO-66(zirconium)-3fluoride and UiO-66(hafnium)-3fluoride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The cycloaddition reaction involves (i) using high-pressure reactor as reaction device, using epoxide, activated fluorine-containing super-hydrophobic modified MOFs material as the catalyst and tetrabutyl ammonium bromide in the PTFE lining, placing in the high-pressure reactor, sealing, introducing carbon dioxide gas, repeating three times for gas replacement, reacting at 80 degrees C for 12 hours, and detecting the yield by gas chromatography. The epoxide comprises one or more of propylene oxide, butylene oxide, isobutylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, styrene oxide, and cyclohexene oxide. Preferred Components: The cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide is catalyzed by using tetrabutyl ammonium bromide as a co-catalyst.

PN CN111729660-A
TI Preparing a magnetic nanomaterial with carbon            dioxide responsiveness used for separating a variety of            emulsions containing different surfactants, involves            dispersing the magnetic halloysite dopamine            ultrasonically in ethanol.
AU DAI X
   QIANG C
   CHENG D
   ZHANG H
   DAI J
   CHEN L
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2020994690
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a magnetic nanomaterial with carbon                dioxide responsiveness involves dispersing the                magnetic halloysite dopamine ultrasonically in                ethanol. The poly(2-(dimethylamino)ethyl                methacrylate) (PDMAEMA)-SH is dissolved in ethanol                and added to the magnetic halloysite dopamine,                stirred at room temperature for 24 hours, and a                magnetic nanomaterial with CO2 responsiveness is                obtained.
   USE - Method for preparing a magnetic nanomaterial                with carbon dioxide responsiveness used for                separating a variety of emulsions containing                different surfactants, where the emulsion is an                oil-in-water or water-in-oil emulsion                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a magnetic                nanomaterial with carbon dioxide responsiveness                realizes the efficient separation of various                oil-in-water and water-in-oil emulsions, and has                broad application prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  PDMAEMA-SH dissolved in ethanol is 0.3 g/ml. The                  mass ratio of magnetic halloysite dopamine to                  PDMAEMA-SH is 3:1. The molar ratio of RAFT chain                  transfer agent to N,N-dimethylaminoethyl                  methacrylate is 1:200. Preferred Process: The                  preparation method of the magnetic halloysite                  dopamine includes adding trihydroxyaminomethane                  to deionized water, dissolving, adjusting the pH                  to 8.5 with dilute hydrochloric acid to obtain a                  buffer solution. A certain proportion of magnetic                  halloysite powder, ethanol and dopamine is added                  to the buffer solution, stirred at room                  temperature and then suction filtered. The                  obtained powder is washed 3 times with deionized                  water, and then dried under vacuum at 40 degrees                  C, the dopamine-modified magnetic halloysite                  powder. The ratio of trihydroxyaminomethane,                  deionized water, magnetic halloysite, ethanol and                  dopamine is 1.5 mmol: 300 ml: 6.0 g: 10 ml: 0.3                  g. The preparation method of the PDMAEMA-SH                  includes dissolving PDMAEMA in tetrahydrofuran                  (THF). A magnetic stirring rotor is used to stir,                  added n-butylamine to react at room temperature                  for 4 hours. The molar ratio of n-butylamine to                  PDMAEMA is 50:1. The preparation method of the                  PDMAEMA includes first taking dimethylaminoethyl                  acrylate (DMAEMA), and dissolving the reversible                  addition fragmentation chain transfer                  polymerization (RAFT) chain transfer agent in the                  dried 1,4-dioxane. The components are completely                  dissolved, and added azobisisobutyronitrile                  (AIBN) to the system and sealed. The evacuated                  and ventilated nitrogen three times, then moved                  the system to a preheated 80 degrees C oil bath,                  and reacted for 18 hours. The ratio of the RAFT                  chain transfer agent to AIBN is 10mol:1mol. The                  molar ratio of RAFT chain transfer agent to                  N,N-dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate is 1:40 to                  1:400. The PDMAEMA refers to PDMAEMA with a                  molecular weight of 5-30 K. Preferred Condition:                  The ultrasonic dispersion time is 30 minutes. The                  volume ratio of water to oil in the oil-in-water                  emulsion is 99:1. The anionic surfactant is                  sodium lauryl sulfate and non-ionic surfactant is                  Tween (RTM: Non-ionic surfactant)-80. The 1mg                  sodium lauryl sulfate and Tween (RTM: Non-ionic                  surfactant)-80 added to each mL of oil-in-water                  emulsion. The volume ratio of oil and water is                  1:99 in a water-in-oil emulsion. The non-ionic                  surfactant is Span (RTM: nonionic surfactant)-80,                  and added 1mg Span (RTM: nonionic surfactant)-80                  per mL of water-in-oil emulsion. The 20 mg of                  magnetic nanomaterial weighed with CO2                  responsiveness, placed in an oil-in-water or                  water-in-oil emulsion, and kept in CO2 or                  nitrogen (N2) for 20 minutes. A magnet is used to                  absorb and separate, and taken the upper clear                  liquid for testing.

GA 2020025696
AB    NOVELTY - Triazine-based covalent organic framework (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing confined metal catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction reaction of carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild reaction conditions; and produces covalent triazine organic framework in large quantities, and produces confined metal catalyst having multi-electron transfer product generation activity and selectivity in electrocatalytic reduction reaction of carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Triazine-based covalent organic framework of formula (I) is new.
   X, Y1 = C or N; and
   n = not defined.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) metal catalyst confined, prepared by complexing hetero atom N in (I) with anchor metal ions; and
   (3) use of confined metal catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction reaction of carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal ion is copper, cobalt, nickel and/or iron, and the metal source of the metal ion is copper(II) chloride, copper(I) chloride, copper(II) bromide, copper(I) iodide, copper(II) nitrate, copper(II) acetate, copper(II) sulfate, iron(III) chloride, iron(III) bromide, iron(II) chloride, iron(II) bromide, iron(III) acetate, cobalt(II) acetate, iron(III) nitrate, cobalt(II) thiocyanate, cobalt(III) chloride, cobalt(II) chloride, cobalt(II) acetate, cobalt(II) sulfate, nickel(II) chloride, nickel(II) bromide, nickel(II) sulfate and/or nickel(II) thiocyanate. The working electrode base material used for electrocatalytic reduction reaction is porous electrode, polytetrafluoroethylene-treated carbon paper electrode or carbon paper electrode. The working electrode comprises quantitative spray coating, knife coating, or quantitative drip coating. The electrolytic cell used for electrocatalytic reduction reaction is flow electrolytic cell, membrane reactor, or H-type electrolytic cell.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises taking dimethanol-based compound and diamidine-based compound, and carrying out oxidation and polycondensation reaction under catalysis of alkaline reagent in solvent to obtain (I), where at least one dimethanol-based compound and diamidine-based compound contains heterocyclic ring, and the heterocyclic ring is 2,2'-bipyridine. Preferred Components: The diamidine-based compound is 2,2'-bipyridine-5,5'-bis(carboximidamide) or 1,1-biphenyl-4,4'-bis(carboximidamide), and the dimethanol-based compound is 1,1-biphenyl 4,4'-dimethanol or 2,2'-bipyridinyl-5,5'-dimethanol.

PN CN109825534-A; CN109825534-B
TI Preparing (R)-1-phenyl-1,2-ethanediol by supercritical carbon dioxide/buffer solution-phase system enzymatic method comprises e.g. catalyzing asymmetric hydrolysis reaction of styrene oxide by mung bean hydrolase as a catalyst.
AU WANG B
   ZHANG C
   HONG L
   YANG F
   CHEN Z
   QIN L
   YE Z
   LIU W
AE UNIV JIANGSU TECHNOLOGY (JIAA-C)
   UNIV JIANGSU TECHNOLOGY (JIAA-C)
GA 2019500294
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (R)-1-phenyl-1,2-ethanediol by supercritical carbon dioxide/buffer solution-phase system enzymatic method comprises catalyzing asymmetric hydrolysis reaction of styrene oxide by mung bean hydrolase as a catalyst, using a supercritical carbon dioxide and tris buffer solution as a two-phase system, using sodium bicarbonate as a pH stabilizer, where tris buffer solution is a tris-hydrochloric acid buffer solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing (R)-1-phenyl-1,2-ethanediol by supercritical carbon dioxide/buffer solution-phase system enzymatic method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has yield of over 49% and ee value of more than 97%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) adding mung bean hydrolase into the supercritical high pressure reactor, adding epoxy styrene, sodium bicarbonate and Tris-hydrochloric acid buffer solution at 31-50 degrees C, passing carbon dioxide gas, pressurizing, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis, (ii) reacting for 3-12 hours, vacuum extracting carbon dioxide, performing separation of carbon dioxide and Tris buffer solution, and (iii) ultra filtering the buffer solution to remove enzyme protein, adding sodium chloride into the buffer solution to be saturated, then extracting with ethyl acetate, separating organic matter from the carbon dioxide phase, then subjecting to rotary evaporation to remove ethyl acetate to obtain final product. In step (i), adding 6-12 vol.% Tris-hydrochloric acid buffer solution into the reaction vessel. The method comprises adding 8 vol.% Tris-hydrochloric acid buffer solution in batches before the enzyme-catalyzed hydrolysis reaction into the reactor, then adding Tris buffer solution in an amount of 1 vol.% into the reaction vessel for every 1 hour until the amount of addition to the reactor volume ratio is 12%. In step (i), final pressure of the pressurization is 8-15 MPa. The reaction temperature is 37-45 degrees C, and reaction time is 8-12 hours. Preferred Components: The concentration of the epoxy styrene in the two phase system is 10-120 mM, preferably 50-80 mM. The concentration of the sodium hydrogencarbonate in the buffer solution is 0.4-1 M, preferably 0.7-0.9 M.

   ZHU H
   JIANG M
AE UNIV NORTHEAST PETROLEUM (UYPT-C)
   UNIV NORTHEAST PETROLEUM (UYPT-C)
GA 201892556L
AB    NOVELTY - An organic carbon recovery coefficient of biogas source rock calculating method preparing the components of biogas are mainly methane, the carbon-containing molecules in other components are heavy hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide, according to the principle of conservation of carbon, the content of heavy hydrocarbons with less content is neglected, which is simplified and the portion of the organic carbon consumed in the original source rock is converted into the methane produced and carbon dioxide, preparing the raw organic carbon content = residual organic carbon content + carbon content in the formation of methane + carbon content in the formation of carbon dioxide, calculating the organic carbon recovery coefficient represented by the expression as given in the specification.

PT P
PN CN108250346-A; CN108250346-B
TI Synthesizing copolymer of diacetone acrylamide and acrylic acid involves taking carbon dioxide as medium and using persulfate and bisulfite redox system as initiators, precipitating and polymerizing diacetone acrylamide.
AU LAN P
   LV J
AE UNIV JIAXING (JIXA-C)
   UNIV JIAXING (JIXA-C)
GA 2018545271
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing copolymer of diacetone acrylamide and acrylic acid involves taking carbon dioxide as the medium and using persulfate and bisulfite redox system as initiators, precipitating and polymerizing diacetone acrylamide and acrylic acid comonomer above the critical temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide to obtain non-aqueous copolymer of diacetone acrylamide and acrylic acid.
   USE - Method for synthesizing copolymer of diacetone acrylamide and acrylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesize copolymer of diacetone acrylamide and acrylic acid which is non-toxic and non-polluting medium supercritical carbon dioxide instead of an organic solvent, avoids environmental pollution caused by the use of toxic, harmful, volatile and flammable organic solvents, and achieve green environmental protection, energy saving and resource conservation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The copolymer of diacetone acrylamide and acrylic acid is obtained by adding diacetone acrylamide, an acrylic acid monomer and an initiator to a reaction vessel, sealing, adjusting the initial concentration of the initiator is 0.05-0.5 mol/l. The carbon dioxide is introduced to discharge the reaction kettle air, and the initial concentration of the total monomer of the diacetone acrylamide, acrylic acid is 1-8 mol /l, and then inlet and outlet valves of the reaction kettle is closed. The pressure in the reaction kettle rises continuously as the temperature increases. The pressure in the reaction kettle is controlled to be 8 MPa or more and 45 mega Pascals or less when the reaction temperature reaches 32-40 degrees C. The stirrer is started, and the reaction is started. The time is 3-20 hours until the end of the reaction. Preferred Conditions: The persulfate in the redox system is one of potassium persulfate, sodium persulfate or ammonium persulfate, the bisulfite in the redox system is potassium hydrogen sulfite, sulfurous acid A type of sodium hydrogen or ammonium hydrogen sulfite. The diacetone acrylamide accounts for 0-70% by mole of the two monomers. The acrylic acid accounts for 30-100% by mole of the two monomers. The diacetone acrylamide and the acrylic acid have a total monomer initial concentration of 2-6 mol/l. The initial ratio of persulfate to bisulfite in the initiator is 1:1, and the initial concentration of the initiator is from 0.08-0.3 mol/l. The pressure in the autoclave was 20-30 mega Pascal. The reaction temperature is controlled at 33-38 degrees C. The reaction time is 4-10 hours. The cooling water is passed through, and the reaction kettle is cooled to room temperature. The outlet valve of the reaction vessel was opened, and the pressure was reduced to normal pressure, and the product was taken out, i.e., a non-aqueous white product, i.e., a copolymer of diacetone acrylamide and acrylic acid.
DC A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.)

TI Method for producing co2 synthesis gas ddition of hydrogen to carbon dioxide increases the conversion of CO2 and coal into carbon monoxide addition of hydrogen to carbon dioxide increases the conversion of CO2 and coal into carbon monoxide.
AU TARASOV A L
   KUSTOV L M
AE UNIV MOSC NAT RES TECHNOLOGICAL (UYMO-Non-standard)
GA 201860502E
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to coal gasification technology and can be used to produce synthesis gas. Method for producing synthesis gas is as follows. On the coal impregnation method, catalytically active metal-iron is applied. Carbon dioxide conversion is carried out in a flow reactor at 450-700 degrees C. AB    NOVELTY - A low-concentration ethylene detecting method involves utilizing an oxidized sensor and a room-temperature catalytic material to catalyze the complete oxidation of ethylene to carbon dioxide, and detecting the concentration of carbon dioxide by infrared spectroscopy to determine the concentration of ethylene.
   USE - Method for detecting low-concentration ethylene using an oxidized sensor. Uses include but are not limited to composition of the gas and effective removal of the interference of C3 or more olefins, C2 or more alkanes, alkynes, aldehydes, ketones and other gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables detecting the low-concentration ethylene with high sensitivity and strong specificity, and is suitable for humidity ranges from 10-95% relative humidity, and pressure range from 0.1-10.0 MPa and ethylene concentration range from 0.2 ppm-1%.

TI Fumigation vaporizer comprises e.g. vaporization            unit for vaporizing fumigant and discharging as            vaporized fumigant, and fumigant supply unit including            fumigant supply pipe provided to supply fumigant to            vaporization unit.
AU PARK Y W
AE SAFE FUME INC (SAFE-Non-standard)
GA 2022D2427P
AB    NOVELTY - Fumigation vaporizer comprises a vaporization                unit (100) for vaporizing a fumigant and                discharging it as a vaporized fumigant; a fumigant                supply unit (200) including a fumigant supply pipe                (220) provided to supply the fumigant accommodated                in a fumigant tank (210) to the vaporization unit;                a gas supply unit (300) comprising a gas tank (310)                provided outside the vaporization unit and filled                with an inert gas, and an inert gas supply pipe                body (320) provided to supply the inert gas filled                in the gas tank to the vaporization unit; a branch                pipe (322) branching from the inert gas supply pipe                body of gas supply unit is installed to be                connected and operated using the inert gas supplied                through the branch pipe as a power source; and an                air diaphragm pump (400) for supplying fumigant to                vaporization unit by allowing the fumigant supplied                through the fumigant supply pipe to be                transferred.
   USE - Used as fumigation vaporizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The fumigation vaporizer has an effect that a                liquid fumigant that can be widely used for                agriculture or quarantine can be used in a safer                and more efficient way than before since it has                insecticidal, sterilizing, and herbicidal effects;                operates the chemical liquid pump by applying an                air diaphragm pump, using a part of nitrogen and                carbon-dioxide in the form of high-pressure gas as                a power source, if there is no gas inflow, the pump                operation stops, simplifying the carburetor                control, and it has the effect of fundamentally                blocking the possibility that only one of the two                fluids will flow; having the air diaphragm pump                does not use electrical equipment in delivering a                flammable fluid, so it has a basic explosion-proof                performance, and the possibility of failure is also                significantly lowered; has an effect of increasing                the temperature of the ejected fluid to minimize                the amount of carrier gas used; stably maintains                the target temperature, thus it has the effect of                enabling fumigation work even if a failure occurs                in one heater; and increases the heat transfer                efficiency by installing a diaphragm in the heating                space, and to keep the non-vaporized liquid from                being ejected immediately.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                fumigation vaporizer (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

PN WO2022104226-A1; TW202233874-A
TI Thermal atomic layer deposition method for            selectively depositing silicon and oxygen containing            film into surface features on substrate, involves            providing substrate having both dielectric surface and            metal surface in reactor.
AU KANJOLIA R
   LIU G
   POTYEN M
   WOODRUFF J
   ZOPE B
   LEI X
AE VERSUM MATERIALS US LLC (VESR-C)
GA 202268958V
AB    NOVELTY - Thermal atomic layer deposition method for                selectively depositing silicon and oxygen                containing film into surface features on a                substrate, involves e.g.: (a) providing substrate                having both dielectric surface and metal surface in                a reactor; (b) heating the reactor and optionally                maintaining pressure; (c) introducing at least one                self-assembled monolayer (SAM) volatile precursor                comprising organic thiol compounds into the                reactor, to anchor more abundantly on the metal                surface than on the dielectric surface; (d) purging                using inert gas; (e) introducing silicon compound                comprising tetraisocyanatosilane (TICS),                triisocyanatosilane, and triisocyanatomethylsilane,                and optionally a catalyst, to anchor the silicon                compound more abundantly on the dielectric surface                than on metal surface; (f) purging inert gas; (g)                providing oxygen source and optionally catalyst                into the reactor to form silicon and oxygen                containing dielectric film on the dielectric                surface; and (h) purging inert gas.
   USE - The method is useful for thermal atomic layer                deposition for selectively depositing a silicon                oxide, silicon oxynitride, carbon doped silicon                oxide and carbon dioxide-containing dielectric film                onto surface features on a substrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables deposition of silicon and                oxygen-containing film on dielectric, and not on a                metal or metal hydride, avoiding/minimizing                oxidation of the metal and metal hydroxide                layer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Thermal atomic layer deposition method for                selectively depositing a silicon and oxygen                containing film into surface features on a                substrate, comprises: (a) providing at least one                substrate having both a dielectric surface and a                metal surface in a reactor; (b) heating the reactor                to at least one temperature from ambient                temperature to 350 degrees C and optionally                maintaining the reactor under a pressure of less                than or equal to 100 torr; (c) introducing at least                one self-assembled monolayer (SAM) volatile                precursor comprising organic thiol compounds into                the reactor to anchor more abundantly on the metal                surface than on the dielectric surface; (d) purging                the reactor using inert gas; (e) introducing                silicon compound comprising tetraisocyanatosilane                (TICS), triisocyanatosilane, and                triisocyanatomethylsilane, and optionally a                catalyst into the reactor, to anchor the silicon                compound more abundantly on the dielectric surface                than on metal surface; (f) purging the reactor                using inert gas; (g) providing oxygen source and                optionally a catalyst into the reactor to form a                silicon and oxygen containing dielectric film on                the dielectric surface, where the catalyst                comprises a Lewis base; and (h) purging the reactor                using inert gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dielectric surface                  comprises silicon oxide, carbon doped silicon                  oxide, silicon oxynitride, carbon doped                  oxynitride, silicon nitride, and metal oxide. The                  metal surface includes metal comprising cobalt,                  aluminum, copper, tantalum, ruthenium, manganese,                  molybdenum and/or tungsten. The organic thiol                  compound comprises methanethiol, ethanethiol,                  propanethiol, butanethiol, pentanethiol,                  hexanethiol, octanethiol, nonanethiol,                  decanethiol, undecanethiol, 1-dodecanethiol,                  1-dodecanethiol, 1-nonanethiol, 1-decanethiol,                  1-octanethiol, 1-heptanethiol, 1-hexanethiol,                  1-pentanethiol, perfluorodecanethiol,                  di-tert-butyl disulfide, di-heptane disulfide,                  2-propene-1-thiol, tetrahydro-2H-pyran-4-thiol,                  4-methyl-6-trifluoromethyl- pyrimidine-2-thiol,                  ara-xylene-alpha-thiol, 4-trifluoromethylbenzyl                  mercaptan, 4-(trifluoromethoxy)benzyl mercaptan,                  4-fluorobenzyl mercaptan,                  3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)benzenethiol,                  2-(trifluoromethyl)benzenethiol,                  4-trifluoromethyl-2,3,5,6-tetrafluorothiophenol,                  3,5-difluorobenzyl mercaptan,                  4-trifluoromethyl-2,3,5,6-tetrafluorothiopheno                  and thiophenol. The oxygen source comprises                  water. The catalyst comprises trimethylamine,                  triethylamine, tri-n-propylamine,                  tri-iso-propylamine, tri-n-butylamine,                  phenyldimethylamine, tri-iso-butylamine,                  pyridine, and piperazine. The silicon and oxygen                  containing film comprises silicon oxide film,                  silicon oxynitride film, carbon doped silicon                  oxide film, and carbon doped silicon oxynitride                  film. The metal oxide comprises zirconium oxide,                  hafnium oxide, silicon doped zirconium oxide, and                  silicon doped hafnium oxide. Preferred Method: In                  the method: the catalyst is provided into the                  reactor in step (g); the oxygen source and the                  catalyst are mixed prior to be provided into the                  reactor in step (g).
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chemical                thermal atomic layer deposition (ALD) of silicon                oxide using tetraisocynatosilane, water and                trimethylamine that do not support the                claims.

PN US11319779-B1
TI System for powering borehole sensor with thermal energy, comprises annular space formed between the casing and tubular pipe, and power source placed in borehole to power sensor in response to temperature gradient between surface of the casing and surface of the tubular pipe.
AU DEWERS T
   KORIPELLA C R
   HEATH J E
   BRYAN C R
AE NAT TECHNOLOGY & ENG SOLUTIONS SANDIA (HONE-C)
GA 2022580294
AB    NOVELTY - The energy harvesting system (10) comprises a tubular pipe inserted into a subsurface borehole (18), a casing (22) in thermal contact with an inner wall of the borehole, coaxially set with the tubular pipe to form an annular space (20), and a power source set within the borehole in electrical communication with the sensor, and the power source is configured to generate an electrical power in response to a temperature gradient between a surface of the casing and a surface of the tubular pipe. The power source comprises at least one thermopile (12) comprising an array of thermoelectric generator (TEG) modules, and at least one thermopile is set within the wall surface and embedded into a cement or rock formation.
   USE - System for powering a borehole sensor with thermal energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The system includes thermoelectric modules or thermoelectric generators to convert heat directly into electric power. Solid-state heat engines require no moving parts, are robust and are very reliable in long-life applications. The system uses a reliable long-term power source for wireless, self-sufficient sensor networks for real-time to long-term monitoring of carbon dioxide plumes and the health of injection/monitoring wells and fluid migration towards underground sources of drinking water. TEGs utilize the small temperature differentials that exist in actively pumped wells. When additional power is required, thermal pulses, or intermittent pumping, generates greater temperature gradients to increase power output. Long-term power supplies may be coupled with a power conditioning circuit and a rechargeable battery. The thermoelectric generators are scalable, so increased voltage or power output can be achieved by simply adding additional TEGs in series or in parallel. The system provides support for real-time high-resolution monitoring, for faster response time in cases of pressure build-up and leakage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: 1. a system for sensing environmental parameters in a subsurface borehole; and 2. a method for harvesting thermal energy for powering a sensor in a subsurface borehole in a geological formation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an energy harvesting system.
   Energy harvesting system (10)
   Thermopiles (12)
   Tubing (14)
   Borehole (18)
   Annular space (20)
   Casing (22)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The thermopile further comprises a thermal conductor portion set between the tubing and the casing, where the thermal conductor portion comprises a metallic element having efficient heat transfer properties. The metallic element comprises one of multiple flexible metal fins, a mesh material, and a wire brush.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); Q49 (Mining (E21)); U14 (Memories, Film and Hybrid Circuits); W05 (Alarms, Signalling, Telemetry and Telecontrol)

PN CN114420528-A
TI Fixed isotope magnetic mass spectrometer receiver            for isotope analysis instruments, comprises Faraday            cups arranged along flight trajectories of            corresponding isotope ions and installed between            receiver plates, receiver plates fixed on support            plate, support plates fixed on slit seat, and slit            seat.
AU YUAN X
   HUANG B
   TANG Y
   YANG S
   CHEN Y
   SHE Y
   ZOU S
   LIN Y
   LI H
   MIAO K
AE SICHUAN HONGHUA IND CO LTD (CNNU-C)
GA 2022647772
AB    NOVELTY - Fixed isotope magnetic mass spectrometer                receiver comprises several Faraday cups, a receiver                plate A (7), a receiver plate B (8), a support                plate A (9), a support plate B (10) and a slit seat                11, where the Faraday cups are arranged along the                flight trajectories of the corresponding isotope                ions and are installed between the receiver plate A                and B, the Faraday cup is perpendicular to each                other with the receiver plate A and B, the receiver                plate A and B are respectively fixed on the support                plate A and B, the support plate A and B are fixed                on the slit seat, and the cup mouth of all Faraday                cups faces the slit seat.
   USE - Fixed isotope magnetic mass spectrometer                receiver for use in isotope analysis                instruments.
   ADVANTAGE - The fixed isotope magnetic mass spectrometer                receiver has simple structure, less parts, low                processing requirements, low production and                processing cost, is installed in simple and quick                manner, cleverly designs the size of the Faraday                cup mouth and the slit through the analysis and                calculation of the ion mass dispersion, reduces the                number of Faraday cups required by the receiver,                greatly improves the evacuation rate and makes it                easier and faster to reach high vacuum, minimizes                ion sputtering and effectively reduces secondary                electrons and reduces the splash rate of secondary                electrons generated by the ion beam hitting the                receiver, thus obtaining better receiving                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for determining the Faraday cup arrangement of a                fixed isotope magnetic mass spectrometer receiver,                which involves:
   a. performing mass dispersion calculation on                ions generated by electron bombardment of                tetrafluoromethane, germanium tetrafluoride and                carbon dioxide gases; and
   b. analyzing the dispersion relationship of                different substances and determining the entrance                slit width of the Faraday cup, the width of the                suppression gate, the width of the cup mouth, and                the center distance between adjacent Faraday cups,                namely determining the arrangement mode of Faraday                cup.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic perspective view                of a fixed isotope magnetic mass spectrometer                receiver.

   7Receiver plate A
   8Receiver plate B
   9Supporting plate A
   10Supporting plate B
   11Slit seat
   11AHole
   12Inner hexagonal cylindrical head                screw
   13Grooved flat end fastening screw
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The tetrafluoromethane gas                  molecules are bombarded by electrons to form                  fluoride ions including 12CF3+and 13CF3+ions. The                  germanium tetrafluoride gas molecules form                  germanium trifluoride ions including 70GeF3+,                  72GeF3+, 73GeF3+, 74GeF3+, 76GeF3+ ions by                  electron bombardment. The carbon dioxide gas                  molecules produce oxide ions including 12C16O2+,                  13C16O2+, 12C16O17O+, 12C17O2+, 13C16O17O+ ions                  by electron bombardment.

AU LI F
   WU Q
   YANG J
AE ZHEJIANG ZHONGKE YINGHUA ECOLOGICAL NEW (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202225939S
AB    NOVELTY - Degradable resin comprises 70-95 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 1-10 pts. wt. toughening agent, 0.5-15 pts. wt. filler, 0.1-1 pt. wt. lubricant, 0.05-0.2 pt. wt. nucleating agent, 0.08-0.3 pt. wt. compatibilizer, 0.1-2 pts. wt. plasticizer, 0.1-1 pt. wt. antioxidant and 0.1-3 pts. wt. coupling agent. The polylactic acid is PLAlx175. The toughening agent is polybutylene succinate, poly-methyl ethylene carbonate (PPC) and/or carbon dioxide-based thermoplastic polyurethane (PPCU). The filler is starch and/or bamboo fiber powder. The coupling agent is titanate and/or lauric acid.
   USE - The degradable resin is useful in preparing suction tube (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The degradable resin solves the problems that the existing suction tube have poor heat resistance, the filler is easy to move out, and the food contact requirements cannot be met.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing suction tube by using the degradable resin, comprising (1) firstly mixing starch, bamboo fiber powder or talc powder with certain amount of lauric acid and titanate coupling agent, stirring 70-86degrees Celsius for 20 minutes to activate the surface to generate amphiphilicity, (2) uniformly mixing the modified and stirred material obtained in step (1) with polylactic acid, toughening agent and other raw materials in low speed mixer, (3) adding the uniformly mixed material into the double-screw extruder, dividing the double-screw extruder along the extruding path into 13 sub-areas, where the first area is 100-140degrees Celsius, the second area to the sixth area is 175-210degrees Celsius, the seventh area to the ninth area is 175-200degrees Celsius, the tenth zone to the twelve zone is 170-200degrees Celsius, and the thirteenth zone is 175- 185degrees Celsius,air cooling after extruding, then granulating to obtain the special resin particles, (4) adding resin particles into the sucker forming machine, dividing the extrusion forming path into five areas, where the first area is 110-180degrees Celsius, the second area to the fourth area is 175-210degrees Celsius, the fifth area is 165-180degrees Celsius, cooling the extruded material by the purified water tank and then cutting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The nucleating agent is titanium dioxide, talc, TMC nucleating agent and/or nano- silicon dioxide. The lubricant is ethylene bis-stearic acid amide, glycerol monostearate and/or fine talc. The compatibilizer is epoxy crosslinking agent and/or glycidyl methacrylate. The plasticizer is vegetable oil and/or glycerol. The antioxidant is antioxidant 2,6-tertiary butyl-4-methylphenol (SIC), bis(3,5-tertiarybutyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)sulfide, tetrakis(beta-(3,5-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoate)pentaerythritol ester, thiodipropionate diester, double dodecyl ester, ditetradecyl ester and dioctadecyl ester, antioxidant 1010, antioxidant 1076, Antioxidant 164 and/or Antioxidant CA. The starch can be tapioca starch, corn starch or potato starch.Preferred Method: In step (4), the rotation speed of the main body of the straw forming machine is 50-200 revolutions/minute, the pulling speed is 500-1000 revolutions/minute, and the cutting speed is 200-500 pieces/minute.

GA 2021E8194F

The fluid through the first                wellbore into the subterranean formation at an                injection rate less than or equal to R2 for a                duration of time D1 is injected (304). The fluid                through the first wellbore into the subterranean                formation at an injection rate of R3 is injected                (305). The fluid enters the subterranean formation                at a pressure greater than or equal to the minimum                horizontal stress in the subterranean formation.                The injection rate R3 is no less than 150% of the                injection rate R2.
   USE - Method for injecting fluid into subterranean                formation for production of hydrocarbons from lower                permeability underground reservoirs.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                non-transient computer-readable medium including                instructions that.
