PN CN113648273-A; CN113648273-B
TI Soothing facial mask useful for recovery after            facial skin repair, comprises facial mask fragrance            including adipose stem cell exosomes, drug-loaded            nanoliposome, birch sap, allantoin, hydroxyethyl            cellulose, glycerin and deionized water, and facial            mask base material.
AU XIAO Z
   ZHAO Y
AE ZHAO Y (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 2021D5402G
AB    NOVELTY - Soothing facial mask comprises facial mask                fragrance including 0.1-2 pts. wt. adipose stem                cell exosomes, 10-15 pts. wt. drug-loaded                nanoliposome, 3-5 pts. wt. birch sap, 0.5-1 pts.                wt. allantoin, 0.2-1 pts. wt. hydroxyethyl                cellulose, 5-10 pts. wt. glycerin and 66-81.5 pts.                wt. deionized water and facial mask base                material.
   USE - The soothing facial mask is useful for                recovery after facial skin repair.
   ADVANTAGE - The soothing facial mask solves the problem                that the redness, inflammation, pain and other                symptoms after facial skin repair by injection                therapy e.g. water and light needles cannot be                quickly relieved, promotes rapid skin absorption,                can reduce swelling, has antiinflammatory,                analgesic, anti-allergy and antibacterial effects,                quickly alleviates the symptoms of postoperative                skin redness, pain, inflammation, and allergies, so                facial skin can achieve skin repair effects as soon                as possible.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for preparing soothing facial mask,                comprising (i) extracting adipose stem cell                exosomes by inoculating 50% adipose stem cells in                the induction culture medium and culturing at                37degrees Celsius, and 5% carbon dioxide for 48                hours, sucking out medium, adding low-sugar                Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM), collecting                the supernatant, centrifuging supernatant at 300                revolutions/minute for 10 min, 2000                revolutions/minute for 10 minutes and 10000                revolutions/minute for 30 minutes, and                ultracentrifuging at 14000 revolutions/minute for                90 minutes, and collecting precipitate as adipose                stem cell exosomes, (ii) completely dissolving egg                yolk phosphatidylcholine, cholesterol, luteolin,                madecassoside, bisabolol, methyl o-hydroxybenzoate                and peppermint oil in absolute ethanol, spin-drying                under reduced pressure to obtain lipid film,                dissolving obtained lipid film with absolute                ethanol and spin-drying again to obtain a secondary                lipid film, dissolving and hydrating obtained                secondary lipid film with diethyl pyrocarbonate                saline, subjecting hydration system to ultrasonic                treatment, so the liposomes are fully and uniformly                dispersed in the hydration system, and extruding                polycarbonate membrane from the hydration system to                obtain drug-loaded nano-liposomes, (iii) uniformly                mixing adipose stem cell exosome, drug-loaded                nanoliposome, allantoin, and glycerin to obtain                system 1, and dissolving hydroxyethyl cellulosein                deionized water to obtain system 2 , mixing system                1 and system 2 with birch sap to obtain facial mask                fragrance, (iv) immersing mask substrate with the                fragrance, and encapsulating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The facial mask                  fragrance includes 1.5 pts. wt. adipose stem cell                  exosomes, 12 pts. wt. drug-loaded nano liposomes,                  5 pts. wt. birch sap, 1 part of allantoin, 0.5                  part of hydroxyethyl cellulose, 10 pts. wt.                  glycerol and 70 pts. wt. deionized water. The                  adipose stem cell exosome is human adipose stem                  cell exosome with a particle size of 20-200 nm.                  The drug-loaded nanoliposome contains egg yolk                  phosphatidylcholine, cholesterol and drug                  composition in a mass ratio of 65-70:20:10-15.                  The drug composition includes luteolin, asiatic                  acid, bisabolol, methyl o-hydroxybenzoate and                  peppermint oil in mass ratio of                  3-5:1-3:1-3:1-5:1-3. In the step (i), the                  induction culture medium is based on low-sugar                  DMEM medium with inducer including 50 microg/ml                  recombinant albumin, 20 microg/ml recombinant                  epidermal growth factor, 20 microg/ml vitamin C,                  8 microg/ml nicotinamide, 12 microg/ml sodium                  pyruvate, 30 microg/ml fibroblast growth factor,                  0.5 microg/ml 3-isobutyl-1-methylxanthine, and                  0.5 microg/ml retinoic acid.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  method for obtaining adipose stem cells comprises                  washing the adipose tissue with                  phosphate-buffered saline for 3 times, cutting                  into pieces to obtain 1 mm3 pieces, adding 0.1%                  collagenase I and 0.25% trypsin to digest for                  30-45 minutes, allowing to stand for                  stratification and sucking the upper layer of fat                  cell fluid, adding obtained adipocyte liquid to                  an equal volume of DMEM-F12 medium, stopping the                  digestion with 10% fetal bovine serum,                  centrifuging at 300 revolutions/minute for 10                  minutes, discarding supernatant, adding 20 ml                  serum-free cell culture medium into the cell                  pellet, carrying out constant temperature                  incubation at 37degrees Celsius, and 5% carbon                  dioxide for 48 hours of constant temperature                  incubation, changing the medium, changing medium                  every 5 days, observing growth of spindle cells,                  and when the cell confluence reached 70-80%, and                  freezing to obtain adipose stem cells. In the                  steep (ii), the ultrasonic treatment is carried                  out at 300 W for 10 minutes; and the                  polycarbonate membrane is used to extrude the                  hydration system by using a polycarbonate                  membrane with a pore size of 200 nm, and after                  repeated extrusion for 3-5 times, the system is                  extruded through a polycarbonate membrane with a                  pore diameter of 100 nm for 2-3 times to obtain                  drug-loaded nanoliposomes with a particle size of                  100-150 nm.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

PN US2021340092-A1
TI Forming porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin used for forming an alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid or salt by contacting at least one phenol compound, formaldehyde, and aqueous base under polymerization conditions, and contacting with aqueous acid and metal-containing base.
AU HLAVINKA M L
   IACONO P
AE CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
GA 2021C6040B
AB    NOVELTY - A porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin is formed by (a) in the presence of a basic particulate template, contacting at least one phenol compound, formaldehyde, and an aqueous base under polymerization conditions sufficient to form a templated crosslinked polyphenol resin comprising a crosslinked polyphenol resin in contact with the basic particulate template; (b) contacting the templated crosslinked polyphenol resin with an aqueous acid under pore forming conditions sufficient to remove the basic particulate template and form a porous crosslinked polyphenol resin; and (c) contacting the porous crosslinked polyphenol resin with a metal-containing base to form a promoter comprising a porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin comprising associated metal cations.
   USE - Formation of porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin used for forming an alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid or a salt (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resins may result in high yields of the desired alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid, such as acrylic acid. The use of the heterogeneous, porous crosslinked sodium-appended cocatalyst provides a more facile way of separation the acrylate product from the catalyst system, because it is not soluble in the process diluent, the polyphenoxide can be regenerated in a reactor setting by sodium base treatment by hydroxyl deprotonation and/or by base absorption into the solid porous matrix, it retains its robustness and structural integrity such that it does not degrade under the reaction conditions or regeneration conditions using sodium treatment, and its porous crosslinked structure allows for high sodium deposition density and facile sodium site access by incoming catalyst intermediates (such as metalalactones), as well as ease of regeneration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for forming an alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid or a salt which involves (a) contacting in any order (1) a transition metal precursor compound comprising a group 8-11 transition metal and at least one first ligand; (2) optionally, at least one second ligand; (3) an olefin; (4) carbon dioxide (CO2); (5) a diluent; and (6) a promoter comprising a porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin comprising associated metal cations, to provide a reaction mixture; and (b) applying reaction conditions to the reaction mixture suitable to form the alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid or the salt; where the porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin is in particulate form and has a particle size of 2-500 microm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin comprises a phenoxide-formaldehyde resin, a polyhydroxidearene-formaldehyde resin, and/or polyhydroxidearene- and fluorophenoxide-formaldehyde resin. The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin comprises a resorcinol-formaldehyde resin, and/or resorcinol- and 2-fluorophenol-formaldehyde resin.Preferred Parameters: The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin is in particulate form and has an average particle size of 2-500 microm. The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin is in particulate form and has an average particle size of 10-250 microm. The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin has an average pore diameter 2-250 nm. The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin has an average pore diameter 50-250 nm. The basic particulate template has a solubility in water of less than 0.25 g/L at 25degrees Celsius. The basic particulate template has an average particle size from about 2-200 microm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The basic particulate template comprises an alkaline earth metal carbonate, phosphate, monohydrogen phosphate, or dihydrogen phosphate. The basic particulate template comprises magnesium carbonate, calcium carbonate, strontium carbonate, tribasic calcium phosphate, calcium monohydrogen phosphate, or calcium dihydrogen phosphate. The aqueous base is an alkaline metal hydroxide. The aqueous base is sodium hydroxide (NaOH) or potassium hydroxide (KOH). The aqueous acid is hydrochloric acid (HCl) or hydrobromic acid (HBr). The associated metal cations are selected from Group 1, 2, 12, or 13 metal. The associated metal cations are sodium or potassium. The metal-containing base comprises an oxide, hydroxide, alkoxide, aryloxide, amide, alkyl amide, arylamide, or carbonate of an alkali metal or an alkaline earth metal. The metal-containing base comprises an alkoxide or a hydroxide of sodium or potassium.
   EXAMPLE - The examples do not provide sufficient details of a process of forming the porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin, so they do not support the claims.

PN KR2236588-B1
TI Cultivating soybean sprout comprises selecting soybean sprouts, washing with running water, soaking washed bean sprouts beans by immersing in water, and immersing bean sprouts in liquid composition containing pine needle extract.
AU KIM S
AE JEONGDAM FOOD & BEVERAGE INC (JEON-Non-standard)
GA 202138468K
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating soybean sprout comprises selecting soybean sprouts, washing with running water, soaking washed bean sprouts beans by immersing in water for 10 minutes to 6 hours, immersing bean sprouts in a liquid composition containing pine needle extract at 10-25 degrees C, repeatedly spraying liquid composition containing pine needle extract to germinate, placing germinated bean sprouts in cultivation container of cultivation room, spraying the liquid composition repeatedly at intervals of 1-4 hours to cultivate for 3-7 days, where the liquid composition includes 50-80 pts. wt. distilled water, 1-20 pts. wt. pine needle extract, 5-10 pts. wt. cucumber juice, 0.01-3 pts. wt. fructooligosaccharide, and 0.1-5 pts. wt. banana concentrate, cultivating the germinated bean sprouts, blocking the ventilation of the cultivation room during the cultivation, adding diluted solution prepared by mixing water and pine needle extract in a mass ratio of 1:20 in the bean sprouts.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating soybean sprout.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves productivity; prevents problems e.g. sores and rot; reduces cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating soybean sprout comprises selecting soybean sprouts, washing with running water, soaking washed bean sprouts beans by immersing in water for 10 minutes to 6 hours, immersing bean sprouts in a liquid composition containing pine needle extract at 10-25 degrees C, repeatedly spraying liquid composition containing pine needle extract to germinate, placing germinated bean sprouts in cultivation container of cultivation room, spraying the liquid composition repeatedly at intervals of 1-4 hours to cultivate for 3-7 days, where the liquid composition includes 50-80 pts. wt. distilled water, 1-20 pts. wt. pine needle extract, 5-10 pts. wt. cucumber juice, 0.01-3 pts. wt. fructooligosaccharide, and 0.1-5 pts. wt. banana concentrate, cultivating the germinated bean sprouts, blocking the ventilation of the cultivation room during the cultivation, adding diluted solution prepared by mixing water and pine needle extract in a mass ratio of 1:20 in the bean sprouts, cultivation container, and cultivation room once a day, disinfecting by spraying repeatedly for 1-60 minutes until the carbon dioxide concentration of 1300-2000 ppm, and ventilating the cultivation room after spraying the diluents.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the method for cultivating soybean sprout (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Extraction solution useful in preparing active            light calcium carbonate and co-producing magnesium            hydroxide and magnesium carbonate, comprises organic            extraction solvents, organic amines and carboxylic            acids.
AU ZHONG J
AE XIAMEN HUICHUANGYUAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
GA 2020A5000L
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction solution comprises organic                extraction solvents, organic amines and carboxylic                acids. The organic extraction solvent is hardly                soluble in water and can dissolve the organic                amines, the organic extraction solvent is difficult                to dissolve in water, capable of dissolving the                organic amine, the carboxylic acid and the                carboxylic acid amine salt, the organic amine and                the carboxylic acid to obtain the carboxylic acid                amine salt, the organic amine and the carboxylic                amine salt are easily dissolved in the organic                extraction solvent and the carboxylic acid                magnesium obtained by the reaction of carboxylic                acid and magnesium hydroxide is easy to dissolve in                water.
   USE - The extraction solution is useful in preparing                active light calcium carbonate and co-producing                magnesium hydroxide and magnesium carbonate                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves the selective extraction                and separation of calcium and magnesium in dolomite                ash, and reduced production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing active light calcium carbonate                and co-producing high-purity magnesium hydroxide or                magnesium carbonate; and
   (2) preparing active light calcium carbonate                and co-producing high-purity magnesium hydroxide or                magnesium carbonate comprising (i) reacting                dolomite, water and carboxylic acid ammonium                salt/carboxylic acid amine salt, distilling                ammonia/amine to provide gas phase ammonia/organic                amine, magnesium hydroxide precipitation and                aqueous phase, aqueous calcium carboxylate, (ii)                precipitating and extracting the extraction                solution according and the magnesium hydroxide to                provide a aqueous phase and oil phase, the aqueous                phase is aqueous magnesium carboxylate solution,                and the oil phase includes the organic extraction                solvent and the organic amine, (iii) passing carbon                dioxide into the provided oil phase, mixing with                the provided calcium carboxylate aqueous solution                to provide calcium carbonate precipitation, aqueous                phase and oil phase, the oil phase including                extraction solution, (iv) (a) passing the gas phase                ammonia/organic amine provided in step (i) into the                magnesium carboxylate aqueous solution provided in                step (ii) and reacting to provide magnesium                hydroxide precipitation and carboxylic acid                ammonium salt/carboxylic acid amine salt, (iv) (b)                passing the gas phase ammonia/organic amine and CO2                provided in step (i) into the water to provide                ammonium carbonate/amine carbonate solution, mixing                and reacting with the magnesium carboxylate aqueous                solution provided in step (ii) to provide magnesium                carbonate and ammonium carboxylate/amine                carboxylate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic extraction                  solvent has density of less than the density of                  water; the organic extraction solvent has 10-90%                  of the mass of the extraction solution, the                  organic extraction solvent includes amides,                  ethers, alcohols, esters and/or ketones; the                  amount of organic amine is 5-80% of the mass of                  the extraction solution; the organic amine                  includes 6-24C organic amine; and the amount of                  carboxylic acid is 5-40% of the mass of the                  extraction solution. The organic amine includes                  di-n-propylamine, di-n-butylamine, n-hexylamine,                  di-n-hexylamine, diisooctylamine, isooctylamine,                  dodecylamine, stearylamine, tributylamine,                  trioctylamine and/or N,N-dimethyldodecylamine.                  The carboxylic acid includes formic acid, acetic                  acid, propionic acid and/or butyric acid. The                  mass ratio between white cloud ash and water is                  1:1-1:10; the molar ratio between calcium and                  carboxylic acid ammonium salt/carboxylic acid                  amine salt in the dolomite ash is 1:2-1:2.2; the                  reaction temperature is 30-130 degrees C;                  detecting the dissolution of calcium in the                  solution after the reaction, which accounts for                  more than 95 wt% of the total calcium in the                  dolomite ash; and the organic amine is a 1-6C                  organic amine with a boiling point lower than 130                  degrees C; and the calcium carboxylate formed by                  carboxylic acid and calcium ions is easily                  soluble in water. The organic amine includes                  n-propylamine, n-butylamine, diethylamine,                  n-pentylamine, piperidine, pyrrolidine,                  ethylenediamine and propylenediamine; and the                  carboxylic acid includes 1-4C organic acids. In                  step (ii), the molar ratio between carboxylic                  acid and magnesium hydroxide in the extraction                  solution is 1.9:1-2.2:1; the reaction temperature                  is room temperature to 120 degrees C; and the                  amount of magnesium dissolved in the solution is                  measured, which accounts for more than 90% the                  total amount of magnesium in the dolomite ash. In                  step (iii), the molar ratio between carbon                  dioxide and calcium is 1:1-1.5:1; the carbon                  dioxide gas includes pure carbon dioxide gas,                  kiln gas and flue gas; and method comprises                  drying the provided calcium carbonate precipitate                  to obtain active light calcium carbonate product.                  In step (iv), the molar ratio between nitrogen                  and magnesium in gas phase ammonia/organic amine                  is 1.9:1-2.5:1; the reaction temperature is room                  temperature to 100 degrees C; the pH of the                  aqueous magnesium carboxylate solution is 9.5-13;                  the reaction time is 0.5-4 hours; the magnesium                  hydroxide precipitate provided is washed and                  dried to obtain high purity magnesium hydroxide,                  the molar ratio between nitrogen and carbon                  dioxide in gas phase ammonia/organic amine is                  1.8:1-2.5:1; the molar ratio between ammonium                  carbonate/amine carbonate and magnesium is                  0.9:1-1.3:1; the reaction temperature is room                  temperature to 100 degrees C; the aqueous                  magnesium carboxylate solution has pH of 9-13.5;                  the method includes washing and drying the                  provided magnesium carbonate to obtain light                  magnesium carbonate, and adding into the ammonium                  carboxylate/amine carboxylate provided.

   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
GA 2020A3304L
AB    NOVELTY - Iron-based catalyst comprises iron, copper, potassium, rare earth metal (R) and silicon dioxide in a weight ratio of 100:0-10:0.1-9:0.1-18:5-50.
   USE - The iron-based catalyst is useful in Fischer-Tropsch synthesis for producing hydrocarbon compounds in synthesis gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The iron-based catalyst: has low pore content and excellent chemical stability; can meet long-term use in the process of syngas to hydrocarbons; and can reduce selectivity of carbon dioxide and methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Iron-based catalyst comprises iron, copper, potassium, rare earth metal (R) and silicon dioxide in a weight ratio of 100:0-10:0.1-9:0.1-18:5-50.
   R = rare earth metal including lanthanum, cerium, praseodymium, neodymium, ytterbium, samarium, gadolinium or erbium.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing iron-based catalyst, comprising (i) co-precipitation reaction of aqueous solution containing iron salt, copper salt, and rare earth metal salt with precipitating agent to obtain precipitation slurry and filtering to obtain first filter cake, (ii) mixing the first filter cake, silicon source, inorganic acid and water for aging to obtain aged slurry, and quantitatively adding the obtained slurry with water, filtering, and stirring to obtain second filter cake, (iii) mixing second filter cake, water and potassium salt to obtain catalyst slurry, spray-drying the catalyst slurry and then baking to obtain semi-finished catalyst product, where the weight ratio between iron and potassium in catalyst slurry is 100:10-20, and (iv) contacting the semi-finished product of the catalyst with water and/or ammonium salt to remove excess alkali metal elements, and then filtering and drying to obtain final product; and
   (2) use of iron-based catalyst, comprising (a) carrying out reduction reaction of iron-based catalyst at 180-280 degrees C for 1-48 hours and 0.01-2.8 MPa gauge pressure in reducing atmosphere to obtain reduced activated catalyst, (b) heating the synthesis gas containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen at a temperature of 210-290 degrees C in presence of the reduced activated catalyst, carrying out Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction under a gauge pressure of 1.2-2.8 MPa, where the molar ratio where hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the synthesis gas is 0.5-3.5, and the space velocity of the synthesis gas is 2-14 NL/g-cath.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the inorganic acid is added in parallel flow, the addition time is 30-120 minutes, and the pH value at the end of the addition is 4.5-6.5. Preferred Components: The weight ratio between iron, copper, potassium, rare earth metal (R) and silicon dioxide is 100:0-7:2-7:3-15:5-30. The weight ratio between potassium and rare earth metal is 1:0.4-3, preferably 1:1-3. The specific surface area of the catalyst is 50-200 m2/g, and the pore volume is 0.3-0.7 cm3/g, preferably the proportion of the specific surface area contributed by the pores with a pore diameter of 1-3.5 nm in the catalyst is 10-25%, the pores with a pore diameter of 3.5-11.5 nm contribute 35-50% of the specific surface area, and the pores with a pore diameter of 11.5-100 nm contribute 30-45% of the specific surface area based on the total specific surface area of pores with a pore diameter of 1-100 nm. The iron salt and copper salt are iron and copper nitrate, chloride, carbonate, bicarbonate, ammonium salt, sulfate, organic salt and hydroxide, preferably nitrate; the silicon source is potassium silicate and/or silica sol, the inorganic acid is nitric acid, carbonic acid and/or sulfuric acid, preferably nitric acid, the potassium salt is potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, potassium nitrate, potassium silicate and/or potassium-containing silica sol, preferably potassium nitrate, and the ammonium salt is ammonium carbonate, ammonium bicarbonate and/or ammonium nitrate. The precipitating agent is sodium carbonate, ammonia water, potassium carbonate, ammonium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, potassium silicate and/or silica sol.

TI Aerated sugar-free lactic acid bacteria flavored beverage comprises erythritol, skim milk powder, dietary fiber, whey fermentation liquid, soluble soybean polysaccharide, sweetener, acidulant, buffer salt, potassium sorbate and flavor.
AU ZHANG L
   ZHANG Y
   YU S
   TIAN H
   YANG Z
AE PINGDINGSHAN JINJING BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLO (PING-Non-standard)
GA 2020A15585
AB    NOVELTY - Aerated sugar-free lactic acid bacteria flavored beverage comprises 1-10 %mass erythritol, 0.1-1 %mass skim milk powder, 1-3 %mass dietary fiber, 0.1-1 %mass whey fermentation liquid, 0.2-1 %mass soluble soybean polysaccharide, 0.01-0.1 %mass sweetener, 0.1-0.3 %mass acidulant, 0.01-0.3 %mass buffer salt, 0.01-0.05 %mass potassium sorbate, 0.02-0.2 %mass flavor and remaining of water.
   USE - As aerated sugar-free lactic acid bacteria flavored beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage does not include white granulated sugar, adopts soluble soybean polysaccharide as low-viscosity stabilizer, can protect protein by matching or not matching with pectin, ensures the stability of product after being aerated and carbonated, does not increase the viscosity of product, and achieves excellent sugar-free effect, avoids precipitation and has refreshing taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the beverage, involving (a) uniformly stirring skim milk powder for 15 minutes with hot water at 60-70 degrees C, uniformly dispersing and dissolving, leaving still at 45-55 degrees C for 30 minutes, homogenizing at 200 bar/60-66 degrees C, and cooling to less than 25 degrees C to obtain solution (A), (b) dry mixing erythritol, dietary fiber, soluble soybean polysaccharide and pectin, adding 60-70 degrees C hot water, uniformly stirring for 20 minutes, completely dispersing and dissolving, and cooling to less than 25 degrees C to obtain solution (B), (c) completely dissolving sweetener, acidulant and buffer salt in water at room temperature, and cooling to less than 20 degrees C to obtain solution (C), (d) pouring obtained solution (A) into solution (B) under stirring state, uniformly stirring, and dripping whey fermentation liquid and solution (C) into solution (B) in stirring state to obtain solution (D), (e) dissolving potassium sorbate in small amount of water, and adding solution (D) to obtain stock solution, (f) accurately determining obtained stock solution, adding flavor, homogenizing at 200 bar/60-65 degrees C, sterilizing at 95 degrees C for 10 minutes, and cooling the mixture to 6 degrees C to obtain constant volume liquid, and (g) aerating carbon dioxide gas into the obtained constant volume liquid and sealing to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The dietary fiber comprises polydextrose, resistant dextrin, inulin and/or fructooligosaccharide. The sweetener comprises aspartame, acesulfame potassium, sucralose, steviol glycoside, cyclamate, neotame and/or mogroside. The acidulant comprises citric acid, lactic acid, malic acid and/or ascorbic acid. The buffer salt comprises sodium citrate and/or phosphate. The beverage further comprises 0.01-0.2 %mass pectin.

AU PARK J
AE DAEYOUNG IND CO LTD (DAEY-Non-standard)
GA 202090745W
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing urethane foam waterproof mortar comprises preparing a prepolymer urethane A agent comprising 32 wt.% toluene diisocyanate (TDI) and 68 wt.% polyol having a molecular weight of 1,000 to 5,000 g/mol, taking 35 wt.% polybasic polyol mixture, 55 wt.% filler, 2.0 wt.% amines, 5.0 wt.% pigment, and preparing a urethane B agent in which 3.0 wt.% of an additive, where an antifoaming agent, a surface strengthening agent, a self-leveling agent, and an ultraviolet ray inhibitor are are uniformly mixed. The composite urethane is prepared by mixing the urethane A agent, the urethane B agent, and the zeolite catalyst in a ratio of 1:1:1. The prepared composite urethane is prepared at 120 to 140 degrees C for 5 to 10 hours. The heated composite urethane is cooled and dried in a vacuum chamber for 24 hours.
   USE - Method for manufacturing urethane foam waterproof mortar.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to manufacture urethane foam waterproof mortar, which reduce management and repair costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing urethane foam waterproof mortar comprises preparing a prepolymer urethane A agent comprising 32 wt.% toluene diisocyanate (TDI) and 68 wt.% polyol having a molecular weight of 1,000 to 5,000 g/mol, taking 35 wt.% polybasic polyol mixture, 55 wt.% filler, 2.0 wt.% amines, 5.0 wt.% pigment, and preparing a urethane B agent in which 3.0 wt.% of an additive, where an antifoaming agent, a surface strengthening agent, a self-leveling agent, and an ultraviolet ray inhibitor are are uniformly mixed. The composite urethane is prepared by mixing the urethane A agent, the urethane B agent, and the zeolite catalyst in a ratio of 1:1:1. The prepared composite urethane is prepared at 120 to 140 degrees C for 5 to 10 hours. The heated composite urethane is cooled and dried in a vacuum chamber for 24 hours. The amount of 40 to 60 wt.% of the prepared composite urethane, 10 to 20 wt.% silicone oil, 1 to 10 wt.% boron nitride, and 10 to 30 wt.% carbon dioxide is mixed, and the urethane mixture is mixed by transmitting vibration using an ultrasonic vibrator. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for foam waterproof construction member, where the molding is achieved by spraying the urethane foam waterproof mortar.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The mixture composition of 30 to 50 wt.% a thermosetting unsaturated polyester resin, 10 to 30 wt.% urea resin, 10 to 30 wt.% alkoxysilane, and 1 to 10 wt.% sorbitan olivate are used as an organic gelling agent.

PN WO2020181233-A1; US2022175595-A1
TI Wheelchair for improving muscular skeletal system alignment of users, has hand mechanism assembly whose actuation of first arm drive-wheel by upper extremity is configured to actuate first leg drive-wheel of foot mechanism assembly.
AU SWERDLOW L S
   ZIEGER H
AE ALIGNED DESIGNED LLC (ALIG-Non-standard)
   ALIGNED DESIGNED LLC (ALIG-Non-standard)
GA 202090153P
AB    NOVELTY - The wheelchair (500) has a seat assembly (502) that comprises a seat and a backrest, and two ground-engaging wheels. A hub assembly includes a first drive-wheel and a first chain, a second drive-wheel and a second chain. The first drive-wheel is coupled to the second drive-wheel. A hand mechanism assembly (508) includes a first arm drive-wheel that is coupled to the first drive-wheel through the first chain. A foot mechanism assembly (510) includes a first leg drive-wheel that is coupled to the second drive-wheel through the second chain. The first leg drive-wheel is configured to impart motion of a first lower extremity of the user on a side opposite the first upper extremity. The actuation of the first arm drive-wheel of hand mechanism assembly by the upper extremity actuates the first leg drive-wheel of the foot mechanism assembly to impart the motion on the first lower extremity. The ground-engaging wheels are rotated, which results in contralateral locomotion.
   USE - Wheelchair for improving muscular skeletal system alignment of users.
   ADVANTAGE - The locomotion keeps the joints aligned inside the body natural base of support (BOS), also keeps the skeletal muscles and facia strong and flexible. The locomotion helps the body to produce synovial fluid, which serves to lubricate the joints, absorb shock, reduce friction, bring essential nutrients to the joints, and remove carbon dioxide and metabolic waste from the joints. The lateral outside edges of the seat is slightly raised for improved stability of the user and for improved comfort. The optional backrest is shaped and sized to minimize interference with movement of the user arms during locomotion, which enables a more naturally aligned arm swing during actuation. The armrests are positioned around a torso of the user to help maintain the user torso in a desired orientation and position during locomotion, which enables the user elbows to swing backward, while enabling continued alignment between the user shoulder girdles and the user arms.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of wheelchair.
   Wheelchair (500)
   Seat assembly (502)
   Hand mechanism assembly (508)
   Foot mechanism assembly (510)
   Foot rest assembly (560)

PN US2020269196-A1; WO2020176586-A1; US11007492-B2; CN113631247-A; EP3924089-A1; KR2021129701-A
TI Membrane for separating components of sour natural            gas feed, comprises distinct moieties polymerized            together comprising            2,2-bis(3,4-dicarboxyphenyl)hexafluoropropane            dianhydride based moiety,            2,4,6-trimethyl-m-phenylenediamine based moiety.
AU YAHAYA G O
   HAYEK A
   ALSAMAH A
   BAHAMDAN A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202082695L
AB    NOVELTY - Membrane comprises three or more distinct                moieties polymerized together, where the moieties                includes a 2,2                -bis(3,4-dicarboxyphenyl)hexafluoropropane                dianhydride (6FDA) based moiety, a                2,4,6-trimethyl-m-phenylenediamine (DAM) based                moiety, and component/s selected from a 4,4                -(hexafluoroisopropylidene)dianiline (6FpDA) based                moiety, a 9,9-bis(4-aminophenyl) fluorene (CARDO)                based moiety, a                2,3,5,6-tetramethyl-1,4-phenylenediamine (durene                diamine) based moiety, a 2,2                -bis(trifluoromethyl)benzidine (ABL-21) based                moiety, a 3,3 -dihydroxybenzidine based moiety, and                a 3,3 -(hexafluoroisopropylidene)dianiline based                moiety.
   USE - Membrane for separating components of sour                natural gas feed (claimed), particularly for CO2                removal from gaseous streams.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane comprising aromatic block                co-polyimides frequently give superior efficiency,                productivity, selectivity, permeability, and                resistance to penetrant-induced plasticization                compared to cellulose acetate, which is presently                the industrial standard membrane material for                carbon dioxide separations. The membrane structures                made from co-polyimides can withstand deterioration                due to the wet conditions often found with natural                gas streams. The membranes are promising materials                for natural gas separations, particularly with sour                gas, due to their chemical, thermal, and mechanical                stability. Membrane-based natural gas separations                are advantageous due to their lower capital cost,                higher energy savings, smaller size, being                eco-friendly, and being more economically viable as                compared to conventional technologies such as                stand-alone pressure swing adsorption (PSA) and                stand-alone absorption processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method of gas separation, which involves                applying the membrane to separate two or more                components of a mixed gas stream; and
   (2) a method for preparing membrane for                separating components of a sour natural gas feed,                the method comprising the steps of: combining three                or more different monomers to form a co-polyimide,                where the monomers includes 2,2                -bis(3,4-dicarboxyphenyl)hexafluoropropane                dianhydride, 2,4,6-trimethyl-m-phenylenediamine                (DAM) and component/s selected from 4,4                -(hexafluoroisopropylidene)dianiline (6FpDA),                9,9-bis(4-aminophenyl) fluorene (CARDO),                2,3,5,6-tetramethyl-1,4-phenylenediamine (durene                diamine), 2,2 -bis(trifluoromethyl)benzidine                (ABL-21), 3,3 -dihydroxybenzidine, and 3,3                -(hexafluoroisopropylidene)dianiline; and preparing                a dense film from the co-polyimide using a solution                casting process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The membrane comprises                  random co-polymers, preferably block co-polymers                  comprising 6FpDA based moiety, or CARDO based                  moiety, ABL-21 based moiety. The mixed gas stream                  comprises carbon dioxide, methane, nitrogen,                  ethane, and hydrogen sulfide. Preferred                  Conditions: The molar ratio of 6FpDA based moiety                  to DAM based moiety is 1:3-3:1. The molar ratio                  of CARDO based moiety to DAM based moiety is                  1:3-3:1. The molar ratio of ABL-21 based moiety                  to DAM based moiety is 1:3-3:1. The block                  co-polymers includes a polymer block of length L                  of the 6FDA and the DAM based moieties, and                  includes a polymer block of length M of the 6FDA                  and the 6FpDA based moieties, where block length                  ratio of L to M is (1,000-20,000) to                  (1,000-20,000), or includes a polymer block of                  length L of the 6FDA and the DAM based moieties,                  and includes a polymer block of length M of the                  6FDA and the CARDO based moieties, where block                  length ratio of L to M is (1,000-20,000) to                  (1,000-20,000), or includes a polymer block of                  length L of the 6FDA and the DAM based moieties,                  and includes a polymer block of length M of the                  6FDA and the ABL-21 based moieties, where block                  length ratio of L to M is (1,000-20,000) to                  (1,000-20,000). The feed pressure of the mixed                  gas stream to a feed side of the membrane is up                  to about 800 pound per square inch gauge and                  hydrogen sulfide content of the mixed gas stream                  is up to about 20 volume percent. The method for                  preparing membrane further comprises the step of                  using the dense film to separate components of a                  gas stream. The step of combining is carried out                  to create random co-polymers, preferably block                  co-polymers and includes combining the 6FDA, the                  DAM, and the 6FpDA, preferably 6FDA, DAM, and                  CARDO, more preferably 6FDA, DAM, and ABL-21. The                  molar ratio of the DAM to 6FpDA is 1:3 to 3:1.                  The molar ratio of the DAM to CARDO is 1:3 to                  3:1. The molar ratio of DAM to ABL-21 is 1:3 to                  3:1.

PN WO2020142472-A1; CN113260595-A; KR2021111251-A; EP3877337-A1; US2021300778-A1; JP2022518113-W
TI Nano-crystal cerium-zirconium mixed oxide material for particulate filter in flow-through three-way catalytic converter, comprises zirconium oxide, cerium oxide and rare earth metal chosen from lanthanum, neodymium, praseodymium, or yttrium.
AU BORTUN A
   SHEPARD D
   CHO J
   BORTUN M
   LI Y
   WU W
   LACHAPELLE J
   ZHAO J
   SHEPHERD D
AE PACIFIC IND DEV CORP (PACI-Non-standard)
   PACIFIC IND DEV CORP (PACI-Non-standard)
   PACIFIC IND DEV CORP (PACI-Non-standard)
   PACIFIC IND DEV CORP (PACI-Non-standard)
GA 202061912G
AB    NOVELTY - Nano-crystal cerium-zirconium mixed oxide material comprises at least 30 %mass zirconium oxide, 5-55 %mass cerium oxide and at least 25 %mass rare earth metal chosen from lanthanum, neodymium, praseodymium, or yttrium, where the nanocrystal-sized cerium-zirconium mixed oxide exhibits a hierarchically ordered aggregates having a D50 particle size less than 1.5 mu m, a specific surface area (SSA) and pore volume (PV) before and after aging at 1000 degrees C or higher for at least 6 hours, such that the SSA and PV after aging is 70% or more of the SSA and PV before aging.
   USE - The mixed oxide material is useful for particulate filter in flow-through three-way catalytic converter (TWC) application, gasoline particulate filter (GPF) application, or diesel particulate filter (DPF) application (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed oxide material enables the oxidation of carbon dioxide and volatile organics and reduction of nitrogen oxides, has high efficient catalytic performance to high specific surface area and thermal stability, and high oxygen storage capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for forming the nano-crystal cerium-zirconium mixed oxide material, involving (i) preparing an acidic solution containing polymerized zirconium oligomers, (ii) mixing an acidic solution of cerium and rare earth salt with the zirconium oligomer containing solution to make a polyvalent metal containing mixture, (iii) mixing the acidic polyvalent metal containing mixture with a solution of a complexing reagent, (iv) allowing the mixture formed in step (iii) to form a zirconium-based precursor slurry containing constituent metal hydroxides, (v) neutralizing the zirconium containing precursor slurry with a base to achieve co-precipitation of the constituent metal hydroxides and formation of a precipitated mixed oxide material, (vi) washing the precipitated mixed oxide material with water to remove unreacted cationic and anionic admixtures, (vii) collecting the washed and precipitated mixed oxide material, (viii) aging the washed and precipitated mixed oxide material at ambient temperature or at an elevated temperature for a predetermined time, (ix) drying the aged mixed oxide material and calcining to form the cerium-zirconium mixed oxide material, and (x) milling the calcined cerium-zirconium mixed oxide material to a particle size (D50) of less than 1.5 pm; and
   (2) a particulate filter comprising the nanocrystal-sized cerium-zirconium mixed oxide material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The oxide material comprises at least 40 %mass zirconium oxide. The polymerized zirconium oligomers comprise 30-100 %mass zirconium octamers. Preferred Properties: The hierarchically ordered aggregates have a particle size that is less than 500 nm. The hierarchically ordered aggregates consist of oxide crystallites having a size ranging from 5-20 nm. The SSA and PV of the mixed oxide material after aging is at least 80% of the SSA and PV before aging. The mixed oxide exhibits a pore size distribution that remains unchanged over the temperature range from 600-1100 degrees C. The mixed oxide exhibits facile oxygen storage mobility. Preferred Conditions: The milling of the calcined cerium-zirconium mixed oxide material yields D50 particle size of less than 500 nm. The amount of complexing reagent and the amount of zirconium in the acidic polyvalent metal containing mixture that form the zirconium-based precursor slurry are present such that the molar ratio of complexing agent to zirconium is in the range of 0.35-1.05. The nanocrystal-sized cerium-zirconium mixed oxide material has a D50 particle size that is less than 1.5 mhi and a specific surface area that is at least 40 m2/g after aging at 1000 degrees C for 5 hours. The nanocrystal-sized cerium-zirconium mixed oxide material is applied as a washcoat to a substrate in order to form the particulate filter. Preferred Components: The polymerized zirconium oligomers do not contain zirconia sol particles. The complexing reagent comprises anions that have an affinity towards zirconium. The complexing agent is chosen from sulfate, oxalate, succinate and/or fumarate. The polyvalent metal-containing mixture comprises water soluble nitrate, chloride, sulfate and/or acetate salt. The base is chosen from alkali metal hydroxide, aqueous ammonia or tetraalkylammonium hydroxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Nitriding three-layer steel pan comprises (i) processing drawn three-layer steel cookware product, polishing and sandblasting in a series to obtain three-layer steel cookware and placing on shelf, (ii) adding 1-2 kg ammonium chloride in the gas nitriding furnace, (iii) hanging the three-layer steel cookware into the furnace, covering the furnace cover, tightening the screws of the furnace cover and turning on the fan in the furnace, upper, middle and lower zones for heating, turning on the circulating water with the pressure in the furnace at 0.01 MPa, (iv) placing the resultant at 280 degrees C, warming for 60-90 minutes, decomposing the cookware absorbs the temperature and oxide film by ammonium chloride, the pressure is U-shaped tube of the water meter, the water pressure is 100, and opening the exhaust valve, (v) when the temperature rises to 400 degrees C, the ammonia gas is 0.5 cubic meters, and the constant temperature is 460-480 degrees C and the ammonia gas is 10 cubic meters, the carbon dioxide is 1.5, the water pressure is 120, and placing the temperature for 270 minutes.

   HAMMELMANN A
AE LEDWITH A (LEDW-Individual)
   LEDWITH A (LEDW-Individual)
   LEDWITH A (LEDW-Individual)
   LEDWITH A (LEDW-Individual)
GA 202045593A
AB    NOVELTY - The foundation system has a support stand (2)                that is attachable to a structural beam. The                support stand comprises a pedestal and a beam                carrier (7) mounted on the pedestal. The beam                carrier comprises a wingnut construction defining                vertical wing connectable with a structural beam.                The beam carrier is threadedly mounted on the                pedestal to define a beam carrier height adjustment                mechanism on the support stand. The beam carrier is                threadedly mounted on the pedestal at an insert                arranged between the beam carrier and the pedestal.                The support stand comprises nylon (RTM: synthetic                polymer).
   USE - Foundation system for constructing domestic,                industrial and commercial buildings such as houses.                Uses includes but are not limited to data centers,                pharmaceutical facilities, hospitals, hotels,                retail, drive through restaurants, pods, garages,                decks, stages, bridges, playground                structures.
   ADVANTAGE - The foundation system is a fast, strong, green                and a cost-effective construction method that                significantly reduces construction lead times and                is up to 8 times faster than traditional cement                foundations and reduces carbon dioxide (CO2)                emissions by up to over 90% in buildings bases by                eliminating concrete requirements. The foundation                system is not weather dependent unlike construction                methods employing concrete foundations, saves up to                25% of overall build costs, ensures completion in                up to 80% less time as a traditional build, and                improves environmental impact by cleaner                efficiencies as no water is required and                critically. The energy requirements are reduced                which cement production requires temperatures of                1000 degrees C while the support stand, and                structural beams and other elements of the                foundation system are manufactured with                comparatively minimal manufacturing energy                requirements. The traditional construction methods                use vast amounts of stone aggregates and cement                which adds to the environmental impact whereas the                foundation system greatly reduces the volume of                materials required by a factor of 3 times per                average build. The height change difference per                revolution is reduced while mechanical or lifting                power is increased. The foundation system                facilitates quiet construction while the engineered                ground requires minimal vibrations so risk of                structural damage is greatly mitigated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for constructing the foundation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the                support stand.
   Support stand (2)
   Beam carrier (7)
   Base (9)
   Wing (19)
   Tool access hole (28)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The support stand comprises a composite                  polymer material.

TI System for converting heat energy into mechanical power, comprises a combustion chamber, outlet of which is connected to inlet of a gas turbine of a main gas turbocharger, and the outlet of the gas turbine is connected to second gas turbine.
AU NIKOLA K
   KOLEV N T
AE NIKOLA K (NIKO-Individual)
   NIKOLA K (NIKO-Individual)

GA 202020711S
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a combustion chamber, outlet of which is connected to the inlet of a gas turbine of a main gas turbocharger, and the outlet of the gas turbine of the main gas turbocharger is connected to a second gas turbine, as the outlet of the centrifugal compressor of the main gas turbocharger is connected to a mechanical module, the coupling of the centrifugal compressor (2) to the mechanical module (17) which is incorporated as a cylinder block, is carried out via successively connected a first pressure transducer (20), a fourth valve (19), an intake manifold (16) and corresponding branching (18) to the volume of each cylinder (27) of the cylinder block and the outlet of each cylinder is connected to an exhaust manifold (21). The outlet of the exhaust manifold is connected via a second pressure transducer (22) and via a fifth valve (23) to the atmosphere, where the outlet of the exhaust manifold is also connected to an internal pipe (4) of an ejector (3).
   USE - System for converting heat energy into mechanical power for systems that consume power produced by carbon fuels combustion used both in the reconstruction of the existing internal combustion engines as well as in the manufacture of new power systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has reduced fuel consumption, low carbon dioxide emissions without toxic waste products due to the complete oxidation of the fuel in a permanent combustion process at efficient carburation with a high amount of oxygen, and has high efficiency which is achieved by applying efficient units and equipment used to convert heat energy into mechanical power through the most efficient thermodynamic processes carried out in the system at low temperature and pressure of the energy carrier, i.e. the compressed air. The increase in efficiency is also due to the removal of units and equipment that are not needed for the system, such as cooling, air-fuel mixture distribution and fuel injection equipment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for converting heat energy into mechanical power.

   Fourth valve (19)
   First pressure transducer (20)
   Exhaust manifold (21)
   Second pressure transducer (22)

TI Phosphogypsum-based self-generating gas-expanding slurry fire-extinguishing material used in industrial waste slag, comprises phosphogypsum, hydroxyethyl cellulose, sodium methyl allyl sulfonate, sodium bicarbonate, and micro silicon powder.
AU LU Y
   FANG X
   WU F
   YAN Z
   GU W
AE UNIV HUNAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHC-C)
GA 2020195147
AB    NOVELTY - Phosphogypsum-based self-generating gas-expanding slurry fire-extinguishing material comprises 55-60 pts. wt. phosphogypsum with a pH of 2-3, 25-30 pts. wt. water, 8-9 pts. wt. cement, 0.07-0.18 pt. wt. polyoxyethylene alkylamine, 0.7-0.9 pt. wt. hydroxyethyl cellulose, 0.22 pt. wt. acrylic acid, 0.39 pt. wt. sodium methyl allyl sulfonate, 0.01 pt. wt. ammonia persulfate, 3.4-4.0 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate, and 0.3 pt. wt. micro silicon powder.
   USE - The phosphogypsum used in industrial waste slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The slurry has excellent fluidity and high compressive strength, and achieves reuse of resources and the effect of preventing fire extinguishing. The carbon dioxide generated by the reaction expands the slurry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDNT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the phosphogypsum-based self-producing gas-expanding slurry fire-extinguishing material comprising (a) carrying out pH value detection on phosphogypsum generated by wet method phosphoric acid, selecting phosphogypsum with a pH value of 2-3, and carrying out preliminary screening on the selected phosphogypsum to remove solid impurities, putting the phosphogypsum into an electric heating blast drying box, carrying out drying treatment for 30 minutes at 100 degrees C, grinding the dried phosphogypsum in a dry grinding mode by using a cement ball mill, and enabling the particle size of the phosphogypsum to be 20-30 microns, (b) adding water, polyoxyethylene alkyl amine and acrylic acid into a slurry stirrer, setting the rotating speed of 1500 rpm, stirring for 3 minutes, and completing the stirring to form a mixed solution, (c) adding phosphogypsum, cement, sodium bicarbonate, sodium methyl allyl sulfonate, ammonium persulfate, micro-silica powder, hydroxyethyl cellulose after the step (a) into the dry mixing stirrer, setting the rotating speed of 1500 rpm, stirring for 3 minutes, and uniformly stirring and mixing to form a mixed powder, and (d) mixing the mixed powder formed in the step (c) into a slurry stirrer to be mixed with a mixed solution formed in the step (b), setting the rotating speed to be 2000 rpm, stirring for 5 minutes, and uniformly stirring and mixing to form an intumescent slurry anti-extinguishing material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The material comprises 55 pts. wt. phosphogypsum, 30 pts. wt. water, 9 pts. wt. cement, 0.18 pt. wt. polyoxyethylene alkylamine, 0.9 pt. wt. hydroxyethyl cellulose, 4.0 pt. wt. sodium bicarbonate. Preferred Components: The cement is ordinary portland cement.

TI Extracting exosomes from a cell supernatant based on a precipitation reagent method involves placing the cells to be exosomes extracted into the culture medium, and adhering to the culture under the aseptic conditions.
AU ZHAO K
AE ZHAO K (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 2020116826
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting exosomes from a cell supernatant based on a precipitation reagent method involves placing the cells to be exosomes extracted into the culture medium, and adhering to the culture for 70 to 72 days under the aseptic conditions of 36 to 38 degrees C and 3 to 6% carbon dioxide. 0.5 to 1.0 mL of the supernatant is taken and centrifuged at 2 to 6 degrees C and 1500 to 2000 x g for 10 to 20 min, discarding the bottom sediment after centrifugation to obtain the cell supernatant. The cell supernatant is passed through a 0.22 mu m filter to obtain a filtered supernatant. The filtered supernatant is shaken with 10 to 40% PEG solution at a volume ratio of 1: 4 to 8. After standing at 2-6 degrees C for 10-12 hours, centrifugation is performed at 2500-3000 x g for 20-30 minutes, discarding the supernatant after centrifugation, and obtaining a bottom precipitate by the inverted absorption paper method. 5 mL to 7.5 mL of PBS solution (pH 7.2 to 7.4) is added to the bottom precipitate.
   USE - Method for extracting exosomes from a cell supernatant based on a precipitation reagent method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract exosomes from a cell supernatant based on a precipitation reagent method, which removes impurities of large molecules through a filter membrane, and improves the purity of exosomes in the supernatant, streptavidin precipitant binds to the exosomes with higher purity after resuspension, and the specific magnetic probe in the streptavidin precipitant binds to the exosomes and precipitate is obtained by centrifugation, and then the low-abundance protein is removed to further improve the purity of exosomes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - After resuspension, adding 0.5 to 1.0 mL of streptavidin precipitant at 6.0 to 10.0 mg / mL, shaking, and leaving to stand at 2 to 8 degrees C for 20 to 30 minutes. The liquid is centrifuged at 2500-3000 x g for 20-30min under 2-8 degrees C and aseptic conditions. The supernatant is discarded and the magnetic beads are removed with a magnetic stand. The resulting precipitate is the desired exosomes. 0.5 to 1.0 mL of a PBS solution (pH 7.2 to 7.4) is added to the exosomes, resuspended, and put at -70 to -80 degrees C to refrigerate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The medium is one of RPMI1640 medium and DMEM medium. The PEG in S4 is selected from PEG6000 and PEG8000. The PEG concentration is selected from 10 to 40%. The inverted absorption paper method involves inverting centrifugal tube from which the supernatant after centrifugation on an absorbent paper, and air-drying for 5-10 minutes under sterile conditions at 2-8 degrees C, adding 0.5 to 1.0 mL of a mixed solution of ethanol and ether (1: 1, V / V) to the bottom precipitate after air drying. After shaking, centrifuged at 2 to 6 degrees C and 1500 to 2000 x g for 10 to 20 minutes and discarding the supernatant after centrifugation. The centrifugal tube after centrifugation is inverted on the absorbent paper again, and air-dried under the aseptic condition of 2-8 degrees C for 5-10 minutes to obtain a bottom sediment.

TI Blue-green light-emitting fluorescent transparent            ceramic, prepared by using oxides containing e.g.            calcium, cerium, scandium, silicon, potassium or their            corresponding salts, and grinding to obtain mixed            powders, and placing into mold.

   XIAO W
   ZHANG X
   WU H
   PAN G
   ZHANG L
   HAO Z
AE CHANGCHUN INST OPTICS FINE MECHANICS & P (CAIO-C)
GA 202004058N
AB    NOVELTY - Blue-green light-emitting fluorescent                transparent ceramic having chemical formula of                (Ca1-x-yCexRy)3 (Sc2-zLz)2 (Si1-mKm)3O12 (I), is                claimed.
   USE - Used as blue-green light-emitting fluorescent                transparent ceramic.
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramic: can exciting by blue light.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Blue-green light-emitting fluorescent                transparent ceramic having chemical formula of                (Ca1-x-yCexRy)3 (Sc2-zLz)2 (Si1-mKm)3O12 (I), is                claimed.
   R = yttrium, gadolinium, lanthanum, lutetium,                terbium, lithium, and/or sodium;
   L = zirconium, hafnium, and/or                magnesium;
   K = aluminum and/or phosphorus;
   x, y, z, and m = elemental mole                fractions;
   x = 0.0002-0.02;
   y = 0-0.02;
   z = 0-0.03;
   m = 0-0.02; and
   y+z+m = 0-0.05.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing blue-green light-emitting fluorescent                transparent ceramic comprising (i) in the                stoichiometric ratio of each element in the general                formula (I), using oxides containing calcium,                cerium, yttrium, gadolinium, lanthanum, lutetium,                terbium, lithium, and/or sodium, scandium,                zirconium, hafnium, and/or magnesium, silicon,                potassium or their corresponding salts, and                grinding them to obtain mixed powders, (ii) placing                mixed powder into a mold to dry-press into a sheet                shape, and annealing sheet in a high-temperature                furnace to obtain a sheet-like body, (iii) placing                a sheet-like blank sample in an air suspension                furnace equipping with a dual-beam carbon dioxide                laser, and using high-purity oxygen as a carrier                gas to suspending and melting the sample,                maintaining the sample in the molten state for                20-40 seconds, obtaining glass spheres of the                corresponding composition, placing smelted glass                spheres into de-ionized water, and let the glass                spheres burst into small fragments due to rapid                cooling, (iv) drying a few small pieces and placing                them into a clean ball mill tank, using ethanol as                a ball milling medium, drying and sieving to obtain                a vitreous micropowder precursor, dry-pressing the                precursor powder, and performing cold isostatic                pressing to obtain a ceramic body, (v) placing                ceramic body into a vacuum furnace to crystallize                at high temperature, maintaining at 900-950 degrees                C for 3-6 hours, and then heating to 1400-1500                degrees C for 10-60 hours to obtain ceramics, and                (vi) polishing ceramic to obtain a fluorescent                transparent ceramic with blue-green light                emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The blue-green                  light-emitting fluorescent transparent ceramic                  has a garnet crystal structure, belongs to a                  cubic crystal system, and the space group is                  Ia3d. The blue-green light-emitting fluorescent                  transparent ceramic emits 505 nm green light                  under the excitation of blue light of 410-470 nm.                  The thickness of the blue-green light-emitting                  fluorescent transparent ceramic is 0.1-2 mm. In                  the step (i), the grinding method is manual                  grinding or ball milling. In the ball milling,                  using ethanol as ball milling medium, zirconium                  dioxide, corundum or agate balls and zirconium                  dioxide or corundum ball milling tank, are using                  for ball milling and mixing for 0.5-5 hours,                  after mixing powder, it is taking out, and then                  drying and sieving to obtain a mixed powder. In                  the step (ii), the annealing temperature is                  1000-1100 degrees C, and the annealing time is                  4-6 hours. In the step (iv), the particle size is                  0.2 mu m. In the step (iv), the pressure of the                  cold isostatic treatment is 100-300 MPa.

TI Aerobic reactor of cow manure processing apparatus            (claimed) used for treating cow manure waste water, has            exhaust pipe that is configured to discharge air and            treated water and to remove bubbles generated in            reactor.
AU RHU D
   JUNG M
   JANG J
   RHU D H
   JUNG M K
   JANG J G
AE BKT CO LTD (BKTB-Non-standard)
   BKT CO LTD (BKTB-Non-standard)
   BKT CO LTD (BKTB-Non-standard)
   BKT CO LTD (BKTB-Non-standard)
GA 2019A8481X
AB    NOVELTY - The aerobic reactor (430) has an air supply                unit that is configured for supplying air. A                vertical membrane is configured to prevent the                outflow of raw water. An exhaust pipe is configured                to discharge air and treated water and remove                bubbles generated in the reactor. An acid pipe is                provided to convert ammonium ions into ammonia by                supplying air to the aerobic reactor and degas the                carbon dioxide to raise the pH in the aerobic                reactor, such that calcium and phosphorus are                combined in the cow manure wastewater.
   USE - Aerobic reactor of cow manure processing                apparatus (claimed) used for treating cow manure                waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The organic matter components of cow manure is                converted to methane gas by using anaerobic                digestion and the fibrous component in the organic                material not converted to methane gas, is separated                to separate the separated fiber and recycled to the                rug of separated fibers. The aggregation and                precipitation characteristics of the fine solids                are improved by using the aerobic reaction tank.                Hence, the amount of chemicals required for                aggregation and precipitation is significantly                reduced. The pH of the fine solids and calcium and                phosphorus in the cow manure is increased to react                to form hydroxyapatite by using the aerobic                reaction tank, so that the phosphorus in the                lactation manure wastewater is effectively removed.                Thus 30 degrees or more of the ammonia nitrogen is                removed and the residence time of the subsequent                process is shortened. The cow manure can be reused                as a compost fine particles containing large amount                of calcium and phosphorus, by attaching air to the                fine solid particles by using flocculant and                coagulant adjuvant. The nitrogen components in the                effluent flowing into the farmland is reduced by                removing the nitrogen remaining in the cow manure                by using a uniaxial nitrogen removal reaction by                introducing the cow manure with micro-solids                removed to the Anamox reaction tank.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a Cow manure processing apparatus;                and
   (2) a method for treating cow manure                wastewater using cow manure processing                apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the cow                manure processing apparatus. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Cow manure processing apparatus (400)
   Anaerobic digestion tank (410)
   Fiber separation tank (420)
   Aerobic reactor (430)
   Flotation tank (440)

TI Forming inner-cooled screw hole piece material comprises e.g. mixing different raw material powders into a mixture, using polyethylene glycol as a molding agent, and grinding in a wet mill with ethanol as the wet grinding medium.
AU SUN H
   ZHANG H
   YAO H
   SI S
   LI C
   LIU X
   FU D
   LI X
   CHANG J
   WANG H
AE PENGLAI SUPERHARD COMPOSITE MATERIAL CO (PENG-Non-standard)
GA 2019980745
AB    NOVELTY - Forming inner-cooled screw hole piece material comprises e.g. mixing different raw material powders into a mixture, using polyethylene glycol as a molding agent, grinding in a wet mill with ethanol as the wet grinding medium, uniformly mixing the raw material powder, completely recycling the wet milling medium ethanol through volatilization, condensing, and grinding the mixed material after spray drying, spray drying by using nitrogen as protective gas, performing the whole process in a closed environment and the mixed material is not polluted, preparing the mixed material according to the shape size and the specification requirements of the bar, designing and manufacturing mold, pressing and forming by isostatic pressing molding process, then grinding by semi-finished product required for processing to obtain the bar blank, and taking hydrogen as carrier under the vacuum sintering furnace to the compact to removing molding agent PEG.
   USE - The method is useful for forming inner-cooled screw hole piece material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces inner-cooled screw hole piece, which has light weight, improves the coordination of dimensional accuracy and use performance; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Forming inner-cooled screw hole piece material comprises (i) mixing different raw material powders into a mixture, using polyethylene glycol (PEG) as a molding agent, grinding in a wet mill with ethanol as the wet grinding medium, uniformly mixing the raw material powder, completely recycling the wet milling medium ethanol through volatilization, condensing, and grinding the mixed material after spray drying, spray drying by using nitrogen as protective gas, performing the whole process in a closed environment and the mixed material is not polluted; (ii) preparing the mixed material according to the shape size and the specification requirements of the bar, designing and manufacturing mold, pressing and forming by isostatic pressing molding process, then grinding by semi-finished product required for processing to obtain the bar blank; and (iii) taking hydrogen as carrier under the vacuum sintering furnace to the compact to removing molding agent PEG, PEG hydrogen and steam by combustion is water vapor and carbon dioxide, and performing pressing and high temperature sintering in the sintering furnace by argon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The powder of raw material comprises superfine tungsten carbide powder and carbon monoxide powder. The mold comprises a forming cavity and a spiral pipe for shaping the hard alloy bar is screw with inner cooling hole. The hard alloy inner-diameter of the spiral hole bar is 4-40 mm, the length is 38-330 mm, helix angle precision is: greater than or equal to plus minus 0.5 degrees , the smallest aperture is 0.05mm, and eccentricity distance is greater than or equal to 0.2 mm.

PT P
PN US2019265082-A1; WO2019168855-A2; WO2019168855-A3; AU2019229185-A1; CA3087717-A1; EP3759937-A2
TI Sensor pod for detecting or providing information            about characteristics of environment, for use in crop            storage applications has three-dimensional round            enclosure comprising permeable area, allowing sensors            to detect environment.
AU ZAFAR N
   GOESSERINGER P M
   GARNER N W
   KUMAR P
   SANGHI A J
   GOESSERINGER P M J
AE TELESENSE INC (TELE-Non-standard)
   TELESENSE INC (TELE-Non-standard)
   TELESENSE INC (TELE-Non-standard)
   TELESENSE INC (TELE-Non-standard)
GA 201974450X
AB    NOVELTY - The sensor pod (705) has a circular or                approximately circular, three-dimensional round                enclosure (701) containing a power supply, a                memory, a processor, a radio receiver, a radio                transmitter, antenna and sensors. The sensors                comprise at least one of a temperature sensor, a                humidity sensor, an oxygen sensor, a carbon dioxide                sensor, a phosphine sensor, an acoustic sensor, an                ethylene sensor, a positioning sensor, an                accelerometer, and a pressure sensor. An on-off                switch is coupled to the power supply. The                three-dimensional round enclosure comprises a                permeable area, allowing the sensors to detect the                environment.
   USE - Sensor pod for detecting or providing                information about characteristics of an                environment. Can be used for monitoring, logging,                and/or acting on sensory data using data analytics                and a sensor structure uniquely well-suited to crop                storage applications, and in particular, grain                storage.
   ADVANTAGE - Improves ease of monitoring grain in storage,                reducing quality degradation of stored grain, and                optimizing decisions related to the purchase and                sale of grains. The three-dimensional round                enclosure increases the durability of the sensor                pod apparatus, making it more resistant to damage                caused by physical force. The three-dimensional                round enclosure also facilitates placement of the                sensor pod, by making it easy to roll or throw, and                facilitates retrieval of the sensor pod e.g.,                allowing it to roll during vacuum cleaning of a                grain storage bin. The sensor pod can transmit data                to a gateway over a local network, where it can be                forwarded to a cloud computing system and proxied                through a gateway for analysis. The sensor pods can                directly communicate with each other, for example,                over a meshing wireless network, thus expanding the                scope of the local wireless network. The sensor                pods can be programmed to enter a sleep mode, thus                saving power and extending the battery life of the                system. The sensor pods can be provided with                provisioning data over the local network, thus                allowing the user to control various settings for                each sensor pod.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of an                enclosed sensor unit in which a sensor pod is                enclosed in a ball-shaped three-dimensional round                enclosure.
   Enclosed sensor unit (700)
   Three-dimensional round enclosure                (701)
   Opening for sensor access (702)
   Openings for rolling the ball (703)
   Sensor pod (705)

PT P
PN EP3530301-A1; US2019262523-A1; CN110180044-A; US11110215-B2
TI Degasser manifold for use with degasser in dialysis system, has multiple passageways fluidly connectable to several inlets and several outlets in dialysis system.
AU GOMES C W
   GOMES G W
AE MEDTRONIC INC (MEDT-C)
   MEDTRONIC INC (MEDT-C)
   MEDTRONIC INC (MEDT-C)
GA 2019742112
AB    NOVELTY - The degasser manifold (601) has multiple passageways (606, 609, 612) fluidly connectable to several inlets and several outlets in a dialysis system. Several inlets comprise a first inlet fluidly connectable to a first fluid line. The first fluid line is fluidly connectable to a dialysate flow path, and a second inlet (608) is fluidly connectable to a second fluid line, that is fluidly connectable to an outlet of a degasser (602). Several outlets comprise a first outlet (605) fluidly connectable to an inlet (604) of the degasser and a second outlet (611) fluidly connectable to a third fluid line, which is fluidly connectable to the dialysate flow path downstream of the first fluid line.
   USE - Degasser manifold for use with degasser in dialysis system (claimed) such as sorbent based systems for conveying fluid, gas, or combination of fluid and gas, to and from degasser.
   ADVANTAGE - The vacuum that can be created by pulling the fluid through the degas restrictor helps to draw dissolved gases, including carbon dioxide, out of solution by reducing the pressure of the fluid below the partial pressure of the dissolved gas in the liquid. The parallel configuration provides control flexibility to adjust the degassing loop flow rate for optimal degassing without requiring the dialysate flow rate through the dialyser to change. A hydrophobic membrane prevents the fluid from escaping the degassing vessel through mechanical vent valve. The vacuum pump is protected from damage by liquid and prevents undesired loss of liquid from the system while still enabling gas to be removed. The configuration with degasser upstream of the microbial filter can provide for the removal of gas from the dialysate before reaching the microbial filter, thus reducing gas accumulation in the microbial filter. The gas flow restrictions is reduced out of the degassing vessel, allowing for a higher gas removal volume and faster headspace recovery, by reducing foaming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for removing gas generated from the breakdown of urea in the sorbent cartridge; and
   (2) a dialysis system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a degasser manifold for use in a degassing system.
   Degasser manifold (601)
   Degasser (602)
   Inlet (604)
   First outlet (605)
   First fluid passageway (606)
   Second inlet (608)
   Second fluid passageway (609)
   Second outlet (611)
   Third fluid passageway (612)
