UT DIIDW:2019742112
ER

PT P
PN CN110124492-A
TI Treating gas containing hydrogen sulfide comprises introducing a raw material gas containing hydrogen sulfide into the gas supply-gas distribution unit, mixing, performing hydrogen sulfide decomposition reaction, analyzing and recycling.
AU ZHANG J
   REN J
   WANG L
   ZHU Y
   SHI N
   ZHANG T
   XU W
   SUN F
   LI Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC QINGDAO SAFETY ENG INST (SNPC-C)
GA 201973589W
AB    NOVELTY - Treating gas containing hydrogen sulfide comprises (i) introducing a raw material gas containing hydrogen sulfide into the gas supply-gas distribution unit and mixing with a carrier gas to obtain a reactant; (ii) introducing the reactant into a plasma reactor of a plasma reaction unit and performing hydrogen sulfide decomposition reaction under dielectric barrier discharge; (iii) introducing the product in the plasma reaction unit into a gas-liquid separator of the product separation unit for gas-liquid separation, introducing the first gas phase material into the alkali liquid absorption tower to obtain a second gas phase substance and a hydrogen sulfide containing alkali liquid, introducing the second gas phase material into the carrier gas separator to separate the carrier gas and analyzing the hydrogen sulfide-containing alkali solution to obtain hydrogen sulfide gas; and (iv) recycling the hydrogen sulfide gas to the gas supply-gas distribution unit.
   USE - The method is useful for treating gas containing hydrogen sulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has higher hydrogen sulfide conversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating gas containing hydrogen sulfide comprises (i) introducing a raw material gas containing hydrogen sulfide into the gas supply-gas distribution unit and mixing with a carrier gas to obtain a reactant, where the carrier gas is nitrogen, hydrogen, helium, argon, water vapor, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, ethane or propane; (ii) introducing the reactant into a plasma reactor of a plasma reaction unit and performing hydrogen sulfide decomposition reaction under dielectric barrier discharge at a voltage of 5-30 kV, frequency of 200-30000 Hz and current of 0.1-100 A, where the ratio of a discharge air gap to a thickness of the barrier medium in the plasma reactor is 0.05-100:1 and the length of the discharge zone is 1-500 cm; (iii) introducing the product in the plasma reaction unit into a gas-liquid separator of the product separation unit for gas-liquid separation, introducing the first gas phase material into the alkali liquid absorption tower to obtain a second gas phase substance and a hydrogen sulfide containing alkali liquid, introducing the second gas phase material into the carrier gas separator to separate the carrier gas and analyzing the hydrogen sulfide-containing alkali solution to obtain hydrogen sulfide gas; and (iv) recycling the hydrogen sulfide gas to the gas supply-gas distribution unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier gas is hydrogen gas, argon gas, helium gas or nitrogen gas. The amount of the carrier gas is such that the content of hydrogen sulfide in the reactant is 0.01-100 vol.%, preferably 10-80 vol.%. The dielectric barrier discharge is carried out at a voltage of 5-20 kV, frequency of 500-15000 Hz and current of 0.5-10 A. The ratio of the discharge air gap to the thickness of the barrier medium in the plasma reactor is 0.1-30:1 and the length of the discharge region is 3-200 cm. The alkali absorption tower comprises an alkaline organic amine solvent, comprising monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, diisopropanolamine or N-methyldiethanolamine. The concentration of the alkaline solution is 2-80 wt.%. The raw material gas containing hydrogen sulfide is hydrogen sulfide-containing tail gas from petroleum refining and petroleum refining process and natural gas is accompanied by hydrogen sulfide, the coal cleaning process contains hydrogen sulfide tail gas or hydrogen sulfide containing waste gas generated during mineral processing, preferably the raw material gas containing hydrogen sulfide is hydrogen sulfide-containing tail gas from petroleum refining, petroleum refining process or hydrogen sulfide associated with natural gas. Preferred Method: The hydrogen sulfide decomposition reaction is carried out at 0-800 degrees C, preferably (40-500 degrees C) at a pressure of -0.06 to 0.6 MPa (preferably -0.06 to 0.3 MPa) with the average residence time of the reactant in the plasma reactor of 1x 10-5-120 seconds (preferably 2x 10-5-60 seconds). The method further comprises introducing the first vapor phase material into a particulate purifier of the product separation unit to separate residual sulfur solids, introducing the first vapor phase material into the alkali liquid absorption tower and introducing the gaseous substance into the lysate absorption column and subjecting the hydrogen sulfide-containing feed gas to hydrogen sulfide-rich treatment and introducing the hydrogen sulfide-containing feed gas into the gas supply-gas distribution unit. In the step (iii), the analysis is carried out at 70-180 degrees C at a pressure of 0.01-0.2 MPa. The hydrogen sulfide-rich treatment comprises contacting the hydrogen sulfide-containing raw material gas with the amine liquid to be adsorbed and analyzing the amine solution to which hydrogen sulfide gas has been adsorbed.

Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. AB    NOVELTY - Casing comprises 30-40 wt.% 200-400 mesh animal endothelium, 10-15 wt.% fleece-flower root, 15-20 wt.% swim bladder, 4-8 wt.% sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, 5-10 wt.% sweet potato powder, 2-3 wt.% D-isoascorbic acid sodium salt, 1-1.5 wt.% alliin, 1-2 wt.% medlar polysaccharides, 3-6 wt.% polyoxyethylene sorbitan fatty acid ester, 0.5-1.5 wt.% neryl acetate, 0.5-1 wt.% citral, 1-3 wt.% alpha -tocopherol, 1-2 wt.% isoascorbic acid, 1-1.5 wt.% adipic acid and 2,3-butanediol ester, 3-5 wt.% fish oil, 1-1.5 wt.% 1,4-dibutyl-6-(1-ethylpentamethylene)phenol (sic).

   (2) color fixative for casing comprises 3-5 pts. wt. butylated hydroxyanisole, 3-5 pts. wt. ascorbic acid, 2-4 pts. wt. AP, 5-10 pts. wt. chlorophyll, 6-8 pts. wt. glucosamine, 8-12 pts. wt. ginkgo flavonoids mixed with 60-80 pts. wt. tea polyphenol solution to obtain 30% solution; and
   (3) a salt for casing comprises 10-20 pts. wt. sodium chloride, 10-20 pts. wt. ginger powder, 10-15 pts. wt. disodium hydrogen phosphate, 5-10 pts. wt. EDTA and 3-5 pts. wt. metal ion chelating agent. The preparation of salt comprises mixing at 80 degrees C, cooling and ball milling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The casing comprises 40 wt.% 400 mesh animal endothelium, 15 wt.% fleece-flower root, 20 wt.% swim bladder, 8 wt.% sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, 10 wt.% sweet potato powder, 3 wt.% D-isoascorbic acid sodium salt, 1.5 wt.% alliin, 2 wt.% medlar polysaccharide, 6 wt.% polyoxyethylene sorbitan fatty acid ester, 1.5 wt.% neryl acetate, 1 wt.% citral, 3 wt.% alpha -tocopherol, 2 wt.% ascorbic acid, 1.5 wt.% adipic acid and 2,3-butanediol ester, 5 wt.% fish oil and 1.5 wt.% 1,4-dibutyl-6-(1-ethylpentamethylene)phenol. The salt for casing is prepared by mixing at 80 degrees C, cooling and ball milling.

PN WO2019123129-A1
TI Method for controlling fuel consuming engine e.g. gasoline consuming engine, involves adjusting conditions in cylinder by engine control unit during specific stage heat release to terminate reactions in cylinder at end of heat release.
AU SARATHY S M
   ALRAMADAN A S
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 201954053S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining (110) that a three-stage heat release in a cylinder in the engine is to be performed, by an engine control unit. The conditions within the cylinder are adjusted (120) by the engine control unit to cause the three-stage heat release within the cylinder. The conditions in the cylinder are monitored (130) by the engine control unit during a second stage heat release of the three-stage heat release. The conditions in the cylinder are adjusted (140) by the engine control unit during the second stage heat release to terminate reactions in the cylinder at an end of the second stage heat release.
   USE - Method for controlling fuel consuming engine e.g. gasoline consuming engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The recognition that a three-stage heat release can occur under certain conditions allows for adjusting the conditions to minimize or avoid three-stage heat release conditions to reduce the amount of carbon dioxide (CO2) produced during the combustion process. The production of syngasses during engine combustion for use by exhaust after- treatment systems reduces or avoids the need for certain components in the exhaust after-treatment system which reduces the cost and the installation space of the exhaust after-treatment system. The termination of reactions during a three-stage heat release at the end of the second stage allows for the generation of desirable gasses while minimizing the amount of undesirable gasses.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for controlling operation of an engine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method of terminating reactions within a cylinder at the end of a second heat release stage of a three-stage heat release.
   Step for determining that a three-stage heat release in a cylinder in the engine is to be performed (110)
   Step for adjusting the conditions within the cylinder by the engine control unit to cause the three-stage heat release within the cylinder (120)
   Step for monitoring the conditions in the cylinder by the engine control unit during a second stage heat release of the three-stage heat release (130)
   Step for adjusting the conditions in the cylinder by the engine control unit during the second stage heat release to terminate reactions in the cylinder at an end of the second stage heat release (140)

PN CN109694447-A
TI Multi-scale halloysite-montmorillonite aerogel composite has nanoscale and microscale pores, and is prepared by obtaining first and second dispersion, and dispersing it with tris(dibromo)propyl borate solution in dispersion medium.
AU LI H
   ZHANG H
   ZHU J
   LI R
   LIU H
   YANG A
   LIAO X
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201941756F
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-scale halloysite-montmorillonite aerogel composite has average specific surface area of 600-605 m2-g-1. Both the nanoscale and microscale pores exist in the halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material. The nanoscale pores have average size of 10-11 nm, and the micrometer pores have average size of 2-4 mu m.
   USE - Multi-scale halloysite-montmorillonite aerogel composite.
   ADVANTAGE - The multi-scale halloysite-montmorillonite aerogel composite has synergistic flame retardant effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing multi-scale halloysite-montmorillonite aerogel composite, adding 0.77-12 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes to a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, and performing the ultrasonic dispersion to obtain an halloysite dispersion; adding 0.7-12 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.06-1.6 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.3 pts. wt. initiator, 0.08-8 pts. wt. tetrabromobisphenol A to a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol; stirring the contents uniformly, adding the solution to the halloysite dispersion, and subjecting it to ultrasonic dispersion; maintaining the vacuum for a definite time, returning to normal pressure and repeating the vacuuming step three times; washing the obtained product, dispersing it in 100 pts. wt. water, heating in a water bath to 70-80 degrees C to initiate polymerization, performing the polymerization reaction for greater than or equal to 50 hours, and dispersing the obatined product to 100 pts. wt. water to obtain the first dispersion; drying 0.08-12 pts. wt. montmorillonite, preparing montmorillonite dispersion by dispersing montmorillonite in 0.08-24 wt.% solevnt, stirring the dispersion, and treating it ultrasonically to obtain second dispersion; dispersing 0.8-12 pts. wt. first dispersion, 0.7-32 pts. wt. second dispersion, 0.4-16 pts. wt. mixture of 0.05-18 wt.% tris(2,5-dibromo)propyl borate solution in a dispersion medium, and adding 0.01-2 pts. wt. initiator; stirring the contents uniformly, heating in water-bath at 70-90 degrees C to initiate polymerization, and performing the polymerization reaction for 37 hours; and placing the obtained product in supercritical high pressure extraction device with carbon dioxide as the medium under pressure of 1-20 mega Pascal at 10-300 degrees C for greater than or equal to 1 hour to obtain the desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is selected from dibenzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile. Preferred Process: The multi-scale halloysite-montmorillonite aerogel composite is prepared by adding 1-10 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes to a mixture of deionized water and ethanol. The contents are uniformly dispersed ultrasonically for 1 hour to obtain a halloysite dispersion. 1-10 pts. wt. Sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.1-1 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. initiator, 0.1-5 pts. wt. tetrabromobisphenol A is added to a mixture of deionized water and ethanol. After stirring uniformly for 10-60 minutes, the obtained solution is added to the halloysite dispersion, subjected to ultrasonic dispersion for 20-35 minutes, and vacuum pressure is applied. Vacuum is maintained for 0.5-2 hours, and then returned to normal pressure. After repeated vacuuming steps three times, the product is washed and dispersed in 100 pts. wt. water, the water bath is heated to 70-80 degrees C to initiate polymerization for 30-60 minutes. The polymerization reaction is performed at 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours. Subsequently, it is separately polymerized in a constant temperature water bath at 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C, and 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours. The obtained product is washed and dispersed into 100 pts. wt. water to obtain the first dispersion. 0.1-10 pts. wt. Montmorillonite is dried at 60-18 degrees C for 5-20 hours and its dispersion is prepared in a solvent having mass fraction of 0.1-20 wt.%. The montmorillonite dispersion is mechanically stirred uniformly with a stirring speed of 150-400 rotation per minute at 5-35 degrees C for 5-10 hours, and then ultrasonically treated to under power of 50-300 Watt for 6-15 hours to obtain second dispersion. 1-10 pts. wt. First dispersion, 1-30 pts. wt. second dispersion, 0.5-5 pts. wt. of a mass fraction of 0.05-18 wt.% tris(2,5-dibromo)propyl borate solution is mixed, dispersed in 10-1000 pts. wt. dispersion medium, and added with 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator. The obtained material is stirred uniformly for 1 hour, and heated in a water bath to 70-90 degrees C for 30-60 minutes to initate polymerization. The polymerization reaction is continued at 30-50 degrees C for 12-24 hours. The obtained mixture is sequentially polymerized in a constant temperature water bath at 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C and 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours, and further dried under supercritical conditions for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours to obtain the desired product.

TI Growing of single crystal diamond involves cleaning ultrathin single crystal diamond substrate, drying, placing dried substrate in growth chamber, etching, introducing reaction gas, and initiating growth on surface of diamond substrate.
AU LI S
   LIU H
   ZUO H
AE XIAN TANXING SEMICONDUCTOR TECHNOLOGY CO (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201930990P
AB    NOVELTY - Growing of single crystal diamond involves cleaning an ultrathin single crystal diamond as a substrate with an acetone cleaning solution, ultrasonically cleaning the soaked diamond substrate, drying, connecting an inlet and outlet pipes in a growth chamber, introducing cooling water, placing the dried diamond substrate in the growth chamber, evacuating the growth chamber, etching the diamond substrate for 35 minutes by introducing hydrogen into the growth chamber, adjusting the temperature of the diamond substrate to 1150 degrees C, introducing a reaction gas into the growth chamber, initiating growth on the surface of the diamond substrate, terminating the reaction gas, introducing hydrogen into the reaction chamber, etching the diamond substrate for 35 minutes, cooling the post-treated diamond to room temperature, turning off the power supply and the cooling water, opening the vent valve to restore the pressure in the growth chamber to the normal pressure, and removing the grown diamond.
   USE - Growing of single crystal diamond.
   ADVANTAGE - The single crystal diamond is grown at high speed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Growing of single crystal diamond involves selecting an ultrathin single crystal diamond having high quality and few surface defects as a substrate, placing the diamond substrate in a cleaning container with an acetone cleaning solution at 45 degrees C, soaking for 10 minutes, ultrasonically cleaning the soaked diamond substrate, drying, connecting an inlet and outlet pipes in a growth chamber, introducing cooling water, placing the dried diamond substrate in the growth chamber, evacuating the growth chamber, adjusting diamond substrate temperature to 850 degrees C, and pressure to 35 kPa, etching the diamond substrate for 35 minutes by introducing hydrogen with a flow rate of 500 sccm into the growth chamber, adjusting the temperature of the diamond substrate to 1150 degrees C, introducing a reaction gas into the growth chamber, initiating growth on the surface of the diamond substrate, terminating the reaction gas, introducing hydrogen with flow rate of 500 sccm into the reaction chamber, etching the diamond substrate for 35 minutes, cooling the post-treated diamond to room temperature, turning off the power supply and the cooling water, opening the vent valve to restore the pressure in the growth chamber to the normal pressure, and removing the grown diamond. Hydrogen, methane, carbon dioxide and argon are introduced into a gaseous medium, and mixed, to obtain reaction gas. The content of methane in the reaction gas is 6-20%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of carbon dioxide and argon in the reaction gas is 7% and 20%, respectively.

   WEN J
   FAN S

   ZHAO W
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 201917716H
AB    NOVELTY - Low-cost high-speed extruded high-strength                tough wrought magnesium alloy comprises 2-4 wt.%                bismuth, 2-4 wt.% tin, 1-3 wt.% aluminum, 0.05-0.5                wt.% calcium, and magnesium (balance amount), where                the mass ratio between bismuth and tin is                0.8-1.2:1.
   USE - Used as low-cost high-speed extruded                high-strength tough wrought magnesium alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the production                efficiency; reduces the production cost; extrudes                the finished product grain is uniform and fine and                matrix dispersion with a second phase; and has                excellent comprehensive mechanical performance, and                excellent flame retardant property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation of magnesium alloy comprising (i)                cleaning the magnesium alloy melting furnace and                crucible, preheating at 400-500 degrees C for 1                hour, adding preheated pure magnesium ingot into                the crucible of the melting furnace, and heating                the magnesium ingot at 710-730 degrees C, (ii)                melting the magnesium ingot, pre-heating pure                bismuth, pure tin, pure aluminum and                magnesium-calcium intermediate alloy, and raising                the furnace temperature to 10-30 degrees C, and the                temperature is maintained until alloy melting,                (iii) stirring for 2-5 minutes after the raw                materials in step (ii) are all melted, adjusting                the temperature of the furnace to 720-750 degrees                C, and allowing to stand for 8-10 minutes, (iv)                removing the scum from the surface of the melt, and                then preparing the as-cast billet of the magnesium                alloy by metal casting or semi-continuous casting,                protecting the carbon dioxide/sulfur hexafluoride                mixed gas from the smelting to the whole process                before pouring, and no gas is required in the                casting process protection, (v) firstly heating at                400-415 degrees C for 16-36 hours, then reacting at                440-460 degrees C for 6-24 hours, and no gas                protection in the heating and insulation process of                homogenization, (vi) cutting the ingot after                solution treatment in step (v) into a corresponding                blank and peeling, and (vii) adding the blank                obtained in the previous step into a mold for                extrusion deformation treatment, the extrusion                deformation speed is 0.1-30 m/minutes, the                extrusion ratio is 10-50, and the extrusion                temperature is 270-450 degrees C, heating the                deformed billet to the desired extrusion                temperature within 30 minutes to obtain final                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The mold is a mold for                  forming a rod, a plate, a tube, a wire or a                  profile. The magnesium-calcium intermediate alloy                  is magnesium-20calcium intermediate alloy. The                  composition of carbon dioxide/sulfur hexafluoride                  mixed gas is a volume ratio of 100:1.

PN CN109173996-A
TI Preparation of absorbent for extracting waste liquid extract, involves adding chemical substance capable of releasing hydrogen ions, to aqueous ink printing waste liquid, stirring, reducing viscosity of waste liquid and processing.
AU LIU R
   YU M
   SHI B
   CAI D
   PAN Y
   ZHANG B
AE UNIV QINGDAO (UNQD-C)
GA 201909499U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of absorbent involves adding preset amount of chemical substance capable of releasing hydrogen ions, to high-concentration aqueous ink printing waste liquid, stirring the reaction mixture, suppressing the ionization degree of ionizable group of water-soluble polymer organic substance in the waste liquid, reducing the water-soluble polymer organic substance solubility, destroying the stability of the binder so that extractable material in waste liquid agglomerates into insoluble fine solid particles, rapidly mixing, reducing the viscosity of the waste liquid, preventing fine flocculation of generated fine solid particles, adding preset amount of chemical agent which reacts with waste liquid to generate tiny carbon dioxide bubbles, adhering the bubbles to fine solid particles in waste liquid, and forming gas, liquid and fine particles solid three-phase contact interface to prevent flocculation of solid particles.
   USE - Preparation of absorbent for extracting waste liquid extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides absorbent having improved dye adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method further involves gradually stirring and heating waste liquid to temperature higher than the softening point of aqueous organic material by 5-30 degrees C, releasing fine solid particles to the bound water, gradually bonding fine solid particles to each other by heat, forming large particle solid extract that encapsulates tiny bubbles and water inside, gradually reducing the stirring speed until the stirring is stopped, maintaining warm condition for preset time, completely bonding and deforming large particle solid extract by contraction of molecular chain of high molecular organic substance and intermolecular hydrogen bond, continuously contracting the volume to form massive extract, exhausting most of the free water that is converted from combined water, forming blocky extract with developed internal pores, utilizing thermal action of the organic molecular chain to make the pores between the internal particles of the block extract uniform, draining water from bulk extract having sufficient volume shrinkage, rapidly cooling the surface of the block extract using saturated potassium hydroxide solution, hardening the surface of the extract, reducing the surface viscosity of the extract, removing the massive extract, cooling, crushing, drying the extract at low temperature which is less than the softening point temperature of the water-soluble polymer organic substance or drying naturally to prevent the extract from softening and destroying the pore structure, carbonizing dried extract, activating at 700 degrees C for 1-4 hours under nitrogen protection and crushing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The chemical substance is an acid, an acid salt, acid gas or acid release material, preferably mixture of water-soluble hydrogencarbonate or carbonates. The volume of saturated potassium hydroxide solution is 3-5 times the volume of the extract.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN109081675-A
TI Multi-scale halloysite-silica composite aerogel material comprises nano-scale and micro-scale pores which are present in halloysite-silica composite aerogel material halloysite nanotubes to mixture of deionized water and ethanol.
AU LI H
   LI R
   LIU H
   YANG A
   LIAO X
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201901590D
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-scale halloysite-silica composite aerogel material comprises average specific surface area of halloysite-silica composite aerogel material is 600-604 m2g-1, nano-scale and micro-scale pores are present in halloysite-silica composite aerogel material, and average nano-scale pores are 10-11 nanometer, micron-scale pores average 20-21 micrometer. The 0.6-16 pts. wt. by weight of halloysite nanotubes are added to mixture of 50 pts. wt. of deionized water and 50 pts. wt. by ethanol, ultrasonic dispersion is uniform, and halloysite dispersion is obtained, which is 0.6-16 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.06-4 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.4 pts. wt. initiator, and 0.05-12 pts. wt. tetrabromobisphenol A into 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol in mixed solution, stirred them uniformly.
   USE - Multi-scale halloysite-silica composite aerogel material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The solution is added into halloysitedispersion solution, ultrasonically dispersing, keeping vacuum after vacuumizing, then to return to normal pressure. after repeatedly vacuumizing for three times, washing product, and then dispersed in 100 pts. wt. of water, water bath heating to 60-120 degrees C to initiate polymerization, polymerization reaction time is 48 hours to obtain product, poly-vinyl siloxane has number average molecular weight is 500-5000, preferably, vinyl content is 1000-3000 mol.% of 0.1-5 % amino terminated polydimethylsiloxane divinylsiloxane or amino-terminated dimethyl vinylsiloxane. The washed product is dispersed in 100 pts. wt. water, adding 74-154 pts. wt. tetraethoxysilane, 0.07-1.5 pts. wt. of 12 mole/liter hydrochloric acid, 0.01-1.8 pts. wt. initiator and 0.08-16 pts. wt. boric acid tri2,3-dibromo-propyl, stirred uniformly, water bath heating to 30-75 degrees C to initiate polymerization, then adding 0.01-0.4 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, stirred to dissolve it completely, polymerization reaction time is 48 hours to obtain multi-scale halloysite-silicon dioxide composite wet gel, add 0.08-1.2 pts. wt. methyl dodecyl trimethylammonium chloride in multi-scale halloysite-silicon dioxide composite wet gel, after keeping, carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extracting device, drying through supercritical medium drying at temperature of 32-58 degrees C and pressure of 6-12 megapascal to obtain multi-scale halloysite-silicon dioxide composite aerogel material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves adding de-ionized water and mixed solution of ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing for 1-2 hours, adding 80-150 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate into dispersion, 0.1-1 pts. wt. 12 mole/liter hydrochloric acid, 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator and 0.1-15 pts. wt. boric acid tri2,3-dibromo-propyl, stirring for 10-70 minutes, selecting polymerization for 10-40 minutes at 30-75 degrees C, adding 0.01-0.2 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, stirring to dissolve it completely, then reducing temperature to 20-50 degrees C in water bath to polymerize for 20-35 hours. The 0.1-1 pts. wt. methyl dodecyltrimethyl ammonium chloride is added, keeping time is 1-6 hours, condition of supercritical drying is performing, supercritical drying for 2-4 hours at temperature of 33-50 degrees C and pressure is 7-10 megapascal, preferably for 3 hours. The method involves mixing 0.6-16 pts. wt. of indianite nanometre tube into mixed liquid of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain thehalloysite dispersion, 0.6-16 pts. wt. styrene, 0.06-4 pts. wt. poly-vinylsiloxane and 0.01-0.4 pts. wt. initiator, tetrabromobisphenol A into 50 pts. wt. of deionized water and 50 pts. wt. of ethanol in mixed solution, stirr them uniformly, add solution into halloysite dispersion solution, ultrasonically dispersing, keep vacuum after vacuumizing, then returning to normal pressure, repeatedly vacuumizing three times, washing product. The method involves adding 80-150 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate into dispersion, 0.1-1 pts. wt. 12 mole/liter hydrochloricacid, 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator and 0.1-15 pts. wt. boric acid tri2,3-dibromo-propyl, stirring for 10-70 minutes, selected polymerization for 10-40 minutes at 30-75 degrees C, adding 0.01-0.2 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, stirred to dissolve it completely,then reducing temperature to 20-50 degrees C in constant temperature water bath to polymerize for 20-35 hours. Preferred Conditions: The polymerization temperature selected at 60-120 degrees C for 10-60 minutes, then reducing temperature to 15-50 degrees C to polymerization for 12-24 hours, and polymerized for 2-8 hours at 80-100 degrees C. The supercritical drying for 2-4 hours at temperature of 33-50 degrees C and pressure is 7-10 megapascal. Preferred Components: The initiator is selected from dibenzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile.

PN CN108936282-A
TI Potato noodle comprises selenium-enriched garlic extract, potato dietary fiber, potato, sweet potato powder, wheat flour, cassava starch, sweet potato starch, Gu Yuan powder and edible salt.
AU WEI L
AE WEI L (WEIL-Individual)
GA 2018A0796C
AB    NOVELTY - A potato noodle comprises 1-3 wt.% selenium-enriched garlic extract, 2-6 wt.% potato dietary fiber, 20-35 wt.% potato, 10-20 wt.% sweet potato powder, 40-60 wt.% wheat flour, 5-20 wt.% cassava starch, 1-3 wt.% sweet potato starch, 2-5 wt.% Gu Yuan powder and 0.5-3 wt.% edible salt.
   USE - Potato noodle.
   ADVANTAGE - The noodle has rich nutritional components, unique flavor and increased healthcare function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the potato noodle comprising:
   (A) crushing fresh garlic, pulping into extracting kettle, opening high-pressure pump, filling carbon dioxide fluid at flow rate of 30-32 L/hour, extracting at 20-30 MPa at 40-50 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours to obtain garlic oil, drying selenium-rich garlic residue, adding deionized water and protease, ultrasonically processing at 40-50 degrees C for 30 minutes, standing for 2 hours, mixing, centrifuging at speed of 1000 revolutions/minute for 20-30 minutes, collecting supernatant liquid and filtering to obtain organic selenium;
   (B) cleaning fresh sweet potato, cutting, crushing, filtering, drying at 80-90 degrees C, passing through 60-mesh sieve to obtain sweet potato powder, soaking in water for 20-30 minutes, filtering, adding acid water into filter residue for 20-30 minutes, washing filter residue with deionized water until neutral, adding sodium hydroxide solution, soaking for 20-30 minutes, washing filter residue with deionized water until neutral, adding 5% sodium hypochlorite solution, heating, filtering, washing filter residue with deionized water until neutral, drying in oven at 80-90 degrees C, crushing and passing by 80-mesh sieve to obtain sweet potato dietary fiber;
   (C) cleaning sweet potato, peeling, cutting into blocks, putting on steamer, steaming at 80-150 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, crushing and cooling to obtain sweet potato paste;
   (D) treating potato starch paste with boiled water, adding into steamed sweet potato paste, sweet potato powder, wheat flour, cassava starch, Gu Yuan powder and edible salt, uniformly mixing, adding garlic oil, organic selenium, sweet potato dietary fiber, uniformly mixing, forming dough, placing dough into noodle press, rolling for 3-6 times for 5-30 minutes and re-rolling for 4-8 times to obtain fresh potato noodle; and
   (E) drying noodle by dryer at 80-300 degrees C to water content of 20-50%, UV sterilizing and vacuum-packing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The potato noodle comprises 2 wt.% selenium-enriched garlic extract, 4 wt.% potato dietary fiber, 26 wt.% potato, 15 wt.% sweet potato powder, 50 wt.% wheat flour, 15 wt.% cassava starch, 2 wt.% sweet potato starch, 3 wt.% Gu Yuan powder and 2 wt.% edible salt. The selenium-enriched garlic extract comprises garlic oil and organic selenium. Preferred Method: The adding amount of protease is 10-12%. Treating with boiled water is done at 95-100 degrees C for 20-22 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid water comprises 20-30% acetic acid or 5-8% hydrochloric acid.

PN CN108812869-A
TI Fresh-keeping method of oil-tea mushroom comprises e.g. selecting mature fresh oil tea mushroom, sorting and grading, spraying sterilizing liquid, placing wood pulp paper layer in turnover box, stacking, vacuumizing and passing fresh gas.
AU LI J
   LI W
   CHEN D
   LIAO Z
   CHEN J
   XIAO S
AE GUIZHOU GUIFU FUNGUS IND DEV CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2019124476
AB    NOVELTY - Fresh-keeping method of oil-tea mushroom comprises (i) selecting mature fresh oil tea mushroom body integral, sorting and grading and spraying sterilizing liquid, (ii) placing wood pulp paper layer in turnover box wall upper cushion, stacking sterilized oil-tea mushroom fresh mushroom in the turnover box of 3-6 cm thick and placing wood pulp paper between the layers, where the top layer is paved by wood pulp in order to seal the container, the thickness of the pulp layer is 0.12-0.15 mm and size of the wood pulp paper is adopted to container size, (iii) placing container of oil tea mushroom in the air conditioning bag, vacuumizing, passing fresh gas into the bag and placing for storage.
   USE - The method is useful for fresh-keeping method of oil-tea mushroom.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has function of preserving water and preserving moisture, reduces the loss of the mushroom, slows down the deterioration of mushroom, inhibites deterioration of the oil tea in the transporting and storing process and prolongs the oil-tea mushroom shelf life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fresh-keeping method of oil-tea mushroom comprises (i) selecting mature fresh oil tea mushroom body integral, sorting and grading and spraying sterilizing liquid, (ii) placing wood pulp paper layer in turnover box wall upper cushion, stacking sterilized oil-tea mushroom fresh mushroom in the turnover box of 3-6 cm thick and placing wood pulp paper between the layers, where the top layer is paved by wood pulp in order to seal the container, the thickness of the pulp layer is 0.12-0.15 mm and size of the wood pulp paper is adopted to container size, (iii) placing container of oil tea mushroom in the air conditioning bag, vacuumizing, passing fresh gas into the bag, placing at 5 degrees C environment for storage, where fresh gas comprises 80-85% nitrogen, 0.5-1.3% sulfur dioxide, 6-9% carbon dioxide and 8-11% oxygen, the sterilizing liquid comprises 1-3% tea polyphenol, 4-7% chitosan, 3-8% sodium alginate and water (remaining amount), the sterilizing liquid in the spraying process adopts spraying the fresh oil tea mushroom on the surface of 10-20 ml of the sterilizing liquid is sprayed according to 1 kg of fresh oil tea mushroom.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The fresh-keeping gas comprises 83% nitrogen, 0.8% sulfur dioxide, 6.2% carbon dioxide and 10% oxygen. The sterilizing solution comprises 2% tea polyphenol, 6% chitosan, 7% sodium alginate and water (remaining amount). Preferred Method: The sorting comprises clearing culture medium of mushroom base and removing the stem base aged portion. In the step (ii), the fresh oil tea mushroom is placed in the manner of placing the mushroom handle on the edge of the turnover box, and the mushroom handle is placed in the middle of the turnover box. The sterilizing liquid in the spraying process adopts spraying the fresh oil tea mushroom on the surface of 15 ml of the sterilizing liquid is sprayed according to 1 kg of fresh oil tea mushroom.

PN CN108815165-A
TI Enhancing effect of drug for treating blood cancer, by extracting blood mouse erythroleukemia cells, culturing satatinib tablets in mother liquor, taking cell count in logarithmic growth phase and administering to patient.
AU LIN J
AE FOSHAN CHANCHENG QIZHI INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201893714F
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing effect of drug for treating blood cancer, by (a) grinding satatinib tablet, adding dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) and phosphate buffered saline (PBS) to obtain mother liquor, filtering, dispensing into an Eppendorf (EP) tube and attaching a QR code with patient's own information on EP tube, (b) preparing blood culture medium, adding into culture container, taking blood containing mouse erythroleukemia (MEL) cells from patient, adding into blood sample chamber, extracting blood MEL cells, taking MEL cells in culture container, incubating and subculturing, and (c) taking mother liquor EP tube, culturing satatinib tablets in mother liquor, taking cell count in a logarithmic growth phase, diluting with Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM), adding into cultured mother liquor EP tube, performing phagocytosis or killing MEL cells, and administering mother liquor after culture to patient.
   USE - The method is useful for enhancing effect of drug for treating blood cancer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for enhancing effect of drug for treating blood cancer, involves (a) taking satatinib tablet, grinding into a powder, taking in container, adding dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) to dissolve, adding phosphate buffered saline (PBS) to obtain mother liquor, filtering, sterilizing the filtrate, dispensing into an Eppendorf (EP) tube, attaching a QR code with patient's own information on EP tube, and separately storing at -20 degrees C, (b) preparing blood culture medium, adding into culture container, taking blood containing mouse erythroleukemia (MEL) cells from patient, adding into blood sample chamber, extracting blood MEL cells using optical tweezer device, taking MEL cells in culture container, applying a QR code with patient's own information on the container, placing in incubator, incubating under saturated humidity at 34-40 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, and subculturing every 3 days, and (c) taking mother liquor EP tube, after being kept at room temperature, placing in an incubator at 34-40 degrees C for 3-4 hours to culture satatinib tablets in mother liquor, taking cell count in a logarithmic growth phase, diluting with Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM), adding the diluted MEL cells into cultured mother liquor EP tube, performing phagocytosis or killing MEL cells by culture mother liquor, administering mother liquor after culture to patient by which increasing phagocytosis or killing or inhibiting effect of mother liquor on blood cancer cells in patient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The blood cancer medium is 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) containing Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM) comprising glutamine, 10 mM (4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid) (HEPES), 1 mM sodium pyruvate, 100 U/ml penicillin and 100 mu g/ml streptomycin. The optical tweezer device comprises microscope and nano-optical conversion device arranged on the microscope. The DMEM is high glucose type cell culture medium.

PN CN108793188-A
TI Preparing amino modified material composite molecular sieve comprises e.g. adding tetraethylammonium hydroxide and ethyl orthosilicate in three-necked flask, and dissolving triblock copolymer P123 in deionized water.
AU WEI J
   MEI D
   GENG L
   CHEN S
   LIN Z
AE UNIV GUILIN TECHNOLOGY (UYGI-C)
GA 201892753R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing amino modified material composite molecular sieve comprises e.g. (i) adding tetraethylammonium hydroxide and ethyl orthosilicate in a three-necked flask, uniformly stirring, marking the obtained solution as S1, adding sodium hydroxide, sodium metaaluminate, tetraethylammonium hydroxide and deionized water to the beaker, stirring until dissolved, marking the obtained solution as S2, slowly dripping the solution S2 into the solution S1 for rapid stirring, charging obtained solution in high pressure reaction tank for crystallization, cooling reaction tank at room temperature to open, filtering the obtained product, washing and drying to obtain a solid product of Beta molecular sieve, or (ii) dissolving polyethylene glycol-polyglycerol-polyethylene glycol triblock copolymercopolymer P123 in deionized water, stirring until completely dissolved, adding hydrochloric acid to adjust to an acidic environment, adding beta molecular sieve and n-butanol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing amino modified material composite molecular sieve.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, convenient to operate and environmentally friendly; has excellent adsorption effect; and produces product that enhances the adsorption effect of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing amino modified material composite molecular sieve comprises (i) adding tetraethylammonium hydroxide (TEAOH) and ethyl orthosilicate in a three-necked flask, uniformly stirring, and marking the obtained solution as S1, adding sodium hydroxide, sodium metaaluminate, tetraethylammonium hydroxide and deionized water to the beaker, stirring until dissolved, marking the obtained solution as S2, slowly dripping the solution S2 into the solution S1 and rapid stirring, charging the obtained solution in a high pressure reaction tank for crystallization, cooling the reaction tank to room temperature and open, filtering the resulting product, washing and drying to obtain a solid product of beta molecular sieve, (ii) taking polyethylene glycol-polyglycerol-polyethylene glycol triblock copolymer P123, dissolving in deionized water, stirring until completely dissolved, adding hydrochloric acid to adjust to an acidic environment, adding beta molecular sieve, and n-butanol, dripping tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS) with rapid stirring, crystallizing in a high pressure reactor, cooling the reactor to room temperature, opening for vacuum filtration, drying the white pulp obtained by suction filtration and calcining to obtain a solid product of Beta/KIT-6 composite molecular sieve, and (iii) adding the Beta/KIT-6 composite molecular sieve to a conical flask containing a solvent, mixing, adding tetraethylene pentamine, placing the conical flask on a magnetic stirrer, stirring, evaporating the solvent and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), taking 25.95 g TEAOH with a mass fraction of 25 wt.% and 21.43 g ethyl orthosilicate, stirring in a three-necked flask at the speed of 350 revolutions/minute (rpm) at room temperature, marking the obtained solution as S1, adding 0.19 g sodium hydroxide, 0.38 g sodium metaaluminate, 3.5 g TEAOH and 2 g deionized water, stirring until dissolved, marking the obtained solution as S2, then slowly dripping the solution S2 to the solution S1, rapidly stirring at 400 rpm for 4 hours, adding the stirred solution to a high pressure reaction tank, crystallizing at 140 degrees C for 24 hours, then cooling the reaction tank at room temperature to open, filtering the resulting product, washing, drying at 80 degrees C for 10 hours, and calcining at 550 degrees C for 6 hours to obtain amine-modified Beta/KIT-6 composite molecular sieve. In the step (ii), dissolving 4 g polyethylene glycol-polyglycerol-polyethylene glycol triblock copolymer P123 in 144 ml deionized water, stirring at 350 rpm for 1 hour at 35 degrees C until completely dissolved, adding 6.7 ml 35% hydrochloric acid to adjust into acidic environment, continuously stirring for 1 hour, adding 1.5 g beta molecular sieve, continuously stirring for 2 hours, adding 4.94 ml n-butanol, heating to 40 degrees C, dripping 9.24 ml TEOS while rapidly stirring at the speed of 450 rpm, continuously stirring for 24 hours, crystallizing in the reaction tank at 100 degrees C, cooling the reactor to room temperature to open, performing vacuum filtration to obtain white slurry, drying the slurry at 100 degrees C for 24 hours and calcining in a muffle furnace at 550 degrees C for 6 hours to obtain a solid product of amine-modified Beta/KIT-6 composite molecular sieve. In the step (iii), adding 200-700 mg Beta/KIT-6 composite molecular sieve to a conical flask containing 25 ml solvent, adding 200-1000 mg preferably, 600 mg tetraethylene pentamine (TEPA), placing the conical flask on the magnetic stirrer, stirring at the speed of 400 rpm for 5 hours at room temperature, evaporating at 80 degrees C for 8 hours to remove the solvent, then placing into a natural convection and drying in an oven at 100 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain amine-modified Beta/KIT-6 composite molecular sieve. Preferred Components: The mass of TEPA is 30-70 wt.% and the solvent is anhydrous ethanol.

PN CN108670937-A
TI Plant whitening and freckle mask comprises rose extract, white muscardine silkworm extract, mulberry leaf extract and lavender extract, where the rose extract is a flavonoid extract, and the flavonoid extract includes rutin and quercetin.
AU WANG J
AE SHANXI BAORENTANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201884626Q
AB    NOVELTY - Plant whitening and freckle mask comprises 10-12 pts. wt. rose extract, 5-7 pts. wt. white muscardine silkworm extract, 15-20 pts. wt. mulberry leaf extract and 15-20 pts. wt. lavender extract. The rose extract is a flavonoid extract, and the flavonoid extract includes rutin, quercetin and gallic acid.
   USE - Plant whitening and freckle mask.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant whitening and freckle mask eliminates chloasma, freckles, pregnancy spots, senile plaques and acne scars, and achieves the effect of lightening melanin and ecchymosis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing plant whitening and freckle mask, which involves:
   (A) weighing rose flower powder;
   (B) using70% ethanol to 1 30 material liquid ratio andextracting for 180 min in the water bath of 60 degrees C;
   (C) extracting for three time;
   (D) separating and combining the supernatant;
   (E) preparing white batryticated silkworm extract;
   (F) weighing the 50g white muscardine moccasin;
   (G) dissolving in distilled water to prepare white stiffsilkworm powder solution, where extractingtemperature is 100 degrees C and extraction time is 160 min;
   (H) filtering and taking the supernatantmaterial with mass concentration of 40 g/l;
   (I) adding 4 times volume of absoluteethyl alcohol;
   (J) placing at 4 degrees C for 12 hours;
   (K) drying theprecipitate to obtain white muscardine silkworm extract;
   (L) weighing 50g mulberry leaf powder;
   (M) using 45% ethanol to 1 25 material liquid ratio and extracting for 120 min in the water bath of 60 degrees C;
   (N) extracting for three times;
   (O) separating and combining the supernatant;
   (P) weighing 50g lavender flower spike powder into an extraction tank, where analysis pressure is 6.5\103 kpa and extracting pressure is 22 x 103 kpa;
   (Q) introducing carbon dioxide at aflow rate of 20 l/h;
   (R) extracting for 120 min afterdischarging the lavender extract from the analysis tank;
   (S) taking 10-12 pts. wt. rose extract, 5-7 pts. wt. white muscardine silkworm extract, 15-20 pts. wt. mulberry leaf extract and 15-20 pts. wt. lavender extrac;
   (T) adding in a beaker;
   (U) heating, stirring and mixing uniformly;
   (V) obtaining pasty facial mask;
   (W) dospersing in water;
   (X) heating and stirring to make itcompletely dissolved;
   (Y) stirring to uniform transparent;
   (Z) obtaining matrix facial mask; and
   (() placing mask base in the matrix mask liquid to be infiltrated for 30 minutes to obtain a facial patch type mask.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The white muscardine silkworm extract ispolysaccharide extract, the polysaccharide extractcomprises cellulose, chitin, cell wall substance,pectin, dextran, polysaccharide and protein polysaccharide. The mulberry leaf extract is a polyphenolextract, the polyphenol extract compriseschlorogenic acid, rutin, shed, shed, gallic acid,morin, chlorogenic acid or caffeic acid. The lavender essential oil comprises linalool,linalyl acetate, terpineol and lavender acetate.

TI Solid electrolyte for negative electrode of            secondary battery, electrode assembly and            electrochemical cell comprises first and second solid            electrolytes having ionic conductivity effective for            deposition metal.
AU THOMAS-ALYEA K E

AE SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
   CORNING INC (CORG-C)
   SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
   CORNING INC (CORG-C)
   SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 201856404P
AB    NOVELTY - A solid electrolyte (3) for negative electrode                of secondary battery comprises 1st solid                electrolyte having 1st surface (7), and 2nd solid                electrolyte on 1st solid electrolyte which consists                of 2nd surface (8) where 1st solid electrolyte and                2nd solid electrolyte each have ionic conductivity                effective for deposition metal, and 1st surface and                2nd surface are different in composition and/or                structure.
   USE - A solid electrolyte for negative electrode of                secondary battery, electrode assembly and                electrochemical cell (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Solid electrolyte has improved mechanical                strength and flexibility and excellent ion                conductivity. Deposited metal will charge                into/discharge from pores of negative electrode                region over most conditions of operation, without                filling of pores in buffer region, even at state of                full charge of electrochemical cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) electrode assembly for electrochemical                cell comprising greater than or equal to 1 of                current collector (2) and separator, and solid                electrolyte (3) where solid electrolyte is disposed                on current collector if present, on separator if                present, or between current collector and separator                if both are present, 1st surface of 1st solid                electrolyte and 2nd surface of 2nd solid                electrolyte define a void, which extends from                current collector to separator;
   (2) electrochemical cell (1) comprising                positive electrode, and electrode assembly disposed                on positive electrode;
   (3) manufacturing electrode assembly which                involves providing 1st composition containing solid                electrolyte and solvent, disposing 1st composition                on substrate, removing solvent to form layer                containing solid electrolyte, heat-treating layer                in absence of carbon dioxide and water to provide                porous solid electrolyte layer on substrate,                disposing surface modifier on portion of surface of                porous solid electrolyte to provide 2nd solid                electrolyte layer having 2nd surface; and                contacting porous solid electrolyte layer with air                to form 1st solid electrolyte layer adjacent to                substrate, 1st solid electrolyte layer containing                1st surface different from 2nd surface to                manufacture electrode assembly; and
   (4) manufacturing electrochemical cell which                involves disposing positive electrode on electrode                assembly to provide electrochemical cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                electrochemical cell with porous solid electrolyte,                surface favorable for metal deposition adjacent to                current collector, and surface unfavorable for                deposition adjacent to separator.
   Electrochemical cell (1)
   Current collector (2)
   Solid electrolyte (3)
   1st surface (7)
   2nd surface (8)

First surface                  has composition that is different from bulk                  composition of 1st solid electrolyte, 2nd surface                  has composition that is different from bulk                  composition of 2nd solid electrolyte, or 1st                  surface has composition that is different from                  bulk composition of 1st solid electrolyte and 2nd                  surface has composition that is different from                  bulk composition of 2nd solid electrolyte. First                  surface and second surface each independently                  further comprise carbonate, halogen, oxide,                  polymer, silicide, phosphide, sulfide, and/or                  metal of Groups 2-16. First surface contians                  carbonate, halogen, oxide and/or polymer. Second surface further contains                  oxide, silicide, phosphide, sulfide, carbonate,                  aluminum oxide, magnesium, niobium, gold, silver,                  calcium, indium, tin, antimony, zinc, and/or                  polymer. Electrolyte contains non-porous                  insulating frame surrounding one or both of 1st                  and 2nd solid electrolyte.D=monovalent or divalent cation;Me=trivalent, tetravalent, pentavalent, and                  hexavalent cation;A=Li, Na, Ca, Sr or Ba;X=Cl, Br, I, or super halide;R=O or S;M1=Al, Ga, In, Sc, Cr, Fe, Ta, or Nb;M2=Ti, Zr, Hf, or Ge; andM=Ge, Si, or Sn.Preferred Parameters: First solid                  electrolyte and second solid electrolyte each                  independently have ionic conductivity for Li, Na,                  K, Mg, Zn, Ag and/or Al of greater than or equal                  to 10-3 S/m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: Second surface further                  contains lithium oxide, lithium carbonate,                  lithium silicide, lithium phosphide, lithium                  sulfide, lithium aluminum oxide,                  lithium-magnesium alloy, lithium nitride,                  lithium-niobium alloy, lithium-gold alloy,                  lithium-silver alloy, lithium-aluminum alloy,                  lithium-calcium alloy, lithium-indium alloy,                  lithium-tin alloy, lithium-antimony alloy, and/or                  lithium-zinc alloy.

PN US2018171768-A1; WO2018119139-A1; CA3048096-A1; IN201947028747-A; EP3558496-A1; CN110997113-A; EP3558496-A4; IN359799-B; US11078767-B2
TI Extracting potable water from pressurized methane saturated brine by depressurizing pressurized methane saturated brine, collecting methane gas, extracting thermal energy, depressurizing again, and collecting another portion of methane gas.
AU TIFFANY H D
   BRYANT S
   CLARK G A
   DE CLARE B H
   POPE G A
   TIFFANY H D I
   DE C B H
AE TIFFANY H D (TIFF-Individual)
   BRYANT S (BRYA-Individual)
   CLARK G A (CLAR-Individual)
   DE CLARE B H (DCLA-Individual)
   POPE G A (POPE-Individual)
   TIFFANY H D (TIFF-Individual)
   BRYANT S (BRYA-Individual)
   CLARK G A (CLAR-Individual)
   DE CLARE B H (DCLA-Individual)
   POPE G A (POPE-Individual)
   TIFFANY H D (TIFF-Individual)
   BRYANT S (BRYA-Individual)
   CLARK G A (CLAR-Individual)
   DE CLARE B H (DCLA-Individual)
   POPE G A (POPE-Individual)
   TIFFANY H D I (TIFF-Individual)
   BRYANT S (BRYA-Individual)
   CLARK G A (CLAR-Individual)
   DE CLARE B H (DCLA-Individual)
   POPE G A (POPE-Individual)
GA 201848495X
AB    NOVELTY - Potable water is extracted from pressurized methane saturated brine by depressurizing pressurized methane saturated brine to 1st pressurized methane brine; collecting 1st portion of methane gas (114) outgassed from 1st pressurized methane brine; extracting thermal energy from 1st pressurized methane brine; depressurizing 1st pressurized methane brine to 2nd pressurized methane brine; and collecting 2nd portion of methane gas outgassed from 2nd pressurized methane brine.
   USE - Extraction of potable water from pressurized methane saturated brine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method closes the loop (the extracted brine is re-injected into the same formation) while deriving energy from methane and thermal energy removed from the brine, does not require large injection pressures during injection of carbon dioxide (CO2) dissolved in brine into deep geopressured formations, requires significantly smaller injection pressures because the greater density of brine compared to the CO2 gives a larger bottom-hole pressure in deep formations, and allows use of the hot brine in these formations as a source of geothermal energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) closed loop desalinizing system for extracting potable water from pressurized methane saturated brine, comprising depressurizing wellhead (101) is connectable to geopressured aquifer (120) containing methane saturated brine, 1st methane collector connected to depressurizing wellhead for collecting methane gas outgassed from depressurized methane saturated brine, 1st heat exchanger (103) connected to depressurizing wellhead for extracting thermal energy from methane saturated brine, pressure reducer connected to 1st heat exchanger for further depressurizing methane saturated brine, and 2nd methane collector connected to pressure reducer for collecting methane gas outgassed from the further depressurized methane saturated brine; and
   (2) closed loop method for extracting potable water and methane gas from geopressured methane saturated brine in a geopressured aquifer, which involves depressurizing geopressured methane saturated brine to 1st pressurized methane brine, collecting 1st portion of methane gas outgassed from 1st pressurized methane brine, extracting thermal energy from 1st pressurized methane brine, depressurizing 1st pressurized methane brine to 2nd pressurized methane brine, collecting 2nd portion of methane gas outgassed from 2nd pressurized methane brine, and desalinizing 2nd pressurized methane brine by powering distillation process with energy derived from 1st and/or 2nd portions of methane gas and extracted thermal energy to generate potable water and reject stream (111).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a desalination system.
   Wellhead (101)
   Heat exchanger (103)
   Reject stream (111)
   Methane gas (114)
   Aquifer (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method further includes providing distillation processor and desalinizing 2nd pressurized methane brine with distillation processor by powering distillation process with energy derived from 1st and/or 2nd portions of methane gas or extracted thermal energy. Providing distillation processor further includes providing multistage flash processor. Desalinizing the 2nd pressurized methane brine includes generating potable water and generating a reject stream. The method further includes extracting rare earth elements from the reject stream. The method further includes mixing the reject stream with the 2nd pressurized methane brine. The method further includes mixing the 2nd pressurized methane brine mixed with the reject stream with carbon dioxide (CO2) gas to produce a pressurized CO2 brine mix; and collecting 3rd portion of methane gas outgassed from the 2nd pressurized methane brine mixed with the reject stream and mixed with the CO2 gas. he method further includes injecting the pressurized CO2 brine mix into an aquifer.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PN CN108164111-A
TI Biological enzyme purifying ferment for removing            volatile organic compounds in air, where the volatile            organic compounds comprises any one or a mixture of            formaldehyde, toluene, xylene, and ammonia, and can            also inhibit pathogenic bacteria.
AU LI Y
AE SHANGHAI QIWU ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201851479N
AB    NOVELTY - Biological enzyme purifying ferment, in which                the chemical constituents of the biological enzyme                purifying ferment are 0.9% crude protein, 2.6%                carbohydrate, 1% ash and 95.5% moisture, where the                micro-organisms contained in the enzyme-purifying                ferment is classified into three categories, i.e,                bacteria, fungi, and actinomycetes according to the                classification of Haeckel's classification. It                consists of Bacillussubtilis group, filamentous                flora, yeast group, lactic acid bacteria group,                photosynthetic bacteria group, actinomycete group                and coliform group. The number of viable cells per                gram of product is 1x 1012/ml Bacillussubtilis, 1x                105/ml filamentous fungi, 1x 107/ml Actinomycetes,                1x 107/ml yeast using aerobic culture method, 1x                1012/ml lactic acid bacteria, 1x 1012/ml                photosynthetic bacteria using anaerobic culture                method, and Negative / 6.66 ml Coliform using the                brilliant green lactose bile (BGLB) method.
   USE - Biological enzyme purifying ferment is used in                kitchen waste water, which can decompose and                convert these organic substances into nutrients,                and become the dominant species in the environment,                which can effectively inhibit the reproduction of                spoilage bacteria in the kitchen, and decompose in                the environment, where ammonia and hydrogen sulfide                reduce the odor concentrate or in septic tanks,                where sludge in the septic tank will be less and                less affected by the decomposition of                microorganisms, and it will be dissolved in water                and discharged through the water pipe and ion or                reduce the odor of the environment, where the water                quality of the discharged water will be reduced by                the chemical oxygen demand (COD), biological oxygen                demand (BOD) and pathogens after being treated by                microorganisms or for removing volatile organic                compounds in the air, and the volatile organic                compounds include any one or a mixture of                formaldehyde, toluene, xylene, and ammonia, where                it can also inhibit pathogenic bacteria and produce                odorous bacteria, which compete with nutrients and                discharge anti-biomass to eliminate harmful                bacteria, and the probiotics contained in the                enzyme are beneficial to humans, animals and plants                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biological enzyme purifying ferment is                hygienic and environmentally friendly, and can                decompose or reduce and transform the odorous                substance by converting it into odorless hydrogen,                oxygen, water, carbon dioxide or microbial                nutrients while achieving the purpose of                eliminating odor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The lactic acid                  bacteria group is composed of Lactobacillussp.,                  Lactobacillusacidophilus, Lactobacillusbifidus,                  and Streptococcuslacticus. The yeast group is                  composed of Saccharomycescerevisiae, Klein's                  yeast, and Hansen's yeast. The Bacillussubtilis                  group is composed of Bacillussubtilis and                  liquefied starch. The filamentous flora is                  composed of koji bacteria and enzyme bacteria.                  The actinomycetes are composed of high                  temperature Actinomycetesand Streptomyces. The                  photosynthetic bacteria group is composed of                  purple sulfur-free photosynthetic bacteria and                  purple sulfur bacteria.

PN EP3333123-A1; WO2018103890-A1; CN108217595-A; CN207845154-U; AU2017373373-A1; IN201917025050-A; US2019308877-A1; EP3333123-B1; RU2019119914-A; AU2017373373-B2; RU2760013-C2; IN382751-B
TI Producing a synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen and freed of acid gases, comprises separating air into a stream of oxygen, a stream of residual gas and a stream of nitrogen by cryogenic separation.
AU CHAUBET L
   BONNE F
   BOUET C
   SCHLICHTING H
   WAGNER M
   SAWBBE L
   BOEHNER F
   BUEY C
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   GEORGE CLAUDE METHOD AIR LIQUIDE CORP (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2018461337
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen and freed of acid gases, starting from a hydrocarbon-containing fuel, and air and steam, comprises separating air into a stream of oxygen, a stream of residual gas and a stream of nitrogen by cryogenic separation, the residual gas and the nitrogen stream has an ambient temperature and the nitrogen stream has an increased pressure, mixing the hydro carbonaceous fuel with the oxygen stream and the steam at elevated temperature and pressure, converted into a synthesis gas and followed subsequently, by low-temperature absorption in an absorption column, where the absorbent is cooled by a compression refrigeration system, sour gas is separated off, and the nitrogen stream produced by cryogenic separation of air is passed through an expansion turbine and cooled and then used to cool the absorbent or the circulating in the refrigerant circuit of the compression refrigeration system refrigerant.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has a lower consumption of electrical energy for its operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for plant for the production of synthesis gas comprising (a) apparatus for the separation of air using an under the principle of cryogenic rectification under elevated pressure air separation plant, which is capable of generating a stream of gaseous oxygen and a stream of gaseous nitrogen of elevated pressure and ambient temperature, and a residual gas stream, (b) plant for the conversion of a carbonaceous fuel with a gasification agent to a synthesis gas comprising mainly hydrogen and carbon monoxide, where the units (a) and (b) are connected so that the oxygen produced in part (a) is added to the gasification agent used in part (b), (c) plant for the separation of the acid gases carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide from the raw synthesis gas produced in unit (b) by absorption at low temperature with a liquid absorbent, e.g. methanol, where the plant is equipped with a compression refrigeration system, an expansion turbine through which the nitrogen stream produced in (a) can be expanded and thereby cooled and a heat exchanger, for the heat exchange between this relaxed nitrogen stream and the liquid absorbent used in (c) or with the refrigerant of the compression refrigeration system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: A heat exchange is performed between the nitrogen stream produced by cryogenic separation of air, before its passage through the expansion turbine and the same stream, after its passage through the expansion turbine and after its heat exchange with the absorption or with the refrigerant. The nitrogen stream produced by cryogenic separation of air, after the heat exchange with the absorption or the refrigerant passed through another expansion turbine and thereby further cooled and then used once more for cooling the absorption or the refrigerant. The energy obtained in the expansion of the nitrogen stream by means of the expansion turbine is used to drive a compression turbine to compress the residual gas stream obtained by the air separation, and then the residual gas stream is cooled by heat exchange and expansion in another expansion turbine and then used to cool the absorption or refrigerant. The liquid absorbent used is methanol. The cooling of the refrigerant is carried out by heat exchange with the nitrogen and the residual gas flow through each one upstream of the expansion nozzle in the refrigerant circuit of the compression refrigeration system installed heat exchanger. Preferred Components: The system comprises an additional heat exchanger for the heat exchange between the nitrogen stream produced in the unit (a) before its expansion, and the same stream, after its expansion in the expansion turbine and after its heat exchange with the liquid absorption or refrigerant. The system comprises a second turbine via which the nitrogen stream, after its expansion in the first turbine and heat exchange in the heat exchanger with the liquid absorption or the refrigerant can be further relaxed and characterized in that this heat exchanger is designed so that in it the simultaneous heat exchange between the liquid absorption or the refrigerant, the nitrogen flow after the relaxation in the first and the nitrogen flow after relaxation in the second turbine is possible. The plant additionally comprises a compression turbine for compression of the residual gas stream driven by the expansion turbine serving to depressurize the nitrogen stream, e.g. by the compression and the expansion turbine are mounted together on a turbine axis, where the system further comprises a heat exchanger for cooling the compressed residual gas stream with cooling water and a expansion turbine to relax the residual gas stream and the system further comprises a heat exchanger with two passages for the residual gas stream and the heat exchanger for the heat exchange between the nitrogen stream and the absorption or the refrigerant additionally comprises a passage for the residual gas stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108101699-A
TI Soil repairing agent comprises waste decolored clay, modifying liquid, zein and nutritional additive, where modified liquid is prepared by taking water, peptone, sucrose, egg yolk, ammonium nitrate, and ferric nitrate.
AU 
AE CHANGSHA WUDAO IND DESIGN CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201844510E
AB    NOVELTY - Soil repairing agent comprises waste decolored clay, modifying liquid, zein and nutritional additive in ratio is (26-32):(16-22):(6-9):(3-6), where modified liquid is prepared by taking 50-60 pts. wt. water, 30-35 pts. wt. peptone, 13-18 pts. wt. sucrose, 11-16 pts. wt. egg yolk, 8-12 pts. wt. ammonium nitrate, 7-10 pts. wt. ferric nitrate, 2-4 pts. wt. sodium dihydrogen phosphate, 1-3 pts. wt. disodium hydrogen phosphate, stirring and mixing, sterilizing and disinfecting to obtain fermented solution. The distiller's grains, pond sludge and soybean roots are placed in pulverizer for crushing i9n ratio of 1:3:2-6. The crushed mixture is mixed with fermenting liquid in ratio of 1:3, placed into fermentation tank, and mixed gas is used to protect. The fermentation is performed for 2-4 days at 28-35 degrees C. The mixing gas oxygen is mixed with carbon dioxide in ratio 1:4. The fermented product is collected, pH is adjusted to 5.0-5.5, filtered to obtain modified solution.
   USE - Soil repairing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The soil repairing agent has good repairing effect, improves soil structure, soil fertilizer effect, reduces the content of the heavy metal in the soil, and increase the additional value of waste decolored clay.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The zein comprises 26-29 pts. wt. carbonized bamboo fiber, 16-23 pts. wt. black tourmaline powder, 2-6 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 1-3 pts. wt. kyanite. The 30-35 pts. wt. glucose, 17-20 pts. wt. yeast extract, 8-12 pts. wt. mannite, 3-5 pts. wt. sodium chloride are added, uniformly mixed, sterilized and disinfected to obtain finished product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing soil repairing agent, which involves:
   (A) humidifying waste decolored clay, removing oil, mixing with additive;
   (B) putting in ball mill by 1100 revolution per minute milling, collecting ball. mixing ball and nutrition additive and keeping, filtering and collecting the filtrate, putting the filter liquid with the modified mixing for 2-6 hours at 28-35 degrees C, freezing and drying, collecting the dry particles; and
   (C) mixing dried particles with zein, and completely dipping in 60% of ethanol solution, stirring with 150 revolution per minute for 50 minutes, freezing and drying, crushing, collecting to obtain soil repairing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The ratio of waste decolored clay, modified solution, zein, and nutritional additive is 30:20:8:4. Preferred Process: The additive is prepared by mixing black tourmaline powder, magnesium oxide, kyanite for milling, calcining at 1800-2300 degrees C, collecting the calcined product, mixing calcined substance and carbonized bamboo fiber to mill, collecting to obtain product.
