TI Composition used to inhibit corrosion and/or            remove hydrocarbonaceous deposits in oil and gas            applications, comprises iron sulfide dissolver having            e.g. allaric acid, organic solvent, and corrosion            inhibitor having e.g. imidazoline compound.
AU BENNETT B M
   SOLOMON K R
   SILVERNAIL C
AE ECOLAB USA INC (ECON-C)
   ECOLAB USA INC (ECON-C)
   ECOLAB USA INC (ECON-C)
GA 201843707K
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises: an iron sulfide                dissolver comprising allaric acid, altaric acid,                altraric acid, altronic acid, arabinaric acid,                arabinonic acid, dihomocitric acid, fructuronic                acid, fuconic acid, galactaric acid, galactonic                acid, galacturonic acid, glucaric acid,                glucoheptonic acid, gluconic acid, glucuronic acid,                gulonic acid, homocitric acid, homoisocitric acid,                idaric acid, idonic acid, iduronic acid, isocitric                acid, mannaric acid, mannonic acid, octulosonic                acid (Sic), rhamnonic acid, ribonic acid,                tagaturonic acid, xylonic acid and/or xyluronic                acid and its salts or derivatives; an organic                solvent; and a corrosion inhibitor comprising an                imidazoline compound, a quaternary ammonium                compound and/or a pyridinium compound.
   USE - The composition is useful: for inhibiting                corrosion and/or removing hydrocarbonaceous                deposits in oil and gas applications (claimed); and                in sweet systems i.e. system having a relatively                high carbon dioxide concentration or in systems                having sour conditions i.e. relatively high                hydrogen sulfide concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: provides protection to the                equipment against corrosive fluids and gases;                removes the iron sulfide to reduce the risk of                corrosion failures due to under deposit corrosion;                exhibits superior cleaning performance resulting                from dissolution of iron sulfide at treated                surfaces e.g. interior of pipeline and dirty                production equipment; utilizes the iron sulfide                dissolver that act as a corrosion inhibitor and is                more compatible with steel piping and equipment                e.g. mild steel as compared to conventional iron                sulfide dispersants used in oilfield applications;                improves production in an oil and/or gas                application by reducing a corrosion rate and/or                removing hydrocarbonaceous deposits e.g. schmoo                from metallic or mineral surfaces in contact with a                fluid in oil and gas applications; enhances oil and                gas production by keeping surface facility                equipment, pipelines, downhole injection tubing and                infrastructure and pore throats around injector                clean to accept an optimum water volume; and                provides 100% corrosion protection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                inhibiting corrosion at a surface and removing                hydrocarbonaceous deposits from the surface,                comprising adding either the composition or the                iron sulfide dissolver to a fluid which contacts a                surface of a wellbore or equipment used in                production, processing, transportation, storage or                separation of the fluid to inhibit corrosion and/or                remove hydrocarbonaceous deposits from the surface,                and the fluid comprising natural gas or a liquid                hydrocarbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition:                  comprises 5-95 (preferably 15-30) wt.% of the                  iron sulfide dissolver, 5-95 (preferably 20-40)                  wt.% of the organic solvent, and 5-95 (preferably                  15-30) wt.% of the corrosion inhibitor; further                  comprises either a sulfur-containing compounds                  (HS-(CH2)nOH) (VI), or a sulfur-containing                  compound comprised of thioglycolic acid,                  3,3'-dithiodipropionic acid, thiourea,                  2-mercaptoethanol, L-cysteine, tert-butyl                  mercaptan, sodium thiosulfate, ammonium                  thiosulfate, sodium thiocyanate, ammonium                  thiocyanate and/or sodium metabisulfite; and at                  least one of an asphaltene inhibitor, a paraffin                  inhibitor, a scale inhibitor, an emulsifier, a                  water clarifier, a dispersant, an emulsion                  breaker, a hydrogen sulfide scavenger, a gas                  hydrate inhibitor, a biocide, a pH modifier or a                  surfactant.n = 2-6.Preferred Components: The iron sulfide                  dissolver comprises: gluconic acid and/or its                  salt, preferably sodium gluconate, ammonium                  gluconate, potassium gluconate, lithium                  gluconate, magnesium gluconate, calcium                  gluconate, cesium gluconate, gluconic acid,                  sodium glucarate, ammonium glucarate, potassium                  glucarate, lithium glucarate, magnesium                  glucarate, calcium glucarate, cesium glucarate                  and/or glucaric acid. The organic solvent                  comprises: an alcohol, a hydrocarbon, a ketone,                  an ether, an alkylene glycol, a glycol ether, an                  amide, a nitrile, a sulfoxide, an ester and/or a                  polyol and the composition optionally comprises                  water; and preferably methanol, ethanol, ethylene                  glycol, glycerin propanol, isopropanol, butanol,                  2-ethylhexanol, hexanol, octanol, decanol,                  2-butoxyethanol, methylene glycol, 1,2-propylene                  glycol, 1,3-propylene glycol, diethylene glycol                  monomethyl ether, diethylene glycol monoethyl                  ether, ethylene glycol monobutyl ether, ethylene                  glycol dibutyl ether, pentane, hexane,                  cyclohexane, methylcyclohexane, heptane, decane,                  dodecane, diesel, toluene, xylene, heavy aromatic                  naphtha, cyclohexanone, diisobutyl ketone,                  diethyl ether, propylene carbonate, N-methyl                  pyrrolidinone and/or N,N-dimethylformamide. The                  corrosion inhibitor comprises at least one of (a)                  substituted imidazoline compounds of formula (I)                  or their tautomers, (b) substituted imidazoline                  compounds of formula (II) or their tautomers                  and/or (c) substituted bis-quaternized                  imidazoline compounds of formula (III). The                  corrosion inhibitor comprises substituted                  pyridinium compounds of formula (IV). The                  quaternary ammonium compound comprises either the                  quaternary ammonium salt, and the quaternary                  ammonium salt comprises a tetramethyl ammonium                  salt, a tetraethyl ammonium salt, a tetrapropyl                  ammonium salt, a tetrabutyl ammonium salt, a                  tetrahexylammonium salt, a tetraoctylammonium                  salt, a benzyltrimethylammonium salt, a                  benzyltriethylammonium salt, a phenyl trimethyl                  ammonium salt, a phenyl triethyl ammonium salt, a                  cetyl benzyldimethyl ammonium salt, a hexadecyl                  trimethyl ammonium salt, a dimethyl alkyl benzyl                  quaternary ammonium salt, a monomethyl dialkyl                  benzyl quaternary ammonium salt, a trimethyl                  benzyl quaternary ammonium salt, or a trialkyl                  benzyl quaternary ammonium salt, where the alkyl                  group has 6-24 carbon atoms, or substituted                  quaternary ammonium compounds                  (R1a-N+(R2a)(R3a)-R4a) (X2-) (V). The corrosion                  inhibitor further comprises an alkoxylated amine,                  a phosphate ester, or a monomeric or oligomeric                  fatty acid. Either the iron sulfide dissolver                  comprises sodium gluconate, the corrosion                  inhibitor comprises the imidazoline and the                  bis-quaternized imidazoline compound, and the                  solvent comprises methanol, isopropanol,                  methylene glycol and/or propylene carbonate, or                  the iron sulfide dissolver comprises sodium                  gluconate, the corrosion inhibitor comprises the                  imidazoline and the bis-quaternized imidazoline                  compound, and the solvent comprises methanol,                  ethanol, ethylene glycol and/or glycerin.R10 = 1-20C alkyl or 1-20C                  alkoxyalkyl;R12, R13 = H or 1-6C alkyl;R11, R14 = H, 1-6C alkyl, 1-6C hydroxyalkyl                  or 1-6C arylalkyl;X- = halide, carbonate, sulfonate, phosphate                  or an anion of an organic carboxylic acid;R1, R2 = unsubstituted branched, chain or                  ring 1-29C alkyl or 1-29C alkenyl and/or                  partially or fully oxygenized, sulfurized and/or                  phosphorylized branched, chain, or ring 1-29C                  alkyl or 1-29C alkenyl;R3, R4 = unsubstituted branched, chain or                  ring 1-29C alkylene or 1-29C alkenylene and/or                  partially or fully oxygenized, sulfurized and/or                  phosphorylized branched, chain, or ring 1-29C                  alkylene or 1-29C alkenylene;L1, L2 = absent, H, -COOH, -SO3H, -PO3H2,                  -COOR5, -CONH2, -CONHR5 or -CON(R5)2;R5 = alkyl (optionally branched), aryl,                  alkylaryl, alkylheteroaryl, cycloalkyl or                  heteroaryl group having from 1-10C;n = 0 or 1, and when n is 0, L2 is absent or                  H;x = 1-10;y = 1-5;R9 = 1-18C alkyl, aryl or arylalkyl,                  preferably benzyl;X1- = halide, preferably chloride;R1a-R3a = 1-20C alkyl;R4a = methyl or benzyl;    EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of: 5 wt.%                sodium gluconate; 60 wt.% distilled water; 5 wt.%                benzyl dimethyl 12-18C alkyl quaternary ammonium                chloride; 15 wt.% ethanol; and 15 wt.% propylene                carbonate.

PN CN107897625-A
TI Processing nutrition mushroom carbonated beverage comprises providing Gardenia, pomegranate flower, sesame flower and Styphnolobium japonicum flower, crushing, storing, removing out, cooking, fermenting, mixing, sterilizing and packaging.
AU JIANG K
AE BENGBU KUNPENG FOOD & BEVERAGE CO LTD (BENG-Non-standard)
GA 201830211D
AB    NOVELTY - Processing nutrition mushroom carbonated beverage comprises (i) providing Gardenia, pomegranate flower, sesame flower and Styphnolobium japonicumflower, crushing, storing, removing out, cooking, fermenting, (ii) mixing Hericium erinaceus, mushroom and white fungus and pulping, fermenting, (iii) crushing and pulping lemon, fig, orange peel and mulberry leaves, stirring, concentrating, (iv) mixing mushroom liquid and juice extraction solution, sterilizing and (v) mixing water, fermented mixed solution and extracting liquid, rotating, sterilizing and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for processing nutrition mushroom carbonated beverage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing nutrition mushroom carbonated beverage comprises (i) providing Gardenia, pomegranate flower, sesame flower and Styphnolobium japonicumflower, crushing and mixing, firstly storing under the temperature low temperature of -11 to 8 degrees C for 11-13 hours, removing out and adding 5-8 times of total mass of water, cooking at 60-70 degrees C for 50-60 minutes, where the pressure is 1-2Mpa, adding microbial inoculum to fermenting for 30-32 hours, centrifuging and filtering at low temperature, concentrating to 1/5 of the original volume to obtain the flower extracting solution, (ii) mixing the Hericium erinaceus, mushroom and white fungus and pulping to obtain mixed slurry, where the frequency of ultrasonic wave is 17 kHz and 25 kHz ultrasonic oscillating for 25-30 minutes, alternating frequency is 1/2 minutes, adding water and microbial inoculum into the sealed fermentation, fermenting at 26-30 degrees C for 35-38 hours, filtering to obtain mushroom residue and extracting liquid, concentrating the extracting liquid at low temperature to 1/4 of the original volume to obtain the mushroom liquid, (iii) crushing and pulping the lemon, fig, orange peel and mulberry leaves, adding mushroom, stirring and mixing for 24-28 hours, centrifugally filtering, concentrating to 1/5 of the original volume to obtain the juice extraction solution, (iv) mixing the mushroom liquid and juice extraction solution according to mass ratio of 3-4:1, adopting 12-14 kHz ultrasonic wave oscillating for 23-27 minutes, adding 0.5-1 wt.% microbial inoculum fermenting at 16-18 degrees C for 32-35 hours, sterilizing to obtain the fermented mixed liquid and (v) mixing water, fermented the mixed solution and extracting liquid according to the mass ratio is 10-12:5-7: 3, firstly rotating at the speed of 3100-3400 revolutions/minutes, stirring for 20-30 minutes, filling carbon dioxide, sterilizing, packaging to obtain the mushroom carbonated beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the microbial inoculum, includes Bifidobacterium bulgaricus and Lactobacillus delbrueckii the quality ratio is 3:2 and the low-temperature concentration temperature is 37-40 degrees C. In the step (ii), the amount of water added is 4-6 times of the mixed slurry mass. The amount of microbial inoculum added is 2-3 % of the water quality. In the step (ii), the mushroom carbonated beverage comprises 20-30 pts. wt. Hericium erinaceus, 11-14 pts. wt. mushroom, 8-12 pts. wt. white fungus, 7-10 pts. wt. lemon, 6-8 pts. wt. fig, 4-6 pts. wt. orange peel, 3-5 pts. wt. mulberry leaves, 3-5 pts. wt. Gardenia flower, 2-5 pts. wt. pomegranate flower, 2-4 pts. wt. sesame flower, 1-3 pts. wt. Styphnolobium japonicumflower and 1-2 pts. wt. loquat.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN107827148-A; CN107827148-B
TI Preparing cross indium oxide nano-material comprises e.g. adding urea into anhydrous ethanol, adding indium(III) chloride tetrahydrate and deionized water, transferring final solution into stainless steel reactor and centrifuging.
AU LIANG S
   ZHU J
   WANG C
   HU Z
   MA D
   QUAN S
AE UNIV LINYI (UNLY-C)
   UNIV LINYI (UNLY-C)
GA 2018251070
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cross indium oxide nano-material comprises e.g. (i) adding urea into anhydrous ethanol, stirring until to dissolve urea, (ii) weighing indium(III) chloride tetrahydrate in small beaker, adding deionized water to obtain colorless and transparent InCl3.4H2O solution, (iii) adding urea solution into InCl3.4H2O solution, stirring to obtain mixed solution, then adding triton into mixed solution, (iv) transferring final solution into PTFE-lined stainless steel reactor, reacting at 120 degrees C for 12 hours, (v) centrifuging product, washing with ethanol, and (vi) drying product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cross indium oxide nano-material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves gas-sensing performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cross indium oxide nano-material comprises (i) adding urea into 120 ml anhydrous ethanol, and stirring until urea to dissolve urea, (ii) weighing indium(III) chloride tetrahydrate (InCl3.4H2O) in small beaker, then adding 10 ml deionized water, and stirring until to dissolve InCl3.4H2O to obtain colorless and transparent InCl3.4H2O solution, (iii) adding urea solution into InCl3.4H2O solution, stirring for 30 minutes to obtain mixed solution, then adding 2 ml triton into mixed solution, and stirring for 20 minutes, (iv) transferring final solution into 100 ml polytetrafluoroethylene-lined stainless steel reactor, moving reactor into oven, and reacting at 120 degrees C for 12 hours, (v) cooling reactor, centrifuging product in reactor, washing with ethanol for many times, and removing possible impurity ions, (vi) drying product in oven at 60 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain white powder as indium(III) hydroxide (In(OH)3(t)), (vii) placing obtained white powder in muffle furnace, synthesizing indium oxide nano-material having good dispersibility by heating at 500 degrees C in air for 1 hour to obtain product as indium(III) oxide (In2O3(t)), and (viii) in hydrothermal process, urea reaction is: reacting urea with water to obtain 2 moles of ammonia and liberating carbon dioxide, then reacting ammonia with water to obtain ammonium cation and hydroxy ion, then reacting indium with 3 moles of hydroxy ion to obtain indium(III) hydroxide, then reacting indium with n moles of urea to obtain n moles of indium-urea, and then reacting n moles of indium-urea with 3 moles of hydroxy ion to obtain indium(III) oxide-m moles of urea and (n-m) moles of urea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), adding certain amount of urea, and taking anhydrous ethanol as solvent; and in step (iii), adding 2 ml triton, performing hydrothermal synthesis and heat treatment to obtain cross-type indium oxide gas sensitive material. In step (iv), temperature of oven is 120 degrees C, and duration is 12 hours. In step (vi), oven drying temperature is 60 degrees C, and drying time is 24 hours. In step (vii), temperature of muffle furnace is 500 degrees C, and heat treatment time is 1 hour.

PN CN107751684-A
TI Preparing fruit juice by soaking dragon fruit piece in crabapple juice, subjecting to e.g. pressurizing process, placing dried crabapple powder in dragon fruit juice, subjecting to e.g. UV irradiation and mixing dragon fruit piece and juice.
AU WU Y
AE WU Y (WUYY-Individual)
GA 201820154M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing fruit juice involves (a) cutting one portion of red-heart dragon fruit into piece and juicing another portion, (b) freeze-drying one portion of crabapple and crushing and pulping another portion, (c) freeze-drying dragon fruit piece, adding to reaction kettle, adding crabapple juice, soaking, subjecting to pressurizing and depressurizing process repeatedly, (d) sealing dried crabapple powder with cloth bag, placing in dragon fruit juice, subjecting to UV irradiation and microwave irradiation repeatedly, and (e) mixing dragon fruit piece and dragon fruit juice.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fruit juice.
   ADVANTAGE - The crabapple enriches dragon fruit juice flavor and widens the drinking population.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing fruit juice involves (a) taking one portion of red-heart dragon fruit, peeling, cutting into small block cubes to obtain dragon fruit piece, taking another portion of the red-heart dragon fruit, peeling and juicing to obtain dragon fruit juice, (b) taking one portion of the crabapple, freeze-drying and crushing to obtain dried crabapple powder, taking another portion of the crabapple fruit, pulping, adding the pulp into a reaction kettle, passing carbon dioxide to a pressure of 100 MPa for 30 minutes, reducing the pressure to atmospheric pressure within 60 seconds, removing, adding pectinase, carrying out enzymolyzis at 35-40 degrees C for 90-150 minutes and filtering to obtain crabapple juice, (c) freeze-drying the dragon fruit piece obtained in the step (a), adding into the reaction kettle, adding the crabapple juice to the kettle, soaking the dragon fruit piece, firstly pressurizing to 500 kPa for 5-10 minutes, depressurizing to 20 kPa for 5-10 minutes and repeating the pressurizing and depressurizing operation for 3-5 times, (d) sealing the dried crabapple powder obtained in the step (b) with a cloth bag, placing into the dragon fruit juice, subjecting to UV irradiation at a frequency of 20 kHz for 3-5 minutes, subjecting to microwave irradiation at a power of 100 W for 30-40 minutes, and repeating the UV irradiation and microwave irradiation for 3-5 times, and (e) mixing the dragon fruit piece obtained in the step (c) and the dragon fruit juice obtained in the step (d) in a volume ratio of 1:4.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Component: The dragon fruit small block in step (a) is cuboid or cube having a volume of 1 cm3. Preferred Method: In step (a), the dragon fruit juice is prepared by peeling the dragon fruit, slicing, juicing and sieving with 100 mesh sieve. In step (b), the crabapple seed is removed before drying. The step (d) involves heating the dragon fruit juice at 30-35 degrees C and placing into a bag filled with dried crabapple powder. The step (e) further involves maintaining the mixture at 500-1000 MPa for 10-20 minutes.

PT P
PN CN107668593-A
TI Processing low sugar yellow peach can involves peeling the yellow peach, immersing yellow peach in treating liquid for peeling process, soaking yellow peach in the mixed enzyme solution after peeling, and then sealing peeled yellow peach.
AU SUN J
AE JIESHOU ZHONGJIAN FAMILY FARM (JIES-Non-standard)
GA 201814770A
AB    NOVELTY - Processing low sugar yellow peach can involves peeling the yellow peach. The yellow peach is immersed in the treating liquid for peeling process, firstly at 57-60 degrees C for 8-10 min, then under 0-3 degrees C for 6-8 minutes, repeatedly soaking for 5-6 times at high and low temperature. The yellow peach is soaked in the mixed enzyme solution for 12-15 minutes after peeling. The peeled yellow peach is sealed in the container.
   USE - Method for processing low sugar yellow peach can.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing low sugar yellow peach can involves peeling the yellow peach. The yellow peach is immersed in the treating liquid for peeling process, firstly at 57-60 degrees C for 8-10 min, then under 0-3 degrees C for 6-8 minutes, repeatedly soaking for 5-6 times at high and low temperature. The yellow peach is soaked in the mixed enzyme solution for 12-15 minutes after peeling. The peeled yellow peach is sealed in the container. The color protection solution is steamed into the closed container for color protection treatment. The temperature is 72-75 degrees C. The pressure is 0.4-0.6mpa. The time is 40-45 minutes. The concentration of the carbon dioxide is 13-15%. 280-300g yellow peach is added in the 500g tank. 1/2 of the sugar solution is added in the tank. The temperature of the obtained mixture is raised to 63-66 degrees C for 4-6 minutes. 1/2 of the sugar solution is again added in the tank. The temperature of the obtained mixture is raised to 75-80 degrees C for 12-14 minutes. The obtained product is placed in the exhaust box heater at 75-80/oC for 15-17 minutes. The sealed can is preheated at 68-70 degrees C for 6-8 minutes, and then heated at 120-123 degrees C for 15-20 seconds. The obtained product is cooled at 35-40 degrees C with cooling speed of 3 degrees C/min. The obtained product is illuminated in presence of ultraviolet light irradiation for 24-27 minutes with illumination intensity is 270-300lux. The obtained product is stored in a shady place at 7-10 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The processing liquid is prepared by weighing 13-16 pts. wt. lemon, 11-14 pts. wt. orange peel, 7-10 pts. wt. mango peel and 4-6 pts. wt. grape juice. The obtained mixture is ground. The obtained ground mixture is added to the total mass 4-6 times water. The obtained mixture is boiled at 105-110 min, filtered and concentrated to 1/4 of the original volume to prepare a treatment solution. Preferred Conditions: The mixed enzyme liquid has a mass ratio of pectinase: protease: cellulase of 2-3: 0.4-0.6:1, its mass concentration is 3-5%. Preferred Components: The mixed liquid comprises 100-120 pts. wt. water, 4-6 pts. wt. citric acid, 3-5 pts. wt. vitamin C, 2-4 pts. wt. follic acid, 1-3 pts. wt. tannin and 4-7 pts. wt. aloe juice. The sugar solution comprises 100-120 pts. wt. sterile water, 23-27 pts. wt. plant polysaccharide, 7-10 pts. wt. xylitol, 1-3 pts. wt. citric acid and 2-4 pts. wt. sucrose. The extracting solution is prepared by mixing Hericium erinaceus, fig, kiwi fruit, Tremella and medlar. The obtained mixture is added with 40-50 wt.% anhydrous ethanol to form a precipitate. The obtained mixture is filtered, and then dried.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN107646554-A
TI Breeding high temperature resistant and high-yielding loquat coriander comprises softening the small leaf coriander seeds, magnetizing breeding, greenhouse seedling and greenhouse planting.
AU HU C
AE DANGTU HAINA AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DANG-Non-standard)
GA 201811625U
AB    NOVELTY - Breeding high temperature resistant and high-yielding loquat coriander comprises (i) immersing the small leaf coriander seeds in organic acid solution and immersing in protein solution to soften the seed, (ii) obtaining mutagenized seed, (iii) spraying organic acid solution and protein solution to greenhouse seedling bed soil, covering film, sowing the mutagenized seed for greenhouse breeding and culturing to obtain lobular coriander seedlings, (iv) planting the small leaf coriander seedling in green house, spraying the protein solution and the organic acid solution and culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for breeding high temperature resistant and high-yielding loquat coriander.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Breeding high temperature resistant and high-yielding loquat coriander comprises (i) immersing the small leaf coriander seeds in organic acid solution for constant temperature culturing and immersing in protein solution at 4-6 degrees C for 5-6 days to soften the seed, (ii) softening the seeds gently using the plate to obtain broken seeds, soaking the broken seeds first in the protective solution at 24-28 degrees C under a pressure of 0.2-0.3 MPa for 1-2 hours, immersing in the mutagen solution for 1-2 hours, breaking the shell seeds, immersing in the protective solution for magnetization breeding at 30-34 degrees C, where the volume concentration of the carbon dioxide is 0.1-0.14 %. the volume concentration of the oxygen is 26-28 %, switching frequency alternatively with a frequency of 2-3MPa and 5- 6MPa for every 1/20min, circulating for 4-5 times, culturing at 30-34 degrees C for 3-4 hours, performing magnetization breeding, circularly magnetization breeding and culturing for 3-4 times to obtain mutagenized seed, (iii) spraying organic acid solution and protein solution to greenhouse seedling bed soil, covering film after allowing to stand for 1 day at 40-45 degrees C, sowing the mutagenized seed for greenhouse breeding and culturing until emergence to obtain lobular coriander seedlings, (iv) planting the small leaf coriander seedling in green house at 32-37 degrees C, spraying the protein solution once for every 2-3 days, spraying the organic acid solution once for 5-6 days, selecting the plant with fast growth to collect the seeds, culturing the small leaf coriander seed to the 3 generation at 15-20 degrees C for one season, removing slowly growing plants, culturing the remaining plants and collected seeds for large-scale cultivation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the organic acid solution comprises 75-82 pts. wt. water, 7-9 pts. wt. citric acid, 4-6 pts. wt. malic acid, 3-5 pts. wt. tartaric acid and 1-3 pts. wt. theine. In the step (ii), the protective solution comprises vitamin C, vitamin E and folic acid in a mass ratio of 3: 2-3: 1, the mass concentration of the mutagen solution is 11-14% and the mass concentration of the diazo serine is 1 -2%. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the preparation of the protein solution comprises mixing 27-33 pts. wt. fresh soya milk, 19-24 pts. wt. corn syrup and 12-16 pts. wt. fresh milk, adding 1 -2% of total mass of the protease, carrying out enzymolysis at 28-32 degrees C for 2-3 hours, adding 3-4% of total mass of fermentation microbial inoculums and fermenting for 24-27 hours, where the fermentation microbial inoculums comprises lactic acid bacteria and Bacillus subtilis in a mass ratio of 2-3:1-2. In the step (iii), the greenhouse seedling, sowing the mutagenized seeds sown to greenhouse bed, maintaining the temperature to 27-30 degrees C and the humidity to 53-58% on 12 days before sowing and maintaining the temperature to 32-37 degrees C and the humidity to 65-70% on 12 days after sowing.

PT P
PN CN107599234-A; CN107599234-B
TI Waste aluminum-plastic separation recycling technology comprises e.g. adding waste aluminum-plastic composite package material into crushing machine to crush into particles and placing crushed aluminum-plastic material into stirring barrel.
AU ZHANG S
AE ANHUI TENGYUE ALUMINUM PLASTIC CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018081013
AB    NOVELTY - Waste aluminum-plastic separation recycling technology comprises e.g. (i) adding waste aluminum-plastic composite package material into crushing machine to crush into particles, placing crushed aluminum-plastic material into stirring barrel, adding organic solvent to completely immerse aluminum-plastic material, stirring, floating organic solvent on surface of plastic material and precipitating aluminum foil at the bottom of stirring barrel, and (ii) cleaning back of plastic material to granulate remodeling, drying to obtain finished product of plastic and depositing aluminum foil.
   USE - The method is useful for waste aluminum-plastic separation recycling technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes complete separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Waste aluminum-plastic separation recycling technology comprises (i) adding waste aluminum-plastic composite package material into crushing machine to crush into particles and then placing the crushed aluminum-plastic material into stirring barrel, adding organic solvent to completely immerse the aluminum-plastic material, stirring at rotating speed of 200-500 revolution/minute (rpm) for 2-8 hours, floating organic solvent on the surface of the plastic material and precipitating the aluminum foil at the bottom of stirring barrel respectively by clean water, (ii) cleaning the back of the plastic material to granulate remodeling, drying to obtain finished product of plastic and depositing the aluminum foil on the bottom of the stirring barrel into a reaction container to carry out secondary separation, (iii) adding 1-2 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution in the reaction container, stirring at 30-70 degrees C for 2-5 hours, filtering and washing the filtrate, filtering and separating to obtain the plastic material to granulate remodeling, (iv) adding 1-2 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution in the filtrate obtained in the step (iii), stirring and adding poly-aluminum chloride, reacting for 3-5 hours at 60-90 degrees C, (v) taking the filter liquid, adding ammonia water until the solution pH to 10, and then filtering to obtain the precipitate, adding 0.3-1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution until completely dissolved, filling carbon dioxide until the solution pH to 5-7, then aging for 18-48 hours, then filtering, washing and drying to obtain aluminum hydroxide precipitate, and (vi) placing the aluminum hydroxide precipitate at 1200 degrees C to obtain aluminum oxide solid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is acetone, chloroform, diethyl ether, benzene, and/or toluene. The mass ratio of the aluminum foil and the hydrochloric acid in stirring barrel bottom is 1:10-2. In the step (iv), volume ratio of filtrate and the sodium hydroxide is 1:0.9-1.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.)

PT P
PN CN108409530-A; CN108409530-B
TI Device useful for producing a low-carbon mixed alcohol from organic waste comprises a thermal decomposition carbonization section of an organic waste, a synthesis gas preparation section and a catalytic synthesis section.
AU WU H
   QIAN F
   LIU A
   JIANG H
   QIN W
   ZHONG T
AE WU H (WUHH-Individual)
   WU H (WUHH-Individual)
GA 2018659228
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a thermal decomposition carbonization section of an organic waste, a synthesis gas preparation section and a catalytic synthesis section, where the thermal decomposition carbonization section of the organic waste comprises a reaction system and a heating system. The reaction system includes a feeder, a cracking reactor, a vacuum pump and a discharge conveyor. The feeder is connected to the feed port of the cracking reactor through a pipe. An automatic grate is disposed in the cracking reactor, which is connected to the vacuum pump to be cracked. The discharge pipe of the reactor is connected to the discharge conveyor, which is connected to the milling machine of the synthesis gas preparation section through a pipe. The heating system comprises a molten salt heating furnace, a cracking reactor and a molten salt circulation pump and the heat outlet pipe of the molten salt heating furnace is directly connected to the cracking reactor and the cracking reaction.
   USE - The device is useful for producing low-carbon mixed alcohol from organic waste (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of landfill reduction and harmlessness, realizes resource utilization of organic waste, turns waste into valuable and lowers cost of low-carbon mixed alcohols e.g. methanol and ethanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises a thermal decomposition carbonization section of an organic waste, a synthesis gas preparation section and a catalytic synthesis section, where the thermal decomposition carbonization section of the organic waste comprises a reaction system and a heating system. The reaction system includes a feeder (101), a cracking reactor (102), a vacuum pump (112) and a discharge conveyor (105). The feeder is connected to the feed port of the cracking reactor through a pipe. An automatic grate is disposed in the cracking reactor, which is connected to the vacuum pump (112) to be cracked. The discharge pipe of the reactor is connected to the discharge conveyor, which is connected to the milling machine of the synthesis gas preparation section through a pipe. The heating system comprises a molten salt heating furnace, a cracking reactor and a molten salt circulation pump and the heat outlet pipe of the molten salt heating furnace is directly connected to the cracking reactor and the cracking reaction. The molten salt outlet of the device is connected to the molten salt heating furnace via a molten salt circulation pump. The synthesis gas preparation section includes a gasification system and a purification system. The gasification system includes a mill, a carbon slurry tank, a carbon slurry pump, a gasifier and a ash separator. The mill is connected to the carbon slurry tank. The carbon slurry tank is connected to the gasifier through a carbon slurry pump and the gasifier is provided with a compressed air inlet and a high pressure steam inlet, an outlet pipe of the gasifier is connected to the ash separator and a slag outlet of the gasifier is connected to the solid waste treatment system. The gas phase outlet pipe of the ash separator is connected to the regulator and the gas phase adjustment pipe and the slag pipe of the ash separator is connected to the solid waste treatment system. The purification system includes a regulator, a syngas compressor, a purification preheater, a syngas gas liquid separator, a purification filter and a carbon dioxide absorption tower. The regulator is connected to the purification preheater through the syngas compressor and the outlet pipe of the purification preheater is sequentially combined with the syngas gas liquid separator and the purification filter and connected to the carbon dioxide absorption tower. The top gas phase outlet of the carbon dioxide absorption tower is connected to the gas phase adjustment tube after heat exchange by the purification preheater and the gas phase adjustment tube and the catalytic synthesis section is connected to the syngas preheater. The catalytic synthesis section includes a syngas preheater, a reaction gas heat exchanger, a reaction start heater, a synthesis reactor, a boiler water heat exchanger and product cooling, high pressure gas-liquid separator and a low pressure gas-liquid separator. The syngas preheater is connected to the reaction gas heat exchanger and the reaction starter heater is connected to the synthesis reactor and the outlet pipe of the synthesis reactor is sequentially passed through the reaction gas. The heat exchanger, the boiler water heat exchanger and the product cooler are then connected to a high pressure gas-liquid separator. The high pressure gas-liquid separator is connected to a low pressure gas-liquid separator and the outlet tube of the low pressure gas-liquid separator is connected to the mixed alcohol storage tank. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing a low-carbon mixed alcohol from organic waste using the device comprising (i) subjecting to thermal cracking carbonization, adding the organic waste to the feed pyrolysis reactor, which is subjected to thermal cracking carbonization reaction under the conditions of a vacuum degree of 8-22 Kpa at an anaerobic environment with a reaction temperature of 420-650 degrees C, and a reaction time of 1.5-4.5 hours to obtain biochar, which is used as a raw material for syngas generation; (ii) preparing synthesis gas by grinding the biochar, transferring to a carbon slurry tank and adjusting to a concentration of 80-85% carbon water slurry, then transferring to a gasifier for pressurized gasification with compressed air and high pressure steam, where the reaction pressure is 2.8-8.7 MPa, reaction temperature 1250-1400 degrees C, producing carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and other syngas through the ash separator to further remove solid, liquid waste, treated syngas through the gas regulator, which controls the molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide to 1:1.2-2.5, purifying the synthesis gas and delivering it to the catalytic synthesis section; and (iii) pressurizing the synthesis gas to 10-12 MPa by a compressor, then adding into the synthesis reactor at a temperature of 280-350 degrees C, subjecting to catalytic synthesis under the action of a catalyst and calculating the volumetric space velocity by dry gas at 2000-15000 h-1 to obtain crude mixed alcohol of methyl, ethanol and other alcohols, and removing most of the gas of the crude mixed alcohol by depressurization and flash distillation, and separating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is cobalt-molybdenum catalyst, modified zinc oxide/chromium catalyst, copper-zinc catalyst, copper-cobalt catalyst and molybdenum disulfide catalyst, and the ratio is 1:0.2-1. Preferred Method: The method comprises dying the organic waste to a moisture content of less than15% and pulverizing before being fed to the feeder. The method comprises when the step (i) is thermally cracked, the organic matter in the material is thermally decomposed to form a gas phase, and is quickly pumped away from the cracking reactor by a vacuum pump under vacuum, and sequentially conveyed through a heavy oil condenser and a light water condenser for gas-liquid condensation separation. The non-condensable gas separated by the bio-fuel and the light water condenser condensed by the heavy oil condenser is led back to the molten salt heating furnace for combustion utilization and the processed water is condensed by the light water condenser, which is sent to the mill of the syngas section for carrying out use. In step (ii) the gas adjustment is that the treated synthesis gas is first treated by a regulator, then pressurized by a purification preheater for heat exchange, and then subjected to gas-liquid separation and removal of the liquid phase through a synthesis gas gas-liquid separator, and synthesized, where the gas enters the purification filter to filter and remove impurities, and finally passes into the carbon dioxide absorption tower to remove carbon dioxide and other gases to obtain purified purified synthesis gas. The purified syngas is then subjected to heat exchange through a preheating device, and the purified syngas is in compliance with a molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide of 1:1.2-2.5, and then transported to a catalytic synthesis section through a gas regulating tube to continue to flow back to the regulator for cyclic adjustment.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The material dryer is added in front of the feeder for cracking the carbon chemical section. The outlet pipe of the vacuum pump is sequentially connected to the heavy oil condenser and the light water condenser and the light water condenser. The condensing water pipe of is sent to the milling machine of the synthesis gas preparation section through the process water pump. The non-condensable gas pipe of the light water condenser is connected to the molten salt heating furnace through the non-condensable gas pump. The condensation pipe of the heavy oil condenser is passed through the heavy oil pump and the molten salt heating furnace. The syngas preparation section further includes an absorbent delivery system including a high pressure flash irrigation, a normal pressure flash irrigation, an absorbent circulation pump, an absorbent pressurizing pump and a high pressure. A liquid heater, an absorbent heat exchanger and a fresh absorbent delivery tube is connected to the atmospheric pressure flash irrigation and the absorbent circulation pump. The outlet of the absorbent circulation pump is connected to the absorbent heat exchanger and the absorbent heat exchanger is connected to the upper portion of the carbon dioxide absorption tower through an absorbent pressurizing pump. The absorbent waste liquid pipe at the bottom of the carbon dioxide absorption tower, which is sequentially connected to the high pressure flash irrigation, the absorbent heat exchanger, the high pressure liquid heater and the atmospheric pressure flash irrigation. The atmospheric pressure flash irrigation is connected to the absorbent circulation pump through a pipe. The catalytic synthesis section further includes a medium pressure steam package, a suction irrigation and a recycle gas compressor and the intermediate pressure steam package is bidirectionally coupled to the synthesis reactor. The circulating gas compressor is disposed in front of the syngas preheater line, the high pressure gas liquid separator and the low pressure gas liquid separator, the gas phase tube and the suction tube and the synthesis gas preparation. The purification system of the section is connected to the suction irrigation, which is connected to the recycle gas compressor.

   YANG B
   WU S
   WU Z
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202262964R
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical chain reaction method driven by biomass with high nitrogen content involves introducing biomass with high nitrogen content into pyrolysis reactor, pyrolyzing at 600-900degrees Celsius, generating pyrolysis gas with components of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, nitrogen and bio-coke with component carbon, conveying pyrolysis gas into first reduction reactor, reducing metal oxygen carrier at 600-1000degrees Celsius into metal simple substance or low-valent metal oxide, obtaining mixed gas of carbon dioxide, water and nitrogen, introducing metal simple substance or low-valent metal oxide into hydrolysis reactor, reacting at 600-1000degrees Celsius with introduced water to generate hydrogen, obtaining high-valent metal oxide, introducing high-valent metal oxide into oxidation reactor, generating oxidation reaction with oxygen in air at 600-1000degrees Celsius, realizing regeneration of metal oxygen carrier, and introducing regenerated metal oxygen carrier into first reduction reactor.
   USE - The method is used for chemical chain reacting driven by biomass with high nitrogen content.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide generated in process can be used for culturing nitrogen-containing algae after recycling so that whole synthetic ammonia process has characteristic of carbon negative. The method realizes energy recovery, step utilization and reduces synthetic ammonia process energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system of chemical chain driven by biomass with high nitrogen content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves introducing bio-coke into second reduction reactor (9) as reduction agent, carrying out reduction reaction with nitrogen-carrying body under nitrogen atmosphere at 700-1600degrees Celsius to obtain metal nitride, introducing metal nitride into synthesis ammonia reactor, reacting at 100-1200degrees Celsius with introduced water to generate ammonia, realizing regeneration of nitrogen carrier and introducing regenerated nitrogen carrier into second reduction reactor. The method further involves introducing hydrogen into synthesis ammonia reactor, generating ammonia and imino compound at 250-500degrees Celsius, obtaining corresponding alkali (earth) metal hydride as hydrogen carrier, introducing hydrogen-carrying body into regeneration reactor, performing nitriding reaction with nitrogen at 200-400degrees Celsius, obtaining imino compound corresponding to alkali (earth) metal hydride, realizing regeneration of imino compound, and introducing imino compound into synthesis ammonia reactor.Preferred Components: The nitrogen carrier is alumina, iron(III) oxide, chromium nitride and manganese nitride encapsulated by molecular sieve. The metal nitride obtained after reduction reaction is high valence state metal nitride corresponding to nitrogen carrier. The hydrogen-carrying body is alkali (earth) metal hydride lithium hydride, barium hydride, calcium hydride and magnesium hydride encapsulated by molecular sieves and reacted with nitrogen to obtain corresponding imino compound.

PN CN114149071-A
TI Producing autoclaved porous phosphorus removal biological filter material, involves using carbon-dioxide hardened water glass waste sand, mechanically crushing, washing, rubbing, and stirring waste sand to remove residual binder film.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG L
   ZHAO Z
   FANG C
   LIU Y
   MA H
AE UNIV WEIFANG (UNWF-C)
GA 2022400104
AB    NOVELTY - Producing autoclaved porous phosphorus removal biological filter material, involves using carbon-dioxide hardened water glass waste sand, mechanically crushing, washing, rubbing, and stirring the broken waste sand at high temperature to remove the residual binder film on surface of sand grains, and then discharging the sewage with the binder dissolved in the old sand, and the waste water is for use. The washed sand is ball milled and sieved. The ball mill sieved waste sand, cement, lime, fly ash, gypsum and other materials are mixed uniformly and set aside. The pore-forming agent is added in proportion, stirred uniformly to prepare the premix. The waste water is added standby to the premix, and used a granulator to obtain raw pellets of a certain size after stirring. The raw pellets are pre-cured and autoclaved in accordance with the technical requirements of conventional autoclaved curing concrete, and then discharged.
   USE - Method for producing autoclaved porous phosphorus removal biological filter material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to effectively recycles carbon-dioxide hardened water glass foundry waste sand, and does not generate additional waste water, so that the prepared a high value-added porous autoclave curing phosphorus removal biological filter material has high economic value and environmental benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pore-forming agent is aluminum powder or commercially available aluminum powder paste. Preferred Conditions: The number of mechanically crushed meshes is not less than 40 meshes. The water temperature is not lower than 60degrees Celsius. The scrubbing time is not less than 20 minutes. The number of the screened meshes are not less than 150 meshes. The water amount required is 30-35 wt.% total powder. The curing is performed at 150-160degrees Celsius for 8-10 hours.

TI Preparation of catalyst used for dry reforming reaction of methane and carbon dioxide, involves weighing cerium sulphate dissolved in water, weighing potassium hydroxide dissolved in water, stirring both solution and reacting under room temperature, then performing hydrothermal synthesis.
AU ZHANG Y
   HUANG Z
   YIN Z
   ZHOU Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202232135X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of catalyst, involves 1) weighing cerium sulphate dissolved in water, obtaining solution A, weighing potassium hydroxide dissolved in water, obtaining solution B, stirring and reacting under room temperature state, then performing hydrothermal synthesis method to obtain cerium dioxide 2) centrifuging and washing cerium dioxide drying, grinding and calcining to obtain catalyst carrier, 3) preparing mass fraction is 1-5 % of nickel sulphate solution, dropping ammonia water into continuously stirred nickel sulphate solution, adjusting pH value of solution is 11 to obtain mixed system, to obtain catalyst carrier is soaked in mixed system solution of nickel, stirring for 24h at room temperature, taking out, washing and drying, grinding and roasting to obtain Ni/CeO2, 4) preparing rare earth salt solution with mass fraction of 1-10 %, soaking Ni/CeO2 prepared in step 3) in rare earth salt solution, drying by oil bath, grinding and calcining to obtain catalyst.
   USE - Preparation of catalyst used for dry reforming reaction of methane and carbon dioxide by solar light condensation catalysis(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent catalytic effect on photo-driven carbon dioxide conversion photo-thermal catalysis at low temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a TEM image of rod-like ceria.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Weighing 1.736g cerium sulphate dissolved in 10 mL de-ionized water to obtain solution A, weighing 10.6g potassium hydroxide to dissolve in 70 mL deionized water to obtain solution B, pouring the solution B into the solution A, the stirring speed is reaction of 400rmin-1, then performing hydrothermal synthesis to obtain cerium dioxide, hydrothermal synthesis temperature is 200 degreesC, heat preservation time is 24h.The cerium dioxide is dried for 24h at 60 degreesC, and calcining is processed for 2h at 400-600 degreesC.The mass percentage of ammonia water is 28 %, in the step 3), washing method is to wash for 5-6 times with de-ionized water, drying method is vacuum drying at 80 degreesC, time is more than or equal to 24h, roasting temperature is 400 degreesC, salt solution of catalyst carrier and nickel is mixed according to dosage ratio of 1 g: 100.Calcining temperature is 300-400 degreesC for 2h, oil bath pot oil bath 48h.The load amount of rare earth metal is 1-10 % of catalytic carrier.

AB    NOVELTY - The refrigeration system (800) has a rotary                pressure exchanger (802) fluidly coupled to a low                pressure loop (804) and a high pressure loop (806). PN CN113431546-A
TI Method for improving shale porosity and permeability, involves carrying out granular shale oil deposited on rock surface during rapid flowback process, which improves surrounding area of well, and occurring hydration after injected water penetrates to improve sheet structure.
AU ZHAO J
AE DAQING JINJU PETROLEUM TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (DAQI-Non-standard)
GA 2021B4769S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves piercing and broking the small particle bridges formed into single particles or relatively high pressure. The small clusters of particles are carried to the wellbore by a large amount of supercritical carbon dioxide flowback. A large amount of calcium and magnesium in the shale react with the injected carbon dioxide and waste triethylene glycol liquid around the well to generate triethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, and carbon dioxide complexes, and the products destroy the rocks near the well. The upper adsorbed oil film transforms the rock is smoothed by the adsorbed oil film into a rough rock that adsorbs granular oil droplets. The granular shale oil deposited on the rock surface is carried out during the rapid flowback process, which improves the surrounding area of the well. The hydration occurs after the injected water penetrates to improve the sheet structure and increase the permeability.
   USE - Method for improving shale porosity and permeability in shale well.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the hole permeability condition of the reservoir, obtains the obvious oil increasing effect, and improves the hydration function after the infiltration of the injected water, which improves the permeability.

PN CN111437862-A
TI Catalyst used for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to            produce methanol, is prepared by dispersing graphite            phase carbon nitride, copper, zinc oxide and aluminum            oxide on layer structure of graphite phase carbon            nitride.
AU MA Q
   ZHANG Y
   ZHAO T
   ZHANG J
   GAO X
   LI P
   FAN H
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 202071779X
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst is prepared by dispersing graphite                phase carbon nitride, copper, zinc oxide and                aluminum oxide on the layer structure of the                graphite phase carbon nitride, where the mass ratio                of the total mass of copper, zinc oxide and                aluminum oxide to the mass of graphite phase carbon                nitride is (80-100):(0-20), and the amount of the                graphite phase carbon nitride is not zero.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for hydrogenating                carbon dioxide to produce methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst utilizes the special structure of                graphite phase carbon nitride, has excellent                adsorption performance, increases the specific                surface area of the catalyst, enhances adsorption                capacity, and improves the conversion rate of                carbon dioxide and the selectivity of                methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the catalyst involving (i) mixing the                soluble copper salt, soluble zinc salt, soluble                aluminum salt and water to obtain mixed solution,                (ii) mixing the mixed solution, sodium carbonate                solution and water to perform co-precipitation                reaction to obtain precipitate, (iii) subjecting                the precipitate to the first roasting to obtain                intermediate product, (iv) mixing the intermediate                product and graphite phase carbon nitride, and                performing second calcination to obtain catalyst                precursor, and (iv) using mixed gas of hydrogen and                nitrogen as a reducing gas, and reducing the                catalyst precursor to obtain the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  copper, zinc oxide and aluminum oxide is                  (4-6):(2.5-3.5):(2.5-3.5). Preferred Conditions:                  The temperature of the co-precipitation reaction                  is 60-80 degrees C, and the pH of the reaction                  liquid in which the co-precipitation reaction                  occurs is 6-8. The co-precipitation reaction is                  carried out under stirring conditions of 650-900                  rpm for 1-5 hours. The first calcination process                  is carried out at 200-400 degrees C for 2-6                  hours. The second baking is performed under                  nitrogen atmosphere, and the temperature of the                  second calcination is 400-700 degrees C, and the                  time of the second calcination is 2-5 hours. The                  volume ratio of hydrogen and nitrogen is 5:95,                  the flow rate of the mixed gas of hydrogen and                  nitrogen is 45-55 ml/minute, and the temperature                  of the reduction is 250-350 degrees C. Preferred                  Method: The method involves sequential aging at                  20-50 degrees C for 10-15 hours, solid-liquid                  separating and drying at 60-100 degrees C for                  5-15 hours of the product liquid obtained after                  the co-precipitation reaction. The graphite phase                  carbon nitride is prepared by thermal                  polymerization of cyanamide, dicyandiamide,                  melamine or urea at 400-700 degrees C.

AB    NOVELTY - The respiratory device (10) comprises a respirator (1) with a controllable respiratory gas source, a programmable control unit (21) and a sensor unit (3) for determining pressure and flow of a respiratory gas. The control unit controls the respiratory gas source to specify a first respiration pattern. A first sensor unit generates and measures electrical potentials and noninvasively determining an electrical impedance of lungs of a patient. A second sensor unit noninvasively determines a carbon dioxide supply, and a third sensor unit noninvasively determines an oxygen supply of the patient. The control unit evaluates measured values of first sensor unit to determine an instantaneous ventilation of lungs during respiration using the first respiration pattern. The control unit evaluates measured values of the third sensor unit to determine an instantaneous oxygen supply.

PT P
PN IN201841019613-A
TI Isolating Poneeru used as natural preservative, comprises fractionating soil, heavy minerals separation from resultant, isolating trona from resultant in water by purging of carbon dioxide gas, and isolating trona salt from mother liquor.
AU PARTHASARATHY K
   SHAILAJA R
   TAMILARASAN G
   SATHIYARAJESWARAN P
   RAMASWAMY R S
AE CENT COUNCIL RES SIDDHA (COUN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5853E
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating Poneeru (trona), a natural exudate, that exists as efflorescent crusts on semi-arid region soils, by leaching in water medium, comprises: (i) fractionating soil to obtain fine and very fine sand fractions; (ii) heavy minerals separation from the fractionated portion of the fine sand and very fine sand, (iii) isolating trona from fine and very fine sand fractions in water by purging of carbon dioxide gas into the reaction vessel; and (iv) isolating trona salt from the mother liquor by using freeze-drying technology.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating Poneeru, where the Poneeru is useful as a natural preservative for inhibition of growth of microorganisms (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the fractionation of soils that are necessary to obtain fine and very fine sand portions to improve the yield of trona and to develop a simple, facile and efficient process instead of using the whole soil sample; utilizes the purging of carbon dioxide that facilitates the isolation of carbonate-based sodium salt in the presence of other counter anions; extends the shelf-life of aqueous herbal decoction of polyherbal formulation Nilavembu Kudineer (NVK) upto 12 hours at higher concentrations; and exhibits minimum inhibitory concentration of 2.25 mg of trona per ml of decoction formulation of NVK (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The percentage of fine and very fine sand fractions is directly proportional to the percentage yield of trona. The isolated salt has as trona, a non-marine evaporite by modern sophisticated analytical techniques from the soils.
   EXAMPLE - Light mineral fractions obtained from fine and very find sand fractions were homogenized completely and four equivalents of water to the weight of soil was added in a double neck round bottom flask and slowly stirred by using semi-moon rod stirrer and high pure carbon dioxide gas was purged at the rate of 0.8 ml/minute, through the solution constantly for 2 hours. Soil was acidified in the earlier process, expelling of carbon dioxide Might have been occurred and purging of carbon dioxide was carried out in order to ensure the sufficient carbonate environment to get higher yield of trona. According to the Hardie-Eugster model of evaporite formation, high proportion of carbonate relative to calcium2+ was the major requirement for the formation of sodium carbonate evaporites. Soil samples contain feldspar grains necessary to increase the local concentration of carbonate ion to ensure the formation of sodium carbonate evaporites. After 2 hours the purging of carbon dioxide was stopped and the slurry solution was allowed to settle for 30 minutes. Supernatant liquid was filtered through activated carbon to remove any organic/dust particles and the volume of the liquid was measured. Entire solution was transferred into a lyophilizer flask and lyophilized by freeze-drying technology to obtain Poneeru (trona).
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PT P
PN WO2019220073-A1; GB2574117-A; GB2574117-B; CA3098393-A1; AU2019269094-A1; CN112105596-A; IN202017050796-A; EP3793970-A1; US2021371362-A1; EP3793970-B1
TI Synthesizing methanol used as fuel, by passing            feed gas to methanol synthesis loop, recovering product            gas mixture, separating crude methanol from unreacted            gas mixture and contacting crude methanol and purge gas            in stripping unit.
AU EARLY S R
   TURNBULL D M
AE JOHNSON MATTHEY DAVY TECHNOLOGIES LTD (JOHO-C)
   JOHNSON MATTHEY DAVY TECHNOLOGIES LTD (JOHO-C)
   JOHNSON MATTHEY DAVY TECHNOLOGIES LTD (JOHO-C)
   JOHNSON MATTHEY DAVY TECHNOLOGIES LTD (JOHO-C)
GA 201998272S
AB    NOVELTY - Process for synthesizing methanol, involves                (i) passing a feed gas comprising a make-up gas                containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide to a                methanol synthesis loop, (ii) recovering a product                gas mixture containing methanol from the methanol                synthesis loop, (iii) cooling the product gas                mixture to below the dew point to condense crude                methanol, (iv) separating the crude methanol from                an unreacted gas mixture, (v) passing a portion of                the unreacted gas mixture to the methanol synthesis                loop, (vi) recovering the portion of the unreacted                gas mixture as a purge gas stream, (vii) contacting                the crude methanol and a portion of the purge gas                in a stripping unit to strip dissolved gases from                the crude methanol and (viii) forming a stripped                crude methanol and an enriched gas mixture, and                feeding at least a portion of the enriched gas                mixture to the methanol synthesis loop.
   USE - The process is used for synthesizing methanol,                which is used as a fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses a portion of the purge gas,                hence stripping efficiency of dissolved gases                including carbon dioxide from the crude methanol is                maintained, the carbon dioxide requirement of the                process is reduced, and the methanol production                capacity is more readily enhanced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The make-up gas is                  generated by one or more steps of steam                  reforming, autothermal reforming, gasification of                  coal or biomass (all preferred), or partial                  oxidation. The make-up gas is generated by                  catalytic steam reforming a hydrocarbon with                  steam and optionally carbon dioxide in a fired                  steam reformer, or by combined reforming of the                  hydrocarbon by subjecting a first fraction of the                  hydrocarbon and steam to primary reforming in a                  primary steam reformer and secondary reforming a                  second fraction of the hydrocarbon, combined with                  the effluent of the primary reformer, with an                  oxygen-containing gas in an autothermal reformer.                  The carbon dioxide gas stream is added to the                  make-up gas. The crude methanol and the portion                  of the purge gas are fed to the stripping unit                  and contacted in it in a counter-current or                  co-current manner, or where the crude methanol is                  sparged with a portion of the purge gas. In the                  method, at least a portion of the purge gas is                  separated into a hydrogen-rich gas stream and a                  hydrogen-depleted gas stream and at least a                  portion of the hydrogen-rich gas stream is fed to                  the stripping unit. The hydrogen-depleted gas                  stream is used as a fuel, or is fed to the                  synthesis gas generation step to form part of the                  make-up gas. The methanol synthesis loop                  comprises one, two or more methanol synthesis                  reactors each containing a bed of methanol                  synthesis catalyst, where the product gas mixture                  is recovered from at least one methanol synthesis                  reactor, each fed with a feed gas comprising                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and each producing                  the product gas mixture, where the unreacted gas                  mixture separated from the product gas mixture                  recovered from one methanol synthesis reactor may                  be returned to the same or a different methanol                  synthesis reactor. The methanol synthesis                  reactors are cooled by a synthesis gas or by                  boiling water. The methanol synthesis catalyst is                  a copper-containing methanol synthesis catalyst.                  The methanol synthesis is effected in the                  methanol synthesis reactors at pressures of                  10-120 bar abs, and at 130-350 degrees C. The                  stripped crude methanol is subjected to one or                  more steps of distillation to produce a purified                  methanol product.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

TI Block structure used as building material, is obtained by injecting carbon dioxide into mixture containing calcium carbonate, sodium silicate and composition comprising calcium hydroxide mixed water, and forming block solid component.
AU IMAGAWA N
   AKAIWA D
AE TIS & PARTNERS CO LTD (TISP-Non-standard)
   TIS & PARTNERS CO LTD (TISP-Non-standard)
GA 201834185V
AB    NOVELTY - A block structure (AAA) is obtained by mixing and stirring (DD) a mixture (HH) obtained by stirring a composition (FF) comprising calcium silicate (M) mixed with water (CC), and a composition (EE) comprising calcium hydroxide (BB) mixed with water, sand (KK) containing silicon oxide, calcium carbonate (OO), and sodium silicate (MM), obtaining intermediate mixture (QQ), injecting (SS) carbon dioxide (TT) into the obtained intermediate mixture, and forming a solid component into a block having a fixed shape.
   USE - Block structure is used as material for inverted-T-shaped building structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous block structure has excellent durability, water resistance, strength, and compressive elasticity, and specific gravity smaller than concrete (due to the impregnation with the polymer resin). The service life of the block structure is chemically extended.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of block structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of manufacture of block structure. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Solid component as block structure (AAA)
   Calcium hydroxide (BB)
   Water (CC)
   Stirring (DD)
   Compositions (EE,FF)
   Mixture (HH)
   Sand (KK)
   Calcium silicate (M)
   Sodium silicate (MM)
   Calcium carbonate (OO)
   Intermediate mixture (QQ)
   Injection step (SS)
   Carbon dioxide (TT)
   Polymethacrylic acid (VY)
   Polymer resin (WW)
   Polyester (XX)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The block structure further comprises a polymer resin (WW), a polyester (XX) and/or a polymethacrylic acid (YY) impregnated in the solid component. The polymethacrylic acid causes a neutralization reaction with sodium hydroxide generated in the solid component during the stirring and mixing process, to improve durability of the solid component. The polyester generates carboxylic acid for neutralizing sodium hydroxide generated in the solid component during the stirring and mixing process, by being hydrolyzed by reaction with sodium hydroxide, to improve durability of the solid component.

GA 201827979E
AB    NOVELTY - Performing a laser-assisted heating chemical vapor deposition coating involves preheating, preheating the glass substrate by using a heating device, increasing local temperature, using carbon dioxide infrared laser to generate a linear light spot to warm up the preheated glass substrate, and then assisting the chemical vapor deposition reaction coating.
   USE - Method for performing a laser-assisted heating chemical vapor deposition coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform a laser-assisted heating chemical vapor deposition coating, which uses characteristic function of high energy density on substrate for rapid warming and auxiliary chemical vapor deposition reaction of plating film, has easy control, high temperature adjusting precision and is good for improving coating quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for performing laser assisted heating chemical vapor deposition coating, which comprises a fume hood, a sliding preheating system for penetrating the side of fume hood and for mounting a glass substrate, a laser heating system penetrating other side of fume hood and a sliding preheating system, and a control system connected to the laser heating system, where laser heating system is located above the slide warm-up system, and laser heating system includes an infrared carbon dioxide laser and a beam shaper for changing the path of the laser beam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: A galvo mirror is used to change laser beam path during the local temperature increase of glass substrate by the infrared laser. The linear spot has a length of 0.5-110mm and a width of 0.2-0.5mm. The power density distribution of linear spot satisfies hx Ax rho x Cx Delta t=E/ eta =Px t, where h denotes heating depth, A denotes heating area, rho denotes the glass substrate density, C represents specific heat of glass substrate, Delta t represents the temperature rise, E represents the laser heating energy, eta denotes the laser heating efficiency, P denotes the laser working power, and t denotes the laser irradiation time.

AU YAO Y
   QU L
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 201827561Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate compounds (I) comprises carrying out addition reaction alkylene oxide compounds (II) with carbon dioxide under action of catalyst in presence of quaternary ammonium salt to obtain (I), where the catalyst is ethylenediamine bridged tetraaryloxy rare earth-zinc heterobimetallic compound (III).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) have: high yield, mild reaction conditions, high catalytic efficiency under normal temperature and pressure conditions, wide reaction substrates; and is suitable for single-substitution epoxy alkane also applies to disubstituted alkylene oxides.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cyclic carbonate compounds of formula (I) comprises carrying out addition reaction alkylene oxide compounds of formula (II) with carbon dioxide under action of catalyst in presence of quaternary ammonium salt to obtain (I), where the catalyst is ethylenediamine bridged tetraaryloxy rare earth-zinc heterobimetallic compound of formula (III).
   either R1 and R2 = H, ester group (-C(=O)O-R3), alkyl, alkoxy, aryl, preferably optionally substituted 1-18C alkyl, 1-18C alkoxy or 6-14C aryl (substituted with nitro, cyano, hydroxy or halo (fluoro, chloro, bromo or iodo)); or
   R1 and R2 = alkyl, alkoxy; or
   R1R2 together = ring including 3-18C carbon ring or 2-17C oxygen-containing heterocyclic ring;
   R3 = H, 1-10C alkyl or aryl; and
   Ln = rare earth metal ion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quaternary ammonium salt is tetrabutylammonium iodide, tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetraoctylammonium bromide or bis(triphenylphosphine)ammonium chloride. The molar ratio of (III) to (II) is 1:100-500. Preferred Method: The temperature of the addition reaction is 25-90 degrees C. The preparation of (III) comprises (i) refluxing ethylenediamine, formaldehyde and 2,4-di-tert-butyl phenol in a mass ratio of 1:4:6-1:4:8 at 75-85 degrees C for 60-72 hours to obtain 6,6',6'',6'''-(ethane-1,2-diylbis(azanetriyl))tetrakis(methylene)tetrakis(2,4-di-tert-butylphenol) (IV), (ii) reacting (IV) with LnCp3 (THF) in a ratio of 1:1-1:1.05 in an ether solvent under anhydrous and oxygen-free condition at 20-50 degrees C for 4-12 hours to obtain rare-earth metal bridged complex of formula (V), and (iii) reacting hexane or toluene solution of diethyl zinc and LnL tetrahydrofuran(THF) tetrahydrofuran solution in a molar ratio of 1:05/201/1:1 at -5 to 50 degrees C for 8-12 hours under anhydrous and oxygen-free condition to obtain (III).
   Preferred Definitions:
   rare earth metal = yttrium, ytterbium or samarium.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The alkaline                  industrial solid waste is carbide slag, steel                  slag, desulfurization slag, and blast furnace                  slag. The cement is ordinary Portland cement, and                  the grade of the cement is not lower than                  P.O.42.5. The supplementary cementitious material                  is fly ash, carbide slag and desulfurized gypsum.                  The admixture is acrylic compounds, preferably                  acrylic acid and/or methacrylic acid. Preferred                  Conditions: The particle size of the alkaline                  industrial solid waste is 100-500 &#956;m,                  the mass ratio of the alkaline industrial solid                  waste and water is 1: 5-15, preferably, the pH of                  the slurry is not lower than 12. The time of                  primary carbonation reaction is 20-30 minutes,                  the mass ratio of CO2 in the alkaline industrial                  solid waste and the first CO2 gas is 1:0 .5-2,                  the volume concentration of CO2 is 10-30 wt.%,                  preferably, the volume flow of the first CO2 gas                  is 100-500 mL/min, and the particle size of the                  gelling dispersant is less than or equal to 500                  &#956;m. The coal gangue has a particle size                  of 4.75-20 mm. The mass ratio of coal gangue,                  cement, auxiliary cementitious material, gelling                  dispersant and water is 1400-1700: 50-100:                  250-350: 250-300: 100-200, also includes adding                  no more than 0.5 wt.% admixture of the coal                  gangue-based paste. The time of the secondary                  carbonation reaction is 5-10 minutes. The volume                  concentration of CO2 in the second CO2 gas is                  60-99 wt.%, preferably, according to the                  production of the carbonated coal gangue-based                  paste 180 m3 /h, the mass flow rate of the second                  CO2 gas in terms of CO2 is 60-120 kg /h. The                  first CO2 gas and the second CO2 gas are                  respectively from thermal power generation flue                  gas CO2, petrochemical relaxation gas CO2, coal                  chemical exhaust gas CO2 and carbon-containing                  exhaust gas in industrial processes of steel and                  cement industries.

AU MOU L
   LIU S
