   WANG L
AE CATARC CHANGZHOU AUTOMOTIVE ENG RES INST (CATA-Non-standard)
   CATARC TIANJIN AUTOMOTIVE ENG RES INST (CAUM-C)
GA 2021C70776
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining temperature and humidity data in a passenger compartment of an automobile. Judgment is made to check whether heating, humidification or cooling operation is required. Carbon dioxide refrigerant is entered into a heating and humidifying device of an air-conditioner when temperature is lower than a preset temperature range or humidity is lower than a preset value. Air sent by an air-conditioning blower is heated or humidified. The air is entered into the compartment. The refrigerant is circulated. The refrigerant is passed through an outdoor heat exchanger. The air is cooled with carbon dioxide refrigerant when the temperature is higher than the range. The cooled air is entered into the compartment. The refrigerant is passed through a gas-liquid separator to pass through an intermediate heat exchanger to heat and enter a compressor. The refrigerant is entered into an outdoor heat exchanger to release heat and enter into the exchanger to cool.
   USE - Humidified automobile air-conditioner controlling method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving heating efficiency of a heat pump air-conditioning system and ability of humidify the air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a humidified automobile air-conditioner controlling system.

TI Preparing metal-organic framework-based hydrogel            gas separation membrane useful for gases in different            water environment involves adding polymerization            monomer and cross-linking agent into water, adding            metal-organic framework and photoinitiator and coating            precursor solution on quartz glass.
AU GUO X
   HUANG H
   QIAO Z
   SUN Y
   TIAN L
   ZHONG C
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 2021C2836J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal-organic framework-based                hydrogel gas separation membrane comprises (i)                adding the polymerization monomer, and                cross-linking agent into water, adjusting the pH                value of the solution to neutral, adding                metal-organic framework and photoinitiator into the                mixed solution, mixing uniformly; and (ii) using                ultraviolet light to initiate free radical                polymerization method, uniformly coating the                prepared polymerization precursor solution on the                quartz glass to obtain metal-organic framework                based hydrogel gas separation film under the                ultraviolet light illumination condition, where the                film can be directly used for gas separation. The                different gas components comprises                carbon-dioxide/methanediol/hydrogen,                carbon-dioxide/methane and carbon-dioxide/nitrogen                separation.
   USE - The method for preparing metal-organic                framework-based hydrogel gas separation membrane is                useful for gases in different water environment                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The film is environmentally-friendly. The                preparation method is simple, the condition is                mild, suitable for water stability metal-organic                framework and different water environment gas                system separation and industrial production,                provides a reference for expanding the application                of metal-organic framework-based mixed matrix                hydrogel membranes in gas separation. The                metal-organic framework has good water stability                and hydrophilicity, and the average contact angle                is 10-60degrees.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The metal-organic                  framework can be a multi-dimensional structure.                  The two-dimensional metal-organic framework                  thickness is 2-10 nm, the three-dimensional                  metal-organic framework particle size is 100-500                  nm. The material ratio in the mixed homogeneous                  solution of metal-organic framework/polymer                  monomer/cross-linking/initiator/water is                  0.03-1.30:3:0.01-0.03:0.01-0.03:4-25                  (g/g/g/g/ml). The polymer monomer used in the                  polymerization precursor is acrylic acid,                  acrylamide, methacrylic acid and N-isopropyl                  acrylamide. The crosslinking agent is N, N                  '-methylene-bisacrylamide. The photoinitiator is                  Irgacure 2959. The wavelength of the ultraviolet                  light is 312 nm.Preferred Method: In the step (i), adjusting                  the pH of the polymerization environment by                  sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. In the                  step (ii), polymerizing for 10-60 minutes,                  preparation process of the film material is                  carried out at room temperature without adding                  organic solvent.

PN CN113149003-A
TI Synthesizing nitrogen-doped porous carbon by            in-situ ultra-small zinc nanocrystalline template used            as an adsorbent for acetylene, ethane, propane and            carbon dioxide adsorption, comprises e.g. performing            carbonization and acid etching.
AU YANG Z
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 2021911009
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing nitrogen-doped porous carbon by                in-situ ultra-small zinc nanocrystalline template                comprises using poly(4-vinylpyridine) (P4VP) and                zinc chloride (ZnCl2) as raw material, and by                carbonization and acid etching, controlling the                thermal stability of zinc(II) (Zn2+) to P4VP based                on interaction of P4VP and Zn2+ in the                carbonization process, converting ZnCl2 into zinc                oxide (ZnO) nano-cluster in the carbonization                process, reacting ZnO nano-cluster with surrounding                C atom to consume a part of carbon, changing into                gaseous elementary substance Zn leaving producing                hole structure, and further removing the residual                ZnO or simple substance Zn in the carbonization                process to generate hole structure in the acid                etching process.
   USE - The synthesized nitrogen-doped porous carbon                by in-situ ultra-small zinc nanocrystalline                template is used as an adsorbent for acetylene is                used as an adsorbent for acetylene, ethane, propane                and carbon dioxide adsorption with x/methane and                carbon dioxide/nitrogen-IAST selectivity (x =                acetylene, ethane, propane and or dioxide)                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen-doped porous carbon: has large                specific surface area, high nitrogen doping amount                and concentrated pore size distribution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                synthesized nitrogen-doped porous carbon by in-situ                ultra-small zinc nanocrystalline template, is                prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the                  mass ratio of P4VP to ZnCl2 is 0.1-1.0:0.5-5, the                  mol ratio of Zn2+/N in coordination compound                  Zn-P4VP is 2 and the solvent is absolute ethyl                  alcohol. The specific surface area of the porous                  carbon is 600-1500 m2/g, the volume of the                  micropore is 0.35-0.85 cm3, the nitrogen doping                  amount is 6-11 at.% and the aperture is 0.3-2 nm.                  Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) mixing                  P4VP andZnCl2 in a solvent, stirring, then                  obtaining coordination compound Zn-P4VP by vacuum                  extracting and distilling; (b) under the N2                  atmosphere, the Zn-P4VP carbonized to obtain                  Zn/nitrogen-doped carbon composite material                  Zn-NDC; (c) washing the Zn-NDC with an acid to                  remove the remaining Zn template; and (d) washing                  the acid after washing the Zn-NDC with de-ionized                  water to neutral, filtering to obtain filter                  cake, drying filter cake to obtain the Zn-NDPC,                  namely the nitrogen-doped porous carbon. In the                  step (b), the carbonizing temperature is 500-800                  degrees C and the time is 1-4 hours, the heating                  speed is 2.5-7.5 degrees C/minute. In the step                  (c), the acid is hydrochloric acid, the                  concentration of the hydrochloric acid is 0.5-2.5                  M, and the washing time is 5-24 hours. In the                  step (d), the drying temperature is 40-80 degrees                  C and the time is 12-36 hours.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC J01-E02B
IP B01D-053/02; C01B-032/318; C01B-032/348

PN DE102019125083-A1; EP3795814-A1; US2021079863-A1; CN112523884-A; KR2021033432-A; KR2022043091-A; US11441508-B2; KR2450979-B1
TI Procedure for Carbon Dioxide-Certification or            carbon dioxide-dependent homologation of vehicles, such            as car, involves identifying fuel that is taken into            account in which aerodynamics or lightweight            construction has efficiency of engine.
AU GOBRECHT S
AE VOLKSWAGEN AG (VOLS-C)
   VOLKSWAGEN AG (VOLS-C)
   VOLKSWAGEN AG (VOLS-C)
GA 2021263371
AB    NOVELTY - The procedure involves identifying a                CO2-reduced fuel that is taken into an account. The                CO2-reducing design features of an aerodynamics or                the lightweight construction has an efficiency of                an engine. An ecologically is generated proportion                of green electricity in a plug-in hybrid vehicle to                be certified. The homologated is determined by the                CO2-Certification or CO2-dependent homologation                taken into account. The fuel is determined                objectively by unit of a fuel sensor (300). A                specific chemical composition pattern is created                from the specific chemical composition of the fuel.                Multiple standardized comparison composition                patterns CO2-reduced fuels is compared as                CO2-reduced valid fuel.
   USE - Procedure for CO2-Certification or                CO2-dependent homologation of vehicles, such as a                car, truck, motor vehicle, a rail vehicles, an                aircraft or watercraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the positive effect of                the Carbon Dioxide (CO2) in the overall balance of                fuels more effectively in the application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                arrangement of a fuel sensor in a fuel supply                system of an internal combustion engine for                carrying out the method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of an arrangement.
   Fuel sensor (10)
   Display element (20)
   Internal combustion engine (100)
   Fuel tank (200)
   Fuel sensor (300)

PT P
PN WO2021045101-A1; KR2022053579-A; CN114341083-A; JP2021543805-X; EP4026822-A1; US2022298086-A1
TI Production of methane from industrial waste gas            such as iron mill, involves supplying raw material gas            comprising hydrogen gas, oxygen gas, and carbon dioxide            gas to vessel containing catalyst, methanating, and            continuing methanation.
AU CHOJI F
   MASAO S
   HIROSHI A
   TOWN F
   SUDOH M
   AKAMA H
   CHI J
   FUKUHARA C
AE UNIV SHIZUOKA NAT CORP (USHK-C)
   AMANO TECHNOLOGY INST (AMAN-Non-standard)
   AMANO TECHNOLOGY INST (AMAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV SHIZUOKA (USHK-C)
   UNIV SHIZUOKA NAT CORP (USHK-C)
   AMANO TECHNOLOGY INST (AMAN-Non-standard)
   AMANO TECHNOLOGY INST (AMAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV SHIZUOKA NAT CORP (USHK-C)
GA 202124868C
AB    NOVELTY - Production of methane involves supplying a raw                material gas comprising hydrogen gas, oxygen gas,                and carbon dioxide gas to a reaction vessel                containing a catalyst, starting a methanation                reaction by using heat comprising reaction heat                from catalytic combustion of the hydrogen gas, and                continuing the methanation reaction.
   USE - Production of methane from industrial waste                gas, such as thermal power station and iron                mill.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is efficient, and enables                production of methane with reduced energy cost for                heating, and high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                manufacturing system, which consists of a                production system for producing methane by the                methanation reaction from raw material gas                containing carbon oxide, hydrogen and oxygen, the                reaction vessel containing the catalyst, a hydrogen                supply line that supplies hydrogen gas to the                reactor, an air supply line that supplies air                containing oxygen gas to the reactor, an exhaust                gas supply line that supplies exhaust gas                containing carbon dioxide gas to the reactor, in                which the catalyst comprising a catalyst that                functions as a catalyst for both hydrogen gas                combustion and a methanation reaction, or                comprising a catalyst (c1) for hydrogen gas                combustion and a catalyst (c2) for a methanation                reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The reactor comprises a                  reaction chamber (g1) and a reaction chamber (g2)                  capable of exchanging heat with each other, the                  catalyst (c1) is provided in the reaction chamber                  (g1), and the catalyst (c2) is provided in the                  reaction chamber (g2), the reactor forms a gas                  flow path in which the gas introduced into the                  reaction chamber (g1) passes through the reaction                  chamber (g2) and then discharged to the outside                  of the reactor, the hydrogen supply line, the air                  supply line, and the exhaust gas supply line are                  connected to the flow path. The reactor further                  comprises a gas flow path (h1) in which the gas                  introduced into the reaction chamber (g1) passes                  through the reaction chamber (g2) and then                  discharged to the outside of the reactor, a gas                  flow path (h2) in which the gas introduced into                  the reaction chamber (g2) which is discharged to                  the outside of the reactor without passing                  through the reaction chamber (g2), and the                  reaction chamber (g1), the hydrogen supply line                  and the exhaust gas supply line are connected to                  gas flow path (h1), and the hydrogen supply line                  and the air supply line are connected to the gas                  flow path (h2).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves supplying the raw material gas                  containing the hydrogen gas and the oxygen gas to                  the reaction vessel, after starting the catalyst                  combustion of the hydrogen gas, supplying the raw                  material gas further comprising the carbon                  dioxide gas to the reactor, controlling the                  concentration of the hydrogen gas and the oxygen                  gas in the raw material gas so that the                  methanation reaction starts and continues,                  controlling the concentration of the hydrogen gas                  and the oxygen gas in the raw material gas based                  on the temperature of the catalyst, after                  starting the methanation reaction, lowering the                  concentration of the oxygen gas in the raw                  material gas until the temperature of the                  catalyst reaches a predetermined temperature,                  after that, controlling the temperature of the                  catalyst to be maintained above the predetermined                  temperature, and placing a catalyst structure                  containing a metal molded component and a                  catalyst layer containing the catalyst formed on                  the metal molded component in the reactor.                  Preferred Components: The portion or all of the                  carbon dioxide gas is a gas derived from exhaust                  gas. The portion or all of the oxygen gas is a                  gas supplied by introducing air containing oxygen                  gas. The catalyst (c1) and the catalyst (c2) are                  arranged in series along a gas flow path in the                  reactor in this order.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN212504853-U
TI Carbon dioxide fermentation tank useful for improving photosynthetic function of crops, comprises a fermentation tank body provided with a filler chamber, where one side of bottom of the fermenting tank body is connected with a connecting pipe.
AU GAO W
AE YONGCHUN KANGLVLONG FRUIT & VEGETABLE CO (YONG-Non-standard)
GA 202122662N
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide fermenting tank for improving photosynthetic function of crops, one side of the bottom of the fermenting tank body is connected with a connecting pipe; the top of the connecting seat is fixedly connected with a gas pipe; the outer side end of the top of the gas pipe is provided with a limiting groove; the inner side of the limiting groove is embedded with a fastening ring; the inner side end of the fastening ring is fixedly adhered with a flexible rubber sheet; the utility model through the limiting seat, a limiting groove, a skid-proof rubber pad, fastening ring, flexible rubber sheet, connected with the rotating seat; the fixing plate and the fixing bolt can be connected between the film channel and the gas conveying pipe through fastening ring to quickly clamp and fix the film channel and the gas conveying pipe, ensuring the stability of the film channel after the connection; at the same time, the film channel is provided with higher tightness by the skid-proof rubber pad and the flexible rubber sheet; so as to prevent the leakage of carbon dioxide caused by the gap generated at the joint between them.

GA 2021189204
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide protection welding wire feeding device, belonging to the technical field of welding machine. The carbon dioxide protection welding wire feeding device comprises a shell assembly, a winding assembly, a wire pressing assembly, a first straightening assembly and a second straightening assembly. the fixing rod is set on one side of the first locating rod body and the second locating rod body; the pressing plate is fixedly connected on one end of the fixing rod; the third roller is connected below the pressing plate. the screw rod is rotatably connected with the output end of the second motor; the screw thread sleeve is connected on the outer surface of the screw rod; one end of the moving rod is hinged with the screw thread sleeve; the supporting block is hinged with the other end of the moving rod; the bottom plate is connected on the upper surface of the supporting block; the fourth roller is rotatably connected above the bottom plate. so that the welding wire out of the wire outlet pipe can be stably transmitted at uniform speed, which is good for improving the welding quality.

The flame engine main body is fixed with a shell (19) and a water tank. The shell is provided with a first liquid storage chamber (21) and a second liquid storage chamber (20). The shell is formed with a foam cavity that is filled with foaming agent. A sliding rod is sleeved with a spring. The inner wall of mounting cavity fixed with sliding rod. The inner top part of the mounting cavity is provided with a strip-shaped opening. An upper end of a moving block passes through a strip-shaped opening.
   USE - Carbon dioxide foam flame engine for removing snow on a road.

PT P
PN CN110746954-A
TI Preparing modified nano silicon dioxide particles for improving crude oil recovery rate of oil reservoirs, involves dissolving nano silicon dioxide powder, adding silane coupling agent, circulating, adding formaldehyde and storing product.
AU LAI N
   ZHU Q
   QIN J
   WANG D
   TANG L
   LI J
   DENG J
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202011686K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing modified nano silicon dioxide particles, involves (i) stirring and dissolving nano silicon dioxide powder in a solvent under a nitrogen atmosphere, adding a silane coupling agent, stirring and reacting for 8-14 hours, carrying out suction filtration, washing by ethanol, circulating 3 times and drying the silane coupling agent in a constant-temperature oven to obtain silane coupling agent-modified nano silicon dioxide, and (ii) adding the silane coupling agent-modified nano silicon dioxide in a solvent under a nitrogen atmosphere, stirring for 10-15 minutes, slowly adding formic acid, adding formaldehyde while stirring, raising the temperature to 85-95 degrees C, stopping heating, reacting for 6-18 hours, carrying out suction filtration, washing by ethanol, circulating 3 times, drying in a constant-temperature oven and storing to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified nano silicon dioxide particles for improving the crude oil recovery rate of unconventional oil reservoirs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides modified nano silicon dioxide particles having excellent response to carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas, which is recycled to absorb carbon dioxide while satisfying the injection property, analyzes carbon dioxide by introducing nitrogen into the system, and expands the spreading efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent in step (i) is toluene. The silane coupling agent comprises 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane or 3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane. The solvent in step (ii) is N,N-dimethylformamide. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio (g:ml) of the nano silicon dioxide powder to the volume ratio of the silane coupling agent in step (i) is 5:2.5. The reaction step (i) is carried out at 75-85 degrees C. In step (ii), the volume ratio (g:ml) of the mass of the silane coupling agent-modified nano silicon dioxide to the volume of N,N-dimethylformamide is 1:(20-60). In step (ii), the weight ratio of the silane coupling agent-modified nano silicon dioxide, formic acid, and formaldehyde is 1:(5-7):6. Preferred Property: The particle diameter of the nano silicon dioxide powder in step (i) is 20-40 nm.

PT P
PN CN110735056-A; CN110735056-B
TI Pre-separating cerium, samarium, europium, gadolinium and dysprosium involves slowly adding high lanthanum rare earth oxide to sodium carbonate solution, heat-preserving, introducing carbon dioxide gas, filtering and baking precipitate.
AU YANG Y
   ZHANG X
   NIU F
   LIU D
   YANG S
   MENG Y
   LAN Q
AE UNIV JIANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJL-C)
GA 202011278Y
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-separating cerium, samarium, europium, gadolinium and dysprosium involves preparing sodium carbonate solution, slowly adding high lanthanum rare earth oxide to sodium carbonate solution, heat-preserving at 75-85 degrees C, slowly introducing carbon dioxide gas to maintain pH of 10.5-11.5 and stirring, filtering while hot to obtain filter residue containing lanthanum (III) oxide and leaching solution, slowly passing carbon dioxide gas into leaching solution at room temperature, reducing pH to 8.0, stop aerating, stirring, quickly filtering to obtain precipitate, baking precipitate in an oven at 65-75 degrees C for 24 hours, cooling to room temperature, dissolving using water and filtering to obtain filtrate containing sodium carbonate solution and obtaining insoluble solid phase rare earth carbonate double salt containing cerium, samarium, europium, gadolinium and dysprosium.
   USE - Pre-separating cerium, samarium, europium, gadolinium and dysprosium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method dissolves the medium rare earth oxide in the sodium carbonate solution according to the difference of complexing properties between medium rare earth oxide and heavy rare earth oxide in sodium carbonate aqueous solution to achieve the purpose of pre-separation, which reduces the number of medium and heavy rare earth element extraction grouping and the number of stages of extraction and separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The high lanthanum rare earth oxides are ball milled to average particle size of 150-200 mesh before adding to sodium bicarbonate solution. Preferred Composition: The total amount of heavy rare earth added to 1.7-2.0 mol/L sodium bicarbonate solution does not exceed 100 g/L.

PN CN110194491-A
TI Preparation of basic nickel carbonate involves adding carbonate solution and nickel salt solution to reactor, adjusting pH of carbonate solution, releasing carbon dioxide, reacting, centrifuging, and washing with pure water and drying.
AU XU K
   SU T
   CHEN L
   WANG C
   WU W
   CHEN T
AE JINGMEN GEM NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JMGL-C)
GA 2019778247
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of basic nickel carbonate involves adding carbonate solution having ion concentration 100-160 g/L and nickel salt solution having ion concentration of 0.5-2 mol/L to the reactor by keeping flow rate of nickel salt constant, adjusting the pH of the carbonate solution to 8.0-8.3 to obtain nickel carbonate mixture, releasing carbon dioxide having concentration 8000-8100 ppm, reacting at 50-90 degrees C for 5-10 hours, centrifuging the mixture to obtain crude basic nickel carbonate, washing the obtained crude basic nickel carbonate with pure water having conductivity of 100 mu s/m, and drying at 65-95 degrees C for 2-3 hours.
   USE - Preparation of basic nickel carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process of makes the decomposition rate of carbonate ions in the reaction process low, thereby obtained nickel carbonate content in the basic nickel carbonate is high with excellent performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation device for basic nickel carbonate comprises reaction component, carbonate feeding tube is arranged in reaction component, nickel salt material tube, stirring component placed in the center of the reaction component, carbon dioxide detecting component is arranged at the top and bottom of the reaction component and a pH measuring component arranged at the lower end of the reaction component.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The carbonate is at least one of sodium carbonate, ammonium carbonate and ammonium hydrogen carbonate. The nickel salt is at least one of nickel sulfate and nickel nitrate. Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of the sodium carbonate solution is 5-500 L/hour and the nickel salt solution is 50-500 L/hour.

TI Manufacturing method for ceramic substrate involves baking ceramic green sheet in which interior of through-hole is filled with electrically conductive paste and repeatedly performing irradiation of carbon dioxide laser.
AU NASU T
AE NGK SPARK PLUG CO LTD (NITS-C)
GA 201828629H
AB    NOVELTY - The manufacturing method involves filling an electrically conductive paste into the interior of a through-hole (S30), forming a wiring pattern on a ceramic green sheet (S40), and baking the ceramic green sheet in which interior of through-hole is filled with the electrically conductive paste (S60). The irradiation of carbon dioxide laser with frequency of 2000 hertz or more and pulse width of 30 microseconds or less is repeatedly performed.
   USE - Manufacturing method for ceramic substrate.
   ADVANTAGE - Suppresses generation of glass ball since cooling-off period is made short if pulse width is made constant and frequency is made to increase. The mechanical strength of ceramic substrate is improved by suppressing generation of void. The generation of open fault in the via conductor is suppressed by suppressing generation of glass ball. The heat damage given to a ceramic green sheet is reduced, and generation of glass ball inside the through-hole and generation of void at the time of the baking in the surroundings of through-hole are suppressed by irradiating carbon dioxide laser to ceramic green sheet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the manufacturing method of a ceramic substrate. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Preparation step (S10)
   Laser irradiation step (S20)
   Filling step (S30)
   Wiring formation step (S40)
   Baking step (S60)

TI Producing electrocatalyst useful to selectively reduce carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. dissolving metal precursor in organic solvent, primarily heat treating to obtain metal precursor solution, and ultrasonically dispersing carbon support.
AU JEONG H Y
   BYOUNG K M
   KIM C
   HWANG Y J
   KOUN M B
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 201816785V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing electrocatalyst, comprises (a) dissolving metal precursor in organic solvent, and primarily heat treating to produce metal precursor solution, (b) ultrasonically dispersing carbon support in mixed solution containing fixing agent and organic solvent to prepare carbon support solution, and (c) adding the carbon support solution into the metal precursor solution, and secondarily heat treatment. The fixing agent is linear or branched alkene (R-CnH2n-R') (I).
   USE - The electrocatalyst is useful for selectively reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrocatalyst improves carbon monoxide Faraday efficiency by greater than or equal to 2 times; suppresses hydrogen generation reaction during selective reduction from carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide; and has low over-potential and high mass activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing electrocatalyst, comprises (a) dissolving metal precursor in organic solvent, and primarily heat treating to produce metal precursor solution, (b) ultrasonically dispersing carbon support in mixed solution containing fixing agent and organic solvent to prepare carbon support solution, and (c) adding the carbon support solution into the metal precursor solution, and secondarily heat treatment. The fixing agent is linear or branched alkene of formula (R-CnH2n-R') (I).
   n = 1-10; and
   R, R' = amine.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for electrocatalyst produced by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal of the metal precursor is silver, gold or zinc, preferably silver. Preferred Method: In the step (a), the primary heat treatment is carried out by raising the temperature from room temperature to 40-60 degrees C. In the step (b), the fixing agent and the carbon support are mixed in weight ratio of 1:10-30. In the step (c), the secondary heat treatment is carried for 1-7 hours after raising temperature from 40-60 degrees C to 150- 250 degrees C. The method further comprises maintaining the temperature at 40-60 degrees C for 5-30 minutes, before the secondary heat treatment. The primary and secondary heat treatments are carried at heating rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fixing agent is ethylenediamine. The carbon support includes carbon black, acetylene black, carbon nanotubes, graphite, graphene, graphite nanofiber or fullerene. The solvent is glycol solvent including ethylene glycol, pentanediol, 1-octadecyne or benzyl ether.

PN KR1816677-B1
TI Mineral carbonating concentrated water in seawater desalination plant, comprises e.g. precipitating magnesium in solid state by increasing pH of concentrated water containing calcium, magnesium and sodium, and separating precipitate.
AU JUN H B
   SEUNGWOO L
   SOO C C
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 201805446Y
AB    NOVELTY - Mineral carbonating concentrated water in seawater desalination plant, comprises e.g. (a) precipitating magnesium in solid state by increasing pH of the seawater desalination plant concentrated water containing calcium, magnesium and sodium into first range, and separating the magnesium precipitate from the concentrated water, and (b) precipitating calcium in a solid state by adjusting the pH of the concentrated water in which magnesium is separated, into a second range higher than the first range, and separating the calcium precipitate from the concentrated water.
   USE - The method is useful for mineral carbonating concentrated water in seawater desalination plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly, reduces generation of carbon dioxide in desalination plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mineral carbonating concentrated water in seawater desalination plant, comprises (a) precipitating magnesium in solid state by increasing pH of the seawater desalination plant concentrated water containing calcium, magnesium and sodium into first range, and separating the magnesium precipitate from the concentrated water, (b) precipitating calcium in a solid state by adjusting the pH of the concentrated water in which magnesium is separated, into a second range higher than the first range, and separating the calcium precipitate from the concentrated water, (c) reacting the magnesium precipitate with carbon dioxide to precipitate magnesium carbonate, and (d) precipitating calcium carbonate by reacting calcium precipitate with carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises either: supplying carbon dioxide into the concentrated water in which calcium and magnesium are separated to precipitate sodium in carbonate form; or separating concentrated water containing monovalent cation including sodium and concentrated water containing divalent cation including calcium and magnesium, and supplying carbon dioxide into the concentrated water containing sodium to precipitate sodium in carbonate form. The method is carried out by supplying concentrated water containing the divalent cation after the electrodialysis into the concentrated water source in the magnesium treatment step. The method comprises performing sedimentation of sodium, separating the concentrated water through solid-liquid separation used as raw water for the desalination process or after obtaining sodium hydroxide through electrolysis, and precipitating calcium and magnesium using sodium hydroxide as pH regulator. The method comprises supplying the precipitate with water to form aqueous solution, supplying carbon dioxide into aqueous solution to form a precipitate, separating precipitate formed by reacting with carbon dioxide, and aqueous solution by solid-liquid separation, recyclying the obtained water as raw water of the desalination process, mixing the concentrated water in which the calcium and magnesium are separated with the magnesium precipitate separated in the magnesium separation step, and precipitating magnesium and sodium in carbonate form, respectively. The method further comprises supplying ammonia and carbon dioxide into the concentrated water in which calcium and magnesium are separated to precipitate sodium with sodium bicarbonate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Producing aqueous electrolyte solution containing            nitrate based compounds, comprises reacting substituted            amine based compound with hydrogen peroxide in carbon            dioxide containing aqueous solution.
AU GAERTNER F
   NISSEN F
AE EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 202160788S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing aqueous electrolyte solution                containing at least one nitrate based compounds (I)                or (II), comprises reacting at least one                substituted amine based compound (III) or (IV) with                hydrogen peroxide in carbon dioxide containing                aqueous solution (L2) to form compounds (I) or                (II), (b) partially removing aqueous solution (L2)                using carbon dioxide, when hydrogen peroxide is                contained in the aqueous solution (L2) to obtain                carbon dioxide containing aqueous solution (L3)                comprising at least one compound (I) or (II), and                (c) adjusting pH, and partially removing carbon                dioxide from aqueous solution (L3).
   USE - The method is useful for producing aqueous                electrolyte solution containing at least one                nitrate based compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method minimizes content of interfering                foreign ions in the solutions, and is                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing aqueous electrolyte solution                containing at least one nitrate based compounds of                formula (I) or (II), comprises reacting at least                one substituted amine based compound of formula                (III) or (IV) with hydrogen peroxide in carbon                dioxide containing aqueous solution (L2) to form                compounds (I) or (II), (b) partially removing                aqueous solution (L2) using carbon dioxide, when                hydrogen peroxide is contained in the aqueous                solution (L2) to obtain carbon dioxide containing                aqueous solution (L3) comprising at least one                compound (I) or (II), and (c) adjusting pH, and                partially removing carbon dioxide from aqueous                solution (L3).
   R1, R2, R3, R4, R1', R2', R3', R4' = 1-6C                alkyl radical;
   R9, R10, R9', R10' = H or 1-6C-alkyl;
   R5, R6, R5', R6' = H, alkyl, cycloalkyl, aryl,                aralkyl, heterocyclyl, radicals ofalkyl,                cycloalkyl, aryl, aralkyl, heterocyclyl with                alkoxy, hydroxy, carboxyl, carboxyl ester,                carboxylamide, sulfonate, sulfonic acid ester,                sulfonamide, halogen substituted alkyl, adjacent                divalent radicals containing oxygen, or                imino;
   R5+R6 = alkylene group which is interrupted by                divalent radicals which are not directly adjacent                to one another of oxygen and imino;
   R5'+R6' = alkylene group which is interrupted                by divalent radicals which are not directly                adjacent to one another of oxygen and imino;
   R7 = alkyl radical which is substituted by                alkoxy, OH, nitro, carboxy, carboxy ester,                carboxylamide, sulfonate, sulfonic acid ester,                sulfonamide, or halo and which are not directly                adjacent to one another of oxygen or imino;
   R8 = p-valent organic bridging group which is                substituted by alkyl, hydroxy, nitro, carboxy,                carboxy ester, carboxylamide, sulfonate, sulfonic                acid ester, sulfonamide, or halo, and not directly                adjacent divalent radicals of oxygen, or                imino;
   Xa1 = organic anion and/or inorganic                anion;
   a = valence of the anion Xa1, preferably                1-10000;
   p = 2-6;
   m = p-1;
   n = 1/a; and
   o = (1+m)/a.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried                  out at 20-100 degrees C and pH of 7.5-10.5 under                  pressure of 0.5-2 bar. The method comprises                  dissolving carbon dioxide in the form of dry ice                  and/or gaseous form in water before step (a) to                  form carbon dioxide containing aqueous solution                  (L2). The step (b) is carried out by removing                  hydrogen peroxide by adjusting pH of the aqueous                  solution (L2) to less than or equal to 10.5 in                  the presence of catalyst. Preferred Components:                  The molar ratio of the amount of substance of the                  carbon dioxide dissolved in the aqueous solution                  (L2) and the total amount of substance of all                  compounds (III) and (IV) used in step (a) is                  10:1-1:1000. The molar ratio of the total weight                  of all compounds (III) and (IV) used in step (a)                  and the total weight of the hydrogen peroxide                  used in step (a) is 9:1-1:9. The molar ratio of                  the total weight of all compounds (III) and (IV)                  used in step (a) and the total weight of the                  hydrogen peroxide used in step (a) is 1:1.05-1:9.                  The proportion of all compounds (I) and (II) in                  the aqueous electrolyte solution (L1) is 10-80                  wt.% based on the total weight of the aqueous                  electrolyte solution.

PN WO2020185107-A1; IN202117046468-A; CN114340789-A
TI Regenerating or reactivating catalyst used in a reforming reaction, comprises providing the catalyst in reactor and providing first oxidizing gas comprising carbon dioxide to reactor for regeneration or reactivation of catalyst.
AU ELBASHIR N O
   CHALLIWALA M S A
   CHOUDHURY H A
AE QATAR FOUND (QATA-Non-standard)
GA 202091164M
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating or reactivating a catalyst used in a reforming reaction, comprises: providing the catalyst in a reactor, where the catalyst was used in a carbon and syngas production reaction; and providing a first oxidizing gas comprising carbon dioxide to the reactor for regeneration or reactivation of the catalyst, where the catalyst is not further reduced for the regeneration or reactivation of the catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating or reactivating catalyst (claimed), which is used in reforming reaction to carbon and syngas production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is single step regeneration and activation; utilizes carbon dioxide is more effective than existing conventional two-step process that includes separate reduction and oxidation steps; and provides pure carbon monoxide as a byproduct.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) regenerating or reactivating (P1) the first catalyst comprising providing a first catalyst in a first reactor for a first carbon and syngas production reaction, providing a first mixed gas comprising CH4 and carbon dioxide in the first reactor for the first carbon and syngas production reaction, allowing a first reacted gas from the first carbon and syngas production reaction to leave the first reactor, and providing a first oxidizing gas comprising carbon dioxide to the first reactor for a first regeneration or reactivation of the first catalyst, where the first catalyst is not further reduced for the first regeneration or reactivation of the first catalyst; and
   (2) regenerating or reactivating (P2) the second catalyst comprising providing a first catalyst in a first reactor for a first dry reforming of methane reaction or a first carbon generator (CARGEN) reaction, providing a first mixed gas comprising methane and carbon dioxide in the first reactor for the first dry reforming of methane reaction or the first CARGEN reaction, allowing a first reacted gas from the first dry reforming of methane reaction or the first CARGEN reaction to leave the first reactor, providing a first oxidizing gas comprising carbon dioxide to the first reactor for a first regeneration or reactivation reaction of the first catalyst, providing a second catalyst in a second reactor for a second dry Reforming of methane reaction or a second CARGEN reaction, providing a second mixed gas comprising methane and carbon dioxide in the second reactor for the second dry reforming of methane reaction or the second CARGEN reaction, allowing a second reacted gas from the second dry reforming of methane reaction or the second CARGEN reaction to leave the second reactor, and providing a second oxidizing gas comprising carbon dioxide to the second reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: providing to the reactor a second oxidizing gas comprising carbon dioxide and oxygen for the regeneration or reactivation of the catalyst; and producing pure carbon monoxide from the regeneration or reactivation of the catalyst. The regeneration or reactivation of the catalyst is conducted at 650-900 degrees C. The regeneration or reactivation of the catalyst has a duration of 10 minutes to about 4 hours or until the carbon monoxide generation diminishes to zero while regeneration or reactivation. Preferred Components: The carbon and syngas production reaction is a dry reforming of methane reaction or a CARGEN reaction, and the catalyst comprises solid carbon produced from the dry reforming of methane reaction or the CARGEN reaction. The catalyst comprises nickel as an active metal. The catalyst is deactivated from the carbon and syngas production reaction or has an activity less than that before being used in the carbon and syngas production. The first carbon and syngas production reaction is a first dry reforming of methane reaction or a first CARGEN reaction. The second carbon and syngas production reaction is a second dry reforming of methane reaction or a second CARGEN reaction. The first catalyst comprises nickel as an active metal. The second catalyst comprises nickel as an active metal. The second oxidizing gas further comprises dioxide. The first regeneration reaction of the first catalyst and the second dry reforming of methane reaction or the second CARGEN reactions are about concurrent. Preferred Process: The process (P1) further comprises: providing a second catalyst in a second reactor for a second carbon and syngas production reaction, providing a second mixed gas in the second reactor for the second carbon and syngas production reaction, allowing a second reacted gas from the second carbon and syngas production reaction to leave the second reactor, and providing a second oxidizing gas comprising carbon dioxide to the second reactor for a second regeneration or reactivation of the first catalyst, where the second catalyst is not further reduced for the second regeneration or reactivation of the second catalyst; providing to the first reactor a first additional oxidizing gas comprising carbon dioxide and oxygen for the first regeneration or reactivation of the first catalyst; and producing pure carbon monoxide from the first regeneration or reactivation of the first catalyst; and producing pure carbon monoxide from the second regeneration or reactivation of the second catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - 260 mg 20% Nickel/ gamma -aluminum oxide catalyst was mixed with 500 mg quartz silica and loaded in the U-tube quartz reactor. In-situ reduction of the catalyst was performed as mentioned in the previous section. After complete reduction of the catalyst, the reactor was purged with helium for 2 hours, and cooled to reaction temperature of 550 degrees C. Feed gas of 15% methane, 10% carbon dioxide, 83% helium, and 2% argon was introduced at a rate of 248 ml/minutes to the quartz tube reactor from the carrier port of the micromeritics system. Dry reforming of methane reaction was conducted for next 10 hours of time on stream and then purged with helium for 30 minutes. The temperature programmed oxidation was performed with pure carbon dioxide gas at 650 degrees C for 1 hour, and the regeneration reaction was confirmed by evolution of carbon monoxide. Immediately after the carbon dioxide-temperature programmed oxidation, the reactor was cooled to 550 degrees C while purged with helium.

PN CN109879510-A; CN109879510-B
TI Recovering ammonia from high ammonia nitrogen wastewater comprises preheating wastewater, introducing carbon-dioxide and ammonia vapor, precipitating, stripping ammonia, gas-liquid separation, biochemical treatment and dehydrating.
AU XIE H
   HUANG J
   LIAO Y
   CHEN S
   YANG D
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 201954957V
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering ammonia from high ammonia nitrogen wastewater comprises (a) pre-heating waste water, pumping to the upper part of decarbonizing tower, introducing carbon dioxide, ammonia-containing steam and ammonia vapor from the lower part of the decarbonizing tower, formation of calcium magnesium precipitate from reaction with ammonia and carbon dioxide, precipitating in storage tank and discharging to the tower, (b) placing the water outlet pump to upper part of tower, introducing carbon dioxide, bringing the tower to a negative pressure state, stripping ammonia, formation and collection of ammonia-containing mixed steam, partially refluxing, condensing remaining part, performing gas-liquid separation, allowing the separated aqueous ammonia to enter into the carbonizing tower, passing the effluent for biochemical treatment and (c) introducing carbon dioxide to react with aqueous ammonia to form ammonium hydrogen carbonate, cooling, crystallizing, centrifugation and dehydrating.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering ammonia from high ammonia nitrogen waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves deamination effect; ammonia recovery efficiency; and prevents processing equipment from scaling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering ammonia from high ammonia nitrogen wastewater (a) pre-heating high ammonia nitrogen waste water using preheater, pumping to an upper part of a decarbonization tower, introducing carbon dioxide vapor, ammonia-containing steam and ammonia vapor from the decarbonization tower, formation of calcium magnesium precipitate from reaction of calcium and magnesium ions in the waste water with ammonia and carbon dioxide, deposition in a storage tank on the bottom of the tower, discharging to the tower replacing storage tank, (b) placing the outlet pump to the upper part of the tower, introducing carbon dioxide containing steam from the bottom of the tower, bringing the tower to a negative pressure state by a negative pressure device, stripping the ammonia at 80-90 degrees C, ammonia nitrogen changing to vapor phase, formation and collection of high-concentration ammonia-containing mixed steam to the upper part of the tower, partially refluxing the mixed steam to the tower, condensing the remaining part using condenser, performing gas-liquid separation, allowing the separated aqueous ammonia to enter into the carbonizing tower, passing the effluent from the tower to subsequent treatment facility for biochemical treatment and (c) introducing carbon dioxide containing steam from the bottom of the carbonization tower to react with aqueous ammonia to form ammonium hydrogen carbonate, cooling in the carbonizing tower using a bypass cooling system, crystallizing gradually to form ammonium hydrogen carbonate crystal in water, performing centrifugation and dehydrating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The ratio of carbon dioxide vapor to the substance containing carbon dioxide and ammonia in the ammonia vapor in step (a) is 1:1. Preferred Method: The ammonia containing vapor in step (a) comprises a portion of the gas refluxed from the carbonizing column. The gas at the top of the decarbonization tower enters the bottom of the carbonization tower. The tower outlet pump in step (b) comprises heating, freezing, cooling, discharging into aqueous ammonia tank and passing to the subsequent treatment facility for biochemical treatment.

TI Neutralizing alkaline waste water, comprises e.g. vaporizing liquefied carbon dioxide, reducing vaporized carbon dioxide gas, and introducing decompressed carbon dioxide gas in carbonic acid gas flow rate control valve.
AU HAN Y S
AE HAN Y (HANY-Individual)
GA 201921369H
AB    NOVELTY - Neutralizing alkaline waste water, comprises (i) vaporizing liquefied carbon dioxide, (ii) reducing the vaporized carbon dioxide gas, (iii) introducing the decompressed carbon dioxide gas into the carbonic acid gas flow rate control valve, (iv) introducing the alkaline wastewater and the carbonic acid gas into the alkaline wastewater neutralization device from the carbonic acid gas flow rate control valve, and (v) neutralizing the alkaline wastewater by stirring the alkaline wastewater and the carbonic acid gas in the alkaline wastewater neutralization apparatus. The neutralization device is provided with a sealed reaction tank. The method further comprises cooling the alkaline wastewater neutralization device, before the step (iv). The flow rate control valve of the alkaline wastewater is fed back controlled in conjunction with pH of the alkaline wastewater in the alkaline wastewater neutralization device. The sealed reaction tank comprises e.g. reactor body.
   USE - The method is useful for neutralizing alkaline waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly. The alkaline wastewater neutralization device need small installation space and has less maintenance cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Neutralizing alkaline waste water, comprises (i) vaporizing liquefied carbon dioxide, (ii) reducing the vaporized carbon dioxide gas, (iii) introducing the decompressed carbon dioxide gas into the carbonic acid gas flow rate control valve, (iv) introducing the alkaline wastewater and the carbonic acid gas into the alkaline wastewater neutralization device from the carbonic acid gas flow rate control valve, and (v) neutralizing the alkaline wastewater by stirring the alkaline wastewater and the carbonic acid gas in the alkaline wastewater neutralization apparatus. The neutralization device is provided with a sealed reaction tank. The method further comprises cooling the alkaline wastewater neutralization device, before the step (iv). The flow rate control valve of the alkaline wastewater is fed back controlled in conjunction with pH of the alkaline wastewater in the alkaline wastewater neutralization device. The sealed reaction tank comprises a reactor body, an alkaline wastewater inflow line provided on one side of the reactor main body, a neutralized water discharge line provided on the other side of the reactor body, a carbonic acid gas injection line connected to the alkaline wastewater inflow line, and an agitator for agitating the alkaline wastewater and the carbonic acid gas. The carbonic acid gas supplied from the carbonic acid gas flow rate control valve further includes a pressure control step before entering the alkaline wastewater neutralizing device. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method for treating alkaline wastewater.

TI Smart proactive thermos for respiratory mask used by ordinary people, in which air is supplied to mask from thermos through inlet hose of mask supported by pressure of air created during prior exhalation during inhalation and suction.
AU PERUNICIC D
AE PERUNICIC D (PERU-Individual)
GA 202268974G
AB    NOVELTY - The thermos has a hot liquid with antiviral effect on air for respiratory mask comprising a thermos (1f) or bottles/jars made of thermo insulated material in a round/flat shape. The motive inlet (5i) or the convergent section (5c) of the venturi tube (5) is coupled to the outlet hose (10b) of the mask (10) while the other end of outlet hose is coupled to the outlet (10a) of the mask. The end of main tube (4m) of the cap (4) is located above the liquid (2) in the thermos. The pressure of exhaust gases in the mask prevents that the air from the thermos enters into the mask through the inlet hose (11b) of the mask during each exhalation. The outlet valve (12) in the outlet hose stops the suction from the thermos through the outlet hose of the mask during each inhalation. The air is supplied to the mask from the thermos through the inlet hose of the mask supported by the pressure of air created during prior exhalation during each inhalation and suction.
   USE - Smart proactive thermos having hot liquid with antiviral effect on air for respiratory mask used by ordinary people, workers, medical staff in hospitals and in restaurants. Can also be used as air filter for any industry in which workers need protection from harmful gases and dust and used as inhalator or micro sauna for lungs and used as an alternative solution to vacuum cleaners, air-conditioners or water pipes for cigarettes.
   ADVANTAGE - The new reusable product is used restaurants due to easy maintenance since thermos is available at any time with a cooling effect on the air for inhalation. The new product is used as an alternative for water pipes such as hookah and nargile for cigarettes to be inserted in the air inlet of the venturi tube. The thermos is used as an alternative for air-conditionings and vacuum cleaners when the product is driven by electric motors. Suction is performed through the air inlet of the venture tube. The thermos is used as an air filter for any industry in which workers need protection from harmful gases and dust beside the antiviral application in the health and medical sector. Producers of thermos and toasters increase sales of their products when they offer supplementary products which contributes to antiviral protection. About 30% of the population avoid using textile conventional masks because they do not believe that masks provide protection from viruses. The groups of people prolongs spreading of virus infection. The tea or cigarettes are acceptable protection since the tea or cigarettes can enjoy the aromatic smell of coffee, tea or tobacco for such groups of people masks combined with thermoses having coffee. The thermos prevents spreading of viruses until a vaccine is developed. The thermos inactivates viruses before inhalation of the air through a respiratory mask. The thermos uses exhaling carbon dioxide from the nose/mouth as a motive fluid. The thermos provides additional heat to exhaling carbon dioxide (3rC) from the nose/mouth and incoming air to reach the common temperature of about 70 degrees C which inactivates viruses. The thermos reaches a high temperature and keeps long. The thermos slowly prolongs the time of antiviral effect of heat for inactivation of viruses. The thermos traps and deposits carbon dioxide from the nose/mouth. The thermos reduces emission of carbon dioxide from the nose/mouth as a greenhouse gas. The thermos motivates people to carry respiratory masks. The thermos provides respiratory masks for multipurpose.

PN WO2021244980-A1
TI Converting feed streams comprising gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen rich gas stream and/or methane rich gas stream involves introducing feed streams and oxygen into reaction vessel and performing partial oxidation reaction.
AU BALAJI S P
   KLOKKENBURG M
   SCHOUWENAAR R
   UNRUH D J M
   QUEVEDO ENRIQUEZ J A
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 2021E0290V
AB    NOVELTY - Converting feed streams comprising (a) a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide (2) and a hydrogen rich gas stream (3) and/or (b) a methane rich gas stream (1) and (c) a combination of feed streams (a) and (b) into a product stream comprising carbon monoxide, water and hydrogen comprises introducing feed streams comprising (a), (b) or (c) and oxygen into a reaction vessel, where the process comprises in switching mode performing method I or method II in the reaction vessel, no catalyst is present, the reaction vessel is provided with a burner located at the top of the reaction vessel, the burner comprises coaxial channels for the separate introduction of the different gas streams (method I) performing a reverse water gas shift reaction at elevated temperature; and (method II ) performing a partial oxidation reaction at elevated temperature, where at least feed stream and an oxygen rich gas stream are introduced into the reaction vessel via the burner in separate feed streams.
   USE - The process is useful for: converting feed streams; and reducing the carbon footprint of certain industrial technologies.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: requires synthesis gas as a feedstock; and is inexpensive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting feed streams comprising (a) a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide (2) and a hydrogen rich gas stream (3) and/or (b) a methane rich gas stream (1) and (c) a combination of feed streams (a) and (b) into a product stream comprising carbon monoxide, water and hydrogen comprises introducing feed streams comprising (a), (b) or (c) and oxygen into a reaction vessel, where the process comprises in switching mode performing method I or method II in the reaction vessel, no catalyst is present, the reaction vessel is provided with a burner located at the top of the reaction vessel, the burner comprises coaxial channels for the separate introduction of the different gas streams (method I) performing a reverse water gas shift reaction at elevated temperature, (a1) at least feed stream (a) and an oxygen rich gas stream (4) are introduced into the reaction vessel via the burner in separate feed streams, the hydrogen rich gas stream is introduced into the reaction vessel at a temperature of 15-500 degrees C, (b1) the hydrogen rich gas stream and oxygen rich gas stream is introduced in close vicinity of each other, the hydrogen and oxygen in the hydrogen rich gas stream and oxygen rich gas stream undergo a combustion reaction upon entering the reaction vessel, thus providing the heating energy required for the reverse water-gas shift reaction and (c1) the temperature in the reaction vessel is maintained in 1000-1500 degrees C by varying the molar ratio of hydrogen to oxygen, which are introduced into the reaction vessel in the hydrogen rich gas stream and oxygen rich gas stream, respectively; and (method II ) performing a partial oxidation reaction at elevated temperature, where (d1) at least feed stream and an oxygen rich gas stream are introduced into the reaction vessel via the burner in separate feed streams, (e1) feed stream and the oxygen rich gas stream being introduced in close vicinity of each other, feed stream and the oxygen rich gas stream undergo a partial oxidation reaction upon entering the reaction vessel, and (f1) the temperature in the reaction vessel is maintained in 1100-1500 degrees C by varying the flow rate of oxygen and/or feed stream, during the switching mode from method I to method II or vice versa, the feed streams are gradually changed to the relevant feed streams, so that feed stream is present in an intermediate phase, the feed streams are introduced via the appropriate channels, while also changing the temperature of the reactor to the desired temperature for the relevant method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows the flexible partial oxidation and reverse water gas shift process.
   Methane rich gas stream (1)
   Carbon dioxide (2)
   Hydrogen rich gas stream (3)
   Oxygen rich gas stream (4)
   Product gas stream (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The feed streams can be selected without change in equipment or stopping the process by either gradually increasing the flow of feed stream (a) while gradually decreasing the flow of feed stream (b) and vice versa, by opening and closing valves of the respective feed streams feeding to the burner, while continuing the flow of oxygen and adapting that as required. In the process the percentage of the feed streams (a) and (b) in feed stream (c) may be varied over time as required along with the variation of the oxygen rich gas stream. The reaction vessel is preheated to a temperature of up to 1100 degrees C. At least one feed streams comprising feed streams (a) and (b) along with the oxygen rich gas stream are preheated before being introduced into the reaction vessel. At least part of the hydrogen rich gas stream and/or at least part of the oxygen rich gas stream in the feed is obtained from a water splitter. In method I part of the gas stream containing carbon dioxide is introduced via a channel in between the hydrogen rich gas stream and the oxygen rich gas stream and the remaining part of the gas stream containing carbon dioxide is introduced in an outer channel of the burner, being outside of the channels for the hydrogen rich gas stream and oxygen rich gas stream. In method I the oxygen rich gas stream is introduced via the inner channel(s) of the burner. In method II the oxygen rich gas stream is introduced via the inner channel(s) of the burner. In both methods I and II the oxygen rich gas stream is introduced via the inner channel(s) of the burner.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of the product stream (main product is synthesis gas) compositions that do not support the claims.

PN CN113563151-A
TI Device used for purifying electronic grade acetylene, has adsorption tower with input end on lower side and output end on upper side for removing carbon dioxide from acetylene raw materials, low-pressure compressor, and absorption tower.
AU XU C
   WANG X
   CHEN J
   WANG M
   WEN H
AE SUZHOU JINHONG GAS CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021C5482W
AB    NOVELTY - A 4N electronic grade acetylene purification device uses natural gas cracking acetylene as acetylene raw material, and has an adsorption tower with an input end on the lower side and an output end on the upper side for removing carbon dioxide from acetylene raw materials, a low-pressure compressor, an absorption tower provided with an inlet through which the absorption solvent can pass, where the top of the absorption tower is used to discharge less soluble light component impurities and discharge to exhaust gas treatment unit, and the purity of the absorption solvent is 4N and the moisture content is less than 10 ppm, and desorption tower to desorb acetylene from the absorption solvent, so as to obtain 4N electronic grade acetylene and discharge from the top of the desorption tower, and a gas stripping tower, where the bottom of desorption tower is connected to top of the gas stripping tower through desorption tower kettle pump.
   USE - Device used for purifying 4N electronic grade acetylene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses adsorption and separation method to separate and purify acetylene according to absorption characteristic of acetylene and portion of the impurity. The different solubility of light component and heavy component in the solvent by absorption analysis method for separating and purifying acetylene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A 4N electronic grade acetylene purification device uses natural gas cracking acetylene as acetylene raw material, and has an adsorption tower with an input end on the lower side and an output end on the upper side for removing carbon dioxide from acetylene raw materials, a low-pressure compressor, which connects the adsorption tower and absorption tower, to compress acetylene raw material in adsorption tower and send to the absorption tower, an absorption tower provided with an inlet through which the absorption solvent can pass, where the top of the absorption tower is used to discharge less soluble light component impurities and discharge to exhaust gas treatment unit, and the bottom of absorption tower is an absorption solvent which absorbs acetylene and heavy component impurities and enters analysis tower through absorption tower kettle pump, and the purity of the absorption solvent is 4N and the moisture content is less than 10 ppm, and desorption tower to desorb acetylene from the absorption solvent, so as to obtain 4N electronic grade acetylene and discharge from the top of the desorption tower, and a gas stripping tower, where the bottom of desorption tower is connected to top of the gas stripping tower through desorption tower kettle pump, and is used to send the absorption solvent with heavy component impurities dissolved at the bottom of the desorption tower to the gas stripping tower. The absorption solvent is a combination of one or more of methanol, ethanol, ethylene glycol and tetrahydrofuran, and the absorption solvent is continuously passed into absorption tower from inlet with inlet flow rate of 1-3 kg/hour. The temperature of the absorption solvent is -60-50degrees Celsius, the pressure is 6-8 bar, the absorption temperature of the absorption tower is -50-0degrees Celsius, and the absorption pressure is 5-10 bar. The absorption solvent with heavy component impurities dissolved in the bottom of the analysis tower is discharged. The bottom of the gas stripping tower is connected with hot nitrogen. The heavy component impurity is separated from the absorbing solvent in tower gas. The separated absorbing solvent is discharged from the bottom of the gas stripping tower through the gas stripping tower kettle pump and is recycled. The heavy component impurity is discharged to the exhaust gas processing unit.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for purifying process of 4N electronic grade acetylene, which involves:
   a. using natural gas cracking acetylene as acetylene raw material;
   b. removing carbon dioxide in acetylene raw material and using absorption solvent to dissolve acetylene and heavy component impurities in the acetylene raw material;
   c. removing less soluble light component impurities in the acetylene raw material and analyzing and separating acetylene from absorption solvent;
   d. dissolving heavy component impurities in the absorption solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent used for adsorbing carbon dioxide in the adsorption tower is one or more of sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, lithium hydroxide and calcium hydroxide.

   USE - The method is useful for producing natural gas and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces natural gas rich raw material, is economical and meets the national coal comprehensive utilization direction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing natural gas and hydrogen comprises (1) taking low rank coal by gasification and reduction process to prepare upgrading coal, by using upgrading coal and low-rank coal to obtain water-coal-slurry, preparing the first synthesis gas by the upgrading coal by the coal water slurry preparation to obtain a second synthesis gas prepared by the rich third synthesis gas, (2) adding first syngas, second syngas, and third synthesis gas, carrying out pre-desulfurization technique to remove sulfur in the synthesis gas to obtain desulfurized synthesis gas, adding predesulfurated composite in all unsaturated hydrocarbon gas and converting into the corresponding saturated hydrocarbon via hydrogenation technology, where the organic sulfur is converted to hydrogen sulfide to obtain the synthesis gas through the desulfurizing process, removing sulfur in the hydrogenation synthesis gas to obtain the desulfurized synthesis gas, (3) carrying out desulfurized synthesis gas conversion technology to obtain conditions such that the molar ratio of hydrogen and cobalt in converting is (3-10): 1, (4) carrying out the decarburization process, removing the carbon dioxide in the gas, purified gas, such that the purified gas, hydrogen-carbon ratio is (2.95-3.05): 1, (5) adding the purified gas into the first reactor, pre-heating the purified gas such that the temperature of the purge gas is 150-500 degrees C, (6) using environment purifying gas obtained in the step (5) into the first methane synthesis material into the second reactor in the presence of a first temperature methane catalyst, carbon monoxide of the first methane synthesis material, carbon dioxide and hydrogen reaction, synthesizing methane so as to obtain first methane logistics, when the first methane logistics enter into second methane synthesis material into the third reactor and carbon monoxide environment at the second temperature methane in the presence of a catalyst, carrying out second methane synthesis using carbon dioxide and hydrogen react, synthesizing methane, so as to obtain the second methane logistics, (7) taking second methane logistics through first gas-water separating device in the environment to obtain principal component is a liquid and gas phase material of water, adding gas phase material into third methane synthesis material, (8) adding the third methane synthesis material into the fourth reactor, and the third heat resistant methane catalyst, carbon monoxide synthetic material of the third methane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen reaction, synthesizing methane, so as to obtain third methane logistics, so as to obtain the methane product stream, (9) taking the methane product logistics obtained in the step (8) through the second gaswater separating device; separating to obtain raw LNG stream, (10) subjecting raw LNG stream into liquefaction process, methane produced by cryogenic liquefaction process to obtain the volume percentage is not less than 90% to obtain the product LNG and hydrogen co-volume percentage is not less than 90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The third synthesis gas through the desulfhurization process, the hydrogenation process, the desulfurization process and the decarburization process, to obtain a third purge gas. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the upgraded coal to oxygen is higher than 3:1. In the step (8), the third methane stream enters a fifth reactor, and in the fifth reactor, a fourth high-temperature resistant methane catalyst in the existing environment, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of the third methane stream react to synthesize methane to obtain a fourth methane stream with a carbon monoxide volume fraction content of less than 5%, obtaining the methane product stream and passing a third purge gas into the second reactor and/or the third reactor, when making methane and reaction temperature is 200-500 degrees C.
