TI Device adapted for diffusion of fluid, preferably carbon dioxide into grow medium adjacent to root mass of plant, comprises geometric shape comprising wall portion and cavity portion, inlet port, and outlet port.
AU GLAUDE T
   PROULX N M
AE GLAUDE T (GLAU-Individual)
   PROULX N M (PROU-Individual)
GA 201951711B
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises geometric shape comprising a wall portion and a cavity portion, a inlet port, and an outlet port, where the wall portion comprises several holes penetrating wall portion for diffusing fluid from the cavity portion to grow medium and inlet port connected to at least one tube which is connected to a source of fluid, where the fluid is in communication with the grow medium by way of holes, to the cavity to the tubing for diffusing the fluid and to grow medium, where the device is located a distance from the root mass of the plant so that the grow medium is capable of absorbing fluid and distance providing an accumulation buffer for the fluid.
   USE - The device is adapted for the diffusion of a fluid into the grow medium adjacent to the root mass of a plant. The fluid is chosen from carbon dioxide, air, pest control agents, disease control agents, water, and liquid fertilizer, gas emission of a fossil fuel burning device, oxygen and nitrogen, preferably carbon dioxide. The grow medium is chosen from water, soil, coco coir, perlite, peat moss, vermiculite, rockwool, growstones or clay pebbles (all claimed). The device is also used as a distribution node for the liquids or gases such as carbon dioxide bearing gas or water, or for injecting oxygen into the soil, or preventing root rot.
   ADVANTAGE - The device increases the yield and allows plants to uptake more water and nutrients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises (1) geometric shape comprising a wall portion and a cavity portion, a inlet port, and an outlet port, where the wall portion comprises several holes penetrating wall portion for diffusing fluid from the cavity portion to grow medium and inlet port connected to at least one tube which is connected to a source of fluid, where the fluid is in communication with the grow medium by way of holes, to the cavity to the tubing for diffusing the fluid and to grow medium, where the device is located a distance from the root mass of the plant so that the grow medium is capable of absorbing fluid and distance providing an accumulation buffer for the fluid, (2) geometric shape comprising a wall portion and a cavity portion, an inlet port, an outlet port, and a sponge portion located within the cavity portion, where the device is located at a distance from the root mass, and the sponge portion comprises a beneficial agent, where wall portion comprises holes penetrating the wall portion for diffusing fluid, where the fluid is in communication with the inlet port, outlet port, cavity, wall portion, holes and sponge with the beneficial agent and grow medium, the fluid is capable of flowing through the device through two paths, the first path defined as the fluid moving from the inlet port to the cavity portion, to the sponge portion containing the beneficial agent, to the holes, to the grow medium, to the root mass or the second path defined as the fluid moving from the inlet port to the cavity portion, to the sponge portion containing the beneficial agent, to the outlet port, or (3) a geometric shape comprising of a wall portion and a cavity portion, an inlet port, an outlet port, and a porous metal filter portion made from titanium dioxide located within the cavity portion, where the porous metal filter portion comprises beneficial agent, and wall portion having holes penetrating the wall portion for diffusing the fluid i.e. in communication with the inlet port, outlet port, cavity, wall portion, holes and sponge with the beneficial agent, and the grow medium, the fluid is capable of flowing through the device through two paths. The first path defined as the fluid moving from the inlet port to the cavity portion, to the sponge portion containing the beneficial agent, to the holes, to the grow medium, to the root mass or the second path defined as the fluid moving from the inlet port to the cavity portion, to the sponge portion containing the beneficial agent, to the outlet port.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The geometric shape is chosen from balls, round disk, triangles rectangles, squares, octagons, pyramids, tubes or cones. The device is made from a material chosen from plastic, ceramic, glass, stainless steel copper, aluminum or sponge, where the sponge is made from a material chosen from polyester, polyurethane, or vegetal cellulose, or poly(vinyl alcohol) (PVA). The distance from the device to root mass of the plant is 3 mm not greater than 13 mm. Preferred Agent: The beneficial agent is chosen from silver, copper, pest control agents, disease control agents, fertilizer and mycelia, vanadium pentoxide and tungsten trioxide and molybdenum trioxide dispersed on a high surface area titanium dioxide.

PN CN109369320-A
TI Purifying 5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon during crude benzene hydrotreating process, involves feeding hydrogenated oil into exhaust gas stabilization tower, cooling, condensing gas phase containing 1-5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon and ammonia.
AU LI H
AE ACRE COKING & REFRACTORY ENG CONSULTING (CMEG-C)
GA 201921470E
AB    NOVELTY - The method for purifying 5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon during crude benzene hydrotreating process, involves feeding hydrogenated oil into exhaust gas stabilization tower, producing light component in the form of a gas phase from the gas outlet at the top of the column, discharging liquid phase product, feeding the gas phase produced at the top of the exhaust stabilizer tower into condenser (I), cooling, feeding the formed gas-liquid mixture into reflux tank, returning the liquid phase in the gas-liquid mixture to exhaust gas stabilization tower, condensing gas phase containing 1-5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon, hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and carbon dioxide into condenser (II), condensing 5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon into liquid state, forming the gas-liquid mixture into the gas-liquid separator, releasing gas phase containing 1-4C non-aromatic product and hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and carbon dioxide from the top of the gas-liquid separator, discharging 5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon.
   USE - Method for purifying 5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon during crude benzene hydrotreating process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls the top operating temperature of the exhaust gas stabilization tower and the outlet temperature of the condenser (I) by controlling the steady state operation, and separates 5C non-aromatic hydrocarbons from the hydrogenated oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for purifying 5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon during crude benzene hydrotreating process, involves (1) feeding hydrogenated oil into exhaust gas stabilization tower through inlet of the raw material for separation and purification, controlling top of the tower at temperature of 50-120 degrees C and pressure of 0.3-0.8 MPa, producing light component in the form of a gas phase from the gas outlet at the top of the column, discharging liquid phase product from the bottom of the column into the extraction unit or the extractive distillation unit for further separation, (2) feeding the gas phase produced at the top of the exhaust stabilizer tower into condenser (I), cooling to 45-115 degrees C, feeding the formed gas-liquid mixture into reflux tank, (3) returning the liquid phase in the gas-liquid mixture to exhaust gas stabilization tower through a reflux pump and a valve, condensing gas phase containing 1-5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon, hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and carbon dioxide into condenser (II), (4) controlling the condensation temperature in the condenser (II) at 40-110 degrees C, condensing 5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon into liquid state, forming the gas-liquid mixture into the gas-liquid separator, (5) releasing gas phase containing 1-4C non-aromatic product and hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and carbon dioxide from the top of the gas-liquid separator, discharging 5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon from the bottom of the gas-liquid separator in the form of a liquid phase. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for purifying 5C non-aromatic hydrocarbon, which comprises an exhaust gas stabilization tower, a condenser (I), a reflux tank, a condenser (II) and a gas-liquid separation device. The exhaust gas stabilization tower is provided with a multi-layer tray and a raw material inlet arranged on a central side of the exhaust gas stabilization tower. A liquid product outlet is arranged at the bottom of exhaust gas stabilization tower, and a gas outlet is arranged at the top of exhaust gas stabilization tower. The gas outlet is connected to the inlet of the gas-liquid mixture at the top of the reflux tank through a condenser (I), and the liquid phase outlet provided at the bottom of the reflux tank is connected to the reflux inlet of the upper portion of the exhaust gas stabilization tower through a return line, and a reflux pump and a valve are arranged on the return line. The gas phase outlet at the top of the reflux tank is connected to the mixed liquid inlet of the upper portion of the gas-liquid separator through a condenser (II). The bottom of the gas-liquid separator is provided with a liquid product outlet, and a mist trap layer and a gas phase outlet are arranged at the top of separator.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

TI Concentrated mixture useful in alimentary multifunctional composition for foodstuff e.g. ada, bagels, and baked goods, comprises ethanol, and Cannabis volatiles.
AU LEO D M
AE LEO D M (LEOD-Individual)
GA 201896719B
AB    NOVELTY - Concentrated mixture comprises: ethanol; and Cannabis volatiles, where the mixture is free of wax.
   USE - The mixture is useful in alimentary multifunctional composition, which is used in foodstuff including ada, bagels, baked goods, biscuits, bitterballen, bonda, breads, cakes, candies, cereals, chips, chocolate bars, chocolate, coffee, cokodok, confectionery, cookies, cooking batter, maize starch mixtures, crackers, crepes, croissants, croquettes, croutons, dolma, dough, doughnuts, energy bars, flapjacks, french fries, frozen custard, frozen desserts, frying cakes, fudge, gelatin mixes, granola bars, gulha, hardtack, ice cream, khandvi, khanom buang, krumpets, meze, mixed flours, muffins, multi-grain snacks, nachos, nian gao, noodles, nougat, onion rings, pakora, pancakes, panforte, pastas, pastries, pie crust, pita chips, pizza, poffertjes, pretzels, protein powders, pudding, rice krispie treats, sesame sticks, smoothies, snacks, specialty milk, tele-bhaja, tempura, toffee, tortillas, totopo, turkish delights and/or waffles (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixture is free of wax.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Concentrated mixture comprises: ethanol; and Cannabis volatiles, where the mixture is free of wax. The mixture is made by: (a) providing a source of Cannabis including an energy content of 2500-65000 British thermal units/pound, a carbon content of 15-66 wt.%, an oxygen content of 10-60 wt.%, a hydrogen content of 2-25 wt.%, an ash content of 2-35 wt.%, a fat content of 4-45 wt.%, a volatiles content of 20-92 wt.%, a cannabidiol content of 0.00001-25 wt.%, and a tetrahydrocannabinol content of 4-66 wt.%; (b) extracting volatiles from the cannabis with carbon dioxide to form a carbon dioxide and volatiles mixture; (c) removing the carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide and volatiles mixture to produce extracted volatiles; (d) mixing the extracted volatiles with ethanol to produce an ethanol and volatiles mixture having 0.016-0.33 pounds of extracted volatiles per pound of ethanol; (e) cooling the ethanol and volatiles mixture to form a cooled ethanol and volatiles mixture; (f) filtering wax from the cooled ethanol and volatiles mixture to form a filtered ethanol and volatiles mixture; (g) evaporating the ethanol from the filtered ethanol and volatiles mixture under vacuum conditions to produce vaporized ethanol and a concentrated mixture of ethanol and cannabis volatiles; (h) condensing the vaporized ethanol to produce liquid ethanol and non-condensable gas, where the non-condensable gas has a reduced amount of ethanol vapor relative to the vaporized ethanol; and (i) introducing the non-condensable gas to a vacuum system. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) an alimentary multifunctional composition made by (a1) providing the concentrated mixture of ethanol and cannabis volatiles, and (b1) producing an alimentary multifunctional composition by mixing the concentrated mixture of ethanol and cannabis volatiles with at least one ingredient including a fiber-starch material, a binding agent, a density improving textural supplement, a moisture improving textural supplement, and insects, where the fiber-starch material includes at least one of cereal-grain-based materials, grass-based materials, nut-based materials, powdered fruit materials, root-based materials, tuber-based materials, and/ot vegetable-based materials, the binding agent includes at least one of agar, agave, alginin, arrowroot, carrageenan, collagen, maize starch, egg whites, finely ground seeds, furcellaran, gelatin, guar gum, honey, katakuri starch, locust bean gum, pectin, potato starch, proteins, psyllium husks, sago, sugars, syrups, tapioca, vegetable gums and/or xanthan gum, the density improving textural supplement includes at least one of extracted arrowroot starch, extracted maize starch, extracted lentil starch, extracted potato starch and/or extracted tapioca starch, the moisture improving textural supplement includes at least one of almonds, brazil nuts, cacao, cashews, chestnuts, coconut, filberts, hazelnuts, Indian nuts, macadamia nuts, nut butters, nut oils, nut powders, peanuts, pecans, pili nuts, pine nuts, pinon nuts, pistachios, soy nuts, sunflower seeds, tiger nuts and/or walnuts, and the insects include at least one of Orthoptera order of insects, grasshoppers, crickets, locusts, cicadas, ants, mealworms, agave worms, scorpions, tarantulas, termites and insect lipids; and
   (2) a foodstuff made by (a2) providing the alimentary multifunctional, and (b2) producing a foodstuff from the alimentary multifunctional composition
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (b), the carbon dioxide includes supercritical carbon dioxide (preferred) and/or subcritical carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The Cannabis further includes: a bacteria; decarboxylated Cannabis; a fungus; a Cannabis sativa subspecies sativa content of 15-85 wt.%; and a Cannabis sativa subspecies indica content of 15-85 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Detecting electronic smoke aerosol water extract active oxygen to the cell secretion comprises pre-treating sample, pumping, collecting, filtering, culturing, observing, removing, washing, incubating, diluting, detecting, and calculating.
AU GUAN Y

   XIONG G
   GAO Q
   LU S
   MI Q
   YAO J
   ZHU Z
AE CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 201824319V
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting electronic smoke aerosol water extract active oxygen to the cell secretion comprises (i) pre-treating sample with linear type smoking machine, pumping, collecting, filtering, (ii) preparing single cell suspension of human lung fibroblast, culturing, observing, removing, washing, incubating, (iii) calculating cell concentration, (iv) diluting counted single cell suspension into fibroblast medium cell culture medium, (v) removing cell culture liquid, detecting, (vi) removing cell culture medium, (vii) washing cells, and measuring and (viii) calculating active oxygen level.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting electronic smoke aerosol water extract active oxygen to the cell secretion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting electronic smoke aerosol water extract active oxygen to the cell secretion comprises (i) pre-treating the sample with linear type smoking machine, where the pumping capacity is 55.0 ml, pumping the duration for 3 seconds, the pumping frequency for 30 seconds, continuously pumping the 100 electronic cigarette, collecting bottle of the fibroblast medium cell culture medium for capturing electronic cigarette smoke, collecting the concentration is 20/ml, filtering the collecting liquid for 0.22 mu m sterile filter to obtain the electronic cigarette smoke aerosol water extract as testing solution and stored at -80 degrees C, (ii) preparing single cell suspension of human lung fibroblast after recovering, inoculating into the 25 cm2 cells culture bottle, adding 10 ml fibroblast medium cell culture medium, culturing at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide culture box, observing cell culture confluence with inverted microscope and morphology conditions, when the cell grows to 70-90% confluence rate, removing the fibroblast medium cell culture medium in the culture bottle, washing trypsin solution layer with phosphoric acid buffer solution for two times, discarding the washing liquid, adding 1 ml 0.25 % (w/v) trypsin solution in monolayer, incubating for 1-2 minutes, culture medium suspending with fibroblast medium cell to form a single cell suspension, (iii) calculating the cell concentration of the cell resulting single cell suspension using hemocytometer counting methoptical density to obtain the number of live cells/ml single cell suspension, (iv) diluting counted single cell suspension into 2.0x 105 ml the fibroblast medium cell culture medium, inoculating the 96-well cell culture plate with 200 mu l inoculum, placing the 96-hole cell culture plate at 37 degrees C 5% carbon dioxide incubator cultivating for 24 hours, (v) removing the cell culture liquid, adding cell culture plate as the detecting sample group according to the tested liquid and culture medium is has given in the speciation , detecting dose 4 holes parallel, placing into a culture box at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, and setting the positive control group, where the positive control stock solution is 25 mg/ml of Rosup solution, (vi) removing the cell culture medium from the cell culture plate after the completion of the exposure culture, adding 100 mu l of 2',7'-dichlorofluorescein diacetate fluorescent probe with a final concentration of 10 mu mol/well and incubate at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 20 minutes, (vii) removing the liquid cell culture plate, washing the cells in each well with phosphate buffer solution for 3 times, removing dichlorofluorescein diacetate fluorescent probe in the cell, and measuring the photometric value with a fluorescence spectrometer at excitation wavelength of 488 nm within 1-6 hours and (viii) calculating active oxygen level according to the obtained absorbency value, active oxygen level of sample is calculated according to the following formula, characterized by relative fluorescence intensity, if the optical density increases after stimulation, calculate the rate of increase in Reactive oxygen reduction: Reactive oxygen reduction increase rate = (optical density value after stimulation - optical density before stimulation)/optical density value before stimulationx100%, If the optical density decreases after stimulation, calculate the reduction rate of Reactive oxygen reduction: Reactive oxygen reduction rate = (optical density value before stimulation - optical density value after stimulation)/optical density value before stimulationx100%.

PN CN114011342-A; CN114011342-B
TI System for converting industrial exhaust gas            carbon dioxide into cyclic carbonate, includes            dehydration reactor, cycloaddition reactor, epoxy            feedstock storage tank, carbonate storage tank, and            ethylene glycol or propanediol storage tank.
AU GAO X
   SUN L
   FAN Y
   LI Y
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
GA 202228779M
AB    NOVELTY - A system includes a dehydration reactor (1), a                cycloaddition reactor (3), an epoxy compound                feedstock storage tank (4), a carbonate product                storage tank (5) and an ethylene glycol or                1,2-propanediol product storage tank (6). The inlet                of the dehydration reactor is connected with                industrial exhaust gas carbon dioxide input pipe                through flue gas induced draft fan (7) and its                outlet is connected with a booster fan (9) through                a deep desulfurization/denitrification device (2)                and a buffer airbag (8). The bottom of the                cycloaddition reactor is connected to the carbonate                product storage tank via a collection pump (12). A                sprayer is arranged in upper portion of the                cycloaddition reactor, and connected with bottom of                the epoxy compound feedstock storage tank through a                filling pump (11). The collection device of                dehydration reactor is connected to output pipe of                filling pump and its bottom is connected to                ethylene glycol or 1,2-propanediol product storage                tank through a collection pump (10).
   USE - System used for converting industrial exhaust                gas carbon dioxide into cyclic carbonate                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system can save investment and operation                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system includes a dehydration reactor, a                cycloaddition reactor, an epoxy compound feedstock                storage tank, a carbonate product storage tank and                an ethylene glycol or 1,2-propanediol product                storage tank. The inlet of the dehydration reactor                is connected with industrial exhaust gas carbon                dioxide input pipe through flue gas induced draft                fan and its outlet is connected with a booster fan                through a deep desulfurization/denitrification                device and a buffer airbag. The bottom of the                cycloaddition reactor is connected to the carbonate                product storage tank via a collection pump, the top                is provided with a discharge pipe connected with                exhaust gas treatment device and the middle is                provided with an input port for basic catalyst. A                sprayer is arranged in upper portion of the                cycloaddition reactor, and the sprayer is connected                with bottom of the epoxy compound feedstock storage                tank through a filling pump. A carbon dioxide gas                distributor is provided at inner bottom of the                cycloaddition reactor, and the carbon dioxide gas                distributor is connected to outlet of the booster                fan. The collection device of the dehydration                reactor is connected to the output pipe of the                filling pump and its bottom is connected to                ethylene glycol or 1,2-propanediol product storage                tank through a pipeline through a collection pump,                and a solid base catalyst input pipeline is                provided.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for converting industrial exhaust gas carbon                dioxide into cyclic carbonate, which involves: (a)                entering industrial exhaust gas containing moisture                with volume concentration of 5-10% to dehydration                reactor through flue gas induced draft fan and                obtaining the flue gas and water from which the                moisture is removed respectively; (b) entering the                flue gas from which moisture has been removed to                deep desulfurization/denitrification device, and                removing interfering substances, to obtain treated                flue gas with carbon dioxide volume concentration                of 10-100%; (c) adding a basic catalyst to the                middle of the cycloaddition reactor, while adding                solid basic catalyst to the bottom of the                dehydration reactor; (d) spraying epoxy compound at                a uniform speed of 200-1000 kg/hour from top of                cycloaddition reactor through filling pump from the                epoxy compound raw material storage tank,                introducing treated flue gas from bottom of the                cycloaddition reactor through buffer airbag and the                booster fan at a flow rate of 1-2x103 m3/hour and                obtaining first exhaust gas and cyclic carbonate by                cycloaddition reaction at 50-150degrees Celsius,                entering cyclic carbonate to carbonate product                storage tank through the collection pump, entering                the epoxy compound from bottom of the dehydration                reactor at rate of 90-360 kg/hour through the                filling pump and reacting with water, to obtain                second exhaust gas and ethylene glycol or                1,2-propylene glycol, and entering the ethylene                glycol or 1,2-propylene glycol to ethylene glycol                or 1,2-propylene glycol product storage tank                through the collection pump; and (f) collecting                first exhaust gas and second exhaust gas,                performing condensation and adsorption treatment,                collecting unreacted raw materials and products                brought out by gas flow, and further processing and                reusing until the discharge is up to the standard,                regenerating base catalyst in cycloaddition                reaction and solid base catalyst in hydration                reaction by filtering, and centrifuging or removing                product by distillation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   Dehydration reactor (1)
   Deep desulfurization/denitrification device                (2)
   Cycloaddition reactor (3)
   Epoxy compound feedstock storage tank                (4)
   Carbonate product storage tank (5)
   Ethylene glycol or 1,2-propanediol product                storage tank (6)
   Flue gas induced draft fan (7)
   Buffer air bag (8)
   Booster fan (9)
   Collection pump (10)
   Filling pump (11)
   Collection pump (12)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The basic catalyst is                  solid-phase ionic compound catalyst or a                  homogeneous molecular catalyst. The solid-phase                  ionic compound catalyst is basic supported ionic                  liquid or basic polymeric ionic liquid. The basic                  polymerized ionic liquid is imidazole halide                  polymer material containing hydroxyl, imine group                  and styrene, of formula (I).x=more than 10; R1=H or 1-12C alkyl;                  m,n=1-4; and X=Cl, Br or I.Preferred Process: The basic polymeric ionic                  liquid is prepared using azobisisobutyronitrile                  or di-tert-butyl peroxide as initiator,                  dissolving basic ionic liquid monomer containing                  vinyl in solvent, reacting at 80-140degrees                  Celsius for 12-36 hours, washing and                  drying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The basic supported ionic                  liquid is obtained by dispersing 1 pts. wt. basic                  ionic liquid into 1-4 pts. wt. solvent to prepare                  basic ionic liquid solution, adding 0.5-4 pts.                  wt. carrier, ultrasonically impregnating with                  power of 200-800 W at room temperature to                  80degrees Celsius for 1-4 hours, centrifuging or                  filtering, washing, and vacuum drying at                  60-100degrees Celsius. Preferred Components: The                  basic ionic liquid is imidazole, quaternary                  ammonium or quaternary phosphorus halide                  containing hydroxyl and imide group, chosen from                  7 compounds e.g. compounds of formulae (II)-(VI).                  The carrier is one of silica gel, mesoporous                  alumina, molecular sieve, styrene resin,                  graphene, metal organic framework and its                  derivative. The solvent is one of methanol,                  ethanol, acetonitrile, isopropanol, n-propanol,                  acetone or n-butanol. The homogeneous molecular                  catalyst is one of basic ionic liquid of                  1-(4-formate)benzyl-3-alkylimidazole halide of                  formula (VII) and (VIII), 1-(4-sulfonate)                  benzyl-3-alkylimidazole halide of formula (IX)                  and (X) and its derivatives.R,R'=1-12C alkyl; R''=H or 1-4C alkyl;                  M+=metal ion, such as sodium ion or potassium                  ion; and M2+=metal ion such as magnesium ion or                  zinc ion.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid base                  catalyst is one or more of zirconia, calcium                  oxide and sodium carbonate.

PN DE202019001415-U1; EP3632525-A1; WO2020069868-A1; CA3114627-A1; CN112805079-A; AU2019351771-A1; KR2021068547-A; BR112021005915-A2; SG11202103127-A1; EP3860742-A1; US2021339189-A1; JP2022502241-W; IN202117019634-A; CN112805079-B; RU2021107482-A
TI Device for separation of methane from methane,            carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide-containing gas            mixture, has hydrogen sulfide adsorber which is            arranged between recirculation feed point and first            membrane separation stage.
AU WINKLER F
   VINKLER F
AE EVONIK FIBRES GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK FIBRES GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK FIBRES GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK FIBRES GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 201944862X
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a gas compressor (1), and a                feed line (2) which feeds mixed gas into the gas                compressor. A recirculation line (12) is connected                to a recirculation feed point (13) on the feed line                upstream of the gas compressor. The recirculation                line is connected to a second membrane separation                stage (6) for receiving a second permeate (8). A                third membrane separation stage (9) receives a                third retentate (10) or both the second membrane                separation stage and the third membrane separation                stage receives the second permeate and the third                retentate if the optional third membrane separation                stage is present, if the optional third membrane                separation stage is present. A hydrogen sulfide                adsorber (14) comprises an activated carbon bed                having catalytic activity for the oxidation of                hydrogen sulfide with oxygen. The hydrogen sulfide                adsorber is arranged between the recirculation feed                point and a first membrane separation stage                (3).
   USE - Device for the separation of methane from a                methane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen                sulfide-containing gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The device improves the removal of hydrogen                sulfide with activated carbon. The hydrogen sulfide                adsorber comprising an activated carbon bed is                operated at a biogas relative humidity of less than                predetermined range to prevent pore condensation of                water which reduces the rate of hydrogen sulfide                adsorption. The hydrogen sulfide adsorber is                arranged upstream of the gas compressor between the                recirculation feed point and the gas compressor,                which prevents corrosion by hydrogen sulfide in the                gas compressor. The degradation of the oil is                prevented by reaction of hydrogen sulfide or                mercaptans with components of the oil when using an                oil-cooled gas compressor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The first membrane separation stage downstream                of the gas compressor comprises a higher                permeability carbon dioxide gas separation membrane                than methane and provides a first retentate (4) and                a first permeate (5). The second membrane                separation stage is connected to the first membrane                separation stage for receiving the first retentate                as feed stream. The second membrane separation                stage comprises a gas permeable membrane of greater                permeability to carbon dioxide than methane and                provides a second retentate (7) as product gas                enriched in methane and the second permeate. The                third membrane separation stage is connected to the                first membrane separation stage for receiving the                first permeate as feed stream. The third membrane                separation stage comprises a higher permeability                gas separation membrane for carbon dioxide than for                methane and provides the third retentate and a                third permeate (11). The hydrogen sulfide adsorber                is arranged between the recirculation feed point                and the gas compressor. The recirculation line                includes an additional connection to an additional                recycle feed point (15) located between a hydrogen                sulfide adsorber (14) and the gas compressor. A                control valve (16) controls amount of recirculated                gas supplied to the recirculation feed point and                the additional recirculation feed point. An oxygen                concentration measurement (17) at the feed line                between the recirculation feed point and the                hydrogen sulfide adsorber or between the hydrogen                sulfide adsorber and the additional recycle feed                point is provided. A control unit is configured to                maintain the oxygen concentration within a                predetermined range by operating the control valve.                A measurement of relative humidity at the feed line                between the recirculation feed point and the                hydrogen sulfide adsorber or between the hydrogen                sulfide adsorber and the additional recycle feed                point is provided. The control unit is configured                to maintain the relative humidity within a                predetermined range by operating the control valve.                The carbon dioxide condensation stage is connected                to the first membrane separation stage for                receiving the first permeate as feed stream. A                liquid condensate is enriched in carbon dioxide and                a mixture of uncondensed gases. Another return line                is connected to another return feed point between                the gas compressor and the first membrane                separation stage. The return line is connected to                the carbon dioxide condensation stage for receiving                the mixture of non-condensed gases. The hydrogen                sulfide adsorber comprises iodine, an iodine salt                or an iodine precursor doped or impregnated                activated carbon. The hydrogen sulfide adsorbent                comprises doped or impregnated activated carbon                with a basic compound selected from alkali metal                hydroxides, alkali metal carbonates, alkaline earth                metal oxides and alkaline earth metal carbonates.                The membrane separation stages comprise polyimide                hollow fiber membranes.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating the device with three membrane                separation stages.
   Gas compressor (1)
   Feed line (2)
   First membrane separation stage (3)
   First retentate (4)
   First permeate (5)
   Second membrane separation stage (6)
   Second retentate (7)
   Second permeate (8)
   Third membrane separation stage (9)
   Third retentate (10)
   Third permeate (11)
   Recirculation line (12)
   Recirculation feed point (13)
   Hydrogen sulfide adsorber (14)
   Additional recycle feed point (15)
   Control valve (16)
   Oxygen concentration measurement (17)

PN CN112521263-A; CN112521263-B
TI Preparing formate/formic acid by catalyzing carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction with metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex, comprises reacting carbon dioxide/hydrogen mixed gas in presence of potassium carbonate.
AU WANG W
   DUANMU Y
   BAO M
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202131835V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing formate/formic acid by catalyzing carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction with metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex, comprises e.g. adding potassium carbonate, and anhydrous and oxygen-free ethanol into a reaction kettle under the protection of nitrogen to obtain ethanol solution of potassium carbonate with concentration of 0.5-1 mol/l, adding metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex into the reaction kettle, replacing nitrogen with carbon dioxide/hydrogen mixed gas of 1-4 MPa at room temperature, stirring and reacting at 90-130 degrees C for 24 hours, cooling the reaction kettle to room temperature, releasing the pressure, and collecting formate solution product to obtain high 0.103 mmol/l formate solution having turnover number (TON) 13733; removing the potassium carbonate under the above same conditions to obtain 0.018 mmol/l formic acid solution having TON of 2400. The metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex is (I) and (II).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing formate/formic acid by catalyzing carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction with metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple and low cost preparation method of metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex series, which exhibits high activity and high selectivity of homogeneous catalysts, can obtain regenerated catalysts after simple centrifugation, washing and drying, and realize reuse; can efficiently catalyze the hydrogenation and reduction of carbon dioxide to produce formate at lower reaction temperature and pressure and under the condition of adding a weak alkali; and effectively catalyze the hydrogenation and reduction of carbon dioxide and directly produce formic acid without addition of alkali.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing formate/formic acid by catalyzing carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction with metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex, comprises adding potassium carbonate, and anhydrous and oxygen-free ethanol into a reaction kettle under the protection of nitrogen to obtain ethanol solution of potassium carbonate with concentration of 0.5-1 mol/l, adding metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex into the reaction kettle, replacing nitrogen with carbon dioxide/hydrogen mixed gas of 1-4 MPa at room temperature, where the volume ratio between carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1:1-5:1, stirring and reacting at 90-130 degrees C for 24 hours, cooling the reaction kettle to room temperature, releasing the pressure, and collecting formate solution product to obtain high 0.103 mmol/l formate solution having turnover number (TON) 13733; removing the potassium carbonate under the above same conditions to obtain 0.018 mmol/l formic acid solution having TON of 2400. The metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex is of formulae (I) and (II).
   G = pentamethylcyclopentadienyl;
   R1 = metal substituted alkoxy moieties of formula (1) or metal substituted aryl moieties of formula (2);
   X = Zn, Zr, Al, Cu, Cr or Fe; and
   R2 = H, CH3, OCH3, OH, NH2, N(CH3)2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex is prepared by (i) dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate and 2-aminoterephthalic acid in dimethylformamide and heating it at 100 degrees C for 24 hours to produce cubic crystals of zinc- metal-organic frameworks (MOF), cooling to room temperature, centrifugal separating, washing the cubic crystals repeatedly with anhydrous dimethylformamide and chloroform, and soaking in chloroform for 12 hours, filtering the final product and drying it under vacuum for 48 hours to obtain zinc-MOF, (ii) dispersing the prepared zinc-MOF in anhydrous and oxygen-free dichloromethane, adding solution of picolinaldehyde in anhydrous and oxygen-free dichloromethane into the mixture at room temperature, heating at 60 degrees C under the protection of inert gas nitrogen, maintaining for 30 minutes, cooling to room temperature and maintaining for 3 days until the reaction is completed, rinsing with anhydrous and oxygen-free dichloromethane for three times, and vacuum drying to obtain MOF containing imine ligands, dispersing the MOF containing imine ligands in anhydrous and oxygen-free methanol under the protection of inert gas nitrogen, adding anhydrous and oxygen-free methanol solution of the catalyst precursor pentamethylcyclopentadienyl iridium dichloride dimer into the above mixed solution at room temperature, heating it at 60 degrees C and maintaining it for 24 hours to react, collecting the solid material by filtration, washing with methanol for three times, and vacuum drying to obtain (I), (iii) mixing picolinic acid and N,N-carbonyldiimidazole, sealing one end, arranging the condenser at one end, arranging to a double-row tube, replacing with nitrogen for three times, adding anhydrous and oxygen-free dimethylformamide under nitrogen atmosphere and reacting at 50 degrees C for 2 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding the prepared zinc-MOF into the reaction solution, and reacting at room temperature for 6 hours, collecting the solid materials by filtration, washing repeatedly with anhydrous dimethylformamide and methanol, and soaking in methanol for 12 hours, filtering the final product and drying it under vacuum to obtain MOF containing amide ligands, dispersing the MOF containing amide ligands in anhydrous and oxygen-free methanol under the protection of inert gas nitrogen, adding the anhydrous and oxygen-free methanol solution of the catalyst precursor pentamethylcyclopentadienyl iridium dichloride dimer into the mixed solution at room temperature, heating it at 60 degrees C and maintaining it for 24 hours to react, collecting the solid material by filtration, rinsing with anhydrous and oxygen-free methanol for three times, and drying under vacuum to obtain (II). The regeneration treatment of metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex comprises washing with ethanol as detergent for three times and drying in an oven at 100 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain regenerated catalyst after carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction to prepare formate/formic acid by using metal-organic frameworks supported iridium complex as catalyst.

PN CN107842344-A
TI Storing stratified carbon dioxide of water flow erosion hydrate mining reservoirs comprises e. g. drilling three production wells in gas hydrate reservoirs and drilling multiple water flow channels between two adjacent production wells.
AU SONG Y
   YANG M
   CHEN B
   WANG D
   JIANG L
   LIU Y
   LING Z
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2018252206
AB    NOVELTY - Storing stratified carbon dioxide of water flow erosion hydrate mining reservoirs comprises e. g. drilling three production wells in gas hydrate reservoirs, drilling multiple water flow channels between two adjacent production wells, ensuring the water flow channels are layered over the gas hydrate reservoirs, realizing the communication between three production wells, accessing the right amount of seawater and ensuring the normal flow of sea water and selecting the middle production and natural gas discharge collection channel.
   USE - The method is useful for storing stratified carbon dioxide of water flow erosion hydrate mining reservoirs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to realize, beneficial for mining the hydrate and safe for sequestration of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Storing stratified carbon dioxide of water flow erosion hydrate mining reservoirs comprises (i) drilling three production wells in gas hydrate reservoirs, drilling multiple water flow channels between two adjacent production wells, ensuring the water flow channels are layered over the gas hydrate reservoirs, realizing the communication between three production wells, accessing the right amount of seawater and ensuring the normal flow of sea water; (ii) selecting the middle production and natural gas discharge collection channel, serving the production wells on both sides as reservoir pressure control and carbon dioxide emulsion injection channels, controlling the pressure difference between the two sides of the production well and the middle production well and promoting the flow of seawater; (iii) changing the pressure on both sides of the production well, increasing the pressure difference with the middle production well, controlling the low-speed flow of seawater in the water flow channel at 2 ml/minute, decomposing the hydrates in the peripheral part of the water flow channel, increasing the internal permeability of natural gas hydrate reservoirs, increasing the seawater flow rate when the hydrates are stably collected at the outlet of the intermediate production wells, stabilizing to 10 ml/minute, ensuring the hydrates decompose rapidly and completely within 1 hour by the flow rate adjustment, decomposing the hydrates in the area around each layer of water flow channel in a short time, delaminating and realizing rapid decomposition of hydrates; (iv) collecting methane gas at the outlet of the natural gas discharge collection channel, collecting methane gas, controlling the internal pressure of the natural gas discharge collection channel, storing and transporting the collected gas; and (v) ensuring the outlet of the natural gas discharge collection channel cannot be collected when methane gas cannot be collected or the amount of collected methane is small, injecting the carbon dioxide emulsions into gas hydrate reservoirs through the production wells on both sides in the current state of exploitation, flowing carbon dioxide emulsion in the gas hydrate reservoir at the same pressure difference, ensuring the carbon dioxide emulsion fills the pores of the gas hydrate reservoir sufficiently and making the carbon dioxide hydrate fully and completely fill the extracted natural gas hydrate reservoir.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

TI Method for removing pollutants e.g. carbon dioxide            from flue gas stream, involves dehydrating and chilling            dehydrated gas to condense and separate pollutants, and            pumping condensed pollutants to sequestration            pressure.

   HASAN M M F
   HOLTZAPPLE M T
AE UNIV TEXAS A & M SYSTEM (UTAM-C)
   UNIV TEXAS A & M SYSTEM (UTAM-C)
GA 2022862052
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cooling a flue gas to                remove condensed water, and compressing the gas.                The gas is dehydrated, and the dehydrated gas is                chilled to condense and separate pollutants. The                condensed pollutants are pumped to a sequestration                pressure, and noncondensible gases are expanded,                where the pollutants comprise carbon dioxide and                sulfur dioxide. The dehydrating comprises use of an                adsorbent e.g. lithium bromide, and regenerating                the adsorbents. The chilling comprises utilizing a                multi-stage cascade of refrigerants. The chilled                refrigerant is cycled through a multistage                refrigeration system.
   USE - Method for removing pollutants from flue gas.                Uses include but are not limited to carbon dioxide,                sulfur oxides, and nitrogen oxides from coal, oil,                and natural gas.

TI Adsorbent used as carbondioxide/nitrogen            separation adsorbent, comprises molecular sieves            containing aromatic carboxylic acid and titanate            co-modified; and prepared by e.g. reacting cationic            molecular sieve and titanate in organic solvent and            reacting.

   YANG L
   LIU H
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202283532Y
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorbent, comprises molecular sieves                containing aromatic carboxylic acid and titanate                co-modified; where the molecular sieve is a                cationic molecular sieve.
   USE - The absorbent is used as                carbondioxide/nitrogen separation adsorbent                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent: reduces the size of the surface                window of the molecular sieve; improves the                selectivity of the adsorbent to carbon dioxide; can                reach carbon dioxide/nitrogen separation ratio of                73 and above; has excellent carbon dioxide/nitrogen                adsorption and separation performance, and                excellent industrial application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   preparing adsorbent, comprising (i) obtaining                the cationic molecular sieve, (ii) reacting the                cationic molecular sieve and titanate in an organic                solvent in reaction I, and (iii) subjecting the                reaction solution obtained in step (ii), and                solution containing aromatic carboxylic acid to                reaction II in a closed vessel to obtain final                product;
   carbondioxide/nitrogen separation adsorbent,                comprising adsorbent; and
   use of adsorbent comprising adsorbing carbon                dioxide and nitrogen at 25&#8451; under the                reaction pressure of 100 kPa, where equilibrium                separation ratio of carbon dioxide and nitrogen is                not less than 32.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises ion                  exchanging the molecular sieve to obtain the                  cationic molecular sieve, preferably the ion                  exchange includes the temperature at                  20-90&#8451;, the ion exchange time is 2-24                  hours, and the number of exchange is 1-5 times,                  and the molecular sieve is ion-exchanged in the                  solution containing cation; and the solid-liquid                  ratio of the ion exchange is 1:5-1:100 g/ml. The                  method comprises (i), ion-exchanging the                  molecular sieve in a salt solution corresponding                  to the cation at 20-90&#8451;, then washing                  and drying to obtain the cationic molecular                  sieve, (ii) dispersing the cationic molecular                  sieve in an organic solvent, then adding                  titanate, and reacting at 15-50&#8451; for                  1-20 minutes, and (iii) adding the solution of                  aromatic carboxylic acid into the system, adding                  into a closed reaction vessel within 10-60                  minutes, and reacting at 60-200&#8451; for                  5-20 hours, then washing and drying to obtain the                  adsorbent. In step (ii), the boiling point of the                  organic solvent is 60-90&#8451;; in step                  (ii), the organic solvent includes methanol,                  ethanol, cyclohexane, n-hexane, dichloromethane,                  trichloromethane, methane and/or carbon                  tetrachloride; in step (ii), the mass ratio of                  the cationic molecular sieve and the organic                  solvent is 1:1-1:20; the mass ratio of the                  cationic molecular sieve and titanium ester                  compound is 1:0.01-1:0.5 and in step (ii), the                  temperature of the reaction I is                  15-50&#8451;, and the time is 1-20 minutes;                  in step (iii), the mass ratio of the aromatic                  carboxylic acid and titanate is 1-5:1; in step                  (iii), the solvent in the solution containing                  aromatic carboxylic acid is methanol and                  dimethylformamide; and the mass ratio of the                  dimethylformamide and methanol is 3-15:1; in step                  (iii), the concentration of aromatic carboxylic                  acid is 0.05-50 wt.% and in step (iii), the                  temperature of reaction II is 60-200&#8451;,                  and the time is 5-20 hours.Preferred Components: The aromatic                  carboxylic acid includes 7-18C aromatic                  carboxylic acids; and at least one carboxyl group                  is directly connecting to benzene ring,                  preferably the aromatic carboxylic acid includes                  trimesic acid, terephthalic acid, benzoic acid                  and/or its functionally substituted derivatives,                  where the functionally substituted derivatives                  includes aminoterephthalic acid,                  2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid or aminobenzoic                  acid. The elements in the cationic molecular                  sieve are sodium, potassium, cesium, magnesium,                  calcium, barium, strontium, chromium, manganese,                  iron, cobalt, nickel, copper and/or zinc. The                  titanate is of formula (I), preferably titanate                  includes methyl titanate, tetraethyl titanate,                  isopropyl titanate, tetrabutyl titanate and/or                  isooctyl titanate.R1,R2,R3and R4 = 1-8C alkyl group.

PN CN114522512-A
TI Improving aquatic carbon sinks by absorbing atmospheric carbon dioxide during weathering of terrestrial carbonate rocks, capturing and storing carbon dioxide in soil pores, mixing with rainwater and converting into organic carbon.
AU XIE F
   YANG W
   ZHANG M
   YANG M
AE GUIZHOU TIANYI HENGSHENG MINERAL RESOURCES ENG DESIGN CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202279577F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving aquatic carbon sinks involves (a) absorbing atmospheric carbon dioxide during the weathering of terrestrial carbonate rocks and temporarily capturing and storing the carbon dioxide in the form of gas in the soil pores to realize the enrichment of carbon dioxide in the soil, (b) mixing the inorganic carbon captured and stored in soil pores with rainwater during the rainfall process to form dissolved inorganic carbon, which flows into the surface water body through surface runoff for increasing the dissolved inorganic carbon in the surface water body and (c) increasing the amount of inorganic carbon required by aquatic plants in the water body by steps (a) and (b), converting the dissolved inorganic carbon in the water body into organic carbon for storage by the photosynthesis of aquatic plants.
   USE - The method is useful for improving aquatic carbon sinks.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can greatly increase the dissolved inorganic carbon content in the surface water, so as to increase the fertilizing effect, improve the water generating amount of carbon sequestration, and will not generate the environment negative effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves (i) spreading a layer of peat soil that can store a large amount of carbon dioxide on the basis of the original soil in the field, uniformly mixing and obtaining a soil layer with high porosity and (ii) utilizing the effect of terrestrial carbonate rock weathering to absorb carbon dioxide, so that a large amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere is temporarily captured and stored in the soil layer for realizing the enrichment of dissolved carbon dioxide in the soil layer. The step (b) involves (i) arranging several rainfall dredging channels in the field to guide the rainfall in the field and (ii) dredging the channel through rainfall, so that a large amount of rainwater in the field infiltrates into the groundwater, and flows into the surface water body through the groundwater, increasing the amount of dissolved inorganic carbon in the surface water body. In the method, the dissolved inorganic carbon includes carbon dioxide, bicarbonate and carbonate ion. In the method, two sources of dissolved inorganic carbon are provided in the surface water body, one of the sources of dissolved inorganic carbon is dissolved inorganic carbon in atmospheric rainfall and another source of dissolved inorganic carbon is atmospheric carbon dioxide absorbed by rock weathering.

TI Producing synthetic diamond by capturing carbon dioxide from atmosphere, performing electrolysis of water, reacting carbon dioxide with the hydrogen, and using hydrogen and methane to produce synthetic diamond by chemical vapor deposition.
AU VINCE D
AE ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD (ECOT-Non-standard)
GA 2021A8917S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a synthetic diamond involves: (a) capturing carbon dioxide from an atmosphere; (b) performing electrolysis of water to provide hydrogen; (c) reacting the carbon dioxide obtained from step (a) with the hydrogen obtained from step (b) to produce methane; and (d) using the hydrogen obtained from step (b) and the methane obtained from step (c) to produce a synthetic diamond by chemical vapor deposition (CVD).
   USE - The method or system is useful for producing a synthetic diamond (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - At least one benefit of the process is that a number of methane-production (methanation) methods may be used in step (c).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for producing a synthetic diamond, comprising an electrolyzer for performing electrolysis of water to provide hydrogen; a reactor positioned downstream from the electrolyzer and arranged to react carbon dioxide with the hydrogen provided using the electrolyzer to produce methane; and CVD machine positioned downstream from the reactor and arranged to use the hydrogen provided using the electrolyzer and the methane produced using the reactor to produce a synthetic diamond by CVD.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The methanogens are prokaryotes, where the prokaryotes are archaea.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: Step (a) involves capturing carbon dioxide using an amine-containing sorbent-material. Capturing carbon dioxide using the amine-containing sorbent material is conducted at a temperature below 25degrees Celsius. The captured carbon dioxide is subsequently released by heating the amine-containing sorbent material. Step (b) involves performing the electrolysis of water using at least one polymer electrolyte membrane (PEM) cell. The reaction of step (c) is performed using methanogens. CVD is carried out on a substrate comprising diamond. The method further involves using energy from at least one renewable source to perform at least one of the steps (a)-(d); or using energy from at least one renewable source to perform the electrolysis. The method further involves an additional step, after step (d), of annealing the synthetic diamond. It involves an additional step of at least one of cutting and polishing the diamond produced in step (d).Preferred Conditions: The electrolysis is performed at a temperature below 150degrees Celsius. CVD is carried out at a pressure of 0.5-100 kPa. CVD is carried out at 700-1000degrees Celsius.Preferred Composition: The ratio of hydrogen to methane used in step (d) is 1:1-9:1.Preferred Components: The water comprises rainwater. The system further comprises an amine-containing sorbent-material for capturing the carbon dioxide from an atmosphere positioned upstream of the reactor, and the reactor is arranged to react the carbon dioxide captured using the amine-containing sorbent-material with the hydrogen provided using the electrolyzer to produce the methane.

PN CN112316902-A
TI Composite magnesium oxide adsorbent used for adsorbing carbon dioxide in power plant flue gas mixture gas, comprises porous activated carbon carrier and active component of magnesium oxide dispersed in porous activated carbon carrier.
AU XUN B
   TONG D
AE NANJING ZHENGSEN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021153482
AB    NOVELTY - Composite magnesium oxide adsorbent comprises a porous activated carbon carrier and an active component of magnesium oxide uniformly dispersed in the porous activated carbon carrier, where the active component of magnesium oxide is synthesized by calcining an organic magnesium precursor comprising magnesium ethoxide, magnesium acetate, magnesium oxalate, magnesium lactate, magnesium citrate or magnesium gluconate.
   USE - The adsorbent is used as a carbon dioxide adsorbent, and for adsorbing carbon dioxide in power plant flue gas mixture gas and industrial waste gas, adsorbing carbon dioxide at 200 degrees C (all claimed), and chemical adsorption and physical adsorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent ensures synergistic effect of physical adsorption and chemical adsorption, and increases adsorption capacity and adsorption rate of the composite adsorbent for carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the adsorbent, involving (a) mixing organic magnesium precursor with water to obtain an aqueous solution, ultrasonically dissolving the organic magnesium precursor, adding porous activated carbon, uniformly mixing and drying to obtain an intermediate and (b) calcining and decomposing the intermediate in a muffle furnace under air atmosphere to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass fraction of the organic magnesium precursor in the formed aqueous solution in step (a) is 1-10%. The molar ratio of the organic magnesium precursor to the porous activated carbon in step (a) is 1:10. Preferred Method: The condition for mixing the organic magnesium precursor and the porous activated carbon in step (a) is carried out in a normal temperature ultrasonic environment with an ultrasonic condition of 700 W and 40 KHz. The step (b) involves calcining at 300-600 degrees C for 30 minutes.

   USE - New                N,N-bis-pyridin-2-ylmethyl-benzene-1,4-diamine used                as absorbent additive for carbon dioxide adsorption                process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound effectively solves the problem of                corrosion of equipment in carbon dioxide adsorption                process and enhances the carbon dioxide adsorption                performance of the solvent absorption liquid, even                when the compound (I) is added in reduced                amount.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A                N,N-bis-pyridin-2-ylmethyl-benzene-1,4-diamine of                formula (I), is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are                included for the following:
   (1) preparation of compound (I), which                involves reacting dioxane solution of di-tert-butyl                dicarbonate with para-phenylenediamine at 40-70                degrees C, then reacting with triethylamine at                40-75 degrees C for 10-25 hours, reacting purified                product with 2-(chloromethyl)pyridine hydrochloride                and sodium carbonate in ethanol or dichloromethane                solvent at 55-95 degrees C for 10-49 hours and                purifying to obtain                4-amino-N,N-di-(2-pyridylmethyl) aniline; and
   (2) use of compound (I) to inhibit equipment                corrosion in a carbon dioxide capture environment                of 35-50 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of sodium                  carbonate to 2-(chloromethyl)pyridine                  hydrochloride is 0.5-2.6. The ratio of purified                  product and 2-(chloromethyl)pyridine                  hydrochloride is 1:1.8-2.4. The concentration of                  di-tert-butyl dicarbonate in the dioxane solution                  is 0.3-2.8 mol/L. The ratio of dioxane solution                  and para-phenylenediamine is 2.0-2.6:1. Preferred                  Properties: The concentration of pyridine-based                  organic amine in the carbon dioxide capture                  solution is 2x 10-6 to 1x 10-3 mol/L. The alcohol                  amine concentration in carbon dioxide capture                  solution is 3.3 mol/L. The molecular ion peak of                  compound (I) in mass spectrum is 291.1. The                  proton NMR spectrum of compound (I) shows                  chemical shift at 4.19-4.45 ppm for two hydrogen                  on aniline, 4.51-4.73 ppm for four hydrogen on                  methylene, 6.34-6.48 ppm for two hydrogen at                  ortho-position of amine group on benzene ring,                  6.40-6.55 ppm for two hydrogen at meta-position                  of amine group on benzene ring, 7.07-7.21 ppm for                  two hydrogen at para-position of two substituted                  pyridine ring, 7.14-7.32 ppm for two adjacent                  hydrogen of two substituted pyridine ring,                  7.45-7.63 ppm for two hydrogen at para-position                  of nitrogen on two pyridine ring and 8.31-8.58                  ppm for two hydrogen of nitrogen on two pyridine                  rings. Preferred Component: The alcohol amine is                  one or more of ethanolamine, diethanolamine and                  sterically hindered amine.

PN KR2018098509-A
TI New diketopyrrolopyrrole derivative useful in indication solution of sensor for indicating carbon dioxide, and in various fields e.g. environment field and industrial field to provide carbon dioxide pollution degree information.
AU PARK S Y
   LEE B M
   KIM H
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 201870686N
AB    NOVELTY - Diketopyrrolopyrrole derivative (I), are new.
   USE - (I) is useful in indication solution of sensor for indicating carbon dioxide (all claimed), and in various fields e.g. environment field and industrial field to provide carbon dioxide pollution degree information.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) improves solubility, dispersibility and dispersion stability of highly atomized pigment, exhibits stable dispersibility over long period of time, provides basic data on air quality of various indoor spaces, can promptly confirm information on whether or not carbon dioxide is generated, regardless of time and place, and can be systematically accumulated and managed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Diketopyrrolopyrrole derivative of formula (I), are new.
   R1, R2 = 1-20C alkyl, 1-20C alkoxy (6-30C) aryl or halo, provided that R1 and R2 are same or different from each other; and
   R3 = 6-30C aryl (4-20C) alkyl (optionally substituted with 1-20C alkyl, halo (1-20C) alkyl, halo, cyano, 1-20C alkoxy, 6-30C aryloxy and 6-30C aryl), or 4-20C alkyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) indication solution comprising (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises reacting diketopyrrolopyrrole derivative of formula (II), alkali metal alkoxide and halide of formula (III). Preferred Method: The reaction is carried out at 20-70 degrees C. Preferred Components: The indication solution comprises 0.01-5 wt.% (I) as main agent; and further comprises 1-30 wt.% auxiliary indicator comprising quaternary alkyl ammonium hydroxide, 50-70 wt.% polar solvent and 1-15 wt.% binder resin. The auxiliary indicator includes tetramethyl ammonium hydroxide, tetraethylammonium hydroxide, tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide, tetrabutyl ammonium hydroxide, tetrapentyl ammonium hydroxide, tetrahexyl ammonium hydroxide, tetraheptyl ammonium hydroxide, tetraoctyl ammonium hydroxide, tetranonyl ammonium hydroxide, and/or tetretecyl ammonium hydroxide.X1 = halo.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 8 Compounds (I) are specially claimed, e.g. 3,6-di((1,1'-biphenyl)-4-yl)-2-hexyl-2,5-dihydropyrrolo(3,4-c)pyrrole-1,4-dione of formula (Ia), 3,6-di((1,1'-biphenyl)-4-yl)-2-decyl-2,5-dihydropyrrolo(3,4-c)pyrrole-1,4-dione, 3,6-di((1,1'-biphenyl)-4-yl)-2-tetradecyl-2,5-dihydropyrrolo(3,4-c)pyrrole-1,4-dione, 3,6-di((1,1'-biphenyl)-4-yl)-2-(4-(tert-butyl)benzyl)-2,5-dihydropyrrolo(3,4-c)pyrrole-1,4-dione, and 3,6-bis(4-(tert-butyl)phenyl)-2-hexyl-2,5-dihydropyrrolo(3,4-c)pyrrole-1,4-dione.
   EXAMPLE - 700 ml N,N'-dimethylformamide was added to 1 l three-neck round bottom flask provided with an oil bath, a reflux condenser, a stirrer and a thermometer, 20 g pigment red-264 and 9.6 g sodium tert-butoxide solution were added, and the mixture was heated to 60 degrees C with stirring. 7.65 ml 1-bromohexane was added when the temperature reached 60 degrees C, stirring was continued for 6 hours, and reaction was traced using thin-layer chromatography using 2:8 ethyl acetate and hexane as developing solvent. The reaction solution was filtered and the filtrate was concentrated under reduced pressure to dryness. The dried mixture was dissolved by using 150 ml methylene chloride, and recrystallized with 1.5 l hexane to obtain solid by filtration, and obtained solid was washed several times with hexane and dried in a vacuum oven to obtain 11.2 g 3,6-di((1,1'-biphenyl)-4-yl)-2-hexyl-2,5-dihydropyrrolo(3,4-c)pyrrole-1,4-dione with 47% yield.

   USE - As system for mineralizing distilled water.
   ADVANTAGE - The system mineralizes distilled water with replenishable mineral concentrate solutions, where the consumer can have the choice of taking hot water, temperate water, or cooled water and can adopt the cooling and heating units that can operate continuously, so that cold or hot water is instantly available.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - System (50) for mineralizing distilled water comprises (a) an inlet for distilled water input, where the water input with substantially reduced mineral content; (b) a splitter (54) operationally connected to the inlet, configured for splitting the input into a primary portion and secondary portion; (c) at least one primary conduit operationally connected to the splitter, configured for conveying the primary portion of the distilled water input; (d) at least one secondary conduit configured for conveying the secondary portion of the distilled water input; (e) a carbonator (62) comprising a carbon dioxide supply module, where the carbonator is operationally connected to the at least one secondary conduit, configured for dissolving carbon dioxide in the secondary portion of the distilled water input; (f) at least one first column, comprising an interior volume, operationally connected to the carbonator; (g) at least one second column, comprising an interior volume, operationally connected to the carbonator; (h) a first mineral matrix filling the interior volume of the at least one first column, the mineral matrix is configured for been infiltrated by water of the secondary portion of the distilled water input and generating a first mineralizing concentrate solution; (i) a second mineral matrix filling the interior volume of the at least one second column, the mineral matrix is configured for been infiltrated by water of the secondary portion of the distilled water input and generating a second mineralizing concentrate solution; (j) a first concentrate stock tank, operationally connected to the at least one first column, configured for collecting a stock of the first mineralizing concentrate solution; (k) a second concentrate stock tank, operationally connected to the at least one second column, configured collecting a stock of the second mineralizing concentrate solution; (I) a first mixing module (70A) operationally connected to the first concentrate stock tank, the first mixing module is configured for combining a predefined amount of the first mineralizing concentrate solution with the primary portion of the distilled water input in the first conduit; (m) a second mixing module operationally connected to the second concentrate stock tank, the second mixing module is configured for combining a predefined amount of the second mineralizing concentrate solution with the primary portion of the distilled water input in the first conduit; and (n) a dispensing module (72) operationally connected to the first and the second mixing modules, configured for dispensing a mineralized water mixture to a consumer.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process of mineralizing distilled water.
