PN WO2021110806-A1; AU2020395887-A1; CN114746170-A; KR2022104736-A; EP4069411-A1; IN202217033699-A
TI Reactor system for producing product gas            comprising carbon monoxide, comprises supply of            feedstock comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen,            structured catalyst, pressure shell, heat insulation            layer, conductors and outlet.
AU MORTENSEN P M
   AASBERG-PETERSEN K
   HULTQVIST M
   KLEIN R
   LARSEN K E
   CASPEREMIL L
   AASBERG P K
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   TOPSCHE AS (TOPS-C)
   TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 202163149U
AB    NOVELTY - A reactor system (100) carries out reverse                water gas shift reaction to produce a product gas                comprising carbon monoxide, and comprises a supply                of feedstock comprising carbon dioxide and                hydrogen, a structured catalyst (10) comprising a                macroscopic structure (5) of an electroconductive                material and a catalytically active material                capable of catalyzing both the reverse water gas                shift reaction and a methanation reaction, and                arranged for being operated under such temperature                and pressure that both the reverse water gas                reaction and the methanation reaction take place, a                pressure shell (20) housing structured catalyst,                and comprising an inlet for letting in feedstock                and an outlet for letting out product gas, a heat                insulation layer (30) between structured catalyst                and pressure shell, at least two conductors (40)                and (40') electrically connected to catalyst and to                an electrical power supply placed outside pressure                shell, and an outlet for product gas containing                carbon monoxide.
   USE - Reactor system for reverse water gas shift                reaction for producing product gas comprising                carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor system is compact, safe and                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A reactor system carries out reverse water gas                shift reaction to produce a product gas comprising                carbon monoxide, and comprises a supply of                feedstock comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen, a                structured catalyst comprising a macroscopic                structure of an electroconductive material and a                catalytically active material capable of catalyzing                both the reverse water gas shift reaction and a                methanation reaction, and arranged for being                operated under such temperature and pressure that                both the reverse water gas reaction and the                methanation reaction take place, a pressure shell                housing structured catalyst, and comprising an                inlet for letting in feedstock and an outlet for                letting out product gas, such that feedstock enters                catalyst in one end and product gas exits catalyst                from other end of catalyst, a heat insulation layer                between structured catalyst and pressure shell, at                least two conductors electrically connected to                catalyst and to an electrical power supply placed                outside pressure shell, and an outlet for product                gas containing carbon monoxide. The electrical                power supply is dimensioned to heat at least                portion of catalyst to a temperature of at least                500 degrees C by passing an electrical current                through macroscopic structure. The conductors are                connected to the structured catalyst at a position                on the catalyst closer to one end of catalyst than                to other end of catalyst. The catalyst is                constructed to direct an electrical current to run                from one conductor to the other end of the catalyst                and return to other end of conductors. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) process for converting feedstock                comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen, which                involves supplying said pressurized feedstock to                pressure shell, allowing the feedstock to undergo a                reverse water gas shift reaction over the                structured catalyst, discharging product gas from                pressure shell; and
   (2) method for rapidly switching reverse water                gas shift reaction of feedstock comprising carbon                monoxide in reactor system from a steady-state                reaction condition (A) to a steady-state reaction                condition (B) or vice-versa. The steady-state                reaction condition (A) involves supplying feedstock                to the reactor system in a first total flow,                supplying a first electrical power via electrical                conductors, and heating the catalyst to temperature                (T1) at which feedstock is converted to a first                product gas. The steady-state reaction condition                (B) involves supplying feedstock to the reactor                system in a second total flow, supplying a second                electrical power via electrical conductors, heating                at least portion of the structured catalyst to a                temperature (T2) at which feedstock is converted to                product gas. The second electrical power is higher                than first electrical power, and second total flow                is higher than first total flow.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                the reactor system.
   Macroscopic structure (5)
   Structured catalyst (10)
   Pressure shell (20)
   Insulating material (30)
   Conductors (40,40')
   Reactor system (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The macroscopic                  structure supports a ceramic coating, which                  partially supports a catalytically active                  material in the most downstream portion of the                  structured catalyst, such as from 10-100%,                  preferably 50-100% of the extension along the                  length of the structured catalyst from one end to                  other end. The structured catalyst has a reaction                  zone (a) provided closest to the one end of                  catalyst, and having overall exothermic reaction,                  and a reaction zone (b) provided closest to the                  other end of catalyst, and having overall                  endothermic reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The zone (a) has an                  extension of 5-60% of the length of the                  structured catalyst from one end to other end.                  The concentration of methane is higher in the                  partly catalyzed feedstock inside at least a                  portion of the structured catalyst than in the                  feedstock and in the product gas. The temperature                  of the structured catalyst is continuously                  increasing from one end to other end of the                  structured catalyst. The electrical power supply                  is dimensioned to heat at least portion of                  structured catalyst to 500 degrees C or more,                  preferably 1000-1100 degrees C. The pressure of                  the structured catalyst is 2-30 bar, preferably                  8-12 bar. The product gas containing synthesis                  gas is fed to an upgrading unit and separated                  into an upgraded synthesis gas stream and one or                  more off-gas stream. The ratio of total gas feed                  flow in reaction condition (A) to reaction                  condition (B) is 1:10 or more.

PT P
PN CN112608774-A
TI Method for upgrading quality of natural gas comprises desulfurizing and decarbonizing raw natural gas by alcohol amine method, passing desulfurization product gas through dehydration unit and dehydrating using triethylene glycol.
AU ZHU H
   ZHOU Z
   ZHU X
AE SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202138244E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for upgrading quality of natural gas comprises (i) desulfurizing and decarbonizing the raw natural gas by alcohol amine method to obtain semi-desulfurized and decarbonized gas and residual gas, and removing the hydrogen sulfide in the obtained semi-desulfurized and decarbonized gas to less than 200 mg/m3; (ii) passing the semi-desulfurized and decarbonized gas to the carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide hydrolysis reaction unit to obtain carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide semi-product gas; (iii) passing the de-carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide semi-product gas to the fine desulfurization unit, leading to the fine desulfurization unit after temperature adjustment and separation of free liquid in the fine desulfurization unit to obtain the desulfurization product gas; and (iv) passing the desulfurization product gas through the dehydration unit and using triethylene glycol for dehydration by the dehydration unit to obtain product gas.
   USE - The method is useful for upgrading quality of natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method upgrades the natural gas quality index of the desulfurization device, need to add carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide hydrolysis and fine desulfurization unit, and can be sold as a high-value by-product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for upgrading quality of natural gas comprises (i) desulfurizing and decarbonizing the raw natural gas by alcohol amine method to obtain semi-desulfurized and decarbonized gas and residual gas, and removing the hydrogen sulfide in the obtained semi-desulfurized and decarbonized gas to less than 200 mg/m3, preferably less than 50 mg/m3 and carbon dioxide content less than 4%, preferably less than 2%; (ii) passing the semi-desulfurized and decarbonized gas to the carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide hydrolysis reaction unit to obtain carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide semi-product gas, humidifying the carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide hydrolysis reaction unit and adjusting by spraying saturated steam or deoxygenated water by bubbling, and separating the free liquid through a separation tank and passing to carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide hydrolysis reactor to generate carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide semi-product gas; (iii) passing the de-carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide semi-product gas to the fine desulfurization unit, leading to the fine desulfurization unit after temperature adjustment and separation of free liquid in the fine desulfurization unit to obtain the desulfurization product gas, where the fine desulfurization device includes a washing device of a strong alkali aqueous solution; and (iv) passing the desulfurization product gas through the dehydration unit and using triethylene glycol for dehydration by the dehydration unit to obtain product gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for upgrading the quality of natural gas, comprising an alcohol amine method desulfurization and decarburization unit, a carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide hydrolysis reaction unit, a fine desulfurization unit, a dehydration unit, a sulfur recovery or injection unit and a sulfur dioxide refining unit. The alcohol amine desulfurization /decarburization unit is used to take the raw natural gas from the upstream filter at 30-40 degrees C, after selectively desulfurized by the alcohol amine desulfurization and decarburization device, the obtained semi-desulfurized and decarbonized gas is mixed with hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon dioxide residual gas. The residual gas containing hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon dioxide is recycled to the sulfur recycling or re-injecting unit to be recycled. The carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide hydrolysis reaction unit is used to pass the semi-desulfurized and decarbonized gas through a hydrolysis reactor to hydrolyze almost all carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide to hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide to obtain the de-carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide semi-product gas. The fine desulfurization unit is used to pass the de-carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide semi-product gas into the alkali to generate desulfurization product gas and residual liquid. The residual liquid is sent to the sulfur dioxide refining unit for alkali regeneration, and the regenerated alkali is separated from the disulfide, dissolved oxygen, desalted, filtered, and sent to the fine desulfurization device of the fine desulfurization unit. The dehydration unit is used for dehydrating the desulfurized product gas to obtain product gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the hydrolysis reaction temperature of the carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide hydrolysis reactor is 60-200 degrees C, preferably 110-140 degrees C and the gas saturation humidity corresponding to the three ranges of the hydrolysis reaction temperature are respectively 45-160 degrees C, 70-140 degrees C and 100-130 degrees C. The sulfide-containing alkali produced by the carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide hydrolysis reaction unit undergoes oxidation regeneration and disulfide separation and the obtained fresh alkali is sent to the fine desulfurization device for recycling after removal of dissolved oxygen, salt, and filtration. In the step (iii), the temperature adjustment range of carbonyl sulfide/carbon disulfide semi-product gas is 40-50 degrees C and the temperature adjustment method comprises heat exchange, air cooling or water cooling of the gas at the inlet and outlet of the hydrolysis reactor. The alkali solution in the washing device of the strong alkali aqueous solution contains 50-300 ppmv of the alkali solution regeneration catalyst, the residue output from the washing device of the strong alkali aqueous solution is passed to the oxidation tower to realize the regeneration of the alkali and the regenerated alkali is separated from the disulfide, dissolved oxygen, desalinated, filtered, and sent to the fine desulfurization device. In the step (i), the residual gas containing hydrogen sulfide or/and carbon dioxide generated by the alcohol amine desulfurization and decarbonization unit is recovered in the form of sulfur through a sulfur recovery device or processed and re-injected into the ground. The disulfide separated by the oxidation tower is further purified and refined by C6-11 heavy hydrocarbons, preferably heavy naphtha. Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the alkali used in the washing device of the strong alkali aqueous solution is sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide aqueous solution, and the mass fraction of the alkali is 10-30%. The regeneration catalyst is composed of cobalt metal phthalocyanine or cobalt polyphthalocyanine, monoethanolamine, ammonium chloride, urea, cobalt polybutanethiol, and morpholine.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PT P
PN CN111394120-A; CN111394120-B
TI Recovering light hydrocarbon, comprises e.g. conveying hot low oil and/or cold low oil from hydrocracking unit to debutanizer for separation, conveying liquid phase at bottom of debutanizer to fractionation tower, and separating diesel.
AU HUANG M
   WU D
   HAO S
   YAO A
   LIU K
AE SINOPEC ENG INC (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2020666890
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering light hydrocarbon, comprises e.g. (i) conveying hot low oil and/or cold low oil from the hydrocracking unit to a debutanizer for separation, conveying the liquid phase at the bottom of the debutanizer to the fractionation tower for further separation of naphtha, diesel and tail oil, condensing the gas phase at the top of the tower by the condenser, and sending to a reflux tank, where the gas phase at the top of the reflux tank and the liquid phase at the bottom of the tank are removed, (ii) compressing gas phase at the top of the tank top separated from the reflux tank, cooling in the rich gas desulfurization tower to remove hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide using lean amine liquid as the absorbent, and conveying to the gas-liquid separation tank, and (iii) sending liquid phase at the bottom of the tank bottom separated from the reflux tank to the gas-liquid separation tank, and removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in the liquid hydrocarbon desulfurization tower.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering light hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; has mild operating conditions, small cold energy consumption; requires less equipment; and achieves high-efficiency separation and recovery of ethane, propane and carbon four components, where the recovery rate of ethane is over 97 wt.%, the recovery rate of propane is over 98 wt.%, the methane content in the recovered ethane is not greater than 1 vol.%, and the ethane content in the recovered propane is not greater than 300 ppmv.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering light hydrocarbon, comprises (i) conveying hot low oil and/or cold low oil from the hydrocracking unit to a debutanizer for separation, conveying the liquid phase at the bottom of the debutanizer to the fractionation tower for further separation of naphtha, diesel and tail oil, condensing the gas phase at the top of the tower by the condenser, and sending to a reflux tank, where the gas phase at the top of the reflux tank and the liquid phase at the bottom of the tank are separately removed, (ii) compressing gas phase at the top of the tank top separated from the reflux tank, cooling in the rich gas desulfurization tower to remove hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide using lean amine liquid as the absorbent, and conveying to the gas-liquid separation tank, (iii) sending liquid phase at the bottom of the tank bottom separated from the reflux tank to the gas-liquid separation tank, and removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in the liquid hydrocarbon desulfurization tower, (iv) mixing gas-phase light hydrocarbons and liquid-phase light hydrocarbons that have been removed again in the gas-liquid separation tank, performing pre-absorption and gas-liquid equilibrium, and conveying the separated gas and liquid phases to the absorption tower and separated respectively unit, (v) using mixed C4 as the absorbent to absorb C2 and components above C2 in the gas phase from the top of the gas-liquid separation tank, where the part of the methane is absorbed together, sending the gas phase at the top of the absorption tower to the downstream device, and cooling the bottom liquid returning to the gas-liquid separation tank, and (vi) separating liquid phase from the bottom of the gas-liquid separation tank into C2 and C3 through the demethanizer, deethanizer, depropanizer I and optional depropanizer II and isobutane tower in the separation unit and C4 component, where the separated C4 component is conveyed to the absorption tower as a mixed C4 absorbent, and the remaining is extracted as a C4 product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for light hydrocarbon recovery device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the light hydrocarbon recovery device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (vii) separating naphtha as an absorbent to recover C4 in the gas phase from the top of the absorption tower and for components above C4, producing the gas phase at the top of the absorbent recovery tower as dry gas, and returning the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower to the debutanizer. In the step (vi), the separation comprises (via) removing the liquid phase from the bottom of the gas-liquid separation tank removes methane in the demethanizer, and at the same time removes a small part of C2 and above components, and demethanizes the gas phase at the top of the tower is cooled and returned to the gas-liquid separation tank, and the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower is sent to the deethanizer, ethane removal: the liquid phase from the bottom of the demethanizer is separated from the C2 component in the deethanizer, the separated top gas phase is extracted as an ethane product, and the liquid components above C3 and C3 are sent to the demethanizer, propane tower I, depropanizing: the liquid phase components from the bottom of the de-ethanizer are further separated in the de-propanizer I, the separated propane is extracted from the upper part of the de-propanizer I, and at least a part of the bottom components is used as a mixed C4 absorbent send to the absorption tower, and the rest is extracted as C4 product, (vib) the steps of separation include: demethanization: the liquid phase from the bottom of the gas-liquid separation tank removes methane in the demethanizer, and at the same time removes a small part of C2 and above components, and demethanizes The gas phase at the top of the tower is cooled and returned to the gas-liquid separation tank, and the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower is sent to the isobutane removal tower, de-isobutane: the liquid phase from the bottom of the demethanizer is separated from the components below C3 and C3 in the de-isobutane tower, and the separated top gas phase is sent to the deethanizer, and at least a part of the bottom liquid phase is used for mixing the C4 absorbent is sent to the absorption tower, and the rest is extracted as n-butane product, ethane removal: the liquid phase from the bottom of the isobutane removal tower separates C2 components in the deethanization tower, the separated top gas phase is extracted as an ethane product, and the liquid phase components at the bottom of the tower are sent to the depropanization tower I, depropanizing: the liquid phase components from the bottom of the de-ethanizer are further separated in the de-propanizer I, the separated propane product is extracted from the top of the de-propanizer I, and the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower is extracted as an isobutane product, (vic) the separation steps include: demethanization: the liquid phase from the bottom of the gas-liquid separation tank removes methane in the demethanizer, and at the same time removes a small part of C2 and above components, and demethanizes the gas phase at the top of the tower is cooled and returned to the gas-liquid separation tank, and the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower is sent to the de-propanizer I, depropanizing: the liquid phase components from the bottom of the demethanizer are separated in the depropanizer I, and the separated C3 and C3 components are extracted from the top of the depropanizer I and sent to the deethanizer, at least a part of the bottom components is sent to the absorption tower as a mixed C4 absorbent, and the rest is extracted as a C4 product, De-ethanization: the gas phase from the upper part of the de-propanizer I is further separated in the de-ethanizer, the separated top gas phase is extracted as an ethane product, and the bottom liquid phase is extracted as a propane product, and (vid) the separation steps sequentially include: demethanization: the liquid phase from the bottom of the gas-liquid separation tank removes methane in the demethanizer, and at the same time removes a small amount of C2 and above components, and demethanizes the gas phase at the top of the tower is cooled and returned to the gas-liquid separation tank, and the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower is sent to the de-propanizer I, depropanization I: the liquid phase components from the bottom of the demethanizer are separated in the depropanizer I, and the separated top gas phase is sent to the deethanizer, and at least a part of the liquid phase in the bottom of the tower is used as a mixed C4 absorbent conveyed to the absorption tower, and the rest is extracted as C4 products, de-ethanization: the gas phase from the top of the de-propanizer I is separated in the de-ethanizer, the separated top gas phase is extracted as an ethane product, and the bottom liquid phase is sent to the de-propanizer II for further separation, depropanization II: The liquid phase from the bottom of the de-ethanizer is further separated in the de-propanizer II, the separated top gas phase is extracted as a propane product, and at least a part of the bottom liquid phase is sent to the mixed C4 absorbent the rest of the absorption tower is extracted as C4 product.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The operating temperature of the debutanizer is 50-80 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 1-1.6 MPaG. The temperature of the reflux tank at the top of the debutanizer is 35-45 degrees C. The initial boiling point of the naphtha absorbent is 25-45 degrees C, and the dry point is 165-200 degrees C. The operating temperature of the rich gas desulfurization tower is 35-45 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 0.9-1.5 MPaG. The operating temperature of the liquid hydrocarbon desulfurization tower is 35-45 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 2.55-3.15 MPaG. The operating temperature of the gas-liquid separation tank is 5-25 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 2.15-2.75 MPaG. The operating pressure of the absorption tower is 2.1-2.7 MPaG, and the operating temperature is 5-25 degrees C. The absorbent used in the absorption tower does not require to-be introduced from outside the system. The operating temperature of the top of the demethanizer is 15-40 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 2.25-2.85 MPaG. The operating temperature of the top of the deethanizer is 10-40 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 2.6-3.2 MPaG. The operating temperature of the top of the depropanizer I is 45-70 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 1.4-2 MPaG. The operating temperature of the top of the de-propanizer II is 45-65 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 1.5-1.9 MPaG. The operating temperature of the top of the isobutane removal tower is 45-65 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 0.4-1 MPaG.

PN CN108424935-A
TI Preparing high-yield dairy cow blastomere reconstituted embryo, by placing cow ovary in sterile physiological saline, culturing, treating frozen semen by liquid floating method, processing sperm and performing cell fusion.
AU ZHANG L
   LIN Z
   ZHEGN X
   BI Y
   LIU X
   XIAO H
   HUA Z
   HUA W
   CAO H
AE HUBEI ACAD AGRIC SCI ANIMAL HUSBANDRY (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 201866713M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high-yield dairy cow blastomere reconstituted embryo, involves placing cow ovary in sterile physiological saline, storing cow ovary in laboratory, washing the cells in sterile saline, pre-heating in vitro maturation culture medium, placing the medium in carbon dioxide incubator, culturing the medium, treating the cattle frozen semen by liquid floating method, processing sperm, preparing in vitro fertilized embryos of high-yield dairy cows, constructing high-yield dairy cow embryo blastomere reconstructed embryos, digesting in vitro fertilized embryos, removing the genetic material containing the first polar portion, transferring the oocyte from which the first polar body is removed to an operational droplet placed with blastomere, washing the prepared reconstituted embryo using fusion solution, transferring the reconstituted embryo to fusion tank, performing cell fusion using BTX-2001 fusion device, and washing the fused embryos with CRlaa.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-yield dairy cow blastomere reconstituted embryo (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces damage to embryo, and improves survival rate of reconstructed embryo.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing high-yield dairy cow blastomere reconstituted embryo, involves placing the cow ovary in sterile physiological saline of 25 degrees C supplemented with double antibody at slaughterhouse, storing the cow ovary in laboratory for 7 hours, washing the cells in sterile saline of 30-35 degrees C for twice, obtaining cumulus-oocyte complex (COCs) by 10 ml disposable syringe aspiration method in water bath of 39 degrees C, washing COCs with 3 times Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline (DPBS) buffer containing 5% fetal bovine serum (FBS), pre-heating the mixture in water bath of 39 degrees C for 2 hours, pre-heating in vitro maturation culture medium at incubator of 39 degrees C for 3 times, placing the medium in carbon dioxide incubator for in vitro maturation culture, culturing the medium in five-well plate at concentration of 100-150 pieces/500 mu l, the culture condition is 39 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, and culture time is 22-24 hours, and in vitro maturation medium comprises TCM-199, 10% FBS and 10 mu l/ml double antibody (100 IU/ml) and 10 IU/ml human chorionic gonadotropin (HMG) and 1 mu l/ml estradiol (E2), treating the cattle frozen semen by liquid floating method, processing sperm, preparing in vitro fertilized embryos of high-yield dairy cows with 32 cell stage, where the formulation of solution comprises 6.55 mg/ml sodium chloride, 0.3 mg/ml potassium chloride, 0.249 mg/ml calcium chloride, 0.0497 mg/ml magnesium chloride, 0.0869 mg/ml sodium dihydrogen phosphate, 3.104 mg/ml sodium hydrogencarbonate, 2.5 mg/ml glucose, 0.055 mg/ml sodium pyruvate, 0.065 mg/ml penicillin and 0.05 mg/ml streptomycin sulfate, constructing high-yield dairy cow embryo blastomere reconstructed embryos using Piezo operating system, digesting in vitro fertilized embryos to the 32-cell stage with 0.25% pronase to obtain single blastomere, where the fixed needle has inner diameter of 40 mu m and an outer diameter of 180 mu m, breaking zona pellucida and the cell membrane, removing the genetic material containing the first polar portion, transferring the oocyte from which the first polar body is removed to an operational droplet placed with blastomere, washing the prepared reconstituted embryo using 3 times fusion solution, transferring the reconstituted embryo to fusion tank, performing cell fusion using BTX-2001 fusion device, where the fusion parameter is 110V 30 mu s 2DC, washing the fused embryos with CRlaa for 3 times, where the culture conditions include 39 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity for 24 hours, and culture time is 7 days, where the culture medium CRlaa comprises 114.7 mM sodium chloride, 3.1 mM potassium chloride, 26.2 mM sodium bicarbonate, 0.4 mM sodium pyruvate, 5 mM lactic acid, 3 mg/ml bovine serum albumin and 1 mM L-glutamine.

PN WO2022150601-A1
TI Producing syngas by reacting hydrocarbon fuel and            enriched-oxygen containing feed gas in internal            combustion engine reactor, where feed gas comprises            carbon dioxide diluent and enriched-oxygen.
AU PARVATHIKAR S
   CARPENTER J R
AE RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
GA 202290464G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing syngas involves reacting                a hydrocarbon fuel and enriched-oxygen containing                feed gas in internal combustion engine reactor,                where the feed gas comprises 5-50 mol.% carbon                dioxide diluent and 25-95 mol.% enriched-oxygen to                produce the syngas.
   USE - The method is used for producing syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method results in low compression costs,                small reactor volumes, and higher efficiencies in                separations resulting in high carbon                conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a system for the conversion of biogas to                methanol, dimethyl ether (DME) and/or hydrocarbons;                and
   (2) a system for the conversion of natural gas                to synthetic crude oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The enriched-oxygen feed                  gas is obtained by vacuum pressure swing                  adsorption, pressure swing adsorption, cryogenic                  separation, permeable membrane gas separation, or                  their combinations. The internal combustion                  engine reactor is run under initially under a                  stoichiometric to lean fuel-oxygen ratio and then                  shifted to a rich fuel-oxygen ratio so as to                  maximize the production of syngas. The carbon                  dioxide from the internal combustion engine                  reactor is initially ran in a full oxidation mode                  so as to produce carbon dioxide, the carbon                  dioxide is separated, and is added to the feed                  gas stream. The syngas is conditioned by a                  water-gas shift reactor to convert excess carbon                  dioxide to carbon monoxide, to adjust the                  temperature, to adjust the pressure, to separate                  the excess carbon dioxide, or their combinations.                  The syngas is conditioned by separating the                  excess carbon dioxide or diluent by a membrane, a                  pressure swing adsorber, a solvent-based                  separation system, or their combinations. The                  syngas is converted to methanol in a methanol                  synthesis unit. The methanol is subsequently                  converted to DME in a two-step DME synthesis                  unit. The syngas is directly converted to DME in                  a one-step DME synthesis unit. The syngas is                  converted to a lower olefin, a liquid fuel, or an                  aromatic in a hydrocarbon synthesis unit. The                  syngas is converted to synthetic crude oil in a                  Fischer Tropsch (FT) reactor. Preferred                  Components: The hydrocarbon fuel is a gaseous                  hydrocarbon fuel. The gaseous hydrocarbon fuel is                  natural gas, and a biogas. The gaseous                  hydrocarbon fuel is from a gas well, or an                  associated gas from an oil well. The gaseous                  hydrocarbon fuel is a fuel mixture comprising at                  least a portion of the carbon dioxide diluent.                  The fuel mixture is a biogas from a landfill or a                  biogas from anaerobic digestion. The carbon                  dioxide diluent is obtained by separation from                  the syngas downstream from the internal                  combustion engine reactor prior to a syngas                  processing step. The carbon dioxide diluent is                  obtained from an output of a syngas processing                  step. The carbon dioxide diluent is obtained from                  a separate source. The feed gas further comprises                  hydrogen and hydrocarbons added to increase the                  flame speed. The hydrogen and hydrocarbons added                  is obtained from a syngas conversion                  system.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

   CHEN C
   LI H
   LIU Z
   WANG F
   ZHANG X
   WU W
   LI J
   ZHANG T
   DI X
   CUI D
   MA Y
AE SHANDONG HANBO YUZHOU NEW MATERIAL CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202226778S
AB    NOVELTY - A highly efficient carbon-absorbing mineralized aerated concrete comprises 10-1000 pts. wt. highly efficient carbonized material, 10-1000 pts. wt. cement, 0-1000 pts. wt. additive, 10- 1000 pts. wt. thickening material, 10-2000 pts. wt. water, 1-100 pts. wt. admixture and 10-200 pts. wt. carbon dioxide bubble foam or common foam.
   USE - Highly efficient carbon-absorbing mineralized aerated concrete is used for building applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-efficiency carbon absorbing and mineralizing aerated concrete has high carbon absorption efficiency and mechanical property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the concrete.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The highly efficient carbonized material is gamma-type dicalcium silicate, steel slag and/or magnesite. The mass ratio of highly efficient carbonized material and thickening material is 1:(0.01-0.5), preferably 1:(0.03-0.3). The usage amount of the admixture is 1-5 wt.%, preferably 2-4 wt.% based on total amount of the carbonized material, additive, cement and thickening material. The usage amount of water is 25-40 wt.% based on total amount of the carbonized material, additive, cement and thickening material. The usage amount of carbon dioxide bubble foam or common foam is 10-25 wt.% based on total amount of the carbonized material, additive, cement and thickening material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The cement is ordinary Portland cement and/or sulfoaluminate cement. The mass ratio of highly efficient carbonized material and cement is 1:(0.1-10), preferably 1:(0.5-2).

PN CN113277923-A; CN113277923-B
TI Preparation of p-xylene with coproduction of low-carbon olefin, involves conversion reaction using toluene, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen in fixed, fluidized or moving bed, under action of catalyst comprising bifunctional catalyst comprising metal oxide and modified zeolite molecular sieve.
AU PAN X
   MIAO D
   BAO X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2021997401
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of p-xylene with coproduction of low-carbon olefin, involves carrying out conversion reaction using toluene, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen in a fixed bed, fluidized bed, or moving bed, under the action of a catalyst comprising a bifunctional catalyst comprising a metal oxide as active component, and modified zeolite molecular sieve.
   USE - Preparation of p-xylene with coproduction of low-carbon olefin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high product yield, a p-xylene selectivity of 50-99%, and a low-carbon olefin selectivity of 50-85%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compounds: The metal oxide is zinc oxide, indium oxide, zirconia, zirconium-chromium oxide of formula: ZnCrxO(1+1.5x), zinc-aluminum oxide of formula: ZnAlxO(1+1.5x), zinc-chromium-aluminum oxide of formula: ZnCrxAlyO(1+1.5x+1.5y), zinc-zirconium oxide of formula: ZnZrxO(1+2x), manganese-indium oxide of formula: MnInxO(1+1.5x), zinc-gallium oxide of formula: ZnGaxO(1+1.5x), zinc-indium oxide of formula: ZnInxO(2+1.5x), cerium-zirconium oxide of formula: CeZrxO(2+2x), indium-aluminum-manganese oxide of formula: InyAlzMnO(1+1.5y+1.5z), and/or indium-gallium-manganese oxide of formula: InyGazMnO(1+1.5y+1.5z). The zeolite molecular sieve is ZSM-5, ZSM-22, ZSM-35, SAPO-11, MCM-22, ZSM-11 and/or MCM-49. The zeolite molecular sieve is modified using silicon, phosphorus, magnesium, boron, calcium, strontium, barium, titanium, cerium, lanthanum, vanadium, zirconium and/or tungsten.x = 1-3.5;y = 0.1-10;andz = 0.1-10.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The active component is impregnated in component (II), or the active component and molecular sieve are physically mixed, or the active component and molecular sieve are stacked in bed layer such that the active component bed layer is stacked above the molecular sieve bed layer. The mass ratio of active component and molecular sieve is 0.2-5:1. The preparation is carried out at a hydrogen/carbon dioxide ratio of 0.2-5, preferably 1-4, a carbon dioxide and hydrogen pressure of 0.5-10 MPa, preferably 1-8 MPa, a carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas airspeed of 300-50000 ml/g catalyst/hour, preferably 5000-30000 ml/g catalyst/hour, a toluene liquid hourly space velocity of 0.1-10 hour-1, preferably 0.3-3 hour-1, and a temperature of 300-600 degrees C, preferably 350-550 degrees C.

PN CN112745210-A; CN112745210-B
TI Preparing alpha, beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid compound comprises e.g. heating hydrogen silane and copper catalyst containing mixture in carbon dioxide atmosphere, and reacting with alkyne and nickel catalyst containing raw materials.
AU LI C
   WANG M
   LU S
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2021503815
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid compound comprises (i) heating hydrogen silane and copper catalyst containing mixture in carbon dioxide atmosphere to react to obtain reaction system I, (ii) adding alkyne and nickel catalyst containing raw materials into the reaction system I, and heating to react to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes simple, easily available, cheap and stable raw materials, and common and easily available and stable catalysts; has mild reaction conditions, simple post-treatment, and high product yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the molar ratio between copper catalyst, carbon dioxide and hydrogen silane is 1:50-20000:125-1000; the number of moles of the copper catalyst is based on the number of moles of the copper element contained in the copper catalyst; the number of moles of carbon dioxide is calculated as the number of moles of carbon dioxide itself; the number of moles of the silane compound is based on the number of moles of the silane compound itself; the heating reaction temperature is 40-100 degrees C; and the heating reaction time is 20 minutes to 6 hours. The hydrogen silane is at least one of compounds containing Si-H bonds, polymethylhydrogensiloxane, triethoxysilane and/or triphenoxysilane. The copper catalyst is at least one complex formed by a copper salt and bisphosphorus ligand compound. The copper salt is copper inorganic salt, copper carboxylate, copper alkoxide and/or copper ketone salt, preferably copper(II) acetate, copper(II) acetylacetonate and/or copper(II) methoxide. The bisphosphorus ligand compound is of formula (I), preferably 1,2- 1,2-bis(diphenylphosphino)benzene, 1,2-bis(bis(3,5-di(t-butyl)phenyl)phosphino)benzene, 1,2-bis(dicyclohexylphosphino)benzene and/or 1,2-bis(diisopropylphosphino)benzene. The molar ratio between copper salt and double phosphorus ligand compound is 1:1-6. In step (ii), the molar ratio between nickel catalyst and alkyne is 1:10-1000; the number of moles of the nickel catalyst is based on the number of moles of nickel contained in the nickel catalyst; the number of moles of the alkyne is based on the number of moles of the alkyne itself; and the heating reaction temperature is 80-120 degrees C, and the heating reaction time is 6-58 hours. The alkyne is of formula (R5-(C triple bond C)-R6) (II), preferably phenylacetylene, 4-methylphenylacetylene, 3-methylphenylacetylene, 2-methylphenylacetylene, 4-methoxyphenylacetylene, 4-fluorophenylacetylene, 4-chlorophenylacetylene, 4-ethynyl biphenyl, 1-hexyne, 1-phenyl-2 (trimethylsilyl) acetylene, 1-phenyl-1-propyne, 1-phenyl-1-butyne and/or diphenylacetylene. The nickel catalyst is at least one of the complexes formed by nickel salt and bisphosphorus ligand compound. The molar ratio of the nickel salt between bisphosphorus ligand compound is 1:1-5; the nickel salt is nickel inorganic salt, nickel carboxylate, nickel alkoxide, nickel ketone salt and/or bis(cyclooctadiene)nickel(0), preferably nickel acetate, nickel chloride, nickel acetylacetonate and/or bis(cyclooctadiene)nickel(0). The mixture in step (i) includes organic solvent A. The raw material in step (ii) includes organic solvent B. The organic solvent A is 1,4-dioxane, tetrahydrofuran and/or cyclopentyl methyl ether. The organic solvent B is 1,4-dioxane, toluene, acetonitrile, tetrahydrofuran, anisole, dibutyl ether and/or diphenyl ether.R1-R4 = 2-18C alkyl or aryl groups;R5 = aryl (optionally substituted), or 1-4C alkyl;R6 = aryl (optionally substituted with alkyl substituents, groups (asterisk-O-M1) (1), -NO2, -F, -Cl, -CF3, and phenyl groups), 1-4C alkyl or H; andM1 = H or 1-10C alkyl groups.Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises performing separation after the heating reaction is completed. The separation method includes adding an alkali aqueous solution into the reacted system to layer the system, after taking the water layer and adding an acidic aqueous solution for neutralization, performing extraction with an organic solvent E to obtain alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid compound, where the alkali aqueous solution is sodium hydroxide solution, potassium hydroxide solution, sodium carbonate solution, potassium carbonate solution and/or sodium bicarbonate solution; the acidic aqueous solution is hydrochloric acid solution and/or citric acid solution; the organic solvent E is ethyl acetate and/or dichloromethane.

PN CN112741847-A; CN112741847-B
TI Removing heavy metal in Chinese herbal medicine comprises crushing Chinese herbal medicine material or Chinese herbal medicine, adding plant polyphenol fatty acid ester and stirring and extracting raw material.
AU LOU D
   GE J
   NI X
   JIN Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202150366E
AB    NOVELTY - Removing heavy metal in Chinese herbal medicine comprises (i) crushing the Chinese herbal medicine material or the Chinese herbal medicine to 20-300 meshes, adding plant polyphenol fatty acid ester and stirring uniformly to obtain extracting raw material; and (ii) extracting raw material by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method at 50-80 degrees C, carbon dioxide fluid pressure of 13-35 MPa and cosolvent comprising 40-70% ethanol aqueous solution or water.
   USE - The method is useful for removing heavy metal in Chinese herbal medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is non-toxic and harmless; and removes heavy metals in Chinese herbal medicines based on supercritical pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The plant polyphenol fatty acid ester is made of plant polyphenol and fatty acid chloride. The plant polyphenol comprises tea polyphenols, grape seed polyphenols or tannic acid. The fatty acid chloride comprises at least one of caprylic acid chloride (sic), octanoyl chloride, lauroyl chloride, myristic acid chloride, oleic acid chloride or stearic acid chloride. The mass ratio of the plant polyphenol to 1,4-dioxane is 1:10, the mass ratio of the plant polyphenol to pyridine is 1:0.5-1.4 and the mass ratio of the plant polyphenol to the fatty acid chloride substance is 1:1-5. The amount of the cosolvent is 90-110 ml/kg based on the weight of the extracted raw material. The added amount of the plant polyphenol fatty acid ester is 0.1-1 wt.% of the Chinese herbal medicine. Preferred Method: The preparation of plant polyphenol fatty acid ester comprises mixing plant polyphenols with 1,4-dioxane, stirring to dissolve, heating up to 40-60 degrees C, pouring in nitrogen, adding pyridine, adding fatty acid chloride drop wise to complete the reaction, washing the reaction solution for three times with water, separating the aqueous layer is separated, adding a water absorbing agent to remove trace water, evaporating the solvent under vacuum and drying. The method of removing heavy metal in Chinese herbal medicine comprises carrying out extraction for 15-40 minutes. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method is carried out at 50 degrees C, carbon dioxide fluid pressure of 22 MPa and cosolvent and carrying out extraction for 20 minutes.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PN CN111138773-A; WO2021017580-A1
TI Preparing polystyrene foam material involves blending polystyrene and polydimethylsiloxane to obtain polystyrene/polydimethylsiloxane blend, polystyrene/polydimethylsiloxane blend is foamed under supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU ZHAO L
   QIANG W
   HU D
   LIU T
   YAO S
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
   UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2020440634
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polystyrene foam material involves blending polystyrene and polydimethylsiloxane to obtain polystyrene/polydimethylsiloxane blend. The polystyrene/polydimethylsiloxane blend is foamed under supercritical carbon dioxide. The polydimethylsiloxane number weight molecular weight is 2000-17000g/mol.
   USE - Method for preparing polystyrene foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare polystyrene foam material that is safe and environmentally friendly, has simple preparation method, large nucleation density, small cell diameter, large foaming ratio, does not produce bad smells and by-products, meets needs of development in related field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a polystyrene/polydimethylsiloxane blend, which comprises 98.5-99.5 wt.% polystyrene and 0.5-1.5 wt.% polydimethylsiloxane, number-weight molecular weight of the polydimethylsiloxane is 2000-17000g/mol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polystyrene material is Yangzi BASF Petrochemical 158K general-purpose polystyrene. Preferred Conditions: The number-weight molecular weight of the polydimethylsiloxane is 2000-6000g/mol. The content of the polydimethylsiloxane in the polystyrene/polydimethylsiloxane blend is 0.8-1.2 wt.%. Preferred Process: The method involves foaming under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions. The polystyrene/polydimethylsiloxane blend is placed in autoclave and filled with carbon dioxide to replace the air three times. The autoclave is heated to the foaming temperature, after holding for half hour, filled with supercritical carbon dioxide set pressure to saturate polystyrene/polydimethylsiloxane blend and pressure relief to obtain polystyrene foam. The method involves preparing polystyrene foamed material, before the foaming under the supercritical carbon dioxide condition, adding co-blowing agent. The amount of the co-blowing agent added is 5-7 wt.% of supercritical carbon dioxide at the foaming temperature and pressure. The co-blowing agent is absolute ethanol or n-pentane.

AU YANG M Y
   SEOK J Y
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2020207466
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode comprises a planar dendrite metal structure formed on a porous conductive substrate.
   USE - The electrode is useful in device, preferably energy storage device (including battery, preferably lithium ion battery, sodium ion battery, lithium air battery, sodium air battery, lithium metal battery, sodium metal battery, lithium ion hybrid capacitor and sodium ion hybrid capacitor, and supercapacitor), sensor, or electrochemical cell electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, electrolysis of water, and photoelectrochemical decomposition of water (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode has maximized surface area and very flat characteristic, and accelerates oxidation and reduction reactions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The thickness of the branches included in the two-dimensional dendritic metal structure is less than or equal to 100 nm. The aspect ratio of the branches included in the two-dimensional dendritic metal structure is greater than or equal to 10. The substrate has a mesh, foam, or porous foil form. The two-dimensional dendritic metal structures are laminated in parallel across the entire surface of the porous conductive substrate and the pupil. The two-dimensional dendritic metal structure includes copper, tin, silver, gold, lead, nickel, iron, cobalt, molybdenum and/or zinc. The electrode is nanoporous electrode. The electrode further comprises an active material formed on the surface of the two-dimensional dendritic metal structure. The active material includes iron oxide, tin oxide, copper oxide, rudenium oxide, molybdenum oxide, cobalt oxide, germanium oxide, chromium oxide, manganese oxide, nickel oxide, silicon, and/or germanium.

TI Separating oil from shrimp involves putting shrimp and/or crab to grind into slurry, collecting average slurry, adding oil-soluble solvent with amino, stirring, collecting liquid portion, adding water, and introducing carbon dioxide.
AU MAO X
   HUANG W
   XUE C
AE UNIV CHINA OCEAN (UYOC-C)
GA 201963761W
AB    NOVELTY - Separating oil from shrimp involves putting shrimp and/or crab to grind into slurry, collecting average slurry, adding oil-soluble solvent with amino, stirring, collecting the liquid portion, adding water at the same time, introducing carbon dioxide to obtain solution layered upper and lower, and taking the upper solution to obtain oil.
   USE - Method for separating oil from shrimp.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separate oil from shrimp that avoids the energy waste caused by heating and separating, save the production cost and energy, realizes the efficient, and in simple and eco-friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The average slurry fineness is 10-20 meshes. The stirring temperature is 10-40 degrees C for 1-10 hours. The inletting speed of carbon dioxide is 0.02-0.2 L/minute. Preferred Composition: The amount of oil-soluble solvent added is 0.5-100 times the weight of the raw material. Preferred Components: The oil-soluble solvent with an amine group is dimethylcyclohexylamine, N,N-diisopropylethanolamine, N,N-dimethylbenzylamine, N,N-dimethyl-2-phenylethylamine or N,N-dibutylethanolamine is preferably N,N-dibutylethanolamine. The inert gas is nitrogen. Preferred Process: The collecting liquid portion of the method is the filtering or centrifugation.

PN CN109956490-A; CN109956490-B
TI Preparing globular light calcium carbonate by            superfine grinding of heavy calcium as a seed crystal            comprises e.g. adding ultrafine ground heavy calcium as            seed crystal to the calcium hydroxide concentrate, and            introducing carbon dioxide.
AU SHI X
   LIU Y
   FU B
AE DONGSHENG NEW MATERIALS SHANDONG CO LTD (SHDS-C)
GA 201959884D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing globular light calcium carbonate by                superfine grinding of heavy calcium as a seed                crystal comprises adding ultrafine ground heavy                calcium as a seed crystal to the calcium hydroxide                concentrate, and introducing carbon dioxide into                the carbonization reaction to obtain spherical                globular calcium carbonate, adding ultrafine ground                heavy calcium in an amount of 5-20 wt.% of the mass                of the spherical spheroidal calcium                carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing globular                light calcium carbonate by superfine grinding of                heavy calcium as a seed crystal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has an extremely narrow particle                size distribution, and excellent rheological                properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ultrafine ground                  heavy calcium has a particle diameter D90 of                  0.35-0.55 mu m. The ultrafine grinding heavy                  calcium is prepared by further grinding the                  ultrafine grinding heavy calcium puree, the solid                  content of the ultrafine grinding heavy calcium                  puree is 60%-75%, the viscosity is 200-400 cP,                  and the D90 is 1.2-1.5 mu m. The calcium                  hydroxide concentrate has a solid content of                  10-17 wt.%,. The calcium hydroxide concentrate                  has a particle size of not more than 80 meshes.                  Preferred Method: The specific parameters of the                  carbonization reaction are: an initial                  temperature of 35-55 degrees C, a volume                  concentration of carbon dioxide of 20-30%, a gas                  flow rate of 40-80 l/hours, and a stirring speed                  of 400-800 revolutions/minute (rpm). The progress                  of the reaction is controlled by a conductivity                  meter and a pH meter during the carbonization                  reaction. The flower-shaped light calcium                  carbonate has a particle diameter D90 of 4.5-12.5                  mu m, a D50 of 2-8.5 mu m, and a specific surface                  area SSA of 6.5-9.5 m2/g.

TI Preparation of lithium metal which can be stably            placed in air involves taking metal lithium, heating            and melting, mixing with polymer material, stirring            form micro or nano lithium, dispersing, and adding            supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU HUANG J
   ZHANG L
   TANG Y
AE UNIV YANSHAN (UYAN-C)
GA 2018A1915C
AB    NOVELTY - A lithium metal which can be stably placed in                air is prepared by taking metal lithium, heating                and melting, mixing with polymer material according                to material proportion of 1-10:1-10, stirring form                micro or nano lithium, dispersing, adding                supercritical carbon dioxide, ball milling,                releasing pressure, performing conventional                plasma-assisted chemical vapor deposition at 100-15                degrees C for 1-5 minutes onto two-dimensional                layered material, obtaining metallic lithium                powder, and tableting under 1-50 MPa.
   USE - Method for preparing lithium metal which can                be stably placed in air.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and low-cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The lithium metal which                  can be stably placed in air is prepared by taking                  metal lithium, heating and melting at 200-300                  degrees C, mixing with polymer material according                  to material proportion of 1-10:1-10, stirring                  form micro or nano lithium, dispersing at rate of                  5000-10000 revolutions/minute (rpm), adding                  supercritical carbon dioxide, ball milling for                  3-5 hours at rate of 500-800 rpm, releasing                  pressure, performing conventional plasma-assisted                  chemical vapor deposition at 100-15 degrees C for                  1-5 minutes onto two-dimensional layered material                  comprising molybdenum disulfide and tungsten                  disulfide, obtaining metallic lithium powder, and                  tableting under 1-50 MPa.

AE HEFEI XUANSHENG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201895716G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves ultrasonically washing and drying the commercially available clean polyurethane sponge through dichloromethane, ethanol and deionized water.    ADVANTAGE - The method obtains laevo borneol active ingredient with higher content and has higher extraction rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active ingredients comprise laevo borneol. The B.balsamifera raw materials are B.balsamifera leaves and/or twigs. Preferred Method: The steps (ii) and (iii) are carried out simultaneously. The method involves contacting the B.balsamifera raw material with dry carbon dioxide flowing in a circulating manner in a sealed container to obtain the dried product. The method further involves (iv) soaking the dried product using water in a carbon dioxide environment to obtain a soaked product, (v) treating the pretreated material with water vapor or pretreating the soaked product for 10-100 minutes to obtain a coarse powder, (vi) performing purification (A) by separating impurities having a first melting point from the coarse powder to obtain a refined powder, and (vii) performing purification (B) by separating impurities having a second melting point from the refined powder, so as to obtain an extract having a purity of more than 90%. The purification step (A) involves pressing coarse powder, and/or the purification step (B) involves purifying the refined powder using a technique chosen from high-pressure distillation, sublimation and recrystallization. Preferred Condition: The first period of time is within 24 hours.

PN EP3421416-A1
TI Process for heterogeneous photocatalytic oxidation of hydrogen chloride involves passing gas mixture generated using hydrogen chloride and carbon dioxide over photocatalyst and reacting on catalyst surface by action of ultraviolet radiation.
AU RITTERMEIER A
   BURBACH T
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 201900460F
AB    NOVELTY - Process for heterogeneous photocatalytic oxidation of hydrogen chloride by ultraviolet radiation, involves forming a gas mixture generated using hydrogen chloride, carbon dioxide and optionally further secondary constituents, passing the gas mixture over a solid photocatalyst, and initiating reaction on the surface of the catalyst by the action of ultraviolet radiation in a selective energy range.
   USE - Process for heterogeneous photocatalytic oxidation of hydrogen chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, safe and energy-efficient heterogeneous photocatalytic oxidation of hydrogen chloride with improved degree of hydrogen chloride conversion of more than 90%. The method is carried out at low catalyst temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The photocatalyst comprises at least one photoactive material such as a transition metal or transition metal oxide or a semiconductor material. The photo-catalyst comprises 1st, 7th or 8th-subgroup of the periodic table of the elements or metal compounds, preferably copper chloride, ferric chloride, chromium oxide, ruthenium oxychloride, ruthenium chloride, cerium oxide or cerium oxychloride as additional catalytic active component (co-catalyst) metals, and one or more compounds chosen from aluminum-copper oxide, aluminum-gallium-indium nitride (AlxGayIn1-xyN), aluminum-indium nitride (AlxIn1-xN), aluminum nitride, boron oxide, barium titanate, cadmium selenide, cerium oxide, ferric oxide, gallium nitride, mercury sulfate, indium oxide, potassium titanate, lithium-magnesium nitride, sodium tantalate, niobium pentoxide, niobium oxide, lead-hafnium oxide, lead titanate, lead zirconate, antimony chloride, antimony oxide, silicon carbide, tin oxide, strontium titanate, titanium oxide, tungsten oxide, zinc oxide, zinc sulfide and zinc selenide, preferably cerium oxide, tin oxide and titanium oxide as a photoactive material. Preferred Process: Process for heterogeneous photocatalytic oxidation of hydrogen chloride is carried out at an elevated pressure, preferably up to 10 bar at a temperature of at most 250 degrees C, preferably 20-150 degrees C using one or more catalyst chosen from ruthenium oxide, ruthenium chloride and ruthenium oxide chloride is used as a co-catalyst. The photocatalytic hydrogen chloride oxidation is combined with one or more other types of hydrogen chloride oxidation reactions chosen from catalytic gas phase oxidation, thermal gas phase oxidation and electrolysis of hydrogen chloride gas. An ultraviolet LED lamp is used as the ultraviolet light source for ultraviolet radiation. Preferred Properties: The ultraviolet radiation has an energy range of 3.2-4 eV, preferably 3.26-3.94 eV.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. TI Forming an alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid            or a salt involves contacting a transition metal            precursor comprising at least one first ligand,            optionally, at least one second ligand, an olefin,            carbon dioxide and a diluent.
AU IACONO P
   HLAVINKA M L
AE CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
   CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
   IACONO P (IACO-Individual)
   HLAVINKA M L (HLAV-Individual)
   CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
GA 2018A1271G
AB    NOVELTY - Forming an alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic                acid or a salt involves contacting a transition                metal precursor comprising at least one first                ligand, optionally, at least one second ligand, an                olefin, carbon dioxide, a diluent and a sulfur                oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin or a                phosphorus oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic                resin, where the polyaromatic resin further                comprises associated metal cations, to provide a                reaction mixture; and applying reaction conditions                to the reaction mixture suitable to form the                alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid or the                salt.
   USE - Method for forming an alpha,beta-unsaturated                carboxylic acid or a salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction mixture                  comprises a metalalactone compound. The sulfur                  oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin or                  the phosphorus oxoacid anion-substituted                  polyaromatic resin comprises a sulfonated                  polyaromatic resin, a phosphonated polyaromatic                  resin, a sulfinated polyaromatic resin, a                  thiosulfonated, or a thiosulfinated polyaromatic                  resin. The sulfur oxoacid anion-substituted                  polyaromatic resin or the phosphorus oxoacid                  anion-substituted polyaromatic resin comprises a                  sulfonated, a phosphonated, a sulfinated, a                  thiosulfonated, or a thiosulfinated styrene                  polymer or a styrene-divinylbenzene copolymer.                  The sulfur oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic                  resin is an Amberlite (RTM: ion-exchange resins).                  The diluent comprises an ether diluent, a                  carbonyl-containing diluent, an aromatic                  hydrocarbon diluent, a halogenated aromatic                  hydrocarbon diluent, or an alcohol diluent. The                  diluent comprises carbon dioxide and/or the ,                  -unsaturated carboxylic acid or the salt. The                  olefin comprises ethylene, propylene, butene,                  pentene, hexene, heptane, octene, or styrene, and                  where the transition metal precursor comprises a                  Group 8-11 transition metal. The sulfur oxoacid                  anion-substituted polyaromatic resin or the                  phosphorus oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic                  resin of the contacting step is arranged as a                  fixed bed, a bubbling bed, a moving bed, or a                  stirred bed. The sulfur oxoacid anion-substituted                  polyaromatic resin or the phosphorus oxoacid                  anion-substituted polyaromatic resin comprises a                  sulfonated polyaromatic resin, a phosphonated                  polyaromatic resin, a sulfinated polyaromatic                  resin, a thiosulfonated, or a thiosulfinated                  polyaromatic resin. Preferred Conditions: The                  sulfur oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic                  resin or the phosphorus oxoacid anion-substituted                  polyaromatic resin has an average particle size                  from about 0.1 mm to about 1.0 mm. The associated                  metal cations are selected from a Group 1, 2, 12,                  or 13 metal. The sulfur oxoacid anion-substituted                  polyaromatic resin or the phosphorus oxoacid                  anion-substituted polyaromatic resin has an                  average pore diameter from about 50 nm to about                  250 nm. Preferred Process: The method further                  involves contacting or the applying step is/are                  conducted at a pressure from 5 psig (34 KPa) to                  10,000 psig (68,948 kpa) and at a temperature                  from 0-250 degrees C, isolating the                  alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid or the                  salt. Forming an alpha,beta -unsaturated                  carboxylic acid or salt involves contacting a                  metalalactone compound, a diluent and a sulfur                  oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin or a                  phosphorus oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic                  resin; where the polyaromatic resin further                  comprise associated metal cations to provide a                  reaction mixture; and applying reaction                  conditions to the reaction mixture suitable to                  form the alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid                  or the salt.

AB    NOVELTY - The synthesis method of nano-microspheres                involves weighing melamine formaldehyde resin                prepolymer solution and then concentrating, adding                composite surfactant, co-surfactant and oil phase                and then stirring, controlling temperature and then                leaving, adding strong acid and then adjusting pH                value, leaving the mixture and then performing                resinification reaction, adding demulsifier and                then centrifuging, washing using detergent and                freeze-drying.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesis of                nano-microspheres, which are useful for absorption                of carbon dioxide, in adhesive (all claimed) and as                additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables synthesis of                nano-microspheres with high specific surface area                and excellent bonding strength.

The strong                  acid is analytically pure hydrochloric acid or                  formic acid. The detergent is anhydrous                  ethanol.

PN US2018282478-A1
TI Producing poly(tetramethylene ether) diester for catalyst preparation, involves making morphologically modifying acid-based halloysite catalyst then subjecting halloysite catalyst to supercritical carbon dioxide treatment.
AU KIM H J
   LEE H B
   LEE J K
AE KOREA PTG CO LTD (KOPT-Non-standard)
GA 2018773113
AB    NOVELTY - Producing poly(tetramethylene ether) diester involves making a morphologically modifying acid-based halloysite catalyst then subjecting a halloysite catalyst to a supercritical carbon dioxide treatment. The modified halloysite catalysts having a total internal area of from about 2 to about 60 m2/g and polymerizing tetrahydrofuran or 3-methyl tetrahydrofuran with an anhydride having a total of 4-14 carbon atoms in the presence of modified halloysite catalysts to produce poly(tetramethylene ether) diester.
   USE - Method for producing poly(tetramethylene ether) diester for catalyst preparation, and use in alcohol transesterification reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce poly(tetramethylene ether) diester, which high quality of polymer product, and improves properties of mechanically stronger catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The polymerization temperature is 30-65 degrees , and where the number average molecular weight of poly(tetramethylene ether) diester is from about 500 to about 5,000. The polymerization temperature is 35-65 degrees C, and where number average molecular weight is from about 650 to about 4,000. The polymerization temperature is 40-60 degrees C, where number average molecular weight is from about 1,000-3,000, and where anhydride has from 4-10 carbon atoms. The anhydride is represented by group of formulae (I-II), where R1 and R2, independently, have from 1-6 aliphatic or aromatic carbon atoms, and polymerization temperature is from about 30-65 degrees C and where n is from 4-60. The polymerization temperature is from about 35-65 degrees C and where n is from 10-40. The polymerization temperature is from about 40-60 degrees C, and where n is from 20-30. The polymerization temperature is from about 30-65 degrees , where the number average molecular weight of poly(tetramethylene ether) diester is from about 500 to about 5,000. The polymerization temperature is from about 35-65 degrees C, where number average molecular weight is from about 650-4,000. The polymerization temperature is from about 40-60 degrees C, where number average molecular weight is from about 1,000-3,000, where anhydride has from 4 to about 10 carbon atoms. The polymerization temperature is from about 30-65 degrees C, where number average molecular weight of poly(tetramethylene ether) diester is from about 500 to about 5,000. The polymerization temperature is from about 35-65 degrees , where number average molecular weight is from about 650 to about 4,000. The polymerization temperature is from about 40-60 degrees C, where n is from 20-30, where halloysite catalysts in a Korean halloysite or a New Zealand halloysite.. Preferred Process: The process for producing poly(tetramethylene ether) diester involves making a morphologically modified acid-based halloysite catalyst, which comprises subjecting a halloysite catalyst to a supercritical carbon dioxide treatment, where the supercritical treatment temperature is from about 200-290 degrees C. The supercritical treatment pressure is from about 1200-3000 psi, where halloysite catalyst has linear formula of Al2Si2O5(OH)4.2H2O, where the ratio of silicon dioxide (SiO2)to aluminum oxide (Al2O3) is from about 1.7 to about 3.0. The amount of any zeolite compound is about 10 wt.% or less based upon the total weight of halloysite catalyst, where the total internal area of halloysite catalyst is from about 3 to about 20 M2/g and polymerizing tetrahydrofuran or 3-methyl tetrahydrofuran with an anhydride having a total of from 4-14 carbon atoms in the presence of modified halloysite catalysts to produce poly(tetramethylene ether) diester.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Degreasing silkworm pupa useful for extracting silkworm pupa protein or extracting other active substances, comprises e.g. placing silkworm pupa in supercritical carbon dioxide unit, performing supercritical extraction and collecting solids.
AU CHEN G

AB    NOVELTY - Degreasing silkworm pupa comprises placing the silkworm pupa in a supercritical carbon dioxide unit, performing supercritical extraction, where the cosolvent is used as an ethanol solution containing 5-10% ethyl acetate and 0.01-0.02% ofloxacin, collecting solids, de-coloring and deodorizing.
   USE - The degreased silkworm pupa is useful for extracting silkworm pupa protein or extracting other active substances.
   ADVANTAGE - The silkworm pupa retains unsaturated fatty acids, and has high nutritional value, degreases a pupa having high-efficiency, a beautiful milky white color and has no odor; preparation method: maintains the structure and biological activity of protein in silkworm pupa and improves protein solubility.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The supercritical extraction conditions are: an extraction temperature of 35-40 degrees C, an extraction pressure of 30-40 MPa, and an extraction time of 0.6-1 hours; separation tank I pressure is 10-12 MPa, temperature is 40-45 degrees C; separation tank II pressure is 5-7MPa and temperature 15-20 degrees C. The preparation of silkworm pupa comprises placing the pupa in a hydraulic oil pressing device, and pressurizing to oil at room temperature to obtain the primary oil and oil residue. The pressure is 40-60 kg/cm2. The preparation of crude oil further comprises crushing the oil residue, and placing in a screw press device and squeezing.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The ofloxacin composition comprises 80-90% levofloxacin and 10-20% D-ofloxacin.

PN CN108250503-A
TI Preparation of electric cable high temperature-resistant water-resistant material involves adding zinc glutarate and nano kaolin into propylene oxide, dispersing, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and reacting.
AU CHEN Q
   ZAN H
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
GA 201854523B
AB    NOVELTY - An electric cable high temperature-resistant water-resistant material is prepared by adding zinc glutarate and nano kaolin into propylene oxide, dispersing, introducing carbon dioxide gas, reacting to obtain pre-crosslinked polyoxyethylene ester, swelling natural rubber into solvent, adding pre-crosslinked polyoxyethylene ester, mixing to obtain modified natural rubber, adding high temperature silicone rubber, banburying, and cooling.
   USE - Method for preparing electric cable high temperature-resistant water-resistant material.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has good expansion property under high temperature environment, fast expanding speed, high repairing efficiency, has good compression resistance, waterproof and moisture-proof performance, and has good durability. The method is simple and low-cos.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The electric cable high temperature-resistant water-resistant material is prepared by adding 1-2 pts. wt. zinc glutarate and 4-6 pts. wt. nano kaolin into 40-50 pts. wt. propylene oxide, dispersing, introducing excess carbon dioxide gas, reacting for 10-14 hours to obtain pre-crosslinked polyoxyethylene ester, swelling 40-50 pts. wt. natural rubber into 25-45 pts. wt. solvent including benzene, toluene, chlorobenzene, chlorinated naphthalene or carbon tetrachloride, adding 15-25 pts. wt. pre-crosslinked polyoxyethylene ester, mixing at rate of 25-30 revolutions/minute (rpm) to obtain modified natural rubber, adding high temperature silicone rubber according to mass ratio of 1:5-1:4, banburying, and cooling at rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute.

   USE - The protective gas is useful for packaging meat products, preferably fresh meat or fresh meat products under a modified atmosphere (all claimed).

AU XIANG A
   QIN Y
