AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY & BUSINESS (UYTB-C)
GA 201832899Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method of promoting transfer gas separation membrane material composition involves functional group that promotes transfer and zeolite between polyvinyl alcohol matrix, which is a polymer containing amino unit. The amino unit reacts with carbon dioxide to increase the efficiency of carbon dioxide transmission. The substance that promotes the transfer, further includes zeolite containing main crystal cavity of alpha -cage and faujasite cage and containing preset functional group.
   USE - Method of promoting transfer gas separation membrane material composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The transfer gas separation membrane is efficiently prepared by simple method without affecting the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of transfer-promoted gas separation membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The main cavity of alpha -cages and faujasite cages, is 3A, 4A, 5A, 13X, Y or zeolite molecular sieves. The functional groups contained in zeolite molecular sieves are cations. The cation is tetravalent ammonium ion, divalent calcium ion or sodium ion, preferably tetravalent ammonium ion. The mass ratio of zeolite and polyvinyl alcohol is 10:50. Preferred Property: The mean particle size of zeolite is 0.6-1.2 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polymer containing amino unit, is polyimide, polyamide-amine dendrimer (PAMAM) and/or polyethyleneimine, preferably PAMAM. The concentration of polyvinyl alcohol is 5%. The content of PAMAM/polyvinyl alcohol is 5-50 wt.%. Preferred Properties: The degree of alcoholysis of polyvinyl alcohol used is 78%, 88% or 98%. The degree of polymerization of polyvinyl alcohol is 1700-2200, preferably 1700.

PN CN114609322-A
TI Gas phase chromatography analysis of blast furnace            gas constant component involves adding six-way valve            injectors to injection port, installing dryer,            connecting air inlet of one valve with air outlet of            other valve to achieve detection of sample, setting            chromatography conditions, and analyzing.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHENG L
   MA X
AE XINJIANG BAYI IRON & STEEL CO LTD (BAOS-C)
GA 2022831233
AB    NOVELTY - Gas phase chromatography analysis of blast                furnace gas constant component involves: (1) adding                two six-way valve injectors to an injection port,                installing a dryer in front of the injector,                connecting air inlet of one valve with air outlet                of other valve using a latex pipe in series to                achieve the simultaneous detection of sample                between two six-way valves, analyzing hydrogen,                oxygen, nitrogen, carbon monoxide and methane using                5A molecular sieve packed column, and analyzing                carbon dioxide using GDX-104 organic carrier packed                column, and taking a carrier gas comprising not                less than 99.99% argon; (2) setting the                chromatography working conditions, which include an                injection volume of 1 mL, an injector temperature                of 100degreesC, a column oven temperature of                150degreesC, a detector temperature of 90degreesC,                a bridge temperature of 180degreesC, and a carrier                gas flow of 50 mL/minute; (3) setting                chromatography integration parameter; and (4)                analyzing.
   USE - Gas phase chromatography analysis of blast                furnace gas constant component.
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves device cost and carrier gas                use cost, shortens the detection time, improves                working efficiency, reduces labor intensity of the                worker, and accelerates the timeliness of blast                furnace gas analysis. The precision and accuracy of                the detection result satisfy the national standard                requirement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gas phase chromatography analysis of blast                furnace gas constant component involves: (1) adding                two six-way valve injectors to an injection port,                installing a dryer in front of the injector,                connecting air inlet of one valve with air outlet                of other valve using a latex pipe in series to                achieve the simultaneous detection of sample                between two six-way valves, taking a double thermal                conductivity detector and two chromatography                columns with a diameter of 4 mm and a length of 2                m, analyzing hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, carbon                monoxide and methane using 5A molecular sieve                packed column with size of 0.23-0.18 mm and 60-80                mesh, and analyzing carbon dioxide using GDX-104                organic carrier packed column with size of                0.23-0.18 mm and 60-80 mesh, and taking a carrier                gas comprising not less than 99.99% argon; (2)                setting the chromatography working conditions,                which include an injection volume of 1 mL, an                injector temperature of 100degreesC, a column oven                temperature of 150degreesC, a detector temperature                of 90degreesC, a bridge temperature of 180degreesC,                and a carrier gas flow rate of 50 mL/minute; (3)                setting chromatography integration parameter, which                includes a drift rate of 0.3 mV/minute, a noise of                0.02 mV, a minimum peak width of 2, and a relative                window width of 5%; and (4) analyzing by connecting                a conduit of a rubber bladder to an exhaust pipe of                a standard gas storage tank, opening a valve of the                tank, introducing the standard gas into the                bladder, closing the exhaust pipe when the bladder                is full, replacing 3 times, and taking the full                bladder, connecting a bladder catheter to air                inlet, connecting the outlet to the other six-way                valve inlet, connecting the other six-way valve                outlet to a hose, placing in a water tank, sealing                the hose with water, injecting the standard gas                into the six-way valve by extruding the bladder,                replacing air in the valve, switching six-way valve                sampling device to introduce the standard gas into                the chromatography column when the air outlet hose                of the valve placed in the water tank has bubbles,                making chromatography workstation to record                chromatogram, repeating the operation twice, where                the relative deviation of the two peak heights or                peak areas is not greater than 1%, and taking the                average of the two repeatability qualified values                as the standard value, connecting the conduit                filled with the sample bladder to the sampling                device, introducing the sample into the valve inlet                for repeated flushing, then switching the sampling                device to introduce the sample into the                chromatography column, recording the chromatogram,                and recording the chromatography peak data through                a computer data processing software, repeating the                operation twice, where the relative deviation of                the two peak heights or peak areas is not greater                than 1%, and taking the mean used as analytical                value, comparing the chromatogram of the sample                with the retention time of the chromatographic                peaks of the gas samples with known components to                determine the components of each chromatography                peak, and analyzing the content of each component                in the sample using a correction factor by                correction method, and calculating by using a                formula involving numerical value (Xi: %) of the                calculated content of the component the sample,                value (Ai) of chromatograpy peak area of the                component in the sample and correction factor (fi)                for the components in the sample given by,                Xi=Aixfi/total of Aixfix100%. Concentration                correction factor for each group comprises oxygen                at correction factor of 3.86x10-5, nitrogen at                correction factor of 20.57x10-5, carbon monoxide at                correction factor of 23.51x10-5, carbon dioxide at                correction factor of 1.81x10-5, hydrogen at                correction factor of 1.76x10-5 and methane at                correction factor of 8.75x10-5.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. PN CN110079498-A
TI Preparing human placental mesenchymal stem cells            comprises e.g. placing collected placenta in placental            protection solution, storing, passing it to stem cell            bank of collection site, collecting maternal blood,            detecting, and discarding.
AU REN P
   CHEN S
   WEI R
   WU X
   SU Y
   LI J
   ZHANG Y
   LIU X
   LI L
   LI X
AE SHANXI STEM CELL GENE ENG CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201970307U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing human placental mesenchymal stem                cells comprises (i) placing the collected placenta                in placental protection solution containing 40,000                IU/l gentamicin, storing at a constant temperature,                and passing it to stem cell bank of collection site                within 36 hours, (ii) collecting maternal blood,                detecting infectious disease, taking out placenta                after passing test, discarding amniotic membrane                covering outer side, using sterile operation for                cutting placental chorion of about 5 cm2 near the                attachment of the umbilical cord, (iii) soaking the                cut placental chorion into a beaker containing 75%                alcohol, (iv) taking out, washing with D-Hanks                solution, and removing blood vessels, and villus                tissue attached to chorion of placenta, (v) again                washing placental chorion obtained in step (iv)                with D-Hanks solution until there is no blood                color, (vi) cutting the washed chorion into tissue                block of 1-3 mm2, and (vii) uniformly inoculating                tissue block at density of 200 pieces per                bottle.
   USE - The stem cells is useful for treating cell                preparations, cosmetics, health care products, and                medicines for diseases (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: obtains high-purity, and                high-activity mesenchymal stem cells; simplifies                the process flow; saves the cost of enzyme                digestion; accelerates the adherence and growth                rate of primary cells; shortens the cell culture                cycle; avoids introducing more external sources of                interference; makes process stability easier to                control; obtains proliferative ability of human                placental mesenchymal stem cells which is more                stable than other types of mesenchymal stem cells,                and after reaching the P20 generation, it is still                able to proliferate stably, and its cell                morphology; molecular surface antigens and                adipogenic differentiation potential are in line                with the minimum standards for MSC identification                by the International Cell Therapy                Association.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing human placental mesenchymal stem                cells comprises (i) placing the collected placenta                in placental protection solution containing 40,000                IU/l gentamicin, storing at a constant temperature                of 2-8 degrees C, and passing it to stem cell bank                of collection site within 36 hours, (ii) collecting                maternal blood, detecting infectious disease,                taking out placenta after passing test, discarding                amniotic membrane covering outer side, using                sterile operation for cutting placental chorion of                about 5 cm2 near the attachment of the umbilical                cord, (iii) soaking the cut placental chorion into                a beaker containing 75% alcohol for 10-15 seconds,                (iv) taking out, washing with D-Hanks solution, and                removing blood vessels, and villus tissue attached                to chorion of placenta, (v) again washing placental                chorion obtained in step (iv) with D-Hanks solution                until there is no blood color, (vi) cutting the                washed chorion into a tissue block of 1-3 mm2,                (vii) uniformly inoculating the tissue block in the                step (vi) at a density of 200 pieces per bottle                using a climbing method, then culturing at 37                degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide for 3-5 hours, then,                taking out the culture flask, adding 15 ml complete                medium containing 10% fetal calf serum to each                flask, then continuously culturing in an incubator,                (vii) culturing for 5-7 days, taking out the                culture flask, observing the culture bottle wall                under microscope, when a small number of cells of                different sizes and dense cells or the medium has                turned yellow, performing a full amount of liquid                exchange, continuously culturing for 3-5 days, when                multiple cell clusters of different sizes, and                dense cells are observed under a microscope,                obtaining primary cells of human placental                mesenchymal stem cells, (ix) digesting primary                cells of human placental mesenchymal stem cells                with 0.25% trypsin, centrifuging, collecting the                cells, counting, inoculating the cells at a density                of 1x 104-2x 104 cells/cm2 , adding whole medium,                and placing in a 37 degrees C at 5% carbon dioxide                humidified incubator for subculturing, (x)                freeze-storing when the subcultured cells reach                more than 80% confluence, adding human placental                mesenchymal stem cells to cryopreservation                solution, freezing at a density of 2x 106-4x 106                cells/ml, cooling the freeze-stored cells to -80                degrees C for overnight, and transferring to a                liquid nitrogen tank for long-term storage, (xi)                detecting the following items for human placental                mesenchymal stem cells obtained in the step (x),                comprising cell viability, cell number, mycoplasma                detection, sterility testing, cell surface markers,                determining whether the prepared placental                mesenchymal stem cells meet the general                characteristics, and freeze-storing requirements of                mesenchymal stem cells, (xii) taking out frozen                tube, thawing at 37 degrees C, mixing with D-Hanks                solution, centrifuging for removing the frozen                solution, and fetal bovine serum, resuspending the                cells in complete medium containing 10% fetal                bovine serum at a volume fraction of 5% carbon                dioxide, the temperature is 37 degrees C for static                culture, observing the cell adhesion under the                microscope on the next day, and if the cells were                attached, the cell recovery is successful, and                (xiii) establishing a database of human placental                mesenchymal stem cells containing all the                information of the above steps, and associating the                database with the freeze-storing cells of step (x).                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for human                placental mesenchymal stem cell prepared by the                above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (ix),                  centrifuging at the rotation speed of 2000-3000                  rotations per minute for 10 minutes. In the steps                  (ix), and (x), the counting method is subjecting                  the obtained cell suspension to cell counting,                  uniformly mixing 50 mu l cell suspension with 50                  mu l 0.4% trypan blue, pipetting into                  hemocytometer, allowing to stand for 1 minute,                  and observing the count under the microscope. In                  the step (xii), centrifuging at the rotation                  speed of 1200-1800 rotations per minute for 5-7                  minutes. Preferred Components: In the step (ix),                  the cell seeding density during the subculture is                  1x 104 cells/cm2. In the step (x), the                  freezing-stored solution is a mixture of fetal                  bovine serum, and dimethyl sulfoxide in a volume                  ratio of 7:3. The cell density is 2x 106 cells/ml                  when freezing. The complete culture medium                  comprises Dulbecco's modified eagle medium-F12                  (DMEMF12) (Gibco), 0.025 mu g/ml epidermal growth                  factor (Egf), and 10% fetal calf serum.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); T01 (Digital Computers)

PT P
PN WO2022104015-A1; US2022153657-A1; CA3138540-A1; CN114765978-A; TW202225128-A
TI Conversion of carbon oxides to olefins e.g.            ethylene involves generating oxidative coupling of            methane feed stream using renewable hydrogen feed            stream and carbon oxide feed stream, and generating            oxidative coupling of methane effluent comprising using            obtained feed stream and oxidant feed stream.
AU PODREBARAC G G
   KATENDE E
   LIU Z
AE LUMMUS TECHNOLOGY LLC (CBRI-C)
   LUMMUS TECHNOLOGY LLC (CBRI-C)
GA 2022689645
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of carbon oxides to olefins                comprising ethylene involves (a) directing a                renewable hydrogen feed stream and a carbon oxide                feed stream comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbon                monoxide to a methanation reactor to generate an                oxidative coupling of methane (OCM) feed stream                comprising methane, and (b) directing the OCM feed                stream and an oxidant feed stream comprising oxygen                to an OCM reactor comprising an OCM catalyst and                performing an OCM reaction to generate an OCM                effluent comprising (i) 2C or more compounds                comprising ethylene and ethane and (ii) non-2C or                more impurities comprising one or more of carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen and methane. The                method produces olefins with negative carbon                emissions.
   USE - Conversion of carbon oxides to olefins e.g.                ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method converts carbon oxides to olefins                having high carbon efficiency and achieve negative                carbon emissions, and reduces greenhouse gas                emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                (200) for converting carbon oxides to olefins,                which comprises (a) a renewable hydrogen subsystem                (202) configured to generate a renewable hydrogen                feed stream, (b) a methanation subsystem (201)                fluidly coupled to the renewable hydrogen subsystem                and configured to receive the renewable hydrogen                feed stream and a carbon oxide feed stream                comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide                and to generate an oxidative coupling of methane                feed stream comprising methane, (c) an OCM                subsystem (204) downstream of and fluidly coupled                to the methanation subsystem, and (d) a separations                subsystem (206) downstream of and fluidly coupled                to the OCM subsystem and configured to receive the                OCM effluent and separate the OCM effluent into at                least (i) a stream (S1) comprising carbon oxide of                formula: COx, hydrogen, and methane and (ii) a                stream (S2) comprising 2C or more compounds                comprising ethylene and ethane. wherein the OCM                subsystem is configured to receive the OCM feed                stream and an oxidant feed stream comprising oxygen                (O2) to generate an OCM effluent comprising (i) C2+                compounds comprising ethylene and ethane and (ii)                non-2C or more impurities comprising one or more of                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and                methane. The OCM subsystem is configured to receive                the OCM feed stream and an oxidant feed stream                comprising oxygen to generate an OCM effluent                comprising (i) 2C or more compounds comprising                ethylene and ethane and (ii) non-2C or more                impurities comprising one or more of carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and methane.                The system is configured to operate such that                olefins comprising ethylene are produced with                negative carbon emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block flow diagram of the                system for producing olefins.
   System (200)
   Methanation subsystem (201)
   Renewable hydrogen subsystem (202)
   OCM subsystem (204)
   Separations subsystem (206)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The renewable hydrogen                  subsystem comprises an electrolysis unit powered                  by a renewable energy source. The electrolysis                  unit is upstream of and fluidly coupled to the                  OCM subsystem and the methanation subsystem. The                  electrolysis unit is configured to receive water                  and convert the water to renewable hydrogen and                  renewable oxygen and to direct the renewable                  hydrogen as the renewable hydrogen feed stream to                  the methanation subsystem and to direct the                  renewable oxygen as at least a portion of the                  oxidant feed stream to the OCM subsystem. The                  system further comprises a carbon dioxide removal                  unit fluidly coupled to the OCM subsystem, the                  methanation subsystem, and the separations                  subsystem. The carbon dioxide removal unit is                  configured to remove carbon dioxide from the OCM                  effluent, to direct at least a portion of the                  removed carbon dioxide to the methanation                  subsystem, and to direct a substantially carbon                  dioxide-free OCM effluent to the separations                  subsystem. The separations subsystem comprises a                  demethanizer unit and a 2C purification unit. The                  demethanizer unit is fluidly coupled to the                  methanation subsystem and to the 2C purification                  unit. The demethanizer unit is configured to (i)                  receive the OCM effluent, (ii) separate the OCM                  effluent into the streams (S1) and (S2), (iii)                  direct at least a portion of the stream (S1) to                  the methanation subsystem, and (iv) direct the                  stream (S2) to the 2C purification unit. The 2C                  purification unit is fluidly coupled to the OCM                  subsystem and is configured to receive the stream                  (S2) and to separate the stream (S2) into at                  least the stream (S3) comprising ethylene and the                  stream (S4) comprising ethyl. The OCM subsystem                  comprises (i) an OCM reactor containing an OCM                  catalyst and (ii) a post-bed cracking (PBC) unit                  downstream of the OCM catalyst, and the PBC unit                  is configured to receive the stream (S4) and to                  generate ethylene. At least a portion of the                  carbon oxide feed stream is provided by a source                  of captured carbon dioxide. The system further                  comprises a gas treatment unit upstream of and                  fluidly coupled to the methanation subsystem. The                  gas treatment unit is configured to (i) receive a                  natural gas stream containing carbon dioxide,                  (ii) remove carbon dioxide from the natural gas                  stream, and (iii) direct the removed carbon                  dioxide to the methanation subsystem as at least                  a portion of the carbon oxide feed stream.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The renewable hydrogen                  feed stream is generated by at least one of (i)                  water electrolysis, (ii) biomass gasification,                  (iii) ammonia cracking or (iv) hydrogen sulfide                  decomposition, preferably water electrolysis. The                  water electrolysis generates renewable oxygen                  that is used as at least a portion of the oxidant                  feed stream. The carbon oxide feed stream is a                  sole source of carbon utilized in the method. The                  carbon oxide feed stream comprises captured                  carbon dioxide. Preferred Process: The method                  further involves removing water from the OCM feed                  stream and directing the water to an electrolysis                  unit for performing the water electrolysis,                  separating the OCM effluent into at least (i)                  stream (S1) comprising carbon oxide of formula:                  COx, hydrogen, and methane and (ii) stream (S2)                  comprising 2C or more compounds ethylene and                  ethane, directing at least a portion of the                  stream (S1) to the methanation reactor, removing                  carbon dioxide from the OCM effluent and                  directing at least a portion of the removed                  carbon dioxide to the methanation reactor,                  separating the stream (S2) to produce a stream                  (S3) comprising ethylene and a stream (S4)                  comprising ethane, directing the stream (S4) to a                  post-bed cracking unit downstream of the OCM                  catalyst, and directing a makeup stream                  comprising methane to the OCM reactor. The method                  involves directing a natural gas stream                  containing carbon dioxide to a gas treatment unit                  to generate a carbon dioxide feed stream and a                  substantially carbon dioxide-free natural gas                  stream. The method of converting carbon dioxide                  to olefins involves (a) directing a feed stream                  comprising carbon dioxide to a carbon dioxide                  electrolysis unit to generate a renewable                  electrolysis stream (S1) comprising carbon                  monoxide and a renewable electrolysis stream (S2)                  comprising oxygen, directing the feed stream (S1)                  comprising carbon dioxide and the feed stream                  (S2) comprising water to a co-electrolysis unit                  to generate a renewable syngas stream comprising                  carbon monoxide and hydrogen and a renewable                  oxidant stream comprising oxygen, (b) directing                  the renewable syngas stream to a methanation                  reactor to generate an oxidative coupling of                  methane feed stream comprising methane, and                  directing the OCM feed stream and the renewable                  oxidant stream to an OCM reactor comprising an                  OCM catalyst and performing an OCM reaction to                  generate an OCM effluent comprising (i) 2C or                  more compounds comprising ethylene and ethane and                  (ii) non-2C or more impurities comprising one or                  more of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                  hydrogen, and methane. Preferred Composition: The                  molar ratio of the methane in the makeup stream                  to the carbon dioxide in the carbon oxide feed                  stream is 0.01:1-5:1.

PN CN114479055-A
TI New bifunctional catalyst i.e.            (E)-N1-(3,5-di-tert-butylbenzylidene)cyclohexane-1,2-diamine-metal            complex useful in preparing alkylene oxide, cyclic acid            anhydride and carbon dioxide copolymer.
AU LV X
   YUE T
   REN W
   WANG W
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202270121H
AB    NOVELTY - Bifunctional catalyst i.e.                (E)-N1-(3,5-di-tert-butylbenzylidene)cyclohexane-1,2-diamine-metal                complex of formula (I) is new. The bifunctional                catalyst is heteronuclear bimetallic complex in                which two metal centers are connected by biphenyl                skeleton, and is matched with cocatalyst quaternary                ammonium salt to form bicomponent catalytic system.                The cocatalyst quaternary ammonium salt is                tetramethylammonium chloride, tetraethylammonium                chloride, tetraethylammonium bromide,                tetraethylammonium iodide, tetrabutylammonium                fluoride, benzyltriethylammonium chloride,                benzyltripropylammonium chloride,                benzyltributylammonium chloride,                benzyltributylammonium bromide,                benzyltriethylammonium iodide,                cetyltrimethylammonium chloride,                dodecyltrimethylammonium chloride, or                bis(triphenylphosphoranyl)ammonium chloride.
   USE - The bifunctional catalyst is useful for                preparing alkylene oxide, cyclic acid anhydride and                carbon dioxide copolymer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has high activity heteronuclear double                metal complex catalyst. The copolymer has the                properties of aliphatic polyester, polycarbonate                and polyether, has excellent biocompatibility and                biodegradability, and controllable and adjustable                structure. The method: has simple post-process; and                does not discharge the waste, and is suitable for                industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bifunctional catalyst i.e.                (E)-N1-(3,5-di-tert-butylbenzylidene)cyclohexane-1,2-diamine-metal                complex of formula (I) is new. The bifunctional                catalyst is heteronuclear bimetallic complex in                which two metal centers are connected by biphenyl                skeleton, and is matched with cocatalyst quaternary                ammonium salt to form bicomponent catalytic system.                The cocatalyst quaternary ammonium salt is                tetramethylammonium chloride, tetraethylammonium                chloride, tetraethylammonium bromide,                tetraethylammonium iodide, tetrabutylammonium                fluoride, benzyltriethylammonium chloride,                benzyltripropylammonium chloride,                benzyltributylammonium chloride,                benzyltributylammonium bromide,                benzyltriethylammonium iodide,                cetyltrimethylammonium chloride,                dodecyltrimethylammonium chloride, or                bis(triphenylphosphoranyl)ammonium chloride.
   X = Cl-1;
   Y = Cl-1, NO3-1, CH3COO-1or BF4-1;
   M1= Y3+, Lu3+or In3+;
   x, y and z = natural numbers;
   and
   M2= Zn2+or Mg2+;
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                alkylene oxide, cyclic acid anhydride and carbon                dioxide copolymer i.e. compound of formula (IV)                using bifunctional catalyst, comprising adding                two-component catalytic system composed of                hetero-nuclear bimetallic complex and cocatalyst                quaternary ammonium salt into high-pressure                reactor, heating, then adding small amount of                alkylene oxide and initiator, and selectively                adding organic solvent, reacting, filling with                carbon dioxide at a specific pressure, then adding                the alkylene oxide compound of formula (II) and                cyclic acid anhydride compound of formula (III)                into the reactor in batches, keeping the reaction                for a certain period of time, observing that the                system pressure remains constant, and slowly                releasing the unreacted in the reactor, discharging                carbon dioxide, and then vacuum drying to constant                weight to obtain final product. (II) is                dihydrofuran-2,5-dione, furan-2,5-dione,                dihydro-2H-pyran-2,6(3H)-dione, oxepane-2,7-dione,                naphtho[2,3-c]furan-1,3-dione or                isobenzofuran-1,3-dione, and (III) is oxirane,                2-methyloxirane, 2-ethyloxirane, 2-phenyloxirane,                2-(phenoxymethyl)oxirane or                7-oxabicyclo[4.1.0]heptane.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio                  between heteronuclear bimetallic complex and                  cocatalyst in the bifunctional catalyst is 1:1-5,                  the mass ratio between alkylene oxide and                  heteronuclear bimetallic complex is 5000                  -20000:1, and the molar ratio between alkylene                  oxide and cyclic acid anhydride is 2-10:1. The                  initiator is propylene glycol, butylene glycol,                  polyethylene glycol 400, polyethylene glycol 600,                  polypropylene glycol 400 or polypropylene glycol                  600. The organic solvent is dichloromethane,                  dichloroethane, toluene, cyclohexane, n-hexane,                  1,4-dioxane or tetrahydrofuran. The alkylene                  oxide is propylene oxide, ethylene oxide,                  epichlorohydrin, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl                  ether or epoxy cyclohexane. The cyclic acid                  anhydride is succinic anhydride, maleic                  anhydride, glutaric anhydride, adipic anhydride,                  phthalic anhydride or 2,3-naphthalic                  anhydride.Preferred Method: The reaction temperature                  is 25-120&#8451;, preferably                  50-90&#8451;; the carbon dioxide pressure is                  0.1-5 MPa, preferably 0.8-4 MPa; the reaction                  time is 1-12 hours. The reaction temperature is                  50-90&#8451;, and the carbon dioxide pressure                  is 0.8-4MPa. The number-average molecular weight                  of the copolymer is 3,000-100,000 g/mol, and the                  molecular weight distribution is 1.1-1.9, and the                  glass transition temperature of the copolymer is                  -50 to 80&#8451;.

PN US2020061489-A1
TI Method for degassing beer, involves pumping liquid phase from bottom of degas column to preheater, extracting heat from liquid phase preheater and adding heat to bottom of degas column of degas column.
AU VARNAS A K
AE VOGELBUSCH USA INC (VOGE-Non-standard)
GA 2020175414
AB    NOVELTY - The method (500) involves heating (510) beer a preheater. The beer is separated (520) into vapor and liquid phases in a degas column, the degas column having a top and a bottom. The vapor phase is removed (530) from the top of the degas column to a degas condenser. The vapor phase is removed (540) from the top of the degas column to a degas condenser. The condensed portion of the vapor phase is returned (550) to the top of the degas column. The uncondensed portion of the vapor phase is removed (560) to a carbon dioxide scrubber. The liquid phase is pumped (570) from the bottom of the degas column to the preheater. The heat is extracted (580) from the liquid phase the preheater. The heat is added (590) to the bottom of the degas column of the degas column.
   USE - Method for degassing beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The load translates into a reduction in the amount of energy consumed at a given feed rate or allow the existing beer column to handle an increased feed rate for a given beer column diameter. Degassing the beer reduces the load on the existing beer column. Removing carbon dioxide from the beer increases the capacity of existing distillation equipment by reducing the load and energy that is used to handle the carbon dioxide dissolved in the beer. The absence of carbon dioxide in a vacuum system unit that the same amount of degassed beer is processed with a smaller, lower energy vacuum pump and reduced seal water requirements to maintain the vacuum. The capacity of an existing rectifying column overhead condenser is increased by improving the heat transfer coefficient, improving the log mean temperature difference of the condenser, and decreasing the vapor flow to the condenser.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating method for degassing beer using degassing module.
   Method for degassing beer (500)
   Step for heating beer a preheater (510)
   Step for separating the beer into vapor and liquid phases in a degas column, the degas column having a top and a bottom (520)
   Step for removing the vapor phase from the top of the degas column to a degas condenser (530)
   Step for removing the vapor phase from the top of the degas column to a degas condenser (540)
   Step for returning the condensed portion of the vapor phase to the top of the degas column (550)
   Step for removing the uncondensed portion of the vapor phase to a carbon dioxide scrubber (560)
   Step for pumping the liquid phase from the bottom of the degas column to the preheater (570)
   Step for extracting the heat from the liquid phase the preheater (580)
   Step for adding the heat to the bottom of the degas column of the degas column (590)

PN US2018153117-A1; US10548269-B2
TI Computer-based method of aerating or distributing nutrients in saline aquaculture system for use in open water, involves disturbing settlement in liquid.
AU SUN W H
   SUN H M
AE MARINE AGRIFUTURE LLC (MARI-Non-standard)
   MARINE AGRIFUTURE LLC (MARI-Non-standard)
GA 201845018C
AB    NOVELTY - The method (300) involves acquiring (S310) a second set of environmental condition data using a sensor. The second set of environmental condition data comprises oxygen content data, carbon dioxide content data, nitrogen content data, and nutrient content data of a liquid surrounding the platform. The second set of environmental condition data is compared (S320) to first set of environmental condition data acquired at previous time point using the computing device. A change in the amount of oxygen, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, and nutrients in the liquid is determined. The amount of nutrients, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide are reduced, and an aeration device is activated (S330) in the liquid, when determined that the amount of oxygen is increased. A settlement in the liquid is disturbed. The aeration device distributes nutrients and oxygen to provide to a salt-tolerant plant growing in the plant-receiving receptacle of increased carbon dioxide, increased nitrogen, and increased nutrients.
   USE - Computer-based method of aerating or distributing nutrients in saline aquaculture system for use in open water e.g. ocean and dead sea.
   ADVANTAGE - The barrier helps to retain the top surface and bottom surface of the platform in the liquid by adding weight and increasing drag or resistance to movement to prevent the platform from flipping over as a result of wind or waves, especially when the plants on the platform are small and light. The saltwater distribution device functions to reduce or prevent the negative side effects of freshwater accumulation by irrigating the salt-tolerant plants positioned in the top surface of the platform with saltwater. The system automatically alerts the user to enhance the plant growth or make a replacement with new plants to control the growth of algae, when the amount or concentration of pigment in the liquid reaches a threshold, indicating the content of algae is high and the plants are getting weak. The distributing saltwater on a surface of the platform results in reduced accumulation of insects and caterpillars on the platform and the salt-tolerant plants, and overall improved survival of the salt-tolerant plants, especially after monsoon-like or storm conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a saline aquaculture system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for aerating and distributing nutrients in a saline aquaculture system.
   Method of aerating or distributing nutrients in saline aquaculture system (300)
   Step for acquiring a second set of environmental condition data (S310)
   Step for comparing second set of environmental condition data to a first set of environmental condition data acquired at a previous time point using the computing device (S320)
   Step for reducing the amount of nutrients, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide, and activating an aeration device in the liquid, when determined that the amount of oxygen is increased (S330)
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

GA 202293981B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alpha-olefin by hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide comprises (1) mixing raw material                gas carbon monoxide with hydrogen and circulating                gas and preheating to certain temperature as                reaction feed, (2) sending the preheated reaction                feed to the reactor and fully mixing with the                catalyst to carry out hydrogenation reaction, (3)                after the reaction effluent is cooled, performing                the gas-liquid separation, (4) separating the                liquid-phase hydrocarbons into light hydrocarbons                of 1-6c and heavy hydrocarbons of 6c+ in the                lightening tower, and (5) separating the heavy                hydrocarbons of 6c+ into light hydrocarbons of                7-10c and heavy hydrocarbons of 11c+ in the                deweighting tower, sending one part to the                absorption tower to be used as an absorbent, and                using the other part as a product out of the                device.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                alpha-olefin by hydrogenation of carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process flow, mild                operating conditions, and high carbon dioxide                single-pass conversion rate and alpha olefin                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing alpha-olefin by hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide comprises (1) mixing raw material                gas carbon monoxide with hydrogen and circulating                gas and preheating to certain temperature as                reaction feed, (2) sending the preheated reaction                feed to the reactor and fully mixing with the                catalyst to carry out hydrogenation reaction,                during the hydrogenation reaction, continuously                removing the heat released by the reaction, and the                mixed gas generated by the reaction, i.e., the                reaction effluent, flowing out from the outlet of                the reactor, (3) after the reaction effluent is                cooled, performing the gas-liquid separation,                firstly absorbing the separated gas phase by the                absorbent in the absorption tower after the light                hydrocarbon components in the gas phase are                absorbed, after purification, adding to the                reaction feed as recycle gas, and conveying the                separated liquid-phase hydrocarbons to the                light-removing tower, (4) separating the                liquid-phase hydrocarbons into light hydrocarbons                of 1-6c and heavy hydrocarbons of 6c+ in the                lightening tower, taking out the light hydrocarbons                of 1-6c of the device as products, and sending the                heavy hydrocarbons of 6c+ to the deweighting tower,                and (5) separating the heavy hydrocarbons of 6c+                into light hydrocarbons of 7-10c and heavy                hydrocarbons of 11c+ in the deweighting tower,                discharging the light hydrocarbons of 7-10c from                the device as products, dividing the heavy                hydrocarbons of 11c+ into two parts, sending one                part to the absorption tower to be used as an                absorbent, and using the other part as a product                out of the device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The feed gases carbon                  dioxide and hydrogen and the recycle gas are                  separately pressurized before mixing. The                  reaction feed is preheated from the reaction                  effluent. During the hydrogenation reaction, the                  heat released from the reaction is removed by                  co-producing steam or a heat carrier. The                  reaction effluent is cooled by the reaction feed,                  or the reaction feed is cooled first, and then                  further cooled by other medium fluids. After                  absorbing the light hydrocarbon components in the                  gas phase, the absorbent is mixed with the liquid                  phase hydrocarbons separated from the gas and                  liquid, and enters the light removal tower                  together. The volume ratio of the raw material                  gas carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1:2-4. The                  reaction temperature of the hydrogenation                  reaction is 200-500&#176;C, and the reaction                  pressure is 1-10 MPa. The separation temperature                  of the gas-liquid separation of the reaction                  effluent is 10-60&#176;C and the separation                  pressure is 1-5 MPa. The volume ratio of the raw                  material gas carbon dioxide and hydrogen is                  1:2-4. The reaction temperature of the                  hydrogenation reaction is 200-500&#176;C and                  the reaction pressure is 1-10 Mpa. The separation                  temperature of the gas-liquid separation of the                  reaction effluent is 10-60&#176;C and the                  separation pressure is 1-5 MPa.

PN CN111099977-A
TI Preparation of 3-acetylpropanol by adding furfural or furfuryl alcohol to preparation device, evaporating with high temperature steam, mixing water vapor and furfural evaporating gas, and reacting with carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
AU CHENG J
   HONG J
   ZHU L
   CHENG S
   FANG B
   CHENG D
AE ANHUI JIXI HUIHUANG CHEM CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202041967Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 3-acetylpropanol includes adding furfural or furfuryl alcohol as reaction raw material to preparation device, introducing high temperature steam to evaporate raw material, mixing water vapor and furfural evaporating gas to obtain mixed gas, and flowing upwards; introducing carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the middle of the device, mixing with prepared mixed gas to obtain reaction alkali, and partially generating carbon dioxide and water vapor to form an in-situ acid system; placing supported ruthenium-based catalyst at the position where the reaction alkali material passes; and reacting with in-situ acid system, adding mixed gas to condenser tube, cooling, liquefying, and collecting high-purity product. The supported ruthenium-based catalyst's carrier is activated carbon and/or mesoporous carbon (CMK-3).
   USE - The method is for preparation of 3-acetylpropanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively solves the problem of prior art, such as relatively high cost, small contact area between the reactants and catalyst during the reaction, low reaction efficiency and product purity, can directly collect unreacted raw materials, reduces production costs, increases utilization rate of raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of 3-acetylpropanol comprises adding furfural or furfuryl alcohol as reaction raw material to preparation device, introducing 290-310 degrees C high temperature steam from the bottom of device to evaporate raw material, mixing furfural evaporating gas and water vapor to obtain mixed gas, and flowing upwards together in the device; introducing hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the middle of the device, mixing with prepared mixed gas to obtain reaction alkali, and partially generating carbon dioxide and water vapor to form an in-situ acid system as reaction medium; placing supported ruthenium-based catalyst at the position where the reaction alkali material passes; and reacting with in-situ acid system, adding mixed gas to condenser tube to fully cool down, liquefying, and collecting high-purity product. The preparation device comprises tank body, reaction tube, condensation tube, cavity opened inside the tank body, pressure regulating plate provided with uniformly arranged first through holes, air inlet hole, high pressure hot steam connected below the air inlet hole, heating wire provided inside the holding tank used for holding furfural, baffle plate provided with air guide holes and mounting hole, air inlet duct connected to gas storage tank, condensation ring, annular condensation chamber, supported ruthenium-based catalyst fixed on inner surface of reaction tube, collection tank, storage tank, and outlet duct. The supported ruthenium-based catalyst's carrier is activated carbon and/or mesoporous carbon (CMK-3). The supported ruthenium-based catalyst has ruthenium active component loading amount of 1-5 wt.%.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110540226-A; CN110540226-B
TI Preparing high-density calcium carbonate powder involves mixing and stirring calcium hydroxide and hot water to prepare calcium hydroxide slurry, adjusting concentration and temperature of the slurry and removing impurities.
AU YAN P
AE ZHEJIANG JIANDE ZHENGFA PHARM CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019A7035Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-density calcium carbonate powder involves mixing and stirring calcium hydroxide and hot water to prepare calcium hydroxide slurry, adjusting concentration and temperature of the slurry and removing impurities, adding calcium hydroxide slurry with adjusted concentration and temperature in carbonization tank. The slurry level is 20-50% of the height of the carbonization tank, start stirrer and pass in carbon dioxide gas to make calcium hydroxide and carbon dioxide synthesize, slurry pH gradually decreases. The reaction is completed and slurry pH reaches 7. The calcium hydroxide slurry is added into carbonization tank again until slurry pH reaches 13 or more, stirred, and continue carbonization reaction to return calcium carbonate slurry pH to 7, repeat operation until material level in the carbonization tank reaches 100%, turn on slurry pump to remove slurry with a pH value of 7 out of carbonization tank until slurry level in the carbonization tank is 20-70%.
   USE - Method for preparing high-density calcium carbonate powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-density calcium carbonate powder that ensuring product density and reduces production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-density calcium carbonate powder involves mixing and stirring calcium hydroxide and hot water to prepare calcium hydroxide slurry, adjusting concentration and temperature of the slurry and removing impurities, adding calcium hydroxide slurry with adjusted concentration and temperature in carbonization tank. The slurry level is 20-50% of the height of the carbonization tank, start stirrer and pass in carbon dioxide gas to make calcium hydroxide and carbon dioxide synthesize, slurry pH gradually decreases. The reaction is completed and slurry pH reaches 7. The calcium hydroxide slurry is added into carbonization tank again until slurry pH reaches 13 or more, stirred, and continue carbonization reaction to return calcium carbonate slurry pH to 7, repeat operation until material level in the carbonization tank reaches 100%, turn on slurry pump to remove slurry with a pH value of 7 out of carbonization tank until slurry level in the carbonization tank is 20-70%. The calcium hydroxide slurry is sufficient, continue to repeat until the raw pulp has completely synthesized calcium carbonate particles, pH value is 7 and reaction is considered complete, cut off the introduction of carbon dioxide gas and remove calcium carbonate slurry. The calcium carbonate slurry is dehydrated with centrifuge to form filter cake with 20-22% moisture, filter cake density is 2.3-2.5g/ml. The filter cake is dried until moisture is below 0.5%. The dried filter cake is powdered by depolymerization machine and packaged to make high-density calcium carbonate powder having tap density of 1.1-1.3g/ml.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The density of the filter cake obtained is 2.4g/ml, and density of the high-density calcium carbonate powder obtained is 1.2g/ml.

PN WO2019215763-A1; IN201811017844-A; IN396965-B
TI Shelf-life extending sheet used for fresh fruits, vegetables and flowers comprises substrate coated by chemical e.g. ethylene adsorbers, ethylene oxidizers and carbon dioxide releasers providing with over-coat or additional substrate layer.
AU SAREEN S
   SAREEN M
AE SAREEN S (SARE-Individual)
   SAREEN M (SARE-Individual)
   SAREEN S (SARE-Individual)
   SAREEN M (SARE-Individual)
GA 201995348G
AB    NOVELTY - Shelf-life extending sheet (S1) for fresh fruits, vegetables and flowers comprises a substrate coated with at least one chemical comprising ethylene adsorbers, ethylene oxidizers, carbon dioxide releasers, oxygen absorbers or reducers, microbial/bio inhibitors and moisture absorbers, which is provided with an over-coat or additional substrate layer/s so as to prevent direct contact of chemicals with food.
   USE - The shelf-life extending sheets are useful for fresh fruits, vegetables and flowers (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The shelf-life extending sheets: provide higher surface area; requires lesser space for installation; are available in different sizes; are cost-effective; and are capable of extending the life of fresh fruits and vegetables by up to 3 times or more compared to unprotected storage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) shelf-life extending sheet (S2) comprising a substrate coated with a mixture of a carrier and at least one chemical comprising ethylene adsorbers, ethylene oxidizers, carbon dioxide releasers, oxygen absorbers or reducers, microbial/bio inhibitors and moisture absorbers, which is provided with an over-coat or additional substrate layer/s so as to prevent direct contact of chemicals with food;
   (2) shelf-life extending sheet (S3) comprising a substrate impregnated with at least one chemical comprising ethylene adsorbers, ethylene oxidizers, carbon dioxide releasers, oxygen absorbers or reducers, microbial/bio inhibitors and moisture absorbers, which is provided with an over-coat so as to prevent direct contact of chemicals with food;
   (3) shelf-life extending sheet (S4) comprising a substrate impregnated with at least one chemical comprising ethylene adsorbers, ethylene oxidizers, carbon dioxide releasers, oxygen absorbers or reducers, microbial/bio inhibitors and moisture absorbers, which is provided as a sandwich between substrate sheets to prevent direct contact with the food, alternatively at least one of the chemicals is encapsulated within substrate sheets;
   (4) manufacturing the shelf-life extending sheet (S1) or (S2) comprising coating of at least one of the chemicals on the substrate followed by over coating by the over coat, where the chemical(s) can be mixed with the carrier before the step of coating on the substrate;
   (5) manufacturing the shelf-life extending sheet (S3) comprising mixing of at least one of the chemicals in pulp of the substrate to manufacture the substrate followed by over coating by the over coat; and
   (6) manufacturing shelf-life extending sheet (S4) comprising mixing of at least one of the chemicals followed by encapsulation within the substrates and sealing, alternately impregnation of a substrate with at least one of the chemicals, which is sand-witched between the substrates.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The substrate is having thickness of 10-300 mu /10-10000 gsm which comprises paper, plastic, card board sheet, fabric (non woven fabric or woven fabric made from natural or man made fibers) constituting a carrier for the chemicals. The amount of the chemicals, namely ethylene adsorbers, ethylene oxidizers, carbon dioxide releasers, oxygen absorbers or reducers, microbial/ bio inhibitors and moisture absorbers is of 0.1-500 g/m2. The chemicals include sodium metabisulfite for releasing sulfur dioxide anti microbial vapors, zeolites for absorbing ethylene gas, activated clay for absorbing moisture, activated alumina for absorbing permanganates which further oxidize ethylene vapors, activated charcoal for absorbing gases, permanganates as ethylene oxidizers, organic acids or silver or copper particles as microbial inhibitors, sodium bi carbonate as carbon dioxide releaser. The carrier includes water, solvent includes methanol, ethanol, butanol, toluene and adhesive made from polyurethane or polyvinyl alcohols for the purpose of coating. The over-coat includes a polymer film or aluminum film or another sheet of paper or a breathable/non breathable substrate.
